# Thread: Using mirrors to boost sunlight in winter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2023-12-29T18:20:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329583>

Has anyone here tried using mirrors to amplify sun/UV exposure in winter (or summer if desired)?

For example, a rotating platform with 6 tall mirrors all calibrated/angled to reflect light to the center. Rotate the platform to face the sun as it moves (calibrated to work during a specific hour of day/sun elevation)

Can any reflective material efficiently reflect IR light? Would this effectively create a man-made lighting situation as the frequencies are modified from the reflection?

### Marko Pollo — 2023-12-29T18:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329586>

I have a solar oven that uses reflective panels and it works well. I would be wary of anything that might intensify the sunlight too much like ants under a magnifying glass. Matte white paint might hold in and reflect light while also diffusing it to remove hotspots. A small pod that blocks the wind, retains some heat and intensifies light lives in my head.

### rtd — 2023-12-29T19:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329589>

Big TVs from late 90s early 00s had a fresnel lens. That lens could start fires, melt pennies, etc.

### coho3131 — 2023-12-30T01:02:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329607>

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=329583)
>
> Has anyone here tried using mirrors to amplify sun/UV exposure in winter (or summer if desired)?
>
> For example, a rotating platform with 6 tall mirrors all calibrated/angled to reflect light to the center. Rotate the platform to face the sun as it moves (calibrated to work during a specific hour of day/sun elevation)
>
> Can any reflective material efficiently reflect IR light? Would this effectively create a man-made lighting situation as the frequencies are modified from the reflection?

Yes. Sunlight reflected off glass or metal is nnEMF.

### Kendall Toerner — 2023-12-30T19:07:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329635>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=329607)
>
> Yes. Sunlight reflected off glass or metal is nnEMF.

Maybe I'll make water-based mirrors or use white tree bark lol

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-31T01:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329657>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=329607)
>
> Yes. Sunlight reflected off glass or metal is nnEMF.

Honest question - why? Yes, the type of glass would be a factor but is sun light reflected off water nnEMF? If not then why would light reflected off other shinny surfaced be?

### coho3131 — 2023-12-31T01:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329661>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329657)
>
> Honest question - why? Yes, the type of glass would be a factor but is sun light reflected off water nnEMF? If not then why would light reflected off other shinny surfaced be?

This is my understanding.

Glass and metal absorb important amounts of the native EMF in unnatural ways, and the reflection is nnEMF.

Sunlight reflected off of bodies of water, or ice and snow (frozen water), is similar to sunlight reflected off of clouds (water and ice crystals).

Even on a day with no clouds, the sunlight is reflecting off of water vapor.

We are adapted to sunlight reflecting off of water and other surfaces in Nature, but not to sunlight reflecting off of artificial surfaces.

# Thread: The BioCharger™ NG  - Any thoughts or experience?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biocharger%E2%84%A2-ng-any-thoughts-or-experience.28466/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sara Pugh — 2024-01-01T22:25:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biocharger%E2%84%A2-ng-any-thoughts-or-experience.28466/post-329717>

Dear all
I am not a big fan of ;large plug in the wall devices and prefer the natural way if all possible with quantum biology. but I am a light and bulb person if I must use electricity- but I am not closed to the idea of machines. Not PEMF though

I was asked about this device recently BioCharger [https://biocharger.com/](https://biocharger.com/?fbclid=IwAR0F9GgE-Zxs4IWbj0Xh6XVeoK1w8gygovF6t4fvNs3z4zfdNDSYmuTd56g). “The BioCharger™ NG produces a customizable sweep of digitally pulsed electromagnetic fields that bathes the whole body in a rich field of bio-compatible frequencies, generating bio-photon light via plasma gas spectrum tubes.” This is from one of my clients and she said Tony Robbins has it at his events , this put me off a bit- Has any one tried this or have any thoughts on it ? I have looked at the spec and some of the actual physics is beyond me at the moment. Im skeptical as it is PEMF, has a Wifi element at the Hz at which it operates is not explained in a way I am convinced. I have some understanding of how toroid waves are key to biology but not sure if I am convinced about this machine. So if its BS Im happy to put the word out

Sara

### Brent Patrick — 2024-01-02T03:23:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biocharger%E2%84%A2-ng-any-thoughts-or-experience.28466/post-329723>

This device coped alot of heat (no pun intended) several years ago here in OZ our resident alternative wellness "expert" Peter Evans was promoting it to cure COVID and many other ills but had no proof of work to back up his claims.

I cannot comment on the device in of itself!

### [TGA fines Pete Evans more than $25,000 after coronavirus claims](https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-04-24/pete-evans-fined-25000-by-tga-over-coronavirus-biocharger-claims/12183050)

Celebrity chef Pete Evans is issued two infringement notices by the Therapeutic Goods Administration over claims he made about the BioCharger "hybrid subtle energy revitalisation platform".

 www.abc.net.au

As you already know Sara nothing beats the Sun for all its transitional qualities

### NikkiYork — 2024-01-02T09:15:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biocharger%E2%84%A2-ng-any-thoughts-or-experience.28466/post-329728>

I called the company and tried to get information out of them. The sales people don’t even know how that thing works and what it really does.
The guy kept telling me “you have a band I stead of one instrument” it was completely unsatisfactory.
I sat in front of it 3 times a week for 6 month, because it came with my gym. I don’t know if it did anything.
I’ve talked to a researcher about it and his thoughts were that the technologies would cancel each other out partially….

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biocharger%E2%84%A2-ng-any-thoughts-or-experience.28466/post-329747>

> [Sara Pugh said:](/goto/post?id=329717)
>
> Dear all
> I am not a big fan of ;large plug in the wall devices and prefer the natural way if all possible with quantum biology. but I am a light and bulb person if I must use electricity- but I am not closed to the idea of machines. Not PEMF though
>
> I was asked about this device recently BioCharger [https://biocharger.com/](https://biocharger.com/?fbclid=IwAR0F9GgE-Zxs4IWbj0Xh6XVeoK1w8gygovF6t4fvNs3z4zfdNDSYmuTd56g). “The BioCharger™ NG produces a customizable sweep of digitally pulsed electromagnetic fields that bathes the whole body in a rich field of bio-compatible frequencies, generating bio-photon light via plasma gas spectrum tubes.” This is from one of my clients and she said Tony Robbins has it at his events , this put me off a bit- Has any one tried this or have any thoughts on it ? I have looked at the spec and some of the actual physics is beyond me at the moment. Im skeptical as it is PEMF, has a Wifi element at the Hz at which it operates is not explained in a way I am convinced. I have some understanding of how toroid waves are key to biology but not sure if I am convinced about this machine. So if its BS Im happy to put the word out
>
> Sara

In review of the BioCharger device again, I looked at it many years ago -> here's a link to its specs -> [https://biocharger.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Product-Spec-1.pdf](https://biocharger.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Product-Spec-1.pdf)

Please notice that they do not inform you on the polarity of the electrical charged ion emitted from the device.

What we know from nature is that air can conduct charge. Depending on the polarity of that charge will determine what biological influence it will have onto and into your body.

For example: In Boulder Colorado (and surrounding areas) where the dry air blows above 60 miles an hour, a greater positively charge builds up in the air. Generally speaking, its not a good thing. Boulder Colorado like many places like it are high altitude deserts.

However, in a tropical environment where the moist air blows regularly as in El Salvador, a greater negative charge builds up in the air. Generally speaking this is a good thing for all living things like plants and humans.

May I recommend you study what this "polarity" means and does to your human cells.

I have written up many papers on the subject. Here's one for your reference -> "Melanosomes - Our Photo-Electro-Magnetic Power Plants" [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/page-10#post-329692](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/page-10#post-329692)

Grandpa John

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-03T18:33:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-glasses-when-working-on-screens-outdoor.28470/post-329772>

There should be no need for any blue blocking glasses if you are outside during the day.

You want your eyes to be able to eat all frequencies of sunlight.

The sun has blue light as well... and **you** **need** **the sun's blue** (which is perfectly packaged with protective IR) during the day as part of optimizing cortisol levels and anchoring circadian rhythm.

This will be tough to get if you are wearing glasses that block all blue wavelengths.

I'm wondering if anyone has any relevant information as I begin searching for info on protection from nnEMF via geographical features and how helpful it could be. I'm currently in the process of finding an optimal place to live, as many others here likely are.

The idea I'm pondering is to live somewhere outside of a town or small city, far enough and within geographical features that could protect from the nnEMF. Traveling away from the city for activities outdoors, and periodically traveling to the city when needed to avoid complete social isolation.

In the past few months I have tried living in New Zealand, Martinique, and Colorado Springs. Most recently, Colorado Springs is very appealing for many personal reasons but is a nnEMF nightmare with the military bases and of course the 5G like everywhere else.

Could living in a pocket outside of a place like Colorado Springs as shown below mitigate a large amount of the nnEMF? Does anyone have any blog links or resources relating to this? I'm most concerned about the military bases' radar:

### Saichi — 2024-01-05T02:27:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geographical-nnemf-protection.28474/post-329842>

Everyone here has to measure for themselves with a Safe and Sound Pro II. Even isolated places can have moderate to high levels with invisible towers respect to line of sight especially in forested areas.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-05T08:15:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geographical-nnemf-protection.28474/post-329852>

nnEMF is made up of electric fields, magnetic fields as well as radio frequency (RF). As important as it is to avoid/minimize RF from cell towers, radar etc., for most people their greatest exposure to nnEMF is inside their home, from the wires in their walls and all the appliances and devices plugged into them, PLUS all the unnatural (blue) light. I can’t imagine finding the “ideal” place to live surrounded by military bases. I would recommend maximizing your natural EMF (sun light) to be your priority and find a “cave” as far south as possible. Have you looked into underground and/or dome houses?

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-05T23:53:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geographical-nnemf-protection.28474/post-329877>

Agreed, I use a Cemprotec 34 right now.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329852)
>
> nnEMF is made up of electric fields, magnetic fields as well as radio frequency (RF). As important as it is to avoid/minimize RF from cell towers, radar etc., for most people their greatest exposure to nnEMF is inside their home, from the wires in their walls and all the appliances and devices plugged into them, PLUS all the unnatural (blue) light. I can’t imagine finding the “ideal” place to live surrounded by military bases. I would recommend maximizing your natural EMF (sun light) to be your priority and find a “cave” as far south as possible. Have you looked into underground and/or dome houses?

Absolutely, already on top of all of that.

The main last variable I'm unable to control is what surrounds where I live.

Trying to achieve good sunlight strength, optimal magnetic flux, minimize high altitude for long durations, avoid deserts, airports, military, 5G town centers, power lines, etc. Then there's the whole mold situation so a non hermetically sealed home is ideal in a location optimal for mitochondria with plenty of moisture and conducive to life. And be close enough to others my age, avoiding social isolation. Pretty tough to combine all those variables and also find a place I enjoy living with activities and things to do in nature. All at an affordable price.

Love the idea of a "cave"/underground/dome/eco house. Thank you for bringing this up. Pretty ideal for energy usage as well.

# Thread: What to do... with people(!) at night..

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### wade s neumann — 2024-01-05T00:19:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-with-people-at-night.28477/post-329834>

My choices are mine to honour myself with..
At that point nobody in my family minds me changing out lights and modifying access to stimulants after dark..
But they do mind the change in culture.
If I am to lead my family a little stronger down this path, the major resistance has been to \*talking\* and \*sharing\* in that space at night in which they'd have once 'checked out'.
it seems the appeal of a person that you're with can't compete anymore with diffuse electronic pseudo-connection. sad.
Looking for ideaas on how to add some real quality to that time, into the evening, so that the benefit ratio increases. Wish me luck!! hahah!

How big was that cultural shift for you all, seasoned swans?

### coho3131 — 2024-01-05T02:01:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-with-people-at-night.28477/post-329840>

cooking, board games, cards, puzzles, and other games with candlelight or the fireplace during the short light days

I feel much better not watching movies, shows, or video games.

Which test is safer for breast cancer patient - MRI or mammogram?

### NikkiYork — 2024-01-07T22:00:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mri-or-mammogram.28480/post-329948>

# Thread: Google and Meta Patents for manipulating people through screens
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-and-meta-patents-for-manipulating-people-through-screens.28487/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### petrichor — 2024-01-07T20:52:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-and-meta-patents-for-manipulating-people-through-screens.28487/post-329944>

I heard Dr Kruse on a podcast recently refer to patents owned by Google and Meta that prove that they know how devices can be used to lower dopamine in users. I found the Google patent, but I am not having any luck finding the Meta patent. Does anyone know which patent he was refering to?

Here is Google's:

**Nervous system manipulation by electromagnetic fields from monitors**
[https://patents.google.com/patent/US6506148B2/en](https://patents.google.com/patent/US6506148B2/en)

> ### Abstract​
>
> Physiological effects have been observed in a human subject in response to stimulation of the skin with weak electromagnetic fields that are pulsed with certain frequencies near ½ Hz or 2.4 Hz, such as to excite a sensory resonance. Many computer monitors and TV tubes, when displaying pulsed images, emit pulsed electromagnetic fields of sufficient amplitudes to cause such excitation. It is therefore possible to manipulate the nervous system of a subject by pulsing images displayed on a nearby computer monitor or TV set. For the latter, the image pulsing may be imbedded in the program material, or it may be overlaid by modulating a video stream, either as an RF signal or as a video signal. The image displayed on a computer monitor may be pulsed effectively by a simple computer program. For certain monitors, pulsed electromagnetic fields capable of exciting sensory resonances in nearby subjects may be generated even as the displayed images are pulsed with subliminal intensity.

### NikkiYork — 2024-01-07T22:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-and-meta-patents-for-manipulating-people-through-screens.28487/post-329949>

That’s the expired patent of an individual. Not google‘s from what I see.

I guess the guy wanted to sell his tech and wasn‘t successful

### petrichor — 2024-01-08T20:44:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-and-meta-patents-for-manipulating-people-through-screens.28487/post-329989>

You're right. I made the Google association based on [a comment by Dr Kruse here](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/pfbid0wUWsyhzw7pKPCFyNzhZ4AdECEdxuxFpMnCqFPaZBCANKvYL2DbmdyYh3UEeV4h3ul?comment_id=2335004829897191&reply_comment_id=2335189329878741). It looks like [he may have gotten this](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-certain-are-you-that-things-are-well-in-school.17757/#post-191790) idea originally from Dave Wigington's Geoengineering watch website, but [the article is no longer available](http://www.geoengineeringwatch.org/us-patent-6506148-nervous-system-manipulation-by-electromagnetic-fields-from-monitors/), and I was not able to corroborate that claim doing my own research.

I do see that the patent holder has several patents: [https://patents.justia.com/inventor/hendricus-g-loos](https://patents.justia.com/inventor/hendricus-g-loos)
And appears to have been a real person: [https://obits.ocregister.com/us/obituaries/orangecounty/name/hendricus-loos-obituary?id=15581042](https://obits.ocregister.com/us/obituaries/orangecounty/name/hendricus-loos-obituary?id=15581042)

Beyond that, there's just a bunch of speculation on conspiracy and sci-fi/tech oriented websites.

More importantly though, the patent suggests that this technology is possible, and the fact that it is expired means any company can now make use of it without risk of legal infringement.

### jeremiasbtome — 2024-01-08T13:05:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-5-ldl-cholestrol-danger.28489/post-329965>

Got blood tests and my doctor said I had elevated ldl cholestrol at 3.5, is this bad

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-09T00:13:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-5-ldl-cholestrol-danger.28489/post-329996>

LDL is not inherently bad. A healthy individual can have a high LDL level

Specifically, sdLDL (small, dense LDL) is the one to watch out for that can be easily oxidized and create lots of issues.

LDL gets a bad rep bc of sdLDL. But, just bc LDL is high, doesn't necessarily mean sdLDL is as well

# Thread: POMC and sweeteners POMC and gender
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pomc-and-sweeteners-pomc-and-gender.28493/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sara Pugh — 2024-01-09T19:37:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pomc-and-sweeteners-pomc-and-gender.28493/post-330020>

1.  Do low-calorie sweeteners (LCS) disrupt POMC regulation? This paper (it is mice who are nocturnal and in blue light) suggests that maternal LCS consumption results in POMC dysregulation and increased adiposity and glucose intolerance in male offsprings but not in female offsprings (NB: LCS here included aspartame and rebaudioside A (highly purified form of stevia)). [https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/156397](https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/156397?fbclid=IwAR14uvRKAgjkLVEh65bm61qWcQ1KdpoxavL1vWn9sfI4L3uuARp0yZiyX_Y)
2.  POMC regulation of energy balance and adiposity in males and females? This study suggests that males have less POMC neuronal activity and are less responsive to leptin and more responsive to insulin. This would make sense as leptin has a lot more to do in a female regading the complexity of a cycle. How else or how different is POMC signalling different in males and females as females are more sensitive to light [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9322714/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9322714/?fbclid=IwAR25NZnXQhgeGyDdlTapJCz-WQNuPK2e8o4EzTsr4btjUvrJQQx0MxJT2yU)

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-01-17T03:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pomc-and-sweeteners-pomc-and-gender.28493/post-330311>

Sweeteners are almost ubiquitous in sodas. You have to read every single label. ( Every thing I see in Panama with the exception of Canada Dry has acesulfameK and sucralose, and some have aspartame which is a potent BH4 disrupter. ). It's good in one way as I just buy water and pure juice). But you see a product read the label and think. Poison !

### JanSz — 2024-01-17T16:33:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pomc-and-sweeteners-pomc-and-gender.28493/post-330323>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=330311)
>
> Sweeteners are almost ubiquitous in sodas. You have to read every single label. ( Every thing I see in Panama with the exception of Canada Dry has acesulfameK and sucralose, and some have aspartame which is a potent BH4 disrupter. ). It's good in one way as I just buy water and pure juice). But you see a product read the label and think. Poison !

**low-calorie sweeteners (LCS) do they contain fructose?**

Low-calorie sweeteners (LCS) can be made from various types of sugars, **and some of them do contain fructose.** However, it’s important to note that not all LCS contain fructose.
[For instance, sweeteners like **High Fructose Corn Syrup**, **Agave Nectar**, **Maple Syrup**, **Coconut Sugar**, **Dates**, and **Date Sugar** contain a high ratio of fructose to glucose](https://janeshealthykitchen.com/natural-sweeteners-best-and-worst/)[1](https://janeshealthykitchen.com/natural-sweeteners-best-and-worst/).
[On the other hand, there are sweeteners like **Stevia** and **Erythritol** that are considered safe and contain neither fructose nor glucose](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/healthy-natural-sweeteners)[2](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/healthy-natural-sweeteners).
[It’s also worth mentioning that **Tagatose**, a form of fructose that is around 90% sweeter than sucrose, is used in food production as a low-calorie sweetener](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/323469)[3](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/323469).

\=============================================

**Fructose raises uric acid in the blood.
What about Stevia, Erythriol and Tagatose?
Do they also raise Uric acid in the blood?**

Stevia, Erythritol, and Tagatose are all low-calorie sweeteners that are alternatives to fructose.
Here’s what research says about their effects on uric acid levels:

-   [**Stevia**: A 2018 clinical trial found that stevia supplements reduced uric acid levels in participants with chronic kidney disease](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/319837)[1](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/319837).
-   [Other sources suggest that stevia **may increase uric acid secretion** due to its diuretic properties, which could potentially lower uric acid levels in the blood](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/319837)[2](https://goutpatients.com/stevia-and-gout/).
-   [**Erythritol**: According to a pilot dose-ranging study, acute ingestion of erythritol did not affect blood lipid and uric acid concentrations](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/319837)[3](https://www.mdpi.com/2072-6643/15/2/458).
-   [**Tagatose**: Some sources suggest that high doses of Tagatose **may cause temporary rises in uric acid** blood levels](https://perfectketo.com/tagatose/)[4](https://perfectketo.com/tagatose/).
-   [However, another source states that Tagatose only increases blood uric acid levels by a small margin, not enough to lead to gout](https://naturalpedia.com/tagatose-sources-health-risks.html)[5](https://naturalpedia.com/tagatose-sources-health-risks.html).

\=============


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Sleep timing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-timing.28496/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-11T03:17:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-timing.28496/post-330081>

I’m curious what everyone’s habits are around sleep timing through seasonal changes.

Do you stay up in winter until 10 pm or so with fire, or do you go to sleep within 1-1.5 hours of sunset?

Sunset here in winter is around 5:30 pm.

If you sleep for more than 7-8 hours a night, how do you manage this?

I personally always wake up after about 8 hours of sleep if I go to bed before 10. I still can fall asleep after waking up, but I toss and turn until very abruptly waking up 5 mins before my backup alarm (30 mins before sunrise).

It’s easy in the summer as I fall asleep around 9:30-10 pm and wake up at sunrise with no gaps in between.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-11T14:52:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-timing.28496/post-330090>

I go to sleep 2-3 hrs after sunset. For my location, that is around 7:30-8:30. I typically lay down between 8-8:30

Bc of my work schedule, I have to wake at 5am. If I had a choice, I would sleep in til about 6-6:30, which is roughly 30 min before sunrise.

Sometimes I wake in the middle of the night. When this happens, I usually just get up, walk around a bit, and get a drink of water. Then, I lay back down and usually fall back asleep no problem.

If I wake within ~1 hr. of the time I planned to wake, I will usually just get up for good and do some light reading under dim red or candle light.

I usually stay asleep the full night though. For the times I don't, I think it is usually caused by poor meal timing. I have a bad habit of eating within 1-2 hours before bed (7-8 pm) sometimes. When I do that, I typically wake during the night.

### kris90 — 2024-01-11T17:05:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-timing.28496/post-330098>

Right now sun sets at 4:40 pm, and rises at 7:40 am.

First sleep 7:00 pm to 12:00 am (deep sleep/cellular repair)
Second sleep 1:00 am to 5:00 am (dream state)

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-11T18:13:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-timing.28496/post-330100>

What I don't quite understand is how it's appropriate to wake up before any sunlight is present in the sky, since the sun is responsible for waking us up and initiating so many functions in our body

### kris90 — 2024-01-11T18:56:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-timing.28496/post-330103>

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=330100)
>
> What I don't quite understand is how it's appropriate to wake up before any sunlight is present in the sky, since the sun is responsible for waking us up and initiating so many functions in our body

Sun is not responsible for waking us up. Our natural diurnal cortisol rhythm is. And its not just regulated by light, but also by temperature. Since it's winter right now, if we are properly abiding by our circadian biology, we are not coupled to the light cycle and are supposed to be using temperature as the dominant circadian stimulator. Hence why biphasic sleeping is the norm in winter months because cold/dark environments upregulate autophagy.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-11T19:01:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-timing.28496/post-330104>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=330103)
>
> Sun is not responsible for waking us up. Our natural diurnal cortisol rhythm is. And its not just regulated by light, but also by temperature. Since it's winter right now, if we are properly abiding by our circadian biology, we are not coupled to the light cycle and are supposed to be using temperature as the dominant circadian stimulator. Hence why biphasic sleeping is the norm in winter months because cold/dark environments upregulate autophagy.

AH I see, I remember some of this from the CT blogs. Seems I need to re-read them again. Very fascinating. Thank you for the quick explanation.

### JanSz — 2024-01-11T20:07:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-timing.28496/post-330105>

Jack posted this video on sleep, early on when he started his blog.
Previously he was posting on the Marks daily apple.

# Thread: Letting the light in...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/letting-the-light-in.28501/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Plural — 2024-01-12T22:05:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/letting-the-light-in.28501/post-330143>

I've been making sure a window or two is cracked at home, even during winter to receive scattered sunlight, however, the office I am currently in does not have windows that open (what a fail).

On typical work day, I see sunrise at home then commute 40min to office with windows open entire drive. Will get out for a 15min walk at lunch. (Also wearing less layers on walk for past few months than I would have previously when temperatures are lower.)

It sounds like the best ways to compensate for the lack of sunlight during most of the day would be:

1) Blue blocking lenses when working at computer (Video Editor)
2) Some form of daily CT
3) Prioritizing foods that are in season (Ohio)
4) Natural movement practice outdoors during daylight when not at office

There are days when I've been able to WFH and have the computer set up on porch. This is ideal for me and I enjoy that when I can.

Open to other thoughts/suggestions and/or things to avoid outside of putting in my 2 weeks and starting a landscaping business.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-13T03:35:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/letting-the-light-in.28501/post-330163>

Have you checked your nnEMF levels both at home (bedroom) and office? Not only RF (important) but also electric and magnetic fields. Lots of info here, follow the links: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Plural — 2024-01-17T18:33:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/letting-the-light-in.28501/post-330327>

I will double check these. I know it is important as well.

Question was more directed at mediating for lack of sun, or ways to get more sun (obviously working outdoors is best solution).

But yes, #5 could be low-EMF workzone. Thanks!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-17T21:10:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/letting-the-light-in.28501/post-330338>

>
> I live in Indiana and work for a tech company. I feel our day to days are very similar. My work windows do not open either. So unfortunate. I am curious as to what other mediations can be taken for our locations and work. Best to you!

I have both a NIR (near infrared) light and visible red light behind my screen to help counter (neutralize) the blue light from the screen, as well as adjusting the screen colors to less blue more red.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-18T21:42:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/letting-the-light-in.28501/post-330360>

> [jules3791 said:](/goto/post?id=330348)
>
> Thank you so much! Do you have links to purchase these items?

There is a lot of info on the forum about different types of NIR lights. I cannot comment on them because I do not have any of them. Some of them are only 850nm others offer additional frequencies, they also cost $$$. What I have is a 850nm NIR security light from Amazon. Works for me. There are many other companies to choose from but I have 2 of these, CMVision also have smaller ones. They do not include the power supply so order the CMVision power supply they recommend for each light. $50 vs $500 ;-)

[www.amazon.com/CMVision-IRP24-850nm-WideAngle-Power-Illuminator/dp/B00Q15Z5U8/ref=sr\_1\_6?crid=2AN78LVKM3M0K&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.fP3VNFWHVOWVyrVPIoa6fiUus3LXYz1OodIvTA4B8tI063nrfelOoDuPwFbq3J73OJbO\_XBr5GLTEKlcgxMGMe1NEv1ZtfzbFkdcc7uHG5Tpt\_baAPfQfK2](http://www.amazon.com/CMVision-IRP24-850nm-WideAngle-Power-Illuminator/dp/B00Q15Z5U8/ref=sr_1_6?crid=2AN78LVKM3M0K&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.fP3VNFWHVOWVyrVPIoa6fiUus3LXYz1OodIvTA4B8tI063nrfelOoDuPwFbq3J73OJbO_XBr5GLTEKlcgxMGMe1NEv1ZtfzbFkdcc7uHG5Tpt_baAPfQfK2)

I am in Ostrander, Ohio so I am with you. Do you have a dog? I have a dog who is part Husky and he needs 4-5 walks a day. It definitely gets me outside. I also wear grounding shoes on the walk, and I walk in wet grass whenever possible. I always have a window cracked and I always eat by the open window with red light panel on my abdomen. This way I am getting red light and some scattered UV light. Other than that, I try to plan a trip south in January or February.

# Thread: Canary Island's Thread
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2024-01-14T22:13:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-330223>

We live in Fuerteventura, one of the Canary Islands, but we are now wondering if it is the best option for us to live there.

It has a low population density (approx 120k).
Latitude 28.3968.
1 underwater volcano approx. 209km.
1 active volcano approx. 375km.
**However…**.
No rain or very little rainfall approx. 101mm per year.
Cannot freely grow plants, veg, etc.
We have Calima’s regularly, where sand and dust is blown over from the Shara desert, which causes unhealthy air quality.
Air quality is generally not good.

Would Tenerife be better?

High population density.
Latitude: 28.2916.
1 underwater volcano approx. 25.47km.
1 active volcano approx. 133km.
More rainfall approx. 1000mm per year.
Lush, and you can grow plants, etc.
Air quality good.

My main concern is the air quality being continuously unhealthy and poor as it is similar to a desert here.

Thanks

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-14T22:50:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-330225>

I think your question gives you your answer and since health is a main concern why not much further south? It is very easy to grow things in the tropics.
A side note, I’ve heard a volcanic eruption can ruin your day . . . I live 50 miles from one

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-16T00:44:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-330275>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=330259)
>
> We have Calima’s regularly, where sand and dust is blown over from the Shara desert, which causes unhealthy air quality.
> \================================
> When worrying about dust from Sahara, do not reside within areas where it happens.
> That includes some southern states of USA.

JanSz your map shows the forecast for one day in July 2021. Lots of places get Sahara sand, even Japan, but how much and how often? The estimation of the Sahara dust deposition from the main trajectories is 170 Tg/yr in the Atlantic (Canary Islands), 25 Tg/yr in Mediterranean and 5 Tg/yr in Caribbean. 170 Atlantic vs 5 in the Caribbean. As with many things it is not so much a question of IF something happens but how often and how much?

### JanSz — 2024-01-16T04:01:05+0200

As with many things it is not so much a question of IF something happens but how often and how much?

I think there is an old thread somewhere discussing Sahara's sand.
I think the discussion was not about how unhealthy it is to breathe sand but rather that it reduces amount of sunlight at the land level. IIRC it was about UK area that is affected by that sand.

### healthy2015 — 2024-01-19T13:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-330378>

Thanks for the reply, unfortunately due to work the time zones for the tropics are not suitable this is why we looked at the Canary Islands as they are the same time as the UK

Plus moving south from here the only options are Africa or Cape Verde and they may not be so good from a from a safety prospective for us.

### Pebbles — 2024-01-29T01:10:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-330688>

End of 2019 we almost bought a house there, Costa Calma, nature reserve, top of a hill with no EMF and Wifi, wind turbines shadowed by the stone hill, the lack of natural water and the Sahara sand put me in hesitation, than a few month after cvd misery started and could not travel.
The west side of the island its really harsh, stormy year round. Non of the Canary island has natural real streams, creaks, rivers, only temporary from rains. Lanzarotte is same as Fuerteventura, Gran and Tenerife are crowded. La Gomera is better but very cold and much rain, CT assured in August) and the live volcano is nearby. Its a compromise instead of UK.

### healthy2015 — 2024-01-30T21:52:28+0200

Thanks for your reply, Pebbles. We are located inland in the middle of the island. It hardly ever rains. We have been there 3 years now, and although it has a lot of plus points, the air quality makes us wonder if it is that healthy.

Even with the poor air quality, would you think it would be much better than the UK?

### Pebbles — 2024-01-31T01:11:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-330771>

UK has rain, dark and smog, so for sure is better) The Calima issue is present in Gran C as well, Tenerife may be less. Additionally GC has scorpions ( all Mediteranian countries have, Croatia many), Tenerife has no mosquitos. (pls check, may be reverse). The wester you go, the more rain and humider and colder the air and ocean as well. Cape Verde is not safe and does not allow long stay, so difficult.

### Garry Kirk — 2024-06-14T19:58:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-335787>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=330766)
>
> Thanks for your reply, Pebbles. We are located inland in the middle of the island. It hardly ever rains. We have been there 3 years now, and although it has a lot of plus points, the air quality makes us wonder if it is that healthy.
>
> Even with the poor air quality, would you think it would be much better than the UK?

I think most fruit and veg is grown in Gran Canaria. When you shop in the Canaries and look to buy local, that seems to be where most local grown food comes from.
Have you been to GC? There are a lot of mountains which catch the moisture and any rain, Tenerife is similar north of Teide. You can drive down from the Observatory or top of the Volcano on that north road and you would be forgiven for thinking you were in the UK.
The north of Tenerife also catches a lot more rain. Personally I much prefer the North of the island.

FV is perfect for being naked. The SE of island is something like 40k of unspoilt beaches and you can take your clothes off without any of the issues you can be faced with elsewhere in Europe, as I'm sure you know. that's what I like about FV.

Maybe the air quality would be better higher up in GC or Tenerife?

G.

Thats all true! GC you have also the unspoiled Masspalomas naked beach. The buy local and bio is easy and price is ok as well, the vulcano makes a good soil for these products. The Sahara winds power decreses from E to W, so the wester u go, the cleaner the air, and the colder the Atlantic - La Gomera is less spoiled, but did not find FKK opportunities, and the pirces are much higher.

### Michalis — 2024-07-12T20:25:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-337015>

Anyone has any recommendations in Fuertaventura in terms of accommodation close to the sea but not super busy ?

### Garry Kirk — 2024-07-13T15:37:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-337050>

Anywhere around about Costa Calma and south of there. You can head west to La Pared for sunsets as well, it’s about a 10 minute drive, so you’ll get sunrise and sunset, plus about 40k of naturist beaches.

### eve001 — 2024-08-17T16:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-338548>

Hola! How are the Canary Islands RF EMFs wise?  Anyone knows of good accommodation? I became EHS in a 5G town and joined here recently. Looking for healing places in/near EU (for now).

### Garry Kirk — 2024-08-20T14:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-338680>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=338548)
>
> Hola! How are the Canary Islands RF EMFs wise?  Anyone knows of good accommodation? I became EHS in a 5G town and joined here recently. Looking for healing places in/near EU (for now).

You will get 4g in most built up places around the coasts, most airbnb and hotels have wifi etc. I haven't seen any 5g anywhere but I'm sure it will be on the way to the bigger cities in Tenerife and Gran Canaria if it isn't already there. Best shout is something detached, where you can switch the router off and switch off the fuses, RCBO, RCD's etc, there are even tents in the mountains but you find half the time there is wifi provided in these places as well. Airbnb, booking.com and find your own place away from the herd!

### Pebbles — 2024-09-03T11:08:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-339191>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=338548)
>
> Hola! How are the Canary Islands RF EMFs wise?  Anyone knows of good accommodation? I became EHS in a 5G town and joined here recently. Looking for healing places in/near EU (for now).

As a general rule, you can find secluded areas w very low nnEMF on each island, farther from the beach- cheaper but you need a car permanenetly. Beach - look for nudist or wild beaches, not tourist crowding. The prices are on a large scale, so find what better suits you. Fuerteventura between Costa Calma and Jandia - some secluded apartments near the beach as well.

### Mihaly — 2024-09-11T21:59:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canary-islands-thread.28505/post-339489>

Hey, is anyone up for a meeting in Fuerteventura?
Im living there long time, mainly in the south but now I try it in the north. I cant compare with other places around the world without experience living elsewhere but I still like it after 7 years. Perfect for a simple, nature friendly lifestyle!
The beaches are amazing, especially in the south. Good quality local fuits and veggies you can find on the market in La Lajita on sundays. Or they say there is also a market in La Oliva in the north.

The strong wind and the calima are not so fun. However I have zero issues with the calima (high redox and good detox?), I can still do my outdoor sports without problem while many 'non mitochondriac' people complain a lot for breathing issues and weakness in those days.
About the EMF strength I dont have any measurment, only my intuitive experiences. As long as I can go in the ocean I feel super, compared to living in a big city before.

More to west its probably less significant dust and more opportunities for everything but Tenerife or even GC is too crowded in my opinion. I love La Palma with the green forests, its more rainy and there are also more fruits&veggies but beachwise I dont find it so good. And a lot of driving up&down on the hills... La Gomera, El Hierro similar.

At the end I dont know if there is a perfect island, so it's a matter of consideration which features are more important.

# Thread: DIY grounding products
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-grounding-products.28509/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sweden — 2024-01-16T22:01:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-grounding-products.28509/post-330304>

Good evening spirituall sun seekers. I thought about grounding shoes and the options that are available. And most of them are not suitable for wet weather. But there are grounding shoe straps that you can buy and ground yourself with almost any shoes (see first video) but according to comments these straps falls off easily and needs replacement due to wear and tear. So i thought why not make your own?

All you need is basically a conductive material (maybe even regular silvertejp would be enough?) And then just make a "wire" that goes all the way around and in the shoe. To a total cost of just a couple of dollars, even less. (See video 2 for a grounding tejp)

So i don't have a meter to measure if I'm grounded or not. And i won't buy one for now. But if anyone here has a meter and could do some testing then that would be very interesting to see what kind of low cost grounding ideas we could create.

Bonus video no 3 is a DIY grounding walking cane (start at 5:40)

Bonus video no 4 is a DIY grounding sheet

1:

2:

3:

4:

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-17T05:41:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-grounding-products.28509/post-330312>

### [Cup-Style Heel Grounder - Standard H-936 - ULINE](https://www.uline.com/Product/Detail/H-936/Anti-Static-Equipment/Cup-Style-Heel-Grounder-Standard?pricode=WB3452&utm_source=Bing&utm_medium=pla&utm_term=H-936&utm_campaign=Anti-Static&utm_source=Bing&utm_medium=pla&utm_term=H-936&utm_campaign=Anti-Static&msclkid=c8d7767a1429161044a013dd5dbccfa8)

 www.uline.com

Have you considered something like this for grounding outside in the winter? Grounding makes you part of the circuit, very good outside connecting our DC field to natures DC field. Grounding inside and making oneself part of the house 60Hz AC field/circuit NOT SO GOOD. If you have a 1,000, 2,000, 3,000mV 60Hz AC field in your bedroom (no way of knowing unless you measure) why would you want to be part of that circuit and have that 60Hz AC field flowing thru you all night? You can throw a ground wire outside but you are still in an AC field inside. Just a thought.

Good point. I am not super technical when it comes the electronic side, and im guessing you are exposed to those 60hz AC anyways?. So would it be better to be exposed to this field without grounding? I searched around and found this article on the subject

### [Is Grounding Good for You? | EMF Analysis](https://www.emfanalysis.com/is-grounding-good-for-you/?fbclid=IwAR1sZCcfA85k-0php-iYmNN5z4YfRW0er5nCcW9KYY7XMLzN2DLhx6fPQG4)

Grounding or "Earthing" is popular, but comes with significant risks. In this article I explain how you can avoid the dangers of Earthing or ground products.

 www.emfanalysis.com

And of course how you ground is very important

A: Grounding outlets are highly variable (dirty electricity) so hard to study scientifically. Waterlines on the other hand seem to be the best options

B: If you connect to the earth and the soil is wet and the grounding point is solid, then you should be fine theoretically. Depending on how much EMF is in and around your house.

The website on the bottom is very interesting. The creator of the website mentions that "you should look into how you can diagnose if you have dirty electricity or not, research "Dr Jack Kruse" for that "

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-17T20:43:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-grounding-products.28509/post-330336>

Jeromy at EMF Analysis is very good. He not only is an EMF consultant, he is EMF sensitive and an engineer, that helps. From his web site:
“The Earthing equipment is not always reducing the electric field. In fact, by being connected to the ground, you are now part of the very pathway by which all nearby electromagnetic frequencies (including wireless frequencies) will pass through to the Earth and your home electrical system. This will actually add to the EMF pollution that your body is dealing with, rather than reducing it or protecting you.”

If you notice on the second link “exo-science.com” the grounding study was done on people sleeping in a hotel. Hotels are commercial buildings which by code have metal clad wiring with little to no 60Hz AC fields (Important detail).

As Jeromy explains, grounding while inside is not recommended, not just because of potential “DE” and other EMF that might be on/in the ground but the electric fields (and RF) in the house itself.

Grounding outside is so good because you are connecting with nature . . . to connect with nature you NEED to be IN nature.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-18T00:18:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maternal-mtdna-purpose.28513/post-330342>

Anyone have any thoughts on why mtDNA is only passed down through mothers? My thoughts are that females may undergo less heteroplasmy degredation especially with less lean body mass and potentially less stress.

Does a lack of DNA mixing with mtDNA compared nuclear DNA imply it is influenced purely through epigenetics as an environmental sensor? Does epigenetics apply to mitochondria alongside the traditional nuclear epigenetic influence?

### coho3131 — 2024-01-18T01:02:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maternal-mtdna-purpose.28513/post-330343>

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=330342)
>
> Anyone have any thoughts on why mtDNA is only passed down through mothers? My thoughts are that females may undergo less heteroplasmy degredation especially with less lean body mass and potentially less stress.
>
> Does a lack of DNA mixing with mtDNA compared nuclear DNA imply it is influenced purely through epigenetics as an environmental sensor? Does epigenetics apply to mitochondria alongside the traditional nuclear epigenetic influence?

This might help.

### [Molecular basis for maternal inheritance of human mitochondrial DNA - Nature Genetics](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41588-023-01505-9)

The mitochondrial transcription factor A is excluded from the mitochondria in spermatozoa by virtue of phosphorylation of the mitochondrial presequence. This is associated with transport to the nucleus and loss of mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) from the mitochondria, providing a mechanistic basis for...

 www.nature.com

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maternal-mtdna-purpose.28513/post-330344>

>
> The mitochondrial transcription factor A is excluded from the mitochondria in spermatozoa by virtue of phosphorylation of the mitochondrial presequence. This is associated with transport to the nucleus and loss of mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) from the mitochondria, providing a mechanistic basis for...
>
>  www.nature.com

Very fascinating, although my main interest is in why nature designed it to be this way

I just started investing into BTC and have it on binance, is Binance safe and a good option? Or what are the negatives?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-18T19:43:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitcoin-beginner.28514/post-330355>

BTC held by anyone other than yourself is never safe

I would research how to self-custody and get that BTC off Binance ASAP

Not financial advice...just what I would do in your situation

### JanSz — 2024-01-18T23:03:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitcoin-beginner.28514/post-330365>

> [jeremiasbtome said:](/goto/post?id=330350)
>
> I just started investing into BTC and have it on binance, is Binance safe and a good option? Or what are the negatives?

From my perspective (which is the end of the previous year).
Bitcoin has fallen down.
Good time to buy.
But if you know that it will get lower, then keep waiting.
Always tough decisions.
Easier, (only slightly) if you think of 5 to 7 years span.

Just do it, seven years latter you will find out if it was smart move.

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-01T05:11:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitcoin-beginner.28514/post-335358>

> [jeremiasbtome said:](/goto/post?id=335222)
>
> are there any other investments a black swan mitochondriac can make? i looked at index funds but jack doesnt think they're good. or am i wrong? thanks for anyone who replies

Yes, all of them.

Don’t ask people for financial advice. Get financially literate.
If you have no clue, ir even if you do, I recommend Rich Dad Poor Dad.

Generally diversifying is good, but had you had only gold recently, you’d do great. Bitcoin great, but it depends.

You can’t always win, but in order to win you need to start investing and learn.

I invested really young in stock, won, won, won and lost it all in the dot com crash. It happens.
But I learned. I got apple stocks very early. I bought a tiny house for 200, we made it nicer and built a deck and it is on the market today, 7 years later, for 875. So that worked out well. It’s a mobil home, not classic real estate. The reason it went up is the ocean view. So that’s how you learn. You need to actually do it, make the investments. Think.

Rich Dad will tell you only to buy assets and not liabilities. That’s really important.

Don’t listen to me. At best listen to a really rich person. The last rich man who I asked for advice said, “you make money, by doing something, before everybody else does it. You need to think what that could be, you need to be one of the first.”
I think he’s right. He’s mega rich. But it’s really also the doing part.

It is so fucking easy to invest these days. Back in the day, the bank would tell you your minimum investment is 100k. Now you can invest 10 bucks from your phone barely fees.
Back than the banking fees were horrid.

This is a playground. Just do it. You don’t need nobody’s hand to hold.

thank you all solid advice, my only issue with stocks etc, is, wouldn't all the big stocks now crash, and funds too? cuz they are all based on blue light tech, and in the next 2-4 decades it could get revealed and hence the US market (sp500) would all fall?

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-02T00:04:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitcoin-beginner.28514/post-335373>

> [jeremiasbtome said:](/goto/post?id=335369)
>
> thank you all solid advice, my only issue with stocks etc, is, wouldn't all the big stocks now crash, and funds too? cuz they are all based on blue light tech, and in the next 2-4 decades it could get revealed and hence the US market (sp500) would all fall?

It’s all possible. The reasons for a big market crash are probably different ones, but sure, it can all crash and fail.

if anything would be predictable with no uncertainty, you could be sure that that’s where a ton of smart people park their money.

You can also profit from a crashing market. If you are super sure it will crash, you can bet against it. In any case, you need to get educated and you need to do that by yourself, respectively by actually doing it.

### thejessallen — 2024-06-02T16:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitcoin-beginner.28514/post-335378>

100% - learn how to self custody. In addition to a hardware wallet, have a seed phrase back up on a metal plate or some kind of hard to destroy product. I use a metal plate.

Before moving all of your money, practice restoring your hardware wallet from the backup plate. Lots of YouTube videos on how to do this. You will invest a little bit of money in transaction fees practicing. Just consider it a cost of learning how to be a Bitcoiner who Self custodies. Well worth it and you’ll feel empowered to focus your investing there.

Setup recurring purchasing schedule and ignore price

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-19T16:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anywhere-safe-in-california.28518/post-330381>

I've heard Jack discuss disliking Cali due to nnEMF centers like LA and SF alongside Van Allen belt issues modifying terrestrial sunlight frequencies and reducing Vit D generation potential.

I'm curious if this is a latitudinal effect (extending north and south, and even beyond California), and if this still would affect places like northern Cali and eastern Cali enough for it to be a no go for living there (2-4 hours away from any major city).

Due to cost of living and the remote nature of these parts of California, it seems like a nice place to live amongst plenty of trees at a better latitude than Seattle where I've been living while still getting away from the cities and having plenty of things to do in nature.

Hard to find good information on Van Allen belts that I trust, if anyone has any sources and reasoning for why they are trustable and accurate

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-19T17:57:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anywhere-safe-in-california.28518/post-330383>

There is no place on the entire planet is perfectly safe. There is always some degree of risk.

The overall risk factor due to nnEMFs is a sliding scale value that typically decreases with decreasing population.

But, it is always present no matter where you go.

Just mitigate where you can and if you aren't doing well in a particular location, then move away from populated areas.

**As far as latitude goes, I feel like one can make any sparsely populated latitude work long term, if one adopts the proper lifestyle to go with that environment.**

The further towards the poles you go, the more CT and the more ketosis your body will require to stay optimal. And of course, maximizing sunlight is recommended in any location.

Another random important thing to keep in mind, **as you move away from population density**, your own **nnEMF emitting devices become riskier** as they have to emit higher intensity radiation to compensate for the increased distance from towers.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-21T00:09:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dengue-in-tropics.28523/post-330419>

I recently got Dengue in Martinique and it was not a fun time. Was sick for about a week.

This has me afraid to go to the topics again or settle down there, as the more severe autoimmune reaction can happen if you get another one of the 4 strains. Not to mention the first time was bad enough. Mosquitos love and have always loved me.

I spent the prior 10 days to first symptoms with full day skin and eyes sun exposure, grounding constantly, no nnEMF, great sleep, and a deep tan. It then came on quite aggressively. Zero energy and severe aching in my hip bones to the point where I had to take Tylenol (something I usually avoid at all costs) to get any sleep.

I can only describe it as losing a week of my life. I couldn't do one single thing, and couldn't feel any comfort, for an entire week. Although it sucked, I certainly didn't get any of the low platelet count issues that many others get (aside from very red swollen gums).

Anyone have any thoughts on avoiding this major issue with living in the tropics?

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-01-21T23:50:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dengue-in-tropics.28523/post-330441>

>
> Anyone have any thoughts on avoiding this major issue with living in the tropics?

Mosquito net, plug in mosquito repellent and being in the sea all day. I too get loved by the mosquitos and it's annoying. One could take ivermectin prophylactically too.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-22T00:01:04+0200

### Marko Pollo — 2024-01-23T02:27:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dengue-in-tropics.28523/post-330493>

If you are a favorite meal of insects, it means you are bleeding light and attracting them. The same thing happens with plants. I remember when I first got here and Jack said if you have high redox, the bugs won't bother you. I thought he was full of prunes then, but completely agree with him now. I live between a pond and river, and irrigated pastures on 2 sides. Plenty of bugs. Never use spray when most do and am usually bare chested all day. Only a couple of bites in many years.

# Thread: Is genetic testing worthwhile?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-genetic-testing-worthwhile.28527/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BrunoB — 2024-01-21T15:44:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-genetic-testing-worthwhile.28527/post-330427>

Hello good day, what is the real use of genetic tests beyond taking methyl folate in case you detect a bad mutation of MTHFR? And in addition to know bad metabolism of alcohol, coffee ...(that you can check this yourself). Thank you!

### JanSz — 2024-01-21T17:03:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-genetic-testing-worthwhile.28527/post-330432>

> [BrunoB said:](/goto/post?id=330427)
>
> Hello good day, what is the real use of genetic tests beyond taking methyl folate in case you detect a bad mutation of MTHFR? And in addition to know bad metabolism of alcohol, coffee ...(that you can check this yourself). Thank you!

About MTHFR.
This suggestion many years ago worked (most of the times).
Probably good suggestion for most.
For those (few) that it does not work, do not spend $$, just enjoy your life the best you can in current status.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I did 23andme test.

The biggest thing that I learned from that test is that my Dad is my Dad.
Newer had any reasons to doubt about that, but it helps.

.....................................

**Dr. Amy Yasko** is a well-known figure in the field of MTHFR gene mutations.
[She has a PhD in Microbiology/Immunology/Infectious Disease and has served on the scientific advisory board for various organizations](https://dramyyasko.com/)[1](https://dramyyasko.com/).
Dr. [Yasko has worked in biotechnology for 15 years and has co-developed the first low-cost DNA synthesizer](https://dramyyasko.com/)[1](https://dramyyasko.com/).

[She has created **The Yasko Protocol**, a guide to help individuals understand their genetics and test results, enabling them to make decisions that align with their health goals](https://dramyyasko.com/)[1](https://dramyyasko.com/)[2](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/).
[This protocol is based on noninvasive test data and DNA Nutrigenomic SNP data](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/)[2](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/).
[It uses information from personal test results to help individuals choose from a range of supplements tailored to their needs](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/)[2](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/).

Dr. [Yasko’s work focuses on understanding a central pathway in the body:](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/)
[The Methylation Cycle,](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/)
[a pathway directly related to a range of health conditions](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/)[2](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/).
[She believes that knowledge is power **and provides her resources at no cost** so that individuals and their doctors can access the knowledge they need](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/)[2](https://dramyyasko.com/yaskoprotocol/).

////

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-22T00:22:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-genetic-testing-worthwhile.28527/post-330445>

> [BrunoB said:](/goto/post?id=330427)
>
> what is the real use of genetic tests

I think to determine haplotype, which helps to determine an ideal location in the world for your molecular machinery to exist.

Other than that though, not much else that I'm aware of, since our health is not determined by our DNA and RNA.

It is mostly dictated by epigenetics (environmental effects) on the quantum level.

Hello, good morning. Have you tried his treatment? How did it go?
I read that he says that for it to have a good effect the fasting must be extensive(3 days) and that coffee or TCM break or hinder the fasting.thank you.

### NikkiYork — 2024-01-29T06:23:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-know-ernesto-prieto-gratacos.28542/post-330701>

I could only find Spanish info on him, so it‘s tough.
I don’t think that he and his work is known in the USA or Europe.

Germans are fasting a lot and many people are fasting for quite a while. I could never do it, because my circulation goes down too much and then it’s not healthy.

Overall, I’ve only heard good things from the people who are fasting. I don’t know, if that’s what he says to do.
From what I know coffee is not part of fasting, just water and diluted broth, but there are certainly different ways of doing it.

If you could explain what his treatment is, maybe more people can answer.

Light before food!! That should count for fasting also.

There are different reasons for fasting, not all are for done for health. And not all are healthy.

To induce apoptosis from what I know you don’t really need to fast very long.

The classical way of longer fasting that I know includes a pre-week and a post week and is supposed to be gentle to not!!!! stress out the body. The idea is to make energy available that is usually used for digestion, by not having anything to digest.

It doesn’t cost money to fast  back in the day it cost the price of a book, now it’s probably all online.

It’s not a great sign that this guy has it all behind a pay wall.
Also not great that he claims to cure cancer. Not that cancer can’t be cured, but it looks like a trick to get desperate people to pay him.

>
> He does not claim to cure cancer.
> I read more about his method, and he says that if you go beyond 2 days of fasting then you should do enemas) according to him it is to avoid re-absorbing the feces =, that doesn't make sense to me.
> And then he says that you should eat broths, not eat suddenly after fasting for more than 2 days.
> Health depends on many factors, obviously, not just one or two.

The Germans had this classic book about fasting that everybody read in the 80ies and 90s. That’s what my “knowledge” is based on. Or what I remember from it, hahaha.

There you have one week of getting solid food out of your system which includes a bunch of enemas. And smoothly transitioning to what is eventually going to be filtered broth and diluted fruit juice during the fast.

Then comes fasting, however long, but it does include drinking water, and depending on your situation, broth or juice diluted. And then the same week is taken to smoothly go back to regular food.

To me that makes sense, but like I said, I only tried it once and I could not stand up from my bed in the morning on the second day of fasting. I had to crawl on my knees to the fridge and eat a slice of orange and then my circulation kicked back on. Hahahaha.

If I was you, I would just try out a fast that makes sense to you and doesn’t involve too crazy techniques. I’m talking about those starving gurus who occasionally die. That can’t be good.

However, fasting has been done in forever and if you try it in moderation and with some sort of information about, what can go wrong?

Worst is that you end up like me not being able to do it and then you brake the fast, just like I did.

Like I said, in Germany it was a total hype in the 90s and all my friends did it. It was like going to yoga. People went to retreats or did it by themselves.

Some liked it, some didn’t. Just try it out reasonably.

# Thread: Sensory Processing Disorder
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensory-processing-disorder.28545/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2024-01-29T18:12:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensory-processing-disorder.28545/post-330710>

I am a special education teacher.

What are your thoughts on sensory processing issues?
What is the root cause of the problem?
Hacks/supplements that I could recommend to families with kids who struggle with this?
I believe there is a huge tie with glutamate/GABA regulation and how the balance is maintained in the brain and body.
Some websites say to eliminate dairy, gluten, etc. but there has to be more. Nutrient deficiencies?
One of the struggles is sensitivity to light, so recommending sunlight will have to be a slow process for a lot of these kids.

Any thoughts greatly appreciated!

### JanSz — 2024-01-30T00:21:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensory-processing-disorder.28545/post-330726>

I think that Gabapentin is used too often.
Possibly also in too high doses.

Gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) is a neurotransmitter, a chemical messenger in your brain. It slows down your brain by blocking specific signals in your central nervous system (your brain and spinal cord). [GABA is known for producing a calming effect](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/22857-gamma-aminobutyric-acid-gaba)[1](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/22857-gamma-aminobutyric-acid-gaba).

GABA is the most common inhibitory neurotransmitter in your central nervous system. [Inhibitory neurotransmitters prevent or block chemical messages and decrease the stimulation of nerve cells in your brain](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/22857-gamma-aminobutyric-acid-gaba)[1](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/22857-gamma-aminobutyric-acid-gaba)

### coho3131 — 2024-01-30T17:17:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensory-processing-disorder.28545/post-330747>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=330710)
>
> I am a special education teacher.
>
> What are your thoughts on sensory processing issues?
> What is the root cause of the problem?

> Some websites say to eliminate dairy, gluten, etc. but there has to be more. Nutrient deficiencies?
> One of the struggles is sensitivity to light, so recommending sunlight will have to be a slow process for a lot of these kids.
>
> Any thoughts greatly appreciated!

I would ask how much time they spend outside.

# Thread: Experience with cooling vests
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experience-with-cooling-vests.28549/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sfast — 2024-01-31T10:51:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experience-with-cooling-vests.28549/post-330776>

Dear Freezers,

do you have experience with cools vests? I don't have room for the amounts of ice necessary in my freezer. I used to have just one big block of ice, which I put on my chest and back with a small backpack (double-bagged). I alternated between front and back. I do exercise every 45 min, when I work sitting (e.g. 50 kettlebell swings). So, I need to be able to remove the ice within a couple of seconds.

I am flirting with a simple cooling vest, but am not sure if the normal cooling packs have enough capacity.

So, what is your take on cooling vests.

Live long and prosper
Sascha

# Thread: Fish + Fat In Winter

### Desert.life — 2024-02-02T02:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-fat-in-winter.28552/post-330843>

Hi All.. So I have read that- _Remember with seafood try to combine with mono-saturated fats because saturated fats like butter can hinder DHA absorption into the brain_.

In the Epi Paleo Rx it states -

## Fats: (in order of Optimal)​

**1\. Spring and summer:** [Coconut oil](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001EO5Q64/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B001EO5Q64&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20), ghee, palm oil, duck fat, beef tallow, bacon fat, duck fat, pastured butter if there are no medical issue precluding its use, olive or avocado oils for salads, macadamia nut oils for mayonaise, raw cream if there are no contraindications\* **_When you eat seafood try to use MUFA’s as the added fat._

2\. Fall and Winter:** ghee, pastured butter, duck fat, beef tallow, bacon fat, non hydrogenated lard, raw cream\* Stick to animal fats in colder months. **_When you eat seafood try to use MUFA’s as the added fat.\*_**

But my question is in winter, what mufas are ideal? Since they are mostly listed as spring - summer seasonal fats? What do yall do ? Or simpy up your sat fat in winter with no seafood protein as the Epi Paleo Rx states, and just not add any extra fat with seafood?

Thanks.

that tells our body to shut down, lover bod temperature, get ready to hibernate.

Humans (mentally) do not want to hibernate, but the available food does that to them.

### John Schumacher — 2024-03-04T22:10:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-fat-in-winter.28552/post-332216>

[@Desert.life](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/24745/) - [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) has been posting quite a bit on Linolenic Acid on his journal.
One you may find interesting is -> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jansz-journal.28468/page-14#post-332170](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jansz-journal.28468/page-14#post-332170)

In it, [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) references -> [https://www.cambridge.org/core/jour...growing-pigs/8C1B1E33E822B0B1D981A782D961BF92](https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/animal/article/abs/linoleic-and-linolenic-acid-as-precursor-and-inhibitor-for-the-synthesis-of-longchain-polyunsaturated-fatty-acids-in-liver-and-brain-of-growing-pigs/8C1B1E33E822B0B1D981A782D961BF92)

[https://sci-hub.st/10.1017/S1751731111001479](https://sci-hub.st/10.1017/S1751731111001479)

Which addresses the competition of fatty acid metabolism within the liver. Specifically, "Concentrations of EPA (>35%) and DHA (>20%) were _**decreased**_ by high LA intake... Dietary LA inhibited the biosynthesis of n-3 LC-PUFA in liver."

_**ALA**_ 5 a-linolenic acid;
_**ARA**_ 5 arachidonic acid;

### JanSz — 2024-03-05T15:32:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-fat-in-winter.28552/post-332228>

Work on escribing how excessive seed oils in foods and linoleic acid in them pushes mammals **and humans eating it** toward hibernation process.

Do not concentrate on hibernation process. I am interested in the time right before hibernation. Time when mammals are getting ready to hibernate. Usually end of summer mostly during fall when the actual hibernation happens in the winter.
\--------------

During the pre-hibernation period, mammals undergo significant dietary and physiological changes to prepare for the long winter months. [This period is characterized by increased food intake, often shifting towards a diet rich in fats](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00360-012-0689-0)[1](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00360-012-0689-0)[2](https://www.open.edu/openlearn/nature-environment/natural-history/surviving-the-winter/content-section-1.4.4)[3](https://mdpi-res.com/d_attachment/metabolites/metabolites-14-00134/article_deploy/metabolites-14-00134.pdf?version=1708520767).

Seed oils, which are high in linoleic acid, a type of polyunsaturated fatty acid (PUFA), can play a role in this process. [A study on garden dormice found that the fatty acid composition of a pre-hibernation diet can influence the depth and duration of metabolic suppression achieved during hibernation](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full). [**More specifically, a diet high in omega-6 PUFAs (like linoleic acid) relative to omega-3 PUFAs was found to be essential to maximize torpor expression**](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)**[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full).**

[The study also found that the ratio of linoleic acid (omega-6 PUFA) and linolenic acid (omega-3 PUFA) in the pre-hibernation diet influenced stress-inducible cell signaling in the white adipose, brown adipose, and liver tissues of the dormice](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full). [Dormice fed a high linoleic acid diet regulated NFκB signaling in a protective manner in all tissues](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full).

\===========================

Overall
High fat diet and especially when that fat contains very high Linoleic Acid content
cannot be healthy for humans (that are mammals who do not hibernate).
\-----------------

Overall, before 1900 but more definitely before 1850
human's ratio
Linoleic Acid (O6) to Linolenic Acid (O3)
was around 1:1
some say 4:1
others study parts of human bodies and figuring or finding variations, but still within 1:1 to 4:1 range.
\--
Laboratories and studies of fatty acids came long after 1900.
Laboratories have never seen person with healthy fatty acids.
LabCorp, where I do my analysis assumes healthy ratio of LA/ALA =34
Most peoples I have seen has LA/ALA somewhat slightly over 50.
Maximal ratio I have seen was somewhat over 180.

////

How to get out of torpor is another story, and it is more than just losing some body weight.
If anything, one must be aware of situation he/she found yourself in.

.
