[**More specifically, a diet high in omega-6 PUFAs (like linoleic acid) relative to omega-3 PUFAs was found to be essential to maximize torpor expression**](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)**[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full).**
>
> [The study also found that the ratio of linoleic acid (omega-6 PUFA) and linolenic acid (omega-3 PUFA) in the pre-hibernation diet influenced stress-inducible cell signaling in the white adipose, brown adipose, and liver tissues of the dormice](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full). [Dormice fed a high linoleic acid diet regulated NFκB signaling in a protective manner in all tissues](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full).
>
> \===========================
>
> Overall
> High fat diet and especially when that fat contains very high Linoleic Acid content
> cannot be healthy for humans (that are mammals who do not hibernate).
> \-----------------
>
> Overall, before 1900 but more definitely before 1850
> human's ratio
> Linoleic Acid (O6) to Linolenic Acid (O3)
> was around 1:1
> some say 4:1
> others study parts of human bodies and figuring or finding variations, but still within 1:1 to 4:1 range.
> \--
> Laboratories and studies of fatty acids came long after 1900.
> Laboratories have never seen person with healthy fatty acids.
> **LabCorp, where I do my analysis assumes healthy ratio of LA/ALA =34**
> Most peoples I have seen has LA/ALA somewhat slightly over 50.
> Maximal ratio I have seen was somewhat over 180.
>
> ////

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) -> LabCorp, where I do my analysis assumes healthy ratio of LA/ALA =34

From my labs, I get -> LA(1296)/ALA(22.9) = 56.6

Note: I'm not lethargic; I have more "energy" than the average bear; I am not fat nor skinny - but muscular; I typically feel pretty awesome most of the time. My wife often asked me, "Were you thinking of me this morning?" as she sees the smile on my face. I reply, "Always, always my dear."

John

From Blushield: “When coherent frequencies are available alongside the chaotic ones produced by many non-native EMFs, Blushield says our bodies will preferentially tune into the coherent ones. In other words, the broad spectrum of EMF frequencies in our environment are like channels on a tv – they’re all broadcasting simultaneously, but some channels are static-filled and chaotic and some are peaceful and coherent.”

Ok, so let’s say this device is putting out a “coherent field” (without adding to the electric field from the 60Hz AC circuit it is plugged into) and that a coherent field is healing and our bodies can tune into it. The 60Hz AC field and the 2.4 GHz RF from your cell phone, WiFi etc. are still there and we know they are unaffected by the Blushield Phi because your lights, TV, cell phone etc still work. The damage done to the body by nnEMF‘s is not because we are tuned into them and the damage goes away if we “tune out”, the damage is because they are damaging and we are exposed to them.

I was once told of a study where they exposed some lab critters to nnEMF. One group “A” had a “coherent field” (neutralizer/harmonizer) gizmo of some kind the other group “B” nothing. Group A ran around happy all day. Group B stayed as far away from the nnEMF as they could. Group A lived half as long as Group B . . .

Reduce exposure to harmful things if you want to reduce their harm. These devices do not cancel nnEMF they leave it unchanged.

# Thread: Bunkers, shelters, cabins, basements, exiles, prepping
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bunkers-shelters-cabins-basements-exiles-prepping.28563/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2024-02-04T22:19:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bunkers-shelters-cabins-basements-exiles-prepping.28563/post-331012>

Various forms and shapes of futures are imaginable. Agreeing to Jack, there is no cause and effect, but there are probablities, less and more probable.
Along with probabilities there are also ideals, ideal situations, and less ideal situations.

The latter less ideal futures require prepping in my opinion.

Top idea is exile and exile opportunity, in my opinion options are a good tactic. Clearly people have different strategies of how to maximize an outcome, and diversity is one of thoss strategies, so I would like to apply it to locations as well. As soon as we can get a place in El Salvador, we will.

I don't have the money or even grounds to buy a bunker like the rich are currently building them. The fact that they do is unsettling, if one assumes they didn't just get bored and don't know what else to do with their mullah.

A friend of mine just bought protective suits for herself, her partner, and her cat. And she suggested that I build a metal protective case of some sort in our house. And I said, dang, I was thinking the same thing, I thought about suits also. We compared pictures that we had in our heads, mine lead me to aluminized firefighter suits and she said she had a similar picture in her head. She would think aluminum could be the way. I had done some research and thought of copper mesh for a room.

She and I are quantum entangled in an odd way. We never met, but we have the same input all the time. I know, it's crazy, but whatever. I journaled my crazy diseases, so I might as well share that. She already lives in exile in South America.

Every Californian has a minimum of prepping done, because of the earthquakes, so we are good with that. If anything happenes, I'd prefer to not even be here, but I can't exclude the possibility.
Long story short: What cage or room can be buildt to protect from rays, radiation, frequency, basically waves of all sort? What do you think? The engineers among you, or the people who have an interest?

I found a German company that makes a copper mesh and it's used to shield against x-rays and such. I read in order to protect against waves, you cannot have a tiny hole in the cage, because all the waves come through a tiny hole. Like in a microwave. If the shield has a hole, it all comes through.
So if I would take one room in the house and completely cover it with copper mesh, have a door that can be closed and shape a copper cage, could that work?

Could I sleep with my family in such a room? I'm sorry that it sounds very crazy. I'm not scared, I'm just somebody who hates not bringing an umbrella and getting drenched. If something like that is not needed, it totally makes a cool experiment, so I could invest some time into it at no loss.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-04T23:22:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bunkers-shelters-cabins-basements-exiles-prepping.28563/post-331016>

A Faraday room, depending on how it is made will protect against certain Radio Frequencies. 100% coverage of walls, floor, ceiling, doors and windows is required. Being a metal box/cage it will attract electric fields so must be well grounded. It will not protect against magnetic fields.

You can have NO electric devices, no cell phone RF device etc inside the Faraday room or you will effectively turn it into a “microwave oven” with you inside the oven. If you have any contact with the metal, if you are not insulated from the metal, you make yourself part of the circuit and the electric frequencies attracted by the metal will be going through you as well including any ground affects, dirty electricity etc. AND potentially cut you off from the Schumann resonance.

Wearing a metallic suit will do the same thing, block RF but attract electric fields. Do more research before spending any money.

### Marko Pollo — 2024-02-05T02:20:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bunkers-shelters-cabins-basements-exiles-prepping.28563/post-331023>

Plan to live joyfully in the natural setting you've chosen. Enrich the setting and make it more resilient and beautiful. Plan and prep for the coming winter, not eternity. Plan and prep to carry your own water; don't plan on hiring people to do your tasks. Plan to help your neighbors. Don't live in fear of low probability but horrible shit you can't predict like earthquakes, nukes, solar flares etc. Live joyfully and fearlessly!

# Thread: Mementos of truth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mementos-of-truth.28564/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2024-02-04T23:37:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mementos-of-truth.28564/post-331017>

There is a brain wash in effect that let’s people forget.

There is a human behavior called “hind site rationalization”. What it means is that people make sense of things that don’t make sense, just so that it fits their picture of what the truth is supposed to be.

Example: under hypnosis people were suggested to open a window, as soon as they were “woken up”. There was no reason for this. None.
Most people would go and open the window as suggested. Upon being asked why they did, they would explain that they were hot or needed air. They readily made up a reason. That’s what people do. And it is a lie of course.

Jack says the worst lies are the ones to yourself and I agree. That’s why I am hoping we can collect things in this threat, that we don’t want to forget. Even if society tells us to.

All my life I am repeating certain things to myself, so that I won’t forget them, or make reality smooth. It has helped me immensely with sticking to the truth.

I will start with a quote I just found on Instagram that I can absolutely verify.

“The Pandemic showed me that most of my neighbors are nazis who are simply awaiting instructions from a Führer & that's a lesson I can never unsee.”

My neighbors and housing managers called the police on me for not wearing a mask at the swimming pool while watching my swimming son. Gladly there was no police available because Santa Monica was looted and burned down, because of George Floyd.
Yeah, that happened also. The police did not stop a single person from looting. These guys just walked out of the stores with the goods. I have seen it with my own eyes. It is the truth.

# Thread: NTP halts research on health effects of cellphone RF radiation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ntp-halts-research-on-health-effects-of-cellphone-rf-radiation.28574/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### petrichor — 2024-02-06T20:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ntp-halts-research-on-health-effects-of-cellphone-rf-radiation.28574/post-331097>

[The NTP quietly updated their fact](https://www.niehs.nih.gov/sites/default/files/NTP_cell_phone_factsheet_jan_2024_508.pdf) sheet to indicate in no uncertain terms that they are finished studying the issue:

> **Is the NTP work on cellphone RFR completed?**
>
> Yes. NIEHS scientists conducted follow-up research to better understand some of the findings seen in the earlier RFR rodent studies.
>
> A novel small-scale RFR exposure system was developed. The researchers tested the new exposure system using in vivo rodent studies.
>
> The research was technically challenging and more resource-intensive than expected. No additional RFR studies are planned.

[More info here](https://thehill.com/opinion/technology/4437988-why-did-nih-abruptly-halt-research-on-the-harms-of-cell-phone-radiation/#:~:text=In%20a%20shocking%20reversal%2C%20the,of%20cell%20phone%20radiofrequency%20radiation.)

# Thread: I am looking for cancer advice to help my mother...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlexanderWhite — 2024-02-08T18:59:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/post-331223>

Just a few days ago, my mother was diagnosed with stage 3 cancer in a lymph node near her pelvis, which was discovered during a one-month post-op checkup after a hysterectomy on Jan 10th. She previously had a small polyp on her kidney about a decade ago that was found early and removed with no side effects and no chemotreatments required afterward.

Apparently, this is aggressive, so the doctors are opting to start chemo treatments right away next Monday morning (before defining where else the cancer exists) and then ongoing treatments for the next 5 months once every three weeks.

She is a 65-year-old white German/Irish female in Ohio who is an overweight smoker and lives a generally sedentary lifestyle as an elementary school librarian with otherwise "healthy" biomarkers, according to her medical team (I guess we have very different standards for "healthy").

At this point, I am unsure what questions to ask and where to start, so I am looking for insights from this community.

What foods, tests, habits, medications, or supplements should we consider along this journey?

**For example:**
Should she remove meat and sugar from her diet?
Should she get a PET scan to identify where there may be other tumors?
Should she start RED light treatments at home?
What can we do to mitigate the discomfort of chemo?
What red flags should we watch out for from standard medicine now?

Any advice or direction towards resources is welcomed for consideration. There is SO much out there, and it's all very overwhelming.

Thank you

### giedremail — 2024-02-08T19:31:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/post-331225>

Put into Google search Dr Jack Kruse and the type of cancer

### [Prävention u. Therapie](https://hufeland.com/)

Konventionelle Therapieverfahren in der Onkologie sowie aktive Präventionsmaßnahmen für Körper Geist und Seele. Unser ganzheitliches 5 Säulen Konzept.

 hufeland.com

Dr Demuth works with cancer patients for 30 years. Clinic often has patients from USA.

Prof Frass

### [Univ. Prof. Dr. Michael Frass in Wien](https://www.ordination-frass.at/en)

Die Homöopathie von Univ. Prof. Dr. Michael Frass regt die Selbstheilungskräfte des Körpers an und hilft, mit der Erkrankung selber fertig zu werden.

 www.ordination-frass.at

Dr Somlyai

### [Dr. Somlyai - The Center for Deuterium Depletion](https://www.ddcenters.com/dr-somlyai/)

 www.ddcenters.com

Do check and contact them for advice.

John Ott's book Health & Light. You can print pdf from their website.

I made a decision to move, to change my environment.

Sending my support to your family!

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-08T20:50:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/post-331230>

2 most important things her body needs:

\-Total darkness at night (to optimize melatonin, autophagy and apoptosis)
\-Maximal sunlight during the day (into eyes and onto skin, ideally the area of skin closest to the cancerous tissue)

**She needs to live every possible waking moment outdoors if she wants to reverse the root underlying issues.**

Realize that every moment spent inside of a hospital will only make matters worse (bc of blue light + other nnEMFs, and lack of IR + UV). She needs to limit time inside the hospital as much as possible.

I would also drop carbs like a hot potato. Especially this time of year in her location.

Carbs feed cancer and speed up its proliferation, especially when/where there is a lack of UV

Don't shrug off the part about darkness. It is VERY important to cancer reversal!

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-09T01:01:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/post-331242>

I’m so sorry to hear about your Mom, that is probably very stressful for you. I have no clue about cancer and my Dad has survived it many times, my Mom is also 10 years past lung cancer.

What I know is that the sun does magic and I hope it can do it for your Mom as well. Jack says: „Light before food.“

### JanSz — 2024-02-09T01:14:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/post-331245>

>
> Thank you

About radiation therapy.
There are gamma radiations.
There are proton radiations.
\----
When irradiating you want the least damage to healthy tissue.
So, when directing radiation, the stream of it is the smallest possibly.
but
Gamma goes always thru the whole body, first thru healthy part, then target, but then it still goes thru until it leaves body.

Proton radiation depth can be adjusted, so it ends at the target.

If possible, get proton radiation.

////

# Thread: Change of jobs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/change-of-jobs.28585/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### giedremail — 2024-02-08T19:36:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/change-of-jobs.28585/post-331226>

I am moving to another country and don't speak the language yet. I would love to do a job without PC.
Anyone has done that? Would love to hear your experiences. Thanks

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-09T02:18:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/change-of-jobs.28585/post-331248>

I moved to the US without speaking great English. I started working for a German company. Did not like it, only did it to survive.

And then I opened my own company repairing electronics and hired people who came from all over the place. I hired Mexicans, Russians, Chinese, Polish… Americans, no Germans though, hahaha (I’m German).

So there you go. Work for a US company! Or start one. Or a Swedish company. You are Swedish, right? Sorry, if it was another nationality, you know what I mean though.

People don’t even hire cleaning ladies who don’t speak English, why would that be different in another country? There needs to be some understanding.

In South American countries - in case that’s where you are going - they are generally not happy, if you take away the job of somebody local. That is something to consider as well. Possibly it’s like that everywhere. But you could hire locals as long as they understand you.

So it’s probably working for yourself/opening your own thing.

My experience is, that nobody is waiting for you to show up anywhere, and especially not, if you don’t qualify for something, like you lack a language ability. So you need to do it yourself, it‘s more fun anyways

### markthesunbather — 2024-03-13T20:21:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/change-of-jobs.28585/post-332519>

I'd love to do a job \*with\* a (Iris software installed) PC if it's (remote based) data entry work.

I am very fluent in English, and I'm probably going to wind up teaching it on one of these platforms like Verbling or italki if something else doesn't pan out soon.

# Thread: The deuterium content of tilapia growing in southern China
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-deuterium-content-of-tilapia-growing-in-southern-china.28593/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### budaev — 2024-02-11T01:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-deuterium-content-of-tilapia-growing-in-southern-china.28593/post-331317>

Some low latitude tilapia fish have deuterium levels as high as 171 ppm.Does it mean that some low latitude fish and meat are not suitable for consumption, even in the local area.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Marko Pollo — 2024-02-11T18:29:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-deuterium-content-of-tilapia-growing-in-southern-china.28593/post-331331>

Think of possible water sources in Southern China or anywhere else:
1\. An underground cavern that has collected from evaporated precipitation or snow/ice melt over time could possibly have light water and should be tested.
2\. Rain water that evaporated from the local environment should be in the middle ground and should be tested.
3\. Water in rivers and local environment that is making its way to the sea slowly, evaporating constantly, leaving heavier water. Need to test to make assumptions.

These 3 water sources can vary greatly. Now think of the food the tilapia eat. Water sources, soil type, fertilization, plant variety all can affect D levels in the fish food and hence the fish.

My point is that D levels can vary greatly within a distinct geographic area and without testing individual samples, we are just speculating.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-11T20:25:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-deuterium-content-of-tilapia-growing-in-southern-china.28593/post-331338>

I’ve seen photos of tilapia farms in China . . . the workers were in full hazmat suits. I got the impression deuterium content is the least of your worries. I would never eat/drink anything knowingly that came from China.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-331465>

## 6\. Transfer to the Brain​

"Investigations into photo-transduction have not included any role for the DHA, electron-rich membrane structure surrounding rhodopsin and other opsins. Based on this non-classical behavior described above, it is plausible that a quantum field is responsible for energy transfer from the isomerization to the DHA surrounding rhodopsin. The idea of using quantum field theory in brain function is not new \[[32](https://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/25/11/1520#B32-entropy-25-01520)\].
On the other hand, **the idea is that the isomerization energy surplus is released as heat, which is vibrational energy that is quantized.** With electronic/vibronic coherence already demonstrated, we have a novel and plausible mechanism for the quantized transfer of the isomerization energy to DHA"

## 9\. Summary and Conclusions: DHA as a Photon-Energy-Electron Transducer​

"In this paper, we have set out the limitations of the current model of photo-transduction and provided experimental evidence to support a previously unrecognized key role for DHA in the process. In this revision to the process, the quantum of energy from the photon-induced cis–trans isomerization of retinal is absorbed by a DHA π-electron. **Hyperpolarization** **extracts the energized electron,** which then transmits its information to the brain, **conserving the fidelity** of the original wavelength. **This hypothesis provides a clear rationale to support the observations of the extraordinary high density of DHA and the presence of very long-chain omega-3 PUFAs surrounding the opsins, which serve to absorb the energy, and DHA acts as the photon-energy transducer sending visual information to the brain for image reconstruction in our consciousness.**
In so doing, this thesis provides an explanation for the previously unresolved issues around the speed of information transfer and the purity of the conservation of a photon’s wavelength. It also explains the extraordinary concentration of the hexaenoic motive in the lipids adjacent to and attached to rhodopsin. This relationship has been conserved throughout the evolution of vertebrate species \[[41](https://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/25/11/1520#B41-entropy-25-01520)\]. Our proposal that DHA acts as a **photon-electron transducer** for visual reception places DHA in an unassailable position at the center of visual signaling, and on a par with vitamin A"

### [Docosahexaenoic Acid Explains the Unexplained in Visual Transduction](https://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/25/11/1520)

In George Wald’s Nobel Prize acceptance speech for “discoveries concerning the primary physiological and chemical visual processes in the eye”, he noted that events after the activation of rhodopsin are too slow to explain visual reception. Photoreceptor membrane phosphoglycerides contain...

 www.mdpi.com

Hyperpolarization is when the **membrane potential becomes more negative at a particular spot on the neuron's membrane.**

### Brent Patrick — 2024-02-17T05:03:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-331536>

"Despite the myriad influences of DHA on AD neuropathological mechanisms, **and the association of greater fish intake with a reduced risk of AD** at an epidemiological level, **manipulation of dietary DHA intake by fish oil supplementation has had little consistent effect to prevent or ameliorate AD** \[[30](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B30-ijms-22-11826)\]. More recently, the understanding that different dietary and plasma DHA forms have specific metabolic fates affecting distribution into tissues may help to explain why **fish oil supplementation does not provide the beneficial effects expected from pre-clinical and epidemiological evidence.** It has been argued that the brain has limited uptake of triglyceride (TG)-DHA found in fish oil supplements, **instead favoring DHA in lysophospholipid forms, such as lysophosphatidylcholine (LPC), derived from dietary phospholipid** (PL) \[[31](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B31-ijms-22-11826),[32](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B32-ijms-22-11826),[33](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B33-ijms-22-11826),[34](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B34-ijms-22-11826)\]. Lysophosphatidylcholine-DHA (LPC-DHA) enters the brain via the Mfsd2a transporter, a selective transporter of lysophospholipid-bound fatty acids \[[35](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B35-ijms-22-11826)\].

**Higher dietary N3 PUFA intake** has been associated with **fewer cerebral infarcts** \[[58](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B58-ijms-22-11826)\] as well as **a reduction in the risk of age-related decline in verbal fluency**, with particular benefit to those with hypertension \[[55](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B55-ijms-22-11826)\]. **DHA** therefore has **wide peripheral effects** that may also **indirectly reduce the risk of neurodegenerative disease"**

"Meta-analyses have found **no benefit of N3 supplementation to prevent cognitive decline** in cognitively normal elderly \[[89](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B89-ijms-22-11826)\] **or in the prevention and treatment of AD** \[[90](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B90-ijms-22-11826)\]. Post hoc analysis of cross-sectional studies by Cunnane et al. found no difference in blood DHA values between individuals with AD or cognitive decline and controls \[[91](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B91-ijms-22-11826)\]."

### [Why Have the Benefits of DHA Not Been Borne Out in the Treatment and Prevention of Alzheimer’s Disease? A Narrative Review Focused on DHA Metabolism and Adipose Tissue](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826)

Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), an omega-3 fatty acid rich in seafood, is linked to Alzheimer’s Disease via strong epidemiological and pre-clinical evidence, yet fish oil or other DHA supplementation has not consistently shown benefit to the prevention or treatment of Alzheimer’s Disease...

 www.mdpi.com

### Brent Patrick — 2024-02-17T05:05:36+0200

### Brent Patrick — 2024-03-17T03:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-332604>

### Potential mechanisms of macular degeneration protection by fatty fish consumption.​

Fatty fish have a protective effect on AMD and **DHA/EPA fish oil supplements do not**. Perhaps this can be tempered with the notion that there are multiple pathways and that we do not know the ones in effect. It is assumed that there is some synergistic effect of the diet of fish and we just do no know if there is one particular component or the combination of different nutritional components that are important for this protective effect. In a [randomized controlled trial](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/randomized-controlled-trial), **Johnson et al. found that DHA promoted the accumulation of the [carotenoid](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/carotenoid) [lutein](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/pharmacology-toxicology-and-pharmaceutical-science/xanthophyll) in the blood and in the macula \[[49](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib49)\]. Lutein, which is widely found in fruits and vegetables, was associated with a 37% reduction in the risk of developing late-stage AMD \[[50](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib50)\].** Fish itself is also rich in nutrients such as [vitamin B6](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/pyridoxine), which inhibits the activation of hypoxia-inducible factor (HIF) and significantly inhibits [retinal neovascularization](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/pharmacology-toxicology-and-pharmaceutical-science/retina-neovascularization) \[[51](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib51)\], and the nutrients in fish may have a synergistic effect in delaying the onset and progression of AMD.

Omega-6 LC-PUFA, which comes mainly from vegetable oils and animal fats, has been reported to promote inflammation. Omega-3 LC-PUFA, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties \[[47](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib47)\], and **Omega-3 LC-PUFA can provide protection by replacing Omega-6 LC-PUFA \[[36](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib36)\]

More Omega 3's not less!**

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054)

### Brent Patrick — 2024-03-18T22:55:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-332644>

### Docosahexaenoic Acid and the Aging Brain​

[Walter J. Lukiw](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Lukiw%20WJ%5BAuthor%5D)\* and [Nicolas G. Bazan](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Bazan%20NG%5BAuthor%5D)

DHA is an absolute requirement for the development of the human central nervous system (CNS), and the continuous maintenance of brain cell function, illustrating the strong mechanistic link between an adequate supply of essential PUFA in the diet and the sustenance of cognitive health.

Changes in the redox balance of brain cells, modulated in part by bioavailable antioxidants, may further affect the kinetics of these DHA-processing systems ([19](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib19)–[23](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib23)). For example, **the overall bioavailabilty of DHA may be in part dependent on the redox state of brain cells,** and the supplementation of DHA with antioxidants such as antioxidant carotenoids has been shown to significantly improve cognitive abilities in elderly populations

. The chronic nature of AD suggests that brain survival factors are progressively diminished or exhausted over decades of life as self-perpetuating neuropathology slowly takes over and spreads throughout the neocortex ([38](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib38),[39](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib39)). Part of this pathology promotes an up-regulation in the expression of apoptotic factors coupled to decreases in the expression of antiapoptotic members of the Bcl-2 gene family ([6](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib6),[39](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib39)–[43](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib43)). Unlike cholesterol and HF-C dietary intake, **DHA and DHA-derived NPD1 decrease the rate of A_β_ peptide generation and shedding from brain cells and diminish A_β_ peptide-mediated proinflammatory, proapoptotic, and pathogenic consequences** ([6](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib6),[16](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib16),[19](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib19),[43](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib43))

### [Docosahexaenoic Acid and the Aging Brain](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/)

The dietary essential PUFA docosahexaenoic acid \[DHA; 22:6(n-3)\] is a critical contributor to cell structure and function in the nervous system, and deficits in DHA abundance are associated with cognitive decline during aging and in neurodegenerative ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Brent Patrick — 2024-03-18T23:05:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-332645>

### Neuroprotectin D1-mediated anti-inflammatory and survival signaling in stroke, retinal degenerations, and Alzheimer's disease​

Bazan Nicolas G.

"The name “neuroprotectin D1” was suggested based upon its neuroprotective bioactivity in [BIR](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/reperfusion) and oxidatively stressed retinal pigment epithelial (RPE) cells, as well as its potent ability to inactivate proapoptotic and proinflammatory signaling. **“D1” refers to it being the first identified mediator derived from DHA"**

Specific intercellular trafficking assures the retention/conservation of DHA ([17](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022227520306465#bib17)). **The liver takes up DHA and linolenic acid (18:3,n-3) from the diet and elongates and desaturates linolenic acid to DHA;** then DHA is esterified into phospholipids, secreted as lipoproteins ([18](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022227520306465#bib18)), and delivered to the brain through the neurovascular unit (blood brain barrier) and to the RPE or retina through the choriocapillaris or [microcirculation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/microcirculation), respectively.

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022227520306465](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022227520306465)

# Thread: Poll: What is your optimal CT water temperature, duration & frequency?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis

### ColinGorham — 2024-02-12T10:15:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-331357>

What is your optimal CT water temperature, duration & frequency? I read the full CT series 10 years ago. What is your current thinking and best practice?

I think we can all agree CT is best done outdoors, under the sun, grounded in a natural environment, and with clean water.

This winter I have been running CT for: 90 minutes @ 54-60 F, 1-2 times per week in a cold tub (remineralized reverse osmosis water) on the deck.

During the summer, I would CT for: 60-120 minutes @ 50-75 F, 1-2 times per week in the Ocean in San Diego.

### JanSz — 2024-02-12T17:03:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-331369>

Fantastic results. Congratulations.

### jules3791 — 2024-02-12T21:27:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-331379>

### mitoj3di — 2024-03-03T23:01:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-332169>

### jules3791 — 2024-03-04T15:40:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-332197>

I've moved my tub outside which in turn has raised the water temps. 54-55f seems to be a sweet spot for longer time in the tub, but still much shorter times than most on here. My detox symptoms were so great at the beginning of January, that I had to back off significantly until March.

### Emmanuela — 2024-06-10T04:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335630>

I do 30min around 50-55deg pretty much every day.
But i do it inside with the window open.

### Mat.I — 2024-06-12T06:21:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335717>

In winter I CT outside whilst watching sunrise every day at about 4 degC/39 degF or lower sometimes (subject to outdoor temp) for 30mins then stay outside (without getting warm or drying myself off) for another 30 - 45mins in temps ranging from -2degC/28degF to say 6degC/42degF whilst doing some movement. We are at about 700m above sea level.. Note: this has been a progression over a few years..

### Saichi — 2024-06-14T15:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335778>

We are at about 700m above sea level.. Note: this has been a progression over a few years..

I've never tried not warming up after. Do you not shiver a lot few minutes after you get out of the water and stay outside?

### Mat.I — 2024-06-15T11:06:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335805>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=335778)
>
> I've never tried not warming up after. Do you not shiver a lot few minutes after you get out of the water and stay outside?

No when I get in the cold plunge it feels like my body reacts (warms) whilst in there after the first few minutes. When I get out no shivering at all just a little cold however I do some light movement and get warm again outside without drying off, this way you have used your body to create the heat rather than using external sources.. This is why some may experience after drop. Years ago when I was cold adapting I would dry off and go about my usual day however around 1 hour later I would experience some after drop shivers (not to intense, however enough to make you feel a bit uncomfortable)..

### Saichi — 2024-06-15T16:37:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335814>

Is your sunbathing protocol 'limited' to just your sunrise CT and warmup or do you get more non-CT exposure throughout the day?

### Mat.I — 2024-06-16T12:28:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335822>

No, i get as much sun as possible. For example today me and my family were out from sunrise to sunset. We only came in to make some food for the kids then back out again. Normal days in the week i will do my morning sunrise routine then set up my work station (fold out desk, laptop and screen) outside in direct sun. When it rains I work under our covered area or in the office with the window open (however still get outside and ground in the rain every hour or so for bit of time. Every chance I get I will get any CT and sun. Example on cold days I will still drive with the windows all the way down and only wear shorts and Tshirt or singlet. My summer clothes are my winter clothes everyone jokes about that to me.. However there is intention behind what i'm doing. In the holidays when I take some time off i will be out everyday sunrise to sunset.

### Saichi — 2024-06-16T15:55:17+0300

### Mat.I — 2024-06-18T04:16:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335854>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=335843)
>
> I also work in direct sun and am currently working on the wifi problem. I know this is off topic, but what do you do for internet in your outdoor workstation?

[@Emmanuela](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31113/) - No worries. Just use a long ethernet cable from your nearest wall point (if you are hardwired already) or from your Lan port on router with WIFI disabled. If you searched amazon I'm sure they will sell ones up to 50m or more in length. You can get outdoor UV resistant ones that would be a bit more durable, however they are a bit more expensive. Such as this -

[https://www.amazon.com.au/Abireiv-Resistant-Waterproof-Buried-able-Compatible/dp/B09M6MR2WL/ref=pd\_sbs\_d\_sccl\_2\_3/356-3005478-9653648?pd\_rd\_w=FfWVm&content-id=amzn1.sym.e316aa1d-6e77-4be9-996c-5cb8fd4d5414&pf\_rd\_p=e316aa1d-6e77-4be9-996c-5cb8fd4d5414&pf\_rd\_r=39E0F2YVA72RRZ0H9FK3&pd\_rd\_wg=LMRYG&pd\_rd\_r=84a4e144-1f83-4b41-a04a-a339bc3f70f2&pd\_rd\_i=B09M6MR2WL&th=1](https://www.amazon.com.au/Abireiv-Resistant-Waterproof-Buried-able-Compatible/dp/B09M6MR2WL/ref=pd_sbs_d_sccl_2_3/356-3005478-9653648?pd_rd_w=FfWVm&content-id=amzn1.sym.e316aa1d-6e77-4be9-996c-5cb8fd4d5414&pf_rd_p=e316aa1d-6e77-4be9-996c-5cb8fd4d5414&pf_rd_r=39E0F2YVA72RRZ0H9FK3&pd_rd_wg=LMRYG&pd_rd_r=84a4e144-1f83-4b41-a04a-a339bc3f70f2&pd_rd_i=B09M6MR2WL&th=1)

Did you drill a hole in a window or wall to run the ethernet cable from inside to outside?

### Mat.I — 2024-06-18T11:21:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335861>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=335857)
>
> Did you drill a hole in a window or wall to run the ethernet cable from inside to outside?

I have hardwired most the house, therefore I just plug in at the wall plate next to our sliding door to the outside so run the cable through the door track under the doormat then outside on the ground. I pack this away / set this up everyday that I'm outside. I will be changing out the existing cabling to a higher spec, when I do that I will include an outdoor wall plate for convenience.

Re: your cable run - It depends what is the closest point to your point of connection (router Lan port I assume) to where you are setup outside. It is quite a small cable so you could make a little hole in the bottom corner of the insect screen (if you have one) and run the cable out the window OR just go through the door to outside (dependent on your configuration of your house) OR if you are looking for something more permanent - you could hardwire in a IP rated outdoor ethernet wall plate jack on your external wall (they usually come with a hinged flap with a gasket). I would just run a cable outside and set it up each day as needed, that way it is simple.

### Emmanuela — 2024-06-18T14:57:56+0300

# Thread: Astrophotography
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2024-02-12T18:23:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331373>

One of my greatest passions has been astrophotography. Unforuntately this does not align well with circadian rhythm practices.

What are everyone's thoughts on a way to do this the most optimally with minimal disruption? Given that the only real man-made influence on this hobby is the camera itself, I find it also a great way to connect with nature and stargaze since exposures take several minutes.

My thoughts are to do it between sleep cycles (in between deep sleep and REM sleep) around 12-1 am. Obviously I would need to be camping in the location I do it to avoid much disruption as well, and luckily astrophotography demands zero nnEMF light pollution (or moonlight).

I've read that UV-A minimally disrupts circadian rhythm, so maybe using an external display with the backlight off and shining a black light on it would allow me to see what I'm doing? (color is not very important for this)

Does anyone have any knowledge on what can cause the sleep "program" to end or mess with clock genes too much? I would assume minimal physical activity and no significant light exposure would be key here. But I don't know if being too "awake" from an hour of wakefullness/thinking while doing photography will mess up the subsequent REM cycles I experience after the astro session.

### JanSz — 2024-02-12T19:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331376>

Your greatest passions has been astrophotography.
I think you should continue it.
Should ease your worries if you read this, Jacks post:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-journey-2019-and-beyond.23037/post-330614](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-journey-2019-and-beyond.23037/post-330614)

### JanSz — 2024-02-13T16:06:30+0200

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=331373)
>

But do not worry, most important is to be happy.

If you were happy with what Jack said on Jan 26/2024
then
look again
Friday at 1:18 PM February 9/2024

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-moonlight-as-good-as-sunlight.28590/post-331273](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-moonlight-as-good-as-sunlight.28590/post-331273)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=331273)
>
> Moonlight is toxic. It is reflected sunlight at night. The word "luna"tic comes from moon. In language LUNA = moon

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=331281)
>
> Light at night is how you get cancer quickly.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-13T20:35:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331409>

Typical lunar illuminance (full moon) is around 0.05 to 0.1 lux at temperate latitudes during the summer.
Sky glow on a clear night (no moon away from town) approximately 0.001 lux, (good astrophotography conditions) - in town 0.007 lux +/-
All these measurements depend on location, time of year, distance from town/city lights etc. because sky glow (no moon) on an overcast night in a city can be as bright as full moon. (Sun light is 400,000 brighter than full moon.)
Having said all that what I understand Jack to have consistently told us over the years, is ANY light at night is not good/healthy. That especially includes nnEMF blue light (including reflected city lights) but even moon light (reflected sun light).
We all compromise in some way but ideally, after sunset, crawl into your cave and go to sleep - till dawn.

### JanSz — 2024-02-13T21:29:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331411>

Do not worry, be happy.
Some of my best nights were under moon light.

Does anyone know Jack's opinion on Omega 3 Plasmalogens supplements? Supposedly have the fatty acid at the SN-2 position. Of course seafood is best, but kids wont eat it. Yes, I can force them and do but looking for a partial alternative.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-16T16:05:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331515>

The big issue with oil supplements is they are very prone to oxidation. If you get the omega 3 naturally from seafood, it is much more resistant to oxidation

### coho3131 — 2024-02-16T19:11:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331529>

I also think absorption for substances outside of their natural form is another issue.

Are they even making it past the gastric acid to get to the duodenum?

There are really no shortcuts with regard to DHA. Like Joseph said, just eat the DHA-rich seafood.

The methodology of RCTs for fish oil supplements are significantly weak also.

I think it is most likely a real waste of money for something that lacks evidence.

### JanSz — 2024-02-16T19:55:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331531>

The problem with DHA is that there is no problem with DHA.
Most people have plenty of it.
If you do not believe in that, just test it.
\----
Just make sure that you test DHA and not some "indicators".
....

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2024-02-17T06:59:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331539>

> [Esg999 said:](/goto/post?id=331498)
>
> Does anyone know Jack's opinion on Omega 3 Plasmalogens supplements? Supposedly have the fatty acid at the SN-2 position. Of course seafood is best, but kids wont eat it. Yes, I can force them and do but looking for a partial alternative.

Data on plasmalogens has been known for a long time. I first read about them in the mid-2000s. There is no need to go crazy on expensive supplements. I tell my patients to eat grass-fed beef, heritage-breed pork, lamb, mussels, salmon, oyster, mackerel, and pastured eggs. 'EVERYTHING' you need is right there.

**For the kids**, it's grass-fed burgers, lamb kababs, heritage-breed pork chops, and pastured eggs (cooked however they like). Instead of tuna fish, if they eat that, try using canned salmon. It tastes very similar.

Everything we need already exists. We all need a guide sometimes to help us find what we are looking for.

# Thread: How do you know when you've reached a good level of optimal?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alexandra — 2024-02-16T19:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331530>

Hi, All! From what I've read / heard on the powwows so far (Dec/Jan), it sounds like it's a process for each person looking at their data and figuring out what geo location and amount of light is best for them.

My question is: what is the best way to measure success/whether you've reached a good level of optimal where you can stay for a bit/a good geo location to relocate to? Is it purely through labs or are there other ways? Also, when checking out geographic locations, any thoughts on how do you know what's right/where to relocate? We're in the USA, family is here so we prefer to stay and optimize here if we can. Maybe I just haven't come to it yet but it's not clear to me what my / our metrics should be except, perhaps lab results and the way we feel. Thanks!

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-17T03:54:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331535>

> [Alexandra said:](/goto/post?id=331530)
>
> Hi, All! From what I've read / heard on the powwows so far (Dec/Jan), it sounds like it's a process for each person looking at their data and figuring out what geo location and amount of light is best for them.
>
> My question is: what is the best way to measure success/whether you've reached a good level of optimal where you can stay for a bit/a good geo location to relocate to? Is it purely through labs or are there other ways? Also, when checking out geographic locations, any thoughts on how do you know what's right/where to relocate? We're in the USA, family is here so we prefer to stay and optimize here if we can. Maybe I just haven't come to it yet but it's not clear to me what my / our metrics should be except, perhaps lab results and the way we feel. Thanks!

Here is how I understand it:
There is not “good optimal” as you call it for all, and the guide is nature.

Since we developed and adjusted as humans pretty much all in North Africa around the equator, that’s a starting point for “optimal”, however us white people moved away from the equator and turned white. So we can actually live with less sun, but it’s no longer ideal or optimal.

Same with everything else. There was no artificial light on the planet, no electro-magnetic fields, no environmental stressors of all sorts. So all of that takes away from optimal, but different people have different issues and some are stronger than others.

While there are healthy centenarians who are smokers, it is unlikely to rightfully conclude that smoking is healthy. Those have a different history, environment, physique, it’s all different.

When people ask what you are asking, more often than not the idea behind it is: “I don’t have to change a lot, do I?”

And that is up to you. The sicker somebody is, the more radically they need to change should they want to get better - and not everybody does, believe it or not.

There doesn’t seem to be a great location to live in the US, so you can’t really ask, if there is an okIsh great one. If it’s good enough for you and your loved ones, then it is. It turns out that our “islands” of comfort are getting smaller and smaller and that is unsettling to say the least.

It all adds up, and humans are great at compensating. If you have good mitochondria from your mother and her mother you can worry less. If you already had a history of all sorts of ailments than you can completely ditch your concept of optimal and you need to hurry up making better choices.

Good signs are when: you are healthy and happy, have good hair, good skin, good teeth, good mental health, sexual health, gut health, good relationships with others, good connection to nature, good instincts… success… what else? You know where I am heading at. It’s actually common sense.

So then you can learn and improve from what you are learning.

If you have a deficit, well, then something is up and you need to start looking for changes.

Data is not even the best measurement.
Many of us have things wrong with them for all their life, that’s going to take a while to improve, no need to measure anything, just getting on it.

Of course not everybody can afford the 100percent best thing for them. But again more often than not, it’s not affordability that’s the issue, but some odd form of postponing. There is fear, unwillingness to change, being too comfortable, too lazy, not having discipline, being spoiled, lacking courage, inspiration or aspiration… lots of talent is wasted, time is wasted, opportunity is wasted, because humans have free will and they can override nature because of it. Many bad decisions are made out of disconnection from nature.

I would say most people here are sincerely trying to do the right things. And Jack preaches it really well: connect to nature.

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2024-02-17T07:20:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331540>

**I vote for testing and data as needed tools for optimization** - as well as - good signs are when you are healthy and happy, have good hair, good skin, good teeth, good mental health, sexual health, gut health, good relationships with others, good connection to nature, good instincts… success…

All the above is great, but guessing is the best you will do if you're not testing. Testing can give you clues as to where any potential outpoints may be and/or how things inside of you, or outside of you, or lifestyle is affecting you. Retesting gives you an additional feedback tool to know if your protocol(s) are working or if you need to pivot.

The first symptom 10% of those experiencing cardiac arrest feel is death. Data in the months beforehand may have saved a life and led them to a path to correct any outpoint contributing to their atherosclerosis. A bone density scan on a seventy-year-old woman who feels well but does not exercise may pick up on osteoporosis. This data could save her from a hip fracture or spinal compression fracture if acted upon.

Hello, and thanks for taking the time to reply! I feel very strange sharing private health info on here, but to make it succinct, I realized after having a couple of cervical spine-related issues come up last year that I've been ignoring my own health for a long time and need to make some changes. I've started this process, but as far as getting to a better latitude, that's where my uncertainty lies. I can test by visiting places, but think it would be pretty selfish of me to move my family around willy nilly. So I'm open to changes, but I want to make sure I make the best ones possible and not waste time being foolish. I come from a digital marketing and tech background, so I do appreciate metrics and common sense approaches combined. It makes a lot of sense what you're saying.

Thanks for the reply, I'm on board with testing and common sense combined. I've already done things, have seen some improvement, but not enough for it to be acceptable. Winters there are miserable and I cannot get my vitamin D up and feel good here! Just not sure what actions to take next on that front.

### Alexandra — 2024-02-17T21:26:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331563>

I'm not sure about a coach, but I think I've found a doc who will actually order the labs I ask for and help me to navigate the results a bit (my current PCP refuses to even order a CRP!)...

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-17T21:51:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331564>

> [Alexandra said:](/goto/post?id=331550)
>
> Hello, and thanks for taking the time to reply! I feel very strange sharing private health info on here, but to make it succinct, I realized after having a couple of cervical spine-related issues come up last year that I've been ignoring my own health for a long time and need to make some changes. I've started this process, but as far as getting to a better latitude, that's where my uncertainty lies. I can test by visiting places, but think it would be pretty selfish of me to move my family around willy nilly. So I'm open to changes, but I want to make sure I make the best ones possible and not waste time being foolish. I come from a digital marketing and tech background, so I do appreciate metrics and common sense approaches combined. It makes a lot of sense what you're saying.

How is it selfish of you to move somewhere with your family, if that would mean you don’t die quicker than you need to?

But then again, how can you calculate when you will die, or if you would even die prematurely? What if you don’t die, and you get just awfully sick for a long time? Would that be enjoyable for your family?

And on top of that, is it even proven what Jack says? Because really, it hasn’t been studied as much as all the other stuff, and only because he can back up his theses, it doesn’t mean that other approaches could possibly also have a decent outcome.

So there it depends on the experiences you had with regular medicine. Or the experiences others had with it.

I’ve asked Ettlinger, what coach he is talking about, because I’ve only found bad ones. I’ve only found non-helpful doctors.
I’m doing it all myself at the moment and I’m evaluating the math as well.

How many years do I have? Given not only my history, but my mother’s and my mother’s mother. How many years does my husband have? How much are we messing up our child by sending him to unhealthy places, while trying to optimize his future career and options.

In the end it’s math with a ton of variables and probabilities.
Can humans do that math? I don’t think we can and I’ve always been pretty good in math.
How can people who can’t do any math make good decisions? And how does the spider make its net? Do humans have access to a type of knowledge that supersedes math? I would say yes.

The sun is a good start and from there it’s a process and I’m sure it’ll come to you in one way or the other.

Another plus, you’re a mother. Since men can’t be mothers they can’t even fathom the power of a mother and in our generation or century really, we are vastly underestimated and undervalued. That’s definitely something to remember, because certain forces in effect do not necessarily want us to.

### DrEttinger — 2024-02-17T22:30:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331567>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=331562)
>
> What kind of coach would that be?
> Spoiler alert: I’ve paid a couple of thousand to all kinds of coaches who actually made things worse.
> Is there a good kind of coach?

As an example, myself, Dr. Kruse, Dr. Stillman... First, you interview the doctor/coach and see if their philosophy and practice align with you. The more educated you are, the more you will know if this person is right for you. If, after a bit of time, you find they don't align anymore, you pivot.

I have been practicing and researching for 35 years. I have 2 degrees. I still utilize others who can help me in areas I'm weak in.

Lastly, I have had bad coaches, too. That will never stop me from searching for better coaches.

### JanSz — 2024-02-17T23:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331568>

> I'm not sure about a coach, but I think I've found a doc who will actually order the labs I ask for and help me to navigate the results a bit (**my current PCP refuses** to even order a CRP!)...

Just so you know.
Multitude of tests you are able to get by buying scripts with your own money.
Then the tests are done by the laboratories that most often would do doctor ordered tests.
When you would buy that kind of tests, the cost is much less than the same laboratory would charge your medical insurance.

I do not only the above
but also, other tests done by laboratories that do not want to deal with insurances.
\-----
Insisting that individual person is not supposed to order his/her own tests (because (he she) is too stupid, does not sound smart (to me).
/////////////////

>
> Lastly, I have had bad coaches, too. That will never stop me from searching for better coaches.

Have you ever been able to interview a doctor? Not me, hahahaha.
Last time I asked a question at my annual, which was “what do you recommend for losing weight” and his answer was “any mixed nutrition is good, don’t do anything radical.” That cost $200 out of pocket.

My husband fought it, because it really was not a consultation, and we got it back. I use that doctor to order the blood tests my insurance is paying for. He is a nice guy, has not helped with anything so far.

You might be different, Dr. Ettinger, but that’s not common.

Also, I use the word doctor as a derogatory term these days. Mostly it’s just used to pretend authority.

I really respect Jack, that’s when I stopped calling him Dr.  he is the only medical person I know who makes sense to me as of now. I would t call him a coach though, but I guess that just wording.

I looked you up, Dr. Ettinger and you have a chiropractor Dr. So hm. I don’t believe in great benefits of chiropractic and I’m sorry to tell you that, because it’s your job.

I’ve done hair for the last 6 years and I don’t believe in any of that either. does that seem fair?

I need to look up Dr. Stillman, thanks for your answer. Much appreciated.

I'm sorry you have had such bad experiences. I've not had that, so I don't know what that frustration feels like. My DC degree is just part of what I do; it does not define me or how I practice. It's the degree, along with a BSc in human biology, that offered me the knowledge base I sought, holistic over allopathic.

I believe everyone has a right to know the person they are going to work with, whether it's the body, finance, or home. This is on the home page of my website."Please call me today to schedule your complimentary 15-minute phone consultation. Let’s see if you and I are a good fit. I truly want you to feel both excited and confident that I have an understanding of your situation and a plan that’s right for you. I look forward to hearing from you at 714-639-4360."

Since I see my patients often and share with them the knowledge and wisdom I have acquired over the years, I do use the word coach. I am a doctor who coaches his patients back to health and coaches them on how to maintain it over a lifetime.

# Thread: Oral Health for Children - An Quantum Biology Approach
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-for-children-an-quantum-biology-approach.28621/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### robtherhino — 2024-02-19T18:21:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-for-children-an-quantum-biology-approach.28621/post-331648>

Hi everyone,

TL;DR - what is a quantum approach to remineralising teeth for a 10 month old?
I've been suggested grounding, lots of sunlight, and for my wife to drink sole \[so-lay\] water. Anything else?

**Background**
Recently my 10 month old son started having a black spot and signs of demineralisation on his first two teeth.
For context, he had an anterior and posterior tounge-tie and some oral trauma from birth.
We got the tounge tie sorted around 3 or 4 months old, as well as guidance from an oral myofacial therapist to do a mouth massage daily.
Despite that, he has had a fairly consistent tounge thrust this whole time.

We've been to a conventional dentist and was told it was likely caused by lack of minerals around 6 weeks into development.
We should avoid citrus, brush twice a day, and use remineralising toothpaste.
It's also recommended to do fluoridation treatment when he is a bit older, which neither my wife nor I are keen on.

He is breast feeding and just started weaning.
I've started making raw milk kefir and feeding him bone broth / meat stock.
I've also spoken briefly with Kelly Bento \[soleil well\] who suggested the quantum practises in the TL;DR above.

Would appreciate any further guidance, reading, info, etc. on the subject.

### Niki Stanton — 2024-02-19T19:18:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-for-children-an-quantum-biology-approach.28621/post-331658>

> [robtherhino said:](/goto/post?id=331648)
>
> Hi everyone,
>
> TL;DR - what is a quantum approach to remineralising teeth for a 10 month old?
> I've been suggested grounding, lots of sunlight, and for my wife to drink sole \[so-lay\] water. Anything else?
>
> **Background**
> Recently my 10 month old son started having a black spot and signs of demineralisation on his first two teeth.
> For context, he had an anterior and posterior tounge-tie and some oral trauma from birth.
> We got the tounge tie sorted around 3 or 4 months old, as well as guidance from an oral myofacial therapist to do a mouth massage daily.
> Despite that, he has had a fairly consistent tounge thrust this whole time.
>
> We've been to a conventional dentist and was told it was likely caused by lack of minerals around 6 weeks into development.
> We should avoid citrus, brush twice a day, and use remineralising toothpaste.
> It's also recommended to do fluoridation treatment when he is a bit older, which neither my wife nor I are keen on.
>
> He is breast feeding and just started weaning.
> I've started making raw milk kefir and feeding him bone broth / meat stock.
> I've also spoken briefly with Kelly Bento \[soleil well\] who suggested the quantum practises in the TL;DR above.
>
> Would appreciate any further guidance, reading, info, etc. on the subject.

We had that issue. My kids are 6 and 9 now. What helped was in addition to sun water and magnetism, adding in supplemental vitamin K, making sure no mouth breathing at night. (If so, figure out why- chronic congestion? Mold exposure?) make sure emf is mitigated, no RF baby monitors, look into emf safe for low frequency emf in babies room. Avoid dairy even raw kefir until you have some variables knocked back. You won’t know if dairy is inflammatory causing nasal congestion or mouth breathing til you get other things sorted.

Current issue of the black staining might indicate nutritional status well before now.

If oral motor skills continue to be an issue, especially tongue thrust, find a highly skilled practitioner either in speech language pathologist that is also myofunctional trained, or possibly needs primary reflex training via MNRI therapist.

Avoid fluoride at all cost. Find a different dentist because it can be hard to hold your ground as a new parent. Best wishes to you on your journey.

### robtherhino — 2024-02-19T19:37:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-for-children-an-quantum-biology-approach.28621/post-331661>

(If so, figure out why- chronic congestion? Mold exposure?) make sure emf is mitigated, no RF baby monitors, look into emf safe for low frequency emf in babies room. Avoid dairy even raw kefir until you have some variables knocked back. You won’t know if dairy is inflammatory causing nasal congestion or mouth breathing til you get other things sorted.

>

Thanks! I appreciate the quick response.

What I'm getting from you, in addition to light, water, magnetism, is to focus on the following:
1\. Supplemental Vitamin K - any specific suggestions? Liposomal?
2\. Check mouth breathing at night.
3\. Mitigate EMFs.
4\. Black staining could be to do with low nutritional status.
5\. Work with a specialist if tounge thrust continues.
6\. Avoid fluoride.

Some follow-up questions regarding your children
\- Were you able to remineralise their teeth?
\- Did the black staining go away / not transfer to adult teeth?

Back in the house my meter went off in my son’s room. He has two metal shelves and all of a sudden those are scoring in the hundreds, like 200V/m they are not electric though so I don’t get it.

I also think it’s new, because I pretty much made sure that our son’s room is the cleanest in the house and he isn’t even allowed to keep his electronics in his room.

# Thread: Biohacks for knee pain?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2024-02-22T22:50:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/post-331838>

My mom has had some knee pain that hasn't gone away. She's not sure if it's in her joint or the surrounding area, but walking downstairs is harder than upstairs. This all sort of happened after her shingles shot, so I'm thinking it's either a high viral load or inflammation from the shot. She doesn't recall really hurting it, but does walk a bunch each day and has never had any issues with her knees before.

Any hacks for healing knee pain besides rest?

### JanSz — 2024-02-22T23:56:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/post-331840>

Around 1985 I was still working. 45yo at that time.
From time to time, I would get pain in one knee. Short lasting, in and gone.
After MRI doctor have not found any problems but suggested that there must be some small interferences there. Think of minute grain of sand in the ball bearing in the car. Floating around but getting caught sometimes.
Suggested that he could look it up and remove.
Afterward I myself suggested that he check my other knee.
No problem since.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-23T08:44:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/post-331849>

Most pain is related to inflammation, 42% of sun light is infra red (IR), which helps reduce inflammation. Put those knees in the sun.

### TaylorF — 2024-02-23T16:37:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/post-331856>

Which type of infrared light is best for inflammation? It's winter where she lives, but I can get her an infrared light or knee brace with infrared as a temporary solution.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-23T17:33:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/post-331862>

**SUNLIGHT** **ALWAYS CONTAINS INFRARED** regardless of season. Like Anthony said, "Put those knees in the sun"

No manmade bulb will ever come close to replacing sunlight.

Sunlight is the champion

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-23T19:19:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/post-331874>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=331856)
>
> Which type of infrared light is best for inflammation? It's winter where she lives, but I can get her an infrared light or knee brace with infrared as a temporary solution.

What Joseph said. However, IF direct sun is not possible a NIR 850nm light 15 mins 2x a day is better than nothing. I use a CMVision 850nm “security light” from Amazon but look around the Forum for everyones favorite NIR light.

### Brent Patrick — 2024-02-23T23:08:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/post-331880>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=331838)
>
> My mom has had some knee pain that hasn't gone away. She's not sure if it's in her joint or the surrounding area, but walking downstairs is harder than upstairs. This all sort of happened after her shingles shot, so I'm thinking it's either a high viral load or inflammation from the shot. She doesn't recall really hurting it, but does walk a bunch each day and has never had any issues with her knees before.
>
> Any hacks for healing knee pain besides rest?

Knee pain is a sign of low redox
As an example ...

I had a meniscus tear (very hard to heal I was told due to lack of blood supply to the inner meniscus )it is now fully healed.

The how....

# Thread: Let‘s talk about „the Hum“
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2024-03-05T23:26:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/post-332247>

I heard it two years ago briefly, and now I‘ve been hearing it for about 2 months every night: the Hum!

It’s not in my head, because I just got back from camping two hours up north. And there was no hum, total quiet.

I researched it a bit and the best I came across is an engineer who overlaid the map of Hum hearing people with a map of forced gas pressure pipes. It’s very striking how similar it is.

However, it might still be a different thing and the gas pipes could also just pick up the resonance from somewhere else.

About 5 percent of people hear it. I‘ve talked to many of them online who I think have a mental illness over everything else. Then there are tinnitus ones.

Then we have regional hums - like the Taos hum - that might be different from the world wide hum. Maybe they are all different.

Animals have been reactive to some homes with hums - scared, and it seems that some homes conduct a hum better than others.

My neighbor got me into it, by constantly asking, if I heard it. Now I can’t unhear it.

Interestingly enough it might be related to my vibrating chest at night, but I’ll put that into my journal.

The hum is just made for conspiracy theory. I sure don’t know what it is, but I can hear it. During the day there is usually too much other noise. It’s a very low frequency droning. Like somebody lets his diesel motor run idle. Sometimes it pulses. The pulse can pick up speed. And multiple people hear that speed difference.

Is it intended? Is it industrial? Is it harmful? Maybe even beneficial? Or if it’s not beneficial, can it be turned into something beneficial?

Oh the mysteries of this planet

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-05T23:29:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/post-332248>

Oh, and I tested my hearing. I can’t hear anything below 20hz, so I’m not special. Many people can hear 20hz. I would think that’s about the frequency of the hum.

It’s very base like, if you remember going to clubs and feeling the music in your chest.

### John Schumacher — 2024-03-06T01:37:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/post-332251>

>
> Oh, and I tested my hearing. I can’t hear anything below 20hz, so I’m not special. Many people can hear 20hz. I would think that’s about the frequency of the hum.
>
> It’s very base like, if you remember going to clubs and feeling the music in your chest.

[@NikkiYork](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28233/),
I'm glad to hear you had a "rest" from the "hum" during you camping trip.
I've never been to a club nor loud concert so I don't know what music in my chest might feel like.

I prefer the quiet sounds Nature provides.
Each morning sunrise (for the past fifty years), barefoot on the moist earth, I take time to notice...

Thank you for your post,
Grandpa John

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/post-332279>

I hear a hum at night if I wake up and it drives me insane! I live around smaller scale avocado groves and farms so I keep thinking it’s a water pump generator or something mechanical like that. My husband hears it also but not as loudly as I do, he says it could be semi trucks warming up their engines. I do typically only hear it bw the hours of 3-5 am so could be either theory, or of course something entirely different.

Like somebody lets his diesel motor run idle. Sometimes it pulses. The pulse can pick up speed. And multiple people hear that speed difference.

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-07T22:38:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/post-332296>

Same!

So yesterday the hum was so loud, that my chest vibrated.
I went crazy 3 years ago because of chest vibrating at night. Had it for 2 years, then it stopped. I thought it was me. Now I know it’s not. Or possibly tense muscles vibrate more, but initially it’s actually a sound doing this.
3 years ago I was just so surprised that I would wake up at 4 on the dot every night.

And then it stopped on day. It went on, off, on off and stopped.

I have no idea what it is, pumps? Drilling? I‘ll try to find that photo of the gas line map.

**Posts:** 6
