Hello,

I soon need a new pair of glasses and definetely want to step up the game and upgrade to a combination for my prescription with blue light blockers.
My thought is either a pure blocking solution in one outfit, like a tinted prescription lens - or, my favourite, a combination where I can mount different blockers on the front with magnets or something.

So do we have people here in germany/europe with prescription glasses with blue blocking tints like Jack is wearing? Which manufactured provides a safe solution that guarantees that wavelength are blocked sufficient? With that I could go and buy a frame I like and have the glasses made for the frame and dont look like a douchebag.

The second solution is interesting because one could use multiple tints and change them to fit the situation (driving, day, night ...) and you could go without outside.

Is anyone here who has one or the other solution in use?

**CELLPHONE-FREE COMMUNITY FORMING
After a year and a half of planning,** the twenty-first century’s first cellphone-free community is forming in Central America. Called Intentional Sanctuary Lifestyle Alternative (ISLA), it is located on 40 acres on the Pacific coast of Nicaragua. It is a small part of a 2,500-acre gated real estate development called Gran Pacífica, surrounded by rolling hills and teak forest and within walking distance to miles of volcanic lava sand beach.

So far, 83 homes are planned to be built under the supervision of a Certified Building Biologist to minimize chemicals and wireless radiation. Cell phones, WiFi, wireless mice and keyboards, AirPods, smart watches, wireless printers, and all other wireless devices will be prohibited in public and private spaces throughout the property. Interest is growing, and prospective home-buyers have already invested 1/4 of the amount needed to break ground and begin building. There is space for up to 200 homes in the future. The architectural designs for the property include a salt water pool, yoga center, and space for shared permaculture gardens.
Accommodations near the beach have been recently mitigated to be safe and comfortable (low EMF) for investors interested in visiting ISLA.
As of January 1, 2024, Starlink is not allowed to operate in Nicaraguan air space.

# Thread: what is with the excess UV caution from other people on the Quantum Biology train
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-with-the-excess-uv-caution-from-other-people-on-the-quantum-biology-train.28977/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2024-06-02T23:57:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-with-the-excess-uv-caution-from-other-people-on-the-quantum-biology-train.28977/post-335391>

I see other people who are "into" quantum biology and such advising for slow UV exposure. Don't sit outside for 6 hours in the sun, etc. I few have also claimed that suddenly going out in the sun has caused photoaging.
I am conducting my own personal experiment regarding sun exposure on myself. I first started sitting outside in the sun in April (43 lat here) as much as possible. if I could, I would wear a jacket and work outside all day. Now, it's hotter here and I tan in my kiniki in the early morning after sunrise for as long as possible. Than move to the shade when I or my computer get too hot. I sit in my kiniki almost all day unless I am in a meeting etc. Obviously, if it's raining, I cannot work outside with a computer so I work in from of an open window, no light on.
So far, my skin looks way better not worse and I had a cold of some kind a couple weeks ago and literally napped in my kiniki in the sun for the whole two days.
I almost think is abundance of caution is more a catering to the mainstream than anything else.
Thoughts?

### Brent Patrick — 2024-06-03T00:20:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-with-the-excess-uv-caution-from-other-people-on-the-quantum-biology-train.28977/post-335395>

There is much nuance and personnel context involved in optimisation from full spectrum Sunlight. Perhaps those who claim photoaging educate themselves by reading the Solar Callus Blog.

### [How Do You Build a Solar Callus Nature’s Way?](https://jackkruse.com/how-do-you-build-a-solar-callus-natures-way/)

Eating the exoskeleton of shellfish and certain seafoods provides you with a way to build your solar callus in a very counterintuitive way. The exoskeleton has massive amounts of chitin, iodine and carotenoids that help with solar callus and light assimilation. Carotenoids are organic pigments...

 jackkruse.com

### [Sunlight Energizes Infection-Fighting T Cells](https://www.infectioncontroltoday.com/view/sunlight-energizes-infection-fighting-t-cells)

Georgetown University Medical Center researchers have found that sunlight, through a mechanism separate than vitamin D production, energizes T cells that play a central role in human immunity. Their findings, published today in Scientific Reports, suggest how the skin, the body’s largest organ...

# Thread: DDW
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ddw.28980/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aylagustafson — 2024-06-03T08:01:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ddw.28980/post-335401>

Anyone have insights?
I’ve been getting hydro health (a 12 ppm case they measured at 6ppm imported from Russia)
I know Kruse has mentioned Gabor somalie but I can’t locate where you would actually buy the water from him

Is Litewater better than hydro health?!

Thanks in advance

### JanSz — 2024-06-03T15:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ddw.28980/post-335410>

> [Aylagustafson said:](/goto/post?id=335401)
>
> Anyone have insights?
> I’ve been getting hydro health (a 12 ppm case they measured at 6ppm imported from Russia)
> I know Kruse has mentioned Gabor somalie but I can’t locate where you would actually buy the water from him
>
> Is Litewater better than hydro health?!
>
> Thanks in advance

I use Hydro Health DDW-25

They also have other strengths but by my calculations they are much more expensive.
When evaluating which strength to buy I compare prices of average DDW level that I actually am going to drink.
Goal to have in my body is 130ppm (D/H) or little less.
For over 3 years I was drinking DDW-25 only and testing my outcome.
I was (basically wasting my money, my D levels were very low, without any benefits that I could figure).
Now I drink daily total of about 2.5 to 3 liters of water.
One liter of that is Hydro Health DDW-25.
The 1.5 to 2 liters is RO water from Costco that I tested and it had (149.4 ppm D/H)(assume 150).
DDW water and RO water come in half liter bottles.
That means that my average Deuterium content of water that I actually am drinking is:

(3\*150+2\*25)/5=100 ppm D/H
(4\*150+2\*25)/6=108.3 ppm D/H
\===========
For even numbers
every time I am considering buying any type of DDW
I am comparing cost assuming that I am actually worrying about prices of
DDW-100

So far Hydro Health DDW-25 gives me best prices.
\====
Hydro Health is selling other strengths, but that one is the least expensive by WIDE margin.
\=====
I am residing in NJ USA.
There are transportation costs.
The stronger the DDW the less bulky and less heavy it is.
Transportation costs may be the deciding factor in remote places.

///

> DDW water and RO water come in half liter bottles.
> That means that my average Deuterium content of water that I actually am drinking is:
>
> (3\*150+2\*25)/5=100 ppm D/H
> (4\*150+2\*25)/6=108.3 ppm D/H
> \===========

> every time I am considering buying any type of DDW

> DDW-100
>

> \====
> Hydro Health is selling other strengths, but that one is the least expensive by WIDE margin.
> \=====
> I am residing in NJ USA.
> There are transportation costs.
> The stronger the DDW the less bulky and less heavy it is.
> Transportation costs may be the deciding factor in remote places.
>
> ///

# Thread: Rising before the sun a non-negotiable?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-before-the-sun-a-non-negotiable.28983/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MatthewT — 2024-06-03T20:25:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-before-the-sun-a-non-negotiable.28983/post-335414>

I’m new here so forgive me if I’m being ignorant but a push in the right direction would be appreciated.

I was getting up before the sunrise - which is at 4:50 here, I was following the advice of the app “circadian” and getting up at around 4:30 then walking to a spot with a good horizon and viewing the sunrise, after the sky stopped looking so red I would always fall back to sleep until around 7/8 and feel fatigued all day, I was advised that the sun stays in “sunrise mode” for a few hours after the event happens and that maybe the time I was getting up was too early for me and perhaps I should allow my body to wake me naturally at around 7:00 or whenever I wake and just be mindful to get outside straight away and get the AM sun.

Is this advice accurate or is it imperative that I’m awake before the sunrise to see the entire event? I’m not 100% crystal clear with the details so I don’t know the answer myself, I assume waking up and going back to sleep is NOT optimal for health so is it better for me to wake before sunrise and fight the sleep until my body gets used to it or just allow my body to wake naturally at around 6/7/8 and get outside during that stage of AM sun?

If there are any resources anyone could point me to so I could learn more on this matter would help me greatly, thank you.

### markthesunbather — 2024-06-03T23:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-before-the-sun-a-non-negotiable.28983/post-335416>

I aim for 6:15 myself, but not to the point of setting an alarm clock. I just go with it.

I fight the go-back-to-sleep feeling and just immediately go and pace around outside for an hour. After at least an hour, I'll come back inside and cook breakfast, and then eat what I cooked outside and lounge around outside/ride my bike until 10 AM.

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-04T01:39:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-before-the-sun-a-non-negotiable.28983/post-335417>

What time do you go to bed? It is a weird way to live, but I sleep at 8pm many days. Had to be out till 9 the other day and it was so past my bed time. Hahaha.

### MatthewT — 2024-06-04T09:41:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-before-the-sun-a-non-negotiable.28983/post-335420>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=335417)
>
> What time do you go to bed? It is a weird way to live, but I sleep at 8pm many days. Had to be out till 9 the other day and it was so past my bed time. Hahaha.

I follow the guidance of the “circadian” app here too and go to bed at around 21:15 at the moment, throughout my life 21:00 has been my best bedtime, any earlier it’s too early and any later I don’t feel like I slept enough.

### MatthewT — 2024-06-04T09:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-before-the-sun-a-non-negotiable.28983/post-335421>

> [markthesunbather said:](/goto/post?id=335416)

### markthesunbather — 2024-06-04T17:37:57+0300

# Thread: Avoiding back surgery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoiding-back-surgery.29002/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan M — 2024-06-08T21:14:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoiding-back-surgery.29002/post-335582>

Grade 2 (almost 3) anterolithesis of L5 on S1. NCS/EMG results showing signicant impairment of the nerves. Chief complaints are neuropathy and pain. Have any of you good people dealt with this? Any thoughts on decentralized approaches to help resolve?
Chiropractic? PT exercises? Other? Wanting to avoid surgery if at all possible, and that seems to be what the spine doc is selling.

### JanSz — 2024-06-08T22:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoiding-back-surgery.29002/post-335583>

**Anterolisthesis** is an abnormal alignment of bones in the spine, typically affecting the lower back. It occurs when an upper vertebra slips forward on the one below it.

**Lumbosacral spondylolisthesis** is the forward translation of the fifth lumbar vertebra (L5) over the first sacral vertebra (S1).
\====================
I am now 84yo.
While in college I started dealing with lower back pain.
Must have been about 1959 or so (18 or 19 yo) while still in Poland.
Around 1982, in NJ USA, I am talking with someone about my incoming spinal surgery.
He tells me about his experience, and I am learning about existence of chiropractors.
For the last about at least 30 years, need or not, I am seeing chiropractor at least once a week.
Newer had spinal surgery.

/////


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Relocating to a lower latitude. Advice?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-to-a-lower-latitude-advice.29003/post-335585>

Hey everyone,
After talking with Dr. Kruse last week, I’m leaving Vermont and heading to a lower latitude. I was diagnosed with a brain tumor 3 months ago, had surgery and a successful total resection, and am leaving VT to recover and prevent recurrence of the tumor. I’m looking for suggestions re: safe places with ideal climates in Central/South America. I’ve been to Costa Rica and Colombia and have some familiarity with those places, and am considering Nicaragua. Any suggestions? Thank you so much.

Madison

I’ve been to Costa Rica and Colombia and have some familiarity with those places, and am considering Nicaragua. Any suggestions? Thank you so much.

### AntonisK — 2024-06-15T13:03:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiking-boots.29010/post-335807>

> [Gcazz said:](/goto/post?id=335642)
>
> Does anyone here have any hiking shoes they like or recommend?
> Going to Tasmania soon for a holiday and can’t wait to check out the thermal springs among other things and also doing a few hikes so was hoping someone here might have some shoe suggestions.

First, I have to agree with Anthony Smith, test your shoes in nearby familiar territory before the actual hike!
After more than 20 years of hiking, I would say you can't go wrong with Salomon & La Sportiva, they are the best shoes/boots I've ever used.
Some questions for you:
1) Do you need a waterproof shoe or not ?
2) Do you need a low-cut or boot ? if you hike light and on easy terrain, then a low-cut is a better option. If you hike with a fairly heavy backpack and/or rough terrain, then a higher shoe (boot) is mandatory.
3) Do you need a stiff sole or not ? if not carrying heavy backpack and not steep/rocky terrain, then you don't need a stiff sole.
Remember, more rigid shoes/boots generally have better ankle support but are heavier and less breathable due to reinforced material.
Salomon X Ultra 4 and La Sportiva Ultra Raptor II are both amazing, very light and comfortable with great grip.
Hope that helps!

# Thread: Laptop Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2024-06-10T19:44:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335645>

Hi all,

I am guessing, using a laptop outside in the shade, not plugged into a wall ( battery only), with a long Ethernet cable (no WiFi) is much better than using a desktop pc in doors plugged into a grounded socket, with iris software? This is for work.

So are there better laptops to purchase that emit lower EMFs that anyone has tested?

Thank you

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-10T20:33:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335649>

Get a meter, it’s awesome! You can test everything and you’d be surprised. It’s not expensive.

### healthy2015 — 2024-06-10T20:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335651>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=335649)
>
> Get a meter, it’s awesome! You can test everything and you’d be surprised. It’s not expensive.

Thanks Nikki we have a meter already. What i didnt want to do was buy a laptop that emitted high EMF's especially if someone on the forum had tested already.

### JanSz — 2024-06-10T22:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335657>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=335651)
>
> What i didnt want to do was buy a laptop that emitted high EMF's especially if someone on the forum had tested already.

You
list laptops that emit lower electromagnetic interference

Copilot
Certainly! Based on the latest information available, here are some laptops that are known for lower electromagnetic interference (EMI):

-   [**Dell Latitude E6230**: These laptops are favored for work and are noted for their low EMI emissions](https://www.emfanalysis.com/low-emf-computing/)[1](https://www.emfanalysis.com/low-emf-computing/).
-   [**Toshiba and Acer Models**: Specific models from Toshiba and Acer have been recommended for writing projects due to their lower EMI emissions](https://www.emfanalysis.com/low-emf-computing/)[1](https://www.emfanalysis.com/low-emf-computing/).
-   [**HP 14" Slim Ryzen 3 3200U**: This laptop has a hardware “OFF” switch for Wi-Fi and an Ethernet port, which can help reduce EMI when wireless is not in use](https://forums.anandtech.com/threads/recommendations-for-a-low-emf-laptop.2568518/)[2](https://forums.anandtech.com/threads/recommendations-for-a-low-emf-laptop.2568518/).

It’s important to note that while these laptops are recognized for lower EMI emissions, no laptop is completely free of electromagnetic fields.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-10T22:12:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335658>

There are 3 EMF’s from any computer. Electric fields (high if the computer is plugged in/charging and not grounded), magnetic (from a fan), and RF from WiFi/bluetooth. With ANY computer/lap top/tablet/phone for low EMF:
\- use on battery or a grounded charging connection
\- turn off WiFi & Bluetooth
\- make sure the ethernet connection is also shielded & grounded (not CAT5 but CAT6A+)
If your set up is good I do not believe the specific device matters. Your outside setup is better because you are outside . . . Inside is bad because you are inside and exposed to all the other nnEMF inside, NOT from the grounded computer connected to ethernet.

### Pratima — 2024-06-10T23:44:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335663>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=335658)
>
> There are 3 EMF’s from any computer. Electric fields (high if the computer is plugged in/charging and not grounded), magnetic (from a fan), and RF from WiFi/bluetooth. With ANY computer/lap top/tablet/phone for low EMF:
> \- use on battery or a grounded charging connection
> \- turn off WiFi & Bluetooth
> \- make sure the ethernet connection is also shielded & grounded (not CAT5 but CAT6A+)
> If your set up is good I do not believe the specific device matters. Your outside setup is better because you are outside . . . Inside is bad because you are inside and exposed to all the other nnEMF inside, NOT from the grounded computer connected to ethernet.

thanks, Just checked mine and it says CAT6 which can be remedied.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T00:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335665>

The shielded ethernet cables that can be grounded have metal plugs not plastic. Use a body voltage (BV) meter - touch the ethernet cord, if the body voltage goes up, it is not grounded (you have an electric field on/in the network). If BV goes down, the network is grounded. Same applies to your computer, lap top, tablet, phone.

# Thread: Covid vaccine lot report denied by CDC

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-10T20:24:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/covid-vaccine-lot-report-denied-by-cdc.29013/post-335648>

I just got an email denying access to the lot number report of my vaccine batch.

I kind of expected it, but I don’t understand it either.
I wanted to find out how large my batch of vaccines was. Maybe somebody could tell me? That should be available information, no?
Because then I could put the reported vaccine related injuries into relation and understand how toxic my batch was so far.

There are vaccine batches that do not have a single reported incident. Those must have been placebos. I mean come on, there is always a person who is allergic to anything - accept for maybe saline solution.

I want to know how deadly some batches are. I want to know how many of my batch peers get cancer and what type of cancer. I want to know everything about what was put into my blood stream.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-10T21:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/covid-vaccine-lot-report-denied-by-cdc.29013/post-335655>

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-10T23:39:34+0300

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=335655)
>
> Where did you look?
>
>
> ### [Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS)](https://vaers.hhs.gov/)
>
>  vaers.hhs.gov

I’m not looking for adverse effects.
The CDC offers a report that provides whatever unknown information about the different batches of vaccines.

I would hope to find out how many vials were produced with the number of mine. Would also be cool to see how many were sold and to what locations.

It would just be nice to get information as the CDC apparently has “reports”.

I want to read it, and they said, you can request that report, so I did. But it was denied, because I am a private person.
It doesn’t have information on patients in it, so I don’t understand why I cannot read the report.

ACCESS DENIED

It would be very interesting to me to know how many vials of each batch were produced and/or administered.

Do we have 50 death out of 500 doses of xyz or do we have 50 deaths out of 5,000,000 doses of xyz? It would be nice to know. I doubt that anybody would tell us, but does it hurt to ask?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T00:24:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/covid-vaccine-lot-report-denied-by-cdc.29013/post-335664>

When I did my research 2020 I found 90%+ of the adverse effects reported could be traced to 4% of the batches. These 4% batches were distributed nation wide, where as all the other batches were distributed regionally - NW, SE etc. Much, at least some, of the info you are lookin for could be extrapolated from VAERS. If there are any reports for your batch and they are all regional and/or few or no reported adverse effects then I think it safe to say it is not one of the “nasty juice” 4%. Good to know. I helped people who had to get it to keep a job etc. (I advised against it but their choice) to at least find a batch with little to no adverse reporting. We do what we can do.

Also remember most/all of the official reporting is bogus. Get hit by a car, test positive for COVID, die of injuries, Death certificate says died of COVID. I have been told that vaxed or not vaxed was most of the time not reported. The official “standard” was if you got COVID within 14 days of being vaxed it was NOT vax related. They lied, they are lying, they will continue to lie. Holland removed the people who died WITH COVID from the people who died FROM COVID and their COVID death rate dropped by 90%. Probably safe to apply that globally.

# Thread: Thyroid panel - free t3 vs total t3
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-free-t3-vs-total-t3.29017/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 0.66eV Eva — 2024-06-11T20:59:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-free-t3-vs-total-t3.29017/post-335699>

Hello all,

My appointment with endocrino is the 4th of July, so I'm learning from the newsletter 101 what tests to ask the doctor. I also need to convince her to request those tests from "optimizing labs" document. In Quebec, it is a wrestle game.

One of my questions is about free t3 vs total t3. I never had a free t3. I know they are different, but would like to hear from the mitochondriacs specifically.
What can I say to my doctor to get the free t3 specifically ?

I've been hypo for a very long time but doctors never disclosed that information to me until 2020. Yes, that is normality in Quebec. They say they call if abnormal... but they rarely call in any case.
I never tried levothyroxine, because I was waiting for my liver (Budd-Chiari) to heal to see if things would improve. And from Jack Kruse I understand I have always been hypo, and hospital ref ranges are skewed. My THS 4 or 2 was still hypo...A glimpse at my thyroid panel, for context. Thank you.

Oh, and my intuition, after reading yesterday [@CultKittenSunLiquor](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28339/) "No longer autistic"/T3 post was that I have to try T3 med very soon to get my brain (back)!! But I'll be a bit more patient and read read read.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Mike117 — 2024-06-12T13:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-free-t3-vs-total-t3.29017/post-335723>

Hey Daphnee,

I live in Quebec too (Levis) and I know how frustrating it can be getting the proper care in our public health system.

I see a lot of patients with thyroid problems. Getting the proper lab tests here is very complicated. If your TSH is normal, even if your doctor prescribed a T3-T4 test, the lab won't check them. They work with algorithms that automatically stop them from going deeper if TSH is normal.

Then, if your TSH is abnormal, they will check free T4 but if free T4 is normal, they won't check for free T3. Your best bet is to go to a private clinic to see an endocrinologist that will send you to a private lab like Biron in Quebec. They will test everything he requested. But if you go to the public health system, they won't test what he requested. They will do what their algorithms tell them too.

### 0.66eV Eva — 2024-06-12T14:57:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-free-t3-vs-total-t3.29017/post-335726>

Hey Mike,

Yes, doctors often say, "don't worry, we have an algorithm for that". I'm astounded each time.

May I ask you: so you managed to get your free T3 and not total T3 from Biron ? And all the other tests?

Happy to learn that there is still an option here for proper labs! I had the feeling doctors from private clinics would be ruled tightly by the same algorithms and Quebec College of Physicians...

Hey Daphnee,

Yes, you can get Total T3, Free T3 and even reverse T3 from Biron as long as you have a prescription from a MD.

Doctors working in private clinics are still ruled by the college of physicians but they tend to be more open. Sometimes they have to "work around" the system. I know a private MD in MTL that works with thyroid patients and has to tell them: here's what I'm obligated to do/prescribed. Come back to me in 4 weeks and tell me it's not working so I can document it and then give you what you really need.

### Mike117 — 2024-06-12T18:00:37+0300

Hey JanSz,

My best advice would be to consult a doctor who actually know what he's doing to help you. You might know one. He's called Dr.Kruse

And yes, I do monitor other metrics like body temperature, HRV, iodine status, etc. But even if you fix selenium, iodine, potassium, etc without fixing the environment first, it won't do much of a difference long term.

### JanSz — 2024-06-12T20:13:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-free-t3-vs-total-t3.29017/post-335742>

> [Daphnée said:](/goto/post?id=335739)
>
> Hi!
> Not at all, I'm really new to all of this (and my reading pace is very slow...)
> I'll do my best to get the RT3 too.
> I did monitor heart pulse when put on beta-blockers in 2020 (stopped the same year). I'll start to monitor again.
> I take sprinkles of kelp (might increase), some day a brazil nut for Se, ~1000mg elemental K from potassium bicarbonate and ~500 elemental K from cream of tartar.
> Interested in your suggestions

### [Omega-3 Index Complete | OmegaQuant](https://omegaquant.com/omega-3-index-complete/)

 omegaquant.com

### Consequences of Low-Carb Diets​

-   Keto diets can provide immediate symptom relief and rapid weight loss, but may worsen health in the long run due to the reliance on **gluconeogenesis**, a less optimal metabolic pathway for producing glucose internally
-   Low-carb diets fail to improve metabolic flexibility, inducing physiological insulin resistance instead, as they do not address underlying issues with glucose metabolism. Essential body parts, including the brain, muscles during strength training, and various organs, require carbohydrates for optimal functioning, highlighting **the inefficiency of forcing the body to produce its own glucose through gluconeogenesis**
-   Relying excessively on **gluconeogenesis** for glucose production is **energetically costly and inefficient**, requiring a significant energy investment for a relatively low energy return, and is stimulated by stress hormones, potentially leading to muscle loss over time
-   Low-carb diets **can hinder thyroid function** by reducing the liver's ability to convert T4 into the active thyroid hormone T3, essential for regulating metabolism, due to a decrease in glucose availability for the liver
-   Reintroducing carbohydrates in a balanced manner can improve metabolism and overall health, countering the negative effects of long-term low-carb dieting by enhancing the body's energy production and usage

# Thread: Pain Management Conolidine for my dying husband now for his spine fractures
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-management-conolidine-for-my-dying-husband-now-for-his-spine-fractures.29034/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2024-06-16T23:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-management-conolidine-for-my-dying-husband-now-for-his-spine-fractures.29034/post-335827>

I had not gotten enough information on Conolidine (GDR Labes) for my husband from my in May when Hospice drugs were not working for brake through. Hospice was not productive and my husband and other family disallowed the Red Clover Tea and Broccoli Sprouts I had begun at first observance of possibility of pancreatic mass even though if I was reading scans correctly looked like 1 cm decrease in size of tumor.
He passed under home hospice care on June 4, 2024 the morning of the day I was released from the hospital with a back injury.
Now I am coping trying with my own pain to avoid opioids and OTC along with other natural alternatives for back pain.
X-ray show multiple fractures in my spine they told me unknown if old or new..They refused to do an MRI. Very poor service from a Sentara Hospital. The more natural remedies sunlight and ice are not yet able to compete with **_Dual Advil_** or low dose **_Hydrocodone_**. I do not want to continue to take those but I am in intense pain.
God and Time are at work for my emotional heartache.
I am praying for optimal help to recover physical health. Any ideas appreciated.

Martha Ray

### Martha Ray — 2024-06-16T23:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-management-conolidine-for-my-dying-husband-now-for-his-spine-fractures.29034/post-335828>

> They refused to do an MRI.

If you are not under hospice care, (I think) that they should have allowed MRI. Then either insurance would pay for the cost of MRI or you would.

But if you are under hospice care, then they care for your pain management only, but they would not do anything to medically care for you.

At least that I have learned when my wife was under hospice care.

# Thread: Blocking Car Alternator
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blocking-car-alternator.29040/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2024-06-18T19:53:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blocking-car-alternator.29040/post-335874>

Has anyone had luck reducing the magnetic field significantly coming from the alternator in their vehicle?

I'm not sure if there is a best practice here, but I'm thinking of either:
\-Shielding the alternator (not expecting material thickness to redirect the field lines enough though)
\-Shielding the footwell
\-Creating an on/off setup for the alternator (uncouple from belt, then recouple, in a favorable ratio to cut down on exposure time)
\-Replacing the alternator with another source of energy stored in the trunk to supplement the battery

Long term plan is a larger/higher vehicle/jeep (my BRZ is way too close to the spinning steel in the wheels, and the engine bay)

Come in at birth, leave at death.
At birth we become observers
after death we are (non-judging) witnesses (good news, we are still there)
\====
What I wonder is if she thought about a fact that we produce daily a body weight of ATP?
And if she did, what she thought about it.

Proces of producing ATP goes thru synthesis and a breakdown of about 20 liters (or kilograms) daily.
(H + O) to (H and O) and so on.
(H + O) (or variety of O, and variety of H) (D) are variety of waters.
(H and O) is not a water (but same atoms are involved).
But then if we go into the fact that there are no atoms but only vibrations, and so on....

Synthesis involves combining components or elements to form a connected whole, analysis involves breaking down a complex concept into simpler parts.
Other antonyms for synthesis could include disorganization, division, separation, and sundering.
..
\===========
\===========
Nice thought.
Help other people.
By being of service to others (you are helping yourself).

One stone, two birds.
..........

# Thread: Cars with lowest nnEMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cars-with-lowest-nnemf.29087/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2024-06-30T23:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cars-with-lowest-nnemf.29087/post-336326>

Hi,
I'm looking to buy a car for the first time. I've found some information online, but nothing too specific. What are some cars/brands with the lowest nnEMF? Has anyone tested? I understand it is going to be on the older side.
Thanks!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-01T04:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cars-with-lowest-nnemf.29087/post-336334>

Every car model is different and can change from year to year. Some gas cars have higher magnetic fields because of the position of the alternator. You need a Gauss meter, take a test drive and measure. Odds are an older car will have no WiFi or Bluetooth which are probably the biggest concern, you also need a RF meter and measure. That said, some new cars you can turn off the WiFi & Bluetooth - ask the dealer/sales person. Don’t take their word for it have them prove it.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Mitochondrial haplotype testing interpretation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emily Burgoon D.O. — 2024-07-02T04:19:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-336410>

HI,
I've spent some time searching here, if I missed it I apologize.
Is there a breakdown anywhere that describes interpretation and differences in implementing treatment for the various possible results?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-02T07:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-336419>

Use the advanced search feature . . . Posted by Jack Kruse

### JanSz — 2024-07-02T17:24:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-336441>

[https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results\_web\_optimized.pdf](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf)

I Have the Results of My Genetic Genealogy Test, Now What?
\=======================================

1.  [**Haplogroups:** There are existing mtDNA haplogroups, similar to Y-DNA haplogroups, which can help trace ancestral origins to a particular branch of the mitochondrial genetic family tree](https://www.genealogyexplained.com/dna-testing/mitochondrial-dna-testing/)[1](https://www.genealogyexplained.com/dna-testing/mitochondrial-dna-testing/).
2.  [**Interpretation of Results:** The results of an mtDNA test provides a person’s haplotype](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf)[2](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf). [Family Tree DNA performs additional testing with every mtDNA test to determine an individual’s haplogroup](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf)[2](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf). [The differences are listed as mutations that can be compared to the thousands of other mtDNA mutation listings that are stored in the Family Tree DNA database and in publicly-available databases](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf)[2](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf).

### Daniela Lawler — 2024-07-20T19:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-337391>

> [Emily Burgoon D.O. said:](/goto/post?id=336410)
>
> HI,
> I've spent some time searching here, if I missed it I apologize.
> Is there a breakdown anywhere that describes interpretation and differences in implementing treatment for the various possible results?

Sorry to hijak but where do you test?

### sheas — 2024-08-27T17:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-338970>

> [Daniela Lawler said:](/goto/post?id=337391)

I don’t understand Jack like that - which doesn’t mean I am correct. I don’t understand him as seeing disease as a frequency that we can shield ourselves from via harmony or harmonics.

I see it more as preventing undesired cell death from being maladjusted to our environment. That afore mentioned cell deaths stemming from mitochondrial issues, that are light water or magnetic issues.

How you phrased it isn‘t mutually exclusive to what I said. I do however want to make that distinction because my approach is more mechanical than yours.

### thejessallen — 2024-07-12T14:39:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-aim-mitochondrial-harmony.29109/post-336997>

I appreciate you sharing your perspective. It is interesting how my mind wants a story it can tell. I just bought the Kruse for dummies course. It’s great so far. Maybe once I understand the basics better. I will understand more.

I know jack talks about timing… like the Earth has a magnetic shield around it. Somehow I am imagining The spin inside of mitochondria acting as a shield against things that try to get through, but don’t resonate at that frequency of balance and timing.

thanks for spending some your time with me. Have a beautiful day. By Sunday night, I will be messaging you from my new home El Salvador.

### NikkiYork — 2024-07-14T22:22:15+0300

# Thread: Laptop screen balancing device

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-07-06T02:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336720>

I'm an inventor and designer and have been wanting to shift my work toward meaningful electronics. As a result I am developing a device for balancing the blue light of screens with red (NIR). It mounts to the top of your laptop or computer monitor.

Am I allowed to post it / share here? Even suggestions and thoughts would be great. Using the profits to help promote educating the masses on light and environment.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-08T01:29:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336812>

### [http://www.phasmlight.com](http://www.phasmlight.com) -​

### Technical Specs​

-   Size: 2.6″h x 2.6″w x 1.6″d
-   Weight: 3.2oz
-   Battery: Built-in rechargeable battery
-   Charge and continuous power uses a USB-C cable
-   Battery Recharge Time: from empty about 2 hours
-   Battery Life: about 1-28 hours depending on settings (see Directions Tab for battery life estimates based on settings)
-   Infrared frequency: 850nm
-   Ultraviolet frequency: 395-410nm
-   Distance: IR reaches over 75 feet depending on camera settings (we’re still testing this based off of initial test results with our POV cam)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-08T01:37:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336813>

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=336721)
>
> [View attachment 28519](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/28519/)

Hi Kendall, how far along in development are you? Could you make it with NIR & UV as well as the visible red? Having used the one I have shown which is designed for video/photo work, I would prefer your low profile.

Thanks for that. Great portable solution. When i'm unable to work outside due to weather - I run red led strip lights ~ 660nm and in the process of adding NIR ~ 850nm + some purple UV. I would set this up as background lighting.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-08T02:03:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336816>

When I can’t be outside (it is 106ºF outside right now) In my main set up right behind my screen I have a 100Watt UV light, 2x 500Watt 850nm lights and a full spectrum aquarium light above my head. Be sure to measure for electric fields if the lights are not grounded.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-07-08T16:51:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336828>

> [thejessallen said:](/goto/post?id=336800)

> Interesting interesting. How do you know it’s giving off the right light balance?

I'm measuring the output at the user's average face distance from the screen to use the proper LED/lens combo

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=336809)
>
> Great idea. Have you checked the balance with a spectrometer? Also what wave length is the UV?

Haven't finalized yet but was thinking:

UV
280 nm and 340 nm

Visible
660 nm

IR
830 nm
850 nm

I want the user to be able to turn any of them off or on if desired

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=336813)
>
> Hi Kendall, how far along in development are you? Could you make it with NIR & UV as well as the visible red? Having used the one I have shown which is designed for video/photo work, I would prefer your low profile.

For sure I was thinking just invisible frequencies at first but if the visible can be turned on or off I think it's a nice addition.

Based on the lenses I'm testing with I should be able to get it a little lower profile than shown.

Currently have a working prototype but looking for a manufacturing vendor who can give a good price on the machined body and silicone mount.

Main question at this point is if I can get away with using just the USB C port as power (15 W) since the purpose is just to balance the blue light from the computer screen with IR and add in the lack of UV for aromatic absorption mainly for dopamine in an indoor context

### petrichor — 2024-09-03T21:44:08+0300

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=336816)

What's the reason for the aquarium light? Also, do you have any concerns about looking directly at the phasm light for extended periods while at the computer?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-04T06:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339221>

> [petrichor said:](/goto/post?id=339213)
>
> What's the reason for the aquarium light? Also, do you have any concerns about looking directly at the phasm light for extended periods while at the computer?

Flowers, fish and reptiles need full spectrum light to live . . . So do we. There are lots of lights for critters, not so many for humans. Make like the sphinx outside and a “critter” inside. Outside of course is the best.

### Mat.I — 2024-09-04T06:48:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339223>

Also, worth noting that full spectrum especially for LED's may not always be true full spectrum unless they add NIR. Thing to watch out for are the bulbs marketed as full spectrum, but are only "full-visible" spectrum. Anything LED unless specifically engineered with a continuous spectrum will have a very messy spectra. The best light source which is very rich in IR (with a continuous spectrum) is an incandescent bulb. I'm in AUS and can still get these online.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-04T08:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339224>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=339223)
>
> Also, worth noting that full spectrum especially for LED's may not always be true full spectrum unless they add NIR. Thing to watch out for are the bulbs marketed as full spectrum, but are only "full-visible" spectrum. Anything LED unless specifically engineered with a continuous spectrum will have a very messy spectra. The best light source which is very rich in IR (with a continuous spectrum) is an incandescent bulb. I'm in AUS and can still get these online.

Check out the lights for plants, fish and especially reptiles . . . they usually include NIR and UV.

### Mat.I — 2024-09-04T09:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339227>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=339224)
>
> Check out the lights for plants, fish and especially reptiles . . . they usually include NIR and UV.

Hi Anthony,
They may include it in some, however is a very messy spectra that drops heavily and does not peak in the NIR range. E.g. incandescent peak in the NIR range and would consist of ~ 90% infrared with continuous spectrum with a peak ~ 1 micron or 1000nm etc. Research the spectrum of incandescent and compare with others. I found some examples of advertised full spectrum grow lights below. Also a room that is well illuminated by incandescent bulbs could deliver a reasonable dose of red/NIR which is consistent with exposure levels referenced by PBM experts such as Hamblin and others in the PBM space.

Incandescent are the way to go by far (in the past i was blinded by the majority of full spectrum LED marketing bullshit), however eyes are wide open now. Working on a project at the moment.. Just need to see if I get my delivery/import of incandescent bulbs without being blocked...

### petrichor — 2024-09-04T19:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339253>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=339221)
>
> Flowers, fish and reptiles need full spectrum light to live . . . So do we. There are lots of lights for critters, not so many for humans. Make like the sphinx outside and a “critter” inside. Outside of course is the best.

Oh I am aware of that, I was just curious why specifically an aquarium light. There are many full spectrum options.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-04T21:34:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339259>

> [petrichor said:](/goto/post?id=339253)
>
> Oh I am aware of that, I was just curious why specifically an aquarium light. There are many full spectrum options.

My wife loves fish and plants so we have several aquarium lights and grow lights . . .
In my experience anything “medical”, full spectrum etc for humans are more expensive and only full “visible” spectrum.
Lights for fish, plants and especially reptiles include more UV and NIR. Just a cheeper way of getting there - because incandescent lights can be hard to get (almost impossible in CA).
42% of natural daylight is IR, 5% UVA, 1% UVB (depending on time of day and location).
This is why when inside, especially when in front of a screen I often have an aquarium full spectrum light, a UV light and a NIR (850nm) light close by. Lots of light, little to no heat.

### eve001 — 2024-09-06T15:03:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339326>

Interesting and complex. Love the elegant design.

And I recall these interviews on light with light expert Dr. Alexander Wunsch at Bio360. (German)

Could one place a red light above the computer screen to mitigate the blue light peak? the host asks.
And Dr. Wunsch answers that the source location/angle of the bad blue light and of the red lights above are so different that our sensitive organisms/eyes can notice it and that is not an accurate substitute for healthy light with the frequencies in their natural position respective of eachother.

Dr. Wunsch does seem very meticulous and seems to have a great sense of precision.
He's the one from the aromatic aminoacid slides Dr. kruse further elaborates on.

Is his objection too extreme? It makes me think of nudism.
Clothes interfere with the daylight on some parts of our skin but not elsewhere Is that confusing our non-visual photo-receptors?

If it is, that would not makes us all go naked all the time.
So if there's a place for clothing there could be a place for unopposed blue LED compensating light solutions.
We'd need precize studies to see what the impact is, or trust our senses/guts.

I spent lots of money on LEDS without blue and green and now wonder if that was a good investment.

To design ways to easily create outdoors offices could also be a great path of innovation. = bring PC out, create outdoors working spaces solutions low in nnEMF, but with the minimally necessary electricity, with grounding, daylight, protection of wind/rain/cold/nnEMF.
Or to look for ways to bring the daylight in, in new ways of building.
Mirrors? Do mirrors change light quality?

### petrichor — 2024-09-06T21:08:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339350>

> And I recall these interviews on light with light expert Dr. Alexander Wunsch at Bio360. (German)

> Dr. Wunsch does seem very meticulous and seems to have a great sense of precision.

I wonder what is worse though, having it present but imperfect, or not having it at all? I would think the latter...

### Richard Watson — 2024-07-06T08:52:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-336726>

I bought this light on marketplace for $20, Ive tried to find info on what the IR spectrum is on line with no luck, I know its made in the 70's and was made in Holland and is called Ultraphilips de Luxe. Anyone help me out here...

### Richard Watson — 2024-08-17T00:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338521>

Ive still not managed to learn whats the system that IR light is. I asked Phillips via email but never heard back.
The IR on this unit has been amazing. Three years ago I slipped on wet grass and ripped off three back tendons off the hip, I had them reattached two weeks later. The last year the hip was becoming quite sore on walking, but using this light twice a day for half hour has improved the hip to about 95%. This light throws out a lot more heat than LED's.
Being old I worried about how long this light will last and therefor keen I seeing if I can find something similar.
Even if you dont know anything about this unit, can anyone maybe have a suggestion where to hunt up info.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-17T01:21:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338523>

Can’t find any specs on it. I did notice it has IR and UV.
You say it gets hot on the IR setting. If it gets very hot it is not NIR but far infrared 1000-3000nm
850nm NIR will get warm, so if it gets hot then it is a higher frequency.

### Pratima — 2024-08-17T08:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338536>

Hi Richard. The details do not specify the range, but it does say that it is FIR (far infrared), not NIR (near infrared). I have a frozen shoulder currently and purchased this [https://infraredlights.co.nz/collec...d-infrared-red-light-therapy-300-with-stand-1](https://infraredlights.co.nz/collections/introducing-the-new-lightforce-classic-range/products/copy-of-lightforce-pro-led-infrared-red-light-therapy-300-with-stand-1)

I tend to use it on non-sunny days only and not sure about where you are, its been a very sunny winter in Auckland

Anthony said in another thread that security lights have the right NIR range and are cheaper, but the ones I found online here are only B2B. I would try that first as its more price friendly. The one I got is marketed for healing and therefore had a hefty price tag.

### Richard Watson — 2024-08-17T09:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338537>

I'm in Canterbury Pratima.
I have a LED 670nm light that I use most days for my eyes.
wonder if I would have still achieved the amazing results with NIR

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-17T19:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338550>

> [Richard Watson said:](/goto/post?id=338537)
>
> I'm in Canterbury Pratima.
> I have a LED 670nm light that I use most days for my eyes.
> wonder if I would have still achieved the amazing results with NIR

660nm light is good for skin issues, scars etc. it goes in 2-3mm. 850nm NIR light goes in 70mm+/-, it does not get hot so does not cause inflammation, in fact helps reduce it. I use mine 2 - 3x per day for 15 mins because I read that is optimal to stimulate the mitochondria and longer times can reverse this. I am not a medical person, there may be new data on all this I am not aware of but this works for me. Most infrared security lights are 850nm.

### Pratima — 2024-08-18T02:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338561>

> 660nm light is good for skin issues, scars etc. it goes in 2-3mm. 850nm NIR light goes in 70mm+/-, it does not get hot so does not cause inflammation, in fact helps reduce it. I use mine 2 - 3x per day for 15 mins because I read that is optimal to stimulate the mitochondria and longer times can reverse this. I am not a medical person, there may be new data on all this I am not aware of but this works for me. Most infrared security lights are 850nm.

and you let the device touch your skin right?, and you have grounded your device so its not emitting nnEMF?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-18T03:00:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338562>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=338561)
>
> and you let the device touch your skin right?, and you have grounded your device so its not emitting nnEMF?

I normally do not have it touching, maybe a few inches away when doing a treatment, but it is only warm and may touch occasionally when I move. I also use it about three feet away behind my computer screen sometimes To neutralize the blue light. I do have a ground cord I attach to it.

### Pratima — 2024-08-18T03:25:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338565>

thanks I need to grips with all this grounding stuff. I intended to learn of the mitos in ES but I just got carried away with being a tourist and eating cheap seafood and stuff. We have 3 pins in our plugs and I read that helps a bit?, but I dont really know.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-18T03:32:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338566>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=338565)
>
> thanks I need to grips with all this grounding stuff. I intended to learn of the mitos in ES but I just got carried away with being a tourist and eating cheap seafood and stuff. We have 3 pins in our plugs and I read that helps a bit?, but I dont really know.

Do not assume that three pins means there is no electric field. Depends on internal wiring. Measure and you will know. Very easy in most cases to add a grounding cord BUT the only way to know that is working is to measure. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Pratima — 2024-08-18T05:35:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338570>

do testers bought in America work in NZ.....thats prob a stupid question.......you can buy testers here but they always have mixed reviews. I only recently found the switch in the RCD panel that turned off the power in the bedroom.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-18T06:53:18+0300

# Thread: Still Don’t receive any emails from any Jack Kruse mails
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MikeJustin — 2024-07-08T15:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/post-336826>

Hi guys,

Since I’m a member I don’t get any mails from any jack kruse mails on my email account.

I whitelisted support@jackkruse and info@kruseatdestin.

I only get the mails from the forum and patreon.

Any of you have this problem and does any of you have an idea how to fix this?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-08T17:29:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/post-336831>

I have had the same problem. I get all the Forum notifications no problem but anything directly from the office (Q&A notifications) goes into “junk”. It says they are from the same address but it happens, no idea why or how to fix. I just check my junk folder every day.

### MikeJustin — 2024-07-08T19:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/post-336835>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=336831)
>
> I have had the same problem. I get all the Forum notifications no problem but anything directly from the office (Q&A notifications) goes into “junk”. It says they are from the same address but it happens, no idea why or how to fix. I just check my junk folder every day.

Very strange…

At least it’s in the junk folder. I don’t get them at all. Not on my phone and not on my laptop.

I hope this will be fixxed with the switch to the other platform.

I would very much like the get the e-mails

### Support — 2024-07-09T15:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/post-336864>

> [MikeJustin said:](/goto/post?id=336826)
>
> Hi guys,
>
> Since I’m a member I don’t get any mails from any jack kruse mails on my email account.
>
> I whitelisted support@jackkruse and info@kruseatdestin.
>
> I only get the mails from the forum and patreon.
>
> Any of you have this problem and does any of you have an idea how to fix this?

Hello!

Unfortunately, this is a spam filter issue on the receiving server. For some reason, your provider is not letting our emails through. Our system tracks the sending and shows that they are sent out of our server successful. We aren't not getting any ping back showing errors in delivery which means that once it's hitting your filter on the incoming email server it's getting blocked for some reason.

I have sent you a double opt-in message, subject line "Please confirm your subscription". Check your junk email for this if it's not in your in-box. If this does not work, you will need to contact your provider for assistance and to unblock our address.

Team Kruse

### MikeJustin — 2024-07-09T19:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/post-336885>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=336852)
>
> I'll send this in.

Thank you Jack.

### MikeJustin — 2024-07-09T19:15:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/post-336887>

> [Support said:](/goto/post?id=336864)
>
> Hello!
>
> Unfortunately, this is a spam filter issue on the receiving server. For some reason, your provider is not letting our emails through. Our system tracks the sending and shows that they are sent out of our server successful. We aren't not getting any ping back showing errors in delivery which means that once it's hitting your filter on the incoming email server it's getting blocked for some reason.
>
> I have sent you a double opt-in message, subject line "Please confirm your subscription". Check your junk email for this if it's not in your in-box. If this does not work, you will need to contact your provider for assistance and to unblock our address.
>
> Team Kruse

Thanks, I didn’t receive it, so I will go after it the hotmail bastards.

Thanks for the efforts team!

Mike

### drsongihan — 2024-07-11T05:53:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/post-336944>

This product may take 1-4 weeks from order date to ship. This is the best Shower Filter on the market (in the known world). If you find a better one... let us know and we will start carrying them! In order for a shower filter to be effective it must contain KDF. The twin actions of the ULTIMATE...

 livepristine.com

Is a shower filter worth it or loss of time/money? I´m thinking about buying this one. Any opinion? I do have an RO filter for my drinking water, but since I spend long time with showers and CT baths (and ocasionally hot ones) I wonder if it can be worth it.

# Thread: Skin health and sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-health-and-sun.29128/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChelseaBear — 2024-07-09T23:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-health-and-sun.29128/post-336894>

Working away on my tan here in the 50th parallel. I have been eating well for many years, good protein like local pastured beef and chicken (I raise pastured chicken), wild fish, seasonal veggies. I eat lots of eggs and I make pate with heavy cream and butter and liver from our chickens. Mostly local foods, very low carbs. I drink water with mineral drops. I spend lots of time outside, bare feet on the soil or sand. But my skin (I'm 39) which is GLOWING and clear, is often quite dry or dull on my legs. I wonder if the sun might be damaging it? What are your tips for supporting a healthy skin to receive sunshine? Thanks!!

### coho3131 — 2024-07-10T01:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-health-and-sun.29128/post-336902>

If it was me, I would try eating more fat if you have dry skin or hair. I like to add extra pork fat or suet to our soups and stews.

My skin is less dry after getting full body sun all year round, versus wearing shorts and t-shirts.

Maybe there is a private place where you can get sun on your whole body.

I'm not sure you need the mineral drops if you are drinking well water or bottled spring water.

# Thread: Too Much of a Good Thing Could = Overcompensation for Some Bad Things/Deficits?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-much-of-a-good-thing-could-overcompensation-for-some-bad-things-deficits.29137/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Olivert — 2024-07-11T17:53:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-much-of-a-good-thing-could-overcompensation-for-some-bad-things-deficits.29137/post-336964>

I have been wondering about my unusual labs where some good things like Zinc or B12 come way over reference range or TG that are below referenced range.
Zinc 23.11 umol/l
Vitamin B12 1139 pmol/l (but highest I had was 1800)
TG 0.31 mmol/l at relatively high carb diet (approx. 300-400g/day)
Also hsCRP is below 0.1 so it is considered 0 on my lab results - this despite overtraning and being constantly injured in the past until I learned better from Dr. Jack.

Those things would be considered good, but I wonder if they are not counteracting or coming at the expense or result of overcompensation for some other deficits and shortcomings. For example my white blood cell count is very low, I have neutropenia but I rarely get sick - my WBC is 1.9 but was as low as 1.5 and highest I ever had was still just 2.5. Also my iron and ferritin and sex hormones are extremely low. As you might have guessed centralized medicine had no idea what is going on and could not help provide any guidance in this. I was doing lots of research but clearly not sufficiently enough as I was unable to connect the dots.

So what I was wondering is that could for some reason some of my labs be so "good" or "high" or "low" precisely because it is overcompensation for others being so "bad"? I do not suffer any severe symptoms from anything, but I am curious just what is going on and what could one extrapolate into larger picture view from these "strange" or unusual interactions and results?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Blessing in Disguise?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blessing-in-disguise.29146/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rtd — 2024-07-13T17:15:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blessing-in-disguise.29146/post-337067>

I rent a floor in a multifamily house. Earlier this week my pantry shelving (5 shelves) crashed down. Whoever put up the shelving did not secure the mounting hardware into any studs, sheetrock anchors were used.

After a big cleanup from a few exploded jars and cans, I decided to reduce inventory via donation and garbage. Many products were not aligned with the epi-paleo approach. I did preserve rice, lentils, etc. strictly as a hedge against emergency situations (heaven for bid).

The shelves were replaced with a 5 tier adjustable wire rack. My previous abode I used a method with wire racks in a cool basement to organize items by expiration date using a First In First Out (FIFO) approach. The "fall" allowed me to get back to this method, albeit at a more fluctuating temperature and humidity.

Sometimes a problem (shelving failure) is the solution (cleanse and regroup).

# Thread: Mac users
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mac-users.29153/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Saichi — 2024-07-14T17:52:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mac-users.29153/post-337149>

I finally bought the cheapest of all emf meters, a handheld AM radio as Jeromy Johnson recommends. Despite my in-line DE meters reading under 15mV on all my electrical wiring it doesn't measure what actual devices themselves are emitting why the AM radio as I've learned is also essential.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-02T07:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mac-users.29153/post-337979>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=337930)
>
> I just checked disconnecting my ethernet and thunderbolt to external sound card, no audible decrease in EMI most coming from the laptop itself. About half the amount on the wired keyboard versus laptop keyboard though.

It would likely stem from the circuit board in the keyboard and/or laptop. If it is happening with out a key press then, based on how the circuit board would be configured it may be constantly check for status (even key status). This would likely only be low mag and electrical intensity however it could be in a frequency band that could have detrimental impacts on the user etc.. Everything I'm hearing about Mac's are no good whatsoever in terms of EMF. A mechanical key board could be a solution (for the key board issue). Re: oscilloscope as it is DC you would need something that can pick up the currents / frequency. Maybe a hall effect type sensor / probe / clamp connected to the oscilloscope could work (depending on sensitivity you may also need to consider pre-signal amps). You can get certain meters that have frequency band functions, however this is just a range. Is there an electronics place near you maybe get them to measure it for you? I assume you have also gone through a process of elimination to determine that the PC/Keyboard is the cause and not other emissions around you.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-14T05:49:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mac-users.29153/post-340964>

Wireless uses Bluetooth 2.4 GHz (same as your microwave). A wired connection, depending on the set up CAN pick up an electric field from the computer. It is all about whether the system is properly grounded or not. Measure and find out.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-15T02:33:42+0300

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-18T00:29:10+0300

I do.

I have no medical background or training, all I can share is my personal experience and what I have read.
There are good habits, certain stable things that can help us: getting up, going to bed & eating at set times - But most of os also have bad habits which need to be broken - this for me is were fasting helps. A biome reset, especially when relocating to a new place with different light, water, magnetism (food) is going to happen, why not give it a shove (helping hand) with a fast.

That said, there also appear to be certain stress factors that can help us at times - make the body “clean house“ & become more efficient.
Some even cause the body to produce stem cells (I have no way to confirm this).

-   Get Into Ketosis
-   Intermittent & Extended Fasting
-   CT
-   Extreme Movement & Exercise
-   Use Red Light Therapy

My person experience is our bodies are very intelligent and know what they need . . . But we must listen. Very few people that have access to food have starved to death.

[https://somavedic.com/](https://somavedic.com/) Thanks

I am an independent voice here, I do not speak for Dr Jack. You asked what he thought so I referred you to his posts to speak for himself.

You are asking for my personal opinion: I know practitioners who use various kinds of PEMF devices and get good results in certain people. It is all about vibration and frequency and can a good practitioner using PEMF facilitate healing in the body, it looks like it to me. That said, are there LOTS of people making wild claims, selling devices that are “questionable” - YES.

Can any of these devices neutralize and/or harmonize nnEMF to the point of it no longer being harmful - I would say absolutely NO. If your phone still works, it is still putting out the same frequency at the same power and those frequencies are still going through your body doing the same damage as they did before you spent your money. Do your TV & toaster still work? Then you still have 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric fields going through your body. You want protection from nnEMF - avoid it, turn it off, especially at night and create an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

As far as the Magnetico goes, it is just increasing the static magnetic field of the Earth, no vibration or frequency.

Is there a PEMF device I would recommend - YES. It Is the “BGT” (Big Glowy Thing) from Mother Nature. Mother Nature has world wide distribution and what is really cool - it is FREE. The frequencies are tuned for maximum human benefit and healing.

I do vortex my (well) water and put it in the sun for several hours then into the fridge. I use a $2 vortex device that joins two bottles together and it forms a double helix going from one to the other. This duplicates what goes on in nature. Does this “structure” the water . . . I don’t know . . . but it is fun and FREE.

That said, like allergies, some are clearly affected more than others. Is there a ”safe” level . . . As Saichi says it is probably ZERO. With this in mind we all need to measure and be aware of our (nnEMF) environment and reduce exposure as much as possible. The RF goal during the day should be <10µW BUT that would mean for most people where they now live, never going outside and staying in Faraday room with NO electronics.
Is there a net gain to being outside in the sun with a higher level of RF than being inside with a lower level . . . I believe so (within reason) - but each must choose what works best for them.
What I do know is that advantage of setting up an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary, so that at least at night when we are 10x more sensitive to the effects of nnEMF is HUGE. Over the years we have seen so many people turn things around by doing this. Measure, mitigate & Mend [www.LiveEMFSage.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSage.com/kruse)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-16T21:05:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ehs-sufferers.29159/post-338508>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=338489)
>
> ### [Effects of Radiofrequency Electromagnetic Radiation on Neurotransmitters in the Brain - PMC](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8415840/)
>
> With the rapid development of electronic information in the past 30 years, technical achievements based on electromagnetism have been widely used in various fields pertaining to human production and life. Consequently, electromagnetic radiation ...
>
>  www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Thanks for posting this, proof nnEMF has all kinds of effects on brains/biology.

Note about the power level/scale they are using - milli-Watts/centimeter2 (mW/cm2) not µW/m2 . . .
1 mW/cm2 = 10,000,000 µW/m2 (Safe & Sound Pro2 uses µW/m2))

Most of the tests were done at very high power levels, levels I have never seen. I rarely see a reading above 0.3 mW/cm2 (3,000,000 µW/m2) But do do see this often -
When first turning on a cell phone or when just sitting at the airport.
I often get 0.15 mW/cm2 (1,500,000 µW.m2) from a cell phone call 1-2 inches from the phone. Distance from nearest tower will affect this, the further the tower the stronger the phone signal will be.

But a couple of these tests were done at 0.02 mW/cm2 (=200,000 µW/m2) a reading I see all the time. (Very close to this from Bluetooth headsets.) I wish they had tested lower (real world) levels for several hours a day for weeks or months, but that would probably jeopardize their grant money!

Keep in mind the Building Biology Institute (BBI) say RF below 0.1 µW/m2 is of no concern and above 1,000 µW/m2 (0.000,1 mW/cm2) is of extreme concern.

Hi Anthony,
Yes, power densities are quite high in most the studies with exception to the lower 0.02 mW/cm2 ones. This is a long standing issue about how regulators determine safe public exposure levels as the main focus is based on thermal effects (tissue heating) SAR etc. However, there are quite a number of studies that also look at the non-thermal effects, but this gets brushed under the carpet. Another way to look at it would be as a dosage e.g. power density over days, weeks, months, years etc as it would also have a cumulative effect, hence the chronic health issues. This effect could be partially offset with adequate sun light (subject to environmental conditions), which is why this may be useful for gen pop of indoor living etc for the worst case ( to account for no light controls).

I assume you are already across the information through the Bio initiative.org. Links below -

### [BioInitiative Report 2012: Table of Contents](https://bioinitiative.org/table-of-contents/)

The BioInitiative Working Group report on electromagnetic fields (EMF) and health effects from power lines, cell phones, and wi-fi.

bioinitiative.org

### [Conclusions from the BioInitiative Report 2012](https://bioinitiative.org/conclusions/)

Several conclusions were reached from the BioInitiative Report 2012, including health effects caused by exposures to cell towers, wi-fi, EMF, RFR and more.

bioinitiative.org

### [Henry Lai's Research Summaries - BioInitiative Report 2012](https://bioinitiative.org/research-summaries/)

A collection of data regarding RFR scientific literature and free radical damage from RFR & ELF published between 1990-2012.

bioinitiative.org

### [RF Color Charts Summarizing Several Studies - BioInitiative Report 2012](https://bioinitiative.org/rf-color-charts/)

The RF Color Charts simply summarize several different studies reporting health effects relevant for cell towers, wireless devices and more.

bioinitiative.org

Quite a bit of research available at lower level exposures (including human studies).

ETAIN project however is primarily based on SAR & TWA. Using this method/data sets - [https://www.nature.com/articles/s41370-021-00287-8](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41370-021-00287-8)
