### Mat.I — 2024-08-19T02:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ehs-sufferers.29159/post-338609>

## 11. SUMMARY, CONCLUSIONS AND PERSPECTIVES​

The findings documented in this review are as follows:

1\. The calcium hypothesis of Alzheimer’s disease (AD) argues that increased intracellular calcium (\[Ca2+\]i) is the central cause of AD.

2\. The primary mechanism of action of EMFs is the activation of VGCCs, with such activation producing rapid, in some cases almost instantaneous, increases in \[Ca2+\]i.

3\. Excessive \[Ca2+\]i causes each of the following specific and nonspecific important AD effects: Elevated levels of Aβ; elevated levels of APP; hyperphosphorylated tau protein and consequent neurofibrillary tangles; lowered memory function produced in part by lowered synaptic activity, including the disappearance of dendritic spines; increased apoptotic and autophagic cell death.

4\. AD may be caused, in part, by a vicious cycle with excessive \[Ca2+\]i causing excessive Aβ, which produces, in turn, excessive \[Ca2+\]i.

5\. EMF exposures in animal models of AD or neurodegeneration produce still additional changes that have roles in producing the changes described immediately above: lowered miR107, increased BACE1, elevated LC3-II, oxidative stress, deformed synaptic spines and bouton formation.

6\. Genetic and pharmacological studies show that VGCC activity itself has important role in causing AD. VGCC activity may be important not only for producing excessive \[Ca2+\]i but also because of direct VGCC protein-protein interactions impacting synaptic function.

7\. The two main pathways of action activated by \[Ca2+\]i producing pathophysiological effects, excessive calcium signaling and the peroxynitrite/free radical/oxidative stress/NF-kappaB/inflammation pathway each have essential roles in AD causation.

8\. 12 different occupational exposure studies have each shown that people in occupations causing them to be exposed to substantial EMF exposures have a higher AD prevalence.

9\. Very young people exposed to many hours per day of EMF exposures from Wi-Fi or cell phone usage develop digital dementia.

10\. There has been a decrease in the age of onset of AD in humans over the past 20 years or so, which has been suggested to be caused by increases in human EMF exposures.

11\. Studies reviewed by Tolgskaya and Gordon \[[60](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r60)\] showed that rodents develop over a period of months widespread neurodegeneration from low intensity microwave frequency EMF exposures which impact many regions of the brain including the cerebral cortex and hippocampus, regions that are heavily impacted in human AD patients. This EMF caused rodent neurodegeneration resembles human neurodegeneration in having high levels of brain cell death, changes in the structure brain synapses and massive loss of synaptic connection.

12\. The El-Swefy _et al._ \[[61](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r61)\] study showed that very low intensity mobile phone base station radiation produced massive widespread neurodegeneration in 4 weeks. 11 measured changes and 4 observed changes in this EMF-caused neurodegenerative process were each greatly lowered by the VGCC blocker amlodipine. Among the changes documented were loss of approximately 34% of the brain cells, oxidative stress, high levels of apoptosis and inflammation. Excessive \[Ca2+\]i acts _via_ excessive calcium signaling and the peroxynitrite pathway of ac- tion to produce each of the 11 measured changes neurodegenerative changes.

13\. While many of the studies on EMFs causing neurodegeneration in rodents did not measure any specific correlates of AD, four studies on EMFs causing AD in rats did measure such specific correlates. Two of these were discussed in detail \[[86](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r86), [87](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r87)\].

14\. A series of nanosecond EMF pulses given to two-month old rats produced universal of near universal very early onset AD \[[78](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r78)\].

15\. When these same pulses were started at 2 months of age and continued each subsequent day, universal or near universal AD was seen eight months after the first day of pulsation – that is in 8 month old rats \[[79](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r79)\]. Some signs of AD are seen after only four months after the beginning of irradiation. Because rats typically live to be 36 months old, these EMF pulses are causing universal or near universal very very very early onset AD. The El-Swefy _et al._ \[[61](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r61)\] findings are even worse – there irradiation started in 4 to 5 month old rats and massive neurodegeneration was evident after only 28 days of irradiation.

16\. If humans were to succumb to very, very, very early onset AD at a similar fraction of the human life span as these rats do from EMF exposure \[[79](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r79)\], then we might expect humans to come down with EMF-caused AD at roughly 21 years of age.

17\. The rat studies of EMF causation of AD \[[78](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r78), [79](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r79)\] clearly show that EMFs lower the latency period of AD and epidemiological studies suggest this is also true in humans.

18\. The very early AD onset found in a study \[[78](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r78)\] and the very very very early onset AD or other neurodegeneration found in a few studies \[[61](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r61), [79](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r79)\] should be viewed as remarkable in another context. Up until 30 years ago, early onset AD in humans was caused mainly by relatively rare mutations that each have powerful causal roles in AD \[[23](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r23)\]. However, we see here universal or near universal very or very, very, very early onset AD being caused solely by EMF pulses.

These eighteen findings provide a specific type of evidence arguing that EMFs cause AD, possibly including very very very early onset AD in humans. These must be interpreted in conjunction with two other important findings:

Electronically generated EMFs are coherent, being emitted at a specific frequency, in a specific direction with a specific polarity and phase and for that reason, they generate vastly higher electrical forces and time varying magnetic forces than natural incoherent EMFs \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r22)\]. Those time varying magnetic forces are very highly penetrating and can act both directly and indirectly to place strong forces on the electrical charges on the VGCC voltage sensor to activate the channels \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r22)\]. They can, therefore, produce very highly penetrating effects deep in the human body even when they are present at millimeter wave frequencies where electric fields are largely absorbed in the outer 1 mm or so of the body. Five studies cited in another study \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r22)\] showed that non-pulsed MM-waves produced EEG changes in the human brain, with four of them also showing other neurological effects in the human brain, confirming the presence of penetrating effects.

Pulsations, both modulating pulses and pure nanosecond pulses produce much higher effects than non-pulsed EMFs of the same average intensity. Moreover, the entire telecommunications industry is in a big push towards greater and greater pulsation in order to carry larger and larger amounts of information. This includes, of course, 2G leading to 3G leading to 4G leading to 5G and, in addition, smart meters, smart cities and even smart highways, _etc._ This push for smarter, ever more highly pulsed devices may well be leading us to the ultimate disaster: universal or near universal very very very onset AD in human populations. Both animal and human studies, discussed above, show that EMFs can not only greatly increase the incidence of AD but can also decrease the latency period; the human latency of AD may decrease from about 25 years to perhaps 5 or 10 years. This means that it is possible that exposures we already have, 5G and possibly also 4G and smart meters, may have already caused the ultimate disaster, but we do not know it yet because we are still in the latency period.

The confluence of these changes of our understanding of EMFs put us at great risk of producing this ultimate disaster in the light of the 19 different findings regarding EMFs and AD listed above: the coherence of electronically generated EMFs, which produce vastly greater electrical and time varying magnetic forces than do natural incoherent EMFs \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r22)\]; the high level sensitivity of the voltage sensor controlling the VGCCs and other voltage gated ion channels to those forces; the importance of pulsation; and the push to faster and faster modulating pulsation in order to carry ever more information together put us at unprecedented risk.

As Jack said this is due to _Quantum foam. Causes more perturbation of the atoms in a cell = less favourable thermodynamics. Covered in the Ricci podcast.

Potassium needs to stay bound tightly within cells in order for intracellular water to remain structured to maintain semiconduction. ### Uswebers1 — 2024-07-17T06:39:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip.29165/post-337237>

Hi folks, My question is I haven’t had time to switch out my UV progressive reading glasses. What are your thoughts on wearing blue blockers over my prescription glasses while driving hours a day into the sun to start the day. The wind shield is high in blue light emitance, ive been trying to heal with blue blockers while in stores etc with franken light. I heard Jack say open the windows some but 70 miles an hour with 90 plus degrees will be quite the annoyance. Unfortunately my eyes have acclamated to the progressive glasses so i need them to read close up to focuing and now for more distances things while driving.
TY for any advice
❤️
Joe

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shortness-of-breath-tightness-pressure-in-chest-area.29175/post-337280>

I have been noticing I have been having these symptoms kinda frequently these days and I'm getting a bit concerned. Happens usually in the afternoon/evening about once every 3-4 days. It is quite sporadic.

I get 2+ hours of direct sun (mostly in the morning) every single day.

All ALAN is eliminated at night minus a hooga red book light. I go to bed regularly around 9:30 PM and wake around 5:30 AM.

I have never had symptoms like this in my life and now I get them often it seems. My worry is that this is cardiovascular.

Also, I never got any covid jabs.

Anyone have any ideas?

### jeremiasbtome — 2024-07-18T15:22:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shortness-of-breath-tightness-pressure-in-chest-area.29175/post-337288>

i have noticed this in me when i use wifi again on my laptop and since im back in ireland at the moment, my bed head area is by a electric outlet, i have lungs issues now with it, symptoms whenever i use wireless i think now, which means im more sensitive to elec imo, i grounded alot past month and reconnected with nature more than ever and i think now i get even worse sensitivity symptomss

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-07-18T16:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shortness-of-breath-tightness-pressure-in-chest-area.29175/post-337291>

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-07-29T20:35:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shortness-of-breath-tightness-pressure-in-chest-area.29175/post-337728>

I can certainly believe it.

I've been to Trinidad 3 times in my lifetime. Every time I've gone I felt like a brand new person. I had a certain vitality unlike anything I've ever experienced. I slept like a rockstar also. Actually, the first time I went, I stayed in a remote location on the island without AC. Once I adapted to sleeping hot, it was strangely some of the best sleep I recall ever having in my life.

Every time I left Trinidad and returned to the US, my sleep went back to shit and very noticeable brain fog set back in.

Never could explain this phenomenon until now. My hunch was always that it was related to my phone, bc on these trips I did not use a phone. I would always have a phone, but I kept it powered off for the duration of the trip. At the time, I had no clue the effect could come from the phone signals themselves. I just assumed being docile and staring at a phone was the culprit.

Now that I have the knowledge I've learned here, this is easy to explain now. Trinidad doesn't have near the levels of nnEMF as we have in the states. And also, in the location I stayed at in Trinidad, the levels of blue light at night specifically were non existent. I could go outside and see about 10 times the number of stars that were visible back at my Louisiana home. The night sky was incredible without all the light pollution.

For reference, I went on these 3 trips between 2010 and 2015.

### Mat.I — 2024-07-30T07:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shortness-of-breath-tightness-pressure-in-chest-area.29175/post-337773>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=337641)
>

>

> I feel like sleep could be better. I definitely sleep better than when I lived in my apartment 2 years ago. My intuition tells me my sleep is not fully optimized yet though.
>

>
>
> I currently do not consistently exercise. My lifestyle is a pretty lethargic one of late. I used to be very regimented with weightlifting and HIIT routines. I do a random HIIT workout maybe an average of once every 2 weeks.
>
>
> When I do the HIIT routine, my stamina is awful. Not near what it used to be. For reference, I have been doing these sparse HIIT routines outdoors and barefoot.
>
>
> Physically, energy seems fine. I do seem to be brain foggy often though
>
>
> I'm only at home these days. I return to work beginning of Aug

You likely already know this however, sometimes it is easier giving people advice than taking it your self.

#1 - would be to measure your environment to establish a baseline. Given how close the nearest cell tower is, this would be very important to determine what RF levels you are dealing with. In addition to this you need to be checking/measuring EF (including DE), touch currents where applicable & Mag Fields whilst simulating the normal working conditions vs sleep conditions e.g. breakers isolated etc.. You need to know exactly what you are being exposed to everyday/night.

#2 - all of the above should be measured in accordance with the BB standards/guidelines (all attached) with decent equipment.

#3 - re: cell towers - in AUS we can search for nearby cell towers to determine what frequencies they are using and distance from the target location. Is this available where you are? If so worth looking into also..

#4 - I have looked at the area you live in on google Earth - it looks quite dense and has an airport (Baton Rouge Metro Airport). This would not be good due to all the radar/comms that would be used. Are you close to the airport? As [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) as advised you are likely in a Mud Pit..

#5 - Consider how you are spending the majority of your of your days and think about what advice you would give to others on this.

#6 - look at getting to a better place. Any places further out of the city that are ok?

#7 - You advised that the symptoms started on your break - maybe this is a sign that where you live is no good.

You really need to measure your environment.
Also, hardwiring internet does not have to be "pretty" or "complicated". Just run ethernet cables from your router LAN port (with WIFI disabled) to where you need to use it (you can make it semi neat e.g. using tape or stick on square cable ducts etc. and run at construction intersections for neatness e.g. skirtings, cornices etc or use the roof cavity or underfloor space) Or just use as needed and coil up when done. You can buy the cable already terminated fitted with the necessary jacks. You can get decent Cat 7's that are very well shielded with the ability to ground them also.

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=337802)
>
> Thank you for all the detailed pointers. I don't really know a whole lot about measuring RFs, other than that it is possible to do. Really just haven't dug into the details surrounding it yet. I only just found this forum last year and assimilating all the information feels like drinking from a firehose. Prior to that time, I was completely none the wiser to the concept of mitigating RFs.
>
> And I am about 20 miles from the BR airport
>
> Regarding my work environment that I am on break from, it is a densely populated school with over 2000 students. Every classroom is equipped with it's own wifi router mounted to the ceiling and I am often standing near a presentation board that emits RFs. My classroom is also on the second story of the building.
>
> So, with all that, I can't fathom how my home environment could possibly be worse than that??

No worries. If you don't have the time to research this area it may be best to have a professional EMF building biologist do an assessment for you, however the cost of the assessment may be close to the cost of the equipment.. I know in AUS most standard assessments range from $800 to $1500 (dependant on size area and level of assessment required) this cost could cover decent grade equipment that would last a very long time providing you take care of them. However, if diving into more specialised assessments it would be worth paying the consult rather than buying the equipment for a one off assessment. My advice - research as much as possible, start with the basics and learn how to measure your environment as this will be very valuable skill to have, especially in the world we are living in today..

# Thread: "Measurement Problem" with Heart Rate Monitors for Exercise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measurement-problem-with-heart-rate-monitors-for-exercise.29183/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Olivert — 2024-07-19T16:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measurement-problem-with-heart-rate-monitors-for-exercise.29183/post-337331>

Reading _Electromagnetism and Life_ by Robert Becker and Andrew Marino I could not overlook how EMF, especially nnEMF can affect our physiological responses. Which as avid exerciser obssed with measuring my performance brings me to a question: Does it even make sense to measure your heart rate with bluetooth based heart rate monitors like Polar H10 since they emit huge amounts of nnEMF that can affect heart rate and even performance itself by measuring it?

Based on reading about all the effects nnEMF has on our physiology I can only imagine that having such a blast of nnEMF directly on my chest while exercising can have impact on my physiology and hence on the readings but also possibly on adaptations, recovery and performance. I measured nnEMF of Polar H10 and it is in extreme levels.

What I would be interested is whether there have been any studies on this or how would one go about experimenting with acute or longterm effects of using bluetooth based HR monitors during exercise? I can only imagine that even if such studies were made they would be cancelled as there is no one to profit from it, only losing lots of profits if it shows any effects.

For record what I have been experimenting as N=1 in this regard is to do my exercise as I normally do and then just at the end briefly put on Polar H10 strap to measure my HR whether it is identical to my normal measurements when I wear it during the whole exercise session. This is hard to do and control for all factors, but I think I can see some difference but I might be just making it up. But on other hand data suggest that such strong EMF has to have some impact on our physiology and if you add stress from exercise to it...

### Mat.I — 2024-08-08T03:43:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measurement-problem-with-heart-rate-monitors-for-exercise.29183/post-338177>

> [Olivert said:](/goto/post?id=337331)
>
> Reading _Electromagnetism and Life_ by Robert Becker and Andrew Marino I could not overlook how EMF, especially nnEMF can affect our physiological responses. Which as avid exerciser obssed with measuring my performance brings me to a question: Does it even make sense to measure your heart rate with bluetooth based heart rate monitors like Polar H10 since they emit huge amounts of nnEMF that can affect heart rate and even performance itself by measuring it?
>
> Based on reading about all the effects nnEMF has on our physiology I can only imagine that having such a blast of nnEMF directly on my chest while exercising can have impact on my physiology and hence on the readings but also possibly on adaptations, recovery and performance. I measured nnEMF of Polar H10 and it is in extreme levels.
>
> What I would be interested is whether there have been any studies on this or how would one go about experimenting with acute or longterm effects of using bluetooth based HR monitors during exercise? I can only imagine that even if such studies were made they would be cancelled as there is no one to profit from it, only losing lots of profits if it shows any effects.
>
> For record what I have been experimenting as N=1 in this regard is to do my exercise as I normally do and then just at the end briefly put on Polar H10 strap to measure my HR whether it is identical to my normal measurements when I wear it during the whole exercise session. This is hard to do and control for all factors, but I think I can see some difference but I might be just making it up. But on other hand data suggest that such strong EMF has to have some impact on our physiology and if you add stress from exercise to it...

I wouldn't use a chest strap with bluetooth. Wrist based is not very accurate, however on the basic garmin's you can disable all connectivity functions and it just uses the optical sensor (on the wrist). Generally, they may read ~10bpm higher than actual HR. I personally, wouldn't bother using any wearables to measure any of this. Just tune in and listen to the feedback your body is giving whilst exercising and don't get too obsessed with exercising in the first place.

### sheas — 2024-07-21T18:01:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-what-people-are-using-for-ct.29191/post-337432>

I had a DIY set up for the past 3 years and it finally died. Looking into investing in a real cold plunge tub under 10k for outside. What is everyone here using? Or hope to use one day?

### doctorwu85 — 2024-07-21T19:32:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-what-people-are-using-for-ct.29191/post-337437>

Probably not what you're looking for, but I put my setup [**in this post**](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-336861)

It's an indoor setup with an aquarium chiller that we made some modifications to, feeding into our normal bathtub. It can get the water to 54-55 degrees pretty reliably. As we're starting to want to go longer/colder, we're talking about having more ice on hand to add at the beginning and during the plunge, but getting the water to 55 with zero labor has saved a lot of time and mess.

Need to get a meter and figure out how to use cables instead of wifi....But that is a different thread

### Mat.I — 2024-07-22T10:09:54+0300

# Thread: The Mushroom Problem
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeremiasbtome — 2024-07-22T11:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/post-337471>

I noticed there seems to be more Mushrooms growing in my park very close to me and i think some near lawns by houses

I remember jack said 5g can grow funghi

Does this mean my enviroment is getting worse?

Its in Ireland, and it is currently summer

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-22T23:10:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/post-337484>

Mushrooms are the “fruit” of mycelium in the soil. Mushrooms are a sign of healthy soil.

Mycelium and mold differ in their methods of reproduction:

#### Mycelium: Primarily through spore production​

Mycelium reproduces primarily through spore production. These spores are often produced in fruiting bodies, such as mushrooms, and are dispersed to new areas where they can germinate and establish new mycelial networks.

#### Mold: Releases spores that become airborne​

Mold releases spores into the air, which can be easily dispersed to new locations. These spores can travel through the air and settle on various surfaces, leading to mold colonization under suitable environmental conditions.

>
> Mycelium reproduces primarily through spore production. These spores are often produced in fruiting bodies, such as mushrooms, and are dispersed to new areas where they can germinate and establish new mycelial networks.
>
> #### Mold: Releases spores that become airborne​
>
> Mold releases spores into the air, which can be easily dispersed to new locations. These spores can travel through the air and settle on various surfaces, leading to mold colonization under suitable environmental conditions.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-24T04:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/post-337527>

EMF will make mold grow faster but you can have mold/fungi/mushrooms with no nnEMF also. We have had mushrooms, fungi, mold etc long before 5G (or any ”G” for that matter). As I said mycelium in the soil is normally a sign of healthy soil. I would go with a RF meter before mushrooms.

# Thread: Dogs and Vitamin D
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeremiasbtome — 2024-07-22T22:56:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/post-337483>

Is it true that dogs can only get Vitamin D from diet?

Or can they get it from the sun also?

Whats the truth?

### pfedorow — 2024-07-31T20:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/post-337887>

> [jeremiasbtome said:](/goto/post?id=337483)
>
> Is it true that dogs can only get Vitamin D from diet?
>
> Or can they get it from the sun also?
>
> Whats the truth?

The book Forever Dog says that, but I don't believe it. They have a whole chapter about nonsense about supplements for your dog and say the same thing mainstream science says about humans and vitamin d: that we must supplement because food and natural light will never get enough. The main points I got from the book are that dogs are more sensitive to their environment than humans and need sun to thrive and live with lots of love and attention.Dogs need fat, protein, and no kibble, which probably is not great for your dog.

I don't think any modern dogs they test live an outdoor existence. Plenty of light receptors on dogs, but do they get tanked living in a modern world? My beach dog friends seem really melanated and energized. Always in the shade or in the ocean when they get too much.

### [Why Does My Dog Sunbathe? | Sit Means Sit - The Woodlands, Spring & Conroe](https://sitmeanssit.com/dog-training-mu/north-houston-dog-training/dog-health/why-does-my-dog-sunbathe/)

Why Does My Dog Sunbathe?. There are a couple of reasons why your pooch might enjoy basking in the sun. It’s not only a great source of Vitamin D – but it also just feels good.

 sitmeanssit.com

I use to be involved with Shutzhund (dog sport) and breed working line GSD's (in the past) with great success in producing very solid nerved good temperament lines. I also used early neuro stim techniques (e.g. getting them use to some loud noises, tactile stimulation, light thermal stimulation etc), these techniques are well documented in the literature. Kibble is by far the worst garbage you can give to a dog. A full raw diet - that simulates what they would get out in the wild (nose to tail, inclusive of contents) is by far the best. You can get a bit creative and add certain things in and people have termed this as the "BARF" diet with certain percentage targets e.g. muscle meat, fat content, organs, bones, a bit of veg to simulate undigested gut contents that may be presence after a hunt etc.. The results are directly evident in body comp, performance, behaviour, stools, development, dental/gum health and coat.

### sheas — 2024-09-27T22:21:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/post-340101>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=340098)
>
> What kind a raw meat do you give?
> Can it be any meat from any store? Or do you get it deep frozen and do you unfreeze it naturally then?
> How would you introduce it to a dog new to it?
> Looking for a change for my dog (11y, doodle, adopted with abandonment anxiety, and microwaved like me) He's overweight and said to be allergic to beef and some more things. He got tired of chicken. He got tired of horse.
> We have also an 18y senior dog here that always ate w/ the family.
> And a recently adopted 11 chiuaua. These two didn't get microwaved. They have some health issues.
> But they eat whatever we eat and no problems.
> Mine has chronic ear infections, presumably secondary bc of allergy induced itching.
> Of course doodles/poodles have difficult hairy ears also. Mine came home from trimmers always w/ tiny wounds and itching. Challenging choice for 1st dog.
> Saw 10 or 12 vets. They ruin your bank account. Apoquel, local antibiotics, sometimes injected antibiotics.
> I mentionned EHS and 5G and leaky gut. But they're not interested. Their business is thriving.
> Most vets full of RF security camera's etc.. In case junkies come steal their meds and money.
> I tried one vet who does bioresonance - no idea still how/if that works - but that guy was nuts.

There is a pet food store near me that sells only raw or raw freeze dried food. The brand I get has rabbit, ground rabbit bone, rabbit giblets, and some vegetables, cod liver oil, and sodium bicarbonate. I get raw freeze dried pork that has similar ingredients but with pork just to give them another meat option that has more fat in it. Rabbit alone doesn't have enough fat. I sprinkle the freeze dried pork on top. I did one of those glacier peak analysis so I try to keep them with meat that is not on their list of sensitivities. And I try to find local companies too. You can order through the mail from different places depending on where you live in the world. But ground bone and organ meats are important to add not just the muscle meat. if you can get rabbit, beef, duck, lamb, turkey are meats I stick with for my dogs. You can also give them chicken feet or duck feet as their bone source. It comes frozen and I let it thaw in fridge. Mine comes in individual patties so it is convenient.

My 18 yr old is a border collie Aussie mix and last year started itching really intensely. I couldn't figure it out because I knew her diet was good. Ended up being EHS, and not enough sun. And also started when we switched wifi companies to the fiber optics one! So when I spent more time outside at home, so did she since she follows me like a shadow. And her itchiness totally went away.

### eve001 — 2024-10-12T12:06:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/post-340871>

I recall a biochemist saying dogs need less or no vit D from sun as their furry coat shows. No idea though.
Can they make D somhow themselves without sun on skin, unlike humans?

I am looking for a wagon to take my dog hiking in the woods/unpaved paths.
He's overweight, chronically itchy ears, since he joined me in my (now former) 5G town.
Probably microwaved like me.

He's 11 and now 15kg with overweight.
Anyone ideas on a dog sporty buggy for moderate nature walks?
We're not going to the alps but forests and earth paths would be cool.
Weather here is often rainy.

Any ideas? Experience?

In urban areas RF changes every 10-50 meters and I feel it in my head. My dogs needs to sniff and go slow so walking him here is stressful and he's walk-deprived.
I want to take him to a bigger natural area where we can stay for several hours in low RF area.

He for now cannot always walk for long time = hence buggy.

# Thread: Frogs Melanate to Adapt to Chernobyl Radiation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frogs-melanate-to-adapt-to-chernobyl-radiation.29197/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JeffRouseMD — 2024-07-23T01:06:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frogs-melanate-to-adapt-to-chernobyl-radiation.29197/post-337486>

Not surprising but a good example of melanin and evolution at work.

### [Chernobyl black frogs reveal evolution in action](https://theconversation.com/chernobyl-black-frogs-reveal-evolution-in-action-191034)

Research on Chernobyl frogs has shown that the ionising radiation caused by the accident triggered a process of natural selection among these animals.

 theconversation.com

and the scientific paper

[https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/eva.13476](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/eva.13476)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-23T01:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frogs-melanate-to-adapt-to-chernobyl-radiation.29197/post-337487>

And I think I read somewhere they found a bacteria that “eats” the radiation that they are now using for clean up.

# Thread: Nano Vi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nano-vi.29215/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kathirf — 2024-07-26T20:26:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nano-vi.29215/post-337601>

I have often heard [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) refer to his postulate "If you make it, don't take it." But was wondering about biotech devices like the Nano Vi ([https://eng3corp.com/](https://eng3corp.com/)) Are these devices disruptive or beneficial to signaling pathways in our body?

It's more difficult to understand Jack's postulate when it concerns biophysical technologies vs biochemical with food, supplements, etc.

Thank you to all who may input.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-26T20:41:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nano-vi.29215/post-337602>

I went to the Nano Vi web site and as far as I can tell it is a very expensive way of getting water vapor. Maybe it is breaking down the distilled H2O into hydrogen and oxygen ie ”Browns Gas” but they don’t say what it is actually doing - which makes me very suspicious. If you live in a dry climate sit outside in the sun with a cup of hot water (tea) and breath the vapors.

# Thread: What is the benefit of eating this part of the fish?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-07-29T18:55:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337720>

I recently moved to El Salvador, and I have to say one of the lovely things is the affordability of having someone who cooks and helps with housework.

The beautiful lady that helps me, cooks a lovely fish. One time she cooked the ones with the eyeballs. That was really tough to see eyeballs rolling around on the plate. I did not eat the eyeballs. Should I have eaten the eyeballs?

So she’s gone back to buying the fillets from the fresh fish market. In these photos, it looks like maybe this is the spine? It’s kind of a darker color than the rest of the fish.

If you tell me, there’s tons of extra vitamins here. I’ll most likely eat it.

It’s just like the rest of the fish. I might let it go

### Saichi — 2024-07-29T19:28:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337721>

I eat fish heads often as my local fish mongers sell them for usually $3/lb at the farmers markets since most Americans are culturally averse to sophisticated flavors. Tilefish eyeballs are gooey and delicious, you just have to spit out the semi-hard 'shell' that's in it.

### rtd — 2024-07-29T20:04:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337726>

Fish eyes and stuff in the socket are salty and tasty. I spit out the lens

### NikkiYork — 2024-07-30T06:33:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337767>

> [thejessallen said:](/goto/post?id=337720)
>
> I recently moved to El Salvador, and I have to say one of the lovely things is the affordability of having someone who cooks and helps with housework.
>
> The beautiful lady that helps me, cooks a lovely fish. One time she cooked the ones with the eyeballs. That was really tough to see eyeballs rolling around on the plate. I did not eat the eyeballs. Should I have eaten the eyeballs?
>
> So she’s gone back to buying the fillets from the fresh fish market. In these photos, it looks like maybe this is the spine? It’s kind of a darker color than the rest of the fish.
>
> If you tell me, there’s tons of extra vitamins here. I’ll most likely eat it.
>
> It’s just like the rest of the fish. I might let it go

That’s just regular fish meat and it often turns brown around the spine. I would just eat whatever you like eating. It can taste less good. I cut it out of salmon.

I don’t eat fish brain and that stuff, but some do.

The cheeks of a fish are the best part (when it’s a big enough fish that has cheeks, like a trout or bigger). It’s under the cheek scale, the greatest thing to find for a kid, hahahaha. It’s true. It’s a very tender little piece of fish.

Europeans cook and eat a fish whole and it’s really only what you are used to. It would be awful for me, if somebody poked out the eyes before cooking. Like, what kind of monster did that?

If the fish is fried dead, which is sometimes done by the Salvadorian street vendors - probably a good idea, if it wasn’t a great fish - you can eat the whole thing. And it tastes all the same. I doubt there are much nutrients left.

Pretty much everything in an animal has vitamins and paleo technically we adapted to eating every bit that is somehow eatable. Our ancestors ate all of that… and made a soup from the rest.

Eat the eyeballs, Lady! Hell no, hahahaha.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-30T17:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337782>

Catch 22 . . . You have to eat the eyes to see the advantage.

### thejessallen — 2024-07-31T04:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337832>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=337782)
>
> Catch 22 . . . You have to eat the eyes to see the advantage.

that's funny... they were rolling on the plate... doing all sorts of new things these day - I'll seeee

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-31T09:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337850>

The eyeballs are the highest source of DHA and other nutrients. Its part of the reason sheep's eyes are such a delicacy in the arabian peninsular. Theyre the best source of nutrients. Its literally brain food. Because eyes are brain parts.

2vitamin C content, fish eyes are also **a great source of omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin A, and protein**. "

### thejessallen — 2024-07-31T17:50:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337878>

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-31T18:25:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337880>

>

I would boil the prawn shells and eyes into a stock and then use that for risotto to get as much out of the shells and eyes
If you're anywhere near la Libertad then a trip to the fish market is a must.

### thejessallen — 2024-08-03T00:32:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-338010>

Oh wow - ok - great insights. I am blessed to have a lady who helps with cooking - I live in La Libertad. She picks up fresh fish from the La Libertad market on her way in. I was having her cut off the heads of the shrimp - but now I see its good to keep them and boil them. wow great insights. muchas gracias and yes see you when you visit next time!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-31T19:33:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-red-skin-bad.29226/post-337884>

> [jeremiasbtome said:](/goto/post?id=337882)
>
> i use laptop 5-10 hours a day, no wifi though

If my iPad is plugged in charging with an ungrounded power cord my body voltage (BV) goes up to 40,000mV when I touch it.

IF your laptop (tablet) is plugged in to an ungrounded power cord while you use it you are being zapped with a 60Hz (50Hz) AC field the whole time. Either use the laptop on battery or use a grounded power cord. Measure your BV to know what you are getting.

All nnEMF has a cumulative affect and for most people, along with blue light, the 60Hz (50Hz) AC field in their home (& office) is the largest source of daily nnEMF - and the easiest to mitigate.

> Im almost certain being red is good after sun exposure, but just to confirm is this true? i posted it to my twitter lol and soo many people got concerned, left is 2022 before i knew about jack, right is now
>
> [View attachment 28724](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/28724/)[View attachment 28725](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/28725/)[View attachment 28726](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/28726/)

Hello. I watch Dr. Kruse podcast interviews daily, and I distinctly recall him saying that skin peeling from sun exposure was actually a good thing, as it’s a way of the body eliminating deuterium. Deuterium is good for babies, not good for fully developed adults. Your redness may not have resulted in your skin peeling. In any event, you can research ways of working into building your “solar callus.”

# Thread: Warning - Hidden sources of RF you need to measure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 54
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mat.I — 2024-07-31T06:51:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-337840>

When measuring / checking your environments it is important to leave no stone unturned as every day items could be a source of high RF.

Example, I recently measured a place and checked some smart TV/google remotes and found these remotes are all Bluetooth and continually pulse (even when not in use). The remote was pulsing 100 to 200 mW/m2 not connected (with receiver and TV off). With TV and receiver on 100 to 300 mW/m2 with button presses ~550 mW/m2. This was tested and confirmed on 3 x google chromecast setups. **Solution -** make sure you only use IR remotes for TV's or smart boxes and connect via LAN. It is important to check to make sure certain functions can be disabled on your smart TV (and confirm with testing) e.g. wifi scanning and Bluetooth. In the event you can do this and your smart TV has both Bluetooth and IR remote (on some you may be able to only use the IR function and not activate the Bluetooth smart functions etc). If you cannot, however you can disable BT, then you can get an aftermarket universal IR remote OR get a basic tv and a set top box (connected via HDMI) that does not use BT and has an IR remote. Example in AUS the basic / older Gen Fetch TV smart boxes can be setup with LAN, WIFI disabled, No BT & IR remote only.

This also applies to any smart devices that use WIFI & BT (too many to name). Nothing "smart" is actually smart for your health... Also just because you can buy LAN adaptors for smart devices does not mean that they are not constantly pulsing RF (this is why it is important to measure) as some functions can be bypassed, however not disabled..

Also, just because something says it is disabled does not mean it actually is (hence the importance of measuring this). E.g. when disabling WIFI on some routers I have tested via the on/off toggle switch and/or via advanced setting schedules it is still transmitting RF (just not your specific networks)!! Make sure you check this and turn off at the power adaptor or via a mechanical/digital GPO timer switch that kills the power. Solution is obviously hardwired, however, to avoid disappointment make sure you test prior to purchasing or just purchase a modem without in built WIFI router (if you need more ports use a hub also). If moving to hardwired LAN make sure you use decent shielded cables and earth as required (based on the cable run and hub used).

Hope this helps.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-03T22:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346163>

Just a follow up to this - any smart devices (such as washers, dryers, fridges etc) can be a pain to completly disable RF. I recently did a smart LG washer/dryer and had to dismantle it to disable the wifi module (unplug it). Note depending on how the PCB's are built some will not work if you unplug, therefore internal shielding (Faraday) is the only option if you wish to keep the device. This will typically involve detaching the module and shielding then refitting. Fortunately, the LG still operates with the module disconnected..

### Chlo_e — 2025-02-04T11:00:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346172>

Thanks for highlighting this. We have a smart picture frame TV that I refused to use once I realised what it was doing and measured the RF. There was no way to disable the bluetooth (the WIFI could be disabled but not the bluetooth) so we looked up how to remove the module, opened up the back of the TV and now the problem is solved  we use it with a shielded ethernet connection and IR remotes as you've outlined above (it's unplugged completely when not in use).

We found instructions to disable bluetooth on a bosche washing machine in the manual - and the same with an older cannon printer. Pain in the backside though..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-04T23:57:07+0200

We were able to disable our HP via software settings and have confirmed it with a meter. We do not have any wifi in our house and we are fully hardwired everywhere. Phones are used with ethernet adaptors (the non-charging type). Only issue is normal calls or text (outside whatsapp or similar services) however for the ppl that matter to you, you can advise to contact you via whatsapp and/or use a VOIP dedicated phone handset without POE (works great). Then you can have a time where you can activate cell data to check your phone, then disable again..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-06T03:53:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346259>

We wouldn't be able to achieve this without heavy shielding at our previous home closer to the CBD approx. 20km (12 miles) with high pop density. We moved a few years back due to C19. We live over 130km (80 Miles) from the CBD in a more rural based area with large lots and low pop density. No 5G that serves our suburb. Nearest 3G/4G tower from memory is no less than 1 mile away. The process was very easy due to the house being built to our 2010 Building Code which mandates certain construction requirements for specific climate zones.

Our house is brick veneer - so brick external wall on slab on ground, internal wall is timber stud with plasterboard, cavity between internal / external wall is packed with insulation bats and foil faced wall wrap. Roof - high pitch timber frame, quite dense concrete roof tiles and backed by what we call sarking in AUS (foil face wrap) to address thermal / condensation / last line of defence for water ingress etc..

When all the internal sources were eliminated I could have stopped there, however, when you know you do better, so I mitigated further. Only had to shield 3 x windows with tightly woven (small aperture) insect screen mesh these come in stainless steel or aluminium. Simply fitted directly over the window opening only (not going any further past the opening) after pulling taught and anchoring to the grout between the bricks with concrete nails (clouts). You can't even really notice it...

Additionally, we are on a corner block with quite a bit of buffer zone between nearby lots. The construction definitely helped as power density was already quite low externally thus attenuation & reflectance factor of the existing construction was sufficient. I think If we had a older house without the sarking and wraps we would have had to potentially shield more areas.

Long term will be permanent whole house DC lighting, reconfigure power circuits with metal conduits (in certain areas for required power outlets) that is grounded, my internal office will likely run completely on DC (via a battery pack that I'm going to build).

### Mat.I — 2025-02-09T01:22:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346444>

Only caveat that I'll add is - make sure before doing a DIY EMF assessment you have a thorough understanding on how to measure and use the equipment correctly.. If you are asking about meters and equipment that tells me you are quite "green" to this area.. which is fine just do your research. I generally recommend that people get their area measured professionally to have a baseline to work from (if you are not confident using the equipment or have a good understanding of nn-emf). This is even more important if you are dealing with health conditions. There is alot more to it than what we are discussing here, especially in certain situations where you are only isolating circuits for bedtime (you could still be exposed to DE in the day) which is even more critical to identify and address. BV is good it will tell you push/pull forces based on the pressure differential to your earth/ground ref point, however does not tell you the frequency of the field etc.. It all depends what approach is taken to mitigate the issues. If you only use partial measures such as isolation at night but you are still exposed in the day to EF outside acceptable limits then you would want to make sure there are no artifacts in the wave (DE). This is where you could look into addressing the actual wiring route issues, looking into filters or alternative to limit exposure look at auto demand current switches (these shut off when circuit is not in use). I could go on for a while with other points and scenarios, I think you get the picture that it is a mine field, however, don't let that discourage you. Just do your research and understand what you are doing, what you can and can't measure with basic equipment and understand the risk of that given your individual n=1 situation.. To confirm the Cornet & the BV meter is a very good starting point and will get the basics done. If you mitigate to acceptable levels, you shouldn't have to worry too much about the issues I mentioned above unless there are case specific issues that need to be looked into further.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-09T01:52:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346446>

See my thread last yr re: Cornet Meter
Thread 'New 2024 Cornet EMF Meter' [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-2024-cornet-emf-meter.28911/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-2024-cornet-emf-meter.28911/)

After purchase and talking to many others in the BB industry and using the Cornet in areas measured under the same conditions with the Safe and sound & gigahertz solutions (highest end RF meters), NFA 1000 (for mag/EF) it will do the job of identifying sources, levels pre & post mitigation. It is a great allrounder convenient (pocket sized) meter that has great accuracy/sensitivity for the price you are paying. It also has lots of advanced functions that most do not even use, such as the data log and frequency waveform analyser. From memory this is up to 50hours of rf data and you can even log the EF and Mag data via usb cord.. I'm comfortable and confident with this meter as a decent all rounder with an added bonus of a light flicker meter that is also very accurate when compared to others..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-09T04:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346465>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=346417)
>
> That's because the meters can't occupy the same space and levels are usually very different just a couple inches apart when not in a well shielded space or outdoor space comparable to Mat's yard.

Spatial variations are quite possible within close proximity due to reflection, refraction, diffraction, constructive and destructive interference etc.. and so on. Or it could be what we refer to as calibration drift accross the devices.. If within tolerances no issue providing the accuracy needed for a particular project is achieved.. If outside the tolerance range then you would need to establish the cause e.g. environmental conditions or calibration drift.. Depending on what ISO standard the units are calibrated to this may be in the range of + or - 1 to 3 dB. From memory the s & s had a less than desirable range of + or - 6dB which isn't the best e.g pd could be 4 x lower or higher than measured.. if actual pd is 100uW/m2 the measurement deviation could display values from 25 uW/m2 to 400uW/m2 due to this tolerance... Hence why S & S isn't the end all be all.. I.will add more to this in the future. Cornet is advertised at + - 3 to 6dB (frequency depending) where as S & S is fixed at 6dB based on advertising. If I had to pick my ultimate professional meter for rf it would be the top end gigahertz solutions in the HF series.. great meters if you have the money to spend.

### Woodstock — 2025-02-24T04:59:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-347199>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=337840)
>
> When measuring / checking your environments it is important to leave no stone unturned as every day items could be a source of high RF.
>
> Example, I recently measured a place and checked some smart TV/google remotes and found these remotes are all Bluetooth and continually pulse (even when not in use). The remote was pulsing 100 to 200 mW/m2 not connected (with receiver and TV off). With TV and receiver on 100 to 300 mW/m2 with button presses ~550 mW/m2. This was tested and confirmed on 3 x google chromecast setups. **Solution -** make sure you only use IR remotes for TV's or smart boxes and connect via LAN. It is important to check to make sure certain functions can be disabled on your smart TV (and confirm with testing) e.g. wifi scanning and Bluetooth. In the event you can do this and your smart TV has both Bluetooth and IR remote (on some you may be able to only use the IR function and not activate the Bluetooth smart functions etc). If you cannot, however you can disable BT, then you can get an aftermarket universal IR remote OR get a basic tv and a set top box (connected via HDMI) that does not use BT and has an IR remote. Example in AUS the basic / older Gen Fetch TV smart boxes can be setup with LAN, WIFI disabled, No BT & IR remote only.
>
> This also applies to any smart devices that use WIFI & BT (too many to name). Nothing "smart" is actually smart for your health... Also just because you can buy LAN adaptors for smart devices does not mean that they are not constantly pulsing RF (this is why it is important to measure) as some functions can be bypassed, however not disabled..
>
> Also, just because something says it is disabled does not mean it actually is (hence the importance of measuring this). E.g. when disabling WIFI on some routers I have tested via the on/off toggle switch and/or via advanced setting schedules it is still transmitting RF (just not your specific networks)!! Make sure you check this and turn off at the power adaptor or via a mechanical/digital GPO timer switch that kills the power. Solution is obviously hardwired, however, to avoid disappointment make sure you test prior to purchasing or just purchase a modem without in built WIFI router (if you need more ports use a hub also). If moving to hardwired LAN make sure you use decent shielded cables and earth as required (based on the cable run and hub used).
>
> Hope this helps.

Yes I think my TV remote does this pulsing. I am using a shielding pouch you like you can put your cell phone in to block RF. But of course it will pulse on you every time you use it to change the channel, etc. I missed this at first because of the pulsing so you have to test with a good meter and be patient. Mine will pulse every time you touch it!

Then you can have a time where you can activate cell data to check your phone, then disable again..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-24T23:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-347247>

> [giedremail said:](/goto/post?id=347227)
>
> Hi Mat, we have moved the country and are renting now. There is a WiFi router in the house. I am not very good with technology myself. I have asked around but no one could recommend me a person who could come round and set up the internet without WiFi. What can I do, anyone/anywhere you could recommend? Thank you

If I were renting and wanted to minimise issues with the commercial rental agreement and wanted something that could be easily removed with minimal damage (in summary DIY version on the assumption there are no hardwired points) -
1\. disconnect any router with WIFI.
2\. Purchase a modem such as a Draytek Vigor 160 (without any wifi) with bridge or router mode.. The actual product will vary based on your incoming ISP line configuration e.g. ADSL, fibre, coax etc.. This product is just an example and if you had ADSL you could use something like this.
3\. Figure out your location and how many LAN ports you require (for your devices).
4\. Once you know how many purchase a basic non POE unmanaged switch with adequate ports to support your use.
5\. For cable routing - you can use mini duct (square) conduit and run around architraves, cornices, skirtings (with minimal holes to pass through to rooms). Or you can utilise the floor or roof space (subject to having one) for cable routing.
6\. In summary - incoming ISP connection if ADSL will go to modem (configured in router mode), then ethernet cable to the desktop based (basic) network switch and all your out going connections (to rooms/areas) will connect individually to the network switch / hub..

### Mat.I — 2025-06-26T06:24:21+0300

There are many possibilities on why this may be occurring. Did your friend check it with another meter to rule out any equipment issues or with a spectrum analyser. Was this measured with power on vs off? Any smart devices within the houses?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-26T06:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355295>

I have measured RF from florescent bulbs including mini CFL bulbs. Unfortunately there are still a lot of these out there.
Getting RF from a “wall”?
RF can travel a significant distance. What is on the other side of the wall?
Modern WiFi routers can reach 200ft - 300ft.
Measure outside and try to see where the RF is coming from.

I have also seen people online (videos) talking about high RF readings from something and their meter was set on Electric fields . . .

### michellejorge — 2025-06-26T15:24:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355312>

Does anyone have any contacts in south Florida to remove the smart aspect of smart appliances? I bought a remodeled house and currently disconnected both my stove and microwave (which I don’t need) because they were giving me high ratings while connected but not in use. Otherwise, I’m going to need to buy a new stove possibly used or older bc many are smart. There’s only so much I can make in the toaster oven!

### TB14 — 2025-06-28T08:06:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355399>

> [michellejorge said:](/goto/post?id=355312)
>
> Does anyone have any contacts in south Florida to remove the smart aspect of smart appliances? I bought a remodeled house and currently disconnected both my stove and microwave (which I don’t need) because they were giving me high ratings while connected but not in use. Otherwise, I’m going to need to buy a new stove possibly used or older bc many are smart. There’s only so much I can make in the toaster oven!

I managed to get the RF chip out of the smart stove that came with my place. Was easier than I thought. The piece to remove is generally a little printed circuit board ("PCB") which is attached to a wire with a connector on the end that you just need to unplug. I think with stoves most of them are located somewhere behind the display where the digital clock is (at least that's where mine was). But before just taking stuff apart and looking for it (it can be hidden/tricky to find), I recommend first trying to find the service manual (different from the user manual) online and that should tell you where the part you need to find and remove is. They might call it a Wi-Fi/RF chip or Wi-Fi/RF PCB or something similar, but that's the part you need to remove. That said, there's no guarantee that the Wi-Fi components aren't welded directly onto the main printed circuit board (i.e., not just an easily unpluggable separate piece), and if that's the case it may be impossible to remove them without damaging the appliance (or at least more difficult and beyond the range of my current knowledge). Make sure you unplug the stove before you start taking it apart too of course.

### Saichi — 2025-06-28T17:04:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355409>

All the nice cold plunges being sold on the market today come with wifi app enabled controls for their chillers. I still bought one arriving this week and I'm hoping it won't emit if not used but otherwise I'll have to attempt same.

One company I contacted knew the wifi was unhealthy and wasn't sure if I'd be able to disable it.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-29T01:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355448>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=355409)
>
> All the nice cold plunges being sold on the market today come with wifi app enabled controls for their chillers. I still bought one arriving this week and I'm hoping it won't emit if not used but otherwise I'll have to attempt same.
>
> One company I contacted knew the wifi was unhealthy and wasn't sure if I'd be able to disable it.

Yes, this can be a pain.. I just ordered a low emf FIR sauna for my wife. They address the MAG/EF in the heaters / wiring design but neglect the RF component. I requested a wiring diagram, which they never provided, so I opted for the more basic model (slightly lower powered) without BT, speakers and wifi etc. As I did not want it to be the case of the module being incorporated within the MCB or even separate daughter board but relied upon for handshakes and the boot up. The smart devices are a pain. Fortunately, all of the ones I have chosen based on prior research have been able to be disconnected, however, I have encountered some that will have a separate module/board and when disconnected the device defaults to error mode as it is reliant on that module for handshakes at boot up (like a system check).. This leaves 2 options - go to an electronics tech to do board level mods or just shelid the module (however, if incorporated within the MCB this becomes a pain and needs some creativeness).

Soon we will likley not be able to disable anything without board level mods.. Tying into the 2030 agenda to have us connected to the cloud..

### Saichi — 2025-06-29T01:55:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355450>

I was able to disconnect the wifi in a 10 year old air conditioner few years back but I'm not holding much hope out for the new chiller. I'm going to faraday the greenhouse I'm going to put the plunge inside so having two layers of shielding between the chiller and plunge might suffice.

### TB14 — 2025-06-30T04:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355512>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=355448)
>
> Soon we will likley not be able to disable anything without board level mods..

We seem to be almost there already with TVs, at least in the U.S. market. And it's also the fact that you can't really get functional stand-alone non-Smart TVs either. They still make non-Wi-Fi TVs but those also lack Ethernet, so you have to get a separate streaming device if you want to stream on them. There don't seem to be any more Ethernet-only TVs (like the ones I remember playing Xbox 360 online with my friends on as a teenager less than 10 years ago) being made. None of the recent SmartTVs that I could find have the ability to turn Wi-Fi totally off at the settings level, either. And a few Sonys were the only models I was able to find where people online confirmed that you can remove the Wi-Fi PCB without rendering the TV inoperable.

I almost wonder why they even left the Ethernet port in there for us. Same goes for why Tim Cook has kept the MacBooks and iPhones fairly simply Ethernet-compatible (though removing the port). It sometimes feels like they leave barely enough room where avoiding/addressing these bioweapons is practicable if you have the knowledge to know what to look for and how to fix it.

### rtd — 2025-06-30T15:52:03+0300

Retro engineering appliances was an idea I had around 2011. My place at the time (a rental) had old appliances. Fridge was a Hot Point, no defrost and a small freezer cubby labeled "Ice Box". It was a tank in mass. The washer and dryer were Westinghouse made. All were mechanical, err- no digital electronics- hefty and fixable if need be.

Modern regulations prohibit manufacturing products like that.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progressive-uv-to-progressive-non-uv-glasses.29234/post-337894>

Delivering white paper envelopes and magazines in the reflective glare of the sun. So, as a letter carrier out in the day using prescription progressive lens's glasses with UV reactive tint, is there a problem with changing to non-UV tint as far as the magnification of +3 lenses and burning your retina in direct reflected sun off non gloss and gloss-less white paper. The paper does get very bright even with my UV progressive glasses.

Joe

### Saichi — 2025-06-04T17:59:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-353880>

Unless you live out in the remote country you'll have to shield every surface not just walls but ceiling, windows, floor (if any transmitters located below from within, neighbors or not on ground floor).

### Saichi — 2025-06-04T18:20:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-353882>

Copper, aluminum, stainless steel, lead are all good for shielding.

SLT foil is the best and cheapest, you can easily cover over with regular wallpaper afterwards if you don't want to live in a spaceship.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-04T19:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-353890>

> [jackfoster said:](/goto/post?id=353855)
>
> I'm going back home from El Salvador soon for a few months and will be in a home with wifi so I need to cover the walls of my room. There's a certain type of foil that needs to be used?

Or you could just shield the WiFi.
Put a shield next to the WiFi so your room is protected from the signal. $20 vs $$$$
[Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/emf-101](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/emf-101) for some router shielding ideas. What is the RF reading there?

### jackfoster — 2025-06-04T20:02:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-353892>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=353890)
>
> Or you could just shield the WiFi.
> Put a shield next to the WiFi so your room is protected from the signal. $20 vs $$$$
> [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/emf-101](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/emf-101) for some router shielding ideas. What is the RF reading there?

It's a tricky situation and it is hard to explain but this house is fucked and the wifi can't ever be turned off and it also can't be shielded because the signal strength can't be lessened in order to do what it needs to. I'll only be there about two months and I'll be outside or in my bedroom at night which is fortunately the furthest point from the router.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-04T20:09:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-353893>

Get a canopy. Simple and you can take it with you.

### jackfoster — 2025-06-04T20:17:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-353894>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=353893)
>
> Get a canopy. Simple and you can take it with you.

Canopy made things worse when I had one. When I wrapped the fabric tightly around my meter the readings went to zero but when I hung it up it did the opposite.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-04T20:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-353898>

> [jackfoster said:](/goto/post?id=353894)
>
> Canopy made things worse when I had one. When I wrapped the fabric tightly around my meter the readings went to zero but when I hung it up it did the opposite.

So the canopy material worked. Did you have a 100% enclosure - no openings? Are you measuring just RF or electric field as well?
For a Faraday room to work it must also be 100% - walls, floor, ceiling, windows, doors . . . And NO electronics inside the room.

### Saichi — 2025-06-05T13:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-353999>

> [jackfoster said:](/goto/post?id=353894)
>

### Saichi — 2025-06-06T18:54:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354114>

Been moving out of my Brooklyn apt past couple weeks since my mother's health has deteriorated in California. Here some pics of my emptied setup with a custom canopy bed, Naturell canopy and copper wallpaper. I never got around to painting over the emf paint but learned hard way people don't understand "don't touch the black paint."

Achieved better than Building Biology standards with this, helped I was in the middle of a pre-war building.

### Saichi — 2025-06-06T19:33:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354118>

Three layers of silver curtains with magnetic tape around edges. Ceiling had unrenovated 'popcorn' type ceiling under wallpaper.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-06T20:46:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354125>

I’m glad this worked for you. I have heard of many people that this kind of set up made them worse.
A Faraday room is for RF reduction. It is a metallic “box”. It is important to have NO electronics on the inside.
I guess you have it grounded to the building ground, so you also have direct contact with all those wonderful ground effects in NY.
Have you measured the ground effects? What would an oscilloscope show?
What are the electric field readings in there especially with that red light on?

### Saichi — 2025-06-06T23:25:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354136>

The e and m fields were within BB safe range for my bed, the sofa that used to be below it, my desk chair and middle of the room basically any location I spent most of my time in. Half the room is solid concrete walls with no electrical wiring. Gembared light panel with lowest fields of any on market but regular electronics everyone here should know minimum distance. I still have nine Satic shield DE filters plugged in my room, six daisy chained three plus three on an extension in order to get down to 10mV.

Military installations you mentioned in the past, which always are gov experiments, shouldn't be the model to deter people from achieving what I have.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-07T01:22:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354148>

Make sure you wear a decent mask when working on the ceiling and minimise any disruption to it as it will more than likley have asbestos fibres..

### Saichi — 2025-06-07T02:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354150>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=354148)
>
> Make sure you wear a decent mask when working on the ceiling and minimise any disruption to it as it will more than likley have asbestos fibres..

Wasn't traditional popcorn but sparse mini-stalactite formation so didn't dust during application but I do have traditional popcorn in California. Canopies including a massive custom one used to be sufficient before 2023 Fema "test" but wallpaper applied surprisingly easily on the popcorn afterwards.

I'm sure my mother and I have some asbestos in our lungs after 16 years in the house but sadly that's still the least of our concerns. Temps are mild year round so we keep windows open every day.

### Saichi — 2025-06-07T02:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354151>

This is my friend's apt in Brooklyn who did this herself, notice emf tent inside canopy. Better than nothing but she had to abandon the apt and become an emf refugee in Greenbank since last year.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-07T03:01:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354153>

N O O O . . . anything but that.

### Saichi — 2025-06-07T03:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354158>

This is still a mud pit:

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-07T05:41:50+0300

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=354158)

>

Could well be - BUT . . .
Was he filming on his phone?
What were the readings outside your Faraday room in NY for a comparison ?

Less nnEMF is always better than more.

### Saichi — 2025-06-07T14:17:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354181>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=354165)
>
> Could well be - BUT . . .
> Was he filming on his phone?
> What were the readings outside your Faraday room in NY for a comparison ?
> Just asking.
>
> Less nnEMF is always better than more.

I thought you would know if he were recording on a transmitting phone the readings would be 100k to millions of µw.

The unshielded foyer space right outside my Brooklyn room were the same as the mud pit in Neil's recording of 'nature.' Decades of lead paint helped with that, ironically the city tried to "remediate for free" the lead paint in the unrenovated apts in my building but I refused. Outside my window ranged anywhere from 200 to 5k.

I was a member of World Spa which had a level of 2 in the 200 F deg main banya. The cold plunge area was around 300 which is why I wore an old non-nano emf beanie and emf safari hat while water's a natural faraday. I had a couple sunbathing spots in Prospect Park with peaks no greater than 10. I spent 80% of my living and sleeping time in safe spaces in NYC which I'm confident is better than almost everyone living out in "nature" even though the other 20% were in higher levels. But nnemf is non-linear in effect which is why I recommend people to always use the audio function on their Safe and Sound as you'll realize 30 is closer to 300 than 3.

I used to walk my dog in California in an idyllic marsh that used to have levels of 30 to 50 before the Fema test which I wasn't thrilled about and stopped after the levels went up 6 to 10 fold. "Optimal health" in my experience means maximizing time spent in safe spaces and we should all strive for Mat's environment but not let what's immediately achievable be the enemy of 'perfect' as there is no perfect left on this planet.

### Saichi — 2025-06-07T14:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faradaying-a-safe-space.29239/post-354182>

Yesterday was my first cold plunge in almost two months, not even a cold shower during that time. Water was 50.7 deg which is mild for me yet the first two minutes were objectionably painful to the point I almost gave up before I realized I need to experience how hard being non-adapted was. Within three minutes all the pain left and I was again comfortable, relaxed, and relieved for the rest of my 30 minute plunge.

Here's a pic I took inside the banya last year with my 11 year old smartphone.
