# Thread: Haplo types
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplo-types.29245/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Marianne Walton — 2024-08-02T01:38:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplo-types.29245/post-337951>

Does anyone share with me, Haplo type R?

If so, do you know what latitude would be ideal to live at?

I am primarily of Northern European descent and my family settled early in the USA. India and Sri Lankan descendants entered my bloodline in the 1500’s, and I am guessing that may be what the haplotype is related to, but I know little about it honestly.

Haplogroup R represents a common ancestor that ties you directly to ancestral Mitochondrial Eve (MT).

Thanks to anyone who might have information on this.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-08-02T02:20:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplo-types.29245/post-337952>

Here's some info

### [Haplogroup R (mtDNA) - Wikipedia](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_R_\(mtDNA\))

 en.m.wikipedia.org

### [Mitochondrial DNA Haplogroup R Confers a Genetic Risk Factor for Intrauterine Adhesion in Han Women Population - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26465877/)

These results suggest that mtDNA haplogroup R, one of the main mtDNA haplogroups in Han population, is a strong independent genetic risk factor for women with IUA.

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

I would honestly do a Google search. That's what I did and it turned up this

### Marianne Walton — 2024-08-02T03:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplo-types.29245/post-337957>

Thanks Dr Rohen. Do you share this haplotype?

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-08-02T03:32:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haplo-types.29245/post-337958>

No. I'm an uncoupled U4b1a1 found in Iceland, Norway Latvia and Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan and bits of Northwestern India around Harappa

I produce heat. In my obstetrics section in Final year I always had very warm hands for the pregnant ladies

I was even invited to become an Obstetric and Gynae SHO in Dundee but I didn't want to stay at that latitude I'd had 6 years there and I wanted out.

# Thread: Glioblastoma diagnosis for my brother seeking help and hope
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Punzilla — 2024-08-04T15:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338063>

Hi all
My brother has recently had a glioma tumour surgically removed from his brain. The procedure seemed to have gone well but the professor who performed the surgery has warned us that there is no cure and it doesn't seem to be curable or treatable with a keto diet as per the recommendation of Dr Thomas Seyfried.
He has started radio therapy and chemo last week and I don't want to accept the defeat to cancer without a fight.

A friend of ours has been assisting my brother and has been recommending him to get daily light exposure, he has given him a pair of amber blue blocking glasses and my brother is in the process of ordering the red coloured ones. He's also organised supplements and a diet for my brother to follow low in meat to assist with the cancer fight.

What would be the ideal way to keep my brother alive?

He has always been fit and healthy I'm not sure whether the nature of his shift work has gotten in the way and caused this cancer.

Any beginners advice of where to start. The stakes are quite high so want to explore as many options as I can.

Thank you.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-08-04T15:44:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338065>

I would avoid radio and chemo if at all possible

Maximize sunlight exposure (especially light from dawn to ~9AM)

The shift work has definitely gotten in the way. If your brother values his life, he will drop that permanently.

### John Warner — 2024-08-04T22:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338072>

>
> He has always been fit and healthy I'm not sure whether the nature of his shift work has gotten in the way and caused this cancer.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-06T03:28:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338109>

[@John Warner](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25255/) - Check out Gabor Somlyai (PhD) book -

### Deuterium Depletion: A New Way in Curing Cancer and Preserving Health​

Also, consult with Jack.
All the best.
Mat

### Mat.I — 2024-08-06T06:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338112>

Also, you could check out Dr Zsofia Clemens - who deals with GBM's quite a lot through nutritional interventions. Note she uses a very strict Paleolithic Ketogenic Diet (much higher fat than your traditional keto). Not sure of Jack's opinion on her work. She operates out of paleomedicina in Hungary.

[https://www.researchgate.net/publication/345606592\_4-Year\_Long\_Progression-Free\_and\_Symptom-Free\_Survival\_of\_a\_Patient\_with\_Recurrent\_Glioblastoma\_Multiforme\_A\_Case\_Report\_of\_the\_Paleolithic\_Ketogenic\_Diet\_PKD\_Used\_as\_a\_Stand-Alone\_Treatment\_After\_Fai](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/345606592_4-Year_Long_Progression-Free_and_Symptom-Free_Survival_of_a_Patient_with_Recurrent_Glioblastoma_Multiforme_A_Case_Report_of_the_Paleolithic_Ketogenic_Diet_PKD_Used_as_a_Stand-Alone_Treatment_After_Fai)

### [Treatment of high-grade brain tumor using the paleolithic ketogenic diet (PKD): Three cases](https://www.paleomedicina.com/en/glioblastoma_paleolithic_ketogenic_diet)

Authors: Zsófia Clemens, Andrea Dabóczi, Mária Schimmer, Péter Barsi, Csaba Tóth DOI 10.28973/lindeberg.pm/po1Conference: The Staffan Lindeberg Memorial Conference, Lisbon 2017 ABSTRACT Introduction:

www.paleomedicina.com

**Obviously, she is missing the light story, however, do your research and see what works. Just getting some options out here for you to look at.**

Also, I have come across some information in the past that I assume Dr Seyfried would also be across that relates to Glutamine blocking with 6-Diazo-5-oxo-L-norleucine (DON) is a glutamine antagonist. Again not sure on Jack's view on this and I haven't looked at it in great detail either, however somewhere to also look further. I assume it would be experimental "research" only however, if deemed appropriate you could get your hands on it (obviously under the guidance of someone who knows what they are doing).

There are also quite a few studies on specific IR wavelengths that have been shown to help.

I can't recall which podcast, however, Jack talks about GBM's in one of them.

As time is very sensitive here - best to consult with [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) as soon as you can.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-08-07T08:51:08+0300

### Punzilla — 2024-08-12T13:56:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338301>

Thanks everyone. I'll need to show these messages to my sister-in-law and discuss. There's quite a lot of information out there. We're heading into the warmer months in Melbourne and my brother is spending a lot of time outdoors. I'm not fully immersed in dr Kruses work at the moment but after watching a recent interview of Dr Gabrielle Lyon and Dr Alexis Cowan I've downloaded the circadian app and am trying in the meantime to get my own circadian cycles in order too. I've previously downloaded the d-minder app on the mobile. I know in relation to getting vitamin d I'd virtually need to expose 90 percent of my skin by the solar noon to get any decent level of vitamin d amount.

I'm the meantime he is trying a (mostly) vegetarian diet whereby the first 2 meals can be cooked and the final meal of the night is raw so the body doesn't need to spend time digesting and can focus on its repair. (He is allowed to eat fish 3 days a week).

He is doing radio and chemo I think Dr Zsofia Clemens recommends not doing that which I get anxious with as it's the standard of care and I don't feel qualified to recommend against it. I'm hoping whatever else we can incorporate can include a mixture of western and natural medicine.

Thank you all for your replies appreciate it.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-12T14:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338302>

You are going to be really struggling in Melbourne. As you're not across all of Jack's work. Organise a consult asap that way you can also get a plan in place to hopefully get to a better location at a lower latitude (closer to the equator). The target would be El Salvador. Do your research on the vegetarian diet and the drawbacks this may have and understand what the centralised therapies are doing vs what may be needed at this time.

All the best.
Mat.

### jonjacobsen — 2025-01-27T04:02:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-345810>

My 10 year old daughter has a glial tumor that causes seizures, currently taking oxcarbazepine. The tumor won't be surgically removed as it's scattered throughout her right temporal lobe. She's a typical kid and loves the sweets, though my wife and I are pretty good about eating healthy she still gets things from school etc. The Cleveland doctors recommend this wait and see approach. Wish your brother the best - kick the cancer - good luck! Thanks to all on the thread for posting, I'll be checking them out.

# Thread: Where to start with fatigue?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### adam915 — 2024-08-07T15:52:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338151>

I am a complete beginner here. I have been binging anything on YouTube with Jack's name in the title lately. Looking for some specific advice on what to do to combat feeling tired all the time. I am a 38 year old male, no previous health issues before taking the covid jab. I went through a couple years of issues ranging from severe chest pain to neuropathy to anxiety. I powered through everything but am currently taking an SSRI for the anxiety as the panic attacks can be debilitating. I get a full 8 hours of sleep, and sometimes more. I do take a supplement with anti inflammatory agents like bromelain and nattokinase which is advertised to help with issues aggravated by spike protein. I try to eat clean within reason. I just feel wrecked all day every day. Could take a 2 hour nap every day if I was afforded the luxury. Any help on where to start?

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-07T16:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338152>

could have to do with adrenal fatigue perhaps. Can you handle coffee? Do you have reactive hypoglycemia? Do you sometimes feel lightheaded when standing up? Do you have brain fog? Do you feel you need to eat something every 2-3 hours? You can look at Brain Gut 16: Adrenal Fatigue

Unfortunately fatigue can be due to many reasons so I can also be something else than Adrenal fatigue. I am myself am dealing with chronic fatigue for 8 years. Can't work or exercise etc.

### adam915 — 2024-08-07T16:29:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338153>

I don't drink coffee, but can handle caffeine just fine. No lightheadedness while standing. Yes to the brain fog. Sometimes can't come up with the correct word I'm looking for in conversation. I eat three meals a day and rarely anything in between. I do annual bloodwork and as of June everything was in the target range. Blood sugar is toward the higher end of the scale.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-08-07T18:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338159>

Which SSRI? Prozac has fluoride in it as does citalopram. How much sunshine do you get ?

### 5G Canary — 2024-08-07T19:59:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338160>

I concur with 5G Canary above. I was the same state you describe 5 years ago, after 24 years of shift work the last 6 on graveyard, too much stress, a toxic relationship I refused to see for what it was, depression, etc. Today I am in a much better spot but still not optimal. What I changed was spending regular time in nature, getting a lot more sun, blocking blue light at night and during the day at work in front of screens, changing my diet to epi-paleo, getting regular cold exposure and red light panels. I've easily spent a couple thousand hours listening to Dr. Kruse podcasts, his private member videos, reading his blogs, and in general challenging a lot of beliefs I used to have in those 5 years.

### Rusty — 2024-08-07T22:20:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-find-what-cell-towers-you-have-near-by.29268/post-338170>

Cell tower mapping websites for India

### [Cellular Tower and Signal Map](https://www.cellmapper.net/map)

CellMapper is a crowd-sourced cellular tower and coverage mapping service.

 www.cellmapper.net

### [Home Page | Tarang Sanchar](https://tarangsanchar.gov.in/)

 tarangsanchar.gov.in

### [3G / 4G / 5G coverage in India - nPerf.com](https://www.nperf.com/en/map/IN/-/-/signal)

Compare the network coverage of mobile operators and check their performance at home !

 www.nperf.com

(i use this one)

### [India's leading provider of Prepaid & Postpaid Mobile Services in India - Airtel.in](https://www.airtel.in/opennetwork/)

Airtel is India's leading provider of prepaid & postpaid mobile, Airtel payments bank, broadband, 4G & DTH services in India. Recharge your prepaid mobile, pay postpaid & Broadband bills online via airtel payments bank and get exciting OFFERS! Know more about India's first open network.

 www.airtel.in

### Cameron — 2024-08-08T15:16:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/polyphenols-etc.29272/post-338182>

Can someone explain how polyphenols and other secondary compounds (metabolites) in food help our mitochondria. I would like to mentally tie the ingestion of foods rich in these compounds with the Kruse insights regarding ATP production, circadian health etc. I assume that lowering inflammation, of course, improves mitochondrial efficiency but is there more subtle signaling and other effects from eating real, local healthy foods when it comes to these secondary compounds? I am fascinated by the work of Steven Van Vliet and Fred Provenza and just trying to tie it together. [https://stephanvanvliet.com/](https://stephanvanvliet.com/). He is going way beyond the regular studies and tying in soil health and environment to the food quality in objective ways. For example, regenerative raised Bison versus feed lot finished bison (yes, most bison are the latter).

### Cameron — 2024-08-08T17:23:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/polyphenols-etc.29272/post-338189>

Today Jack reposted this re Tryptophan seems to indicate that foods rich in metabolites and secondary compounds may simply improve biological signaling and therefore reduce chronic disease....? "In my Vermont 2018 talk I warned the audience that light and aromatic amino acids are the key to understanding light. Why do I believe this? Typtophan has only one codon and acts like a time crystal in life and we can see that effect in its biochemistry. The photonics of typtophan pushes my belief paradigm even further down this rabbit hole because it is the only amino acid with an indole moiety, which makes it an ideal precursor for metabolites involved in biological signaling."

### Brent Patrick — 2024-08-09T00:41:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/polyphenols-etc.29272/post-338214>

> [Cameron said:](/goto/post?id=338182)
>
> Can someone explain how polyphenols and other secondary compounds (metabolites) in food help our mitochondria. I would like to mentally tie the ingestion of foods rich in these compounds with the Kruse insights regarding ATP production, circadian health etc. I assume that lowering inflammation, of course, improves mitochondrial efficiency but is there more subtle signaling and other effects from eating real, local healthy foods when it comes to these secondary compounds? I am fascinated by the work of Steven Van Vliet and Fred Provenza and just trying to tie it together. [https://stephanvanvliet.com/](https://stephanvanvliet.com/). He is going way beyond the regular studies and tying in soil health and environment to the food quality in objective ways. For example, regenerative raised Bison versus feed lot finished bison (yes, most bison are the latter).

The most impressive effects of polyphenols are via their epigenetic effects not as antioxidants!

"Epi-"means on or above in Greek, and "epigenetic" describes factors beyond the genetic code. Epigenetic changes are **modifications to DNA that regulate whether genes are turned on or off**. These modifications are attached to DNA and do not change the sequence of DNA building blocks.

[Checking your browser - reCAPTCHA](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9570183/)

### Cameron — 2024-08-09T22:09:53+0300

but i am still confused some. These articles are like a Rhonda Patrick thing where some substance inhibits cancer by interacting with our genome etc but that still seems bio-chemical in approach. Cant our bodies signal and turn on or off genes without these or convert/transmute other substances to do the same thing?. Overall, I cant get comfortable with, for example, a broccoli sprout shake being a great thing even if super rich in a “good” chemical compound (sulforafane). It just seems like that goes back to food guru world. Trying to reconcile…..

The way I reconcile this question is to think of polyphenols and alike as downstream packages born from light for us to consume. Just as Jack says food is lower on the list of priorities, important yes but not the most important.

Yes you are right there are thousands of these substances a whole other rabbit hole to dive down in relation to how they interact with Mitochrondria.

I do think that Rhondra Patrick falls into the catagory of wanting to isolate substances for their virtues but nature is way to smart and gives us what we need in the right proportion via food/fermentation and teas etc in its natural package based on the environment it is in.

Transmutation possibly ...Corentin Louis Kervran done such experiments with chickens,not sure how those experiments stand up today.

**"Cant our bodies signal and turn on or off genes without these"**

Sunlight does this via circadian cycle ,in fact if your Vitamin D levels are low, it will turn off the epigenetic switches on genes that turns on the immune system protector cells and keeps us free of diseases from viruses, bacteria and fungal infections.

**"Our genes don’t control us, but our thoughts do**. Thoughts are epigenetic signals too, but too few think that way. # Thread: HOLD YOUR BREATH! - A UNIQUE 3-day Retreat in El Salvador

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hold-your-breath-a-unique-3-day-retreat-in-el-salvador.29281/post-338247>

Hi everyone

You may have heard my name from my dear friend, "Uncle Jack," who kindly permitted me to post on this forum.
A few months ago, I was honored to be the first speaker at the first Age of Light conference in El Salvador, where I talked about my natural approach to exercise and lifestyle.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I held a two-day BreathHoldWork & Natural Movement workshop on the beach of El Zonte just before the conference, and many of you expressed interest in attending the same kind of event.

I've got great news!

I'm returning to El Salvador for a 3-day BreathHoldWork retreat from October 11 to 14!

[](https://www.breathholdwork.com/breathholdwork-elsalvador-retreat)
(CLICK ON THE IMAGE TO VISIT THE EVENT PAGE)

This masterclass in breathwork, breath-holding, meditation, and nervous system downregulation is set in the stunning San Luis La Herradura on the Costa Del Sol (“Sun Coast” ).

I hold the US National Record for Static Breath-holding with the CMAS organization in the Master category, with an official performance of 7 minutes and 29 seconds.
My personal record stands at 8 minutes and 3 seconds.

To hold your breath for such a long time, you need an impeccable lifestyle and specific physiological adaptations. Most importantly, you must master meditation and relaxation like you master high-level skills.
During this masterclass, I will teach you breath control, meditation, and relaxation in a unique and powerful way through various exercises and by sharing many profound insights.
**You’ll feel the most mentally clear, emotionally relaxed, and happiest you’ve been in a long time!

**

The venue is a private beachfront property with green grass and lush palm trees, providing a beautiful and intimate setting in a serene atmosphere ideal for your transformative experience.

If you live in El Salvador already or need another great reason to visit this amazing country, this is it!

I hope to see you there.

~ Erwan

Now I am consuming daily 2600mg EPA and 1800mg DHA, 600mg turmeric, 1g magnesium glycinate, 1 cup coconut oil and plenty of water in addition to my regular diet. ### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-17T00:55:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-dr-kruse-consult-soon-do-i-need-to-do-labs-before.29318/post-338522>

> [aruetenik said:](/goto/post?id=338520)
>
> Yes, all done with Trifield TF2 and Body voltage meter. (my husband was texting you yesterday
> 0 Body voltage in the bed. No magnetic either.
> Magnetico incoming (currently on grounding sheets, grounded outside)
> Have optional EMF canopy that we can use.
>
> RF inside bedrooms is under 0.050mW/m2
> Outside apartment its around 0.05-0.2mW/m2
>
> Should I get BUN/Creatine labs ?
>
> I havent had labs done in 5 years, and now im TAN

Zero body voltage on the bed is very suspicious and IF you are double grounded with grounding sheets is a false reading. The body voltage meter works by giving the electric field being induced into your body a path of least resistance to ground, through the meter. IF you have grounding sheets on the bed you are double grounded and the field is going to ground via the sheets NOT the meter. It is a false reading. UNPLUG the sheets and measure body voltage again. Let me know.

On the TriField RF 0.05 mW/m2 = 50 µW/m2 (units on the Safe & Sound Pro 2)
Also the TriField in not picking up all the RF that is there.
In my space I just measured with a TriField and the Safe & Sound Pro2, side by side. . . TriField reads 0.005mW/m2 (=5µW/m2) but the Safe & Sound measures 500 µW/m2 . . . Only 100x more!
Todays RF (ie 5G) frequencies require modern meters because of the way 5G technology transmits/handles the signal.

### aruetenik — 2024-08-17T20:19:43+0300

ahhh.. thank you Anthony!

we will redo the body voltage measurements then. Yes, we did it with the grounding sheets.

Should we take the grounding sheet OFF the bed, or simply unplug it?

Just got the magnetico, so will install that next week. Then the grounding sheet comes off anyways...

Yes, aware the trifield isnt picking up all there is... so yes it could be worse!

Thanks for the guidance!

> Doing consult soon for guidance relating issues remaining:
> chronic monthly migraines (worse around period/ovulation)
> gut/vaginal microbiom issues - after 2nd pregnancy - I was on a ton of antibiotics growing up...
> Psoriasis root causes
>
> Should I get labs for Kruse to review, or not? If so, what should I get/where?
>
> We live by the beach in Redondo Beach and live like outdoor cats BUT we can't control the 5G nnEMF.
> Are there labs someone can recommend to get a sense on how bad the nnEMF is affecting me?
>
> Yes, we plan to move south during the winter - that's in the works!
>
> We have no health insurance, so we pay cash for labs. If anyone knows what/where I should turn, I thank you for any help!
>
> A

I am curious if you have done an Optimal Journal? If you write a thorough one up, Jack may give you some specific advice even before your consult - I am curious what your vit D is now since you say you are tan. I would also get the BUN/creatinine ratio, HS CRP, leptin - those are starters. The leptin may not be something you NEED becaue the HSCRP will tell enough of a story I think but I personally like watching data but I also dont like spending money so you have to decide. In your journal, you can list the timeline of implementation, starting issues, how things have improved, etc, what measures you have taken - have you done any CT? I would be curious his thoughts on the magnetico for you... I have been thinking about one myself...along with a cutoff switch from Anthony, hopefully 2 big needle movers in addition to what I am already done in my current desert.

Just wondering if there's any protocol that can be recommended to help recover from this. I lift weights quite often and it's been nearly impossible to do my usual loads due to the knee injury. Have been slowly trying to ease back into it doing 20-30 lb single leg extensions for 20 reps + lots of daytime walks. Patella traction is somewhat painful especially at the top of the contraction when load is added. Been doing CT every evening before bed and added glycine / bone broth to my diet a bit more to try to help collagen repair/synthesis. Obviously following circadian rules as well.

I have never dated a gal named "Iris," but she sounds like a "nice" girl to date during this waiting for the 21 St., century computer - which should be available in early 2025.

I have found using a grounded charger (Apple) grounds my whole network ethernet included. To ground just the ethernet, use a Cat6A or Cat8 cord (metal plugs) and either add a grounding ethernet plug/cord into the bus or clip an alligator clip onto the plug. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/store](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/store)

### Daulatwant — 2024-08-20T22:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-iris.29338/post-338720>

> [optimal2024 said:](/goto/post?id=338695)
>
> I am using a laptop without charger and connected to an Ethernet cable. I have iris installed and have set it to zero night time blue light setting
> Is this enough or do you think I need to add rubylith film to the screen the blue light as well? Thanks

Any screen, except maybe oled. Will have some light bleeding through regardless of what the colors are set by software.

My samsung amoled phones, still produce green light when I set blue and green all the way to zero. That is because the red pixels of the oled are not clean red but rather orange.

The effect is far worse on lcd, since the backight is always putting out white light and it leaks through.

I would probably use a film.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-20T22:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-iris.29338/post-338724>

If you have to spend time in front of any screen, certainly use software and/or filters but also have as much natural light around you Or add a red light and/or NIR light to counter the blue. Any screen time after sunset especially, no matter what action you take - is just bad.

### GFam — 2024-08-23T18:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-body-voltage.29353/post-338824>

This is a question for Anthony. Sent him a direct message but he didn't reply (maybe didn't see it) so putting it here.

I was browsing the forums the other day and came across a post where you had mentioned your business partner was developing a device to test stray voltage in the ground (can't find it again now). I think this is different from your body tester. What was the outcome of that development? Did a device get created?

Another quick question. When using the body voltage tester outside should we be connecting the BVK to ground or not since we are grounded ourselves (assuming our skin is touching the earth).

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-23T18:52:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-body-voltage.29353/post-338827>

I think this is different from your body tester. What was the outcome of that development? Did a device get created?
>
> Another quick question. When using the body voltage tester outside should we be connecting the BVK to ground or not since we are grounded ourselves (assuming our skin is touching the earth).

Hi, I did not receive a direct message here on the Forum so thanks for posting here.

We put the ground test project in hold. The meter required was quite expensive.

The body voltage meter works by giving the field you are in a path if least resistance to ground thru the meter so you can measure it. If you are also grounded in some other way, bare feet, ground pad etc the BV meter does not work. Outside wear shoes and connect the white ground cord to the ground spike.

# Thread: Becoming cold adapted
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2024-08-25T06:41:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/post-338872>

I have been fairly regular with CT at about 55-57 degrees for 30-45mim every other day. For about two months now. Prior to that i did 6 weeks of the face plunging.
But, This week i went camping where it was approximately 8deg C at night and my tent and sleeping bag were not that warm.
Honestly, i was just freezing every night and felt so unadapted to the cold. Now i have a slight cough.

I have not been doing the cold water prior to CT.
What else am i missing? I really thought i would be a little more cold adapted by now.

### Johan Lindøm — 2024-08-25T12:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/post-338874>

I can definitely relate to your experience. I live in a cold country, and I feel that I’m better adapted to the climate than most of my friends. However, I’ve had moments similar to what you described, where I struggle to regain my body temperature and end up shivering through the night. That said, I’m no expert.

I find cold therapy quite challenging. While I can run or ski in the winter without a t-shirt, sitting still in cold water or swimming in it is much more difficult for me. My body constantly urges me to quit as soon as possible. That’s why I prefer to run in nature wearing only shorts; I can do that for hours. I tell myself that this is also a form of cold therapy, and it gives me the much-needed exposure to sun light! Even though the sunlight here is shitty and without UV.

edit: A small addition. At that time when I was freezing at night, I was in the middle of Hardangervidda, in winter, far from people. I was sleeping in a sleeping bag with a sleeping pad, but without a tent, and the temperature that night dropped to below minus 24 degrees Celsius. Hardangervidda is a large mountain area in Norway with almost no people, and large parts of the area are nature reserves.

### rtd — 2024-08-25T19:25:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/post-338890>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=338872)
>
> Honestly, i was just freezing every night and felt so unadapted to the cold.
> What else am i missing?

An extra layer?

### Emmanuela — 2024-08-28T03:43:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/post-338987>

an extra layer? Lol you mean more clothes?

# Thread: How to test safe wifi distance bungalows vietnam?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-26T13:52:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338921>

My twin brother and I are planning to book a bungalow in Vietnam on a beach resort. If we enjoy the bungalow we will book it for a year. Each bungalow has wifi. We contacted the owner and he will remove the wifi station/booster in our bungalow so that we can get cabled internet instead.

The question remains: will the wifi from the neigboring bungalows still be a problem? How do we test for that? Is it as simple as testing whether our phones can receive a wifi signal? And if they do, is 1 bar on the phone still safe? Or do we need other equipment to test?

On the photos below you can see the distance between the bungalows

### OptimalSkr — 2024-08-26T14:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338922>

You'll never know for sure until you get a real RF meter --- for $200 dollars its worth the peace of mind

### Mat.I — 2024-08-26T14:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338923>

> [Thijs Oosting said:](/goto/post?id=338921)
>
> My twin brother and I are planning to book a bungalow in Vietnam on a beach resort. If we enjoy the bungalow we will book it for a year. Each bungalow has wifi. We contacted the owner and he will remove the wifi station/booster in our bungalow so that we can get cabled internet instead.
>
> The question remains: will the wifi from the neigboring bungalows still be a problem? How do we test for that? Is it as simple as testing whether our phones can receive a wifi signal? And if they do, is 1 bar on the phone still safe? Or do we need other equipment to test?
>
> On the photos below you can see the distance between the bungalows

You need to measure this with an microwave RF meter and each n= 1 in terms of how RF effects you is different. If you're not RF sensitive then maybe you can handle more, however, if striving to be optimal and to limit as much EMF as possible especially where you are sleeping you will need to measure it to understand what your are dealing with.

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-26T16:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338928>

Ok thank you. We have a TriField EMF meter model TF2 which includes an RF mode. How many mW/m2 is safe? We are dealing with adrenal fatigue, ashtma and gut issues and need to maximize internal melanin production.

### Saichi — 2024-08-27T01:09:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338946>

Groundhog Day every day here. People who ask for help don't even read this forum and the same who need spoon-fed wouldn't do the mitigation work anyways.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-27T13:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338958>

> [Thijs Oosting said:](/goto/post?id=338921)
>
> My twin brother and I are planning to book a bungalow in Vietnam on a beach resort. If we enjoy the bungalow we will book it for a year. Each bungalow has wifi. We contacted the owner and he will remove the wifi station/booster in our bungalow so that we can get cabled internet instead.
>
> The question remains: will the wifi from the neigboring bungalows still be a problem? How do we test for that? Is it as simple as testing whether our phones can receive a wifi signal? And if they do, is 1 bar on the phone still safe? Or do we need other equipment to test?
>
> On the photos below you can see the distance between the bungalows

Your question is like someone who is allergic to cooking smells and so only eats salad, asking - will my neighbor‘s cooking bother me?

You seem to be going to a lot of effort to in part avoid nnEMF. Why would you not invest in the equipment to measure? Most WiFi routers have a range of 100ft to 200ft these days. Before making a one year commitment I would measure with a decent RF meter - Safe and Sound Pro 2 ($400). It is not just the Wi-Fi router, it’s also the computers and phones etc. connected to them. How close is the nearest cell tower? What are the electric fields like? All those bungalows look pretty close to each other.

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-27T19:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338978>

Thanks Anthony! We just purchased a Safe and Sound Pro 2 a few hours back haha. Do you perhaps know what measurements would be safe? Would that be up to 0.010 uw/m2?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-28T05:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338992>

I believe the BBI recommendation is <0.1 micro watts per meter square is “of no concern”
0.1 - 10 “slight concern”
10 - 1000 “severe concern”
\>1000 “extreme concern”

In this modern world with all the tech for most <10 consider good (unless you are already EHS)

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-29T12:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-339038>

Thanks Anthony. This is really helpful!

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-30T17:45:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-339086>

We may want to sleep in a tent on the beach and we wonder how we can ground ourselves? We have a bought a copper sheet to sleep on. We can connect a copper wire to it and stick it in the ground. Would that be a good way to ground? and do you think we need to buy a body voltage meter for testing? Or is testing on the beach not necessary?

This is the sheet:

[Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07WTQGGNM/ref=pe_386300_440135490_TE_simp_item_image)

This is the copper wire:

[https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07GD8WL5V/ref=pe\_386300\_440135490\_TE\_simp\_item\_image?th=1](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07GD8WL5V/ref=pe_386300_440135490_TE_simp_item_image?th=1)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-31T19:49:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-339122>

> [Thijs Oosting said:](/goto/post?id=339086)
>
> We may want to sleep in a tent on the beach and we wonder how we can ground ourselves? We have a bought a copper sheet to sleep on. We can connect a copper wire to it and stick it in the ground. Would that be a good way to ground? and do you think we need to buy a body voltage meter for testing? Or is testing on the beach not necessary?
>
> This is the sheet:
>
> [Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07WTQGGNM/ref=pe_386300_440135490_TE_simp_item_image)
>
>
> This is the copper wire:
>
> [https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07GD8WL5V/ref=pe\_386300\_440135490\_TE\_simp\_item\_image?th=1](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07GD8WL5V/ref=pe_386300_440135490_TE_simp_item_image?th=1)

The ground IS the ground . . . If you are sleeping dirrectly on the ground you are grounded. If you want something between you and the ground because of dampness, sand critters etc the copper sheep could work instead of a plastic ground sheet that would insulate you. Why add a wire? You could use a canvas cot with metal legs. Still need to measure to find a good low RF location.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unplugged-phone.29374/post-339054>

If you are worried about security and privacy you basically should stop using a cell phone. They are insecure by design. Now, you may not be that worried that you would stop using the cell phones. But understand you can be geo-located just by using the cell towers. 5G is probably the most accurate and gets less accurate as you go down the radio spectrum. If you use 'apps' they have more access to your phone and data (to possibly include conversations), than they would if you used a browser on your phone. This is why there is always a push for you to use the app and not the browser.

# Thread: How to read blogs, tweets, and more on a e-ink screen (no blue light)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-read-blogs-tweets-and-more-on-a-e-ink-screen-no-blue-light.29379/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SanderC — 2024-08-29T19:50:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-read-blogs-tweets-and-more-on-a-e-ink-screen-no-blue-light.29379/post-339060>

Hi everyone.

I read all uncle Jack's blogs, Twitter posts and other things from the internet on the _Reader_ app on an e-ink screen. This SIGNIFICANTLY reduces my blue light exposure, and it's super readable vs blue screens in the sun.

**Hardware**

E-ink tablet. I use BOOX: [https://shop.boox.com/products/tabx](https://shop.boox.com/products/tabx)

I will be switching to the Daylight computer once it arrives (pre-ordered). The Daylight computer is basically an e-ink screen but with fluidity (60 hz).

If you don't use the backlight, it's blue-light free. You can also enable Airplane mode. It has an android app store so you can download any app just like on another tablet.

You also have this in [phone size](https://shop.boox.com/products/palma?gad_source=1&gbraid=0AAAAAC9FI3055B4M4d1UJgax1vX2jG32k&gclid=CjwKCAjwuMC2BhA7EiwAmJKRrLHDZTTd5BX0lZy4aqpKA0IZ83nHJPkuLrHfijPs-7z--TjHwhSb7hoCbP4QAvD_BwE) if you're looking to use have a blue-light free phone (wifi only). Not sure how good it is.

**Software**

Readwise Reader: [https://readwise.io/read](https://readwise.io/read)

It's basically one feed where you can import all the stuff from all over the internet (pdf, ebook, twitter feeds, online webpages...)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

You can also have a [Readwise paid account](https://readwise.io), which enables you to save highlights from these blogs, twittter feeds etc, and receive one email newsletter a day with random highlights/quotes.

Download this Reader app on the e-ink tablet, read them in the sun and off you go!!

Hope this helps and let me know if you have any questions.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clothing-that-doesnt-block-light.29383/post-339072>

White linen is apparently best, but still seems to block 80% of light:

[https://pismin.com/10.1111/php.12292](https://pismin.com/10.1111/php.12292)

Some companies have made special fabrics designed to let more light through, but none publish data, one has a contradictory statements (like "blocks UVB while not blocking vitamin D production"), none specify which types of UV, and none specify effects on other wavelengths. Plus the lines of apparel are very limited and expensive. Aside from getting naked, anyone find any clothing that helps substantially? Though I am not exactly interested in wearing nothing but fish net or see-through items. This guy's top is just shouting, "I'm ready to big my presentation to the executives":

> I avoid all synthetic clothing since it most likely has hydrogel now. Find a place to sunbathe naked is the real solution.

Easier written than done. I try on my apt deck and it is difficult with neighborhood children cutting through ones yard. Don't want that charge. There is a place I know yet it would be legally trespassing. And the property is now starting construction for new apartment complex. Only so much land living on an island.

### metallikat36 — 2024-09-04T11:03:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clothing-that-doesnt-block-light.29383/post-339234>

> [rtd said:](/goto/post?id=339093)
>
> Easier written than done. I try on my apt deck and it is difficult with neighborhood children cutting through ones yard. Don't want that charge. There is a place I know yet it would be legally trespassing. And the property is now starting construction for new apartment complex. Only so much land living on an island.

Maybe buy some sunbathing privacy screens and lay inside them.

### metallikat36 — 2024-09-04T11:12:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clothing-that-doesnt-block-light.29383/post-339235>

There are some really scandalous see-through net / mesh shorts for when you are alone, but ain't gonna try that in public...

In theory, I have an idea. Image a chess board. The black squares and the white squares are out of phase with each other. Imagine the black squares are like one layer of mesh fabric, and the white squares are a second layer of mesh fabric. Then imagine the inside of the first layer of fabric is highly reflective. Light would pass through the holes in the first layer of fabric, and reflectivity on the inside of that layer would help light reach your skin. All while blocking direct vision. In other words, maybe someone can invent clothing that "bends" (or rather redirects) the path of light. Or to say it another way: The visible outside layer of fabric is somehow "one-way" for light. Light can pass through its gaps, but not get out again, and bounce around until it hits the skin.

This one looks good so long as your admirers are at a distance:

[https://www.amazon.com/YiZYiF-Through-Shorts-Underwear-Watershort/dp/B07TWQ8BZH/](https://www.amazon.com/YiZYiF-Through-Shorts-Underwear-Watershort/dp/B07TWQ8BZH/)

### metallikat36 — 2024-09-04T13:59:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clothing-that-doesnt-block-light.29383/post-339238>

So it occured to me that windows can have one-way tinting. We need that, but for clothes. Apparently, in theater they use a fabric called scrim which sounds like it is some sort of one-way fabric:

### [Sharkstooth Scrim Fabrics](https://www.rosebrand.com/fabric-stage-curtains-backdrops/sharkstooth-scrim-fabrics.aspx?)

Extra-wide widths and colors. Scrim is an open weave net used extensively in theatre.

 www.rosebrand.com

Hmm...

# Thread: Tent, mat and camping material recommendations?

### eve001 — 2024-09-01T11:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tent-mat-and-camping-material-recommendations.29389/post-339135>

Hi there. I am looking for recommendations for

\* a 1-2 person tent
\* a sleep mat = hard, flat and no cushion (sleep on belly sometimes)
\* available for EU, Belgium.
\* mild cold proof temperatures are dropping slowly as autumn comes
\* rain proof for Belgium
\* does not offgas (too many) chemicals/synthetic odour

I want to sleep in the garden. My aim is to improve sleep and energy.
If that works I may go camping - alone or w/ my dog.
I am new to camping. Want to spend winter/autumn South.

Has anyone good experiences w/ some tents/mats?

My concern is that the chemicals in tents may leave me wore of then I now am... - given that my brain seems to be struggling due to EHS (leaky BBB, neurotoxins?)

I already spent hours/days looking for tents+mats on amazon etc. but couldn't make up my mind.
The flat mats with no cushioning are expensive. Towards 150-200€. Is that worth it? Or are there good cheaper options? Sleep is an issue here...
What's your experience?

Thanks!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-01T18:23:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tent-mat-and-camping-material-recommendations.29389/post-339141>

Do you have the equivalent of what here in America are Army & Navy surplus stores? Army tents and cots I believe are natural fabrics (canvas).

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-01T21:25:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tent-mat-and-camping-material-recommendations.29389/post-339144>

### eve001 — 2024-09-02T10:21:19+0300

Great tips [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) ! Thank you! I wish you were in continental Europe!
I'd be happy to share your workshops on socials if you want so and if you provide share-able links.
I have acquaintances in US from when I still had a social life!!

From my wifi/bluetooth devices back then
Have a great day!

# Thread: LDL Subfraction success
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-subfraction-success.29392/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Franko54 — 2024-09-02T12:03:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-subfraction-success.29392/post-339157>

Had a high CAC score of 1661 and amongst other less than ideal numbers, small dense LDL Subfractions were high (LDL 4 was 0.25mmol/L and LDL 3 1.36mmol/L) - August 23. Eliminated all sources of nnEMF from the home; and changed all lighting to blue light blocking globes; 6-8 months of sunrise; CT and sauna; plenty of UV.
25 OH Vit D steadily increased over this period 134-158nmol/L. I spent a few months in Italy to escape the Australian Winter and I ate my share of carbs (and local wine and seasonal fruit). The markets labelled all their produce with their origin, which was helpful. I also tested the local tap water to check the deuterium levels out of interest, and it was 151.17ppm. The natural spring water I drink locally is sourced from about 100km away, has a D20 of 150.26ppm.
Significantly, I had never got so much sun in my life during this trip.
Anyway, the punchline is LDL 4 is now zero and LDL 3 within range at 0.1. Intermediate LDL also dropped back by 35%, moving me from Type B to Type A LDL phenotype. Hopefully this is a proxy for CAC score burden, and this will move in concert. So very pleased I discovered Dr Kruse and his teachings last year.
Although, my ferritin has moved up to 549 ug/L after this trip and was 500ug/L in August 23, dipped to 295ug/L and then back up. Iron; transferritin and transferritin saturation all within range. Not sure what to make of that?@Jack Kruse , will retest toward the end of the year. Overall, things appear to be heading in the right direction.

### Amandapants11 — 2026-02-05T04:29:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-subfraction-success.29392/post-366378>

Wow, this all sounds very promising. Have you had any subsequent calcium scoring or any other tests? How are they all looking?

# Thread: CT and recovering from covid
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sheas — 2024-09-03T17:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339199>

I rarely get sick. Rarely catch things going around. However....my husband and I had to fly to MIA from DFW just for a night to take care of something and two days after we got home it hit me. And it hit me hard. I never tested but I knew it was covid just from my symptoms. I baked in the sun as much as I could even with fever and achy and feeling like hell. My routine was get up at 6, go outside with the sunrise then lay on a lounger until about noon. Then I would come inside, take an epson salt bath and sleep.. for 3 days that is all I did. I could not tolerate a cold plunge at all. So I stayed with the heat and listened to my body. Today is day 12 and getting much better just congested still and my energy is not back to 100%. Still baking in the sun and actually quite tan except today and yesterday it has been raining. Anyway, wondering what others have done and how CT could help at this point. I drained my tub yesterday and the cold water felt good when I reached in to pull the plug, so thinking it might be beneficial now. I have been cold plunging for a few years so quite acclimated and I only take cold showers. Except these last 12 days I have been wanting to feel warmth. Wondering what others have done to recover from this and get their energy back. Should I force a plunge or is it too much stress right now.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-03T18:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339201>

I have read in several places (not just one source) that people have experienced relief of symptoms with nicotine patches or gum. People with long Covid report getting taste and smell back in days. They say cut a 7mg patch into 4 and use 1/4 each day.

### Saichi — 2024-09-03T19:31:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339204>

Cold, flu, covid, corona, whatever you want to call it is not contagious hence all tests to determine if you have it are not real. You became sick because of the nnemf onslaught of flying and your environments.

### sheas — 2024-09-03T20:27:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339209>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=339201)
>
> I have read in several places (not just one source) that people have experienced relief of symptoms with nicotine patches or gum. People with long Covid report getting taste and smell back in days. They say cut a 7mg patch into 4 and use 1/4 each day.

Interesting! Luckily my olfactory nerve kicked back on line within a couple of days after I lost my sense of smell. That freaked me out more than anything as I couldn’t even smell peppermint oil. The nicotine patches/gum is interesting. Thank you for the reply

### sheas — 2024-09-03T20:31:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339210>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=339204)
>
> Cold, flu, covid, corona, whatever you want to call it is not contagious hence all tests to determine if you have it are not real. You became sick because of the nnemf onslaught of flying and your environments.

My husband didn’t get it and he was with me. But, I had an MRI about a month ago and in July had to go to CA to teach a class. Never felt quite the same after that trip so I agree. I started off weakened and I believe that’s why I got sick and my husband did not.

# Thread: Structured Water and Conspiracy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-water-and-conspiracy.29412/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ZacharyNow — 2024-09-06T20:10:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-water-and-conspiracy.29412/post-339346>

Hello all,
I have a deep interest in understanding both light and water. With that said, I want to share a story of recovery and healing that made me question some things. I am posting here in hopes you all can help further understanding of the principles and mechanisms involved.

long story short, I have made several bad decisions that span my adolescence and adulthood, including smoking, marijuana use, insane caffeine intake and screen time, and other not so savory decisions.
After deciding to take Jack‘s info and incorporate it my life (along with other things), I have noticed almost zero depression, anxiety, stress, etc.

Morning sun while grounding has been critical, along with minimal exposure to lighting after the sun sets and no blue light on my devices all day. I also have been filling a glass gallon with RO water and placing it in direct sunlight for min. 8 hours. This is all I have been drinking.

After spending time in the province in the Philippines (like the countryside in the US, but tropical and mountainous), I became accustomed to taking a cold shower by manually pouring water over my head via a pitcher instead of the normal shower .
I used this “tech” here at home in the US and made my outdoor minimalistic shower using well water.

I then decided to quit smoking, drinking, caffeine, screen time, and more. But I wanted to do it all at once in order to shock my body instead of the tapering approach I have done in the past. But this time, the sun routine, grounding, cold outdoor shower (naked), cardio exercise (walking/running), and structured water via direct sunlight combined resulted in ZERO issues. No withdrawal issues, no anxiety, depression, aggression, headaches, etc. I was so shocked I had to figure out why…

I say all of that to really ask about water.
Is this placing of water in direct sunlight just woo or what? Anecdotally, I notice more energy and vigor.
I then did some research on well water which is high in sulfates. (mine smells like eggs and farts) Turns out Sulphur is essential in creating insulin as well as many other processes. If S is so important, why are we removing it from our water? Is this just a crazy idea, or can diabetes be linked to the removal of sulphur from our water? Can my own rapid recovery be due in part by this ‘structured water’ and sulphur-rich shower water? Many questions are unanswered. Hope you guys can help provide insight

### La-Shawn Hill — 2024-10-24T18:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-water-and-conspiracy.29412/post-341504>

Dr. Kruse, are "clinical depression" and "poor redox" the same thing? I struggle with calling myself "depressed" because I don't feel sad, I just feel low-energy and literally can't perform certain tasks that I used to, like making my bed, due to executive dysfunction. If I had to describe it, I feel like I've been locked out of certain parts of my brain. Are there any treatments centralized medicine offers that you'd recommend? I have an appointment with a neurologist in December, and I kept my appointment with Environmental Health Center-Dallas. The reason why I kept it is because someone had left a Google review and said they'd been able to help them with their "EMF Brain Injury."

# Thread: CT and sauna - my hypoglycemic reaction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-sauna-my-hypoglycemic-reaction.29424/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2024-09-10T05:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-sauna-my-hypoglycemic-reaction.29424/post-339441>

Hi all,

I am almost done reading the CT series and all the pinned threads here. I was reading the IR and CR pinned thread and got curious.
So, i don’t have t2d or the like. However, i have had issues with hypoglycemia in the past if i would go more than 3 hours without food and if i would eat food too high in carbs.
Now i have been doing the leptin rx and CT on and off (mostly on) for a couple months. My hypoglycemia symptoms evaporated.
BUT, twice, i went with a friend to an outdoor spa. Did the sauna and lots of CT, out in the sun a lot. And both times, i experienced a hypoglycemia episode after getting back into the cold baths after eating lunch. Both times i stuck to a low carb lunch, mostly meat (what was available there) and i got dizzy, woozy, drunky almost and it lasted for quite a while.
Thoughts?

### Emmanuela — 2024-09-14T05:16:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-sauna-my-hypoglycemic-reaction.29424/post-339554>

I finished reading up to CT 11 blog and i actually think i am beginning to understand why i am having such a hard time cold adapting along with issues like the hypoglycemia. Being vegetarian for so long (since birth) up until 10 years ago, i probably have very poor mineral stores. CT 11 mentions hair samples as a measure of mineral deficiencies. But i am thinking i need to transiently supplement magnesium as I am eating seafood and oysters at the max of my affordability and the connection Kevin makes in that blog really resonated with me.

\*ESI LF electric: yes : around the radiator & around wires at ceiling for light - magnetic: no

\*The breaker to the plugs (2) is not off. Impossible for now.
\*The ESI does not sense these LF fields in my bed
\*Bed = (away from wall: 30-40cm but wires seem not to be there but up in ceiling)
\*Bed = away from radiator about a meter and I lay +- another extra 1/2 meter away from
\*Body voltage meter = 5000 milivolts when standing upright lower when lying down. Nit sure if I use it correctly (AC current position)

People travel w/ these tents in hotels etc. all the time.
Anyone here given it a try?

If the tent is within 6ft - 8ft they pick up the field so if you do not turn off these circuits (electric fields) you are wrapped in a 60Hz bubble. # Thread: Vision deteriorate - Infrared/sun/covid/bodypain?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-deteriorate-infrared-sun-covid-bodypain.29432/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ziztine — 2024-09-12T16:52:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-deteriorate-infrared-sun-covid-bodypain.29432/post-339519>

Hi everyone,
I have been following Dr Jack Kruse for more than 8 years. This is my first post. I have pretty high myopia since young and started to have presbyopia many years ago but nothing serious. I am 51. But recently, the **vision in my right eye suddenly deteriorate**. It increased by 400 degrees. The strange thing is there is no change in my left eye.

One or more of these are what I suspect might have caused the sudden increase in myopia in my right eye:
(1) **Infrared heat lamp** - I have used this small table size lamp on the front of my right injured shoulder this year for less than 10 times. I normally have on my blue blockers while I surf on my desktop and use the lamp at the same time. Should I have protected my eyes from the infrared lamp?

(2) **Sun glazing** - I started having a bit more floaters end of last year. So I started to stare at the sun for 2-10mins daily during the sunrise hours in the hope that it will help with the floaters and overall vision and health. normally between 7am (sunrise) to 8am. I do not ever wear sunglasses but I do wear my spectacles when I farm in my two gardens (one on the rooftop). I only throw on a hat when its too hot. I am located just one degree of latitude above the Equator. Could the sun's ray be too strong here for sun gazing for so long?

(3) In July this year, I contacted the **covid virus**. It feels like my vision got a lot worse after this episode. My right eye has always been the weaker eye. The degree is a lot higher than my left all these years. Parasites? I am doing a 30 day liver cleanse now.

(4) **The right side of my body has been in pain** since the mid of last year when I sustained a muscle injury. I felt that besides my overall health, my vision deteriorated during the same period. But only my right eye is affected. Could it be connected to my right side body pains?

I do not know whether I should proceed with my routine of staring at the morning sun. Any advice would truly be appreciated.

### ziztine — 2024-09-12T17:36:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-deteriorate-infrared-sun-covid-bodypain.29432/post-339520>

[https://www.huffpost.com/entry/best-pot-for-houseplant\_n\_66e1f85be4b06d75dd27d6de](https://www.huffpost.com/entry/best-pot-for-houseplant_n_66e1f85be4b06d75dd27d6de)
So THAT'S Why It's So Important To Get Sunlight Every Morning

### [So THAT'S Why It's So Important To Get Sunlight Every Morning](https://www.huffpost.com/entry/sunlight-morning-better-sleep_l_66d864c7e4b0830f6e92e7af?)

Experts say it should be a routine, not a once-in-a-blue-moon occurrence.

 www.huffpost.com

"Experts say it should be a routine, not a once-in-a-blue-moon occurrence."
Dr Kruse has been saying it for years!

### eve001 — 2024-09-12T19:40:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339525>

Practical tips for sleeping in car as comfy as possible and make it more fun?

I am at 50°. Autumn. Chilly and rainy often.
I have no camping experience.

There is a level difference between trunk and the back seats that are folded forward of about 12cm.
\==> how could I even that out best?

E-smog seems low here and my best option now.
I have no energy to go shopping, browse or flirt with the search function.

Body voltage seemed good. Very low.
I took my BV meter and put it on DC.
I then connected it to th earth (not too deep, impossible, wire too short).
Then I sat in trunk with rubber soles.

RF also. Peak below 1microwatt when measuring behind the metal.
Otherwise up to +- 4 when cars and so.

Loading my red/amber lights. Will take water.

Maybe put paper or maybe rescue blanket or aluminium foil against the windows... to block light and other nnEMFs?

So sick of this.

Hope this works for me to get more energy to leave this place.
Africa? That'd make for beautiful contrasts.

Cool song - Weird video clip - first time I see it. Expected a black skinned singer...

PS: If you think this can't happen to you... think again.
PPS: People with off-topic unhelpful responses will be blocked without goodbye.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-12T21:27:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339527>

When sleeping in a car keep in mind it is a metal box. The car needs to away from any AC fields - buildings, overhead wires etc. You also want to be away from cell towers. You MUST turn of your phone and/or computer (WiFi, Bluetooth etc). Most people are not adversely affected by DC voltage from the car BUT some are. A friend has to disconnect the battery when he sleeps in his car.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-12T23:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339530>

It’s a car . . . Park where there is no nnEMF . . . IF DC fields bother you - disconnect the battery.
Rules for nnEMF mitigation:
#1 - turn off, disconnect, eliminate the source
#2 - distance from the source
#3 - shield from the source

### drsongihan — 2024-09-15T19:34:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339605>

A trifold foam mattress or a custom foam mattress works well because the foam allows you to wedge it on uneven surfaces, push it into the foot space of back of car and it can take up many different shapes. Somewhat firm foam will help you not develop back pain. A trifold also allows you to bring that into a simple tent that you can always carry in the car whenever you are in a spot to put up a tent you have a full size mattress. If you have a tarp for wet ground, a tent and a trifold foam and a car, you can sleep almost anywhere anytime. Make sure you buy foam meant for sleeping — I worry about petroleum degassing for a while and the loose foam is bad because they degas for a while. But if you let it degas for a while and sleep with a crack of window open it should be fine. Foam soaks up humidity so important that you have a well absorbing cotton sheet around. I wish there was a shape shifting natural material but you could argue petroleum is “natural” as long as you don’t eat it

Driving to a low EMF place sounds great and maybe the car battery is not a problem for you. If you can drive and work remotely do you need to be at 50 Latitude? Wouldn’t it be better to drive to lower latitude? Even 40 is easier than 50. How about driving to a nearby Forrest or Mountain or at least a botanical garden? I think being near plants and trees are healing and EMF tend to be poor near Forrest’s.

I would always drive and sleep with window open. The windows will anyway let EMF and RF through (since your phone has reception in the car) so driving to a low EMF place would be best. But a closed car window filters all light except for visible in blue/green and only lets a bit of red through.

I would also take your water container and fill the bottom with quartz crystals. They are cheap, like $10 for a pound.

I think nature heals. Which continent are you in and which one is the closest National park?

If I have no kid to take care of, and if my health was to get into trouble or if I got sick from my environment or if I want to take a break from my work, I would totally get a truck and drive to my favorite mountain range, the Sierra Nevadas, like Yosemite and Kings Canyon or Sequoia or the Volcanoes in Lassen National park or to Yellow Stone National parks. Good light, water and magnetism there

We are entering autumn. Wet climate, chilly.

Now moverd from 4000-5000mV BV bedroom (dark) to 50mV BV veranda (light comes in but Bodyvoltage (BV) significatly lower). I feel too weak to travel now. Hoped the melanin renovation past summer would have strengthened me. Only learned about BV last week. RF EMFs okay here.
Hopefully sleeping in veranda will allow me to gain energy.

I hope to have enough energy/resources soon to travel through France/germany down South.

### MatthewT — 2024-09-12T21:18:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycaemia.29434/post-339526>

from a quantum perspective what causes hypoglycaemia episodes and how would you attack it at its root?

I have had come out of nowhere the past two weeks what people describe as “reactive hypoglycaemia” when I wake up my BG levels are just right but the moment I have breakfast onwards from there my BG is like a rollercoaster, if I don’t eat every two hours I’ll have some seriously hypoglycaemic levels leading me with no choice but to consume fast acting carbs as I have got to levels like 1.2 mmol/L / 18mg/dl.

Had labs done and everything is fine, tested thyroid function, liver function, cortisol, a few other things but it’s all good, the Drs were suggesting it potentially some kind of viral infection as everything seems healthy but it doesn’t seem to be going away.

Rx for this situation?

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### vivianrosenthal — 2024-09-14T16:00:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339571>

Hello! I would love help and advice on how to help my insomnia. I have been getting up and seeing the sun for three months but unfortunately I am still not able to sleep through the night, and I'm exhausted and feeling sick from lack of sleep. Is there a red light machine that is recommended to increase light exposure since the morning routine hasn't been enough to help my hormones and melatonin production reset. Would love any advice or tips from folks who have struggled with insomnia, hormonal issues, melatonin production issues, etc. Thank you! Vivian

### rtd — 2024-09-14T16:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339572>

> [vivianrosenthal said:](/goto/post?id=339571)
>
> Hello! I would love help and advice on how to help my insomnia. I have been getting up and seeing the sun for three months but unfortunately I am still not able to sleep through the night, and I'm exhausted and feeling sick from lack of sleep. Is there a red light machine that is recommended to increase light exposure since the morning routine hasn't been enough to help my hormones and melatonin production reset. Would love any advice or tips from folks who have struggled with insomnia, hormonal issues, melatonin production issues, etc. Thank you! Vivian

A supplement mix of Magnesium BiGlycinate (400mg), L-theanine (400mg), and Inositol (1500mg) works for me. I also like NuCalm's Deep Sleep binaural beats.

### drsongihan — 2024-09-14T16:36:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339574>

I don’t know the answer to your situation but I can tell you what my friend and my son had to do for insomnia.

1\. Early morning sun. My friend went out at sunrise, I assume you are going out 30 min before sunrise or at least at sunrise and stay outside for at least 30 min.

2\. Blackout the room for sleep. No photons. So dark you cannot see your hand in front of your face. I closed crevices below door even. My friend also uses eye cover because her husband or kids open door sometimes to bedroom.

3\. Remove all electrical devices in your bedroom. Plug them all out. Remove WiFi, obviously remove phone from your bedroom and overall make sure there is no EMF pollution in your room.

4\. My friend takes over the counter micro dose of lithium. My son takes higher dose of lithium. Lithium helps anybody who has a weak electrical body and no robust bio electric. Lithium is different from supplements in that it does not alter your body’s receptors to down or up regulate things, unlike melatonin.

5\. Ground yourself as much as you can, especially before going to bed.

6\. No blue light after sunset. I have no blue light on after sunset in the entire house. Only amber or red light. If you look at electronics, wear amber blue blocking glasses. That’s critical that your eyes don’t get blue light at least 3 hours before bed.

7\. Go out at solar noon for at least 30 min to get UV light if you prepared your body to be able to do so. And wherever you go, in car, in office or at home, open windows. Far infrared, infrared and uv photons are all missing indoors.

That’s what helped two people close to me. I do the same and sleep better but I never had insomnia to begin with.

### maria@modernhuman — 2024-09-14T16:54:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339576>

Vivian it's good to address the fundamentals before spending money on gadgets, supplements, lotions, and potions. Re-assess your environment and remove things causing the assault.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339578>

# Thread: Woodstock, we are stardust
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/woodstock-we-are-stardust.29443/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1

### Uswebers1 — 2024-09-16T06:54:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/woodstock-we-are-stardust.29443/post-339617>

(Lyrics)
Well, I came upon a child of God
He was walking along the road
And I asked him, Tell where are you going?
This he told me

Said, I'm going down to Yasgur's Farm,
Gonna join in a rock and roll band.
Got to get back to the land and set my soul free.

We are stardust, we are golden,
We are billion year old carbon,
And we got to get ourselves back to the garden.

Well, then can I walk beside you?
I have come to lose the smog,
And I feel myself a cog in somethin' turning.
But then maybe it's the time of man.
And I don't know who I am,
But life is for learning.

We are stardust, we are golden,
We are billion year old carbon,
And we got to get ourselves back to the garden.

We are stardust, we are golden,
We are billion year old carbon,
And we got to get ourselves back to the garden.

By the time we got to Woodstock,
We were half a million strong
And everywhere was a song and a celebration.
And I dreamed I saw the bomber jet planes
Riding shotgun in the sky,
Turning into butterflies
Above our nation.

We are stardust, we are golden,
We are caught in the devils bargain,
And we got to get ourselves back to the garden.

### Chlo_e — 2024-09-17T13:15:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-sided-heart.29446/post-339646>

Some of the sound and cymatics researchers point out that the bass frequencies produced by the heart beat also play a role in some of these processes. For example, John Stuart Reid found that whole-blood (in vitro) was better oxygenated when exposed to music with a strong bass compared with classical music with no bass, but that both types of music improved oxygenation compared to no music.

Steiner also goes into great detail about the hexagonal shape in the development of bee hives, and that the hexagonal shapes produced in nature imparts certain energetic qualities. This theme seems to keep cropping up in other areas like water research.

The solar IR mentioned as driving the gel phase of water- I also do wonder how much of it is solar IR and how much of it is endogenously-produced IR or a combination of both..

### Kirsten F — 2024-09-17T21:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-bun-cr-ratio.29452/post-339667>

New to posting! Hoping to optimize cellular dehydration and retest before reconsultation with Dr. Kruse.

Background: 43 yo F, living in ES. Hx: IOL replacements 3 years ago (vision correction for high myopia), PCOS, BMI 21. Consult with Jack in April 2024 re: long-standing migraines. I have been doing very well re: my light environment and using my “solar panels” since I have a “tarp” over my eyes.

On retesting, vit D rose to 59 from 30. BUN:Cr ratio is shit - 21 (Cr 0.7, BUN 15).

Ideas and questions re: ratio:

1.  Grounding - not easy on my property, but needs to be optimized
2.  Environment - needs testing and optimization. We turn off wifi at a night. Just purchased meters and ethernet cables.
3.  Diet: I assume caffeine and alcohol worsen the ratio? Will focus on decreasing carb intake further.
4.  Body composition: Is there any leeway to what the optimal BUN:Cr ratio is based on body composition? My Cr will always be lowish due to low muscle mass - given the low denominator, is my target still <10?

Any comments greatly appreciated, including areas I have missed. Otherwise, I can take up with Jack in a consult.
