# Thread: Screen sensitivity triggers..Pixel flicker on screens (Temporal dithering)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/screen-sensitivity-triggers-pixel-flicker-on-screens-temporal-dithering.29664/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jordan — 2024-10-22T02:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/screen-sensitivity-triggers-pixel-flicker-on-screens-temporal-dithering.29664/post-341359>

Hello I wanted to share with you guys something I've came across that triggers weird neurological symptoms.

There is a technique called "temporal dithering" or "Frame rate control" which basically rapidly flickers pixels on your screen. You can't notice it to the eye but it's causing me bad symptoms as well as others on other forums.

Is this some sort of mkultra crap?!

You can only see the flicker when using a microscope and a high FPS camera..

So we got blue light, PWM (form of display brightness control that strobes) and temporal dithering that negatively affects us. The reason for dithering/frc is it tricks us into seeing more colors on a display that can't natively produce those colors. Someone has mentioned it can be as low as a 15-30hz flicker. I believe it was the admin at flickersense.org

Anyways it makes me feel super spacey with brain fog, nausea, anxious/depressed, sensitive to red color specifically, and get intense head pressure. (All go away discontinuing use)

I know 100% I can't use Apple devices as they tend to use dithering. Seems to be used on any LCD device from apple such as iphones, Mac's and Mac's external video output regardless of display attached.. other companies use it too on their hardware, just apple has no way to turn it off. They force dcip3 and disallow basic srgb color. I think oled iPhones may be safe from this but their other issue is display PWM and flashing of their IR laser projector.

Any thoughts on this? Anyone else have screen sensitivity issues at all? I try to use eink most of the time. I have a incandescent monitor as well I have been meaning to try soon.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Red light during work hours?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-during-work-hours.29665/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### johnperp304 — 2024-10-22T06:52:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-during-work-hours.29665/post-341369>

Hi all.

I work at a desk all day, looking at computer screens. Obviously a blue light environment. Working outside would be the best, but this is just not practical in my situation, would the next best approach be using a red light device to "balance" out the blue light from the screens, I.e. using it while working on the computer all day.
I do use day time blue blocking glasses (not a dark tint) and sit next to a window (which will now be open all the time when possible).

Thanks.

### moniverdin — 2024-10-25T02:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-during-work-hours.29665/post-341518>

I have a similar question. I have been listening to Jack Kruse podcasts the past few weeks, and I thought I had heard in one that if you look at your phone or computer outside, it balances out the blue light. I work from home on my computer, so I have moved my desk outside to my patio. Do I still need to wear blue light glasses since I am outside? I do feel so much better at the end of the day working outside.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-25T06:33:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-during-work-hours.29665/post-341523>

When I cannot be outside on my iPad I have near infrared lights behind my screen + a UV light + a full spectrum (grow) light above my head. A compromise to being outside with natural light yes - but . . .
Also visible 660nm red light mixed with 850nm NIR light in the bedroom before sleep.

### n3ur0m4n — 2024-10-28T19:34:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-during-work-hours.29665/post-341644>

> [johnperp304 said:](/goto/post?id=341369)

>
> I work at a desk all day, looking at computer screens. Obviously a blue light environment. Working outside would be the best, but this is just not practical in my situation, would the next best approach be using a red light device to "balance" out the blue light from the screens, I.e. using it while working on the computer all day.
> I do use day time blue blocking glasses (not a dark tint) and sit next to a window (which will now be open all the time when possible).
>
> Thanks.

John, what solutions have you found?

I currently use incandescent and some halogen bulbs, and I open doors and windows as much as possible to let in natural light.

I'm still _unclear about the degree of "supplemental light" during office work, winter, and the northern latitudes._
E.g., in what context should we supplement with:

-   Red and Near-Infrared (NIR) light ? what when, duration, products....
-   UVA and UVB light?
-   An optimal spectrum "in between"? I'm considering options like the Chroma Sky Portal 2.1

I am currently at the 67th latitude and need a decent solution while working my way to a southern climate.

I make sure to expose myself to sunrise and sunset, practice cold exposure and grounding, and I avoid excessive artificial blue and green light during the daytime, blocking it before and after sunrise and sunset.

All good questions, and I don't have any of the answers unfortunately! I am new to this whole "light is the key" game. I have started watching the sunrise for about an hour, allows me to get in some podcasts so it works out well. But as far as supplemental light in a sub-optimal environment, I'm thinking Anthony's suggestions will be worth looking into.
I had previously heard red light devices can improve eyesight, and over the last decade mine has definitely degraded (think myopia). I never really gave it any more thought until I recently came across Jack's work. Now I'm looking into using a red light device theraputically, which was going to be my starting point, then I heard about balancing the blue light so I'm also going to investigate having it running during the day whilst on the computer.

# Thread: Cold Thermo - is longer time or colder better?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermo-is-longer-time-or-colder-better.29678/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Espresso — 2024-10-24T14:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermo-is-longer-time-or-colder-better.29678/post-341466>

Hello all!
I have finally finished building my deep freeze cold plunge.
We started off at 10 C (50F) for 5 minutes.
Then moved on to 6 C (43) and we are building up to 15 minutes.
We are sitting in the water up to our necks, and sometimes wear wool socks - this makes a huge difference.
I tried 4 C (40F) but that was only bearable for about 6 minutes.
Is there some magic that happens at 4C? I know that that is the temperature where water starts to get less dense with lowering temperature, rather than less dense up to there.
Should we be trying for colder or longer?
Should we aim for 4 C. or doesn't it matter?

### christine_fp — 2024-10-24T17:34:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermo-is-longer-time-or-colder-better.29678/post-341502>

I've heard Jack say more than once that it doesn't need to be colder than 50-55F to get the benefits.

### eaandkw — 2024-10-28T16:50:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermo-is-longer-time-or-colder-better.29678/post-341636>

I believe at least salt water gets denser at around 34 degrees F. Not sure if fresh water is any different but that is partially what drives ocean currents.

Hi, yesterday the ants were attracted to the { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

laptop charger... It was plugged in to 220V AC.
For the output it's written 15-20V (6-4.5A)
I know we are electric beings but I'm interested in the deeper explanations. Any idea?

### Rusty — 2024-11-15T11:20:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electric-ants.29681/post-342396>

Devices that convert AC to DC, like your laptop charger, can build up a bit of static electricity around the plug or the transformer. Ants, surprisingly, can actually sense these tiny electric fields and might get curious about them.

### rtd — 2024-11-15T14:36:53+0200

"Crazy Ants" in Texas love electrical transformers.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=341572)

standard mag, 6.4+ and yes where I was standing in front of it where I stand to remove or insert something. 1m away , the reading dropped to .5. Just a regular electric fan oven. Standard electric was 70+. That was the meter placed next to the oven on the benchtop. When I pick up the meter, the reading accelerates upwards.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-27T05:51:24+0200

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-really-happening-when-a-seasonal-cold-flu-virus-goes-around-in-your-region-or-community.29693/>

### Woodstock — 2024-10-27T18:29:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-really-happening-when-a-seasonal-cold-flu-virus-goes-around-in-your-region-or-community.29693/post-341591>

I am hearing about a lot of people in my area having similar cold/flu type symptoms going around. The nature and characteristics are similar. Do viruses really exist or did we get zapped with EMFs or chemtrails and that is the cause and who gets it depends on each person’s immune system? I don’t seem to find a clear understanding from the blogs so far. I thought I was just run down from changing my circadian rhythm and just not adjusted yet. But then hearing this is going around at the local University and in my town and others describing that people say this one is really “lingering” which is what I’m experiencing after two weeks. A decade ago most people in my area got a horrible cough that lasted for about 3 months and I had it much longer than that. If it’s all about sunshine and your personal emf exposure then that alone doesn’t explain why everyone gets the same type of symptoms at the same time. Are they dumping toxins on us to cause it? Zapping our region with frequencies to cause it? Or is it really a viral strain that transfers through “droplets” that become airborne or passed by doorknobs or other objects? I am seeing sunrise and getting good sunlight time and blocking blue light and have been using nicotine in case it’s vax shedding. I also wonder if this is going around because people are getting flu shots right now and shedding it and infecting other people who don’t get shots? But these “viruses” go around when it’s not flu season too. Just trying to make sense of it. I know the protocols are the best defense but still want to understand what’s really happening here.

### Pratima — 2024-10-27T21:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-really-happening-when-a-seasonal-cold-flu-virus-goes-around-in-your-region-or-community.29693/post-341603>

They must have existed long ago. I grew up next this elderley lady from the UK who was in WW1 and WW2, and she always downed shots of whisky when she had a cold or flu......she said it was the best. I do think they are worse from EMFs though because I had to work in a smart office last year for 4 months and got sick 4 times. Had not been sick for 3 years prior, and have not been sick since.

# Thread: Shielding AC electric fields
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-electric-fields.29698/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### eve001 — 2024-10-28T11:07:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-electric-fields.29698/post-341623>

Hi everyone,

I am looking into a way to shield LF AC electric fields from the floor in my bedroom where RF EMFs are low.
Currently body voltage in bed is about 200mV (formerly 2000mV). We turned off breakers, removed EMI from coax cables etc.
My aim is to further reduce body voltage below 100mV and preferably to 0mV to improve sleep and recovery.
The mean culprit is that we do not have a grounded socket close. And that it is hard to find a certified electrician for small jobs like these.

To ground my LF shielding solution I will either:

\- use a long ground chord (>20m)
\- see if we can use a ground pin outside in the earth

My concern is
\- EMI from LF EMFs in floor when grounding indoors
\- danger in case of thunder when grounding outdoors
\- what kind of ground chord should I use

Some of these concerns may turn out unnecessary. Better safe than sorry.

Variables of possible importance:
\- resistance of the ground in socket
\- resistance/diameter/coating of grounding chord regarding distance from socket/ground
\- needs such a chord be shielded

I am finding some interesting products at various shops in EU.

Like these:
1) [https://www.biologadanell.com/en/p/rubens-light-nf-fleece](https://www.biologadanell.com/en/p/rubens-light-nf-fleece)
\= fiber fleece containing graphite and it says "open to vapour diffusion"
Anyone knows what that means?
2) [https://www.yshield.com/en/yshield-shielding-fleece-ncv95-lf-width-95-cm-1-meter\_167\_1098/](https://www.yshield.com/en/yshield-shielding-fleece-ncv95-lf-width-95-cm-1-meter_167_1098/)

Talking w/ Y-shield recently I learned:
One can opt for materials that either
\- reflect RF EMFs (like metal mesh)
\- do not reflect RF EMFs (item above)

In my case (low levels of RF and no RF shielding) not reflective seems the better option.

I will call some more sellers that will provide some answers.

### eve001 — 2024-10-28T12:52:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-electric-fields.29698/post-341626>

"open to vapour diffusion" (characteristic of some LF shielding fabric/fleece) means air can get through.
\= less likelihood of condensation/humidity/mold.

Regarding an extra grounding rod in the garden:
\- differences in potential could be generated when creating a second ground besides the already existing ground in the house.
\- these differences can worsen things instead of improve them
\- an electrician can measure how deep the grounding rod must go to reach the resistance (Ohm) needed (humidity, ...)

Regarding old coax cables creating LF EMFs and lowering them
\- I was suggested by a professional to bind together the remaining unused coax cables in the basement and connect them to the ground with the mesh of one of them just below the outer coating. And see how that affects LF EMFs.

# Thread: Life's funny way of opening doors, my beginners journal.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifes-funny-way-of-opening-doors-my-beginners-journal.29704/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vonderkyle — 2024-10-28T20:20:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifes-funny-way-of-opening-doors-my-beginners-journal.29704/post-341649>

Hello to all of you from around this big globe, I'm writing to you from just southeast of Seattle, WA, where I've resided for my 36 years.
I apologize in advance as this may ramble on, but I think I need to get some things on paper to process the load of information that I've been collecting on my path to becoming a "mitochondriac" and am looking forward to connecting with others on the mission to better understanding life, and our health.
I came across Jack's work either because of Kennedy, or through discovering Tetragrammaton, or just the collaboration of all of it.
It feels not long ago even if it was almost a year now that RFK Jr and him came together on the pod.
I've always had a innate distrust towards government, institutions, news; the powers that be. Since an early age I've pushed back upon being told what to do (stubborn was an early learned word), and although it is much more honed sense for me now, it's taken a long way to root into my confidence of my intuition and what to push back against and what isn't worth the energy.
COVID was my awakening as it was for many of us. I'm not a academic, or a scientist, or a doctor. That being said, I consider myself very intelligent, regardless of any college degree to show it. All this being said, I had an innate feeling that something just wasn't right with everything that was happening. Maybe it was my natural distrust, suspiciousness or maybe it was what I was consuming and surrounding myself with, but SOMETHING in my gut just said don't do it. I considered myself very lucky to be semi self employed (running a franchise owner-operator business) during these years and wasn't forced to take the shot to maintain my employment. My wife was not as lucky but lucky enough to not have any major side effects from the one shot she did take.
I grew up in a very liberal, democrat supporting family, and to anyone familiar with the Seattle area, and PNW, this will come as no surprise. And of course with this comes a staunch believe and support of the dogma of science. Although there was never much discussion in my family (extended or immediate) of spiritual life, and I would describe most of my family as Atheist leaning, and I in my younger years very much identified that way as well, in result of my upbringing. There wasn't necessarily a hate projected toward religion or spirituality but there was a definite underlying current of those with views other than their dogma of science and evolution. I hope this brings across the atmosphere properly that I was dealing with, leading into the next part of my COVID journey.
In the summer of '21 one of my cousins on my dad's side got married in Durango, CO, where his bride to be had grown up. My wife and I decided to road trip out to it with our little old 18 foot trailer we'd acquired in summer of '20, eager to bring our two pups with and explore more of this vast country. We arrived safe and sound to some very heartbreaking news, my cousins bride to be younger brother had suddenly died before making his trip from South America back home for the wedding, due to some apparent waterborne illness. A glum darkness lingered in the air surrounding the entire weekend and truly expressed the duality of this life that we live, a new life and family starting, and the death of another.
Unfortunately in addition to this came more conflict pertaining to myself, and my family. It was well known in my family I was the only person that had not taken the jab. My family does not have the best communication style, on either my mothers or fathers sides. Throw in my well known stubbornness and tendency in my youth to not take shit from anyone, that often came out in anger, and you've got a recipe for lots of tip toeing with issues. As I arrived and met up with the rest of my immediate family, there was a slow trickle from various people, my parents mostly, but my older brother as well (we'll get to him later) that "someone" had a expressed worry about me not being "vaccinated" and my unwillingness to wear a mask. I never cared enough to figure out which of my two older women cousins or my aunt had expressed this, or if it was just all 3 of them. While they had their reasoning, one cousin had just had a newborn, and my aunt has some immune system issues among other things, the manner that it was brought to me was very triggering for me at the time. I wore a mask despite this, and was very outwardly not happy about it. It was the beginning of the disconnect from much of my family. As we arrived to the venue, it only intensified for me, finding ourselves at a 90% outside wedding, with just drinks and bathrooms being inside. Of course, as explained earlier, I was the only non vaccinated guest, and it was blatantly obvious to me and everyone there, as I was the only attendee wearing a mask. I was humiliated and angry, being put on display for everyone to see and judge. I choked down my anger to put on as good of a face as I could, to not cause any more distraction that I already was, to a day that should have been about my cousin and his bride's new life, that already had so much attention around the death.
Maybe this story will continue later, to not burden anyone's time that is taking their time to read this. I am here, as I would assume most of you, to take action and make change.

I'm ready to dive into reoptimizing myself and my health, after a steady climb of taking ownership of how my body has gotten to the point that it has, which at this point I'm ready to classify as sick/fit.
I've always been very active, playing sports my entire life from the age of 3.5 starting to ski.
No major illness issues to note aside from some major depression in my childhood, that I've been dissecting for the past decade or so, that the more I learn I wonder how much of it was my environment, food, tech (lots of tech abuse thru the years, even though active, spent plenty of time with video games, the internet as it came about), and how much of it was the SSRI assortment that I'd been on from ~6 y/o to 15 y/o, and another stint in my mid 20's before coming off them completely a few years ago.
But.
Major back problems begin in my mid 20's. I link it to one of two occurrences around the same time.
\-I was playing soccer in a co-rec league, jumping up for a header and got undercut by the opposing player, causing me to plant very awkwardly on one of my legs, shocking my spine. I was walking around trying to shake it off and had to shortly after lay back down as the pain set in. I headed home that night on my own somehow, more than likely the remaining adrenaline helping me along. The next morning I had to have my dad come and help me out of bed, could not find a position without which I had pain. We went to the urgent care, and I can't even remember what they did for me, not sure if they even took x-rays.
\-I was t-boned in my driver door of my car exiting work one afternoon, being waived out by the immediate lane that was stopped, saying the other lane was clear. It was not, a young mom and her kid were flying at about 40 MPH down the neighboring lane. We all walked about from the wreck, albeit cars totaled, and I didn't think two bits about it.
The years following I would continue to have my back lock up from time to time, tried chiropractic, PT, acupuncture, eventually an MRI a couple years ago to find a degenerative disc in my L3/L4. I finally found some relief with a PT that found my pinched nerve that most presented when I was locked up, while starting a yoga practice around the same time. Although I'm functional now, I still am susceptible to "throwing it out" if I over do things.
All this said, something is not right. I have plenty of underlying drive but the energy for the follow thru is diminishing year by year. I feel like my brain is running throttled at times. My body should not be as sore as it is at times for how young I still am.

**Couple direct questions**

\-Where to get lab work done? Bare minimum to start with?

\-What's a good Paleo cook book to get (or do I just buck up and pay the Patreon fee for access to Jack's?) to get started? My diet is fairly close already, my wife and I eat seafood nearly every night, lots of veggies, but do a fair amount of rice, pasta from time to time, and some other "naughty" veggies (one stood out was tomatillos, my wife does a lot of tortilla soup with them pretty regularly)

\-I don't have any EMF testing devices, should I, or is it easy enough to just shut things down at night? I've been starting to implement blue blockers at night, and starting to switch a few bulbs over to more circadian friendly lights recently. But we have a ceiling fan we like to run at night in our bedroom, an air purifier on the dresser next to my bedside, my wife charges her phone on her bedside, I keep mine in my bathroom vanity in our attached master bath.

\-WATER? I checked my city testing for chlorine levels (after the latest court ruling for the EPA) and although we were below the "threshold" of 4, I think we were just over 1. I have a basic water filter on our water line we use for filling water bottles, should I look into a higher end, reverse osmosis or something of that sort?

\-How to survive the winter months? I'm way farther north than I'd like to be anymore, especially after starting to digest all this information. But my wife and I are still another year or two from moving further south, and I become more and more aware every year of how the dark months affect me. I've been extra diligent about getting as much sun as possible this spring and summer, and hope my vitamin d levels are at a good place when I get my labs.

\-What information am I missing to get started? I know I've rambled, trying to unload an overdue cornucopia of thoughts that I've been trying to synthesize in my head for years.

I'm here to connect with other like minded individuals, that have been sick of the status quo of "the science" that has been put forth.
[_**This post rings so true for me**_](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/post-196223) **_,_** something I've struggled with being the extrovert I am, and a strong desire to connect with others through any means, and the convenience of the internet, contrasted against the division is has caused, is something I've struggled to grapple with my entire life, growing up with the internet. A long ramble, that hopefully helps explain the difficulty I've had putting this feeling into words.

### Johan Lindøm — 2024-10-28T20:50:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifes-funny-way-of-opening-doors-my-beginners-journal.29704/post-341651>

**Posts:** 10

### Pratima — 2024-10-29T05:58:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341673>

I had an nn-eMF professional come to inspect and test the house. He put a compass on the bed, and said if the dial turned the bed had strong magnetic field or something to that effect. Anyway, apparently the mattress is OK, but I was just wondering if this test has merit or is it a gimmick.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-29T07:04:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341686>

Did they take any readings with professional equipment? What if there was a high magnetic field BUT it just happened to line up with magnetic north? Measure and know. Less than 0.5mG acceptable, above 0.5mG fix it (or move).

### Pratima — 2024-10-29T07:32:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341692>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=341686)

> Did they take any readings with professional equipment? What if there was a high magnetic field BUT it just happened to line up with magnetic north? Measure and know. Less than 0.5mG acceptable, above 0.5mG fix it (or move).

He did seem to have professional equipment in a suit case......He will not be of your calibre though, but I am just rolling with it. He had me lie in bed holding a BVK and I called out the reading when he turned off certain breakers. He is going to write me a spec to give to an electrician to create a switch at the wall which kills the power to the bedroom at night so I dont have to touch the breakers as he said they are not designed to be turned off and on. I was just curious about the use of the compass.

### DLO — 2024-10-29T16:39:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341703>

Funny i know plenty of workshops that use breakers to turn on and off their lights daily.

### sheas — 2024-10-29T19:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341713>

I turned off the breaker to my bedroom to fix that issue. I never turned it back on since we don't need electricity in there

### Pratima — 2024-10-29T20:22:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341714>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=341703)
>
> Funny i know plenty of workshops that use breakers to turn on and off their lights daily.

well thats what he said, and I don't the risk really....

### Pratima — 2024-10-29T20:23:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341715>

> [sastewie said:](/goto/post?id=341713)
>
> I turned off the breaker to my bedroom to fix that issue. I never turned it back on since we don't need electricity in there

the power to the bedrooms also control the laundry which is the issue.......I have the spec now to isolate it all, so once its one, the bedroom can be off permanently.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-29T21:15:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341717>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=341703)
>
> Funny i know plenty of workshops that use breakers to turn on and off their lights daily.

There are “switch rated” breakers that are designed to be switched on and off regularly BUT many in home breakers, especially old breakers, are NOT switch rated which means the mechanical integrity of the breaker can be compromised if turned on and off daily for an extended period of time.

### Mat.I — 2024-10-30T05:49:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341737>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=341715)
>
> the power to the bedrooms also control the laundry which is the issue.......I have the spec now to isolate it all, so once its one, the bedroom can be off permanently.

You can also look at demand switches e.g. [https://gigahertz-solutions.com/Demand-Switches/Devices](https://gigahertz-solutions.com/Demand-Switches/Devices)

Also, due to your location you would require one that is approved to AS/NZS 3000 (electrical standard in AUS & NZ). There are a few suppliers in AUS if you search for them.

### Mat.I — 2024-10-30T05:56:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compass-to-test-emf-in-matrresses.29707/post-341738>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=341715)
>
> the power to the bedrooms also control the laundry which is the issue.......I have the spec now to isolate it all, so once its one, the bedroom can be off permanently.

You could also split the circuit and back feed to a separate CB for the laundry (depending if laundry is at the end of line) if not you could disconnect the laundry power circuit take off and run a new circuit just for the laundry or do this just for the bedrooms (subject to the configuration).

GWEN TOWERS

GWEN (Ground Wave Emergency Network) allows specific frequencies to be tailored to the geomagnetic-field strength in each area, allowing the magnetic field to be altered.

They are connected to HAARP, smart meters, cell phones, WIFI etc

"GWEN (Ground Wave Emergency Network) transmitters, placed 200 miles apart across the USA, allow specific frequencies to be tailored to
the geomagnetic-field strength in each area, allowing the magnetic
field to be altered.

They operate in the VLF range, with
transmissions between VLF 150 and 175 KHz. They also emit UHF waves
of 225 – 400 MHz.

The VLF signals travel by waves that hug the ground rather than radiate into the atmosphere. A GWEN station
transmits up to a 300-mile radius, the signal dropping off sharply
over distance.

The entire GWEN system consists of, (depending on source of data), from 58 to an intended 300 transmitters, spread across the USA, each with a tower 299-500 ft high.

Three hundred (300) ft. of copper wire fans out in a spoke like fashion from the base of the underground system, interacting with the earth like a thin shelled conductor, radiating radio wave energy for very long distances through the ground.

The USA bathes in this magnetic field
which rises to 500 ft, even going down to basements, so everyone is
subject to mind control.

The whole artificial ground wave spreads out over USA like a web. It is easier to mind-control and hypnotize
people who are bathed in an artificial electromagnetic wave."

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-when-you-thought-it-was-safe-to-ground.29711/post-341748>

Wiki suggests that the partially built GWEN network was shut down thirty years ago. Do you have other evidence?

### [AN/URC-117 Ground Wave Emergency Network - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AN/URC-117_Ground_Wave_Emergency_Network)

 en.wikipedia.org

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-30T18:34:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-when-you-thought-it-was-safe-to-ground.29711/post-341756>

The info I can find says they built 58 of the intended 250 but the program was defunded in 1994 because of the interference it was causing other systems. A few were later converted for GPS use.

# Thread: ChatGPT Locations for Healing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chatgpt-locations-for-healing.29722/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorJamesTJ — 2024-10-30T22:50:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chatgpt-locations-for-healing.29722/post-341769>

I asked ChatGPT what places would be best for me to visit in Mexico and Central America from Las Vegas based on Jack Kruse's principles. This is what it said:

Dr. Jack Kruse's perspective emphasizes areas with strong natural magnetism, low non-native electromagnetic fields (nnEMF), and ample sunlight by the ocean for optimal circadian health and grounding. Based on those criteria, here are a few places in Mexico and Central America that could fit well:

**_Puerto Escondido, Oaxaca, Mexico_**
Magnetism: The area is known for its rugged, volcanic geology, which can contribute to a high natural magnetic field.
EMF: Puerto Escondido remains relatively low in nnEMF exposure, especially as it’s not heavily urbanized.
Healing Environment: This town is rich in sunlight and vitamin D, making it ideal for resetting circadian rhythms.
Flight: From Las Vegas, a connection through Mexico City makes it accessible.

**_Tulum, Quintana Roo, Mexico_**
Magnetism: The cenotes (natural limestone sinkholes) and high mineral concentration could provide unique grounding benefits.
EMF: While Tulum has grown in popularity, areas slightly outside the main tourist zone, like Tankah Bay or the Sian Ka'an Biosphere, offer lower nnEMF levels.
Healing Environment: Tulum offers a tropical climate and high sunlight exposure for a healthy circadian reset.
Flight: Cancun International Airport is a direct flight from Las Vegas; Tulum is a 1.5-hour drive from there.

**_Nosara, Costa Rica_**
Magnetism: Known for its rich, volcanic soils and unspoiled beaches, Nosara has strong grounding potential.
EMF: This coastal area limits high-rise development and infrastructure, maintaining lower nnEMF levels.
Healing Environment: With both beach and forest, Nosara provides powerful natural elements for detoxing and circadian health.
Flight: While there’s no direct flight, a transfer in San Jose, Costa Rica, or Liberia, Costa Rica, makes it manageable.

**_San Pancho (San Francisco), Nayarit, Mexico_**
Magnetism: The area’s natural beauty and proximity to magnetic regions like Puerto Vallarta make it grounding-friendly.
EMF: San Pancho remains less developed, with lower EMF exposure than more urbanized areas.
Healing Environment: It’s known for yoga and wellness retreats, with an abundance of sunlight for circadian benefits.
Flight: A flight to Puerto Vallarta (direct from Las Vegas) and a short drive make it easy to reach.
Each of these places provides proximity to beaches, potential natural magnetic fields, ample sunlight, and controlled nnEMF exposure, balancing Dr. Kruse's priorities for healing.

**_Isla Holbox, Quintana Roo, Mexico_**
Magnetism: Holbox has mineral-rich waters and grounding environments, situated along the Gulf of Mexico and Caribbean Sea convergence.
EMF: Since cars are restricted on the island, nnEMF from vehicles and infrastructure is minimal.
Healing Environment: Pristine beaches, low-impact tourism, and lots of sun make Holbox great for circadian health and stress reduction.
Flight: Fly to Cancun, then transfer by car and ferry (approx. 2.5-hour trip).

**_Punta Mita, Nayarit, Mexico_**
Magnetism: Volcanic landforms in the area may contribute to natural magnetic properties and provide grounding benefits.
EMF: Punta Mita remains lower in nnEMF than major cities, especially near private and less densely populated beach areas.
Healing Environment: Known for luxury wellness resorts and spas, Punta Mita offers abundant sun exposure and pristine beaches.
Flight: A direct flight to Puerto Vallarta from Las Vegas, then a 45-minute drive.

**_Bocas del Toro, Panama_**
Magnetism: The volcanic islands and mineral-rich sands provide grounding potential that aligns with natural magnetism principles.
EMF: Bocas del Toro has minimal infrastructure and maintains low nnEMF levels, especially on smaller, more remote islands.
Healing Environment: The region is abundant in sunlight and nature immersion, ideal for reducing stress and syncing circadian rhythms.
Flight: A flight to Panama City with a connection to Bocas Town makes it accessible (though longer).

**_Mazunte, Oaxaca, Mexico_**
Magnetism: The coastal geology with cliffs and hills provides a grounding atmosphere and magnetic appeal.
EMF: A small fishing town with minimal nnEMF interference, Mazunte remains calm and ideal for nature immersion.
Healing Environment: Known for its eco-conscious community and yoga retreats, Mazunte offers powerful sunlight and low light pollution, enhancing circadian health.
Flight: Fly into Huatulco or Puerto Escondido and take a short drive to Mazunte.

**_Ambergris Caye, Belize_**
Magnetism: Close to the Belize Barrier Reef, Ambergris Caye offers mineral-rich waters and grounding energy.
EMF: Although it's a popular destination, its small size and less-developed infrastructure keep nnEMF lower than in big cities.
Healing Environment: Sunlight and tranquil beaches support circadian health, with ample opportunities for stress reduction.
Flight: Flights to Belize City and a short connection to Ambergris Caye make it accessible.
Each of these destinations combines natural magnetism, a low nnEMF footprint, and a healing environment that prioritizes sun exposure, grounding, and circadian rhythm support.

Could be worth plugging that prompt in for yourself. This is what I used, "I am trying to get to places in Mexico or Central America that are good for healing. I want you from the viewpoint of Dr Jack Kruse, looking at what places have great magnetism, by a beach, not too much nnEMF, and with the best flights from Las Vegas, tell me where I should go."

### coho3131 — 2024-10-31T00:36:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chatgpt-locations-for-healing.29722/post-341771>

I find that ChatGPT and other AI searches contain significant amounts of inaccurate and heavily biased content.

You could test it yourself by searching a topic area for which you have extensive knowledge.

# Thread: Mitochondriac Careers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-careers.29741/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ajohnson90 — 2024-11-03T19:34:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-careers.29741/post-341901>

I’m curious what all yall do for work? As most of our jobs are indoors and sedentary, and that’s where we spend most of our time, it seems important to pick a job that builds our health up, not breaks it down.

I made a change from working in an office to being a mail carrier so I could be outside and walking all day- that said it’s not fulfilling on a personal level or a financial level and I need to make a change.

### Adamo — 2024-11-03T20:03:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-careers.29741/post-341902>

I hear you 100% regarding your focus, changes and wanting to make improvements/growth.
I have had various "careers" and "jobs." Still working towards configuring the "ideal" or "dream" job/position.

### ajohnson90 — 2024-11-03T20:18:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-careers.29741/post-341903>

> [Adamo said:](/goto/post?id=341902)
>
> I hear you 100% regarding your focus, changes and wanting to make improvements/growth.
> I have had various "careers" and "jobs." Still working towards configuring the "ideal" or "dream" job/position.

I have my “dream job”, which is teaching movement. Im working towards it but it’s not my sole source of income, and im struggling with health issues that make it difficult to teach movement. Hence why im here.

Yet there are things to decide about what that looks like as well- ie how do I teach and work in a way that’s closest to nature?

I think it can be more difficult to know what you want to be doing, to have that dream and passion, and not be doing it, as opposed to searching for it, in my experience.

### Adamo — 2024-11-03T21:08:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-careers.29741/post-341904>

Roger that.

Hope that you recover soon, brother. Health is #1.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-11-03T22:01:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-careers.29741/post-341906>

Performance coach, live where I want, work how I want, run my calls off my laptop in full spectrum sunlight. Internet allows truly driven people to turn niche interests into a life and free themselves. My YT is IronWillExecutivePerformance and you can see how I did it. Left a career in project management, that paid a lot more than this, to be fucking free

### sindre — 2024-11-13T20:51:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-careers.29741/post-342316>

Seems to me the only solution is to have a job where the office is at home. Then you can work outside. It might not be a very social job, but it is certainly healthier. An IT type job fits this.

I work from home as a full time translator for an agency, but I want to switch to programmer for better pay and hopefully something more interesting.

I have previously thought that mailman is the ideal job here in Norway, but they are driving electrical cars getting irradiated ...

### sheas — 2024-11-14T06:00:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-careers.29741/post-342349>

I work in the horse industry. Fit saddles that I sell, and also am a craniosacral therapist. Human trained took a 2 year advanced course, but chose to stay in the horse world so I could work outside even before I came across Dr. Kruse. Plus I enjoy working on them, they respond well. I also teach equine craniosacral classes which has been fun. And also teach some online classes. The only downside is driving to appointments. I still work on people if we can be outside. So I set up my table in a pasture under a tree

# Thread: Getting Sun in Cold Climate & Full-Body Sunning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-sun-in-cold-climate-full-body-sunning.29744/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### UncensoringSoftwareDev — 2024-11-04T00:20:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-sun-in-cold-climate-full-body-sunning.29744/post-341908>

Hi,
I'm new here & wondering what suggestions everyone has for getting adequate sunlight (& grounding) in cold climates. In winter, it's hard for me & my family to get enough sun, & nearly impossible to ground naturally due to the cold and snow.
I've thought of making something like a walled gazebo where part of the roof can open to let in enough sunlight for full-body tanning. I could maybe put a heater in there as well so I can last for at least a few minutes before getting cold since exposing any amount of skin to the sun (especially with full-body sunning) will get cold really fast.
And the cold aside, are there any relatively inexpensive products that can be purchased that are similar to my walled gazebo with an opening roof section so that I can do full-body sunning without anyone knowing I'm doing that & without looking conspicuous?
Thanks.

### coho3131 — 2024-11-04T01:18:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-sun-in-cold-climate-full-body-sunning.29744/post-341912>

I walk on snow with bare feet. I lay down in the snow with just shorts.

I sunbathe naked all winter using a privacy panel. (I'm lucky I only have one neighbor that is close enough to see.)

You can keep your tan skin all winter by exposing your skin at the Peak UV Index using the reflection off snow, ice, or water.

I would suggest reading the cold thermogenesis blogs.

You might get criticized severely in the Forum if you use "being cold" or "impossible conditions" as excuses for not spending time outside in the winter.

If you have significant health conditions, you might be better off going close to the Equator in the winter.

### Woodstock — 2024-11-05T08:56:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-sun-in-cold-climate-full-body-sunning.29744/post-341954>

> [UncensoringSoftwareDev said:](/goto/post?id=341908)
>
> Hi,
> I'm new here & wondering what suggestions everyone has for getting adequate sunlight (& grounding) in cold climates. In winter, it's hard for me & my family to get enough sun, & nearly impossible to ground naturally due to the cold and snow.
> I've thought of making something like a walled gazebo where part of the roof can open to let in enough sunlight for full-body tanning. I could maybe put a heater in there as well so I can last for at least a few minutes before getting cold since exposing any amount of skin to the sun (especially with full-body sunning) will get cold really fast.
> And the cold aside, are there any relatively inexpensive products that can be purchased that are similar to my walled gazebo with an opening roof section so that I can do full-body sunning without anyone knowing I'm doing that & without looking conspicuous?
> Thanks.

I’m new here too and I picked up a few ideas about it which inspired me to purchase an inflatable hot tub Which I put on my concrete patio that gets sun at peak hours. So far I have only used hydrogen peroxide to keep it sterilized and I will probably drain it every 1-2 weeks and Refill with fresh water. It has been awesome because I can wear a bathing suit and not worry about neighbors and it’s very relaxing. I got an ice pack because I read about using that on the torso while having your lower body in warm water as a method for cold thermogenesis. Maybe cold plunge is ideal but I’m not adapted to that yet. I was a bit hesitant to do this as it requires proper maintenance and just one more thing to manage in my life, but so far the benefits far outweigh the effort. Getting a great tan in November!

### UlliJoanna — 2024-11-05T09:49:13+0200

### coho3131 — 2024-11-06T01:29:05+0200

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=341954)
>
> I’m new here too and I picked up a few ideas about it which inspired me to purchase an inflatable hot tub Which I put on my concrete patio that gets sun at peak hours. So far I have only used hydrogen peroxide to keep it sterilized and I will probably drain it every 1-2 weeks and Refill with fresh water. It has been awesome because I can wear a bathing suit and not worry about neighbors and it’s very relaxing. I got an ice pack because I read about using that on the torso while having your lower body in warm water as a method for cold thermogenesis. Maybe cold plunge is ideal but I’m not adapted to that yet. I was a bit hesitant to do this as it requires proper maintenance and just one more thing to manage in my life, but so far the benefits far outweigh the effort. Getting a great tan in November!

What are you trying to eliminate with the hydrogen peroxide?

I wonder if bathing in acidic solution is worse for you than anything that might be living in the water.

I just fill mine past the rim for at least a few minutes to remove the surface layer of water before I use it.

The small amount of algae that grows on the walls does not bother me outside of needing to be careful if it is slippery.

# Thread: Incandescent NIR vs LED NIR
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-nir-vs-led-nir.29755/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Annakarenina — 2024-11-06T13:35:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-nir-vs-led-nir.29755/post-342008>

Can someone please advise me, I have been searching and searching without luck to find a definative answer on the above.

As I understand it LED lights emit blue light which emit short (400-450 nm) wave lengths which is harmful as it destroys cells and and has negative impact on eyes whilst incandescent emit longer wavelengths (500-700nm) which helps the body repair cells and gives energy.

Which makes me want to understand why LED NIR is being recommended over incandescent NIR. Does anyone have any information on this. I have been switching my lighting over from LED to incandescent in the house and am about to buy NIR unit but want to make the best choice!

### Ozzy1 — 2024-11-06T16:50:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-nir-vs-led-nir.29755/post-342011>

Just get incandescent. It's the closest to sunlight, you don't have to faff about worrying it there's flicker etc. incandescent have a bit of everything light wise. Light is problematic when you have them in isolation. You want to be as close to full spectrum light as possible when outside the sun. You can get red tinted party bulbs if you want it more red as night, or use fire. I think that's what dr kruse does.

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proposal-winter-ct-2024-25-reading-discussing-the-ct-blogs-shared-learning-experience.29757/>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proposal-winter-ct-2024-25-reading-discussing-the-ct-blogs-shared-learning-experience.29757/post-342036>

Hell-o everyone from 50L and +-10°C at 11AM.

This is my first autumn embracing cold.
I dove into Dr. Kruse's first CT blogs and the CT chapter in his book the past days.
We don't want frostbite, do we?

I am inviting the newbies and eternal students like me to join up online to discuss our reading/understanding and share our experience/best/worst practices.

My idea:
\-To gather online once weekly at a set CET day + daytime + hour.
\-To have read the blog beforehand and share our insights, doubts, inspiration.
\-Everybody will be given the chance to talk should they wish so - circle style.
\-I will be the moderator, at least for starting.
\-Duration 1h-1,5h max.
\- Start: asap.

You will need:
\- internet access - wired if possible - a functioning microphone and camera - enthusiasm about shared=enhanced learning.

This seems FUN to me = An heremite against her will bc of nnEMF overload.
OF COURSE real life interaction is ALWAYS preferable.

Does this resonate w/ you? Please, tell me!
I am thinking still of a suited time. Share your availability. In comments or pm.
To me weekdays early PM are as good as weekends. More of you like me?
May share a doodle to decide on this.

Regarding the platform: I used google meet in the past and jitsy, don't own a zoom account, so any suggestions welcome.
If enough interested (at least 4 to 5 of you + me) I will organize our 1st group video call.

Have a great day.

Eve

### sunny_sardine — 2024-12-10T19:03:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proposal-winter-ct-2024-25-reading-discussing-the-ct-blogs-shared-learning-experience.29757/post-343644>

> [AM\_in\_CAN said:](/goto/post?id=342712)
>
> Has anyone seen or read anything that indicates the progressive beneift (if any) one can expect by submerging in colder waters for same amount of time? I;m wodering if there's some relationship that maybe equates some time in water temp X is equivalent to colder water temp for less time.

Hi,
below is a summary by Consensus (https://consensus.app); I paraphrased your first question

Submerging in progressively colder waters can offer several physiological benefits, particularly related to muscle recovery and circulation. Here are the key benefits:

**Muscle Recovery**

Reduced Muscle Damage: Cold water immersion is known to alleviate exercise-induced muscle damage. This is partly due to reduced muscle blood flow, which is more pronounced in colder water, potentially aiding in recovery and injury rehabilitation (Gregson et al., 2011).

**Circulatory and Vascular Effects**

Blood Flow Redistribution: Immersion in colder water results in a redistribution of blood flow, with more blood being directed to the skin rather than muscles. This can help in reducing muscle inflammation and soreness (Gregson et al., 2011).
Vasoconstriction and Blood Flow: Colder water causes less vasoconstriction in the skin compared to slightly warmer water, which might help in maintaining better skin blood flow while still reducing muscle blood flow (Gregson et al., 2011).

**Core Temperature and Heat Loss**

Core Cooling: Submersion in cold water, especially with the head submerged, increases core cooling significantly. This is due to the stimulation of thermosensitive receptors, which can lead to a redistribution of blood flow and a more efficient cooling effect (Pretorius et al., 2006).
Heat Loss Management: While the head does not contribute more to surface heat loss than the rest of the body, the overall cooling effect is greater due to the body's response to cold water (Pretorius et al., 2006).

**General Health Benefits**

Health Benefits with Caution: Regular, graded exposure to cold water can bring health benefits, such as improved circulation and potential stress relief, when practiced safely by experienced individuals (Knechtle et al., 2020).

**Conclusion**

Submerging in progressively colder waters can enhance muscle recovery by reducing muscle blood flow and inflammation. It also affects blood flow distribution and core cooling, which can be beneficial for recovery and overall health when done safely. However, it is important to approach cold water immersion with caution, especially for those unfamiliar with the practice.

These papers were sourced and synthesized using Consensus, an AI-powered search engine for research. Try it at [https://consensus.app](https://consensus.app)

References

Gregson, W., Black, M., Jones, H., Milson, J., Morton, J., Dawson, B., Atkinson, G., & Green, D. (2011). Influence of Cold Water Immersion on Limb and Cutaneous Blood Flow at Rest. The American Journal of Sports Medicine, 39, 1316 - 1323. [https://doi.org/10.1177/0363546510395497](https://doi.org/10.1177/0363546510395497)

Knechtle, B., Waśkiewicz, Z., Sousa, C., Hill, L., & Nikolaidis, P. (2020). Cold Water Swimming—Benefits and Risks: A Narrative Review. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 17. [https://doi.org/10.3390/ijerph17238984](https://doi.org/10.3390/ijerph17238984)

Pretorius, T., Bristow, G., Steinman, A., & Giesbrecht, G. (2006). Thermal effects of whole head submersion in cold water on nonshivering humans.. Journal of applied physiology, 101 2, 669-75. [https://doi.org/10.1152/JAPPLPHYSIOL.01241.2005](https://doi.org/10.1152/JAPPLPHYSIOL.01241.2005)

# Thread: CT for Babies / Toddlers / Children
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-babies-toddlers-children.29765/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### robtherhino — 2024-11-08T12:42:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-babies-toddlers-children.29765/post-342068>

Would love some input on CT for babies, toddlers, and children.

I live at N47, so by early November until mid February we have no UVB and temperatures drop to 0°C and below.
This makes grounding and full sunlight exposure for my son a challenge (currently 18 months).

I've noticed that his tan is greatly diminished already, while mine is almost as strong as in summer (have been doing CT daily).

There is lots of differing information out there on CT for young children.
They have more BAT, they are less able to thermoregulate, etc. etc.

My first principles thinking is that they have the same biology as adults, so in theory, it would be possible for them.
But obviously milder exposure.

Can anyone shed some light on this topic?

Is there an adapted protocol for this age group?

### robtherhino — 2024-11-10T14:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-babies-toddlers-children.29765/post-342160>

Bumping this up in case someone has input.

### Mat.I — 2024-11-11T02:31:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-babies-toddlers-children.29765/post-342184>

Re: CT in water - My kids 4 & 6 go in voluntarily (they ask) as they see my routine every morning. When they have had enough they get out. It may be only 3 to 5 mins when below 10 C or they will happily play in the plunge (if warmer say ~ 15 C) for 30mins or so and and get out warm up and go back in throughout the day.. They never shiver uncontrollably and I have never encouraged them to go in they just like doing in on their own free will.

Re: CT via air exposure - in winter our kids dress very lightly and see how long they can last outside with frost on the ground (barefoot) (at sunrise) it is a bit of a game for them.

I personally, wouldn't do any CT via water for younger kids. I think a reasonable approach is to dress them lightly, however monitor feed back closely with how they are going and add layers / warmth as necessary then build on that over time (keep it very gentle though & don't forget they are only kids).

If you want your kids to ground and it is too cold to directly do this, I guess it would still be effective if you held your kids whilst barefoot / minimally clothed for maximum contact or utilise grounded shoes for a layer of protection..

### robtherhino — 2024-11-11T20:09:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-babies-toddlers-children.29765/post-342209>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=342184)
>
> Re: CT in water - My kids 4 & 6 go in voluntarily (they ask) as they see my routine every morning. When they have had enough they get out. It may be only 3 to 5 mins when below 10 C or they will happily play in the plunge (if warmer say ~ 15 C) for 30mins or so and and get out warm up and go back in throughout the day.. They never shiver uncontrollably and I have never encouraged them to go in they just like doing in on their own free will.
>
> Re: CT via air exposure - in winter our kids dress very lightly and see how long they can last outside with frost on the ground (barefoot) (at sunrise) it is a bit of a game for them.
>
> I personally, wouldn't do any CT via water for younger kids. I think a reasonable approach is to dress them lightly, however monitor feed back closely with how they are going and add layers / warmth as necessary then build on that over time (keep it very gentle though & don't forget they are only kids).
>
> If you want your kids to ground and it is too cold to directly do this, I guess it would still be effective if you held your kids whilst barefoot / minimally clothed for maximum contact or utilise grounded shoes for a layer of protection..

Thanks Mat! Very reasonable approach.

### Jessnet3 — 2024-12-02T16:12:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-neutralizers.29774/post-343168>

>

>
> It does not work! There is no such thing as “harmonizing”
>
> DO NOT make them your first line of defence.

I emailed Blushield in an attempt to better understand their product, especially since scalar waves are theoretical and unable to be measured. This was their response:

“Thank you for your interest and for reaching out. You're right, there are currently no devices we are aware of that measure can scalar fields. The most direct way to see how Blushield's scalar field is working to protect you from EMFs is by observing and measuring biological effects. Many people feel a difference immediately, or within the first couple of weeks. The differences can be subtle, or more dramatic in some cases. Some people feel nothing at all, because the changes are happening at the cell level and many people can't feel that tangibly, similar to how most people can't tangibly feel the harmful effects of EMFs.

Some of our customers who are less sensitive to the perceived effects of healthy changes they make choose to do before/after tests on themselves using Blushield. In these situations, they will usually find a practitioner who does live blood cell analysis, or some other kind of testing. Some of our customers instead use a health measurement device
called an Oura Ring, which measures and gives them feedback in their sleep cycles, heart rate variability, and other health indicators, and this is a way to "test" the effects of Blushield at home on bodily systems that you're not likely to be able to feel tangible changes with.

Most people will not want to do tests like these, which is why we have done various [human and animal tests before and after using Blushield](https://blushield.com/pages/testing). Our most recent one is an independent review board certified clinical study on 25 adults, which showed statistically significant improvements in several areas related to health and well being. You can read more about our recent study here:

[https://blushield.com/blogs/education/clinical-pilot-study-on-blushield-efficacy-results-are-in](https://blushield.com/blogs/education/clinical-pilot-study-on-blushield-efficacy-results-are-in)

This is how Blushield produces a scalar field: the multiple waveform algorithm (based on natural frequency patterns) is programmed into the microprocessor, the same way information is programmed into computer microprocessors. This microprocessor connects to an LED panel; LEDs are a semiconductor light source that produces photons. Photons can produce scalar fields if they're used in a specific way. The combination of these components is what generates the scalar wave that "carries" the frequency algorithm that our body responds to. This technology was created by a group of engineers over decades of research and experimentation, building on the work that Tesla and others did with scalar fields.

If you want to do some more research on how this is possible, you will want to look into a term called phase conjugation.

It is a term used in nonlinear optics, and is another way of describing a scalar field. LEDs can pump nonlinear optical crystals that can generate phase conjugate waves through nonlinear interactions like four-wave mixing or second-harmonic generation. Nonlinear crystals or photorefractive materials can be used in this context. Basically, using techniques to enhance the coherence of LEDs can allow them to be used to create phase conjugate / scalar fields.

Let me know if you have any more questions!”

Immediately my haunches went up for a number of reasons, notably suggesting an emf device to measure biological effects!

The reality is these frequencies will be soon largely inescapable wherever you live, which is why the light environment is obviously imperative.

Very helpful to have confirmed what I originally suspected in any case, thank you.

> called an Oura Ring, which measures and gives them feedback in their sleep cycles, heart rate variability, and other health indicators, and this is a way to "test" the effects of Blushield at home on bodily systems that you're not likely to be able to feel tangible changes with.
>
> Most people will not want to do tests like these, which is why we have done various [human and animal tests before and after using Blushield](https://blushield.com/pages/testing). Our most recent one is an independent review board certified clinical study on 25 adults, which showed statistically significant improvements in several areas related to health and well being. You can read more about our recent study here:
>
> [https://blushield.com/blogs/education/clinical-pilot-study-on-blushield-efficacy-results-are-in](https://blushield.com/blogs/education/clinical-pilot-study-on-blushield-efficacy-results-are-in)
>
> This is how Blushield produces a scalar field: the multiple waveform algorithm (based on natural frequency patterns) is programmed into the microprocessor, the same way information is programmed into computer microprocessors. This microprocessor connects to an LED panel; LEDs are a semiconductor light source that produces photons. Photons can produce scalar fields if they're used in a specific way. The combination of these components is what generates the scalar wave that "carries" the frequency algorithm that our body responds to. This technology was created by a group of engineers over decades of research and experimentation, building on the work that Tesla and others did with scalar fields.
>
> If you want to do some more research on how this is possible, you will want to look into a term called phase conjugation.
>
> It is a term used in nonlinear optics, and is another way of describing a scalar field. LEDs can pump nonlinear optical crystals that can generate phase conjugate waves through nonlinear interactions like four-wave mixing or second-harmonic generation. Nonlinear crystals or photorefractive materials can be used in this context. Basically, using techniques to enhance the coherence of LEDs can allow them to be used to create phase conjugate / scalar fields.
>
> Let me know if you have any more questions!”
>
> Immediately my haunches went up for a number of reasons, notably suggesting an emf device to measure biological effects!
>
> The reality is these frequencies will be soon largely inescapable wherever you live, which is why the light environment is obviously imperative.
>
> Very helpful to have confirmed what I originally suspected in any case, thank you.

Hey Jess,

Your hunch was correct.

I had clients years ago who spent an obscene amount of money on Blushield as it was recommended in a book by an Australian entrepreneur, so i emailed him and his response was telling, he retracted the recommendation.

Here is part of his response.

"In this book I recommend a technology that at the time showed tremendous promise for protection against nnEMF.

I recommended Blushield as an option of last resort in my hierarchy of control strategy (Chapter 10).

Many readers decided to adopt this technology as the sole option for protection.

While Blushield may offer short term relief for some users it is not a long term mitigation solution.

We now know chronic exposure to artificial standing waves (also known as scalar waves) used by Blushield may actually be deleterious to mitochondrial function.

Blushield tech is based on NASA technology used for astronauts to compensate for lack of connection to Mother Earth and her healing frequencies (Schuman resonance).

A cheaper and more effective strategy to access the healing frequencies of Mother Earth is to get outside and walk or stand barefoot on the ground.

No technology solution can ever compete with nature.

Anyone thinking you can live in a high nnEMF environment and use an artificial technology like Blushield to protect themselves or their family is at risk of developing chronic illness.

I made a mistake by recommending this tech solution in this book."

# Thread: PSA: Android 15 Update - Blue Tooth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psa-android-15-update-blue-tooth.29787/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rtd — 2024-11-12T16:06:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psa-android-15-update-blue-tooth.29787/post-342249>

My phone updated to latest Android OS update (15.X). Prior to the update, blue tooth was turned off. Post update, it was turned on. So I used the quick button to it turn off. Next day it was on. I opened the full menu, there is a new blue tooth "feature" that defaults on= "turn on blue tooth tomorrow". I had to disable it. Now it is off off.

Thanks for this. I read it. My brain hurts. I will read it again. There are some profound implications here.
Rusty - I get that “polarized” nnEMF fields/signals can compound and compound/exacerbate their damage
BUT am I right in understanding some fields can combine and negate each other?

### Rusty — 2024-11-16T11:15:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-polarized-light-affect-us-negatively.29803/post-342436>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=342429)
>
> Thanks for this. I read it. My brain hurts. I will read it again. There are some profound implications here.
> Rusty - I get that “polarized” nnEMF fields/signals can compound and compound/exacerbate their damage
> BUT am I right in understanding some fields can combine and negate each other?

Yes, you are correct.

We can have Constructive Interference & Destructive interference.

Constructive Interference will result in the amplification of the signal/field & Destructive interference can result in partial or complete cancellation of the signal/field similar to how noise cancellation works.

From what I understand, Dimitri argues that Man-made EMFs tend to create more constructive interference (amplification) than destructive interference (cancellation) because they are typically designed to emit

\- consistent signals,
\- operate at specific frequencies,
\- maintain stable phase relationships (peaks consistently align with peaks),
\- use coordinated timing (creates predictable patterns of overlap)

Natural EMF is more random whereas nnEMF is more coordinated which creates hotspots where signals amplify.

These hotspots **aren't just stronger signals**, they can force charged molecules and ions in our cells to oscillate in parallel & disrupt their functioning.

When I was referring to 10Hz beacon signal,

I was talking about the 10Hz pulsed signal that the router uses to announce its presence & allows devices to discover and connect to the network. Wi-Fi beacons are transmitted at intervals of 100 milliseconds, so that's 10 cycles every second. This pulsing signal is short and periodic.

This is in addition to the continuous 2.45 & 5 GHz modulated RF radiation (now 6 GHz too)

That **10Hz _Pulsed_ RF beacon** is my concern.

# Thread: Benefits of Chiropractic Care? Depends on Your Problem
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefits-of-chiropractic-care-depends-on-your-problem.29815/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BruceKing — 2024-11-16T20:53:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefits-of-chiropractic-care-depends-on-your-problem.29815/post-342450>

Probably one of the main benefits of chiropractic care is that it helps keep people off opiate drugs for neck and back pain. According to the CDC, prescription opiate deaths were 10,516 for 2022. How many people do chiropractors kill a year? I could not find an accurate statistic for this. However, the last stat I heard by word of mouth was approximately 100 deaths in the last one hundred years and half of the "adjustments" were not even given by chiropractors.
Of course, the benefit of chiropractic depends on your problem. I saw up close one case where a birth trauma baby had lost its ability to track a finger moving horizontally across its field of vision (it was old enough that it should have been able to track a visual stimulus). The baby had been forcibly removed from its mother's vagina with forceps and had received severe cervical (neck) trauma. The C1 vertebrae was extremely misaligned. After a few simple and safe cervical adjustments over a short period to the C1 vertebra the baby was able to track a finger moving across its field of vision. How is this possible? The medulla oblongata (MO) is situated at the C1 level of the spine. The MO contains the nucleus prepositus hypoglossi (NPH) which is responsible for integration of velocity-to-position signals for horizontal eye movements. Pressure on the MO and thus NPH from extreme lateral misalignment of C1 was probably the main causative factor. Evidence for this can be seen in the effectiveness of external normalizing of the C1 vertebrae with chiropractic adjustment and the rapid recovery of the baby's eye tracking problem. Kind of amazing that simply pushing a baby's vertebrae back into position with a fingertip helped it heal.
On the other hand, chiropractic is pretty ineffective for other issues. I caught a patient with a classic case of angina (heart/chest pain) as I was adjusting him and told him to immediately go to his cardiologist. He just shrugged and said that he had just been to his cardiologist last week and got a clean bill of health. I then told him he needed to go to an emergency room. He blew me off and was dead a week later from a heart attack.
So, what are the benefits of chiropractic? Depends on your problem. However, the fact that people typically see their chiropractors more often than their medical doctors gives chiropractors the opportunity to catch issues and refer appropriately - before it is too late.

### Kpack12 — 2024-11-17T06:12:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefits-of-chiropractic-care-depends-on-your-problem.29815/post-342462>

I have hugely benefitted from chiropractic care, especially upper cervical adjustments (NUCCA). I was a competitive gymnast when I was 9-12 years old and it really did a number on my head and neck. When my chiropractor first adjusted my atlas, I felt a surge of energy go from my coccyx to my forehead. Apparently all those falls I took in gymnastics had totally locked down my occiput and she opened it up. It's been really fascinating to see how my body has also straightened out over the years that I've been having my atlas adjusted. I used to have a really low shoulder and rotated hips and that have almost completely corrected.

### BruceKing — 2024-11-17T14:12:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefits-of-chiropractic-care-depends-on-your-problem.29815/post-342473>

> [Kpack12 said:](/goto/post?id=342462)
>
> I have hugely benefitted from chiropractic care, especially upper cervical adjustments (NUCCA). I was a competitive gymnast when I was 9-12 years old and it really did a number on my head and neck. When my chiropractor first adjusted my atlas, I felt a surge of energy go from my coccyx to my forehead. Apparently all those falls I took in gymnastics had totally locked down my occiput and she opened it up. It's been really fascinating to see how my body has also straightened out over the years that I've been having my atlas adjusted. I used to have a really low shoulder and rotated hips and that have almost completely corrected.

That is awesome! Nothing like direct experience.

### BruceKing — 2024-11-17T17:31:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefits-of-chiropractic-care-depends-on-your-problem.29815/post-342495>

Also, you might enjoy some fairly balanced research information on the effects of chiropractic spinal subluxation and the effects of spinal manipulative therapy. More research is definitely needed but hey, all healthcare is an evolving science (thank god).
