# Thread: Orthodontic advice for three children with many issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/orthodontic-advice-for-three-children-with-many-issues.29825/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gian — 2024-11-18T04:05:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/orthodontic-advice-for-three-children-with-many-issues.29825/post-342547>

Hello, black cygnet here new to the "way of Dr. Jack Kruse" and new member to this forum. Coincidently, about the same time my wife and I learned of Dr. Kruse and his amazing knowledge/teachings, our children had their first orthodontist appointments. We are hoping to get some advice regarding orthodontics for our children. I know that Dr. Kruse is quite skeptical of orthodontics (we listened to an incredibly interesting podcast episode with Uncle Jack and a dentist) and it sounded like Dr. Kruse believes many oral problems can be resolved if the patient's light/environment is optimized. Given our children's cases, I would like to know if they would fall under the category of only needing light & environment optimization or if orthodontic work would be indicated. They are healthy children and thriving otherwise. They have always been homeschooled and they have had very minimal screentime (probably avg less than 1hr/week). I'll also note that this particular orthodontist seems quite knowledgeable and conservative in his approach to orthodontics, his main focus is not aesthetics, he uses Orthotropics instead of some of the new devices for expansion like Invisalign, and most of his patients do not get braces after the expansion phase. To this orthodontist's credit, he seemed like a genuinely curious practitioner that didn't just stay on auto pilot after receiving his license to practice, but like 99%+ of practitioners out there, he doesn't seem aware of how important light & nnEMFs play into the equation . I would actually really like him to talk to Dr. Kruse one day. Below is a summary of his diagnosis for each of our children:

8 year old daughter: Complex. Lower jaw too far back, Upper crowding 7mm, lower crowding 14mm, top teeth ahead of lower by 2mm, inner molar width 32.5mm ideal width is 42mm, issues with jaw joints: left crepitus, right crepitus, jaw deviates left, jaw deviates right, notes distillisation both joints on closing. He is hoping the palette expansion will help with the jaw joint issues; if we can get her palette and jaw in a healthier way it might stop the joint issues from worsening (we had no idea she had these problems with here joints prior to the exam; she never complained about pain and seemingly has no problem eating or chewing). Given her complexities he was unsure at this time if she would need braces after expansion phase.

6 year old daughter: Complex. Upper crowding 10mm, lower crowding 12mm, lower teeth are ahead of the upper teeth by 5mm, Three crossbites: Crossbite A) Some of the front teeth bite behind the lower teeth, Crossbite B) Some of the back teeth bite outside the lower teeth, Crossbite C) Upper teeth on the left bite inside of the lower teeth, inner molar width: 23mm, ideal width is 42mm, jaw joint: opening click on left. Given complexities, he believes she will likely need braces.

4 year old son: Bite is aligned in the back, upper crowding 6mm, lower crowding 8mm, top teeth are ahead of lower teeth by 1mm, inner molar width is 30mm ideal width is 42mm, tonsils look large in size, moderately tongue tied, possible posterior tongue tie. He had signs of wear on this teeth indicative of grinding at night. Ortho said we can wait a year before expansion treatment. We plan to optimize his light environment as much as we can in the meantime.

We also have a 15 month old son that has not been evaluated but he does look like he might be developing an underbite. The 6 teeth he has are gapped which is a good sign. We also are starting to get him outside every morning with us for sunrise, outside more in general, got rid of conventional LED interior lights, have always turned Wifi off at night (and we are working to hardwire everything and eliminate Wifi altogether) and since joining this forum have began switching off all electrical circuits at night besides the circuit for the refrigerator. We are exploring starting him on some type of myofacial therapy.

Given that these issues are complex and somewhat time sensitive, we are seeing what kind of thoughts/information the community here may offer. From what we have learned through this community, metal in mouths = nnEMF antennas, but given how severe their issues are being presented to us by the orthodontist we are left unsure what the best decision is for their health. We would also be happy to book consults with Dr. Kruse, but given how many irons he has in the fire, we are not sure how far out his calendar is right now for consultations.

### digitald3 — 2024-11-18T19:04:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/orthodontic-advice-for-three-children-with-many-issues.29825/post-342594>

I'll chime in until Dr Jack decides to chime in... After spending almost a year attempting to relearn what I was taught my views have changed... and I have a lot to still relearn and learn..

Your kids Jaws are not growing correctly in there given environment...its important to realize that this began before conception. Mom's mitochondria are not functioning properly.. so this is a whole family environment issue... how healthy is Mom? where do you live?

therefore in my mind if you wish to handle this the way nature does you need to find an optimal environment for your family...

As a centralized dentist working with a decentralized mindset, if you don't want to move and you can't improve your local environment enough to get your kids jaws growing then you may need to consider centralized approaches, which could mean some expansion...

dipping into my centralized "pre Dr. Jack" framework the 8 year old with crepitis and her jaw deviating.. this most likely means the temporomandibular joint disk is displaced from the top of the condyle... there is evidence that in a growing child this disk is important to the growth of the entire lower jaw... so this is why many centralized dentists are concerned about bad joints in young kids.. the question is will your orthos approach of expanding and hoping is one to inquire more about...

I do believe in the correct environment your daughter has the ability to heal...

As for the younger kids... you might consider something called a myo munchie, google it! to help get their jaws growing in the correct direction while also optimizing their light environment... the myo munchie may even help the 8 year old...

Again, I believe this is an environment problem and everything else is a band aid to cover the environment..

### Gian — 2024-11-19T05:06:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/orthodontic-advice-for-three-children-with-many-issues.29825/post-342613>

Wow thank you for your response Dr. Sandquist! It was in fact your podcast episode with Dr. Kruse that I was referencing in my above post. Admittedly, I understood very little of this podcast when y’all would talk shop, but it was also the episode that pushed me all the way into the “I’d put my money on Dr. Kruse to debate any centralized doctor from any discipline (specialists included) and he would have a deeper knowledge about their own discipline than they do”. Unfortunately, what he knows is not taught in conventional medical schools (yet). We live in Sonoma, CA.

As far as we know my wife is pretty healthy but we are basically brand new to all of the seemingly immensely important decentralized components of our health that Dr. Kruse has cued in on so we don’t know yet how healthy (or dysfunctional) her mitochondria is. When we were trying to conceive our first child we found out that she was slightly hypothyroid (not Hashimotos), her doctor put her on 50 mcg of Levothyroxine and we got pregnant 2 weeks later. She did not have a lot of the typical, overt signs of hypothyroidism...not overweight, no fatigue, but her periods were long with spotting leading up to menstruation which is what tipped us off that something was not right. She was on hormonal contraceptives on and off for about 10 years prior to trying to get pregnant so we suspect that contributed to her hypothyroidism. All of her pregnancies were uncomplicated, drug-free vaginal deliveries with no complications. The OBGYN insisted on inducing our third child because the amniotic fluid was low, so pitocin was given and he manually broke her waters. All four children were breastfed between 13 to 22 months (15 month old is still nursing). My wife has been working with a naturopath and is now off of the Levo; her thyroid panels have been ok per the ND but her TSH was 3.0 last time it was tested so there is still perhaps some work to be done. Her A1C was also elevated, level was 5.8 which we were surprised by since her glucose, insulin and triglycerides weren’t high. Between our 1st and 2nd child she suffered two miscarriages (the 1st one a DNC was performed), and between our 3rd and 4th child she suffered another miscarriage. Given the fact that she's been pregnant or breastfeeding for the past 9 years with little breaks in between we think she needs to work on replenishing her mineral and vitamin status. We are considering having her do a sleep study because she snores despite not being overweight. She is 41 years old and is very active chasing after 4 children and homeschooling but up until recently didn’t pay attention to or know anything about proper light environment. Prior to motherhood she worked an office job and lived a mostly indoor lifestyle.

At this point it would require a lot things to happen for us to get to the point where we could relocate permanently. So the first order of business is to try to mitigate our environment where we are at (which of course also takes time to research, money to purchase items/materials, designing and executing).

Yes, we've heard of the myo munchie and plan to get one for our baby and maybe the 6 and 4 year old. However, the orthodontist recommended against getting one for our 8 year given her joint issues. He said it could worsen the joint issue?

### digitald3 — 2024-11-19T18:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/orthodontic-advice-for-three-children-with-many-issues.29825/post-342643>

Thanks for listening to the Pod! It was a fairly in the weeds discussion and many of my dental colleagues were lost too.. so don't feel too bad.. I don't understand it all either! Many of my colleagues, which I include your orthodontist have really good intentions are trying to look for ways they can help improve the health of their patents... the challenge is like Dr. Jack says.. incentives dictate outcomes! If a centralized ortho or a regular dentist see a small jaw, they see how they can help that jaw get bigger.. there are thoughts and some data that can be pointed to that the size of the airway is the main contributor to sleep problems.. And yes there are some that have deviated septums and small maxillas and big tonsils etc.. so from a clinical exam view it becomes normal to want to recommend procedures that try to improve things.. The problem is that just solving the skeleton is a half truth and where most well meaning centralized clinicians stop.

Why is the airway small? Why are your kids jaws most likely not growing? Why is your 41 year old thin fit female wife and mom of your kids snoring?

This is where Dr. Jack's message needs to be heard! These are most likely functions of under powered mitochondria... How do you rev up Mitochondria without spending a fortune?

Go out side and see the sunrise... spend as much time outdoors as possible... if inside during the open the windows... in Sonoma, CA that means you will start to embrace the cold.... the cold in northern latitudes is necessary to survive.. your mitochondria will thank you.. then when the sun goes down watch it go with your own eye balls.. this also means the lights go out.. this means devices too! Which leads to the discussion of nnEMF.. the power in your house and the wifi and 5G are working against your mitochondria.. so learning ways to mitigate that will be important as you move along this path to fixing your environment.. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) is an amazing resource in learning how to mitigate the environment in your home..

decentralized medicine is learning how to take control of your own story!... You're on the path... don't get discouraged... its a struggle that is worth it..

# Thread: Cold shower at night?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-shower-at-night.29837/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-11-19T10:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-shower-at-night.29837/post-342619>

Hi all.

I read a recent Instagram post by Dr. Kruse regarding **CT before bedtime as not being a good idea**.

**Does a very cold shower before bedtime count as CT and should be avoided ?**

Grateful if someone could enlighten me on this.

### sheas — 2024-11-20T23:47:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-shower-at-night.29837/post-342710>

I only take cold showers now after doing cold plunges for a few years I prefer the cold water. I usually take one in AM and one in PM. I work outside with horses is why I take 2. In summer sometimes 3 because I am so dirty and sweaty. Anyway, I have not had any issues but usually take them before dinner not right before bed.

### 8Phoenix — 2024-11-21T23:48:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-shower-at-night.29837/post-342755>

> [KinaBibiDiop said:](/goto/post?id=342619)
>
> Hi all.
>
> I read a recent Instagram post by Dr. Kruse regarding **CT before bedtime as not being a good idea**.
>
> **Does a very cold shower before bedtime count as CT and should be avoided ?**
>
> Grateful if someone could enlighten me on this.

the reason you turn on your heating system

### coho3131 — 2024-11-22T01:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-shower-at-night.29837/post-342759>

You could read about body temperature and sleep onset.

A hot shower causes skin vasodilation for a certain period of time which results in a decrease in body temperature, which is a circadian sleep signal.

### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-11-25T12:20:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-shower-at-night.29837/post-342880>

> [8Phoenix said:](/goto/post?id=342755)
>
> the reason you turn on your heating system

Please elaborate

### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-11-25T12:23:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-shower-at-night.29837/post-342881>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=342759)
>
> You could read about body temperature and sleep onset.
>
> A hot shower causes skin vasodilation for a certain period of time which results in a decrease in body temperature, which is a circadian sleep signal.

Thanks for pointing this out. I will read about it.

However, I have felt like I tend to fall asleep quicker after cold showers. I have experimented over 2 weeks or so, taking a cold shower every night and my sleep has improved during those 2 weeks. Prior to the experiment I had weeks of sleepless nights.

### Johan Lindøm — 2024-11-25T12:24:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-shower-at-night.29837/post-342882>

I like sauna in the evening with a cold shower.

# Thread: Sugar during Pregnancy/Early Life - research
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-during-pregnancy-early-life-research.29840/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BruceKing — 2024-11-19T12:53:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-during-pregnancy-early-life-research.29840/post-342624>

### Exposure to sugar rationing in the first 1000 days of life protected against chronic disease​

"...early-life rationing reduced diabetes and hypertension risk by about 35% and 20%, respectively, and delayed disease onset by 4 and 2 years. Protection was evident with in-utero exposure and increased with postnatal sugar restriction, especially after six months when solid foods likely began. In-utero sugar rationing alone accounted for about one third of the risk reduction."

[https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adn5421](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adn5421)

# Thread: EMF considerations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-considerations.29850/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ozzy1 — 2024-11-20T11:12:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-considerations.29850/post-342682>

I saw Kruse post the beautiful stonework he's having tiled around the perimeter of his property that doubles as EMF protection from the composition. I have an older 4g tower built on the side of a motorway about 300m away, and im considering doing something similar on the interior side of my walls facing in that direction. I'm guessing alot of cars from the busy main road about 200 metres in the other direction are pinging the tower constantly and i may be in the middle of it. Now i know the first answer is to move, but I'm interested in tiling the walls for directional protection in the mean time potentially. Does anyone have any advice on what you would make these tiles out of and any potential downsides to doing this to the sides of my house? I know you can run into problems with creating a situation where emfs can bounce around inside the home if you insulate too well, and also cutting yourself off completely from Native emfs is equally problematic so i do not want to mess with faraday beg mesh or any of the sort. I would do it with just metal foil or copper but i would put the effort in for a better aesthetic if i had more know how.

### pfedorow — 2024-11-20T20:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-considerations.29850/post-342704>

I heard emf paint would work on a wall and be a cheaper solution. Tile it after with whatever to hide it or just paint it another color

### Anthony Smith — 2024-11-21T00:20:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-considerations.29850/post-342715>

> [Ozzy1 said:](/goto/post?id=342682)
>
> I saw Kruse post the beautiful stonework he's having tiled around the perimeter of his property that doubles as EMF protection from the composition. I have an older 4g tower built on the side of a motorway about 300m away, and im considering doing something similar on the interior side of my walls facing in that direction. I'm guessing alot of cars from the busy main road about 200 metres in the other direction are pinging the tower constantly and i may be in the middle of it. Now i know the first answer is to move, but I'm interested in tiling the walls for directional protection in the mean time potentially. Does anyone have any advice on what you would make these tiles out of and any potential downsides to doing this to the sides of my house? I know you can run into problems with creating a situation where emfs can bounce around inside the home if you insulate too well, and also cutting yourself off completely from Native emfs is equally problematic so i do not want to mess with faraday beg mesh or any of the sort. I would do it with just metal foil or copper but i would put the effort in for a better aesthetic if i had more know how.

Put up metal (mosquito) window screen on the outside wall. Can hardly be seen even from a few feet away. Inexpensive, easy to install and over a 90% RF reduction. You may want to add a ground cord to it so as not to increase electric field on the inside.

### Ozzy1 — 2024-11-21T10:30:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-considerations.29850/post-342732>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=342715)
>
> Put up metal (mosquito) window screen on the outside wall. Can hardly be seen even from a few feet away. Inexpensive, easy to install and over a 90% RF reduction. You may want to add a ground cord to it so as not to increase electric field on the inside.

Interesting. What about the inside wall? I live in a semi detached so one wall can only be insulated from the inside. I'm also concerned about emfs; bouncing around inside with two opposing walls insulated tbh having thought about it but with limited knowledge and no equipment to test yet

### Anthony Smith — 2024-11-21T13:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-considerations.29850/post-342738>

Metal mesh, paint, any RF shields acts as a mirror to RF. It keeps it out and in. Measure before spending lots of money, at 300m it may not be necessary. Stone, concrete other materials can absorb some of it but you need a lot. Jack house is made of concrete and steel.

### Ozzy1 — 2024-11-21T14:57:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-considerations.29850/post-342741>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=342738)
>
> Metal mesh, paint, any RF shields acts as a mirror to RF. It keeps it out and in. Measure before spending lots of money, at 300m it may not be necessary. Stone, concrete other materials can absorb some of it but you need a lot. Jack house is made of concrete and steel.

Thanks for the info. I'm thinking that a good rf meter would probably be the best investment before i try to do anything more drastic. Maybe put the mesh up sporadically where i normally spend my time to mitigate it.

# Thread: Improved in Short Time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-in-short-time.29855/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BruceKing — 2024-11-20T19:40:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-in-short-time.29855/post-342697>

Discovered Uncle Jack on October 30, 2024, and immediately realized he was right. I was a carbo-craving person (mmm sourdough bread, ice cream, etc.) who already had a superficial understanding of nnEMF, red light, CT, had no idea about blue light or getting sunlight (skin cancer!...right?) or eating seafood, etc. but I only saw red light as helping musculoskeletal disorders, mistakenly thought nnEMF was okay if it wasn't too close to your body and I wasn't about to freeze my ass off in a bathtub full of ice water. Now I wear blue blocker glasses wherever there is artificial blue light, get 4 to 6 hours of sunlight a day on my skin, eat a more paleo diet ( a lot more seafood), watch the sunrise while barefoot in the grass and try to get to bed by 9pm, mitigate my nnEMF exposure and even started the CT based on Uncle Jack's link from his book Epi-Paleo Rx (one time so far).
My gastric reflux went away after 2 days of extended sunlight exposure. The plantar fasciitist in my left foot is gone. My allergies are reduced to an occasional sneeze without the sinus headache, stuffy nose or itchy eyes. I have more energy and think more clearly. Bonus: I'm getting an awesome tan even with my Fitzpatrick 2 or 3 skin. Next up: Move to El Salvador.
Thank you Uncle Jack! You are the solution I didn't know I needed. Rock on!

### mitopower — 2025-01-07T22:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-in-short-time.29855/post-344867>

HI Bruce! My heartburn went away as well. Also I don't have to take suppositories for my colonitis proctosis. Oh, and no more hurting bursa sacs in my knees when I run.
Inflammation runs when it sees a mitochondriac

# Thread: Deuterium Content in Fatty Acids/Seeds
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-content-in-fatty-acids-seeds.29860/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lancelot — 2024-11-21T18:44:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-content-in-fatty-acids-seeds.29860/post-342748>

Hey, I remember Jack mentioning that everything that is a seed (and everything growing below the earth like potatoes, carrots) is high in deuterium.

I just want to make sure that I don't make a wrong assumption based on that: does that include flaxseeds?

If so, does that mean that the precursor essential fatty acids (linoleic acid - omega 6 - , precursor to arachidonic acid and alpha linolenic acid - omega - precursor to EPA and DHA) are always high in deuterium?

I say that, because conventional meat, which is higher in deuterium is also higher in linoleic acid (LA), which usually doesn't occur in high amounts in meat --- comes from unnatural food (grains), drugs and indoor life.

Thanks for your time

### Ryan Carter — 2024-11-25T06:05:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-content-in-fatty-acids-seeds.29860/post-342877>

Unconventional meat usually has little sun to deplete it - alter its own endogenous fatty acid synthesis + intestinal permeability with antibiotics + stress.
Then throw on junk food = recipe for deuterium to be high.

Seeds technically are due to growth - collect and store more deuterium....; think about babies and their growth... And why Deuterium is not evil in certain situations.

Humans require Omega 6s, but usually, they are controlled and influenced via redox, circadian biology (vitamin A), and nutrients like seafood - iodine, and selenium cysteine.

Should you consume seed oils - no
Is conventional meat better than seed oils or vegan food - yes likely. Should you consume conventional beef for health and vitality - likely no, but in some situations, it might not be the worst thing, and there are more important battles to worry about.

Joanna Macy, eco-philosopher and spiritual activist notes, “the crisis that threatens our planet, whether seen in its military, ecological, or social aspects, derives from a dysfunctional and pathological notion of the self…the delusion that the self is so separate and fragile that we must delineate and defend its boundaries; that it is so small and so needy that we must endlessly acquire and endlessly consume; and that as individuals, corporations, nation-states, or a species, we can be immune to what we do to other beings.”

# Thread: Ukraine etc
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ukraine-etc.29888/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-11-27T18:07:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ukraine-etc.29888/post-342986>

Friend of mine , who is a military veteran and who I proofread for has written this ( copyright Richard R Fox)

"
There’s a strategic objective that people miss about NATO’s support to Ukraine: Putin’s Soviet stockpiles of military equipment.

Putin sat on decades worth of tank/APC/artillery production that was a deep resource for any extended conflict Russia might find itself in. All those lots full of USSR gear could also be used to backstop a friendly nation in the event of war.

Putin’s attempts to manufacture weapon systems on par with what the US/NATO have now ended in failure due to corruption (and that Russia can’t manufacture the electronics those systems need).

The longer NATO makes Russia bleed in Ukraine, the less Russia is able to defend itself…or invade Western countries. The few weapon systems Russia can produce are a trickle of what the war demands. If the war ends tomorrow, it will take Russia at least a decade to replenish its military. And what it can make is nearly obsolete.

Meanwhile, Europe has weened itself of Russian energy and Russia lacks the transport capacity to move enough energy to keep its economy afloat. I suspect Trump will pull the secondary sanctions card to get the war to end and stop the Indians and Chinese from buying Russian energy.

Western nations are now free from the threat of the Russian bear for at least a decade, and all it cost was 90s era weapons, plenty of billions of tax payer dollars laundered through Kiev, and 100,000+ dead Ukrainians.

I don’t know about you, but I’d call that a bargain. For the West. Sucks for Ukraine."

It resonated with me.
. He knows what
he's talking about....

Was that what they wanted ,? To Neuter Putin ?

> The longer NATO makes Russia bleed in Ukraine, the less Russia is able to defend itself…or invade Western countries. The few weapon systems Russia can produce are a trickle of what the war demands. If the war ends tomorrow, it will take Russia at least a decade to replenish its military. And what it can make is nearly obsolete.
>
> Meanwhile, Europe has weened itself of Russian energy and Russia lacks the transport capacity to move enough energy to keep its economy afloat. I suspect Trump will pull the secondary sanctions card to get the war to end and stop the Indians and Chinese from buying Russian energy.
>
> Western nations are now free from the threat of the Russian bear for at least a decade, and all it cost was 90s era weapons, plenty of billions of tax payer dollars laundered through Kiev, and 100,000+ dead Ukrainians.
>
> I don’t know about you, but I’d call that a bargain. For the West. Sucks for Ukraine."
>
> It resonated with me.
> . He knows what
> he's talking about....
>
> Was that what they wanted ,? To Neuter Putin ?

The US has sent more than $113 billion to Ukraine (purportedly ) I suspect that this is only the tip of the iceberg of what we really have spent (maybe this is why the pentagon can not pass an audit!!!!!) Since this amount is 2x the Russian Military budget (annually) I wonder if we had just purchased Putin's old military hardware with this money and not tried to put Ukraine in NATO, we could have saved 100,000+ Ukrainian lives, and not be on the brink of Nuclear Conflict......Now, to me, that would be a Bargain!!!!. I guess we must also factor in the cost of rebuilding the infrastructure in Ukraine.....as they say you break it you bought it ,,,,,,that is a bargain we will be laboring under for some time IMO

# Thread: Nearlink : An upgrade to Bluetooth technology

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rusty — 2024-12-01T10:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearlink-an-upgrade-to-bluetooth-technology.29899/post-343116>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

NEARLINK supersedes BLUETOOTH, with up to 60-fold further range, and up to 18-fold higher data rate.

All of these higher data rate improvements, do so by adding even more noisy/pulsing waveforms (aka modulation) which might have the same or even lower power on a meter.

This means POWER is no longer ENOUGH to distinguish the pain that those with EHS suffer from — it’s POWER and BANDWIDTH together.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-01T14:38:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearlink-an-upgrade-to-bluetooth-technology.29899/post-343119>

It has always been about frequency & modulation & power.

Your main point though is very valid and important - it NOT just power/strength of the signal/field.

### Rusty — 2024-12-02T07:52:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearlink-an-upgrade-to-bluetooth-technology.29899/post-343161>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=343119)
>
> It has always been about frequency & modulation & power.
>
> Your main point though is very valid and important - it NOT just power/strength of the signal/field.

**What are the critical factors in assessing bio-disruptive potential of manmade EMF signals?**

1.  **FREQUENCY** – Most bio-disruptive frequencies? Resonance factor?
2.  **INTENSITY** – The most obvious one, but which intensity is vital – Peak or Average?
3.  **BANDWIDTH** – The range of frequencies the signal occupies – the broader the bandwidth, the higher information rate is possible (Shannon Hartley theorem) AND the higher bio-disruptive potential!
4.  **IMPULSE CHARACTERISTIC** – short (narrow) and steep pulses have massive bio-disruptive potential even if they are relatively rare (i.e., seconds apart as in radar) – indicated by high Crest Factor (peak to average amplitude) or PAPR (peak to average power ratio).
5.  **POLARIZATION** – virtually all environmental signals in Nature are not polarized. Conversely, most of manmade EMF’s are polarized in increasingly complex ways.
6.  **BIO-DISRUPTIVE SYNERGY** – the number of different manmade EMFs we are exposed to counts! The relation is non-linear – while each signal alone may not be able to dysregulate the bio-system – two or more in combination increase the bio-disruptive potential exponentially – even if combined intensity (energy) is low!

### rtd — 2024-12-02T22:10:41+0200

> **INTENSITY** – The most obvious one, but which intensity is vital – Peak or Average?

# Thread: What type of radio wave is the worst to hear?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-type-of-radio-wave-is-the-worst-to-hear.29903/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BrunoB — 2024-12-02T13:13:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-type-of-radio-wave-is-the-worst-to-hear.29903/post-343165>

Hello good day I would like to know that, because I would like to buy a shortwave radio so as not to use wifi, but I want to know how much it affects health, thank you

### eaandkw — 2024-12-03T15:37:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-type-of-radio-wave-is-the-worst-to-hear.29903/post-343227>

For shortwave radios if you are only trying to listen to or receive radio stations that should be ok since the radio waves are already in the 'air' and have been transmitted. If you want to transmit like amateur radio not so much. Wifi you want to cut out as much as possible. If you can't completely remove it at least try reducing the power level. Most users have the power set to max thinking that will make their bit rate faster but that isn't how it works.

But basically, what Jack said. All RF is bad.

### Braindoc29 — 2024-12-03T16:32:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-type-of-radio-wave-is-the-worst-to-hear.29903/post-343228>

Not sure what else to do...I keep getting the error message every time I reply to you and have send messages for the last 10 days through email as well. Can you help...here is the error message
"Your content can not be submitted. This is likely because your content is spam-like or contains inappropriate elements. Please change your content or try again later. If you still have problems, please contact an administrator."

### rtd — 2024-12-03T20:19:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-type-of-radio-wave-is-the-worst-to-hear.29903/post-343243>

### PopSickle — 2024-12-05T19:15:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-type-of-radio-wave-is-the-worst-to-hear.29903/post-343335>

> [BrunoB said:](/goto/post?id=343165)
>
> Hello good day I would like to know that, because I would like to buy a shortwave radio so as not to use wifi, but I want to know how much it affects health, thank you

Shortwave is typically related to Amateur Radio usage (Ham Radio). It's not a typical "replacement" for using Wi-Fi. It really goes back to what you are trying to specifically do.

Anything in the Amateur Radio frequencies are governed by ARRL, and they have pages related to RF Exposure: [https://www.arrl.org/rf-exposure](https://www.arrl.org/rf-exposure)

# Thread: Homeschool Question for Members
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeschool-question-for-members.29905/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jessnet3 — 2024-12-02T16:16:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeschool-question-for-members.29905/post-343169>

For those that homeschool: what curriculums do you use to teach your children history? Is there anything that encourages children to think critically and ask questions, most notably about American history in the past 100 years?

### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-22T03:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeschool-question-for-members.29905/post-344241>

I just posted a homeschool question! Hoping to get more feedback.

I was wondering the same thing about this and also science. I’m thinking certainly there aren’t quantum biology for elementary kids curriculum? Would be cool! I’ve been teaching my 9 year old.

I used the word mitochondria and he said wow mom you’re so much smarter than me with all of those big words. Ha!

### Jacks — 2024-12-22T07:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeschool-question-for-members.29905/post-344244>

Best to follow your child's interests and natural curiosity - kids learn best this way, rather than following a specified curriculm decided by somebody else (my 2 cents), at least during elementary school and possibly middle school. We homeschooled one of my two for a few years (just following their interests) but ended up enrolling them in an alternative school (self-directed learning) for the social interaction ------ at least till Convid times.. They are doing really well now academically and very confident in themselves in regular HS, and looked up to as a leader by other kids and teachers. No real loss from not doing all the drudgery work for all those years and it was great to spend more time with them doing fun projects and taking field trips together.

### Jessnet3 — 2024-12-24T15:28:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeschool-question-for-members.29905/post-344304>

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=344244)
>
> Best to follow your child's interests and natural curiosity - kids learn best this way, rather than following a specified curriculm decided by somebody else (my 2 cents), at least during elementary school and possibly middle school. We homeschooled one of my two for a few years (just following their interests) but ended up enrolling them in an alternative school (self-directed learning) for the social interaction ------ at least till Convid times.. They are doing really well now academically and very confident in themselves in regular HS, and looked up to as a leader by other kids and teachers. No real loss from not doing all the drudgery work for all those years and it was great to spend more time with them doing fun projects and taking field trips together.

I agree that nourishing that innate curiosity is immensely important, however children also hold an enormous capacity for learning outside of their immediate interests. My first grader and Kindergartener gobble up classical literature that traditional schools today overlook (namely because attention is a lost art and understanding these texts requires attention). The other day we read together the story of Diogenes and Alexander the Great. Diogenes was the only person to not greet Alexander when he arrived in the city, and upon seeking him out, he found the old man basking in the sunshine. He asked Diogenes why he did not come to greet him, and his response was: “Stand away from my sunlight while you’re talking to me.” My first grader immediately said: “Mom! The wise old man knew how important the sun is, too.” We can cultivate important connections in historical texts that sometimes are missed by more modern means of teaching. Our oldest attended an interest lead school for a few years as well, and so I do understand the appeal of that. Every child learns in a different way, and the beauty of being homeschoolers is we can meet their interests while cultivating open and curious minds.

However, my oldest is now entering middle school and it’s not lost on me how modern history is blatantly inaccurate. Perhaps just continuing to foster critical thinking is the true “curriculum” for this stage.

### Jessnet3 — 2024-12-24T15:49:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeschool-question-for-members.29905/post-344305>

> [Rachel Martin said:](/goto/post?id=344241)
>
> I just posted a homeschool question! Hoping to get more feedback.
>
> I was wondering the same thing about this and also science. I’m thinking certainly there aren’t quantum biology for elementary kids curriculum? Would be cool! I’ve been teaching my 9 year old.
>
> I used the word mitochondria and he said wow mom you’re so much smarter than me with all of those big words. Ha!

Rachel I have a 10, 6 and 5 year old and they have learned in their own various capacities the importance of light/water/magnetism. I have friends who are trying to adopt this lifestyle but fall short in compliance with their children because they do not invest enough time explaining the “why” behind what we do (or lack the understandings themselves). Convincing a child to see the sun rise on a 9 degree day is no easy task, but if they understand how critical it is to their biology, they will be more willing to do so.

My youngest children for “science” are just immersed in nature study at this point - we use beautiful feet books nature study which I can’t recommend enough. Building a solid foundation in observing nature is still imperative at nine and ten, and thus our oldest follows along on the younger two’s nature explorations. I do use the addition of Noeo science for my oldest as they have a CM “living book” approach that I appreciate, but the sciences are siloed and so I still try to emphasize with my oldest that everything is interconnected, and we’re exploring branches of an enormous tree. This helps her to examine interdisciplinary threads throughout everything we read, including bridging the connection to her understanding of light/water/magnetism.

### periwinkle — 2024-12-26T17:23:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homeschool-question-for-members.29905/post-344354>

During the pandemic, we used the Well Trained Mind Academy for my middle school child for History / Social Studies and Spanish. Currently, studying Spanish III in high school. Excellent curriculum. I could not be happier.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-02T17:34:04+0200

Hi! I’m a new member and my husband and I got the J&J vaccine back in 2021. We had our 3rd kiddo and she was diagnosed with a rare disease and has a tracheostomy. We were scared shitless and I absolutely regret it.

Thankfully kids have never had a Covid vaccine. One thing I did correctly!!

I’m working my way through the blogs and Q&As. Any info regarding the J&J vaccine? I’ve read it’s not the same but it also creates spike proteins.

Can anyone point me to a particular blog, Q&A, anything to really understand what consequences I’m dealing with??

>
> Can anyone point me to a particular blog, Q&A, anything to really understand what consequences I’m dealing with??

Here's what I've picked up re: J&J:

\- Jack distinguishes between the J&J and the others in recent Q&A's but there are a lot of unknowns and it should be taken just as serious i.e. get to a lower latitude and follow protocols. There is no direct J&J remedy from what I've seen (or not) but all the blogs inform this situation.
\- jab RX [https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1861064499727335700](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1861064499727335700) via [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treatment-of-the-jabbed-considerations.29616/#post-342911](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treatment-of-the-jabbed-considerations.29616/#post-342911)

\- Jack mentioned that McCullough's detox could be beneficial to J&J but not the others. [https://petermcculloughmd.substack....ars?r=14jb45&utm\_medium=ios&utm\_campaign=post](https://petermcculloughmd.substack.com/p/breaking-clinical-rationale-for-sars?r=14jb45&utm_medium=ios&utm_campaign=post)

### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-02T23:00:27+0200

Thanks so much!! Thankfully we (so far) are pretty good. I’ve fixed most issues with sunlight, grounding, cold exposure, etc. I’m about to get labs to check on things and reassess. May need to move south since we are in Virginia but exhausting other options first. Especially since I believe we are both uncoupled and should do well in colder weather.

I’ve been on turmeric for a while so I’ll get started with the rest of McCullough protocol and look into these resources.

Bought Bitcoin this weekend and today. Need to fund possible future healing!

“Sulfate deficiency is the most common nutritional deficiency you’ve never heard of,” says MIT Senior Research Scientist Stephanie Seneff, PhD, at the recent Clinical and Scientific Insights (CASI) conference in San Francisco. Seneff believes that sulfate deficiency is a major culprit behind...

When comparisons are made of cutaneous melanoma with cutaneous squamous cell cancer (SCC) and basal cell cancer (BCC) of the skin with respect to age dependence, sex ratio, distribution on the body, association with sun exposure, and variation with latitude, it is clear that SCC is due almost...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

# Thread: Turtles All the Way Down - The Real book and The Fake Book

### BruceKing — 2024-12-03T15:06:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turtles-all-the-way-down-the-real-book-and-the-fake-book.29914/post-343224>

The book "Turtles All the Way Down" by Mary Holland and Zoey O'Toole is a great explanation of the dangers of vaccines. It reveals the undisputed fact that NO vaccines currently on the childhood schedule in the U.S. haver been tested against REAL placebos (i.e., saline etc.) instead they're tested against other vaccines or adjuvants etc. Now (as I learned from Steve Kirsch) a pro-vaccine book came out on Nov. 14, 2024 on Amazon (see pic below) with the same name authored by the pseudonym Mira Holden, with a very similar book cover. It is already out-of-print and unavailable fortunately. But, it shows the length some will go to keep your children poisoned.

# Thread: Possible PE, nursing along DVT in leg
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-pe-nursing-along-dvt-in-leg.29920/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### prtdgk — 2024-12-04T05:41:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-pe-nursing-along-dvt-in-leg.29920/post-343268>

Hello beginners thread.
A family member of mine had (what doctors told him) a pulmonary embolism back in 2017. He survived and has been thriving for the most part despite a couple of surgeries.... I believe 2 or 3 fusions in lower back (all a couple years ago already). He's been on blood Thinners since the PE. Over the past couple years he's slowly woken up to the medical system and has cut back on all the useless drugs they've had him on.
Recently he was getting pain in his leg and emergency room discovered that he had a huge DVT. I accompanied him to an appointment with the top blood specialist in our region
After sitting through that together, trying to kindly and tactfully ask questions, it made us both realize once again the sad condition of the medical system.
Despite the doctors orders he went off blood Thinners for about 2 months and did rigorous juicing, cayenne pepper, and other natural anticoagulants.
However, with progressive pain, swelling, and fear of a more serious PE, recently he went back onto the blood thinner.
We've both appreciated recently getting into quantum biology. Grounding everyday, waking up with the sun, and blocking unnatural light. He's continued juicing, and taking potent mixes of cayenne pepper, ginger, garlic. He's also just getting off of a week of fasting.
Curious if anyone has any advice on naturally fighting a potentially huge blood clot, minimizing the danger of a fatal PE, and not being paralyzed by fear.
Many thanks.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-12-15T10:19:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-pe-nursing-along-dvt-in-leg.29920/post-343923>

Heparin injections, personally, warfarin used sensibly will thin blood ( I realise that's a centralised outlook )

He needs to keep moving and to wear compression stockings all the night time you can buy these at the pharmacy, along with cotton gloves if needed

I take it there is metal in his lower back like this ? Or not as the case maybe

Natural ways to thin blood, Oily fish and DHA and EPA so your regular seafood

Also some coQ10 . Wouldn't hurt for a while

Survivor soup would be good too

Whatever he does he needs to be lore mobile. Blood only clots like that with severe immobility and lack of massage to the muscles especially the soleus and gastrocnemius

# Thread: Watching Jack's predictions come through in real time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watching-jacks-predictions-come-through-in-real-time.29926/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Magpie — 2024-12-05T10:35:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watching-jacks-predictions-come-through-in-real-time.29926/post-343312>

Surviving the year 2024 is an enormous achievement. I went to two funerals this year for beautiful women who were both colleagues and friends. One died in her mid 50s of pancreatic cancer leaving behind her husband and two teenage children. She taught me so many things about obstetrics and gynaecology when I was a first year medical student on elective. I learned how to assist a caesarian section by assisting her. And I know how to examine a woman for preeclampsia. She was both smart and funny and had an incredibly kind and caring approach to all the women she looked after. She also made a point of finding new things to teach me when she was one of the consultant OBGYNs responsible for me during my 4th year O&G rotation. She died far too early and her death was a preventable tragedy. It makes me angry. No one should be dying of pancreatic cancer in their 50s. I think that SV40 was the invisible smoking gun.

My other colleague/friend died of a brain tumour. She never had the usual headaches. It was only discovered because she developed a patch of visual loss a scotoma. The tumour was in the visual cortex. My colleague Jack says it would have been caused by jump conduction. She had stopped working to avoid having any COVID vaccinations which were mandated. She would have liked me to join her practice and I would have except that I noticed a cell phone tower sitting above the strip of shops where her "Doctor shop" was located. She lasted only four months after diagnosis. My last memory of her was meeting her at the fish counter in a favourite small independent supermarket where she told me she was hosting a lady from the countryside in her home while the lady sought care for cancer. I spent 6 weekends learning Emmett Technique this year because Kathy informed me it existed. She was a very popular integrative GP that included acupuncture and Biobalance (targeted nutritional supplements for mental health) in her repertoire and my mum was one of her patients. We had great fun sitting in my family room in 2020 doing our Biobalance refresher via Zoom (Biobalance courses were once upon a time face to face at a nice hotel on the Gold Coast). And attending performances of the Queensland Medical Orchestra became a regular occasion to catch up. She left behind her son, daughter in law and two grandchildren. No one should by dying at age 69 of a brain tumour.

The last death I am grieving for this year is the death of my beloved dog Poppy. My poor dog had three cancers. A gastrointestinal stromal tumour of the jejeunum (GIST) almost caused her to bleed out in early 2022, but she survived the surgery to remove the tumour and did not have any recurrence of the GIST. However she had required a blood transfusion so had anaphylaxis when she was given antivenin for tick paralysis in October 2022. (Australia is a very dangerous place for dogs as they did not evolve here). She survived the tick paralysis due to brilliant ICU care. She had been very well and continued to enjoy long early morning walks on the beach even though she looked terrible with her fur shaved every 3 months for review ultrasounds for surveillance because of the GIST. I started worrying about her in September 2023, because she was breathing too quickly at rest on a cool Sunday evening after I had returned from a weekend conference. Emergency vet said to change her diet from raw to cooked. She became worse despite having her diet changed to lightly cooked Lyka. I noticed muscle wasting and progressive worsening of exercise tolerance. After several visits to the vet the cause was only found after I asked for her thyroid function to be checked. It was very very low, but in dogs this can be caused by something else. Her adrenals were enlarged on ultrasound and blood tests confirmed Cushings disease. She was put on medication to suppress the adrenal function.

Over Easter, an abdominal wall tumour that had not been there 8 months prior started bleeding so I took her to the emergency vet. The vet said that the tumour was most likely a malignant haemangiosarcoma due to the way it was behaving. There was induration surrounding the lesion. The plan was for the tumour to be surgically removed the following day. However the vet rang me in the morning to say that she was showing neurological signs and the vet nurses that knew her so well were worried about her behaviour. They requested to do a CT of her head which showed a large pituitary adenoma which was behaving like a space occupying brain tumour causing the neurological signs. I decided to take her home and have a lovely local vet do a home visit for euthanasia. She was suffering too much for me to be selfish and have the tumour treated with radiotherapy which would have required an anaesthetic with each round. Poppy would have turned 13 in May. But no-one's pet dog should have had three malignant tumours. The modern world is a very toxic place.

I have now removed myself from the environment that caused the early death of my dog and my lovely friends. We all had a wonderful time at the Giving Thanks event. And I would like to give thanks to my colleague Dr Jack for his wisdom and insightful analysisis of all of the problems that he has identified in our modern world. I have never gone live on a pow wow to ask questions about myself. I occasionally ask questions about things relating to my patients but Christy tends to misinterpret the questions I have her ask. (No my doctor wasn't suggesting that I take ozempic. I was getting sick of my patients demanding it and wanted to clarify Jack's concerns regarding this pharmaceutical)

I have been following Jack's work since 2011 ( I know because I remember when he was kicked of the cruise that time). I mostly listened to podcasts but joined Patreon when he started the blog there. I finally joined as a member in 2019, because I decided that it would be nice to be able to post and discuss things on the health practioners forum. Sometimes I wondered why I bothered because I kept hearing him berating members for not moving and it wasn't fun listening. Informative but not fun.

Jack was telling us some years ago that our pets are like canaries in the coal mine indicating that there are invisible dangers in our environment. He suggested that we talk to older vets in the community to ask what unusual things they are seeing or if they are seeing more of certain conditions. The GP vet I took my dogs to just retired at age 82. He described seeing more unusual skin tumours but I'm not a vet and they were not familiar pathologies for me. I imagine that over his career the reasons for surgical intervention changed. Dr Feeney was an exceptional surgeon. He served the small beachside pocket community where my mom and my step dad lived. One of the neighbours dogs, a schnauzer, was attacked by an unknown dog. She was taken to Dr Feeney who said that she was very badly injured and he would have a go operating but did not think she would survive. Saffy did survive because Dr Feeney trained in an era when a GP vet was expected to be a good surgeon. Now pets are more likely to need surgery for cancer. Dr Feeney was excising the skin tumours himself.

The emergency vet that I took my dog to was co-located with a state of the art oncology clinic for pets. They offer chemotherapy and radiotherapy as well as advanced surgery for pets with cancer. I have met many people with their pets in the waiting room while I waited for Poppy to have her ultrasounds or waited to pick her up after an admission. A Veterinary oncology clinic such as this one did not exist in the past. Such a business model was not viable in the past because there were not so many pets developing cancer like they do now.

Please do not delude yourself into thinking that things are OK in your current environment. Moving for most people is very inconvenient, but so is a terminal illness. Some podcasters seem to think that Jack is joking when he suggests people purchase term life insurance if they elect not to move. He is not joking, he is providing some sound financial advice. I decided that I was not going to stay in the bad environment waiting for a malignancy to eventuate.

If you consider how poor my environment was, I should probably have died of some type of cancer already. But I have an advantage that many of you do not. The ovum that I originated from came into existance at latitude 1.290270. My mother and grandmother were born at latitude 1.290270. And my great grandmother was born at 5.285153. Unfortunately this trend was broken. I was born at latitude -37.840935 because my mother thought this was a good place to go for her tertiary education.

My dog and I slept in a bedroom that was shielded with several layers of Y shield paint and had an EMF blocking film on the windows. The nnEMF in the bedroom measured low. I had hardwired internet with absolutely no wifi. No smart devices. I kept the electrical circuits to the bedroom off. But we lived too close to an international airport and there were enough cell phone towers in the vincinity for the building biologist to comment on the numbers. The neighbourhood we lived in was not a high density neighbourhood as it had lots of parkland due to flood plains and was near an private Catholic boys school with enormous grounds (13 rugby fields a 9 hole golf course and tracts of remnant untouched forrest along with acres of marginal swamp land.). But sometimes I had ridiculous leg cramps at home despite taking good quantities of magnesium. I think this was from the neighbours doing something like switching on one of their smart devices or a wifi modem

I had a liver resection 20 years ago for what turned out to be a hepatic cystadenoma. Rare but considered to be pre-cancerous and was not even listed in my pathology book when the intern came round to visit me in my hospital bed with the histopathology results. It was case reports only for hepatic cystadenoma. So I have periodic US. The one done in October reported mild hepatic steatosis. It must be the blue light and spending too much time in an office with windows that don't open. Because I actually managed to lose a good amout of weight and do a good leptin reset in May. I had the pleasure of meeting Johan when I was in Oslo in September. He was emphasising that no matter how much you try to spend time outside it is not enough to be enough to mitigate against the toxic blue light. Even though my lunch breaks were taken on the beach in minimal clothing.

I never posted on the public forum until recently. I didn't feel safe to do so, because the government where I lived is the opposite of benign. At the clinic where I worked, we had a trainee registrar for about three weeks before he was suspended from practice for speaking out against the COVID vaccine mandates. If I had prescribed hydroxychloroquin for a COVID infection, I faced a 6 month jail sentence. I would have been suspended if I prescribed ivermectin. We were only be allowed to prescribe ivermectin for scabies after topical treatments had failed. We were not even permitted to suggest anything natural like vitamin C for COVID infection. I was still self employed so I didn't have an employer dictating how I practiced. But socialised medicine means that the government threatens to audit you if they notice too much deviation from the norm in your practice.

I like to learn from other people's mistakes, because it means that I am less likely to need to make those mistakes myself.

Don't keep living in a toxic environment and hoping that things will be ok. They won't. I watched Jack's predictions come through in real time.

# Thread: Curly Hair Help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-06T00:02:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343356>

Hi! I’ve been trying really hard to find the optimal hair care routine for my curly/wavy hair. I’d love tips on curly hair and favorite way to wash & style.

I’m only wearing makeup once a month these days and embracing my natural beauty more and more. Loving this part of my journey.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-12-06T01:00:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343359>

You can just use coconut oil about a teaspoon to damp hair.. I do that. I was taught this trick by someone in the beauty business

As for washing. You can either try to go without washing it for six to seven weeks , only water no shampoo or use the shampoo bars that are made with decent ingredients

I happen to have long wavy curly hair. I tend to over brush it. It's worth just leaving it naturally...

### Sirena — 2024-12-06T06:03:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343377>

Bask in your curls. Many women pay big bucks for curls. If you want to tame your curls a bit, try a few hairstyles until you find one that gives you power. Perhaps decorate or braid your hair with colorful ribbons or flowers. Play around.

### eaandkw — 2024-12-07T04:37:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343475>

Katherine Sewing on YT talks about her curly hair routine and seems relatively natural.

### [Katherine Sewing](https://www.youtube.com/@KatherineSewing)

Hey! I'm Katherine, and I create historical, natural hair care videos, and historical hand-made fashion videos. I share the tips that I have personally used to grow my hair from chin to hip length, as well as how I sew my historical, vintage-inspired wardrobe.

 www.youtube.com

# Thread: Thoughts on light & darkness deficiency
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-light-darkness-deficiency.29942/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-08T15:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-light-darkness-deficiency.29942/post-343522>

I’ve been reading the blogs (thanks to whoever posted the study guides) and binge listening on airplane mode to all the podcasts I can get my hands on..

Here are my musings-

We are indoors with shitty lighting and basically starved of essential light nutrients (like UV light, red light, infrared light). We are also bingeing on blue light, which I’ve begun referring to as the white food of the light world.

At the same time, we are also deficient in darkness. Lighting up the night with our artificial sun thinking we are so clever.

And we are overdoing it with carbs. Cozy all the time at 68-70 degrees.

Our bodies are stuck in eternal summer and mismatched with our environment. It’s so obvious once it really hit me. It explains everything.

I can appreciate in podcast 24 with Alexis how Uncle Jack says that light is more important than water and magnetism (not that those aren’t important).

The steps are brutally simple. The lifestyle changes though are tough with people who (as you’ve mentioned before) just simply can’t be inconvenienced. It’s a damn shame.

I can’t believe I was convinced that it was food and exercise at first. Then I was convinced it was stress. Now I know that all plays a role but sunlight is king. Which honestly is refreshing because it FEELS good to be in the sun. This aligns with my intuition that “it shouldn’t be this hard” when I was formerly trying to use elimination diets, gut protocols, and other nonsense to help myself and patients.

Nothing has helped me more than simply getting the fuck outdoors.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-08T20:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-light-darkness-deficiency.29942/post-343546>

>

The original founders of maps.me now are offering a new open source alternative for offline navigation. Any experience w/ these?
I used libreoffice and openoffice instead of ms office. Those worked fine for me.

### paintballtao — 2024-12-11T12:02:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-343690>

you can look into the kickstarter brax3 phone

### pietermj — 2024-12-19T04:33:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-344102>

F-Droid is good. It indicates if any app contains undesired features (like tracking, ads, ...). Apps are compiled/build directly from source and reproducible builds are offered. This means that the source the app is built from actually does correspond to the source the developer claims it comes from. Use it in combination with [https://reports.exodus-privacy.eu.org/en/](https://reports.exodus-privacy.eu.org/en/) to check for app apk permissions and trackers.
But of course nothing beats installing linux on a mobile with hardware switches for wifi etc...
I use
\- firefox with ublock addin (yes you can install any addin on mobile firefox!) (not f-droid but fully open source) also addin allows neaarly ad free youtube and screenless continuous playback (or try revanced).
\- keepassxc on desktop and one of their approved apps on android (eg Keepass2android).
\- amethyst nostr client (over tor)
\- F-droid client over tor
\- syncthing-fork (keeping "backups")
\- unstoppable crypto wallet (bitcoin)
\- anysoft keyboard (slightly more privacy than built-in keyboards)
Ik ben blij hier ook eens iemand van België tegen te komen

### [x.com](https://x.com/pieter_mj_j)

 x.com

### Honeybadger — 2025-11-27T20:56:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-363289>

Aurora store works great as an alternative to google play.

### urko — 2025-11-28T10:05:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-363297>

Graphene OS is an easy to install operative system to google pixel phones, it gives you a free of google environment and lot of turn on/off features like mic, camera, etc...

### Michalis — 2025-11-28T10:53:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-363298>

e/OS is also de-googled

### Honeybadger — 2025-11-28T10:56:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-363299>

> [urko said:](/goto/post?id=363297)

> Graphene OS is an easy to install operative system to google pixel phones, it gives you a free of google environment and lot of turn on/off features like mic, camera, etc...

### NickD — 2024-12-13T22:52:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplement-stamets7.29964/post-343861>

I looked into Stamets stuff a while ago, and it was going to get too expensive. I ended up with Lifecykel products. But, it's one of those things that you can't really "feel"...

### Adamo — 2024-12-13T23:18:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplement-stamets7.29964/post-343863>

I hate to be the contrarian here, but separate Stamets and his knowledge/experience with mushrooms and his brand/product.

Mushrooms can be grown on any substrate. If you have grown your own, you know. Creating a supplement for health, growing them on rice as the substrate and adding in the mycelium is NOT something traditionally done in China or Japan. Using mushrooms medicinally is most commonly recorded in those countries, and is ABSOLUTELY valid. Mushrooms of many different varieties work very well, and the quality in the "market-place" is very, very different. (The spectrum is great.)

I understand that for business there is a scaling and speed issue at hand, but if we are all here trying to bring natural healthy/balance back to the body based upon what nature intended us for, why take something grown in a non-naturally occurring manner?

They are even growing cordyceps in liquid substrates and trying to synthetically propagate Chaga mushrooms. You might register the same chemicals in a lab-study/test, but does that mean the effect is the same as a cordyceps grown on a live insect or chaga on a birch tree in the colder climate (all natural occurrences)? I doubt it.

Barefoot doctors would rarely put that many different mushrooms or principal ingredients in the same herbal formula as well. Nor do all of those mushrooms grow in the same environment together. Again, we need to use discretion here.

There are small companies that focus on wild-harvested or grow mushrooms outdoors on logs. They are often a bit more pricey.

FYI: I fully believe in and use adaptogenic, natural compounds such as mushrooms, maca, etc. I'm not against them at all.

# Thread: Finally discovered my mom prob got blue light/EMF poisoned
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-discovered-my-mom-prob-got-blue-light-emf-poisoned.29967/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### madliv — 2024-12-13T14:57:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-discovered-my-mom-prob-got-blue-light-emf-poisoned.29967/post-343832>

Not sure if this is the right thread for this but I wanted to share something that it is been puzzling me for years until I saw the light!

Just wanted to comment the case of my mother... just to put a tinny bit of context my family is from Spain and in both sides there are examples of generations of elderly people in great health condition (mentally and physically). The father of my mother died by a stupid mistake at 98 (year 2004) quite deaf but with a very clear mind and quite well preserved motor skills (during many years he would go daily for his morning walk to the beach under the Eastern Spanish sun...).

So my mother was always in great health condition but suddenly she started to lose weight at 73 (she has always been in good shape but then she started to look too skinny) and was diagnosed with diabetes type II (which was unexpected) but so small that she has to have the bare minimum medication to avoid hypoglycaemic episodes - that she actually now and then has -. So she is now 83 and for many years I have been scratching my head wondering where all of that came from! I thought it was stress related as she is a quite nervous person but it didn't quite fit... until I started listening to Jack Kruse and I realised my mom had been poisoned by the light. Also important to notice that my mother was from 3 years old (in the mid 1940s) to 15 (in the early 60s) living in a town in the north of Spain (post civil war) with no electricity! with icy winters and not entirely warm clothes yet very healthy entirely eating locally and basically living outdoors with poisonous snakes running around - that didn't kill anybody because the locals knew what to do to avoid them - haha. She has told me stories about being wet feet frozen in the snow and picking up whatever the trees had of season with her friends... my great grandmother had natural remedies for anything and make do with whatever they had to make creative recipes everyone enjoyed.

So, back to the future, what happened around that time my mom got her diagnose...in 2014... I am guessing must have been around the time my dad decided to switch to all led lights at home and they both got smart phones and tablets to read books... also my mom always using sunglasses didn't help.

So now I am trying to get my mom back to the sun with no make up… not an easy task but I am working on it. Also she got cataracts surgery a bit ago with all the light blocking that that implies.
My dad is a bookworm, as well, so I am trying to dump as much information as I can but with the language barrier it is not as easy as it could be but hopefully I can get him on board and get them both into their terrace in the AM light. I also got them blue light blockers recently.

Thanks for reading if you did


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hi All,
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all.29970/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### annav — 2024-12-13T23:10:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all.29970/post-343862>

I am new member here although I was on the forum back in 2021 for about six months. I solved some of my issues and learned a lot along the way. I am back because I want to learn more, heal more, help my family and share my knowledge with others who want to listen.

I wanted to post something that dawned on me the other day. My Mom had early Alzheimer's that showed up in her early 60's (I am 59) and three of her sisters also had Alzheimer's. She passed away in her 70's. I always thought my Mom's Alzheimer's was due to bad genes, chronic stress, medications and not eating enough seafood. Don't get me wrong, those are still factors, but after listening to Jack and reading his blogs, I believe the bigger factors were: circadian mismatch (from Italy and moved to Chicago), not outside enough, always used suntan lotion and she wore sunglasses when outside.

Guess what? I was headed down the same path! Luckily, I learned about the benefits of light three years ago and I changed my habits. I see sunrise every day, sunset most days, outside as much as possible, eat within an hour of sunrise, ground, no sunglasses, no suntan lotion, shut wifi off at night (hard wire is next), respect darkness at night and I am no longer in front of a computer 16 hours a day. I have resolved a lot of minor health issues but still working on a few and hope to keep improving. What's really nice is my husband has finally turned the corner. Once he listened to Jack's explanation of the history including MKUltra, Robert Becker and the Church Committee, it all made sense to him and now he prioritizes the sun and even takes his shirt off! We are a work in progress. I am jealous and admire all of you moving to a tropical climate and maybe that will be us one day.

### Adamo — 2024-12-13T23:26:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all.29970/post-343865>

Welcome back!

I have seen similar things in my family as well. I tend to remind them about their agrarian lifestyle in 'the Boot' and how they did things to respect the natural biorythym, and when them came to the Americas, it all went out the window. It's tough to get people to acknowledge that we need a change and a lot of our modern habits/addictions are actually harming us, but glad to see so many people embracing the changes and knowledge.

Illinois has a similar environment as Ontario, Canada, so I can relate in many ways.

### annav — 2024-12-15T22:17:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all.29970/post-343954>

> [Adamo said:](/goto/post?id=343865)
>
> Welcome back!
>
> I have seen similar things in my family as well. I tend to remind them about their agrarian lifestyle in 'the Boot' and how they did things to respect the natural biorythym, and when them came to the Americas, it all went out the window. It's tough to get people to acknowledge that we need a change and a lot of our modern habits/addictions are actually harming us, but glad to see so many people embracing the changes and knowledge.
>
> Illinois has a similar environment as Ontario, Canada, so I can relate in many ways.

Thanks Adamo!

You're so fortunate that you can at least try and teach them. My parents passed before I could share this knowledge. My Mom would have embraced all my recommendations. My dad would have been more challenging. I still have family in Italy. They walk everywhere, cook and eat outside. Those three fairly simple habits would have definitely helped my Italian parents. I have found that when I wear my blue blocking glasses I get a lot of questions and it's a perfect time to give the elevator pitch about light to friends and family. Good luck to you!

I have two options for glass bottled water in UK that are financially sensible. Highland spring is what i've bought for awhile, i use VOSS to make survivor soup and to drink every so often, but i recently found a brand called cheddar spring that has a high mineral content and is half the price. As far as I'm aware highland spring has 0.005 fluoride content, cheddar valley has 0.05. Is this even worth considering over whether i should spend more or save with the one with slightly higher fluoride with higher mineral content at half the price? I don't know if im splitting hairs over nothing or this is a considerable difference in PPM that i should be considerate of.
