### Adamo — 2025-01-14T02:55:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-345148>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=344947)
>
> Oh, it’s definitely not the real cause. My mineral levels are still recovering from being raised vegetarian. But I was hoping to find a way to overcome that issue so i can take advantage of drinking supercooled water. I guess ill have to try again after eating oysters even more.

For sure, I just want to be clear I'm not an expert... simply personal experience.

You could add fulvic minerals, if you choose. Things such as shilajit as well. But also eating organ meats can help big time with many minerals.
I personally prefer to find natural means, without going the supplemental route. As the gentleman said above, finding a very high-quality sea salt can be added to water, or make your food a little extra salty to get that intake in. You can look at things such as sardines, and certain dried fruits to get other minerals and content, such as apricots. If I am not mistaken, it's always best to get mineral content through soaking the body (skin), such as being in the ocean, and eating food sources that have "processed" them.

I reside about 1 hour or so north-east of your location, so we share a similar environment. Wishing you the best on your journey to recovery!
You will achieve it!

I'm not familiar with "supercool" water, but would love to know more.

>
>
> yes, incorporating all the above currently, high seafood diet, with organ meats, shilajit, and epsom salt CT
>
> In Uncle Jack’s webinars from 2012 and 2013 he talked about the benefits of drinking super cooled RO water. (He may speak of it again but I am working through all the webinars from oldest to newest)
> Basically, you take RO water and freeze it then drink it the next morning as it will remain liquid only if NO minerals have been added.
> I am interested in tapping into those benefits

Thank you for sharing. Never heard of this at all, or what the benefits are of the super cooled RO. Interesting.

### Emmanuela — 2025-01-18T16:20:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-345358>

> [Adamo said:](/goto/post?id=345357)
>
> Thank you for sharing. Never heard of this at all, or what the benefits are of the super cooled RO. Interesting.

When water is supercooled, it’s as close to coherent water as possible.
He was saying it’s used for PTSD. It’s in his sleep RX and said it’s beneficial when you are doing CT.

### Adamo — 2025-01-18T16:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-345359>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=345358)
>
> When water is supercooled, it’s as close to coherent water as possible.
> He was saying it’s used for PTSD. It’s in his sleep RX and said it’s beneficial when you are doing CT.

Ahhh thank you! I have been filling up quality glass bottles with my RO and putting them outside in the Sun.
Then I drink them.

Not saying this is corollary to your case, but once I opened up the areas around my shoulders/clavicle/neck/jaw, tooth sensitivity was gone for me.
Previous powerlifting and computer work had my upper body very tight. I also had a head trauma over a decade ago, which definitely contributed to this.

Also rebuilding enamel helps, where you can use pure xylitol as a "mouth wash" after meals to cut down the bacteria that may lead to the "acidity" and breakdown of the enamel barrier. Eating many small meals or snacking all of the time doesn't aid in the mouth rebuilding and maintaining it's balance. Also having the mouth open all of time, whether talking a lot, or mouth-breathing, or when sleeping.

**We live in a sh\*t place for sunlight, and that is #1.** I think we have had less than 1 full week of decent light in the past two months. It has been gray skies / covered skies and dark for too long.

For me, EVERYTHING, even the garbage parts of life, feels better when I have Sun. But I have been addicted to the Sun since a child, and have a lot of Leo placements in my natal chart (ruled by the Sun), so for me, no Sun is **hell.**

### Redfish3 — 2025-05-31T17:20:06+0300

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=344989)
>
> Jack Kruse was really clear in the webinars. You cannot supercool RO water if it has been remineralized.

What does supercool mean? I’m still waiting for access to the past webinar so I can’t check it out. thanks

### MTA321 — 2025-05-31T19:07:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-353644>

> [Redfish3 said:](/goto/post?id=353641)
>
> What does supercool mean? I’m still waiting for access to the past webinar so I can’t check it out. thanks

RO is lower density water and is more coherent. Supercooled RO can stay liquid when put in the freezer but the structure can be easily disrupted making it freeze.

The main benefit to supercooled RO with CT is more efficient semiconduction allowing better transmission of photons/electrons.

# Thread: Success Story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-story.30077/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mitopower — 2025-01-07T22:15:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-story.30077/post-344866>

Hello all, I thought I would share my weight loss journey and now my optimal microcondriac life style change.

I started the traditional diet first before discovering Uncle Jack. You will notice starting Oct 2024 i hit a wall and stopped losing weight even thought i was eating between 800-1200 calories a day!
I am a 6' 1" 48yr old male who lives 20 miles outside Seattle.

5/1/2024 Started ~305lbs cut out fast food.
6/20 285lbs started pill mounjaro/lowered carbs.
7/30 285lbs started injection mounjaro/lowered carbs
8/6 276lbs
8/13 276lbs
8/20 270lbs
9/3 267lbs
9/10 262lbs
9/17 258lbs
9/24 254lbs-256lbs
10/1 254lbs-256lbs
Started eating 800-1200cal/day, treadmill
10/8 254lbs-256lbs
10/15 254lbs-256lbs
10/23 254lbs-256lbs
10/30 254lbs-256lbs
11/3 254lbs-256lbs Started biohacks
ENTER JACK KRUSE!
11/11 249lbs
11/18 244lbs
11/25 241lbs
12/2 236lbs
Reduced mounjaro by half
12/9 233lbs
12/17 232lbs
Cancun Vacation
12/31 233lbs
Now Off Mounjaro!
1/7/2025 228lbs

My typical day in winter here in Seallte involves eating twice a day, breakfast and dinner. I get up a half hour before sunrise and eat breakfast within a hour of waking. This will consist of Salmon, grass fed steak, eggs, or bacon. Dinner is around 4pm-5pm. Dinner will be the same but I will sometimes add more variety like shrimp, sometimes an entrée of mushroom chicken at panda express (once a week), halibut, steelhead. SEAFOOD=DHA. I have seafood probably in ~8 meals a week.

There is not a lot of sunlight right now in seattle hence the Vacation to cancun where I got a little pink!

Before dinner I do,
\*Red Light Therapy for 15min front and back everyday.
\*Sperti UV lamp 5min everyday alternating front one day and then back the next.
\*Some sprints on a treadmill every other day.
After dinner I do,
\*Cold Plunge @ ~47degrees for 20min everyday.
\*bottled reverse osmosis water
\*Bluelight blocker glasses
\*All light bulbs in house changed to no blue light.

After starting this regiment I went from sleeping half the day/taking naps/not having energy/not wanting to do things/getting mad or frustrated easily at wife and kids.
TO having lots of energy/no more napping/wanting to do things/and not getting mad or frustrated easily.
I'm down over 75lbs now in 8 months and still losing weight. Thank you uncle Jack!

LIGHT, WATER, MAGNETISM

\*\*\*\*\* [https://jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/](https://jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/)

\*\*\*\*\* [https://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)

# Thread: Reliable heavy metal / mercury tests? B4 considering amalgam removal. (Teeth/amalgam/EHS/nnEMFs)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### eve001 — 2025-01-09T22:31:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-344970>

Hi, Who knows of a reliable way to test for heavy metal toxicity in the body?

In a recent podcast by Cameron Borg with Ole Johanson - Swedish EMF expert - I heard that depending on the brand different amalgam fillings have shown to release more or less amalgam in front of tech devices.

I regularly feel weird stuff around one of my two teeth that have amalgam in them.
I wonder if it is useful to have that amalgam filling removed, since I am very sensitive to nnEMFs.

On facebook years ago Dr. Kruse shared this research article on how MRI and nnEMFs release mercury from amalgams fillings.
Incl. bad artifical light. He also mentioned some heavy metal screening. Does anybody knows what he'd recommend? Something with spectroscopy? Where could one get that?

Some context:
I may have extra mercury load from allergy shots I got as a teenager - or not.
I also had braces and after that a metallic thread keeping my lower teeth in place - I had that for over 20 years and had it removed recently.
My tongue hated it and always was going over it. Nowadays my tongue is more relaxed, except when this amalgam filling gets weird.
I asked several dentists who say all seems fine. My other amalgam filling never causes weird sensations in my mouth.
Yesterday I had lots of exposure, to RF and to bad lighting and today this one tooth is feeling weird again.

It sounded as if such a heavy metal screen Dr. Kruse mentioned long ago may be important when deciding whether to remove/replace a filling or not.

I know of the risks of poor removal: the light in the dentist space, the speed of the drill, the poor extraction of the Hg vapor.
I also know that melanin - and sunshine and melanin Rx - are considered great for healing heavy metal toxicity.
I know deuterium is thought of as a heavy metal.

Who knows what heavy metal test would be reliable? And what kind of test Dr. Kruse would recommend today or has recommended in the past?
Is it true that he is not a fan of HTMA?

### pfedorow — 2025-01-10T20:21:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-345020>

Removing them will cause more issues and the risks outweigh any benefits . I don't think he recommends any tests .

### eve001 — 2025-01-11T16:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-345040>

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=345020)
>
> Removing them will cause more issues and the risks outweigh any benefits . I don't think he recommends any tests .

Hi there, he does - or did - but it's rather vague.

You may want to google Dr. Kruse + mercury or amalgam + resonance to research what he wrote.

I will be very happy to learn from people who know more about this.

### Old Dog New Tricks — 2025-01-11T19:52:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-345057>

I had all of them removed and replaced with amalgam by a Natural dentist back in the early 2000's and still doing well at 74. I also regularly clean my mouth and teeth with 3% Hydrogen Peroxide which I told the hygenist when asked what I do to keep them so clean. When I went for check up last June it had been over a year since I saw the dentist and did not need any work done both times. They also don't like that I use a toothpick instead of fluoride covered floss Hehe!

### eve001 — 2025-01-11T21:00:43+0200

Hi there, thx 4 sharing. I think you mean you had the amalgams removed, not? What did you have them replaced with? The vapour sounds more compromising. I didn't know there may be fluoride on the floss stuff! Good to know.

### Old Dog New Tricks — 2025-01-12T18:25:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-345091>

# Thread: Grounding while cycling?

### Guy Houri — 2025-01-10T19:07:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-while-cycling.30087/post-345014>

Anyone tried something that worked for grounding their bike?
Im cosidering:
1\. just draggin a copper coil from my feet, but i imagne it can be dangerous if it gets stuck on something so i don't think it'll be a good option ..
2\. Maybe connecting a car anti static grounding strip to the bike's frame? Recently i tried grounding my car with a cheap iron strip from [Temu](https://www.temu.com/goods.html?_bg_fs=1&goods_id=601099613270044&sku_id=17592578841452&_x_sessn_id=d2df3ic12j&refer_page_name=bgt_order_detail&refer_page_id=10045_1736528222191_rxy70e8u4o&refer_page_sn=10045). After one uneasy bumpy road it kinda fell aprart, stayed on the exhaust pipe but the tip stopped touching the ground. So i also need to buy a different one.

What do you guys think?
Thanks

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-10T21:32:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-while-cycling.30087/post-345022>

WHY? . . . I can see so many possible dangers with dragging a wire from a bike. Plus - you cannot ground on asphalt. For optimal grounding you need to be outside, away from buildings & power lines . . . with your skin in direct contact with the earth/ground.

I recommend you enjoy your ride and when you get to a perfect low/no nnEMF grounding spot - stop and put your bare feet on the ground, take a deep breath and relax/enjoy.

### Guy Houri — 2025-01-11T18:22:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-while-cycling.30087/post-345048>

>
> I recommend you enjoy your ride and when you get to a perfect low/no nnEMF grounding spot - stop and put your bare feet on the ground, take a deep breath and relax/enjoy.

### TheOnlyEZ — 2025-01-13T18:27:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lucia-eyes-failed-specrometer-test.30091/post-345130>

My favorite blue blockers so far are from Midwest Red Light Therapy and EMR Tek. MRLT is cheapest and they block better than the Lucia Eyes I bought my wife. The Lucia also have a defect or something that makes letters on screens/books hazy and run together. I thought my wife just needed to build up a tolerance when she first started complaining, but I had her my MRLT and clarity was definitely noticeable. I tried both and confirmed. So now we're out $180 for glasses that really just work for environment lighting. Could have just been a bad batch lens during production, but that hole in my wallet hurts.

Thanks, Rusty. I'd still like to get my family members some blue light blockers, but they're expensive; I paid over a $100 for my BLUblox. Do you think any old pair of glasses would work to block blue light so long as they're the appropriate shade of deep orange or red?

### Rusty — 2025-01-14T19:18:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lucia-eyes-failed-specrometer-test.30091/post-345185>

> [La-Shawn Hill said:](/goto/post?id=345171)
>
> Thanks, Rusty. I'd still like to get my family members some blue light blockers, but they're expensive; I paid over a $100 for my BLUblox. Do you think any old pair of glasses would work to block blue light so long as they're the appropriate shade of deep orange or red?

Hey La,

No, the lenses need to be dyed using a specific composition at a specific temperature.

If you're in India, you can get mine ([www.besolarpowered.com](http://www.besolarpowered.com)) (they're just $40 each and block upto 550nm)

or if you are outside, you can get the UVEX glasses, they're cheap and do the job.

### NickD — 2025-02-01T14:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lucia-eyes-failed-specrometer-test.30091/post-346072>

Quick update, the BonCharge glasses I got were prescription yellow lenses. The lenses work great at night (I'm a 24/7 shift worker in a Control Room environment - I need to see colours properly). The arms were too short and didn't rest on my ears properly. I emailed BonCharge and they said they can't do anything. I then went to my optometrist and they were able to straighten the arms out a bit and they fit perfectly now. I'm happy with the product and recommend it. Hope it helps someone

### Daulatwant — 2025-02-06T19:19:53+0200

> [Aaron K said:](/goto/post?id=346262)
>
> Anyone have any experience with anything like this inexpensive smartphone spectrometer?

This is selling you a peice of cardboard with a template printed on it. You would have to make it yourself. It may not end up working as expected, there is gonna be limitations

Might as well make it youtself from scratch, since it may take some trial and error until you can get it working well enough even with the kit from amazon.

Here it shows how to make one using a webcam.

The materials used to make the chamber don't matter, cardboard or whatever you have. You just want it as dark as possible and minimal reflections.

# Thread: Where Do I Start?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-i-start.30092/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LarryInNashville — 2025-01-11T18:58:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-i-start.30092/post-345052>

I've watched many videos of Dr. Jack Kruse over the past week, but I could not find any step-by-step instructions for starting the process. Can anyone provide a link or PDF on how to get started? I learned from the videos that 1. Add blue-blocking apps on my phone and computer. Dr Kruse recommended the Iris app on The Danny Jones podcast. I can download it on my laptop but now my Android phone. How do I resolve this issue? 2 Replace LED lights with incandescent lights. I ordered 100W incandescent lights from Amazon. I ordered 100w lights because I could not find any guidance on what to look for when selecting light bulbs. 3. Get morning sunlight. I could not find any guidance for when to start, what duration when to stop, what time of day, or reasons why certain times may be better than others. 4. Grounded/earthing... I've been grounding/earthing every day for at least 7 years. What do we do about TVs? Can I find blue-blocking filters for my tv? I would truly appreciate any help in getting started. It would be great if there were a check-off list that beginners can use to get started. As a disabled veteran, I'm poor as fuck. I don't have money to buy expensive gadgets or travel the world for better sunlight.

### Jeanne — 2025-01-11T19:39:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-i-start.30092/post-345056>

I would start with Dr. Kruse's book "Epi-Paleo RX." Download it on Kindle. AM Sun is always the first step and you just do it every single day for the rest of your life. Just go outside and make sure your eyes see the am sunlight. It's winter in Nashville so you aren't getting Vit. D sun but as soon as spring rolls around get as much as you can. Nashville is not the best because of so much EMF from population explosion there. I left Franklin 6 years ago and it's worse now. Stop eating at sunset and put on your blue-blocking glasses.

### LarryInNashville — 2025-01-11T19:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-i-start.30092/post-345059>

Thanks... Is the "Epi-Paleo RX" primarily about food? Does it discuss sunlight and sungazing? I've been a carnivore and plan to return. i felt amazing on it.

### Jeanne — 2025-01-31T22:34:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-i-start.30092/post-346045>

> [LarryInNashville said:](/goto/post?id=345059)
>
> Thanks... Is the "Epi-Paleo RX" primarily about food? Does it discuss sunlight and sungazing? I've been a carnivore and plan to return. i felt amazing on it.

Not about food. He does recommend eating lots of seafood but mainly it's about how to start on becoming a mitochondria. Starting with AM sun and eating a big breakfast within 30 mins. Just get the book and follow it! It will help you get started! Good luck!

# Thread: EMR-TEK Red-light Devices

### Paul 22 — 2025-01-12T03:09:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emr-tek-red-light-devices.30094/post-345078>

We are shopping EMR -TEK red-light devices. There are several different combinations of wattages and red light frequencies, and almost no overlap between the models. We are using this for general heath and joint issues. Can anyone please advise on what would be a minimum wattage and specific frequencies to get so we can chose wisely. Thank you.

### NickD — 2025-01-12T05:14:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emr-tek-red-light-devices.30094/post-345080>

I have a couple of EMR panels—the Krypton and Fire Dragon—but I use them because I work shifts. If you want targeted joint gear, maybe check out [https://getchroma.co/collections/all-devices](https://getchroma.co/collections/all-devices).

If you venture down the EMR path, they gave me a code for others to use "NICHOLAS72365". It should give you a discount.

### Paul 22 — 2025-01-12T06:28:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emr-tek-red-light-devices.30094/post-345081>

Thanks Nick.

# Thread: RhoGAM - can it contain harmful substances from COVID-19 vaccines?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rhogam-can-it-contain-harmful-substances-from-covid-19-vaccines.30096/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Leeloo Dallas Multipass — 2025-01-12T20:56:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rhogam-can-it-contain-harmful-substances-from-covid-19-vaccines.30096/post-345096>

Hello everyone!
Me and my husband are planning to start a family. I am Rh-negative, my husband is Rh-positive so I will be getting the RhoGAM shots during any future pregnancy to ensure I don't make antibodies that could attack the Rh-positive blood cells of a fetus (if the fetus is Rh-positive).
I've read these shots are made from human blood and I therefore wonder if there is a chance the shot will be contaminated with something harmful from the covid vaccine. Is that possible?

### HLG2025 — 2025-10-16T14:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rhogam-can-it-contain-harmful-substances-from-covid-19-vaccines.30096/post-361103>

I am new to the group and only learned about Dr Kruse about a week ago...I am wondering if this could explain the issues my wife is having. She is 56 years old is 5'2" and weighs 105 lbs...same as in high school. Never smoked and never drinks alcohol. She eats fish, grass fed lamb and beef, pastured eggs...organic fruits and veggies, plenty of leafy greens...she exercises 7 days per week. She walks 7 miles per day and does other exercises. So on paper, it would seem she is doing everything right...HOWEVER, she had some "pre cancerous" spots removed by a dermatologist in her 20's and 30's and thus has wore sunscreen religiously for the past 30 years. She also always wears sunglasses per the advice of eye doctor. We are both freaking out now as 2 of her cousins about the same age both had widow maker heart attacks in the past 6 months. # Thread: Please advise: heart fluid drain and wet cold feet.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-heart-fluid-drain-and-wet-cold-feet.30106/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kvutborg — 2025-01-13T22:04:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-heart-fluid-drain-and-wet-cold-feet.30106/post-345140>

Fellow savages,

**i.** I learned that my father recently visited the hospital concerning his heart. I only know that he is in pain, near to having his heart fluid drained, and debating if he wants to consume prescription blood thinners. It has to be bad for him to actually go there, he generally stays away. I searched this forum, Kruse’s blogs, and elsewhere online and would very much appreciate guidance on two separate issues.

It seems to me, through my browsing, that his main issue is likely that one way or another his heart is experiencing inflammation. At a minimum, it seems that is how this started. I intend on attempting to persuade him to directly absorb sunlight, to plant his bare feet in the earth, and to direct him to the Leptin Rx. I also want him to learn about melanin and mitochondria.

In the last several weeks, I have noticed a massive difference in my blood circulation from being sandwiched by the sun and earth. I even accidentally experienced chi for the very first time! There was a minimal force pulling my palms toward each other, when I went to pull them apart after 25 minutes of direct sun and earth.

We can help him reduce inflammation, which should reduce the size of his heart (providing it is inflamed). I know there is more to this, though.

Is there a way to shrink the fluid without stabbing it?
If we reduce inflammation, should his pain lessen?
Will deuterium depleted water help his heart?

I remember reading or hearing about something that might be relevant a little while ago, but I have not found it again yet. Allegedly, introducing H2O2 to the body (topically, I think?) allows us to harvest extra oxygen. Do any of you know more about this and could it help him?

**ii.** I got excited to be in the cold (~0°c) yesterday at sunset and immediately played in water with my shins and feet. The idea was to increase conductivity between my body and the sand. I was there for at least an hour, only shorts/beanie/gloves, and now I am having symptoms of what google alleges to be trench foot.

It feels like my left lymph node is inflamed and there is some mucous clotting in the left side of my face (including nostril). I have had a migraine all day, and I have brain fog. My heart rate is elevated (96 bpm) and my forehead is warm. Pushing up on the left side of my forehead relieves pressure temporarily. As I am writing this, I am remembering that drinking hot water and maybe hot compresses might loosen it up. I will do that after posting this.

The sun did not peak through the clouds, today, so no sun for me. I assume the remedy is more earthing and a bunch of oranges (Vitamin C), is there anything else I should do?

I am planning to hop into the cold water tomorrow for at least 5 minutes. No sun, UV bone time! Would that help me or make this worse?

I live in a desert that is always dry and hot, so this windy cold environment is foreign to my body. I have not been taking ice baths or cold showers recently. I clearly shocked my body, but I am thinking it might need another shock.

Any guidance is immensely appreciated.

It is possible that I am not understanding the complicated language involved in this industry, but I definitely tried my best to find answers. Also, remember my brain is not functioning normally. Today, I am moving slow mentally and physically. Thank you for sharing your time with me.

P.S. Jack Kruse is an absolute legend for blowing the whistle as hard as he has. I will absolutely start spending money on his website when I climb out of my current financial position.

Edit:
i. Added increased heart rate and warm forehead for trench foot symptoms.

# Thread: Stetzerizer vs BVK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jackfoster — 2025-01-15T17:47:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/post-345238>

Can a BVK detect dirty electricity as well as a statsorizer can?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-15T19:03:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/post-345246>

> [jackfoster said:](/goto/post?id=345238)
>
> Can a BVK detect dirty electricity as well as a statsorizer can?

BVK is designed to measure body voltage NOT dirty electricity (DE), two different things. The Stetzerizer only measures in a narrow range of frequencies that their filters reduce. There could be a lot there that professional equipment would measure that the Stetzerizer is missing.

When you turn off a circuit you remove the electric field and 100% of the “DE”.

### jackfoster — 2025-01-15T22:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/post-345259>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=345246)
>
> BVK is designed to measure body voltage NOT dirty electricity (DE), two different things. The Stetzerizer only measures in a narrow range of frequencies that their filters reduce. There could be a lot there that professional equipment would measure that the Stetzerizer is missing.
>
> When you turn off a circuit you remove the electric field and 100% of the “DE”.

couldn't you detect d e spots with a BVK but you would just not be able to get a numbered measurement of it as you would with a stetzerizer?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-16T06:13:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/post-345269>

“DE” is an artifact/harmonic of the electric field and it is a tiny % of the nnEMF. The BVK measures the strength of the electric field being induced into your body. Maybe 1% - 2% of that is “DE”. The Stetzerizer measures the “DE“ (just the “DE”) on any circuit. Reduce the ”DE” and you have 98% of the nnEMF 60Hz (50Hz) electric field still in place. “DE” is part of the electric field which is measured separately from the magnetic field which is completely different than the RF.

People seam to be obsessed with “DE” . . . It is a mouse . . . The elephant in the room is the 50/60Hz AC electric field. Turn off the circuits, especially at night, and solve BOTH problems 100%.

# Thread: SAAT - exceptional band aid for those suffering from food or seasonal allergies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gian — 2025-01-16T23:00:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/post-345299>

I just wanted to share this unique acupuncture in the ear treatment with the community as I presume many members on here also have or have had issues with food allergies and seasonal allergies. The procedure is abbreviated as SAAT and it has been around for a many decades but seems relatively unknown to most of us.

About a year ago (well before I discovered Dr. Kruse) I was struggling with my body reacting to most foods (lectins, all forms of sugars, gluten etc.) and through internet searching I read about a procedure that had proven effective for helping folks that were allergic to meat (alpha gal). I had never done any acupuncture before, nor did I believe in acupuncture as a modality, but I was desperate so I figured for a $200 procedure I was willing to roll the dice. The procedure involved muscle testing (which was also new to me) to determine what I was allergic to, then tiny needles were implanted in my ear at specific locations, covered by a small band aid and left there for three weeks. During the three weeks the needles were in my ear, I was told to avoid the foods that we were treating for (in my case I think it was gluten, sugar, sesame, wheat, lectins). After three weeks, my wife removed the small needles with tweezers and I began experimenting with foods. I was shocked with the results. Prior to the SAAT treatment, my stomach was so hypersensitive, and after the treatment, I was regularly having excellent BMs even while eating foods that I was allergic to prior to the procedure. I drank a craft beer for the first time in seven years (I wasn't even sure if I would still like the taste) and did fine.

I will not pretend like I know how it worked, but it sure did for me. The acupuncturist said for some patients the results last for a short while, while others it last for several years and some for a lifetime. For me, it was about a year and I started having issues again and was worse than ever with my system becoming intolerant of everything. I even became intolerant of bone broth (I was told it was the histamine in the bone broth). I went back and did another round of the SAAT, but this time, because of how angry my system was, he only put needles in for mast cell, and this round the needles stayed in for four weeks. Thanks be to God, the SAAT treatment was very successful and I went from the worst my stomach/BMs had ever been, to once again regularly having the coveted Bristol #4s Uncle Jack prides himself on.

The needles go in the right ear and the acupuncturist said that is because that spot in the right ear is associated with the liver. Although I don't believe this treatment is curative and does not likely address the underlying issue(s) with one's health problems, it vastly improved my situation and my quality of life. So, if you have bad seasonal allergies or food allergies, you may want to give the SAAT treatment a try and hopefully it can help you find some relief.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-17T01:25:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/post-345304>

I remember watching an open heart surgery years ago, the patient’s chest was open but he was awake and talking to the Dr.’s. There were no drugs used just a few needles. Changed my mind about acupuncture and at least considering other ”alternative” healing modalities.

### MikeR — 2025-01-18T21:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/post-345365>

I've been going to an acupuncturist for about 5 months for knee issues and to see if he can help me regain a sense of smell. He puts needles in my ears, jaw, arms, and legs. It works for knee pain and the tiniest bit for my sense of smell. Really quite amazing. I stopped with the sense of smell treatment for now because the jaw needles were pretty uncomfortable and I wanted to concentrate on the knee more.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-18T22:11:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/post-345366>

> [MikeR said:](/goto/post?id=345365)
>
> I've been going to an acupuncturist for about 5 months for knee issues and to see if he can help me regain a sense of smell. He puts needles in my ears, jaw, arms, and legs. It works for knee pain and the tiniest bit for my sense of smell. Really quite amazing. I stopped with the sense of smell treatment for now because the jaw needles were pretty uncomfortable and I wanted to concentrate on the knee more.

### Jon Hinman MD DVM — 2025-01-18T23:44:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/post-345369>

I studied acupuncture while in the Army. via the Helm's School of Acupuncture. I performed an array of Cranial Scalp techniques on one of the Family Practice Army docs She had been suffering from a post viral vertigo for the last 6 months. ### Mat.I — 2025-01-24T07:54:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-results.30132/post-345683>

Disclaimer: I'm not a MD.. TSH is slightly elevated based on standard ref ranges ( & higher based on Jack's ranges).. Others appear within the standard ref ranges. Based on a decentralised approach - I would focus on fixing the light environment (especially ALAN) as disrupted melatonin signalling could elevate TSH as the pituitary gland compensates for this. nn-emf can also impair the function. Recommendations:

1\. Fix the light environment,
2\. Limit nn-EMF,
3\. Get all your clocks set via supporting a good circadian rhythm,
4\. Watch the sunrises, plenty of sun throughout the day (stay dark at night),
5\. Eat plenty of seafood
6\. grounding outside everyday

### Laudy Cincotta — 2025-01-24T17:47:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-results.30132/post-345691>

> [mitopower said:](/goto/post?id=345398)
>
> Hello, can anyone help me interpret these results?
> Thank you,
> Aaron (mitopower)

Hey [@mitopower](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/37567/),

I am no expert or doctor however that screenshot is nowhere near enough for anyone to help you with.

Could you please share a screenshot including ranges to be able to put your results into perspective.

Also the following questions.

1\. Do you/have you had thyroid issues in the past?
2\. Why the lab test and wanting to interpret results? Are you hypo or hyper thyroid?
3\. Are you on any thyroid medication?
4\. Do you have other labs to compliment this? For example iron levels, Vitamin D, Vitamin B12, Zinc, Magnesium, Potassium, Sodium, Cortisol, Aldosterone, Renin??????
5\. Do you have liver tests and inflammation markers that could be driving things?

Depending on why you had the testing and your symptoms then alot of these other tests may be relevant.

Just looking at the screenshot without reference ranges it looks to me like both your free T3 and free T4 are low and your reverse T3 looks too high.

Your best to see if you could get a consult with Jack with more testing info so he can see a clearer picture.

Good luck.

It seems without any other info that there may be a conversion issue and instead of becoming useful your T3 is becoming reverse T3 indicating a move towards more hypo thyroid type symptoms.

I only know that becasue i am trying to work out my own thyroid issues and i may be incorrect as i am not a doctor however thats what i have understood so far.

### Emmanuela — 2025-01-20T03:46:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subcellular-melatonin-and-glutamate-mdd-connection.30135/post-345429>

Glutamate in the brain plays a big role in depression
And from the research I was reading, glutamate release increases in darkness and is suppressed by light.

Subcellular melatonin requires IR light and when UVA enters the eye, we make serotonin which then turns into melatonin.

Artificial blue light significantly reduces production of melatonin, seratonin and dopamine. And we can’t make subcellular melatonin either in these conditions.

So having dark dark nights increases glutamate more than light polluted nights and then in daytime, sunlight increases seratonin, dopamine, and melatonin.
Therefore improving mental health

More experienced Krusers -
am I understanding this correctly?

### Mat.I — 2025-01-20T05:27:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subcellular-melatonin-and-glutamate-mdd-connection.30135/post-345435>

Not my field of expertise, however, my understanding is generally as per the description above. **However, in relation to "dark dark nights increasing glutamate more than light polluted nights"** \- my thoughts are maybe this just prevents dysregulations that lead to ecotoxicity and melatonin is needed to protect neurons / mitochondria from ox damage and as I understand it also plays a role in regulating the glutamate ecotoxicity. Therefore ALAN would have a multilayer impact e.g. disrupted clocks > in balance between inhibitory (GABA) & excitatory (glutamate) neurotransmission > higher G levels > more excitatory activity in the brain or can go the other way. Others - poor sleep quality & reduced slow wave sleep, HPA axis & cortisol >sympathetic overdrive > accumulation of glutamate & increased release / excitotoxity etc..

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-vitamin-d.30138/post-345451>

So I don't supplement. I get in the sun and my d was 188 , I'm sick ,brain sick from I'm sure what is hsv1 ,dementia like each time I get an attack ,and spiraling negatively with memory loss etc as time goes on with consequent attacks.
This latest has been by far the most severe so far . Ear eye forehead scalp pain , small blisters in these areas , blurry vision ,clear spot in centre of vision on affected eye jaw and teeth pain ,one sided,with neck pain , major one sided headaches like brain is split in two. No memory, wanting to pass out ,agitated ,angry.
I'm sure this is viral related triggering back into my cns.
My next vit d was 202.
Is any of this possibly related to high d ?

### Mat.I — 2025-01-21T03:29:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-vitamin-d.30138/post-345501>

Your Vit D is not high (assuming you are referencing SI Units) i.e. nmol/L the standard reference ranges are the issue.. Optimal Vit D levels would be ~ Say 70 to 120 ng/ml or 175 to 300nmol/L. Higher range in summer (or year round if jabbed) and lower range in winter etc.. for reference I'm able to maintain no less than 200nmol/L in the dead of winter (without supplementation). Get your environment tested for nn-EMF by a building biologist and implement all of Jack's recommendations with maintaining good circadian rhythms. What do you do for work?

# Thread: Earthing Demonstrations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LarryInNashville — 2025-01-21T03:05:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/post-345500>

### Clint Ober Demonstrates Earthing​

### Measuring Body Voltage & the benefit of Grounding/Earthing​

### The Earthing Movie: The Remarkable Science of Grounding (full documentary)​

### Grounding - The Grounded Documentary Film about "Earthing"​

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-21T21:15:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/post-345526>

In the video above Dr Mercola was sitting in a 60Hz AC electric field. The body voltage meter read 1,000+mV.
He touched the ground pad and the meter reading went down to almost zero . . . BUT . . . he was sill sitting in the same 60Hz AC 1,000+mV field.
THEY DID NOT TURN ANYTHING OFF
Instead do going through the meter to ground the electric field was now following the path of least resistance to ground through his other hand.
It is the difference between being wet in a bath tub vs under a water fall.
If you are in a 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field (inside) and you ground yourself you are now part of that circuit. You have NOT reduced your exposure, you now have that electric field flowing through you the whole time you are grounded. Body voltage meters DO NOT WORK if you are double grounded.

ALSO, if you are in an apartment/condo/house and ground to the building ground, you are now physically attached to a wire array that can consist of several MILES of wire. If you use a ground wire out the window, you have a 50 foot (+/-) metal antenna attached to your body. If metal bed frames and meal springs in a mattress are bad, how can this metal be good?

To reduce your nnEMF 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field exposure, especially at night you NEED to turn off the source of the field. TURN OFF THE CIRCUIT.

I hate to say it but Dr Mercola (and Clint Ober) either do not understand basic electronics OR they are being dishonest to sell products.

Ground outside in nature (away from electric fields) with your body in contact with the ground - NOT a wire.

If they put a meter on the ground pad the reading on it would up by the same amount the other meter went down.

### MikeR — 2025-01-22T00:23:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/post-345546>

Does this mean that all of the electric fields present inside a house are continuously going to ground through all unused electrical receptacle ground ports?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-22T01:04:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/post-345550>

Every home is different. Measure and you will know But basically NO - not through unused receptacles.
Some homes have very high electric fields some very low, it depends on wiring, construction materials etc.
If you have electric fields and you are grounded inside, those fields ARE going to ground through YOU.

Ground (or un plug) all the electrical devices: lamps, computers, printers, toasters etc that are putting out a field.
Which ones are putting out a field? . . . MEASURE. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Mat.I — 2025-01-22T05:10:01+0200

I have posted this elsewhere on the forum in the past however -

In 2000, EPRI published a report called “[\[COLOR=var( --e-global-color-primary )\]The Possible Role of Contact Current\[/COLOR\]](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11015118/) …” which concluded that 1 μA of contact current is sufficient to cause physical health effects in humans.

**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lisa Norton — 2025-01-22T18:24:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/today-marks-my-63rd-trip-around-the-sun-the-last-two-in-el-salvador.30147/post-345583>

I’m Celebrating the second anniversary of my move to El Salvador this month and I’ve never felt better! To all my companions on the journey- ovarian cancer, environmental disregulation, fibromyalgia, migraine, leaky gut, candida, brain fog, ADHD, hypothyroid, Hashimoto’s thyroiditis, HPA disregulation, osteopenia, estrogen dominance, thyroid nodules, onychomycosis, muscle loss, hair loss, libido loss, memory loss, frozen shoulder, myopia, and many more … - deep bow and BUH-BYE…!

Mucho, mucho gracias to Uncle Jack, Aunt Chantal and all my fellow savages!

# Thread: Cryo Chamber Experience?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MJanowiecki1 — 2025-01-22T22:11:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-345589>

Hello everyone,

A local wellness center has a cryo chamber that has a freezer unit in a small room, the size of a closet, which drops the temperature to -50 F to -90 F. Their protocol is to enter with as few clothes on as possible (i.e., shoes and socks, gloves, hat, and underwear). I tried it for the first time today and stayed in for 3 and a half minutes. It's my understanding that most people stay in between 3 and 4 minutes per session. Has anyone tried one of these cryo chambers? It's different from the cold plunge or ice on the torso. I'm curious if anyone has more information on this approach to cryo therapy.

Thanks in advance,
Marty

### DLO — 2025-02-01T17:09:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346079>

It's a giant waste of time and money. Water is far more efficient at transferring heat.

### MJanowiecki1 — 2025-02-01T18:34:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346082>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=346079)
>
> It's a giant waste of time and money. Water is far more efficient at transferring heat.

Have you tried it, and at that temperature range? An external thermometer indicates roughly a 30 degree drop in the external body temperature after a session.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-02T01:22:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346099>

I haven't done cryotherapy however -

Water (Ice Bath): Water has a much higher thermal conductivity (~0.6 W/m·K) than air (~0.025 W/m·K). This means that heat is transferred out of the body 24 times faster in water than in air at the same temperature.

The illusion could be with cryo that the skin cools rapidly (due to evaporative cooling), core temperature does not drop significantly. Most of the effect is superficial and short-lived.

Ergo ice baths are far more effective at cooling the body due to superior conduction and convection properties of water. Cryotherapy may feel colder due to extreme air temperatures, but it does not cool the body as effectively as immersion in water.

### DLO — 2025-02-02T21:42:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346131>

Dont be a pussy and do it the way nature does it in the ocean even when snowing.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-03T00:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346135>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=346131)
>
> Dont be a pussy and do it the way nature does it in the ocean even when snowing.

For those with no ocean readily available . . . I’ve been in some VERY cold rivers. We have a large tub under a carport that once you break the ice (this time of year) does the trick. That said, I have spent some time in some large walk in freezers and I’m not sure why that would not have a similar CT effect. Also anyone that has walked on a cold, damp English afternoon knows what cold is.

### sheas — 2025-02-03T00:24:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346138>

I did cryo chamber a long time ago just to see how it was before I ever tried a cold plunge. Maybe it is a good gateway to the real thing. When I warmed up by the time I walked to my car I realized that it seemed like a waste of money and drove to the store and bought a chest freezer and made a DIY plunge. Once you go into cold water you will see the difference. No comparison.

### DLO — 2025-02-03T01:12:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346140>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=346135)
>
> For those with no ocean readily available . . . I’ve been in some VERY cold rivers. We have a large tub under a carport that once you break the ice (this time of year) does the trick. That said, I have spent some time in some large walk in freezers and I’m not sure why that would not have a similar CT effect. Also anyone that has walked on a cold, damp English afternoon knows what cold is.

To get your internals cooled to the same degree as water you'd have to spend hours in there, while your extremities cry for mommy.
Your internals get way colder and it takes you far longer to warm back up.

# Thread: Epilepsy, seizures, and grounding indoors
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pennypilot — 2025-01-23T01:06:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345599>

My daughter has juvenile myoclonic epilepsy. She has taken generic keppra for 12 years. We recently discovered grounding. But one post mentioned after an hour and a half on a grounding sheet, a girl had a seizure. My daughter lives with a lot of fear and anxiety. Does anyone have any information concerning a connection? We have been grounding outside, and she reports a calmness, but I'm worried about anything that plugs in. Any help or direction would be so appreciated!
Thank you

### Matthew Pettit — 2025-01-28T15:59:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345889>

We are using a ground sheet from intuition physician. They sell a shielded grounding chord called pure ground. We also use an actual ground rod. I don’t trust the wiring in our house. We run it out the bedroom window.

Our daughter has Benign Rolandic Epilepsy. What has helped us is time outside, grounding (feet on the ground), and candlelight at night.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-28T19:14:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345904>

There are many people in the EMF community, including R Blank from Shield Your Body SYB (Dr. Martin Blank’s son) that DO NOT recommend grounding inside with a ground pad and wire. Outside YES.

Most ground wires have a resistor for safety reasons that stops the negative ions from the ground anyway BUT if you are in a 50/60Hz AC field you are actually increasing your exposure to nnEMF as it follows the path of least resistance, through you to ground.

Turn off AC circuits at night and connect to Nature in Nature - ground outside.

### Sunny Day — 2025-01-29T03:10:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345938>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=345904)
>
> There are many people in the EMF community, including R Blank from Shield Your Body SYB (Dr. Martin Blank’s son) that DO NOT recommend grounding inside with a ground pad and wire. Outside YES.
>
> Most ground wires have a resistor for safety reasons that stops the negative ions from the ground anyway BUT if you are in a 50/60Hz AC field you are actually increasing your exposure to nnEMF as it follows the path of least resistance, through you to ground.
>
> Turn off AC circuits at night and connect to Nature in Nature - ground outside.

I've been sleeping on a grounding mat that's connected to a grounding rod in the dirt. My voltmeter shows a reduction in AC body voltage from .3VAC 60 Hz to .01VAC 0 Hz on the mat.

How would this increase my exposure to nnEMF when it's lowering my body voltage and the Hz drop to 0 when on the mat?

I do ground barefoot outdoors but I can't sleep outside and wish to drop the AC in my body while sleeping as I'm seriously ill. I live in an apartment and cannot turn off all the AC circuits at night, plus I have neighbors on the other side of the wall.

### Pratima — 2025-01-29T05:09:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345942>

> [pennypilot said:](/goto/post?id=345599)
>
> My daughter has juvenile myoclonic epilepsy. She has taken generic keppra for 12 years. We recently discovered grounding. But one post mentioned after an hour and a half on a grounding sheet, a girl had a seizure. My daughter lives with a lot of fear and anxiety. Does anyone have any information concerning a connection? We have been grounding outside, and she reports a calmness, but I'm worried about anything that plugs in. Any help or direction would be so appreciated!
> Thank you

I have myoclonic jerks still to this day and been doing light, water, magnetism where possible since 2017. Seizures were always controlled with various medications over the years, but I stopped it 4 years or so ago after hearing a podcast Dr Kruse with an epileptic (dennis porter) Its one of those things where everyone is different. I have not had a seizure since stopping meds, so I am very happy. This is just my experience, but I am not sure if it helps or not. All the best.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-29T10:55:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345953>

> [Sunny Day said:](/goto/post?id=345938)
>
> I've been sleeping on a grounding mat that's connected to a grounding rod in the dirt. My voltmeter shows a reduction in AC body voltage from .3VAC 60 Hz to .01VAC 0 Hz on the mat.
>
> How would this increase my exposure to nnEMF when it's lowering my body voltage and the Hz drop to 0 when on the mat?
>
> I do ground barefoot outdoors but I can't sleep outside and wish to drop the AC in my body while sleeping as I'm seriously ill. I live in an apartment and cannot turn off all the AC circuits at night, plus I have neighbors on the other side of the wall.

The grounding mat is not reducing your body voltage (only turning things off will do that) it is reducing the meter reading because you are double grounded. Try sleeping a few nights without being grounded and see how you feel. Ground outside morning and evening

### pennypilot — 2025-01-29T19:11:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345967>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=345942)

I have not had a seizure since stopping meds, so I am very happy. This is just my experience, but I am not sure if it helps or not. All the best.

How many jerks do you still experience? Are they daily?
Thank you!

### Pratima — 2025-01-29T20:30:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345970>

> [pennypilot said:](/goto/post?id=345967)

Sometimes none, and sometimes daily, and can be many in a hour, but I do not worry about it. I can still function fine, go to work etc. Aside from a some odd looks which I don't care about.....just roll with it. It is worse at work when I got into the office. I used to put this down to stress, but the office environment is packed with blue light and nnemfs.

### pennypilot — 2025-01-30T03:27:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345982>

Thank you so much for sharing your experience!

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-30T04:57:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345985>

If you want to be grounded at night . . . Sleep on the ground - outside.
Getting natural light requires being outside under the sun NOT sitting inside under artificial light.
Being grounded in Nature requires being OUTSIDE in Nature with your body in contact with the ground NOT a metallic pad attached to a wire connected to the ground. A mat connected to a wire is NOT the ground.
If metal bed frames and metal springs in mattresses are BAD how can a metallic pad connected to a metal wire be good? The wire acts as an antenna just like the springs in a mattress. Think about it.
Even if you do turn off the AC current, IF you do use a “grounding” pad attached to a wire connected to the house ground - a 2,000ft2 house has about 1/2 a mile of wire in it. That is a very large antenna . . . Not very natural. A 50ft (+/-) wire out the window is still an antenna and not natural.

### Pratima — 2025-01-30T07:25:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345987>

> [pennypilot said:](/goto/post?id=345982)

> Do you feel better off of meds?

Yes, I do...not sure if some of it is psychological.....The fear factor of having an event is very small now.

# Thread: Unable to turn off current to my bedroom because of GFI's setup

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jackfoster — 2025-01-23T05:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/post-345611>

It might be difficult to explain here and I have really looked into it but I am unable to turn off the current to my bedroom because of the way the GFIs are set up in the home I live in. My body voltage in bed is around .1 I think I have done as much as I can with moving the positioning of my bed unplugging and removing all electronics but is there anything else I could do to try to lower the voltage in my room even further without turning off the current?

### Lukassss — 2025-01-23T08:13:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/post-345615>

The cost version is:Grounded canopy.

Economy version:
If your bed is in the corner of the room, you can cover the walls and floor with aluminum foil and ground it.

### Mat.I — 2025-01-23T08:28:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/post-345616>

> [jackfoster said:](/goto/post?id=345611)
>
> It might be difficult to explain here and I have really looked into it but I am unable to turn off the current to my bedroom because of the way the GFIs are set up in the home I live in. My body voltage in bed is around .1 I think I have done as much as I can with moving the positioning of my bed unplugging and removing all electronics but is there anything else I could do to try to lower the voltage in my room even further without turning off the current?

Is your BV 100mV (0.1 V)? If so and you have measured it correctly I wouldn't be too concerned providing no contact currents are present or use of grounding mats inside. The SBM BB guidelines aim for <10mV for no concern however this is generally not realistic. The slight concern range is 10mV to 100mV, severe is 100mV to 1000mV, extreme is over 1000mV.

Given your earlier posts on the forum you may have to be a bit stricter and mitigate nnemf as much as possible..

### Mat.I — 2025-01-23T08:32:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/post-345617>

> Is your BV 100mV (0.1 V)? If so and you have measured it correctly I wouldn't be too concerned providing no contact currents are present or use of grounding mats inside. The SBM BB guidelines aim for <10mV for no concern however this is generally not realistic. The slight concern range is 10mV to 100mV, severe is 100mV to 1000mV, extreme is over 1000mV.

# Thread: Smart City
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-city.30168/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tera Pfarr — 2025-01-25T22:07:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-city.30168/post-345751>

Hi there,

I have just dove into Dr. Jack Kruse and am blown away. I am a local chiropractor in Racine, WI and have already dramatically changed by life/practice after hearing this information from Dr. Jack.

That being said…Racine, WI is where I live… They just announced that we may be the first “Smart City”. Microsoft data center is currently being built in our backyard. What are your thoughts on this progression with technology. I want to educate my community on the effects of all this non-native EMF and what the possibility of this development of our city may do to our health.

Honestly…it makes me want to move away…but I feel it is my duty to educate the community about the possibility of a Smart City effecting the future of their health.

Thoughts?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-25T22:46:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-city.30168/post-345753>

It is well documented the effect microwave ovens have on food, basically killing it and destroying all nutritional value.
Plants watered with microwaved water will die. Microwave ovens use energy at 2.4GHz.
Everything “smart” uses WiFi and Bluetooth which both utilize energy at 2.4Ghz.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-city.30168/post-345754>

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-28T18:59:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-city.30168/post-345903>

I get the same reading on my Safe and Sound Pro II RF meter 4 ft from a microwave oven (that is on) as I do 4ft from a WiFi router as I do 400ft from a small cell phone tower.

MichelleJorge - “So everyone is just giving up modern conveniences.???” . . . Since it is mainly these “modern conveniences” that are killing us . . . If their harmful affects cannot be “neutralized” - YES. A microwave over uses 2.4GHz and is DEATH at the push of a button. Your WiFi router and Bluetooth both use 2.4GHz . . .

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ionised-water.30171/post-345763>

I’m in Australia and I have heard Jack speak on a couple of podcasts about how terrible our water is down here due to the thinned ozone layer above us. He described it as having something to do with the ionisation of water in the atmosphere but the details are somewhat beyond me at this stage. I’ve heard about ionised water, the apparent purpose of which is to alkalise it. I know Jack is not sympathetic to claims of health benefits afforded by drinking alkaline water, but I wonder if it’s proponents are experiencing benefits due to the fact that it has been ionised. Am I correct in thinking that ionised H+ plays a big role in the cell? If so, could drinking ionised water be a solution to the poor quality of water that we have down here?

### Kristine 2025 — 2025-01-29T14:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristine2025-road-to-optimal-labs.30189/post-345963>

Looking at some of my labs, in bold is what I understand. I am retesting a bunch of labs (from reading epi-paleo RX) in March 2025, anything stand out I should research more? I have a new Endocrinologist and hoping a good one... will soon find out!

**2024 Labs for 56yo, female, on thyroid meds, post meno**
HSCRP .37 **Under .50 (need to get closer to .0001)**
IL-GF1 142 (score 0.5)
LDH 189
Haptoglobin 82
Cholesterol 235 **(not suppose to worry about?)**
Triglycerides 110 **(could be lower)**
HDL 60 **(on the higher end of green)**
LDL 156 **(should be closer to 200?)**
Total Ratio 3.92
Non HDL 175
A1C 5.8 **(this to me is too high)**
Glucose 113 **(was eating carbs when tested, will see if this went down)
Currently testing glucose at 60 - 90 most days**
TSH .757 **(under 1.5)**
T3 2022 2.6 **(should be closer to 3.5) Will ask for a reverse T3 lab**
T4 2022 0.9
Progesterone 2022 0.63 (**Now taking Rx)**
Estradiol 2022 under 12 pg **(Now taking Rx-read patch is not optimal)**
Potassium 4.6 **(not sure what this should be -- but was in green)**
C02 25 **(should be 25 or below?)**
Protein Total 7.1
Bun/Creatinine 9.90 **(under 10)**
FSH 2022 102.3
Vitamin D 2021 63 ng/ml **(need to get this above 70) Getting this retested 2025

I do take supplements - how many days do we stop supplementing before new lab draw?

Thank you!!**

### Kristine 2025 — 2025-01-31T23:12:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristine2025-road-to-optimal-labs.30189/post-346046>

This is interesting!!! This is what centralize medicine is saying for VIT D labs:

The following ranges may be used for the classification of 25OH Vitamin D status:
<10 ng/mL: severe deficiency
10 - 19 ng/mL: mild to moderate deficiency
20 - 50 ng/mL: optimum levels
51 - 80 ng/mL: increased risk of hypercalciuria
\>80 ng/mL: toxicity possible

### Vitamin D 25-Hydroxy​

[View trends](https://www.my.froedtert.com/Portal/app/test-results/past-result-details?ecomponentid=WP-24r0v8cn-2BZpinZqh62HBVlYQ-3D-3D-24aYncV1CklmFGXzx5Va1zunbaQRsiZOGiMIefl6twUYc-3D&eorderid=WP-24ZVfKfeEuyf9KSQNPYqCEDA-3D-3D-248ddVsRElmeEDvw2Z6QiYMgsSSf6hQ9LSntZq-2FlZkNj8-3D)
Normal value: 20 - 50 ng/mL ng/mL
Value
36.1

This is my husband's results today --- He is low... but optimal by these ranges!!! This is madness!

My test back in 2021 says normal range is 30 -100ng/mL --- How can this change so much?? Or am I missing something? I thought our optimal range should be 70 - 100???

Thanks!

### maria@modernhuman — 2025-06-28T20:16:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristine2025-road-to-optimal-labs.30189/post-355431>

Yes the standard "normal" ranges are not reflective of what is actually optimal for humans. Kristine your supplemented vitamin D levels are artificial and not reflective of your body's actual vitamin D synthesis. Getting off the supplements and into the sun, then testing your levels is the way to go. The artificial supplement will be out of your body in approximately 4 months once you stop taking it.

### Kristine 2025 — 2025-06-28T21:55:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristine2025-road-to-optimal-labs.30189/post-355440>

I have my labs updated in my journal: **Kristine2025 Road to Optimal** . Thank you! I wondered how long we need to stop supplementing.... the 43 latitude is finally warm to be in the sun and hope to see some improvement before winter comes! My Endocrinologist already sent me marching as I asked too many questions and was to focused on optimal. Thank you for responding to my post!

### maria@modernhuman — 2025-06-28T22:35:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kristine2025-road-to-optimal-labs.30189/post-355441>

You're welcome! You reminded me of one of my clients (clinical profile + appearance) so I felt a kindred spirit and responded. Yes endocrinologists are some of the most difficult to ask questions of, along with rheumatologists-- I had a client whose doctor literally walked out of the room when I had her ask whether the medications she'd been getting had caused the cushing syndrome she was obviously experiencing and what the doctor was going to do about it. Not acceptable.

I briefly glanced at your journal and it looks like you're in the thick of it! A friendly reminder I give myself and all humans interested: when you're pushing yourself in a stressful environment "when given no choice," you have to take very smart risks and make very calculated strategic decisions because if your body generates a stroke/ blood clot/ seizure/ etc during this time and you become disabled (assuming it's not a fatal stroke), it was all for nothing. Not worth it. Where are you located that no building biologist wants to come out? I'll keep you in mind in case myself/ my team is ever in the vicinity.

# Thread: SPERTI / UVA Questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-uva-questions.30190/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aaron K — 2025-01-30T21:06:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-uva-questions.30190/post-345998>

Warm greetings to you Jack, and to the rest of the Forum.

We're filled with gratitude to have come across your work.
It ties together many things I already knew (or intuited) and helps greatly deepen understanding.

We live in the north (Canada) and get little sun here in winter.
We know the sun is the best light, so we get outside a fair bit (including close to sunrise), but now that we understand light better, we see supplementation as important for us at this time of year.

We got a SPERTI lamp around one month ago and I have a few questions about them.
The supplier's recommendations are for increasing gradually up to 15 minutes every 3 days.
\-Is it advisable to use it more frequently?
\-Is the only concern of overexposure burning or are there other things to watch for

The supplier includes protective goggles to shield from UV. I understand that we want UV in our eyes, but am not sure whether the lamp is too intense to expose eyes to, or whether it would be healthy (or healthy to expose them for a little bit). Any thoughts on this would be welcome.

Aside from whether the SPERTI may be safe/beneficial for eyes, I wonder about any thoughts on additional UVA supplementation. I purchased some LED UVA strips (385-400nm) and put those on sometimes in the afternoon. Not sure if the wavelength might be a bit too high or whether it would be helpful. That light really makes white objects fluoresce.

Wondering what is the optimal wavelength for our UVA receptors? (is it 370? - i think i remember Jack saying that)... and also what sort of range of UV stimulates those receptors (eg. is it 360-380nm?).

I also have some black light UVA incandescent bulbs, but they did not seem to cause much fluorescence of white, so i've been going with the LEDS. I wondered if the same frequencies that fluoresce white also fluoresce our UVA receptors, but I'm not sure that is so. I also suspect the LEDs may have some flicker, so have not been using them a ton.

Thanks for any input.☀
Aaron

# Thread: Adult Acne
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adult-acne.30220/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MTA321 — 2025-02-06T16:11:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adult-acne.30220/post-346271>

I wanted to get get others input on trying to tackle adult acne. I am early 30's male and have struggled with mild acne most of my life (having a couple spots on my face at all times). It isn't a huge deal mostly because I am used to it but it is just one of these annoying things and it would be nice to finally have a clear face for once in my life. From starting to go down the rabbit hole my guess it likely involves a toxicity of blue light/nnEMF which causes a mismatch between vitamin A/D.

That being said I live at the 37th latitude and have started to implement many of the protocols the past couple of months (morning sun, block blue after sunset, CT, reducing nnEMF environment) to change my current environment but may have to wait until I can get better UVB exposure in the coming months to address the vitamin D mismatch. I've been using a reptile UV bulb in concert with red/IR light but I don't think the UVB is strong enough to make much of a difference. I have been eating mostly carnivore the past couple of years but have shifted the ratio the past few months and began eating 50% seafood mostly sardines, oysters, etc.

One thing I have noticed is specific foods such as dairy causes inflammatory cystic acne. I rarely eat dairy anymore but would fixing my light environment "cure" this type of acne or is it just a reaction to the protein within the dairy and will always result in inflammatory acne?

Thanks in advance.

### Saichi — 2025-02-06T18:01:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adult-acne.30220/post-346272>

Raw grass-fed dairy doesn't cause acne.

### MTA321 — 2025-02-06T18:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adult-acne.30220/post-346277>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=346272)
>
> Raw grass-fed dairy doesn't cause acne.

Unfortunately this isn't the case for me. I've tried raw grass-fed dairy from both cow and sheep and still get the same results.

>
> I can't recall but isn't there something about being in front of the engines on an Airplane is a better EMF situation? Or sitting directly over the wing?

Pick your seat wisely...
“While where you sit on an airplane won’t make a huge difference in terms of your overall EMF exposure, different places of the plane could have small differences in EMF levels. If you are using an electronic device, sitting by the window will help the EMF radiation leak out so you won’t be as susceptible to the Faraday Cage Effect. However, windows are also the entry point for cosmic radiation, so sitting in an aisle seat is better if you are sticking to a tech-less flight. Sitting at the back of the plane puts you farthest away from the cockpit, which is full of electronic instruments. Also, try to find out beforehand where the jet engines are located (either on the wings or tail) and select a seat away from those.”

[https://defendershield.com/does-fly...UGHFhVBiD6b8LMwu6b2IUWg2rLZV3LwolHrxmCCnBr3fl](https://defendershield.com/does-flying-airplane-increase-emf-radiation-exposure?srsltid=AfmBOoqaRGgUGHFhVBiD6b8LMwu6b2IUWg2rLZV3LwolHrxmCCnBr3fl)

Personally... I always try to pick a seat that doesn’t have a person sitting behind me. I don’t need tv devices or someone’s iphone streaming right where my head/brain is. Red eyes are great because lights are off and most people just sleep and don’t use devices.

I’ve measured the ”Faraday” fabric at airports with a Safe and Sound Pro2 (so up to 8GHz) and it blocked a significant amount of the RF there 100x reduction. What frequencies are you calling “mmWave” and are they at airports and/or on planes?

### Mat.I — 2025-02-11T06:39:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-seat-location.30224/post-346592>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=346430)
>
> I’ve measured the ”Faraday” fabric at airports with a Safe and Sound Pro2 (so up to 8GHz) and it blocked a significant amount of the RF there 100x reduction. What frequencies are you calling “mmWave” and are they at airports and/or on planes?

I have looked into the air port/craft tech previously.. The majority of airports do in fact use mmWaves. These generally sit ~ 30GHz to 100GHz however, can start as low as ~ 24GHz for 5G based tech.. Standard S & S will not measure these you need the Blue one (mmWave pro). Not sure if you have used this, however subject to the PD measured you may have to use a signal attenuator (20dB)..

In short, they use mmWave tech for the 5G networks and deploy this via small cells & base stations over the radio access network.. It is used within the body scanners and other systems throughout the airport..

On the actual aircraft (some aircraft) - radar, weather, terrain, collision avoidance & sat comm systems use mm Waves. Some aircraft will have highspeed internet that uses 5G based Sat comms which is mm Waves.. Otherwise comms will be in the Ku or ka bands. You could search what airline/craft have this tech currently or measure whilst on the flight with the correct meter.

Re: RF blocking clothing/fabric (in theory) - may not be very efficient at blocking mm Waves (subject to how it designed and layered) however, if effective it could reflect it to other unprotected areas on your body.. Me personally, I would not waste my time with this fabric as if it is only working in an isolated area, you are absorbing, reflecting and concentrating in others..

### Saichi — 2025-02-14T15:28:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-seat-location.30224/post-346711>

Metal does not reflect mmWaves it conducts it! I've had the blue meter since it was released and it's the airport terminals as I've posted before that have can have high to extreme levels of it. The most pernicious part is that most of the transmitters are completely hidden using the horn antenna you'll discover the transmitters are hidden behind airport infrastructure.

>
> Metal does not reflect mmWaves it conducts it! I've had the blue meter since it was released and it's the airport terminals as I've posted before that have can have high to extreme levels of it. The most pernicious part is that most of the transmitters are completely hidden using the horn antenna you'll discover the transmitters are hidden behind airport infrastructure.

I thought ultra thin metal films <1um penertration would occur with minimal reduction.

Thin foils used in most sheilding applications say 1 to 5um you would get significant attenuation.

Thicker metals say >5um in theory should completely reflect.

I agree that the sheilding fabric would be a great conductor especially when gold, silver or copper is used, however in the mm range the fabric due to thickness and design will allow penetration especially when in contact with skin. This is what would be referred to as re-radiation effect, capacitive/inductive/resonate coupling effects.. I know of some manufacturers trying to minimise this with hybrid designs however, never looked into any of the clothing/fabric to that detail.
