### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-08T23:10:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-blue-light-from-a-screen-only-harm-you-if-your-close-to-it-if-your-watching-podcasts-on-your-computer-screen-but-far-is-there-harm-effect.30242/post-346439>

You should do some research on nnEMF. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/101](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/101)
There are electric fields (ie from 50/60Hz circuits in the walls, floor, ceiling and any power cord plugged in and most appliances /devices powered in this way) These fields travel 6 - 8ft in all directions.
Magnetic fields (from power lines, electric motors, fans etc) these travel a few feet.
Radio Frequency (radio, TV, cell towers, radar, WiFi, Bluetooth etc) Some of these can travel many miles.
Specifically Blue light - can you see it? What % of the light you are getting is blue?
You can ALL these types of EMF’s from a computer.

### Jackson K — 2025-02-08T17:59:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-any-website-or-app-we-can-type-our-zip-code-in-to-check-emf-radiation-and-grounding-quality.30243/post-346424>

### [AntennaSearch - Search for Cell Towers & Antennas](https://www.antennasearch.com/)

Search for the locations of cell towers and antennas to determine cell reception.

 www.antennasearch.com

This website will show what towers or antennas are in a 3 mile radius around your address. This will atleast give you a general idea of what EMF is surrounding you.

Proximity is everything. You get more RF exposure from the cell phone in your hand than the cell tower half a mile away. The single largest source of nnEMF most people are exposed to on a daily basis is inside their home and specifically from the wires in their walls.

Metal attracts electric field.
In an airport or airplane the advantages of blocking RF \*may\* out weigh the disadvantages of the electric field.
At home or the office probably not. Turn it off, hard wire and/or use shielding.

# Thread: Can we look directly at the Sun for long periods is it safe with our eyes open looking at the sun for 2 hours at least per day is that safe?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-look-directly-at-the-sun-for-long-periods-is-it-safe-with-our-eyes-open-looking-at-the-sun-for-2-hours-at-least-per-day-is-that-safe.30257/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thomasf75 — 2025-02-10T00:11:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-look-directly-at-the-sun-for-long-periods-is-it-safe-with-our-eyes-open-looking-at-the-sun-for-2-hours-at-least-per-day-is-that-safe.30257/post-346507>

We know about the Sun but can we look directly at it when it's very shiny bright even if it hurts should we try to hold or keep our eyes open and looking it for optimal healing and benefit and can we do this for hours a day? Is it safe.

### AlbertD — 2025-02-11T00:30:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-look-directly-at-the-sun-for-long-periods-is-it-safe-with-our-eyes-open-looking-at-the-sun-for-2-hours-at-least-per-day-is-that-safe.30257/post-346573>

Face the sun. Don't look AT IT (even with eyes closed). Don't go blind bud!

### Thomasf75 — 2025-02-11T00:54:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-look-directly-at-the-sun-for-long-periods-is-it-safe-with-our-eyes-open-looking-at-the-sun-for-2-hours-at-least-per-day-is-that-safe.30257/post-346579>

Are you sure about this one I heard a lot of people say they look directly at it or Sun gaze. I can't find anywhere with Jack says to look directly at it or not and he doesn't really seem to respond to messages so I'm dumb struck

### AlbertD — 2025-02-11T01:29:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-look-directly-at-the-sun-for-long-periods-is-it-safe-with-our-eyes-open-looking-at-the-sun-for-2-hours-at-least-per-day-is-that-safe.30257/post-346583>

> [Thomasf75 said:](/goto/post?id=346579)
>
> Are you sure about this one I heard a lot of people say they look directly at it or Sun gaze. I can't find anywhere with Jack says to look directly at it or not and he doesn't really seem to respond to messages so I'm dumb struck

I remember Jack mentioning it somewhere in a podcast. I don't remember. I think the myopia blog on Jack's website might help.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-11T01:58:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-look-directly-at-the-sun-for-long-periods-is-it-safe-with-our-eyes-open-looking-at-the-sun-for-2-hours-at-least-per-day-is-that-safe.30257/post-346584>

Ok to do some sun gazing at sunrise and sunset (for me personally).. Do what feels good and do not force it (should be no discomfort). There was an ophthalmologist (Dr Bates) that created a method called the "Bates" method that outlined recommendations on improving eyesight naturally which also included open & closed eye sungazing practice.. Again do what feels good for you and definitely do not do (open eye sungazing) in the middle of the day (you will damage your eyes)..

# Thread: Too hot to sleep at night! Need some insights and ideas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KinaBibiDiop — 2025-02-10T08:48:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/post-346525>

This question is mainly for those of you living in the tropics or where it’s hot at night for 6 months of the year:

**How do you make sure your room is cool enough to sleep at night while you limit exposure to nnEMF (fans and air-conditioning units) and while making sure your room is pitch black?**

It is very hot at night where I live especially because I use thick blackout curtains and have to close the door and all windows to block out all artificial lights from the streets.

I don’t use any fans or AC because of the nnEMF which makes it very hard to sleep at night because of the lack of ventilation.

I’ll be grateful for some insight and ideas.

### Kirsten F — 2025-02-10T17:45:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/post-346539>

> [KinaBibiDiop said:](/goto/post?id=346525)
>
> This question is mainly for those of you living in the tropics or where it’s hot at night for 6 months of the year:
>
> **How do you make sure your room is cool enough to sleep at night while you limit exposure to nnEMF (fans and air-conditioning units) and while making sure your room is pitch black?**
>
> It is very hot at night where I live especially because I use thick blackout curtains and have to close the door and all windows to block out all artificial lights from the streets.
>
> I don’t use any fans or AC because of the nnEMF which makes it very hard to sleep at night because of the lack of ventilation.
>
> I’ll be grateful for some insight and ideas.

Hello, I do sleep with airconditioning on, and have measured the electric fields generated by the unit, and they are quite low. So I think measuring is important.

### KinaBibiDiop — 2025-02-11T15:52:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/post-346604>

Thanks for your insight!

### KinaBibiDiop — 2025-02-11T15:54:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/post-346605>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=346527)
>
> Linen. 100%.

Great idea! I’ll try that. Thanks

### 5G Canary — 2025-02-11T16:18:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/post-346607>

Ice packs work great (neck, back and/or chest) and eventually your body should acclimate to the environment.

ok. Thanks for this info. What exactly is an automotive fan? And is it sold in a specialized shop?

### 8Phoenix — 2025-02-13T15:04:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/post-346675>

>
> This question is mainly for those of you living in the tropics or where it’s hot at night for 6 months of the year:
>
> **How do you make sure your room is cool enough to sleep at night while you limit exposure to nnEMF (fans and air-conditioning units) and while making sure your room is pitch black?**
>
> It is very hot at night where I live especially because I use thick blackout curtains and have to close the door and all windows to block out all artificial lights from the streets.
>
> I don’t use any fans or AC because of the nnEMF which makes it very hard to sleep at night because of the lack of ventilation.
>

find a way to allow the hot air to passive leave your room naturally , in other word add some sort of chimney

### coho3131 — 2025-02-13T16:45:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/post-346677>

> [KinaBibiDiop said:](/goto/post?id=346659)
>
> Interesting. Do you have a specific recommendation ? I never heard of fans operating on dc

Search "rechargeable battery dc drum fan".
Keep the fan far enough from your body when using or charging.
Do not choose one with a remote control or a display panel, only choose push button models.
Put black electrical tape on any lights.
Unplug the charger cord when you use it.
Choose a model with a long battery life to make it through your whole sleep time.

### KinaBibiDiop — 2025-02-15T13:39:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-hot-to-sleep-at-night-need-some-insights-and-ideas.30261/post-346778>

As a hack try Glycine 3grm before bed it helps you lower your core body temperature,fans are not going to make much difference if you cannot lower your core body temperature...**WARM** shower does the same to a degree it sounds counter-intuitive but it brings heat from your core to the surface to evaporate reducing core temperature.

This is what I do when its hot or I just put the Air Con on ,I am not fussed with nnEMF as I have good redox and my priority is to induce sleep and I sleep extremely well. Sometimes we fuss over things that we really shouldnt.

### rtd — 2025-02-10T17:49:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genetic-testing-all-newborns.30262/post-346541>

Big nope

### [GeneDx CEO wants genetic testing for every newborn](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/genedx-ceo-wants-genetic-testing-150015672.html)

GeneDx has a big market in mind for its genetic testing: every newborn baby. GeneDx specializes in whole genome sequencing. More common multi-gene panels, in contrast, may look at a couple hundred genes to identify a specific issue like cancer risk.

# Thread: Paper emits blue light due to florescent additives.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-emits-blue-light-due-to-florescent-additives.30268/unread>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jordan — 2025-02-11T01:12:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-emits-blue-light-due-to-florescent-additives.30268/post-346581>

Hello all! I have posted here in the past about my visual issues. Well I discovered something that would be interesting to you guys here. So for myself paper alone bugs my eyes when light is shining directly on it. Obviously it makes the white really glow but I looked into that more and it seems paper today all use **optical brightening agents** / **florescent whitening agents** which gives paper that white white appearance. I did some reading and it seems they react to light in the 300ish nm range and remit in the 400-460nm range. Now I dont know if the range is larger, those numbers just are what I read. Just seems very odd how we have blue lit screens and also paper that emits light back that isnt good at all. I see they sell paper and notebooks without those additives.
Curious on your guys thoughts about this.

### Jordan — 2025-02-11T01:17:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-emits-blue-light-due-to-florescent-additives.30268/post-346582>

Upon more reading I came across this link

### [Harvard Study Shows E Ink’s ePaper Is Up to Three Times Healthier for Your Eyes Than LCD Screens](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20230313005152/en/Harvard-Study-Shows-E-Ink%E2%80%99s-ePaper-Is-Up-to-Three-Times-Healthier-for-Your-Eyes-Than-LCD-Screens)

E Ink (8069.TWO), the originator, pioneer, and global commercial leader in ePaper technology, announced today the results of a peer-reviewed study about the ...

www.businesswire.com

Looks like they mention how eink is better than print paper and mentions those additives being an issue emitting blue light. I am currently typing this on a eink display but I think I am going to order some notebooks without those additives.

### Thomasf75 — 2025-02-11T02:36:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-emits-blue-light-due-to-florescent-additives.30268/post-346585>

I'm not sure but this is getting pretty depressing we can't look at screens and now if it's paper what are we supposed to look at I mean we have to be able to read this sounds all too depressing

### Jordan — 2025-02-11T02:42:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-emits-blue-light-due-to-florescent-additives.30268/post-346587>

> [Thomasf75 said:](/goto/post?id=346585)
>
> I'm not sure but this is getting pretty depressing we can't look at screens and now if it's paper what are we supposed to look at I mean we have to be able to read this sounds all too depressing

I mean there's several healthier options for screens now. They do sell paper /notebooks that don't contain these additives. I may try them to see if it's easier to read on since bright white paper bugs my eyes.

### Jacks — 2025-02-11T06:16:29+0200

### Jordan — 2025-02-11T17:03:39+0200

I have a black and white eink monitor. Its the Dasung 13.3" HD portable monitor. I have tried color but I feel like its not quite there yet. Ill probably try color again once theres a higher PPI color eink display.

### Henry Tokar — 2026-04-06T21:03:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-emits-blue-light-due-to-florescent-additives.30268/post-370370>

Do you use it?

### Jordan — 2026-04-28T20:26:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-emits-blue-light-due-to-florescent-additives.30268/post-372352>

> [Henry Tokar said:](/goto/post?id=370370)
>
> Do you use it?

Sorry, just saw this! I currently use a Boox Mira 13.3" E Ink monitor. I also own two Boox Mira Pro 25.3" E Ink monitors. I find the 13.3" to be less stimulating and less fatiguing for me, given my sensitive visual system.
I did buy a Boox Mira Pro Color E Ink monitor recently to try, and it’s definitely not as sharp as a non color E Ink screen, just like the past color eink devices ive had. It’s very dark, so it’s harder to light up if you’re not in a well‑lit room. I ended up selling it. The only color E Ink I keep around is my Lenovo ThinkBook Plus Gen 4 laptop, which has both an OLED and an E Ink screen—but of course I only use the E Ink side strictly.

# Thread: Putting drops in the eye allow for only very small % of absorbtion. Anyone know how to make the eye absorb much more?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/putting-drops-in-the-eye-allow-for-only-very-small-of-absorbtion-anyone-know-how-to-make-the-eye-absorb-much-more.30317/>

**Posts:** 3

### Thomasf75 — 2025-02-18T02:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/putting-drops-in-the-eye-allow-for-only-very-small-of-absorbtion-anyone-know-how-to-make-the-eye-absorb-much-more.30317/post-346923>

When you put drops in the eye only a very small percentage is absorbed inside it is stated in many articles that the eyes tear film wipes away most of any liquid placed in the eye. does anyone know how or any methods to allow the eye to absorb much more of the drops when placed in the eye? For example I think if you lie down after putting the drops it should absorb more and hold your eyes open do not blink maybe?
But looking for something stronger to accomplish this. can't find anything in any research to assist.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-18T02:41:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/putting-drops-in-the-eye-allow-for-only-very-small-of-absorbtion-anyone-know-how-to-make-the-eye-absorb-much-more.30317/post-346927>

# Thread: Non-Flicker Commercial lighting
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tyler Hanson — 2025-02-19T00:02:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-346968>

Hello,

I need to choose a light, to light an indoor environment for manufacturing.

I want to have a light with the least negative effects for the 30-40 employees working under these, so they need to be non-flicker, but also of an optical spectrum and intensity in which people can actually clearly see what they're working on. If I choose something too low K, or too dim, I'm going to get major pushback.

I've been digging around, but I figured I would ask to see if anyone has found an appropriate light already for such a situation?

Thank you!

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-19T00:22:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-346969>

How about full spectrum grow or aquarium lights? Some have NIR and UV built in. You could supliment with some NIR 850nm “security“ lights.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-347073>

Great question - I actually need a similar solution for my office based in the UK as the current lighting is dreadful LED! Although we have the secondary issue of everyone using computers all day

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-21T21:35:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-347081>

> [Paul-Harrison said:](/goto/post?id=347073)
>
> Great question - I actually need a similar solution for my office based in the UK as the current lighting is dreadful LED! Although we have the secondary issue of everyone using computers all day

NIR 850nm security lights can be placed around the office. 850nm light cannot be seen by the eye so are not distracting but will help to “reduce” the blue light affect from computers and the LED’s. Not perfect but a step in the right direction.

### Paul-Harrison — 2025-02-21T23:07:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-347088>

That sounds like a great idea. Have you seen any data indicating to what extent the 850nm light could reduce the blue light affect of the computers?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-22T03:13:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-347094>

> [Paul-Harrison said:](/goto/post?id=347088)
>
> That sounds like a great idea. Have you seen any data indicating to what extent the 850nm light could reduce the blue light affect of the computers?

No data except . . . 42% of sun light is IR. You will have little to no IR in the office. If you add 50% IR “illumination” you will much closer to outside light frequency balance. OR . . . You could get everyone to wear blue blocker glasses.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-22T05:13:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-347096>

> [Paul-Harrison said:](/goto/post?id=347088)
>
> That sounds like a great idea. Have you seen any data indicating to what extent the 850nm light could reduce the blue light affect of the computers?

Plenty of data if you know how 850nm light interacts with us.. Doesn't technically reduce the effect it is more of a counterbalance which addresses the damage that the high energy blue light causes in isolation... Blue light could be the fire and IR could be the water (if that makes sense). Think fire suppression vs control of the fire by limiting the fire growth curve (heat release rate) and spread beyond area of origin..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-22T05:31:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-347097>

> Thanks for the fast reply. Is there a difference between the 120V and 240V for health?

120V (USA) is 60Hz, 240V (UK) is 50Hz and these will affect differently. But you have what you have where you live, so . . .

### Wouterbekkers — 2025-02-27T20:23:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-lights-or-incandescent.30328/post-347520>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=347518)

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-27T20:55:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-lights-or-incandescent.30328/post-347524>

> [Wouterbekkers said:](/goto/post?id=347516)
>
> Is this accurate? [View attachment 30070](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/30070/)

Yes - but this is just the visible light And different manufacturers bulbs will vary.
Daylight also has 42% IR and 5% (+/-) UV
Incandescent/halogen have IR (heat) but no UV
Normal LED lights and florescent have little to NO IR or UV
Sunlight does not flicker, all (most) artificial lights do to a certain extent (some more than others).

# Thread: A quote from The Crossing by Cormac McCarthy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jackfoster — 2025-02-21T02:10:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347055>

"he said the wicked know that if the ill they do be of sufficient horror men will not speak against it. That men have just enough stomach for small evils and only these will they oppose. He said that true evil has power to sober the small doer against his own deeds and in the contemplation of that evil he may even find the path of righteousness which has been foreign to his feet and may have no power but to go upon it. Even this man may be appalled at what is revealed to him and seek some order to stand against it."

### jackfoster — 2025-02-21T02:14:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347056>

When I read this it made me immediately think of how people refuse to do something as simple as use their eyes to see the trails in the sky, while others are able to use their eyes to see and once they have seen it they cannot unsee it.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-21T03:24:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347059>

I 100% believe in chemtrails but not every line across the sky is a chemtrail. Most of the photos I see are contrails. People who are not used to looking up do not have a frame of reference to understand what they see, so tend to believe the person who told them to look up, as to what they are seeing.

I think this principle is universal which is why people fall for cults, believe the “news” and take untested, experimental jabs.

### Jacks — 2025-02-21T04:08:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347062>

The condensation-trails have to condense on something - such as a soot particle. From what I've learnt from Jim Lee, plane farts (as he calls plane jet exhaust) contain plenty of soot particles (which contain many of the elements, such as Aluminum, that people are finding in rainwater) on which the water vapor can condense to form contrails. The saturation of air with water vapor has to be above a certain level for condensation to occur and to form the man-made cirrus clouds. I see tons of these where I live, though we've had a lot less in the last year. Cloud seeding is a legit thing - using silver iodide and other things and is done to increase rainfall and snowpack. There are other geoengineering projects ongoing here and abroad.

### jackfoster — 2025-02-21T04:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347063>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=347059)
>
> I 100% believe in chemtrails but not every line across the sky is a chemtrail. Most of the photos I see are contrails. People who are not used to looking up do not have a frame of reference to understand what they see, so tend to believe the person who told them to look up, as to what they are seeing.
>
> I think this principle is universal which is why people fall for cults, believe the “news” and take untested, experimental jabs.

The difference between contrails and chemtrails is obvious, no? Chemtrails linger and spread while contrails do not. I think this quote applies to many things but I feel like chemtrails are a simple test to see where a person is at in their awareness. I can't imagine someone who won't recognize what is happening in the sky being able to recognize harmful effects of nnEMF or something else that isn't clear to see.

# Thread: How far should the horizon be for optimal sun rays?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-far-should-the-horizon-be-for-optimal-sun-rays.30348/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Base_Strength — 2025-02-25T19:07:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-far-should-the-horizon-be-for-optimal-sun-rays.30348/post-347281>

I live at the bottom of a hill near Austin, TX. The latitude is about 30.46 with the east behind the hill.

The sun rose at 7:00AM today, but my house doesn’t see any light until about an hour later, around 8:00 AM, when it breaks the top of the hill. That hilltop is only about 500 meters from me. Meanwhile, my sunset view stretches about 15 miles to the horizon, which feels more natural.

Does Dr. Kruse ever mention a minimum horizon distance for catching the full benefits of the morning sunrise?

I usually drive 10 minutes down the hill to a park that gets light earlier—around 7:24 AM, still 40 minutes before my house—but even there, the sunrise point over the hill is about 3 miles away at most.

I want to confirm it’s still safe for my eyes to look at the sun briefly when it crests a closer horizon, and that those rays are still optimal for signaling my SCN and reaching my skin to prime my mitochondria.

Thanks for any advice!

### 8Phoenix — 2025-02-28T05:37:30+0200

# Thread: A question about the sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-question-about-the-sun.30379/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### S Hudson — 2025-02-28T18:09:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-question-about-the-sun.30379/post-347587>

Presently building up my sun callus, but I got to thinking due to all the propaganda that has been pumped into my brain over the years. With the likes of solar flares and CME's, do we ever need to worry about the sun kicking out too much energy? Take this morning, for example (UK and still winter). The 10AM sun was giving a UV grade reading of 6, which is an anomaly to what is normal, and yet the early afternoon was 2 with the same clear skies.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-28T20:05:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-question-about-the-sun.30379/post-347598>

Odds of getting too much sun in the UK, any time of year, (even if it exploded  ) is remote.

I'm out everyday and all day. Even UV 12 /13 or more.. No burning and full sun and feeling more and more charged with the need of minimal food. No negatives from this over the last few years of religious sun exposure with no exceptions. The days are structured around the sun..

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-laptop-from-sun.30388/post-347668>

With summer approaching here in Pennsylvania, I’ve been looking into solutions for using a laptop outdoors without it overheating. I saw some ideas like using reflective foil-lined EPS board to limit heat absorption and improve insulation, but I haven’t seen much feedback on how well it actually works in practice. Has anyone here tried something similar?

I also found this portable laptop tent shade on Amazon but don't know how long it would last or how effective it would be for the heat. [Amazon Laptop Tent Sun Shade](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B01DRD060A)

Any help or suggestions would be appreciated.

# Thread: Ammonia in heavy exertion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ammonia-in-heavy-exertion.30389/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Vanilla — 2025-03-02T01:23:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ammonia-in-heavy-exertion.30389/post-347671>

Under normal conditions my blood ammonia tests at the bottom of the scale.
However, under heavy exertion (martial arts training), I begin to sweat heavy ammonia.
I've tried to support the urea cycle with citrulline, ornithine, and arginine. I have also tried sodium benzoate.
None of this seems to make a difference.
My lactateyruvate ratio is high. My alanine is also low, indicating that I'm not using the ALT pathway to make aKG.
Instead, I'm going through GDH deamination and creating NH4+.
Thoughts on where to go from here?

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-03-02T03:14:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ammonia-in-heavy-exertion.30389/post-347682>

Check your environment both at home and where you train is what I would suspect. The environment you're in is probably suspect and now that I've seen where Fort Collins is I have the same suggestion that Uncle Jack will tell you

Get the hell out of Colorado especially the Denver Boulder area . It's not good for anyone

Move out like your life depends on it. I doubt Jack will disagree on this one.

You're in urban nnEmf soup. Worse than most places

And no you can't mitigate it to the extent where it will be good lest you get trapped in the Faraday cage of your house. You need to be outside

# Thread: Cape Verde in December.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cape-verde-in-december.30393/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Garry Kirk — 2025-03-02T15:16:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cape-verde-in-december.30393/post-347711>

I just wanted to share my experience of a three week trip to Cape Verde in Nov/Dec 2024. I live at the 55th Latitude in Scotland and unfortunately was not granted the 6 month career break over our winter that I wanted this year. What I can do though, is get away every 7-8 weeks, which is what I have been doing the last 4 years now since my bad experience with moxifloxacin. This year I decided to head further south than the canaries and for 3 weeks instead of 2. I just wanted to share the pro's and con's for anyone else who is unfortunate enough to also live in the UK.

I flew from Glasgow to the island of 'Sal' which is one of the north east islands in Cape Verde at the 16th Latitude. I think flights from England normally fly to Boa Vista which is slightly south of Sal, slightly smaller and even more 'chill' apparently. I stayed in an Airbnb in Santa Maria on the south coast of the Island for three weeks and these are my experiences...

The weather was stunning. It was 29 degrees celsius most days and for the first 2 weeks not much really in the way of wind, clouds or rain. Sunrise was approx 6:45am when I arrived and slipped about 30 seconds per day if memory serves me correctly. Sunset was approximately 6pm. I saw 21 sunrises and 21 sunsets. I spent 10 hours a day grounded and in the sun. I even had a pair of full leather flip flops made for heading out there. For the first 4 days I jumped out of the mid day sun, into the shade and read a book and just reduced my time untill after about 5 days, I was able to spend the full day throughout the solar noon in the sun too. UVI hit I think a max of 7 when I was there and was typically 6 or 7 at solar noon. You are normally about UVI 4 in the canaries at that time of the year. I've attached a photo of the tan below!

It took me about 4 days to start to feel better from all the nnEMF, electric and magnetic fields that are screwing me up here in Scotland and into the second week I was feeling a lot better. 10 hours grounded and 10 hours in the sun every day will do that to you. I took both my gauss meter and my safe and sound to test and as far as electric fields and magnetic fields go, in the Airbnb, there were no issues at all which I was surprised about. I don't know whether that can be said in general for other apartments but the apartment I was staying in wasn't anything spectacular so who knows.

In terms of RF, this is where it gets a little trickier... there are cell towers all over the place! Most are 3g, but there are LTE as well and tbh they are an eye sore and are polluting a large part of the beaches there as well. This should be noted. Honestly, it was pretty unreal. I had already checked with opencellID and knew there were towers around and picked a spot away from them, but even so, it’s a shite state of affairs. I have attached a map below of the best beach spots in terms of RF should you decide to go Santa Maria in Sal. Interestingly this is where I would recommend staying as well. If you want a hotel I would recommend RUI on the most SW part of the island and if you want an air bnb then East where I was is probably best. Avoid Santa Maria town for your sanity. It is very loud till very late and littered with cell towers. If I was going back I would look to stay at Murdeira which is an extremely quiet spot, further north and a short, inexpensive bus ride north of Santa Maria. There isn’t much there in the way of shops or anything but it gets you away from the busier Santa Maria at night so you can lights out and wind down.

The beach at the RIU hotel on the SW part of the island is stunning and you can walk from there up the west coast to Ponta Preta beach which is a WC surf destination apparently and where I watched pro surfers at sunset one night. Unforgettable and stunning. The tour of the island is really worth doing as you can do it in a day and see how people are actually living there. This is a third world country and there is extreme poverty and a couple of shanty towns in Espargos which is where most people from the island live. Life for the people that live there is a hussle! And you can feel that when you are there. In terms of safety it is safe, if you are white, you are just a minority, but walking around is safe. There is one road up and down the island and I was walking back to town one night after sunset and a friendly random just stopped and offered me a lift. The ‘no stress’ everyone is friendly is partly true, but you need to be aware of the hussle. Some people are genuinely really nice and others are simply wanting to fleece you and appear at first to be really nice, but when you reject their sales pitch change and are no longer interested in being nice.. My advice is avoid the top section of the Main Street there (R.1 De Junho) be firm in rejecting advances from people wanting to show you their shop and you will be fine. Stick to the back streets.

Food. The food is not great IMO, but food is subjective. Don’t do what I done and travel so light that you only took a couple of bags on the plane thinking you would save money, because food is quite expensive to buy in the supermarkets and my advice is take your meat, veg, coffee etc in the hold luggage and you’ve cracked it, because the apartments are quite inexpensive. If you are doing the hotel then I think some hotels get food shipped from the canaries so will likely be better, but don’t expect to be dining like you’re in the Med! This made three weeks quite challenging as I do like my food. The thing is you will learn the shops to go to for different things and I actually hit up a rapport with one of the Chinese shops who reduced the price of my water everytime I went in there I think. The wee woman in there loved me for whatever reason, which made it nice to go into and less of the hustle. Nothing is labelled, ask the price first and don’t pay it if it’s obscene, just go somewhere else.

The third week seen some higher winds and more clouds and so I did see that is was possible to have a change in the weather, but I was speaking to a volunteer who worked at the turtle conservation centre and he had been there 5 weeks prior to me and told me the weather had been pretty much consistently that 29 degrees for most of that time. The water is lovely and the scuba diving was good too. There was a gym on the beach so trained barefoot the whole time and literally added a rep a week onto the pistol squats. I suffered no doms, just an altogether better way to train. It’s like black and white, train indoors in a blue lit hell hole, train on a beach in your bare feet next to the sea… universes apart! In fact it made me see the difference in living a completely indoor existence at the 55th latitude and by that I mean everyone, I mean the weather drives everyone to that lifestyle to a place where no-one is ever inside, they are always outside. The real issue these people have is they are on their phones all day, just like everywhere else and are polluting their environment with the cell towers which is sad. The kids are smiling though, running around all day and night outside, down the beach after school, I loved that!

What do I think? If you know where to go, can avoid the cell towers and can accept you will not be fine dining anywhere for a couple of weeks you can definitely get better sun in Cape Verde than the canaries. If you plan and are prepared to take your own food and slum it a bit as well you can do more weeks there with a bit of careful planning as apartments can be found cheaper than the canaries as well. I also think there is potential with some of the other islands as well. There is actually an island there with an active volcano on it, FOGO, one of the southern islands, which apparently is amazing and there are a couple of other more ‘tropical’ islands there as well. I would like to have jumped around a few of the islands, but you’re doing it by boat and apparently that is a very laid back affair as well, as in, the boat is meant to sail at 11am, but sails at 2pm and then that knock on effect means you get there when you get there, making planning a little more difficult but yeah, I think I would look at flying from England next time and maybe having a look round Boa Vista.

Oh, the salt is good there too and I brought good quality salt back from Salinas de pedra de lume, a low elevation salt crater (where you can float around in a salt lake) for €2 a packet!

G.

Oh, If you check out Santa Maria Pier at sunset, the fisherman throw their scraps into the sea and you will get BIG mantas swimming around the pier eating it all up. Apparently you get turtles there too some months. That was pretty cool and I forgot to mention power cuts! I think there were two power cuts during my time there, one of which the whole island was out. It really didn't bother me travelling solo, but if that kind of thing could put you off, it's worth bearing in mind. There was also a period overnight with no water, so no shower that night. Some apartments don't have hot water, mine didn't, but again, that didn't bother me. You can get an apartment with hot water so when looking you need to look for apartments that have hot water and most don't have wifi either, so check that if that's your thing. You can pick up a local SIM in town for €1 and for €10 get a sim with a load of data on it.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Adamo — 2025-03-02T16:35:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cape-verde-in-december.30393/post-347715>

I was thinking about Cape Verde myself, a month or so ago.
Sucks about towers everywhere, but looks like a beautiful experience, brother!

As attractive as islands are, the food prices (aside from local fruits and seafood) are always much more expensive.
Thank you for sharing.

### dantothep — 2026-01-30T13:32:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cape-verde-in-december.30393/post-366111>

Thanks for taking the time to do this. I'm off there myself next week!

# Thread: Custom UV-B Lighting for Vitamin D
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/custom-uv-b-lighting-for-vitamin-d.30404/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### wellnesswarrior — 2025-03-03T19:34:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/custom-uv-b-lighting-for-vitamin-d.30404/post-347812>

I'm pretty new to this forum. I was hoping to get some input on safety and exposure time on UV-B lighting. I built a custom setup of 6 UV-B bulbs in my infrared sauna that has a bulb in each corner surrounding my torso and 2 more in front at my shin level. Each bulb is 25 watts and are specifically UV-B only, peak wavelength 306nm. When I was originally testing a single bulb on my bench, I had removed my shirt and stood in front of it for around 8 minutes with my eyes closed the entire time. The burn I received was pretty bad, but of course didn't show up for around 4 hours after. This was a couple years ago. Since then, I've used it in the sauna with all 6 going, but for only about 60-80 seconds then turning it off.

I'm very apprehensive about using it without knowing what the exposure window is in the first place. I reasoning also is I live up north in the middle of the country and and its not easy to get outside in the sun when its below zero here. I've attached a couple photos showing the setup and the wavelength graph.

Anyone have any advise on exposure time and frequency? Total wattage for all 6 bulbs is 150 watts.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-03-04T01:43:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/custom-uv-b-lighting-for-vitamin-d.30404/post-347836>

30 seconds for narrow band Uvb

And eye protection goggles

When Doctor Kessler did my psoriasis outbreak I think it started with a 20 second exposure and then raised to 30 seconds in the hospital with me wearing a thong

The psoriasis took four treatments to clear up

I don't know if it's still in Rotherham general hospital, the poor dermatology department went to hell after he retired in 2012...

Set up looks great! I have an EMR-TEK panel with UVA & UVB along with the IR etc built in... It's called the Krypton. That works well for me for when the sun isn't out or if I have to work early etc.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-03-04T03:40:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/custom-uv-b-lighting-for-vitamin-d.30404/post-347844>

Natural sun light is 42% +/- IR And 5% +/- UV . . . Of this 95% is UVA and 5% is UVB
So 8-1 IR to UV ratio and 160-1 ratio IR to UVB
IF you want to copy Nature that is . . .

### Anthony Smith — 2025-03-05T01:46:05+0200

> [NickD said:](/goto/post?id=347854)
>
> I don’t think you can get close enough to nature, but a little is better than none. That’s how I look at it anyways

The main point I was making is the balance/ratio NIR/UVA/UVB

### Anthony Smith — 2025-03-10T20:40:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/30-grounding-fundamentals.30454/post-348313>

Grounding made simple . . . Natural - GOOD, artificial - BAD

Grounding makes you part of a circuit
Outside - Natures DC circuit flowing through your DC circuits, with you in direct contact with the ground - GOOD
Inside - Your homes AC field flowing through your DC circuits, with you connected to a wire (antenna) - BAD

Natural light = outside, in direct sun light (away from nnEMF) - TINA (There Is No Alternative)
Natural grounding = outside, in direct contact with the ground (away from nnEMF) - TINA

# Thread: UV from the ground question

**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeanny — 2025-03-12T19:26:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-from-the-ground-question.30466/post-348466>

Do the benefits of sunlight bounce back off the earth so that a person could modestly (in a skirt) stand over the lawn and have sunlight benefits to the area "where the sun don't shine?" I'm trying to treat a squamous cell in situ. Thank you.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2025-03-12T19:50:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-from-the-ground-question.30466/post-348473>

Potentially. My understanding is that green plants reflect IR very well. They also do not actively use UV and so reflect that back as well.

Wouldn't be nearly as effective as direct rays though

### Rusty — 2025-03-13T07:54:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-from-the-ground-question.30466/post-348505>

Gold is a great IR mirror.

Silver/Aluminium is a great UV mirror.

Aluminium reflects with over 90% efficiency from 250 to 450 nm

**Fresh Snow & Ice** reflects **up to 80-90%** of UV. This is why snow blindness is a thing.

You know what to do.

### Jeanny — 2025-03-14T01:14:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-from-the-ground-question.30466/post-348560>

Thanks so much guys for all the great info and ideas! Yes, I know now what to do.

# Thread: Sunrise isn’t novel

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jessnet3 — 2025-03-15T14:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrise-isn%E2%80%99t-novel.30485/post-348644>

I chose this book as part of early American history for my daughter who is in fifth grade. Because we’ve been greeting the sunrise for just a year, she was under the impression that the concept is novel. She approached me in surprise yesterday after she read that Native Americans had this ritual as part of their every day, too. It offered an opportunity to discuss modern humanity’s disconnect to the earth and how our lack of reverence for Mother Nature has abundant consequences for human health.

What are your children learning in school? Are they teaching them to be more connected to nature or are they the wall that keeps them from it?

# Thread: 23andme bankruptcy data delete
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-bankruptcy-data-delete.30541/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2025-03-24T15:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-bankruptcy-data-delete.30541/post-349241>

**23&ME users should delete their data as soon as possible, California’s Attorney General [has warned](https://nypost.com/2025/03/23/us-news/calif-ag-issues-stark-warning-to-15-million-23andme-users/), as the beleaguered genetic-testing company faces bankruptcy and sale.**

“Given 23andMe’s reported financial distress, I remind Californians to consider invoking their rights and directing 23andMe to delete their data and destroy any samples of genetic material held by the company,” AG Rob Bonta [said in a statement on Friday](https://oag.ca.gov/news/press-releases/attorney-general-bonta-urgently-issues-consumer-alert-23andme-customers).

Under California’s Genetic Information Privacy Act of 2022, companies must obtain explicit consent for the collection, use and disclosure of any genetic data. The law also guarantees consumers the right to access or delete their data at their choosing.

Of particular concern is the fate of the company’s massive bank of customer data, which could end up being used for purposes never envisaged by the company or by users themselves. The company has data on at least 15 million individuals, including a wide variety of information on personal health and ancestry.

Last year, there [was widespread concern](https://www.reuters.com/fact-check/article-us-genetics-company-selling-all-dna-assets-china-is-satire-2024-05-17/) over a satirical article that suggested the company was selling its genetic data to a Chinese company, in a $10 billion deal.

As recently as 2020, 23&Me was valued at $6 billion, but a botched expansion into research and pharmaceutical partnerships, and an embarrassing data breach, caused the company’s stock to tank. Its share price has fallen from $320 a share to less than $2.

The company has now filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and its CEO, Anne Wojcicki, has resigned.

23andMe [**said**](https://investors.23andme.com/news-releases/news-release-details/23andme-initiates-voluntary-chapter-11-process-maximize) in a press release it plans to continue operating throughout the sale process and that there “are no changes to the way the company stores, manages, or protects customer data.”

### Marshall_Sponder — 2025-03-25T02:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-bankruptcy-data-delete.30541/post-349272>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=349241)
>
> **23&ME users should delete their data as soon as possible, California’s Attorney General [has warned](https://nypost.com/2025/03/23/us-news/calif-ag-issues-stark-warning-to-15-million-23andme-users/), as the beleaguered genetic-testing company faces bankruptcy and sale.**
>
> “Given 23andMe’s reported financial distress, I remind Californians to consider invoking their rights and directing 23andMe to delete their data and destroy any samples of genetic material held by the company,” AG Rob Bonta [said in a statement on Friday](https://oag.ca.gov/news/press-releases/attorney-general-bonta-urgently-issues-consumer-alert-23andme-customers).
>
> Under California’s Genetic Information Privacy Act of 2022, companies must obtain explicit consent for the collection, use and disclosure of any genetic data. The law also guarantees consumers the right to access or delete their data at their choosing.
>
> Of particular concern is the fate of the company’s massive bank of customer data, which could end up being used for purposes never envisaged by the company or by users themselves. The company has data on at least 15 million individuals, including a wide variety of information on personal health and ancestry.
>
> Last year, there [was widespread concern](https://www.reuters.com/fact-check/article-us-genetics-company-selling-all-dna-assets-china-is-satire-2024-05-17/) over a satirical article that suggested the company was selling its genetic data to a Chinese company, in a $10 billion deal.
>
> As recently as 2020, 23&Me was valued at $6 billion, but a botched expansion into research and pharmaceutical partnerships, and an embarrassing data breach, caused the company’s stock to tank. Its share price has fallen from $320 a share to less than $2.
>
> The company has now filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and its CEO, Anne Wojcicki, has resigned.
>
> 23andMe [**said**](https://investors.23andme.com/news-releases/news-release-details/23andme-initiates-voluntary-chapter-11-process-maximize) in a press release it plans to continue operating throughout the sale process and that there “are no changes to the way the company stores, manages, or protects customer data.”

Yes, I saw the news and requested my data be deleted within minutes of hearing about the bankruptcy!!~

**Subforum:** The Cave

### Woodstock — 2025-03-28T22:41:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/costa-del-sol.30577/post-349523>

I just saw a short clip on Facebook of Jack showing Costa Del Sol in the background. Are there any mitos there? I haven’t checked out this part of the coast yet because it seemed pretty far away from other mitos etc. is it low emf? I plan to spend time in Atami as I will be there through the 11th of this month. Then coming back to ES form May-July. Any insight on Costa del Sol or other low emf areas would be welcomed. Even if no other mitos there, I don’t mind having a short stretch checking out other cool areas. Thanks!!!

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-03-30T06:37:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/costa-del-sol.30577/post-349637>

Theres a couple of mitos there. I stayed at Costa Del Sol last year in March Its an hour and half drive to Atami from there. It has transmitters at regular intervals but the beach is lovely. No rocks and the sunsets are delightful. I found it rather too hot in March last year. Even this year Ive found March on the lower elevations too hot. Even at Coatepeque. Thats why I headed for the west of the country where its higher elevation And ended up at 1477 meters above sea level. I came home at 6pm today and the temp was 21 and mild. Still get the tropical sun. If you PM me I'll tell you where that lovely garden and four pools are.

# Thread: Istanbul
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/istanbul.30578/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Allred — 2025-03-28T23:36:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/istanbul.30578/post-349528>

My experience - if you book a roundtrip on Turkish Air the price was cheaper if you book a stopover in Istanbul. If you book business class their lounge is nice. Take advantage of a refreshing shower, but get on the list as soon as you arrive. My advice is book the stopover on the way back to the US.
Old Town is a 45-60 minute drive from the airport, IST. I hate getting screwed by airport taxis so we went into town with the locals. They have coaches leaving IST continuously just outside baggage claim. Each coach has a couple of drop off points all over town. Find the coach to Old Town or cab it.
We stayed at the Radisson Blue in Old Town, across the street from the water, a good location. You can also book a boat cruise from the dock across the street from the hotel.
Turkish baths - if you are like me and prefer not to get bathed by some Turkish dude there is 1 place, close to Old Town where it is staffed with ladies from Indonesia, we also got a massage afterwards. If anyone is interested, I”m sure I can fine out the name of the place.
The food is good, our favorite restaurant was close to the hotel. But it was own by Arabs = no wine. Again, with a little effort I can probably find the name of the place.

Istanbul is a great place to visit!

# Thread: European moving to El Salvador - process?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pakul — 2025-04-05T11:11:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350133>

Hi,

I plan on moving to El Salvador for at least the end of the year and was wondering if anyone from Europe here has recently gone through the process.

Does anyone know of any tax optimization offices that could help with the visa stuff, like finding a "job" or something?

I know you can theoretically stay visa-free for up to 90 days, then leave for a few days and come back for another 90. I'm wondering how that actually works. Could I have trouble entering the country after, say, the third time if I've only been abroad for a few days each time? Has anyone got experience with that approach?

If you've got any other suggestions, please let me know.

My main reason is to become a tax resident in El Salvador. Plus, I want to learn and master Spanish, enjoy the weather, and read books. I'm also open to any kind of opportunities, etc.

Thanks!: )

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-05T11:36:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350135>

I went away for three weeks and the visa got renewed, I don't think they mind. ( Thing is you have to go somewhere else other than Nicaragua, Honduras and Guatemala because they're in a visa agreement. )

### pakul — 2025-04-10T17:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350405>

Thank you, [@Dr Rohen Kapur](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26681/), for your help: )

I have a couple more questions if I can: )

Is it necessary to have a return ticket or so? Do they check that?
Are they asking on the border about some financial proof to cover the cost of traveling, etc?

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-10T20:22:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350413>

Yes they ask how long your stay is, and they ask where you're staying so it's useful to have somewhere booked with an address . Either Airbnb or actual address etc this time I said 179 days. They were fine with it once they process that that was the limit to 180 days visa

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-10T20:23:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350414>

# Thread: Isn't 5G better than 4G for bodily autonomy?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/isnt-5g-better-than-4g-for-bodily-autonomy.30658/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-04-09T03:25:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/isnt-5g-better-than-4g-for-bodily-autonomy.30658/post-350346>

I hear people worrying about 5G cell towers. But, isn't it actually better than 4G if you believe in bodily autonomy?

I have read that 5G uses beam-forming technology, so that if you're using a cell phone with 5G connection, then you (and people within X meters of you) are going to be radiated very highly, and other people will barely get any RF. On the other hand, 4G will radiate more equally in all directions.

Is this true? Or are other people getting non-trivial RF even when there isn't a beam towards you? Curious if baseline RF exposure from being near a 5G tower is lower than active use of 4G near a 4G tower.

Bodily autonomy:
\- Those that don't want to be radiated will just disable 5G on their phones and avoid standing within x meters of others using 5G.
\- Those who want 5G can use it.

This lets everybody choose their level of radiation. Win win. Bodily autonomy.

**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center

### caroline — 2025-04-21T22:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-k-from-june-17th-2014.30732/post-351060>

an oldie but goodie.......

-   [Jun 17, 2014](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-use-almond-flour-the-paleo-darling-be-careful.11091/post-131164)

-   [Add bookmark](https://forum.jackkruse.com/posts/131164/bookmark)
-   [#7](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-use-almond-flour-the-paleo-darling-be-careful.11091/post-131164)

Nature is always fundamental in health but no one wants to understand it is at is smallest levels to see why we are so off track. Paleo suffers from this as does allopathic medicine. This is why I tell people to still read my blog even if the do not understand quantum physics. What I write is important for all living things. The goal of the blog has always been to convince you not to turn away your attention because you do not understand the material. It is to draw your consciousness in to peer into how nature really works and light your curiosity on fire. As for Paleo, they have way too much wrong to get a pass from me. A doctor has a duty to point out where their message is dead wrong. Food is a very small part of the health equation. Paleo advocates paleofying food. I don’t "paleofy" any foods or Rx. This practice is one of the main reasons I want to be an outsider in this community. Sometimes you have to walk away from what you want to find what you deserve. When you fuel your life's journey on the opinions of others, you end up on a dark curvy road holding a gas can......... Understanding QED is why they continue to miss the big points for health reversals. Paleo is step in the correct direction from a SAD but there is so much poor info on basic physiologic functioning of circadian biology and food. Moreover, Their group and its leaders break all the circadian rules and thinks technology is a non issue and its all about grass fed meat and cross fit. Well it's all about electrons and paying attention to light cycles and food and exercise come after those. That is why I have a problem with them and I wont stop pointing it out until they get the message.

“It's a fact—_everyone_ is ignorant in some way or another because of their dopamine level.
Ignorance is our deepest secret. And it is one of the scariest things out there, because those of us who are most ignorant are also the ones who often don't know it or don't want to admit it. We don

# Thread: I got cocky with the no sunglasses and embarrassed myself
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### billytheadult — 2025-04-23T15:44:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351145>

Just wanted to admit this and learn from it.
I didn't wear sunglasses on a boat day in Sanford, FL (29º) while Mito-preaching about light environment etc and well... I burned the shit out of my face and upper torso (and am peeling next day). While a couple friends are intrigued, it was embarrassing and I definitely got laughed at. It was just too much direct exposure and I did't get in the shade soon enough. And I feel like my credibility publicly took a hit.

I guess my solar callus has a long way to go.

### digital — 2025-04-23T16:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351147>

> [billytheadult said:](/goto/post?id=351145)
>
> Just wanted to admit this and learn from it.
> I didn't wear sunglasses on a boat day in Sanford, FL (29º) while Mito-preaching about light environment etc and well... I burned the shit out of my face and upper torso (and am peeling next day). While a couple friends are intrigued, it was embarrassing and I definitely got laughed at. It was just too much direct exposure and I did't get in the shade soon enough. And I feel like my credibility publicly took a hit.
>
> I guess my solar callus has a long way to go.

From someone who was raised and grew up in NYC his entire life, made the sudden change to move down to Florida 8 years ago, we burnt for like 6-8 months before the skin was properly adjusted. Nowadays, we can be in the dead of the equator all day without a burn. Keep it up: tan daily, for as long as you can.
This profile pic serves as a reminder of my pale NYC self.

### Saichi — 2025-04-23T18:16:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351152>

First light AM IR sun helps with sun UV exposure later in the day.
It can still take a few days to build up solar calluses depending on your haplotype.

### Brent Patrick — 2025-04-24T01:12:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351176>

And I feel like my credibility publicly took a hit.
>
> I guess my solar callus has a long way to go.

It doesn't matter what others think,whats important is you keep progressing forward.

As a hack increase lycopene consumption via food and refer to Jacks Solar Callus blog it will take several weeks if not months to build a solid base so you can then develop a Tan, which depends on your Fitzpatrick skin color to start off with.

[https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36606553/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36606553/)

### [Infrared and Skin: Friend or Foe - PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4745411/#:~:text=The%20process%20of%20exposing%20the,the%20spectrum%20\(figure%205\).)

In the last decade, it has been proposed that the sun's IR-A wavelengths might be deleterious to human skin and that sunscreens, in addition to their desired effect to protect against UV-B and UV-A, should also protect against IR-A (and perhaps even ...

 pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

**"The process of exposing the skin to such radiation before its exposure to deleterious UVR wavelengths, closely emulates processes found in nature. This is understandable from an evolutionary standpoint since exposure to these early morning red and IR-A wavelengths in sunlight may ready the skin for the coming mid-day deleterious UVR.

Several _in vitro_ studies have shown that preconditioning fibroblasts with NIR could protect against upcoming UVB damage via p53 cell signaling-induced anti-apoptotic effects \[[45](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4745411/#R45), [46](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4745411/#R46)\]. In the first _in vivo_ study, prior to exposure to UVB, test subjects were pre-treated with 660 nm light as compared to controls who were not \[[30](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4745411/#R30)\]. The progression in the development of erythema following UVB exposure was used as a measure of the deleterious reaction to UVB. The results of the study showed a reduction in the UVB-induced erythema reaction in a significant number of the pre-treated subjects. Also a SPF-15-like sun protection factor effect and a reduction in post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation were observed. Subsequent experiments (unpublished data) using multiple wavelengths showed superior reduction in erythema in favor of the NIR end of the spectrum"**

### ​

### Brent Patrick — 2025-04-25T09:09:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351239>

> [billytheadult said:](/goto/post?id=351220)
>
> I did get AM IR sun in Florida the 3 prior days I was there. And I thought the non-sunglasses was at least partly responsible for my not burning/peeling during my 8 days in El Salvador

And for more context a post from Jack.........

**"People forget when sunlight hits the retina/skin 40-60% of our circulation increases in these capillary beds. They react the same way to sunlight. Their reaction to bright chronic blue light devoid of purple UV or red IR light is a different story.

What does full spectrum sunlight destroy naturally if your skin and eye are in the game of nature?

UVA and B light naturally lowers adrenalin (the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system) while its photons re-zip collagen that cortisol release causes at 4 AM that wakes us up by allowing water flows to occur between glial cells and neurons via the aquaporin 4 gates in the brain. AM sunrise light has no UVA or UVB present initially. It has blue, green, and red present. It has more blue than any other part of the spectrum and this is stimulatory to waking us up. Naturally, UVA light shows up later in the AM, depending upon your location in latitude, and this frequency of UVA light acts to begin to re-zip the collagen in our skin and eyes. That initial blue light stimulus from the sun’s rise is used to unwind our collagen to increase water flows to stretch the interspaces in neurons to wake our body and mind up at dawn from sleep. The aquaporin 4 gates are what is destroyed in Multiple sclerosis, which controls the flow of DDW from astrocytes to neurons, which is another photoelectric autoimmune condition. (Vitamin C is in that water transaction too)

When we are missing UV and/or IR light for any reason, these photoelectric and photochemical are not made. When full spectrum sunlight is absent in someone who is chronically stressed for any reason, sleep cannot be induced because the ocular melatonin cycle requires that these two frequencies of light be present to stimulate the regeneration processes in the eye during the daytime. If you think about your childhood, when you spent the day at the park of the beach, you might remember how easy it was to fall asleep after good solar exposure. If you had a lot AM light exposure you built your solar callus and this would mitigate a sunburn. If you did not you got burned early and had to take refuge, but either way, your sleep improved. The reason is simple, sunlight induces sleep because this light creates melatonin because the regeneration pathways that use melanopsin need daylight to regenerate.

When you did fall asleep, the redness of your skin did not come from the thermal burn, but it was from the increased blood flow due to the release of nitric oxide that acted to bring the arterioles of the dermis layers to the surface. This is a photochemical change induced by sunlight to allow the skin to absorb the UVA and UVB light at the surface. UVA and UVB light does not penetrate deep.

To absorb the UV light we need the circulatory system to come from the dermis and engorged the arterioles with RBC’s. This assumes there is no PEROXIREDOXIN damage in RBC's. (see my circadian RBC redox thread in the Redox forum)

The RBC’s are filled with hemoglobin and porphyrins that absorb both UV and IR frequencies. The sunburn is really an absorption of too much thermal IR energy. Deep sunburns can result from several factors: excessive sun, or thin skin, thick skin, or a poor adaptation to seasonal light due to chronic use of UV blocking makeup, clothing, or sunblock use in strong light cycles.

In fact, falling asleep in the strong daytime sun, is a key clinical sign to the astute clinician your normal daily light environment really is. UV and IR sunlight normally lowers endocrine steroids naturally(CT 7 blog) and lowers the nucleic acids in the surface keratinocytes without any liberation of heat on the skin’s surface = AUGER effect This last photochemical change shows you how UV and IR sun working together helps dissipate surface photonic power by exposing more nucleic acids to the incident rays of the sun."JK**

UV and IR sunlight normally lowers endocrine steroids naturally(CT 7 blog) and lowers the nucleic acids in the surface keratinocytes without any liberation of heat on the skin’s surface = AUGER effect This last photochemical change shows you how UV and IR sun working together helps dissipate surface photonic power by exposing more nucleic acids to the incident rays of the sun."JK**

Would this explain why before CPAP I fell asleep on Waikiki beach face down and burnt my back so badly that my psoriasis disappeared and I peeled the skin off a week later to smooth unblemished skin with no more psoriasis ( but I had the sleep apnea of course ) when I woke up. No one was near me. I must have been snoring very loudly . It was 1994. Pre internet .

# Thread: Dry Skin, Mosquitos, and timing of breakfast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin-mosquitos-and-timing-of-breakfast.30748/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### michellejorge — 2025-04-24T16:16:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin-mosquitos-and-timing-of-breakfast.30748/post-351195>

Hi all, Due to the sun exposure my skin is dry and flaky. I understand we're not supposed to apply anything to our skin that would interfere with sun access. What about at night? Can we apply something, and if so, what?

Being outside, I'm getting attacked by mosquitoes. Any natural advice?

I'm thinking I have heard contradicting advice on when to eat breakfast. Before or after sunrise?

Thank you.

### digitald3 — 2025-04-24T16:30:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin-mosquitos-and-timing-of-breakfast.30748/post-351196>

> [michellejorge said:](/goto/post?id=351195)
>
> Hi all, Due to the sun exposure my skin is dry and flaky. I understand we're not supposed to apply anything to our skin that would interfere with sun access. What about at night? Can we apply something, and if so, what?
>
> Being outside, I'm getting attacked by mosquitoes. Any natural advice?
>
> I'm thinking I have heard contradicting advice on when to eat breakfast. Before or after sunrise?
>
> Thank you.

Hello. Typical advice around here is a loofa for dry skin.

Mosquitos are attracted to UV light. This is why Amazon sells uv light bug zappers. Your body is releasing uv light instead of using it for energy. This is a redox problem. So until you can fix that you will have a mosquito problem. So until then use cintronella candles or get some uv bug zappers for the yard.

And Dr Jacks leptin RX says to eat breakfast 30 min after sunrise so that you have eaten before uva rise.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-27T01:28:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin-mosquitos-and-timing-of-breakfast.30748/post-351354>

You can always apply coconut oil at night and wash it off before sunrise

# Thread: Subject: Trading Fluorescents for Volcanoes — Help Me Crowdsource a Mitohacker Landing Pad in El Salvador
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subject-trading-fluorescents-for-volcanoes-%E2%80%94-help-me-crowdsource-a-mitohacker-landing-pad-in-el-salvador.30769/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-04-26T23:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subject-trading-fluorescents-for-volcanoes-%E2%80%94-help-me-crowdsource-a-mitohacker-landing-pad-in-el-salvador.30769/post-351341>

**Subject: Trading Fluorescents for Volcanoes — Help Me Crowdsource a Mitohacker Landing Pad in El Salvador**

Hey tribe,

I’m neck-deep in blue light and construction bids, trying to map out my escape route to a nature-first lifestyle — and I’m turning to you all to help me zoom out and see El Salvador from 30,000 feet.

### The dream:​

-   **Timeline**: Fall 2025 move — ideally November through April at first.
-   **Budget**: <$250K to buy land or a modest home. Would build if the terrain and vibe are right.
-   **Work**: I’m a **master carpenter turned remote commercial construction estimator**. I’ve put down the tools (for now), but I still live in CAD files, cost breakdowns, and Zoom fatigue.
-   This **job and screen time are frying my brain**, but I need to keep it going until I figure out the next move. I’ve probably got another 8–10 years before I can officially pull the plug… unless I figure out how to live on sardines and sats.

### My situation:​

-   Never left the country.
-   Don’t speak Spanish.
-   Still need to get a passport. (Yeah, yeah, I know.)
-   But I’m ready to **do hard things** if it means trading fluorescent lights and HVAC for sunlight, seafood, and grounding.

### What I’m looking for:​

-   **Cooler, elevated climates** — Comasagua or “Little Switzerland” area has me intrigued. I cannot stand humidity (I’m not a frog).
-   A **gated community if necessary**, but ideally something secure and local, not a Miami expat bubble.
-   I’d love access to a **pool, river, or lake** — I’m CT-friendly and looking to stay that way.
-   **Remote work needs:** I know this is a big one… what’s the _real_ internet situation? Anyone rocking Starlink? How painful is it to stay connected from up in the hills?

### What I need help with:​

1.  **Trusted realtors, lawyers, or fixers** you’ve worked with?
2.  Is Comasagua overhyped or a hidden gem?
3.  **Internet realities** — Starlink, fiber, LTE, carrier pigeons?
4.  What’s the starter kit for an American building or buying in ES who has never owned land abroad?
5.  Would love tips on land buying, utility hookups, dealing with local contractors (especially from a builder’s perspective).

### Listings I’m eyeing:​

I’ve found a few from:

-   [RE/MAX El Salvador](https://www.remax-centralamerica.com/el-salvador/comasagua/land-in-co-002282623003)
-   [Bienes Raíces en El Salvador](https://www.bienesraicesenelsalvador.com/property/terrenos-con-vista-5000vr2-calle-a-comasagua-para-vivir-o-negocio-jda-cea-c?agent=bienes-raices-en-el-salvador)
-   [Elsalvadorsurfrealestate.com](https://elsalvadorsurfrealestate.com/)
-   [Oceansideelsalvador.com](https://oceansideelsalvador.com/)

Any leads, locals, or mitohackers already living this life — I’d love to connect.

I’m not looking for a Whole Foods and good WiFi. I’m looking for **a place to rebuild mitochondrial health**, eat like a fisherman, and start prepping for phase two of life before I get too crusty.

Appreciate any guidance from those ahead of me on the trail.
Let’s quantum leap this thing.

# Thread: What do people think of the Telepathy Tapes?

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chlo_e — 2025-04-30T19:05:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-351570>

Curious to hear what people on the forum think of the Telepathy Tapes docuseries?
I haven't seen much chat on here about it, thought there would be a lot more discussion.

On a related note, this interview is also fascinating, I've screenshotted what one of the Autistic kids has said below at minute 46.
[https://youtu.be/lwx2aGQB10k?si=OhMXpSfDaRF2Gngs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lwx2aGQB10k)
