Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### brianalfredeneuman — 2025-05-06T16:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352035>

I have certainly experienced telepathy. I am surprized it's even a question sometimes. I like to think that love is telepathic, which explains why things happen when you think of a friend or whatever and they call you out of the blue.

Maybe Uncle Jack would say it's quantum entanglement, which I never really understood the full meaning but seems totally accurate from what little I understand of his meaning.

I strongly believe some people are born with very different talents and purposes. Autistic children sometime have obvious 'specialties'. Perhaps they are excellent at Math or some other accepted skill but it makes sense that they might also have other unknow human abilities and have a focused version of that as well. I'm not autistic for the record.

Animals have senses before Earthquakes and other events that threaten their time. We probably have the same thing.

I don't believe much in the type of 'psychics' that people go to for advice but I do believe we all have some puzzle pieces that cross our path and with the correct pattern recognition, we can put together for our selves to benefit our life.

One time for example, I was once kept up all night by a type of anxiety. I kept going through my life and problems in my mind, but kept coming back to this repedative thought, "those are small problems."

That night instead of sleeping, I went to the beach stayed up all night doing some Thai Chi/Chi gong. The only thing that bothered me during my experience were the planes taking off from the airport that I could see taking off over the harbor, it interupted me and the flight path was right over my head. I had a weird symbol I noticed in the clouds going over the sliver of the moon (crecent moon). The symbol made a sort of dragon shape from a book I remembered from my childhood. At that moment I thought about a moon on the top of the minarets (Islam). At the same time I thought out of the blue about why we "America" will bomb the Middle East. Osama bin Laden popped into my head. I knew who he was as I followed world politics closely. Anyway I saw the sun rise and then went back to a Marina and saw this old man watching a little TV in his security booth with some guy staring in. It was the morning of 9/11 and the planes I had watched were two of the planes that left Boston that morning and collided with the World Trade Center. It took a long time to deal with that event for me, for so many reasons it's hard to explain. I had massive quantum entanglement you'd say, that kept on for years. Too many things to deny.

### eve001 — 2025-05-06T18:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352038>

I think it's really fascinating and I wonder how it 'works' and how it links to nnEMF poisoning and to embodiment.
Last week I came across some testimonies of people about severe autistic kids having trouble speaking.
Then they reduced EMFs and one young autistic kid made huge improvements with speaking.

In the series they mention experiments, showing that also when shielded from electromegnetic waves in a bunker still telepathy was possible...
Suggesting it must go through some other medium than EMFs. Scalar waves - of which I know even less?

I also recall them speaking of people receiving radio signals (songs and speech) with their teeth...
It makes me wonder how many poor people ended up in psychiatry for hearing voices, while simply having a dental issue and literally hearing radio voices.

Also, when reduction of nnEMFs improves autism - would that reduce their telepathic skills?
It reminds me of the Rick Rubin podcast where they speak about his surgery and methylene blue.

As I understand the idea: people get poisoned by EMFs and chemicals to the point of kids not embodying well anymore. The poor embodiment however seems to open them up to a supernatural realm. As if bc of incomplete embodiment some gate were kept open. Divinity leaks into our world and spreads and cancels the evil plans of the greedy and power-hungry...

I once knew a person who after a concussion said she got some psychic abilities, talking with the dead. Another man I knew said he received biographical personal info on total strangers he crossed in the city. He would then start talking to these stranger. I heard more anecdotes like this. The theosophical society in the 19th centruy was into clairvoyance and into science. Some thesosophical scientist looked into the structure of atoms with some sort of clairvoyance.

So I do not dismiss the possibility of things discussed on these telepathy tapes, it's very fascinating, but I cannot assess its authenticity.

### Chlo_e — 2025-05-13T20:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352444>

Fascinating to read your comments above.

I did pay to access their website videos (and to support the making of their documentary) and whilst I agree with Dr Powell that their footage wouldn't satisfy heavily sceptical scientists (especially where the parents are more involved) some of the footage is extremely convincing. I do not think facilitated communication nor the ideomotor effect can explain all of what is being shown and described. Even the choice of words from the Autists is completely unique, not to mention all of the circumferential discussions that take place in the talk tracks. I also read several books written by individuals featured in the series and it certainly helps feed in to a larger picture of what's unfolding.

There are now so many accounts (NDEs, OBEs, intuitive experiences/dreams, savant examples, genuine whistle blowers from military / intelligence / governmental organisations, SRA survivor accounts, interviews with staff at the department of perceptual studies (DOPS), and also other clues scattered throughout nature, archaeology. ancient teachings, literature and the arts) that it's hard to deny we are experiencing only a small proportion of reality, and perhaps of our true potential.

In circles where the focus is on the metaphysical/spiritual, it is said that we are supposed to have all of these abilities without loss of motor function or neurological injury. That we have been dumbed down and programmed to believe otherwise, and to actively suppress these abilities from developing through a variety of ways. I also have had experiences that science cannot explain, so I am inclined to take this seriously ..

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-14T02:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352454>

I grew up in a family where these things were considered normal. In a Christian church setting - ”words of knowledge”, in the Spiritualist setting clairvoyance and/or clairaudience. I know many people who experience telepathy - mind to mind communication - to a greater or lesser extent.

An old family friend could read the cards I was looking at. He was a consultant to Interpol in Europe to find people. He could tell them ahead of time if they were still alive or had died. Another friend would “visit“ us and would flash the lights to let us know he was there. The next day he could tell us what we were doing or watching.

This is not woo but science. I believe the biology behind these abilities involves the pineal gland which when “open” acts at a “crystal radio”. nnEMF and fluoride are known to calcify the pineal glad so it stops functioning in this way.

### Chlo_e — 2025-05-14T20:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352492>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352454)

>

Another friend would “visit“ us and would flash the lights to let us know he was there. The next day he could tell us what we were doing or watching.
>
> This is not woo but science. I believe the biology behind these abilities involves the pineal gland which when “open” acts at a “crystal radio”. nnEMF and fluoride are known to calcify the pineal glad so it stops functioning in this way.

Yes, this is what I am hearing more and more. There is also peer-reviewed literature (for what that's worth) showing higher melatonin levels in people who meditate. As well as all the chemical and electromagnetic stressors that would damage the pineal or deplete melatonin (photoreceptor damage), I am also pondering on the thought-based intellectual education system and the impact this has on pineal gland function, particularly if it is a quiet mind that primes the nervous system and allows access to these abilities and knowledge...

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-14T21:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352499>

I listened to the Telepathy Tapes twice. I truly believe we are only beginning to understand consciousness and the soul, and I am not convinced either reside in the body. If DNA has memory then when I think of Dr. Emoto's (sp?) and Luc Montagnier's work with water / DNA...and now telepathic thought, it changes my belief system. Not my faith, but my belief. There are bonus episodes including the cameraman (or producer, I forget) interview, who eventually was able to visit The Hill. I lost my husband young, to cancer. And I will tell you this...he appeared in dreams to three different unrelated people, each time wearing the same clothes, and doled out advice. One night he appeared to me, in voice only, telling me I was doing great raising our daughter, and not to listen to well-meaning family. Another time, he told me it was okay to downsize and sell our albatross of a home...in fact I could hear his voice say if you love me let it go...the memories carry with you, while I was raking leaves. This was 23 years ago. I'm serious. When he talked to me in my sleep, I actually sat up and thought I could grab him, touch him but only heard his voice. I had goosebumps. Way back, my mom died of cancer. I was 16 years old. I sat up in bed at 3:30am and said, she's gone; she had died at 3:15am at the hospital. My dad had not come home to tell us yet. I believe we can do what the kids do in the Telepathy Tapes, but it requires quieting the mind -- silencing your voice. The closest I have ever come is using my red light box behind my head and saying a rosary some nights. It takes 8 minutes for the box to quiet my thoughts --after that, it's like I can manifest things for my future, see things clearly that I want for myself or others, pray in earnest for others etc. When thinking about the Telepathy Tapes we have to remember, these kids, most of them, have no "feeling" of body. They have to be reminded they have limbs -- legs, arms etc. If you were free in your mind, unencumbered by your physical nature, I wonder what it would feel like. Even when I am lying awake on a bed, I am conscious of clothing, the feel of sheets, noises in the yard etc. These kids seem to eliminate all that -- and focus intently on one thing, or at least very few things. I've known people who mediate very deeply and claim to have out of body experiences and see things far away or talk to people in thought. I never really believed it...until the Telepathy Tapes. God bless.

### pfedorow — 2025-05-14T21:25:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352500>

This was 23 years ago. I'm serious. When he talked to me in my sleep, I actually sat up and thought I could grab him, touch him but only heard his voice. I had goosebumps. Way back, my mom died of cancer. I was 16 years old. I sat up in bed at 3:30am and said, she's gone; she had died at 3:15am at the hospital. My dad had not come home to tell us yet. I believe we can do what the kids do in the Telepathy Tapes, but it requires quieting the mind -- silencing your voice. The closest I have ever come is using my red light box behind my head and saying a rosary some nights. It takes 8 minutes for the box to quiet my thoughts --after that, it's like I can manifest things for my future, see things clearly that I want for myself or others, pray in earnest for others etc. When thinking about the Telepathy Tapes we have to remember, these kids, most of them, have no "feeling" of body. They have to be reminded they have limbs -- legs, arms etc. If you were free in your mind, unencumbered by your physical nature, I wonder what it would feel like. Even when I am lying awake on a bed, I am conscious of clothing, the feel of sheets, noises in the yard etc. These kids seem to eliminate all that -- and focus intently on one thing, or at least very few things. I've known people who mediate very deeply and claim to have out of body experiences and see things far away or talk to people in thought. I never really believed it...until the Telepathy Tapes. God bless

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-14T21:36:12+0300

### eve001 — 2025-05-16T22:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352668>

> [Mightygumshoe said:](/goto/post?id=352501)
>
> Thanks for sending this -- I understand what you are saying -- one concern I had in Telepathy Tapes was the researcher family line - the doc whose name escapes me had a dad who worked for Battelle Systems or something like that -- a fed contractor. For me it was a red flag. That said, I truly believe the mind is capable of so much, when the noise is quieted, and removed.

Interesting regarding the ELFs... These are in DE/EMI also. Other LF but les extreme LF are also said to impact humans a lot and Brian Hoyer speaks of this and how meters to measure these are hard to find.

It reminds me again of how Carl Jung didn't want light/electricity/radio at his lake house so that he could connect with other forces.

I am not convinced all humans were used to be able of these skills. Shamans, mystics etc.. enter into great discipline as I understood to aquire the powers and skills they have. But if the magnetic field of earth was much stronger then maybe metabolism and detox required less energy and maybe it was easier... also there were less toxins.

This being said it is remarkable that these people with severe autism developed these skills - since autism is so often linked to nnEMF exposure and to other toxins...

My reserve about the podcast is similar to my reserve towards Greta Thunberg being turned into this child icon of climate change activism --- young and vulnerable people --- it feels like not right to me to put them into such a role of heroes and saviors bringing the much needed change.

Of course the telepathy tapes incites sympathy and wonder and amazement and opens the mind to all that's possible... away from the old paradigm.

I do hope these spellers get to have the educational system changed so that they can participate with their gifts. But society/governments do not yet respect unjabbed/jab-injured/microwaved... And the unjabbed, jab-injured and microwaved are not even yet truly helpful to eachother either, so how do we imagine spellers can be of help? Their caregivers are probably exhausted and in need of help also.

But I have not watched the interview with Veda Austin from which you quoted [@Chlo\_e](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29446/)

In the world of healing and meditation there are these two currents: up and down.
Those who say to feel the feels and see the way through pain as the way past pain: they promote enhanced embodiment; the roots.
Then there are those who promote a detachment and abstraction from the body. Projecting ourselves in imagined realities to manifest their realization - generating feelings of excitement and gratefulness of things we want in life.

To me the turn towards a new paradigm away from newtonian mecahnistic thinking is exciting - but I feel like we have never really respected 'matter' and the materialism we see today is actually an idealism. Materialism in pop terms often stands for a love of brands andof the possession of goods. Respect for matter and for the wonder of the elements that the things in our perceptible universe are made of is rather rare. Attention for the structures and forms. How often do not people embrace quantum physics as a shortcut to reality-despising spirituality? It reminds so very much of how christianism has often despised nature, women, the body, matter, sexuality, the senses. Which is to me what vaccines, nanorobiots etc are also about: total disrespect for the wonder and beauty of the reality we are in and that we are more or less sure of.

There is now this recent idea that our reality is a simulation. Again I feel like it's from people unable to love our reality. I might be wrong but to me it sounds like total BS. What is it a simulation of? Of nothing? Then in what way is it a simulation etc... It sounds like a copy of the homunculus - before people thought there was this little man in our brain steering us. That was an explanation... The one interesting thing about it is how ot leads to the idea of fractality.

I kind of went in a tangent maybe - but it feels related.  I feel like most people have forgotten/un-learned to enjoy their embodiment. Myself incl. often. Bc of nnEMFs and co.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-17T21:35:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352763>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=352662)
>
> What kind of red light do you use?

Bestqool BQ60 from Amazon, got it on a Prime Day. I like the way they test them. I have a mat too, same brand, but foldables with LED/NIR not as strong. The “box” lets you turn on/off NIR/LED independently. I love my units. I pulled a muscle in neck and had ear infection. I put garlic oil in my ear and put the box right up to side of my neck and head, 2” away. 20 min every day for a week. Ear infection gone. At 2 weeks neck healed. But what it does to calm my mind is what blew me away. Takes just about 8 minutes. One night I did 60 min back of skull. Felt wonderful! Like I had been floating in a pool. I also use an earthing mat by Earthings. Love it too. My cats (sadly now have just one) love(d) them too, naturally going to them, and sleeping on them. Especially in winter when their fur is electric.

>

Materialism in pop terms often stands for a love of brands andof the possession of goods. >
> There is now this recent idea that our reality is a simulation. Again I feel like it's from people unable to love our reality. I might be wrong but to me it sounds like total BS. What is it a simulation of? Of nothing? Then in what way is it a simulation etc... It sounds like a copy of the homunculus - before people thought there was this little man in our brain steering us. That was an explanation... The one interesting thing about it is how ot leads to the idea of fractality.
>
> I kind of went in a tangent maybe - but it feels related.  I feel like most people have forgotten/un-learned to enjoy their embodiment. Myself incl. often. Bc of nnEMFs and co.

I've had lots of similar thought processes.

I believe many of us are searching for discernment without paranoia. We are reaching (already have reached?) a point where we can be quite easily deceived by information obtained through our 5 senses, particularly as AI advances. I suppose it's up to each individual what they do with the information presented in the Telepathy Tapes. In my view, this is something to be (re)learned and experienced, starting with a strong cultivation of intuition. No amount of descriptive words could capture the taste of a strawberry- it needs to be experienced.

Some say we are here to experience extremes and opposites. That we can learn the most and the fastest when under extreme discomfort and backed into a corner. The concept of us having such innate abilities depends on one's view of history, human origins and reality in general. I am also struck by how damaged the nervous system can be and yet, these individuals have seemingly tapped into other channels of reality. A recurring theme in my research is the degree to which we identify with the physical body- which as you mention- these kids/young adults need to be reminded that they have limbs. Perhaps this is an important factor. 'Be in the world but not of it' etc.

There are countless interviews with people who've had NDEs or OBEs who also say this is a 'simulation' of sorts- i.e not the totality of reality. However, many of them live their lives much more fully, engaged, present and without fear after these events- they expand, rather than shrink, after experiencing reality from a different perspective.

A final point that I find intriguing which overlaps with JK (this is not mentioned in the TTs but keeps cropping up in my readings and research) is that the amount of light the body can hold is also linked with these abilities- however there may be other inputs which increase this capacity beyond sunlight and environmental mitigation- still an area I am exploring further. In any case... what a time to be alive!

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-22T20:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-353143>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=352814)
>
> Great! Thanks! It's a panel, right? I was wondering if a box was different from a panel. I will try one on batteries then, as I am still pretty sensitive to EMFs.

If you buy a "soft" pad also sold by Bestqool or another brand, you will never get the level of power that you get from a "box" with LEDs -- I mean a hard box. I have a smaller unit that can be pointed at a small area on a face and it runs on batteries. Different brand. Seldom use. That said, NOTHING beats the box. The soft wrap is nice because you can roll it around a sore knee or put it on your lap/belly. But the "boxes" are really powerful. I love mine. I use it every other day, minimum. There are bigger options too. The reason I like Bestqool (again other brands probably offer the same) -- is that their hard box allows you to turn on/off LED and near infrared independently (their pads do not) but I like the option of just using the red light vs both together. Also, I appreciate the way they test their devices. Hooga or something like that, cannot remember the name, also sells boxes. When Amazon Prime days come around you can get them on a discount. My BQ60 was marked "way" down. Best purchase ever. On "full power" if the pad we have is put behind a skull/neck it does the same relaxation thing as the box, but it takes longer and has to be put directly on skin to see the benefit.The box, I can set about 4-6" from the back of my head and in 8 minutes on average, I am in a very special "chill out" as I call it. I have done it for an hour at a time on occasion, and feel like I slept for three hours -- that relaxed. Before you buy anything, look at Amazon reviews. And research. I have a family member extremely affected by EMF, cell phones etc (body rash, skull pain, anxiety, headaches etc....and more) and there has never been a problem using the LED/Near Infrared box. I own both these, Redot and BQ60.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-23T17:53:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-353216>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=353191)
>
> Thanks so much for sharing the details! It is very interesting for me to know it is so helpful for you. I may try it at some point and will keep your recommendations. My concern is that we have lots of dirty electricity (DE) here - it seems to affect me a lot, (esp. on sunny days) which is why I am mindful of using plugged-in devices. I do have an IR/red incandescent 300Watt lamp and don't use it often. All houses/neighborhoods are different so maybe DE is not an issue for others bc there is little of it or bc they are less affected bc of better redox etc.

1) I have a family member VERY affected by non-native EMF. Checks almost every box. Skull pain with microwave running (if behind it in another room because not protected with faraday cage),rashes, pain in head if holds a cell phone nearby, leg pain with laptop on lap; I purchased faraday fabric and want to sew a nice work jacket for protection for this person; This is one of the main reasons why I became a gold member and am hoping to steer her to that as well -- a family membership.

2) Red light has helped a lot for anxiety, sleep...but is not all we did. We also:
\- Eat organic grass fed meat/good shrimp etc, organic veggies.
3) This AM put BQ60 red light box on myself this morning. 20 minutes. 10 upper body, 10 minutes holding it to my face and skull top about 6" away; and maybe added another 5 for a lower arm (radius / ulna- I pulled up big pieces of grass this week).
\- I get such peace afterwards. I do not know much about red light except what I read in like 40 papers (data miner) and this was almost 2 years ago, ditto grounding, which I do -- and funny thing, the cat(s) do too. These items are not cheap, but do some reading; and check Amazon for Prime days. Your health is your wealth. Mine was marked down, way down -- like to $110 or $120 or so I grabbed it.
\- The earthings mat I ordered comes with 2. They also sent me instructions for testing e-field etc...see below.
\- Do some research. Bestqool and others make pads some that you can interconnect if you later buy a second one (like a mattress set up - to make it longer).

As for dirty electricity,

1) I had an antique lamp next to my bed -- 100 yr old or close to it. Not grounded, metal frame, common in the day.
2) 40 volt E field off of it (used voltmeter on AC with one plug in a ground port of outlet, and other in my fingertips -- thumb/forefinger of one hand, as I moved around illuminated fixtures etc). YIKES.
3) To resolve, I got rid of almost all crap in my room. Also, the method I mention to measure, is a great way to be sure your grounding mat works.

PROCEDURE:
1) Plug the voltmeter into the same outlet the grounding mat is plugged into (the ground plug);
2) and touch the pad with a free hand,
3) Hold the other cable end of the voltmeter cord in the other hand's thumb/forefinger finger tips.
4) Be sure meter is set to AC volts.
RESULT: should go to zero on your multimeter/voltmeter if you touch your pad and it works (is grounded).

I do use an old style alarm clock/radio. But I have a wind up one (no kidding) am thinking of using instead.

We cut internet at night, use a grounding pad on bed, lights off, blue light blocking etc, morning sun. I sleep like a BABY. I am 62, and have not felt this good since I was in my early 20s. My skin looks so good now. A neighbor even asked what I was doing -- and I pointed up. For the record, I love frankincense oil too, mixed with castor or fractionated coconut. And now at end of shower I turn it to cold and try to stand there for however long I can stand. Not quite ready for thermogenesis... we have all but ceased using the microwave. No more unstructured water for us.....

Have you ever measured the field (diff than EMF) around your rooms at night / during the day near extension cord outlets, lights when on / off, table lamps, etc. Consider what I mentioned above \[numbered 1-5\] with voltmeter (there are YT videos out there);

Red light helped me relax but getting rid of E field devices near my bedside made a huge difference! Some places in my room I picked up 10 volts AC, other places like lamp 20 to 40; and we found some dead spots in our living room which explains my favorite reading chair where I always say I feel so relaxed.... God bless.

edited to put bullets on recommendation .

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-353232>

So much better.
The voltmeter will drop when touching a grounding pad (confirming it is grounded) because you are now double grounded and the field is going to ground through the pad NOT the meter . . . BUT you are still in exactly the same field.

The field did not change. In fact it is now flowing through YOU go ground. Think water fall vs a bath tub.

AND - if you are using the house ground you are now connected to one mile +/- of house wiring - that is a BIG antenna to be connected to.

AND - if you use the ground wire they sent you it has a resistor in it (so you don’t get electrocuted in the event of a power surge) which also stop the negative ions from the ground/Earth getting to you (the very reason to “ground”). This has been discussed on many posts here on the Forum.

By the way, the 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field you are measuring IS the nnEMF.

More info: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

I have a friend whose sister is vaxed damaged from 50 yrs ago, who become non-verbal at the age of 4 after the vax. And my friend says that her sister communicates with her by sending her thought pictures and mini films in her head for the last 10 years or so. As her sister can understand what is being said, my friend confirms things verbally back to her to get a positive or negative response.

To do this my friend says she almost has to meditate and become very still.

With the covid scam by friend didn't want her sister vaxed, but it all went to court and the court decided that she should be for her safety even though the sister was saying she didn't want it. The court appointed psychiatrist refused to stay in the same room as her sister because she said she felt the sister was making her sleepy and trying to communicate with her and it freaked her out. My friend said that is how she will tell you she doesn't want it, but the psych. was having none of it.

I just get too excited at the thought of it that I cannot calm my mind down!

If the powers that be can weaponise something they will.

# Thread: Outdoor CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-ct.30808/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2025-05-02T01:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-ct.30808/post-351678>

Would anyone mind sharing their outdoor CT setups or any tips/tricks?

I'm most interested in a cheap/easy to move setup for the moment. Something like a galvanized metal trough + water + ice and just figure out the right water:ice ratio to get the temperature where I want it.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-02T02:07:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-ct.30808/post-351686>

> [Up\_only said:](/goto/post?id=351678)
>
> Would anyone mind sharing their outdoor CT setups or any tips/tricks?
>
> I'm most interested in a cheap/easy to move setup for the moment. Something like a galvanized metal trough + water + ice and just figure out the right water:ice ratio to get the temperature where I want it.

Cheap and easy would be a rubbermaid stock tank. I have a 568 litre (150 gallon).. Add a metal drain valve (brass or the like) and have a jumper cable that you bond the valve to a peg in the earth + add some epsom salt for further enhancement..

### Mr.AJ — 2025-05-02T16:25:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-ct.30808/post-351748>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=351686)
>
> Cheap and easy would be a rubbermaid stock tank. I have a 568 litre (150 gallon).. Add a metal drain valve (brass or the like) and have a jumper cable that you bond the valve to a peg in the earth + add some epsom salt for further enhancement..

For keeping the temperature in range do you just start with some ratio of water to ice and measure until it's where you want it? I'm gonna be doing this is a >100 degree climate so it might be tough to keep the water 50 or lower for very long.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-05-02T17:11:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-ct.30808/post-351756>

> [Up\_only said:](/goto/post?id=351748)
>
> For keeping the temperature in range do you just start with some ratio of water to ice and measure until it's where you want it? I'm gonna be doing this is a >100 degree climate so it might be tough to keep the water 50 or lower for very long.

You will undershoot the temp with bags of ice in 100 degree environments, but since the goal is to shock the body, briefly, and be able to warm to the new temperature, it should do fine. The greater the mass of cold water (use a big trough), the more it will maintain temperature, but will require lots of ice, relative to how cold the water that is used to fill the trough.

I have been doing CT, especially as my pool water has been in the low 70's, and the shock is brief, but my reaction is always the same, swimming/moving frantically to get the blood moving. A calm always waits for me, in about 5 minutes or less, and then I can stay in the colder water almost indefinitely. Of course, 70 degrees is pleasant compared to much colder ice-filled troughs.

I did contrast therapy when I used colder water and after 3 cycles I reached nirvana, and the intense feeling lasted long after.

### Jacks — 2025-05-05T03:48:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-ct.30808/post-351911>

Looks like a fairly cheap option.

[Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/ISIDO-Ice-Reusable-Water-Balloons/dp/B0CS2ZKNX2/)

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-05-05T22:30:40+0300

>
>

That will the job. I do enjoy swimming to get the heart pumping and circulate the blood through my body, making the cold intensity go away in about 5 minutes.

### Saichi — 2025-05-05T23:47:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/outdoor-ct.30808/post-351980>

The greater the mass of cold water (use a big trough), the more it will maintain temperature, but will require lots of ice, relative to how cold the water that is used to fill the trough.
>
> I have been doing CT, especially as my pool water has been in the low 70's, and the shock is brief, but my reaction is always the same, swimming/moving frantically to get the blood moving. A calm always waits for me, in about 5 minutes or less, and then I can stay in the colder water almost indefinitely. Of course, 70 degrees is pleasant compared to much colder ice-filled troughs.

I can't imagine 70's ever being considered CT or causing shock. Anything above 55 isn't worth my time. One time I went into a broken banya cold plunge at 63 and I walked right out.

### NickD — 2025-05-06T01:35:39+0300

They make an a/c-type unit that cools pool water, for when the ambient temp increases, but we are moving and I won't put more $ into this house. We are moving to a lake where the water will be colder, as it has much deeper water. I plan on getting a separate cold plunge unit.

# Thread: 5G in rural areas?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-rural-areas.30825/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Asael Enriquez — 2025-05-04T00:12:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-rural-areas.30825/post-351861>

Hi all,

I recently relocated to a new home a couple months back on the outksirts of a small village called Cloudcroft NM. I used to live in another house 10 minutes away down the mountain, in a more populated area of no more than 1,500 people. The nnEMF at this previous house was relatively low at less than 5 uW/m^2 and everything at the house is hardwired, I also have a cut off switch for the 60Hz electric fields. I lived there for a couple of years.

At this new location, the house is located higher up in the mountain, overseeing a large valley behind it. When the home was purchased, we assumed there were no cellphone towers nearby. Based on the findings at antennasearch.com, the closest ones are about 2-2.5 miles away.

About a month ago, a friend of mine went to stay over the weekend and was quite shocked to see that he was able to make calls on his phone. I had the WiFi turned off the entire time, since I only use hardwired connections unless I have guests over. When I asked him to see his phone, he was connected to the verizon wireless 5G network. I had trouble sleeping for about 3 months now, waking up constantly throughout the night and feeling fatigued during the day. Keep in mind that this same friend had visited earlier in January and had no reception. This suggests that the tower/node was installed just recently. As I took RF readings with my GigaHertz solutions devices, the RF is extremely high in all the bedrooms upstairs. However, the bottom floor of the house is relatively low.

In attempts to lower the RF upstairs, I purchased some EMF blocking curtains along with shielding fabric to cover the entire wall that faces the back part where the canyon/valley is. Would this enough to mitigate it, or should other things be implemented as well?

My theory is that since the house is high up on a hill, it is in a direct line of sight where the cellphone tower(s) are. All the bedrooms have large windows and there are no obstructions that help mitigate the RF signals coming from the outside. Additionally, the meter I used is a mid band 5G meter that reads up to 8GHz, would it be necessary to purchase a meter that reads higher bands such as 20-40GHz range? I am aware that these higher bands have poor penetration cababilities and do not travel that far. Could it be that the RF also come from the 4G towers and single antennas that are far away but the singal seems to concentrate in that area due to the high elevation?

I felt relatively well at the previous home, but here it seems that my symptoms are exponentially more noticable such as brain fog, fatigue, loss of sleep, loss of appetite and stress.

Any input on this is greatly appreciated

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-04T06:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-rural-areas.30825/post-351871>

ln a rural area the 5G from Verizon would be 3.7 GHz (C-band similar to AT&T). What are the readings outside, on each side of the house. You have a meter, drive around and find where it is coming from. If your mitigation has worked your RF reading will be lower.

### eve001 — 2025-05-04T14:23:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-rural-areas.30825/post-351876>

Sounds familiar.
Are there new (verizon) street lights in your street?
Some seem to be emitting RFs (SSP2).
Certain plants will also absorb and reduce a lot of the incoming radiation. (evergreens preferably)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Building an outdoor bed
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-an-outdoor-bed.30826/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 3

### Mr.AJ — 2025-05-04T00:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-an-outdoor-bed.30826/post-351862>

So I have sleep apnea and after learning a bunch about grounding I'm considering doing a biohack of only sleeping outside grounded for a few months to see if it improves. I have enough space to do this and I'm in a low nnEMF environment, but I do live in an area with a ton of ticks, bugs, snakes, etc etc.

1) What would be a good material to use as my floor to lay on at night that would still keep me grounded to the earth but would be good protection from the bugs and insects? Temperature is not a huge concern because the coldest it usually gets here is around 10 F in the peak of winter and I can always add blankets if needed. Rain is another big factor because we get a shit ton of rain at times.

2) Would this be better done on grass or dirt or does it not matter?

3) For the actual structure I'm debating between a yurt or tipi at the moment, but open to other suggestions. I suspect it will only be me out here for the time being.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-04T06:58:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-an-outdoor-bed.30826/post-351872>

Are you sleeping directly on the ground or some kind of pad?

# Thread: Colonoscopy Question

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Christopher C — 2025-05-04T22:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colonoscopy-question.30832/post-351902>

Hi everyone!
Jack recently mentioned plans to challenge a well known gastroenterologist about the usefulness of colonoscopies. I did the read paper below. I have a family history of this disease. My father died from cancer that began on his liver and spread to his colon. Nevertheless, I’ve been very weary of getting a colonoscopy for years now, after reading the work of Dr Sherri Tenpenny (The Truth About Colonoscopies) and learning about how many mistakes are made, how dangerous the procedure can be and how it often does not make a difference as far as cancer mortality. My question for this group of people is: Is there any other lab or test that’s recommended for someone like me to gauge their colon health? I’m really not thrilled with the idea of a tube being put up my butt… Note: I am 47 years old, I do get a lot of Sun, I limit my exposure to blue light/nnEMF. I ground everyday at sunrise and usually also at sunset. I wear blue blockers at night and really stay away from screens before bedtime. I use a red light sauna from Therasage as well. My vitamin D is due to be tested but last time I checked it was 82. Any tips are appreciated. Thanks so much!

PAPER: [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5518954/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5518954/)

I just dropped a video inspired by **Decentralized Medicine #44** — and this might be the _only_ part of Jack’s blogs I’ve been able to fully wrap my head around. The image of Earth as a giant lightning rod lit up by volcanic sulfur? Finally, something I could _see_, not just stare at blankly while my mitochondria cried for help.

Honestly, maybe I should’ve stuck to Patreon — I feel like I accidentally enrolled in **Quantum Biology 401** when I was just looking for **"Sunlight for Dummies."**

That said, I’m here for it. I’m trying to suck this stuff up, even if it feels like I’m reading theoretical physics in a foreign language — upside down — on fire.

Shoutout to Jack. The man’s brain operates on a different frequency, but when the gears _do_ connect, it’s like flipping on a light switch you didn’t know you had.

# Thread: MS, Big Pharma, and the Moment I Woke the F*** Up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-big-pharma-and-the-moment-i-woke-the-f-up.30852/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-05-08T04:19:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-big-pharma-and-the-moment-i-woke-the-f-up.30852/post-352131>

My daughter—31—started having weird eye issues **last summer**. Blurriness, trouble focusing—nothing made sense. She saw an eye doc, got referred around, and by **October**, they gave her the diagnosis: **Multiple Sclerosis**.

That alone was a heavy one.

She’s been going through the system ever since. Then in **March**, her neurologist mentioned she’d be **leaving the current hospital**, but told my daughter she could follow her to her new practice if she wanted.

Around that time, they also started her on a drug—**Zeposia**, I think—about **8 weeks ago**.

So earlier this **May**, my daughter logs into the **new hospital’s portal** to message the doc about a new symptom…

And **boom**, first thing that pops up:

> A disclaimer saying the doctor is a **paid representative for the same drug company** that makes the med she just prescribed.

Dauger told me right after she logged in to the new hospital—and that was it.

> This is _exactly_ what Jack Kruse has been warning about for years.

I didn’t get the full picture until I got behind the **Klub paywall**. Before that, I was just getting the media-safe, ad-friendly YouTube version that keeps it palatable and politically correct.

This isn’t about healing. It’s about **pushing product**. The doctor pipeline is wired into pharma. And they don't even hide it—they put it right in the portal now.

> You f\*\*\* with my kid’s health to move product—**Papa Bear wakes up.**

I’m learning now. And yeah, I’m still skeptical—but I’m **wide awake**.

Not staying quiet anymore.

— _One sunrise at a time. For her and for me._


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mangoqueen — 2025-05-08T23:44:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-healing-eyes-and-light.30854/post-352163>

Im asking to find definitive info about how IOL lenses that block UV below 380nm will potentially affect my health, please.

Ive booked an elective RLE op for mid june, and just recently found you ( thanks to a podcast).
Im 65, I live at 17th latitude S for 30 of my years.
So Im now second guessing my decision to go ahead.

Im relatively healthy, never taken meds and now never missing a sunrise. Clean diet, clean air and water, and I spend 2 hours walking and swimming in ocean daily at sunrise.

### Brent Patrick — 2025-05-09T00:30:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-healing-eyes-and-light.30854/post-352166>

Welcome.

If you go ahead with procedure you will have to try and make up the deficit by having majority of skin exposed to full spectrum SUNLight for longer periods of time.

A post from Jack on FB 6TH DEC 2016 and link to study you may find interesting.

**One very exciting possibility mentioned in this paper is that they believe there might be a future production of an intraocular lens where solar UV light is converted to IR-A to repair mitochondria. Guess what my December 2016 webinar was about? How that "lens" like photochemical was built into our mitochondria by nature to increase our yield of this regenerative light for mitochondria. Glaucoma is another disease of the eye tied to myopia and a lack of sunlight in the human environment. Sunlight has UV and IR light buried within its rays and both can help heal a failing eye. It's been shown in recent large meta-analysis that Increased UVB environmental exposure is associated with reduced myopia, particularly in adolescence and young adulthood. If myopia is diminshed we can expect glaucoma incidence to drop as well. Why? The link between myopia and glaucoma has been explored for nearly a century by eye docs. A number of studies, including the Blue Mountains Eye Study and the Beijing Eye Study, have found that the risk of glaucoma appears to increase in persons with high myopia (6 diopters or more). Recently, Kuldev Singh, MD, MPH, at Stanford University, and Shan C. Lin, MD, at the University of California, San Francisco, reported a similar association in a U.S. population. This myopia trend globally is due to our blue lit microwaved world and the eye docs are slowly catching on. The Japanese eye docs are the furthest along in understanding this linkage to our poorly constructed modern world of light engineering. Dr. Law has been quoted as saying that patients with mild myopia appear to follow a course similar to those without myopia. This should concern us all. It means our eye clock is being destroyed by our neighbors choices.** [http://www.sciencedirect.com/.../pii/S1567724916302586](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1567724916302586) **JK**

"The positive effects of red light includes promoting wound healing, cause complement propagation, and reducing oxidative stress, inflammation and cell death (Del Olmo-Aguado et al., 2012, Liang et al., 2006, Wong-Riley et al., 2005, Ying et al., 2008)) as well as improving recovery rates of soft tissue injuries and myocardial infarction (Oron et al., 2001, Simunovic et al., 2000). **In the case of the eye, red light stimulates COX and ATP synthesis in the retina (Begum et al., 2013, Gkotsi et al., 2014) and protects against photoreceptor death in situ"**

### mangoqueen — 2025-05-09T11:12:57+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-healing-eyes-and-light.30854/post-352248>

>
> Not a valid or smart reason for the damage the lenses will cause.. be ok with the glasses as at least you can take them off..

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=352239)

> I always thought the IOL were just for cataracts! I have presbyopia, but I guess it is fairly mild as I just wear basic drug store glasses with some magnification for reading in dim light or for detail work only, and I have blue blocking ones with magnification for nightime reading/computer use from Lucia Eyes.

Lol. Me too originally, but my sight with drugstore strong reader glasses impacts my life daily. Im a beekeeper and , well, it gets very messy constantly pushing glasses up my honey smeared nose. And try fixing something when your head is upside down and you need 2 hands free. Haha! Plus the gremlins are always hiding them from.me....

### mangoqueen — 2025-05-10T22:52:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-healing-eyes-and-light.30854/post-352284>

Thankyou thankyou thankyou MTA321... This is 100% what I needed to study exactly what effect the iol may have on my retina and inner eyes. So interesting too that blue light without UV is very damaging, also that as we age and natural lenses harden, that blue receptvity is only 20% of when our eyes are "born". Thus lenses like i was planning block all uv and let through blue light same as a babies. So no balance or protection to the cornea or retina. Wow. Seriously. Thankyou.

# Thread: sick looking kids
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-looking-kids.30857/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Wanttoknow — 2025-05-09T06:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-looking-kids.30857/post-352184>

I was sitting in the park today eating lunch and watched a group of high school kids walk by. They were walking slow, heads hung down, no life in them no joy. I thought these kids are just like growing a tomato plant from seed. One seed under the sun and one seed inside the house under florescent light. The kids are like the tomato plant grown inside under artificial light straggly, thin, barely growing. Its obvious people are not getting the sunlight needed for health. Very sad and no way to stop this trend from getting worse. I am very thankful I understand how important the sun is for health

They are kept indoors at school all day under fluorescent lights. Younger kids do get more outside breaks, but high school kids only have 40 mins for lunch here. Definitely lots of mental health issues amongst teens these days.

Take a good look around when out in public..

# Thread: El Salvador Travel Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Guy Houri — 2025-05-10T17:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-352269>

**El Salvador Mito Guide**​

Hello everyone. In the last 2 weeks i stayed in El Salvador and thought i would share information for anyone else coming ahead.

If anyone wants to reach out I'd be glad to help
Guy
+972544312639
[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

## What to do?​

### Build Your Redox​

**Sun** - Overall great sun. I sweated buckets there (deuterium?) Some places tough there were too many mosquitoes to the point it outweighed the sun for me (bad redox?) and drinking water not that good. But i got a great tan.

**EMF** - not great in my opinion. No 5G but antennas everywhere so keep in mind you will be able to mitigate only in mito places. Also the wiring of the houses is bad and there can be problems with dirty fields from AC and other stuff. I also had static charge build up in my bed.

**Food** - i liked it ton of seafood and great places to go to. Usually cost 10-15 usd for ceviche and 20 usd for massive portions.

### Meet fellow Mito's:​

Most mito’s in ES i met are people that came there to heal, Id say an average age of about 40-50. And there are some families as well. Though i am 23 and was youngest there overall everyone i met was super nice and i had a great time learning and resonating with fellow Mito's.

**MMM (Monthly Mito Meetup)** - [@India Rose Hopper](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29690/) makes it happen and is very nice, she also has rooms available place in shalpa.

**Bitcoin farmers market** - every second sunday with nice jewelry and food.

**EMF class** - Every thursday [@Olle Kitsing](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22800/) a building biologist organizes, i learned a lot.

**Mito Haus activities** - cold plunging on the weekends and Mito TV where a kruse podcast is watched and discussed was on wednesdays. [@bennefit-coaching](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31032/) is in charge, can also rent there in Atami.

**Sunrise at the beach** - In Atami i think is the largest community and I've met some great people and there was always someone at the beach. Shalpa also has some Mito's but not sure if there are other places like that.

### Bitcoin Activities​

I was actually disappointed with that. Meetups werent that great and very little people.

**Mi Primer Bitcoin** - in san salvador organizes some, also classes.

Also can check here on [telegram](https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador).

**The Bitcoin Hardware Store** - In El Zonte on mondays there is a class which i enjoyed. but i recommend checking beforehand if it happens.

**Bitcoin Farmers Market** - In El Zonte on every other sunday - homemade brownies with no seed oil , soaps, jewleries, foods and drinnks and most importantly bitcoiners, what more can someone want ?

### Nature Activities​

**Hiking** - There is Tamanique waterfall hike which is nice, only 2 hours but is quite difficult. You get there and take a guide for a couple of dollars. Also hiking santa ana volcano pwople do, pay attention to get there early enough to catch a guide, we didn't make it on time.
Overall I'd say not many hiking options you see online and i thought I'll do more waterfalls but the logistics were complicated for me, not knowing spanish and no online info about more hikes.

**Nature Sightseeing** - if you arent much of a hiker, you can still enjoy views in lake cotopaque, we rented there a jetski half an hour cost 35 usd. Also in Usulutan (near san miguel) are great views like espiritu de la montana.

**Surfing** - In the mito beaches you can't surf much because of rocks and the waves are too wild. You can bring your own board and drive to El Sunzal or stay at a hostel near.

### Learn About ES​

We did a day in the city going to museums learning about ES civil war and mayaan culture which was nice.
Chihuatan mayaan ruins was realy good we were alone there and got a guide all to ourselfs i highly recommend it. Guide is free and we tipped 5 usd.

## When to come?​

ES has two seasons. wet season (May-October) and dry season (November-April)

Dry season is also winter months in the northern hemisphere, so that time is more touristy and you'll find more people there.

In rainy season it mostly rains at night. But still strong sun, tough can be some cloudy days.

I'd recommend coming in tourist season for 1-2 weeks. Perhaps even extending from ES to Yucatan (tulum..) or taking a shuttle to Guatemala (only 5 hours).
More than 2 weeks - there aren't many stuff to do here tourist wise.

## Where to come?​

Some say the beach area is too hot for them, so they stay in the mountains where it is a bit colder. I will write about the main places in ES.

### El Tunco​

Hippe touristy vibes. Good for surfing. Also parties there if anyone is interested.

### El Zonte (Bitcoin Beach)​

Overall i didn't see much to do there so I don't recommend, though it's nice maybe having dinner there walking around.

### Atami​

Here you will always find Mito’s at sunrise, and pretty close to everything. You can stay at Maria Therasa house or at Benne's or find airbnb/hotel. The beach here is not comfortable to go into because waves are realy strong.

### Shalpa​

Less Mito's than Atami. And further away. But less EMF, beach is beautiful, and india hopper is realy nice.

### Xandau​

I think Evelyn has a solo place there. I haven't been but some Mito's are there I understand.

### San Miguel​

I haven't been there but it's seems nice calm beaches and great view.

### San Salvador​

I wouldn't sleep in the city. Not more than one night at most. Isn't much to do there.

### Santa Ana​

There is the volcano and lake. There was also some Mito event in hotel Campo Bello and it is beautiful there

## Mito Housing Breakdown​

I think someone should do a list with low EMF airbnbs. But i don't know of one so if that is important you must contact olle to check it first.

I highly recommend staying at one of the mito houses - that way you can get to know people and they'll help you out with your travels.

I stayed with Benne in Atami (mito haus / mansion / german haus) and liked all the activities there. It is not super low emf but quite good. Maria and India's are also recommended. Also [@SunHunter ☀️](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28398/) (Evelyn) has a place in xandau.

**Logistics

Money** - there are some ATM's that you can take out cash. costs 5$. not sure about the situation if bitcoin will not be legal tender anymore in ES, evan starbucks takes bitcoin.

**Transportation** - driving in ES is not for the the faint of heart. no streat lights and sometimes there are slow trucks that slow down the traffic, or fog in the mountains, or bad roads. we rented a car 35$ from our house in Atami and had a great time (+503 7896 1373 Medrano's Rent a car). You could also contact carlos which is a taxi driver that drove Mito's and knows english (+503 7775 7534). It is also common to hitchike, or you can take the buses but i haven't tried looked a bit complicated and not worth the wait - Uber works realy well.

**Language** - Sometimes i had no wifi so i downloaded spanish-english translate beforehand on google but it did not translate well. Might be a good idea to learn some basics words.

## Further details​

If anyone has more questions I'll be happy to answer.
I was realy happy to get to know the Mito's. Thanks to everyone i met you were all very kind and i had a pleasure getting to know you all.
Until next time !

### harezlak19 — 2025-05-11T05:02:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-352301>

Didn't have any issues using card to pay. Always good to have options just in case!

One thing I would add is if you are going to ES to heal and recover, stay away from El Tunco area. This is where most of the surfers stay and where the nightlife is. Very loud and EMF central.

> Most mito’s in ES i met are people that came there to heal, Id say an average age of about 40-50. And there are some families as well. Though i am 23 and was youngest there overall everyone i met was super nice and i had a great time learning and resonating with fellow Mito's.
>
> **MMM (Monthly Mito Meetup)** - [@India Rose Hopper](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29690/) makes it happen and is very nice, she also has rooms available place in shalpa.
>
> **Bitcoin farmers market** - every second sunday with nice jewelry and food.
>
> **EMF class** - Every thursday [@Olle Kitsing](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22800/) a building biologist organizes, i learned a lot.
>
> **Mito Haus activities** - cold plunging on the weekends and Mito TV where a kruse podcast is watched and discussed was on wednesdays. [@bennefit-coaching](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31032/) is in charge, can also rent there in Atami.
>
> **Sunrise at the beach** - In Atami i think is the largest community and I've met some great people and there was always someone at the beach. Shalpa also has some Mito's but not sure if there are other places like that.
>
>
> ### Bitcoin Activities​
>
> I was actually disappointed with that. Meetups werent that great and very little people.
>
> **Mi Primer Bitcoin** - in san salvador organizes some, also classes.
>
> Also can check here on [telegram](https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador).
>
> **The Bitcoin Hardware Store** - In El Zonte on mondays there is a class which i enjoyed. but i recommend checking beforehand if it happens.
>
>
> ### Nature Activities​
>
>
> **Hiking** - There is Tamanique waterfall hike which is nice, only 2 hours but is quite difficult. You get there and take a guide for a couple of dollars. Also hiking santa ana volcano pwople do, pay attention to get there early enough to catch a guide, we didn't make it on time.
> Overall I'd say not many hiking options you see online and i thought I'll do more waterfalls but the logistics were complicated for me, not knowing spanish and no online info about more hikes.
>
> **Nature Sightseeing** - if you arent much of a hiker, you can still enjoy views in lake cotopaque, we rented there a jetski half an hour cost 35 usd. Also in Usulutan (near san miguel) are great views like espiritu de la montana.
>
> **Surfing** - In the mito beaches you can't surf much because of rocks and the waves are too wild. You can bring your own board and drive to El Sunzal or stay at a hostel near.
>
>
> ### Learn About ES​
>
>
> We did a day in the city going to museums learning about ES civil war and mayaan culture which was nice.
> Chihuatan mayaan ruins was realy good we were alone there and got a guide all to ourselfs i highly recommend it. Guide is free and we tipped 5 usd.
>
>
>
> ## When to come?​
>
>
> ES has two seasons. wet season (May-October) and dry season (November-April)
>
> Dry season is also winter months in the northern hemisphere, so that time is more touristy and you'll find more people there.
>
> In rainy season it mostly rains at night. But still strong sun, tough can be some cloudy days.
>
> I'd recommend coming in tourist season for 1-2 weeks. Perhaps even extending from ES to Yucatan (tulum..) or taking a shuttle to Guatemala (only 5 hours).
> More than 2 weeks - there aren't many stuff to do here tourist wise.
>
>
>
> ## Where to come?​
>
>
> Some say the beach area is too hot for them, so they stay in the mountains where it is a bit colder. I will write about the main places in ES.
>
>
> ### El Tunco​
>
> Hippe touristy vibes. Good for surfing. Also parties there if anyone is interested.
>
>
> ### El Zonte (Bitcoin Beach)​
>
> Overall i didn't see much to do there so I don't recommend, though it's nice maybe having dinner there walking around.
>
>
> ### Atami​
>
> Here you will always find Mito’s at sunrise, and pretty close to everything. You can stay at Maria Therasa house or at Benne's or find airbnb/hotel. The beach here is not comfortable to go into because waves are realy strong.
>
>
> ### Shalpa​
>
> Less Mito's than Atami. And further away. But less EMF, beach is beautiful, and india hopper is realy nice.
>
>
> ### Xandau​
>
> I think Evelyn has a solo place there. I haven't been but some Mito's are there I understand.
>
>
> ### San Miguel​
>
> I haven't been there but it's seems nice calm beaches and great view.
>
>
> ### San Salvador​
>
> I wouldn't sleep in the city. Not more than one night at most. Isn't much to do there.
>
>
> ### Santa Ana​
>
> There is the volcano and lake. There was also some Mito event in hotel Campo Bello and it is beautiful there
>
>
>
> ## Mito Housing Breakdown​
>
>
> I think someone should do a list with low EMF airbnbs. But i don't know of one so if that is important you must contact olle to check it first.
>
> I highly recommend staying at one of the mito houses - that way you can get to know people and they'll help you out with your travels.
>
> I stayed with Benne in Atami (mito haus / mansion / german haus) and liked all the activities there. It is not super low emf but quite good. Maria and India's are also recommended. Also [@SunHunter ☀️](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28398/) (Evelyn) has a place in xandau.
>
>
> **Logistics
>
> Money** - there are some ATM's that you can take out cash. costs 5$. not sure about the situation if bitcoin will not be legal tender anymore in ES, evan starbucks takes bitcoin.
>
> **Transportation** - driving in ES is not for the the faint of heart. no streat lights and sometimes there are slow trucks that slow down the traffic, or fog in the mountains, or bad roads. we rented a car 35$ from our house in Atami and had a great time (+503 7896 1373 Medrano's Rent a car). You could also contact carlos which is a taxi driver that drove Mito's and knows english (+503 7775 7534). It is also common to hitchike, or you can take the buses but i haven't tried looked a bit complicated and not worth the wait - Uber works realy well.
>
> **Language** - Sometimes i had no wifi so i downloaded spanish-english translate beforehand on google but it did not translate well. Might be a good idea to learn some basics words.
>
>
> ## Further details​
>
> If anyone has more questions I'll be happy to answer.
> I was realy happy to get to know the Mito's. Thanks to everyone i met you were all very kind and i had a pleasure getting to know you all.
> Until next time !

Thank you for your kind words. It is always a privilege getting to know new Mitos.

### India Rose Hopper — 2025-05-12T16:46:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-352366>

> [michellejorge said:](/goto/post?id=352278)
>
> Thank you! This is SO awesome! Are credit cards routinely used or mostly cash and bitcoin? Headed there at the end of the month!

You can use credit cards at most restaurants, stores, etc. it is good to have cash for Street vendors, Fruit trucks, fish market, etc.
The super selectos grocery store takes bitcoin also.

### Jana C — 2025-05-14T17:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-352484>

> [guy houri said:](/goto/post?id=352269)
>
> **El Salvador Mito Guide**​
>
>
>
> Hello everyone. In the last 2 weeks i stayed in El Salvador and thought i would share information for anyone else coming ahead.
>
> If anyone wants to reach out I'd be glad to help
> Guy
> +972544312639
> [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
>
>
> ## What to do?​
>
>
>
> ### Build Your Redox​
>
>
> **Sun** - Overall great sun. I sweated buckets there (deuterium?) Some places tough there were too many mosquitoes to the point it outweighed the sun for me (bad redox?) and drinking water not that good. But i got a great tan.
>
> **EMF** - not great in my opinion. No 5G but antennas everywhere so keep in mind you will be able to mitigate only in mito places. Also the wiring of the houses is bad and there can be problems with dirty fields from AC and other stuff. I also had static charge build up in my bed.
>
> **Food** - i liked it ton of seafood and great places to go to. Usually cost 10-15 usd for ceviche and 20 usd for massive portions.
>
>
> ### Meet fellow Mito's:​
>
>
> Most mito’s in ES i met are people that came there to heal, Id say an average age of about 40-50. And there are some families as well. Though i am 23 and was youngest there overall everyone i met was super nice and i had a great time learning and resonating with fellow Mito's.
>
> **MMM (Monthly Mito Meetup)** - [@India Rose Hopper](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29690/) makes it happen and is very nice, she also has rooms available place in shalpa.
>
> **Bitcoin farmers market** - every second sunday with nice jewelry and food.
>
> **EMF class** - Every thursday [@Olle Kitsing](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22800/) a building biologist organizes, i learned a lot.
>
> **Mito Haus activities** - cold plunging on the weekends and Mito TV where a kruse podcast is watched and discussed was on wednesdays. [@bennefit-coaching](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31032/) is in charge, can also rent there in Atami.
>
> **Sunrise at the beach** - In Atami i think is the largest community and I've met some great people and there was always someone at the beach. Shalpa also has some Mito's but not sure if there are other places like that.
>
>
> ### Bitcoin Activities​
>
> I was actually disappointed with that. Meetups werent that great and very little people.
>
> **Mi Primer Bitcoin** - in san salvador organizes some, also classes.
>
> Also can check here on [telegram](https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador).
>
> **The Bitcoin Hardware Store** - In El Zonte on mondays there is a class which i enjoyed. but i recommend checking beforehand if it happens.
>
>
> ### Nature Activities​
>
>
> **Hiking** - There is Tamanique waterfall hike which is nice, only 2 hours but is quite difficult. You get there and take a guide for a couple of dollars. Also hiking santa ana volcano pwople do, pay attention to get there early enough to catch a guide, we didn't make it on time.
> Overall I'd say not many hiking options you see online and i thought I'll do more waterfalls but the logistics were complicated for me, not knowing spanish and no online info about more hikes.
>
> **Nature Sightseeing** - if you arent much of a hiker, you can still enjoy views in lake cotopaque, we rented there a jetski half an hour cost 35 usd. Also in Usulutan (near san miguel) are great views like espiritu de la montana.
>
> **Surfing** - In the mito beaches you can't surf much because of rocks and the waves are too wild. You can bring your own board and drive to El Sunzal or stay at a hostel near.
>
>
> ### Learn About ES​
>
>
> We did a day in the city going to museums learning about ES civil war and mayaan culture which was nice.
> Chihuatan mayaan ruins was realy good we were alone there and got a guide all to ourselfs i highly recommend it. Guide is free and we tipped 5 usd.
>
>
>
> ## When to come?​
>
>
> ES has two seasons. wet season (May-October) and dry season (November-April)
>
> Dry season is also winter months in the northern hemisphere, so that time is more touristy and you'll find more people there.
>
> In rainy season it mostly rains at night. But still strong sun, tough can be some cloudy days.
>
> I'd recommend coming in tourist season for 1-2 weeks. Perhaps even extending from ES to Yucatan (tulum..) or taking a shuttle to Guatemala (only 5 hours).
> More than 2 weeks - there aren't many stuff to do here tourist wise.
>
>
>
> ## Where to come?​
>
>
> Some say the beach area is too hot for them, so they stay in the mountains where it is a bit colder. I will write about the main places in ES.
>
>
> ### El Tunco​
>
> Hippe touristy vibes. Good for surfing. Also parties there if anyone is interested.
>
>
> ### El Zonte (Bitcoin Beach)​
>
> Overall i didn't see much to do there so I don't recommend, though it's nice maybe having dinner there walking around.
>
>
> ### Atami​
>
> Here you will always find Mito’s at sunrise, and pretty close to everything. You can stay at Maria Therasa house or at Benne's or find airbnb/hotel. The beach here is not comfortable to go into because waves are realy strong.
>
>
> ### Shalpa​
>
> Less Mito's than Atami. And further away. But less EMF, beach is beautiful, and india hopper is realy nice.
>
>
> ### Xandau​
>
> I think Evelyn has a solo place there. I haven't been but some Mito's are there I understand.
>
>
> ### San Miguel​
>
> I haven't been there but it's seems nice calm beaches and great view.
>
>
> ### San Salvador​
>
> I wouldn't sleep in the city. Not more than one night at most. Isn't much to do there.
>
>
> ### Santa Ana​
>
> There is the volcano and lake. There was also some Mito event in hotel Campo Bello and it is beautiful there

>
> I stayed with Benne in Atami (mito haus / mansion / german haus) and liked all the activities there. It is not super low emf but quite good. Maria and India's are also recommended. Also [@SunHunter ☀️](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28398/) (Evelyn) has a place in xandau.
>

> **Logistics

You could also contact carlos which is a taxi driver that drove Mito's and knows english (+503 7775 7534). It is also common to hitchike, or you can take the buses but i haven't tried looked a bit complicated and not worth the wait - Uber works realy well.
>
> **Language** - Sometimes i had no wifi so i downloaded spanish-english translate beforehand on google but it did not translate well. Might be a good idea to learn some basics words.
>
>
> ## Further details​
>
> If anyone has more questions I'll be happy to answer.
> I was realy happy to get to know the Mito's. Thanks to everyone i met you were all very kind and i had a pleasure getting to know you all.
> Until next time !

This is a wonderful description to share with everyone!! Hope lots are looking at this. I stayed with India Hopper, and really connected with her. The beach in Shalpa is truly perfect. Nice winds, but not over-powering. Nice black sand. And she most certainly runs a hard-core mito house.
