>
>
> ES has two seasons. wet season (May-October) and dry season (November-April)
>
> Dry season is also winter months in the northern hemisphere, so that time is more touristy and you'll find more people there.
>
> In rainy season it mostly rains at night. But still strong sun, tough can be some cloudy days.
>
> I'd recommend coming in tourist season for 1-2 weeks. Perhaps even extending from ES to Yucatan (tulum..) or taking a shuttle to Guatemala (only 5 hours).
> More than 2 weeks - there aren't many stuff to do here tourist wise.
>
>
>
> ## Where to come?​
>
>
> Some say the beach area is too hot for them, so they stay in the mountains where it is a bit colder. I will write about the main places in ES.
>
>
> ### El Tunco​
>
> Hippe touristy vibes. Good for surfing. Also parties there if anyone is interested.
>
>
> ### El Zonte (Bitcoin Beach)​
>
> Overall i didn't see much to do there so I don't recommend, though it's nice maybe having dinner there walking around.
>
>
> ### Atami​
>
> Here you will always find Mito’s at sunrise, and pretty close to everything. You can stay at Maria Therasa house or at Benne's or find airbnb/hotel. The beach here is not comfortable to go into because waves are realy strong.
>
>
> ### Shalpa​
>
> Less Mito's than Atami. And further away. But less EMF, beach is beautiful, and india hopper is realy nice.
>
>
> ### Xandau​
>
> I think Evelyn has a solo place there. I haven't been but some Mito's are there I understand.
>
>
> ### San Miguel​
>
> I haven't been there but it's seems nice calm beaches and great view.
>
>
> ### San Salvador​
>
> I wouldn't sleep in the city. Not more than one night at most. Isn't much to do there.
>
>
> ### Santa Ana​
>
> There is the volcano and lake. There was also some Mito event in hotel Campo Bello and it is beautiful there
>
>
>
> ## Mito Housing Breakdown​
>
>
> I think someone should do a list with low EMF airbnbs. But i don't know of one so if that is important you must contact olle to check it first.
>
> I highly recommend staying at one of the mito houses - that way you can get to know people and they'll help you out with your travels.
>
> I stayed with Benne in Atami (mito haus / mansion / german haus) and liked all the activities there. It is not super low emf but quite good. Maria and India's are also recommended. Also [@SunHunter ☀️](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28398/) (Evelyn) has a place in xandau.
>
>
> **Logistics
>
> Money** - there are some ATM's that you can take out cash. costs 5$. not sure about the situation if bitcoin will not be legal tender anymore in ES, evan starbucks takes bitcoin.
>
> **Transportation** - driving in ES is not for the the faint of heart. no streat lights and sometimes there are slow trucks that slow down the traffic, or fog in the mountains, or bad roads. we rented a car 35$ from our house in Atami and had a great time (+503 7896 1373 Medrano's Rent a car). You could also contact carlos which is a taxi driver that drove Mito's and knows english (+503 7775 7534). It is also common to hitchike, or you can take the buses but i haven't tried looked a bit complicated and not worth the wait - Uber works realy well.
>
> **Language** - Sometimes i had no wifi so i downloaded spanish-english translate beforehand on google but it did not translate well. Might be a good idea to learn some basics words.
>
>
> ## Further details​
>
> If anyone has more questions I'll be happy to answer.
> I was realy happy to get to know the Mito's. Thanks to everyone i met you were all very kind and i had a pleasure getting to know you all.
> Until next time !

VERY helpful; THANK you for the time you invested to help others! (Maybe a dumb question: did you drink the water?)

### warriorT — 2025-05-15T17:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-352543>

> [India Rose Hopper said:](/goto/post?id=352366)
>
> You can use credit cards at most restaurants, stores, etc. it is good to have cash for Street vendors, Fruit trucks, fish market, etc.
> The super selectos grocery store takes bitcoin also.

I’ve not been to E.S yet but am coming from UK in October to find the right location for a permanent move, as UK no longer a viable place to be. Plan is to come initially up to 5 months then return to pack remaining belongings and close off life here in full. Am planning to bring my cat with me and just wondering how pet friendly rental accommodation is and if there are catteries for her whilst I return to uk for possibly a month to get my final exit - if anybody knows or has done similar would love to hear!

### A.Grande — 2025-05-15T17:56:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-352545>

You missed the Bitcoin Farmer's Market!... another great MIto location you missed was Brunate... It is a coffee plantation but is also an Airbnb. It has hiking trails, a pool that is cooled by mountain spring, an abundance of quiet and wildlife, a fire burning sauna and a nest in the trees that overlooks all three countries... ES, Guatamala and Nicaragua ( i think that's the third country). A great area to visit is San Benito with wonderful restaurants, an art museum, and plenty of shopping. I wish everyone could visit Brunate - its a special place with low EMF - check it out on instagram Brunate.sv

### Jacks — 2025-05-15T21:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-352564>

> [guy houri said:](/goto/post?id=352269)
>
> **El Salvador Mito Guide**​
>
>
>
> Hello everyone. In the last 2 weeks i stayed in El Salvador and thought i would share information for anyone else coming ahead.
>
> If anyone wants to reach out I'd be glad to help
> Guy
> +972544312639
> [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
>
>
> ## What to do?​
>
>
>
> ### Build Your Redox​
>
>
> **Sun** - Overall great sun. I sweated buckets there (deuterium?) Some places tough there were too many mosquitoes to the point it outweighed the sun for me (bad redox?) and drinking water not that good. But i got a great tan.
>
> **EMF** - not great in my opinion. No 5G but antennas everywhere so keep in mind you will be able to mitigate only in mito places. Also the wiring of the houses is bad and there can be problems with dirty fields from AC and other stuff. I also had static charge build up in my bed.
>
> **Food** - i liked it ton of seafood and great places to go to. Usually cost 10-15 usd for ceviche and 20 usd for massive portions.
>
>
> ### Meet fellow Mito's:​
>
>
> Most mito’s in ES i met are people that came there to heal, Id say an average age of about 40-50. And there are some families as well. Though i am 23 and was youngest there overall everyone i met was super nice and i had a great time learning and resonating with fellow Mito's.
>
> **MMM (Monthly Mito Meetup)** - [@India Rose Hopper](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29690/) makes it happen and is very nice, she also has rooms available place in shalpa.
>
> **Bitcoin farmers market** - every second sunday with nice jewelry and food.
>
> **EMF class** - Every thursday [@Olle Kitsing](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22800/) a building biologist organizes, i learned a lot.
>
> **Mito Haus activities** - cold plunging on the weekends and Mito TV where a kruse podcast is watched and discussed was on wednesdays. [@bennefit-coaching](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31032/) is in charge, can also rent there in Atami.
>
> **Sunrise at the beach** - In Atami i think is the largest community and I've met some great people and there was always someone at the beach. Shalpa also has some Mito's but not sure if there are other places like that.
>
>
> ### Bitcoin Activities​
>
> I was actually disappointed with that. Meetups werent that great and very little people.
>
> **Mi Primer Bitcoin** - in san salvador organizes some, also classes.
>
> Also can check here on [telegram](https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador).
>
> **The Bitcoin Hardware Store** - In El Zonte on mondays there is a class which i enjoyed. but i recommend checking beforehand if it happens.
>
>
> ### Nature Activities​
>
>
> **Hiking** - There is Tamanique waterfall hike which is nice, only 2 hours but is quite difficult. You get there and take a guide for a couple of dollars. Also hiking santa ana volcano pwople do, pay attention to get there early enough to catch a guide, we didn't make it on time.
> Overall I'd say not many hiking options you see online and i thought I'll do more waterfalls but the logistics were complicated for me, not knowing spanish and no online info about more hikes.
>
> **Nature Sightseeing** - if you arent much of a hiker, you can still enjoy views in lake cotopaque, we rented there a jetski half an hour cost 35 usd. Also in Usulutan (near san miguel) are great views like espiritu de la montana.
>
> **Surfing** - In the mito beaches you can't surf much because of rocks and the waves are too wild. You can bring your own board and drive to El Sunzal or stay at a hostel near.
>
>
> ### Learn About ES​
>
>
> We did a day in the city going to museums learning about ES civil war and mayaan culture which was nice.
> Chihuatan mayaan ruins was realy good we were alone there and got a guide all to ourselfs i highly recommend it. Guide is free and we tipped 5 usd.
>
>
>
> ## When to come?​
>
>
> ES has two seasons. wet season (May-October) and dry season (November-April)
>
> Dry season is also winter months in the northern hemisphere, so that time is more touristy and you'll find more people there.
>
> In rainy season it mostly rains at night. But still strong sun, tough can be some cloudy days.
>
> I'd recommend coming in tourist season for 1-2 weeks. Perhaps even extending from ES to Yucatan (tulum..) or taking a shuttle to Guatemala (only 5 hours).
> More than 2 weeks - there aren't many stuff to do here tourist wise.
>
>
>
> ## Where to come?​
>
>
> Some say the beach area is too hot for them, so they stay in the mountains where it is a bit colder. I will write about the main places in ES.
>
>
> ### El Tunco​
>
> Hippe touristy vibes. Good for surfing. Also parties there if anyone is interested.
>
>
> ### El Zonte (Bitcoin Beach)​
>
> Overall i didn't see much to do there so I don't recommend, though it's nice maybe having dinner there walking around.
>
>
> ### Atami​
>
> Here you will always find Mito’s at sunrise, and pretty close to everything. You can stay at Maria Therasa house or at Benne's or find airbnb/hotel. The beach here is not comfortable to go into because waves are realy strong.
>
>
> ### Shalpa​
>
> Less Mito's than Atami. And further away. But less EMF, beach is beautiful, and india hopper is realy nice.
>
>
> ### Xandau​
>
> I think Evelyn has a solo place there. I haven't been but some Mito's are there I understand.
>
>
> ### San Miguel​
>
> I haven't been there but it's seems nice calm beaches and great view.
>
>
> ### San Salvador​
>
> I wouldn't sleep in the city. Not more than one night at most. Isn't much to do there.
>
>
> ### Santa Ana​
>
> There is the volcano and lake. There was also some Mito event in hotel Campo Bello and it is beautiful there
>
>
>
> ## Mito Housing Breakdown​
>
>
> I think someone should do a list with low EMF airbnbs. But i don't know of one so if that is important you must contact olle to check it first.
>
> I highly recommend staying at one of the mito houses - that way you can get to know people and they'll help you out with your travels.
>
> I stayed with Benne in Atami (mito haus / mansion / german haus) and liked all the activities there. It is not super low emf but quite good. Maria and India's are also recommended. Also [@SunHunter ☀️](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28398/) (Evelyn) has a place in xandau.
>
>
> **Logistics
>
> Money** - there are some ATM's that you can take out cash. costs 5$. not sure about the situation if bitcoin will not be legal tender anymore in ES, evan starbucks takes bitcoin.
>
> **Transportation** - driving in ES is not for the the faint of heart. no streat lights and sometimes there are slow trucks that slow down the traffic, or fog in the mountains, or bad roads. we rented a car 35$ from our house in Atami and had a great time (+503 7896 1373 Medrano's Rent a car). You could also contact carlos which is a taxi driver that drove Mito's and knows english (+503 7775 7534). It is also common to hitchike, or you can take the buses but i haven't tried looked a bit complicated and not worth the wait - Uber works realy well.
>
> **Language** - Sometimes i had no wifi so i downloaded spanish-english translate beforehand on google but it did not translate well. Might be a good idea to learn some basics words.
>
>
> ## Further details​
>
> If anyone has more questions I'll be happy to answer.
> I was realy happy to get to know the Mito's. Thanks to everyone i met you were all very kind and i had a pleasure getting to know you all.
> Until next time !

Yes, this guide and this thread are an excellent place to start. Thanks to all contributors. Will be planning a trip next winter for a couple weeks to check things out, so will be looking for a place to rent for 4 at some point.

I'll be taking over the mito house in Xanadu from July 1st. I'll confirm that on June 19th so if anyone does need room(s). Let me know.

From the 1st of July

Major Bedroom with Large verandah balcony with privacy to sunbathe naked $1450 + 3rd of electricity bill. Features

Walk-in Wardrobe& en suite. Bathroom and large Bed. Plenty of space. Could do a mito couple. You'd get your privacy

Bedroom next to kitchen features ensuite and space for a small desk plus wardrobe

$1225 + 3rd of Electricity bill
Single person only

Bedroom next to pool smaller

also has Ensuite

Not much room to work but has a wardrobe and ensuite as standard

$1015 per month. + 3rd of electricity bill
Single person only

If I'm there you'll all pay less representatively for the electric
bill .
Because I take the studio above and the outside kitchen and if course I'll take some electricity to use

Drinking Water delivery. That will be split for the bill

Do not use tap water internally. Non potable all over El Salvador except for certain places

Kitchen very functional and up to USA standards if a little smaller

The house is grounded and Olle has been around we will do a follow up when I move back in to make sure everything is tip top

Cleaner. We can split the costs so that will work out exceedingly economic fir all of us. None of us want to waste our time cleaning the house.

If you're driving you will need a 4x4 to get up the drive

Two wheel drive can do it if you know how to approach a 43 degree drive with a hairpin bend half way up but you really need experience in doing that

I will of course have a car but several deliveries can be organised.

The beach is sandy and soft. No real rocks except for certain times of the year

All prices subject to change with dollar printing etc ,I will of course accept bitcoin and give slight discount for that

### warriorT — 2025-05-28T15:47:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-353451>

Hey friends, for everyone in El Salvador or having visited, what are the things that you wish you’d brought with you because really difficult to get hold of there?

### India Rose Hopper — 2025-05-31T01:30:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-353615>

You’ll want to bring all your “Mito” stuff like blue-blocking glasses, red light lamps, Earthrunners or grounding sandals, Kiniki or sun through bathing suits etc. you can get Ethernet cables and USB-C to Ethernet and USB to Ethernet but not lightning. It’s easier to just come with them
Bring all your self care products such as toothpaste, shampoo, deodorant etc. as you probably won’t find them here if they are natural.
Also, any particular spices you like to use.
Natural peanut butter (it’s hard to find and some people really miss it)
Turkish cotton towels are good cause they dry fast.
Most come with only 2 swim suits…big mistake! It’s practically all we wear here
Anyway, I’m sure there’s more but this will get you started

### Jana C — 2025-05-31T01:45:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-353616>

> Bring all your self care products such as toothpaste, shampoo, deodorant etc. as you probably won’t find them here if they are natural.
> Also, any particular spices you like to use.
> Natural peanut butter (it’s hard to find and some people really miss it)
> Turkish cotton towels are good cause they dry fast.
> Most come with only 2 swim suits…big mistake! It’s practically all we wear here
> Anyway, I’m sure there’s more but this will get you started

Yes, perfect comprehensive list, Inda Hopper! Miss you!!

### India Rose Hopper — 2025-06-13T20:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-354602>

> [PIAZ said:](/goto/post?id=352541)
>
> VERY helpful; THANK you for the time you invested to help others! (Maybe a dumb question: did you drink the water?)

We don’t drink the water here but there are good sources of spring water to choose from. I use Alpina. There is also Cumbre and, of course, you can find Evian etc

### warriorT — 2025-06-14T09:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-354625>

> [India Rose Hopper said:](/goto/post?id=354602)
>
> We don’t drink the water here but there are good sources of spring water to choose from. I use Alpina. There is also Cumbre and, of course, you can find Evian etc

Is it widely available in glass bottles?

### Justin Goldberg — 2026-03-27T07:48:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-travel-journal.30874/post-369735>

> [Guy Houri said:](/goto/post?id=352269)
>
> **El Salvador Mito Guide**​
>
>
>
> Hello everyone. In the last 2 weeks i stayed in El Salvador and thought i would share information for anyone else coming ahead.
>
> If anyone wants to reach out I'd be glad to help
> Guy
> +972544312639
> [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
>
>
> ## What to do?​
>
>
>
> ### Build Your Redox​
>
>
> **Sun** - Overall great sun. I sweated buckets there (deuterium?) Some places tough there were too many mosquitoes to the point it outweighed the sun for me (bad redox?) and drinking water not that good. But i got a great tan.
>
> **EMF** - not great in my opinion. No 5G but antennas everywhere so keep in mind you will be able to mitigate only in mito places. Also the wiring of the houses is bad and there can be problems with dirty fields from AC and other stuff. I also had static charge build up in my bed.
>
> **Food** - i liked it ton of seafood and great places to go to. Usually cost 10-15 usd for ceviche and 20 usd for massive portions.
>
>
> ### Meet fellow Mito's:​
>
>
> Most mito’s in ES i met are people that came there to heal, Id say an average age of about 40-50. And there are some families as well. Though i am 23 and was youngest there overall everyone i met was super nice and i had a great time learning and resonating with fellow Mito's.
>
> **MMM (Monthly Mito Meetup)** - [@India Rose Hopper](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29690/) makes it happen and is very nice, she also has rooms available place in shalpa.
>
> **Bitcoin farmers market** - every second sunday with nice jewelry and food.
>
> **EMF class** - Every thursday [@Olle Kitsing](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22800/) a building biologist organizes, i learned a lot.
>
> **Mito Haus activities** - cold plunging on the weekends and Mito TV where a kruse podcast is watched and discussed was on wednesdays. [@bennefit-coaching](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31032/) is in charge, can also rent there in Atami.
>
> **Sunrise at the beach** - In Atami i think is the largest community and I've met some great people and there was always someone at the beach. Shalpa also has some Mito's but not sure if there are other places like that.
>
>
> ### Bitcoin Activities​
>
> I was actually disappointed with that. Meetups werent that great and very little people.
>
> **Mi Primer Bitcoin** - in san salvador organizes some, also classes.
>
> Also can check here on [telegram](https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador).
>
> **The Bitcoin Hardware Store** - In El Zonte on mondays there is a class which i enjoyed. but i recommend checking beforehand if it happens.
>
> **Bitcoin Farmers Market** - In El Zonte on every other sunday - homemade brownies with no seed oil , soaps, jewleries, foods and drinnks and most importantly bitcoiners, what more can someone want ?
>
>
> ### Nature Activities​
>
>
> **Hiking** - There is Tamanique waterfall hike which is nice, only 2 hours but is quite difficult. You get there and take a guide for a couple of dollars. Also hiking santa ana volcano pwople do, pay attention to get there early enough to catch a guide, we didn't make it on time.
> Overall I'd say not many hiking options you see online and i thought I'll do more waterfalls but the logistics were complicated for me, not knowing spanish and no online info about more hikes.
>
> **Nature Sightseeing** - if you arent much of a hiker, you can still enjoy views in lake cotopaque, we rented there a jetski half an hour cost 35 usd. Also in Usulutan (near san miguel) are great views like espiritu de la montana.
>
> **Surfing** - In the mito beaches you can't surf much because of rocks and the waves are too wild. You can bring your own board and drive to El Sunzal or stay at a hostel near.
>
>
> ### Learn About ES​
>
>
> We did a day in the city going to museums learning about ES civil war and mayaan culture which was nice.
> Chihuatan mayaan ruins was realy good we were alone there and got a guide all to ourselfs i highly recommend it. Guide is free and we tipped 5 usd.
>
>
>
> ## When to come?​
>
>
> ES has two seasons. wet season (May-October) and dry season (November-April)
>
> Dry season is also winter months in the northern hemisphere, so that time is more touristy and you'll find more people there.
>
> In rainy season it mostly rains at night. But still strong sun, tough can be some cloudy days.
>
> I'd recommend coming in tourist season for 1-2 weeks. Perhaps even extending from ES to Yucatan (tulum..) or taking a shuttle to Guatemala (only 5 hours).
> More than 2 weeks - there aren't many stuff to do here tourist wise.
>
>
>
> ## Where to come?​
>
>
> Some say the beach area is too hot for them, so they stay in the mountains where it is a bit colder. I will write about the main places in ES.
>
>
> ### El Tunco​
>
> Hippe touristy vibes. Good for surfing. Also parties there if anyone is interested.
>
>
> ### El Zonte (Bitcoin Beach)​
>
> Overall i didn't see much to do there so I don't recommend, though it's nice maybe having dinner there walking around.
>
>
> ### Atami​
>
> Here you will always find Mito’s at sunrise, and pretty close to everything. You can stay at Maria Therasa house or at Benne's or find airbnb/hotel. The beach here is not comfortable to go into because waves are realy strong.
>
>
> ### Shalpa​
>
> Less Mito's than Atami. And further away. But less EMF, beach is beautiful, and india hopper is realy nice.
>
>
> ### Xandau​
>
> I think Evelyn has a solo place there. I haven't been but some Mito's are there I understand.
>
>
> ### San Miguel​
>
> I haven't been there but it's seems nice calm beaches and great view.
>
>
> ### San Salvador​
>
> I wouldn't sleep in the city. Not more than one night at most. Isn't much to do there.
>
>
> ### Santa Ana​
>
> There is the volcano and lake. There was also some Mito event in hotel Campo Bello and it is beautiful there
>
>
>
> ## Mito Housing Breakdown​
>
>
> I think someone should do a list with low EMF airbnbs. But i don't know of one so if that is important you must contact olle to check it first.
>
> I highly recommend staying at one of the mito houses - that way you can get to know people and they'll help you out with your travels.
>
> I stayed with Benne in Atami (mito haus / mansion / german haus) and liked all the activities there. It is not super low emf but quite good. Maria and India's are also recommended. Also [@SunHunter ☀️](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28398/) (Evelyn) has a place in xandau.
>
>
> **Logistics
>
> Money** - there are some ATM's that you can take out cash. costs 5$. not sure about the situation if bitcoin will not be legal tender anymore in ES, evan starbucks takes bitcoin.
>
> **Transportation** - driving in ES is not for the the faint of heart. no streat lights and sometimes there are slow trucks that slow down the traffic, or fog in the mountains, or bad roads. we rented a car 35$ from our house in Atami and had a great time (+503 7896 1373 Medrano's Rent a car). You could also contact carlos which is a taxi driver that drove Mito's and knows english (+503 7775 7534). It is also common to hitchike, or you can take the buses but i haven't tried looked a bit complicated and not worth the wait - Uber works realy well.
>
> **Language** - Sometimes i had no wifi so i downloaded spanish-english translate beforehand on google but it did not translate well. Might be a good idea to learn some basics words.
>
>
> ## Further details​
>
> If anyone has more questions I'll be happy to answer.
> I was realy happy to get to know the Mito's. Thanks to everyone i met you were all very kind and i had a pleasure getting to know you all.
> Until next time !

No sunbathing anywhere !

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-experienced-using-earthing-shields-and-this-new-5g-meter.30882/post-352307>

Hi,

1\. I came across an earthing shield you can use beneath/up your mattresses or, for example, beneath your desk. Does anyone have experience with those things?

For your bed:

### [Aarding Slaapmat, 2 pers., 136 x 180cm (incl. kabel 5m en aardingstekker)](https://aarding.org/nl/collections/grounding-mats/products/earthing-sleep-mat-double)

De Aardingsslaapmatten, 2 personen, 136 x 180cm zijn gebaseerd op geleidende koolstof in plaats van zilvergaren, zodat ze na verloop van tijd niet oxideren. Je gebruikt ze onder je eigen reguliere hoeslaken. Aardingsslaapmatten zijn verkrijgbaar in voordelige sets, samen met Aardingskussenslopen.

 aarding.org

For your desk:

### [Set 2 Aardingsmatten Carbonleder 26 x 68cm (incl. kabels en adapters)](https://aarding.org/nl/collections/grounding-mats/products/set-2-grounding-mats-black-25-x-68cm-incl-cables-and-adapters)

Beschrijving Set van 2 hoogwaardige aardingsmatten van geleidend, vegan-friendly carbonleder (26 x 68cm).Ideaal voor contact met de pols (maar ook als muismat of bureaumat) tijdens het computeren, lezen of knutselen of om er met blote voeten op te zitten. Zo profiteer je niet alleen overdag van...

 aarding.org

I also know the Magentico Sleep Pads, but I was wondering if these do the same trick.

2\. Last but not least, has anyone used this new model of Safe And Sound Pro (5G Meter), or does any cheaper model also do the trick? (So I can invest more in Bitcoin hehe)

### [Safe and Sound Pro mmWave Meter](https://safelivingtechnologies.com/products/safe-and-sound-pro-mmwave-meter.html)

New Safe and Sound Pro mmWave Meter is North America’s first-ever, consumer-level, high band detector! Professionally accurate and affordable, Safe and Sound Pro mmWave Meter measures 5G from 24 GHz to 40 GHz with a flat frequency response.

 safelivingtechnologies.com

Thanks,
Pieter

# Thread: Need some emergency advice for momma via cipro
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tyler Hanson — 2025-05-12T19:23:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352382>

Hey yall

My momma has a freak strange wound in her vagina that has an E. coli infection and a small amount of necrotic tissue.

They are firmly recommending cipro and we are putting our feet down. What is a different option for that purpose? She has allergy to Doxycycline.

Thanks, much appreciated.

### Kirsten F — 2025-05-12T20:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352387>

Hi Tyler,

I previously worked as a (centralized) urologist and treated Fournier’s gangrene - necrotizing infection of the scrotum - with surgical debridement and antibiotics. It sounds like your mom’s infection could turn serious, so I encourage broad spectrum antibiotics until the sensitivity results come back from her culture. Please make sure her wound is being reassessed often - if it looks worse instead of better, or her pain is worsening, she might need surgical debridement. If the docs are unsure of the amount of necrosis, a CT can be done (to rule out signs of air bubbles along fascial planes. Air bubbles = infection).

Non cipro options to discuss with the docs. They are IV:
\- piptazo
\- ertapenem/meropenem
\- ceftriaxone

These cases are usually best discussed with an infectious disease doctor, who can advise on the best drug choice based on the antibiotic resistance patterns in her community. Step down to oral antibiotics can happen once she is improving and her sensitivity results are known.

Good luck!

### Tyler Hanson — 2025-05-12T21:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352389>

> [Kirsten F said:](/goto/post?id=352387)
>
> Hi Tyler,
>
> I previously worked as a (centralized) urologist and treated Fournier’s gangrene - necrotizing infection of the scrotum - with surgical debridement and antibiotics. It sounds like your mom’s infection could turn serious, so I encourage broad spectrum antibiotics until the sensitivity results come back from her culture. Please make sure her wound is being reassessed often - if it looks worse instead of better, or her pain is worsening, she might need surgical debridement. If the docs are unsure of the amount of necrosis, a CT can be done (to rule out signs of air bubbles along fascial planes. Air bubbles = infection).
>
> Non cipro options to discuss with the docs. They are IV:
> \- piptazo
> \- ertapenem/meropenem
> \- ceftriaxone
>
> These cases are usually best discussed with an infectious disease doctor, who can advise on the best drug choice based on the antibiotic resistance patterns in her community. Step down to oral antibiotics can happen once she is improving and her sensitivity results are known.
>
> Good luck!

Kristen,

Thanks for the response, I really appreciate it. It's very helpful. I know very little about these particular subjects, and I'm just trying hard to make sure my mother doesn't have to take a flox antibiotic. They are pushing back hard on this.

Is the reason cipro is being pushed just because it's such a powerful broad spectrum?

And these other options you've listed, they're IV only yeah? Is there a cipro alternative thats not IV if for some reason the doctor has an issue with this? I'm guessing the IV antibiotics are just more effective right? The other side of this is it's time consuming to go back and forth, and I know these things are time sensitive. I would hate to have her wait to a harmful degree in the attempts to avoid cipro, but it just seems like cipro is not an option...

Thanks again!
Tyler

### Kirsten F — 2025-05-12T22:08:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352391>

> [Tyler Hanson said:](/goto/post?id=352389)
>
> Kristen,
>
> Thanks for the response, I really appreciate it. It's very helpful. I know very little about these particular subjects, and I'm just trying hard to make sure my mother doesn't have to take a flox antibiotic. They are pushing back hard on this.
>
> Is the reason cipro is being pushed just because it's such a powerful broad spectrum?
>
> And these other options you've listed, they're IV only yeah? Is there a cipro alternative thats not IV if for some reason the doctor has an issue with this? I'm guessing the IV antibiotics are just more effective right? The other side of this is it's time consuming to go back and forth, and I know these things are time sensitive. I would hate to have her wait to a harmful degree in the attempts to avoid cipro, but it just seems like cipro is not an option...
>
> Thanks again!
> Tyler

Hi Tyler,

It would have to do with the drug’s known activity against e.coli, and the degree of tissue penetration. Cipro is good against many E. coli (unless resistant) and has good bioavailability orally - equivalent to IV. Cefixime is an option for oral third generation cephalosporins coverage but the IV cephalosporins (like ceftriaxone) are better.

If they are worried about anaerobes, flagyl orally could be added too.

Essentially, choice comes down to severity of the infection, overall status/health of the patient, how well the drug usually covers for E. coli usually (and how common resistance is), and how well the drug penetrates into the tissues IV vs orally.

### Tyler Hanson — 2025-05-12T22:34:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352394>

> [Kirsten F said:](/goto/post?id=352391)
>
> Hi Tyler,
>
> It would have to do with the drug’s known activity against e.coli, and the degree of tissue penetration. Cipro is good against many E. coli (unless resistant) and has good bioavailability orally - equivalent to IV. Cefixime is an option for oral third generation cephalosporins coverage but the IV cephalosporins (like ceftriaxone) are better.
>
> If they are worried about anaerobes, flagyl orally could be added too.
>
> Essentially, choice comes down to severity of the infection, overall status/health of the patient, how well the drug usually covers for E. coli usually (and how common resistance is), and how well the drug penetrates into the tissues IV vs orally.

Ahh, yes I understand. Makes sense. Lot going on.

In this particular situation do you think I’m right for trying hard to avoid cipro?

Tyler

### AlbertD — 2025-05-12T23:14:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352396>

> [Tyler Hanson said:](/goto/post?id=352394)
>
> Ahh, yes I understand. Makes sense. Lot going on.
>
> In this particular situation do you think I’m right for trying hard to avoid cipro?
>
> Tyler

It is hard to say given we don't have all the info. I think this is ultimately a situation you/your mom need to decide. As you are likely aware, the concern with cipro is that it is a part of a class of antibiotics called fluoroquinolones. "Fluoro" meaning it contains fluorine (part of halogen group) of which we have all learned in this community. Best to ask the Infectious disease docs if there are other gram negative options (like E. Coli) available. No antibiotic is ideal. If it were my mom, I'd prefer her not give cipro if I can help it. Good luck!

### Kirsten F — 2025-05-12T23:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352398>

Agreed with Albert. We don’t have all the info. Other options for antibiotics aren’t necessarily bad but there are cost and convenience considerations (with the IV route). The ID docs can help with the best alternatives.

### Tyler Hanson — 2025-05-12T23:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352401>

Thank you both for your input!

She has her re-check tomorrow to see if it's progressing or not. She is with gynecology not an ID doc. Depending on how tomorrow goes we will reassess if she stays or goes elsewhere.

She has been on the phone all day with her current nurses and doc. She is only willing to prescribe Cipro or Levo unfortunately. Meanwhile my mothers symptoms are getting worse. Its tough, the issue progressing may be worse than taking one of these.

We're going to make another stand for an IV non floxin, but if we can't get any traction we may have to start on one of these and switch once we can get to a different doc. They sure don't make this easy. This just doesn't seem like an issue to wait around on.

I wonder if going to the emergency room to get a different doc would lead to an easier outcome with our choice of antibiotics?

### AlbertD — 2025-05-13T00:21:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352402>

> [Tyler Hanson said:](/goto/post?id=352401)
>
> Thank you both for your input!
>
> She has her re-check tomorrow to see if it's progressing or not. She is with gynecology not an ID doc. Depending on how tomorrow goes we will reassess if she stays or goes elsewhere.
>
> She has been on the phone all day with her current nurses and doc. She is only willing to prescribe Cipro or Levo unfortunately. Meanwhile my mothers symptoms are getting worse. Its tough, the issue progressing may be worse than taking one of these.
>
> We're going to make another stand for an IV non floxin, but if we can't get any traction we may have to start on one of these and switch once we can get to a different doc. They sure don't make this easy. This just doesn't seem like an issue to wait around on.
>
> I wonder if going to the emergency room to get a different doc would lead to an easier outcome with our choice of antibiotics?

If I'm reading between the lines, it sounds like you are speaking to the gynaecologist in an outpatient environment? Which might explain her hesitancy to only prescribe FQs (generally prescribed outpatient). The options suggested by Kirsten are usually given in an inpatient (hospital) environment. Usually outpatient docs don't have the capability to administer IV antibiotics. So to answer you question, yes. it might be an easier choice.

### AlbertD — 2025-05-13T00:39:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352403>

You could ask them if they could refer your mom to an outpatient facility that specializes in administering IV abx in your area. Might be cheaper than a hospital stay. Typically getting IV abx at the hospital usually means getting admitted (to monitor infection progress/side effects -- some of them can be rough on the kidneys). All good questions to ask the dr.

I'll just add one last caveat. Infections are a race against time. If there's necrosis, I am concerned about the infection getting worse. Necrosis is serious! Consider going to the ER.

### Kirsten F — 2025-05-13T01:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352404>

Yes. Necrosis is quite serious, which is why I suggested some strong antibiotics. I had assumed you were in a hospital. Good luck.

### Saichi — 2025-05-13T02:33:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352408>

If I were your mother I wouldn't take any antibiotics but spend at least two hours naked in the sun below 10 µw every day, depending on your latitude one hour late morning and one hour mid afternoon. Of course as Jack says sunrise light in eyes as well.

Mitigating nnemf to building biology standards in living and sleeping spaces is what everyone on this forum should do first, but critically important after any health problems arise.

If your mother has had any vaccines in the past seven years I'd recommend oral and/or IV chelation.

### Chlo_e — 2025-05-13T15:11:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352430>

Background in internal medicine here- lots of good suggestions above. If going down the antibiotic route, I echo the idea of asking about an Ambulatory Care Unit or other intermediate day unit where outpatients can receive IV meds and regular clinical review- not sure where you're based but we have them in more or less every hospital in the UK.

On a separate but related note- has she considered going on a temporary zero carb diet for several weeks? I have seen mundane and unusual infections resolve quickly in such instances with carb removal, in diabetics and non-diabetics- food for thought.

I'm not sure whether douching with something gentle like sterile normal saline might be helpful- perhaps discuss with treating team (some women use sodium bicarb but that is more for external relief of lichen sclerosis etc rather than internal bacterial infection).

Wishing you and your mum the very best.

### Wild Side Blue — 2025-05-13T21:03:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352448>

> [Tyler Hanson said:](/goto/post?id=352382)
>
> Hey yall
>
> My momma has a freak strange wound in her vagina that has an E. coli infection and a small amount of necrotic tissue.
>
> They are firmly recommending cipro and we are putting our feet down. What is a different option for that purpose? She has allergy to Doxycycline.
>
> Thanks, much appreciated.

I’m giving you my input. Having EDS chiro is a hell no. It falls in the Fluoroquinolones category (Has Fluoride). Cipro is known for tendon ruptures. Easy search on Pub med for side effects of Cipro.

### Tyler Hanson — 2025-05-15T06:57:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-emergency-advice-for-momma-via-cipro.30889/post-352527>

Thank you everybody. Mom’s checkup showed remarkable improvements, and I convinced her not to take the meds. Really appreciate the support and comments.

# Thread: Gauss Reading in El Salvador
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauss-reading-in-el-salvador.30919/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alain Dwight — 2025-05-16T17:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauss-reading-in-el-salvador.30919/post-352621>

Anyone taken a gauss reading down here, what were the results?

I tried with my Cornet and only got 0.2 µT which I think comes out to 0.002 Gauss, less than the Earth's magnetic field, I'm assuming it's designed to pick up magnetic fields off AC rather than the Earth's DC field

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-16T19:14:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauss-reading-in-el-salvador.30919/post-352634>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=352621)
>
> Anyone taken a gauss reading down here, what were the results?
>
> I tried with my Cornet and only got 0.2 µT which I think comes out to 0.002 Gauss, less than the Earth's magnetic field, I'm assuming it's designed to pick up magnetic fields off AC rather than the Earth's DC field

“Down here” is a big place.
Every location, street, beach, house, room will be different.
I read 8mG+/- walking, even driving down the street under power lines.
Earths magnetic field is natural DC, power lines in ES put out an 60Hz AC magnetic field.
If the wiring is good in a house the magnetic field should be below 0.2 mG (0.02µT).
A continuous prolonged 4+ mG field is associated with Leukemia in children.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-05-16T19:39:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauss-reading-in-el-salvador.30919/post-352635>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=352634)
>
> “Down here” is a big place.
> Every location, street, beach, house, room will be different.
> I read 8mG+/- walking, even driving down the street under power lines.
> Earths magnetic field is natural DC, power lines in ES put out an 60Hz AC magnetic field.
> If the wiring is good in a house the magnetic field should be below 0.2 mG (0.02µT).
> A continuous prolonged 4+ mG field is associated with Leukemia in children.

Interesting! What I'm trying to do is get a reading of how much the volcanic properties of El Salvador increase the DC magnetic field of the Earth, if at all, like Earth is normally 0.5 gauss at the surface, but is it more in El Salvador?

### Jacks — 2025-05-16T20:48:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauss-reading-in-el-salvador.30919/post-352652>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=352635)
>
> Interesting! What I'm trying to do is get a reading of how much the volcanic properties of El Salvador increase the DC magnetic field of the Earth, if at all, like Earth is normally 0.5 gauss at the surface, but is it more in El Salvador?

This is all I found on magnetic anomaly maps of north and central america but does not cover El Salvador, but you might be able to infer from areas to the north that are similar geologically. Red is a positive anomaly - so greater than "average" magnetic field. As you can see the magnetic field varies significantly from place to place.

### [North American Magnetic Anomaly Map](https://mrdata.usgs.gov/magnetic/map-us.html)

mrdata.usgs.gov

However, looks like a magnetic survey was done for the southeast side of ES ([https://getech.maps.arcgis.com/apps...deb775a74d03ab9992a9d410032d&find=El-Salvador](https://getech.maps.arcgis.com/apps/webappviewer/index.html?id=85abdeb775a74d03ab9992a9d410032d&find=El-Salvador) - site shows the extent of the survey) but I could not find the actual map of the magnetic anomalies for this area.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-17T11:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauss-reading-in-el-salvador.30919/post-352706>

You need a low range high resolution "DC" gauss meter to measure the earth mag field

### Jacks — 2025-05-17T17:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/microseism.30925/post-352735>

### "Microseisms in Global Seismology​

In [seismology](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Seismology), a **microseism** is defined as a faint [earth tremor](https://wiki.seg.org/w/index.php?title=Earth_tremor&action=edit&redlink=1) caused by natural phenomena.[\[1\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-1)[\[2\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-2) The term is most commonly used to refer to the dominant background seismic noise signal on Earth, which are mostly composed of [Rayleigh waves](https://wiki.seg.org/w/index.php?title=Rayleigh_waves&action=edit&redlink=1) and caused by water waves in the oceans and lakes.[\[3\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-LonguetHiggins-3)[\[4\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-Hasselmann-4)[\[5\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-Kedar-5)[\[6\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-Ardhuin-6) Thus a microseism is a small and long-continuing [oscillation](https://wiki.seg.org/w/index.php?title=Oscillation&action=edit&redlink=1) of the ground.[\[7\]](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic#cite_note-7)"

### [Loading…](https://wiki.seg.org/wiki/Microseismic)

wiki.seg.org

Usually detected on broadband seismometers - I've observed them.

What plants have given you the most success in shielding or lowering EMF/RF in your home? I'm looking for both indoor and outdoor plants that can survive in a hot/dry climate.

A few sites I found so far:

### [Top 10 EMF Plants that Absorb Radiation — EMF Safe Family](https://emfsafefamily.com/emf-plants/)

Discover 10 of the top EMF plants and how to use them to reduce your radiation exposure and add to the atmosphere of your home or office.

 www.orgoneenergy.org

### [Indoor Plants That Absorb Radiation: 10 Plants You Should Have In Your Home - EMF Protection Care](https://emfprotectioncare.com/plants-absorb-emf/)

Plants absorb EMF radiation and clean the room of toxins. EMF control using plants has been overlooked for some time. Here are the best 10.

 emfprotectioncare.com

Has anyone had success with types of Ivy or other vine type plants that can grow up/cover an entire wall? Are there any potential downsides?

### Mr.AJ — 2025-05-17T22:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-plants-for-emf-sheilding.30930/post-352768>

### [Star Note #515 GROWING VINES IN THE DESERT | Star Nursery Garden and Rock Centers](https://www.starnursery.com/star-notes/515-growing-vines-in-the-desert/)

Some provide bright color in hot areas, others cover bare walls and fences, dress up posts and columns or make nice shady area ground covers.

 www.starnursery.com

Here are a list of vines that might work well for me, but I'm unsure how well they reduce RF

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-18T03:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-plants-for-emf-sheilding.30930/post-352790>

The best way to reduce nnEMF in your home is to turn off the source - WiFi/bluetooth, all “smart” devices etc. and go hard wire ethernet. The 60Hz (50Hz) from the wires in the walls, floor and ceiling is, for most people, the #1 source of EMF in the home. Turn off bedroom circuits at night and unplug every appliance (coffee pot, toaster, light etc) when not in use. IF the source of RF is from the outside, shielding can help BUT the more you shield IF you still use a cell phone the more it will power up to make the connection to the nearest tower. Your exposure from the phone in your hand is far greater that the cell tower down the road.

All plants/trees block higher 5G frequencies which is why they are cutting down the trees along the roads.

nnEMF:
Blue light
Electric fields (includes “DE”)
Magnetic fields
Radio frequency (RF)
Each is VERY different from the other. Each requires a different measuring device.
There is no reason to be getting any “RF” from solar.
IF your RF readings are primarily coming from cell towers around you and/or your neighbors WiFi routers, when everyone goes to bed the readings will be lower.

Can anyone recommend a tent or blanket setup that will keep you grounded while sleeping. Lots of rocks and scorpions where I'm at so sleeping on the ground is not very pleasant. And AFAIK polyester/ fake fabrics are mostly ungrounding you.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-20T02:19:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-blankets-tents-for-camping.30950/post-352959>

Most tents these days have a built in plastic floor to keep you dry and the critters out. The ONLY time I would recommend a grounding pad is in a tent outside and use a SHORT ground wire WITHOUT a resistor built in and a ground spike right outside the tent.

### MattB99 — 2026-03-21T20:38:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-blankets-tents-for-camping.30950/post-369327>

# Thread: Anyone Bought “Photography” Incandescents Lately? BulbAmerica or Better Sources?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bought-%E2%80%9Cphotography%E2%80%9D-incandescents-lately-bulbamerica-or-better-sources.30951/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-05-20T01:47:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bought-%E2%80%9Cphotography%E2%80%9D-incandescents-lately-bulbamerica-or-better-sources.30951/post-352953>

A while back, I swapped out all the incandescent bulbs in my house—not because of regulations, but mostly for convenience. I liked being able to control lights with my phone, change colors, or just tell a smart speaker to turn things on and off. It felt like a quality-of-life upgrade at the time.

But lately I’ve been rethinking it.

The more I learn about how light affects our biology—especially stuff around circadian rhythms and artificial light hijacking natural cycles—the more I wonder if we traded something important for tech convenience. The vibe of incandescents, the warmth, the simplicity… maybe that mattered more than I thought.

I found a site called **BulbAmerica** that sells incandescent bulbs for photography or “rough service” use—apparently still legal under those labels. Has anyone tried them? Are they comparable to the old-school bulbs in quality and feel?

And if I want to use them with smart switches or dimmers, are there any specs I should look out for?

Would love to hear about any experience, tips, or better sources. Trying to find a middle ground between function and circadian sanity.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-20T02:31:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bought-%E2%80%9Cphotography%E2%80%9D-incandescents-lately-bulbamerica-or-better-sources.30951/post-352961>

It was good talking with you earlier.
Anything “smart” is bad for you. Dimmers create a LOT of dirty electricity AND the more you dim a light the more it flickers.
IF you want/need artificial light (it is all bad compared to sun light) I would look for quartz/halogen bulbs.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2025-05-20T15:43:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bought-%E2%80%9Cphotography%E2%80%9D-incandescents-lately-bulbamerica-or-better-sources.30951/post-352995>

Why halogen over regular incandescent?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-20T16:43:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bought-%E2%80%9Cphotography%E2%80%9D-incandescents-lately-bulbamerica-or-better-sources.30951/post-353001>

Availability - depends where you live.
Halogen are incandescent.
Available in 12v DC - no AC field (little to no flicker) I have mostly DC lighting in my place
Use half the electricity.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2025-05-20T16:54:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bought-%E2%80%9Cphotography%E2%80%9D-incandescents-lately-bulbamerica-or-better-sources.30951/post-353003>

My understanding with incandescent flicker was that it wasn't a big concern due to the filament remaining relatively hot during off cycles. So, the amplitude change in light intensity is not that significant. Is there something I am missing here?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-20T19:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bought-%E2%80%9Cphotography%E2%80%9D-incandescents-lately-bulbamerica-or-better-sources.30951/post-353018>

EVERY bulb is a compromise . . .
Use the slo-mo video on your phone and record the bulbs you have. Incandescent are not as bad as some LED’s but they still ficker.
Some of the new LED’s do not flicker BUT they also have little to no NIR and are mostly blue dominant.
IMO DC incandescents are the best (compromise).

For newbies - the sun is 42%+/- IR 5%-10% UV, changes color throughout the day and does not flicker . . .

# Thread: Magnetic Mystery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-mystery.30965/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### darrenFC — 2025-05-21T14:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-mystery.30965/post-353061>

I tried making my own "magnetico", a ~10 Gauss negative magnetic field to sleep on. I followed as many specs as I could find about it online. It cost about 60-70 USD. including gaussmeter etc. The field is pretty consistent and all negative at mattress level.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

When I sleep on it, I feel really good, and energised. The one problem being that I do not sleep a wink. and by this I mean that I do not feel sleepy at all. The first night, I slept on the bed for about 6 hours and did not sleep until the next night, so about 24 hours of no sleep. Even though I tried to sleep in the afternoon the next day. I only managed to get some sleep after moving the mattress to the ground

Does anyone have an idea of what went wrong?

I think there is a relation to magnetic fields and cold thermogenesis? I am pretty severely cold intolerant, although it has gotten better now. I get painful bumps all over my body and intestinal distress too, which I believe is due to nnemf exposure.

The only other thing I can think of is that living at 15N, maybe the magnetic field is not totally negative seeking but has another component? I don't know if it would need to be that angularly precise..

Appreciate your help & comments!

> [Quintavious the 3rd said:](/goto/post?id=353097)
>
> Read the whole thread [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/)
> case in point #2 [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terrible-insomnia-with-magnetico.29278/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terrible-insomnia-with-magnetico.29278/)

This is perfect, thank you!

### Lukassss — 2025-05-22T10:07:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-mystery.30965/post-353110>

Consider the construction of your magnetic pad.

Read this: [https://magneticosleep.com/why-were-better/](https://magneticosleep.com/why-were-better/).

You have a large gap between the magnets. This can expose you to both positive and negative fields.

# Thread: Leptin Rx when Underweight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-when-underweight.30970/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BiohackersTO — 2025-05-22T17:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-when-underweight.30970/post-353126>

I'm a beginner and a dumbass. The Leptin Rx has often occurred to me personally as not relevant, because I have never been overweight. I thought the Leptin Rx was for those who are overweight. I tend the other way. The foods that would make me fat, give me bad pain, so avoiding them is a no-brainer.

I used to love fasting 1 to 3 days per month, but then I stopped putting the weight back on once I started eating again. Friends started to comment that I was too skinny and then I saw in one picture, I started to look anorexic  So I discontinued fasting. Today, I still do time-restricted eating, but I rarely fast because I would prefer to fatten up, not lose more weight. I am not underweight now but it won't take much for me to get there. I am 5'6" and about 130lbs. I can still wear the clothes I wore as a teenager, but in my 40s, that is not a life goal.

Clearly, I don't get enough AM sun and I'm working on that, as well as increasing my tan the rest of the day.

What's a good way to view the Leptin Rx for people who tend to be underweight, not overweight and people who tend toward autoimmune symptoms? No one in my family is obese. Many have autoimmune like symptoms and some have diagnoses. Every time I read about Leptin problems and weight gain, I think great! Sign me up!

Please help point me to the right thinking about the Leptin Rx for someone who is underweight (or close to it) and deals with growths like endometriosis and fibroids and autoimmune like symptoms.

### Jacks — 2025-05-22T18:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-when-underweight.30970/post-353130>

Suggest you read the Leptin blogs including The Leptin Rx: FAQS on jackkruse.com . You can search for the appropriate ones. Read the answer to "How do I determine if I am leptin resistant" question.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airpods-studies.30974/post-353178>

The photo shows AirPods produce 100x great RF than the ambient RF which was already 1000x greater than “No Concern” according to the Building Biology guild lines.

Remember the AirPods are not just “talking” to your phone (device) they are talking to each other through your head. Bluetooth used 2.4GHz the same frequency as a microwave oven.

Wearing AirPods is like having your head 2 feet from a microwave oven that is running. Scientific enough?

# Thread: Bone broth makes gut / stool worse
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30984/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2025-05-24T11:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30984/post-353254>

Hello,

I started to make my own bone broth from grass fed cows - 20-24 hrs cooked. When its cool it is gelly so high kollagen content.
The problem is, when I drink it (1 cup 250ml) I will fell very full afterwards. I get bloating and bad gas and my stool changes to more soft consistency (not good). Sorry for the description. Sometimes I even feel a little stomach sick. When I quit consuming it after 1-2 days it gets normal again, no gas, bloating and stool gets perfect.

I want to support my gut with it, but what can I do? Whats your idea, what it could be?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Bone broth makes gut / stool worse
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30985/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7

### Butters — 2025-05-24T12:00:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30985/post-353255>

Hello,

I started to make my own bone broth from grass fed cows - 20-24 hrs cooked. When its cool it is gelly so high kollagen content.
The problem is, when I drink it (1 cup 250ml) I will fell very full afterwards. I get bloating and bad gas and my stool changes to more soft consistency (not good). Sorry for the description. Sometimes I even feel a little stomach sick. When I quit consuming it after 1-2 days it gets normal again, no gas, bloating and stool gets perfect.

I want to support my gut with it, but what can I do? Whats your idea, what it could be?

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-05-25T14:44:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30985/post-353300>

Question

Do you leave it to cool covered or uncovered ? Therein may
lie the answer . Cover it whilst it cools.

### Chlo_e — 2025-05-25T15:23:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30985/post-353303>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=353255)
>
> Hello,
>
> I started to make my own bone broth from grass fed cows - 20-24 hrs cooked. When its cool it is gelly so high kollagen content.
> The problem is, when I drink it (1 cup 250ml) I will fell very full afterwards. I get bloating and bad gas and my stool changes to more soft consistency (not good). Sorry for the description. Sometimes I even feel a little stomach sick. When I quit consuming it after 1-2 days it gets normal again, no gas, bloating and stool gets perfect.
>
> I want to support my gut with it, but what can I do? Whats your idea, what it could be?

histamine (not the root of the problem, a downstream effect)

### brianalfredeneuman — 2025-05-25T17:40:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30985/post-353307>

I have tried making my own bone broth in a similar way many times. It shoots through me. Not in a good way. I can't do it.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-25T21:04:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30985/post-353320>

In my experience, at those times I realized “something” was not right OR I found out what was causing the “thing” not to be right and how to fix it . . . the “fix” and necessary changes caused there own problems and a time of adjustment.

Changing your biome can take time and have side effects that take time to settle.

### Butters — 2025-05-26T13:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30985/post-353354>

I let it cool with cover, of course.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-26T13:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-gut-stool-worse.30985/post-353356>

You could shorten cook time to lower histamine content and skim/filter to remove excess fat just until you adapt to it.. Make sure you have checked your place for nn-emf as this could be lowering redox and weaking the gut barrier etc..

# Thread: Suggestions for places in North QLD
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mat.I — 2025-05-26T13:28:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353355>

Hi All,
Does any one have any suggestions for places in North QLD or around there? We are thinking to escape NSW for at least 4 to 5 months of the year.. We initially were looking at and around Port Douglas/Daintree area, however didn't want to get to close to Cairns CBD due to the airport..

Any suggestions of decent areas would be much appreciated. We were going to get a short term rental house that I was going to test for nn-emf prior to signing any agreement.

Thanks
Mat

### NickD — 2025-05-26T15:32:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353358>

Years ago I met a girl who lived at Airlie Beach. She loved the place. Might be something to look into...

### jaedyn — 2025-05-27T11:53:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353407>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=353355)
>
> Hi All,
> Does any one have any suggestions for places in North QLD or around there? We are thinking to escape NSW for at least 4 to 5 months of the year.. We initially were looking at and around Port Douglas/Daintree area, however didn't want to get to close to Cairns CBD due to the airport..
>
> Any suggestions of decent areas would be much appreciated. We were going to get a short term rental house that I was going to test for nn-emf prior to signing any agreement.
>
> Thanks
> Mat

Am considering doing similar. Agnes water sounds like a good spot. Do you think the difference in latitude between Agnes and Daintree would make much of a difference?

### Mat.I — 2025-05-28T03:10:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353442>

> [jaedyn said:](/goto/post?id=353407)
>
> Am considering doing similar. Agnes water sounds like a good spot. Do you think the difference in latitude between Agnes and Daintree would make much of a difference?

Anges water is ~24S and daintree / port douglas area is ~ 16S. You would get stronger UV at the lower latitude, however around Daintree cloud cover may be an issue due to the rainforest.. The only thing is - you should avoid any of the tourist areas as density and tech (nn-emf) would be a lot higher in those areas.. The Daintree area (in forest zones) would have a decent terrain for grounding/telluric earth currents..

I also stumbled on the Atherton Table Lands (slightly inland) youngest area in Aus in terms of extinct Volcanoes. Magma wouldn't be intact or active, however, I assume there would be benefits from the solidified lava (rich in magnetite / hematite and conductive minerals etc)..

I still have a bit of researching and weighing up to do..

Anges Water looks good in in general and has pop density of ~46.3 ppl per km2
Daintree is ~ 14.76ppl per km2
The table lands is ~ 0.4ppl per km2
Port Douglas is ~ 194.7ppl per km2

I just looked up the population and area to get those figures..

Property at Anges Water looks quite reasonable in terms of cost, however, not many rentals available. Unless you look at AirBNB but then you will likely get hit with the premiums..

We looked at FNQ awhile ago, there is a lot of evidence of heavy military grade geo engineering & weather modification activity in that region, hence flooding & saturation. other than some holiday resorts, there is not a lot to support your type of job, unless you can operate remotely. we are going to WA, north of Perth, high UV & a lot of sun in that area, plus away from airports & Mil bases, low pop density.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-29T03:53:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353506>

I operate completely remotely and just focus on QA/QC and tech support for our staff. I'm not taking on any more expert witness jobs and seeing my last one through at the moment (been going for 4 yrs).... Since moving out of Syd (3 yrs ago), I have only had to go in for court and mediation cases, whereas the project work I only oversee and manage the staff remotely part of our QA/QC processes.. So full remote could work, however, I'm just not in a position to leave AUS at the moment.. ### Mat.I — 2025-05-29T04:12:33+0300

### Pete Collins — 2025-05-29T04:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353509>

### Mat.I — 2025-05-29T07:09:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353514>

> [Pete Collins said:](/goto/post?id=353509)
>
> Kununurra is beautiful, however a lot of iron deposits in the land, I think Jack talked about the energy state of Iron??

Good point.. likely stale terrain and in a major stress zone.. I just did a quick check and the dominant forms are Hematite (Fe2O3) - oxidized ferric iron & Goethite (FeO(OH)) - hydrated ferric iron also highly oxidised.. Not a place to go IMO...

### Laudy Cincotta — 2025-05-29T19:09:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353529>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=353442)
>
> Anges water is ~24S and daintree / port douglas area is ~ 16S. You would get stronger UV at the lower latitude, however around Daintree cloud cover may be an issue due to the rainforest.. The only thing is - you should avoid any of the tourist areas as density and tech (nn-emf) would be a lot higher in those areas.. The Daintree area (in forest zones) would have a decent terrain for grounding/telluric earth currents..
>
> I also stumbled on the Atherton Table Lands (slightly inland) youngest area in Aus in terms of extinct Volcanoes. Magma wouldn't be intact or active, however, I assume there would be benefits from the solidified lava (rich in magnetite / hematite and conductive minerals etc)..
>
> I still have a bit of researching and weighing up to do..
>
> Anges Water looks good in in general and has pop density of ~46.3 ppl per km2
> Daintree is ~ 14.76ppl per km2
> The table lands is ~ 0.4ppl per km2
> Port Douglas is ~ 194.7ppl per km2
>
> I just looked up the population and area to get those figures..
>
> Property at Anges Water looks quite reasonable in terms of cost, however, not many rentals available. Unless you look at AirBNB but then you will likely get hit with the premiums..

If we were to move there i would consider Atherton Tablelands, I have friends in the Malanda and its great in there, very low population density, lots of farmers ie access to excellent meat, chicken, organic veggies, raw milk and cheeses etc, good small communities who thrived and bucked the trend during Covid years.

Also would consider Agnes Waters and would not go anywhere near Port Douglas or Palm Cove - too heavily populated with tourists when we were there.

I have heard good things about Mossman - inland from Port Douglas as a good little area but never visited, might be worth looking into as well.

Interestingly we were in Cairns a few years ago and even though its a 5G city, population close to 180k people, the city is very small itself and lots of land surrounding it with lots of vegetation so imo i would not rule it out but rather if access to travel/food is a factor i would look for something outside the city, well away from the airport on acreage and you would obviously need to test to see if it worked for you.

Spoke to friends last night and the geo engineering in Qld specifically Sunshine & Gold Coasts is off the charts - lots of reasons to stay away from there.

Good luck with the move.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-30T05:14:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353560>

Thank you Laudy - appreciate the information, yes the Atherton Tablelands is very high on our list as this seemed to tick all the boxes and cannot really find any obvious negatives that make it a no go..

Our rough references points were around Port Douglas/Cairns (in terms of the latitude) but knew that we would have to go slightly up or inland to avoid the high pop density & nn-emf around the tourist hubs..

I will also check out Mossman - thanks.

Re: the geo engineering - I have heard the same especially around the areas you have mentioned.. It is getting quite bad.

We are in a decent place with low pop density at the moment south of Syd where the majority of the population are in the older demographic, it was a good place for people to retire, more young families have been coming in over the last few years and is becoming a bit more diverse. Semi rural area ~ 800m elevation with the ability to source great local produce straight from farms and local seafood that comes up from further down on the coast. There is quite a solid homeschool community here too that we are a part of.

The aim was to leave our place in the Winter for my wife to get stronger UV for a few months of the year and we were open to a permanent move if we liked the area. The thing I like about where we live is the majority of people (especially in the homeschool community) are good people who are awake. Coming from Sydney, it took me a year or so to adjust to people who truly enjoy others company and wanting to talk for the right reasons. Everyone is very friendly here. So hoping in the long term we can find something similar at a lower latitude for the benefit of my Wife.

Thanks again.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2025-05-30T20:11:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353584>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=353560)

Semi rural area ~ 800m elevation with the ability to source great local produce straight from farms and local seafood that comes up from further down on the coast. The thing I like about where we live is the majority of people (especially in the homeschool community) are good people who are awake. Coming from Sydney, it took me a year or so to adjust to people who truly enjoy others company and wanting to talk for the right reasons. Everyone is very friendly here. So hoping in the long term we can find something similar at a lower latitude for the benefit of my Wife.
>
> Thanks again.

It all sounds good Mat.

I will tell you my friends in Malanda home schooled too, lots of home schoolers in the area so another plus for you.

One thing i thought of to mention is climate.

They have 100% humidity at various times of the year and big issues with mould so this needs to be factored in for you if you haven't already got a plan.

If you haven't looked into mould then ideally renting a house with split system air conditioners or whole house air con if possible with a dry function will act as a dehumidifier and keep the humidity under control so you can control mould growth. Relative humidity needs to be ideally around 55-60% to do this with a low dust load.

Hygrometers are pretty cheap at Jaycar and they will help in rooms to give RH readings to know when to run dehumidifiers.

If you do find an ideal house but it doesn't have these options Aus Climate are a good price and good units so you might need to invest in some small freestanding dehumidifiers to run to control the humidity manually.

All the best, let us know where you end up.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2025-05-30T20:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353585>

> [vereluci said:](/goto/post?id=353570)
>
> Have you checked out the Are around Rockhampton?

No I didn't go to Rockhampton and wouldn't ever consider it, it is not a good spot.

You have a Military Air Base there and Rockhampton Airport is also considered a prime location for lots of military and maritime aircraft, the nnEMF's will be ridiculous.

### MayaB — 2025-05-31T03:39:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-places-in-north-qld.30995/post-353618>

If you really want to escape up in FNQLD you need to cross the Daintree River and head around Cape Tribulation. I'm a HUGE fan of FNQLD and always feel my best there - anything south of Mission Beach is too far south IMO.
