# Thread: GATE / 90s public school gifted programs an offshoot of MKUltra and DARPA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gate-90s-public-school-gifted-programs-an-offshoot-of-mkultra-and-darpa.30998/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### doctorwu85 — 2025-05-27T17:56:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gate-90s-public-school-gifted-programs-an-offshoot-of-mkultra-and-darpa.30998/post-353416>

Fascinating videos here about the gifted and talented elementary school programs of the 1990s and their connection to Operation Paperclip, MKUltra, and DARPA. The reported techniques and aims of these programs lines up with all of Uncle Jack's information about these programs over the last 80 years. I thought this was the appropriate place to post given the other content on this subforum, but feel free to move or close if it's not the right place.

I remember being in one of these programs, where once a week a handful of us would go to a separate classroom and do different activities from other students. I really enjoyed it because we got to play chess, work with computers, and do interesting creative projects. But the video is correct that I don't have a lot of very specific episodic memories of what happened during that time. From what I remember, I was spared some of the more sinister experiences described in the video (being brought to isolated areas, strange field trips), but a lot of it lines up:

\- "Hearing tests" where you had to listen to different tones and conversations with interference in the background
\- Emphasis on technology (we learned HTML as 5th graders and made our own webpages in the mid-90s)
\- Playing lots of Oregon Trail and chess
\- Flash card games with shapes
\- Talking to local businesses (for us it was asking for sponsorships for a fundraiser)
\- Being recruited later on by exclusive groups -- I got a mailing in the late 2000s for some sort of "exclusive professional club" that met in secret on some sort of private grounds. I never responded.
\- Feeling of heightened self-awareness and always being different than others
\- Talent for pattern recognition and intuition, at times verging on uncanny ability to predict things

The "drinking a bright liquid out of a dixie cup" seems vaguely familiar but I can't place where or when that happened.

Curious if anyone else here was part of these programs. It never occurred to me, but makes total sense that this was a government program to identify future assets and potentially unlock MKUltra-type mental abilities of pre-pubescent kids that would be considered to be paranormal by mainstream science. Marshmallow experiment on steroids.

### KFDags — 2025-10-07T19:57:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gate-90s-public-school-gifted-programs-an-offshoot-of-mkultra-and-darpa.30998/post-360591>

Hello,
Well, this is unsettling for a random Tuesday. I do recall having to take these hearing tests in elementary school, possibly twice? Don’t recall drinking any liquid though, but I did belong to the “Crusades” club in school. Never knew or could figure out the actual purpose of Crusades; it was my ticket out of class so I wasn’t about to question it. I don’t recall doing anything nefarious there. We watched movies, played games, ate snacks, and basically just hung out, feeling superior in comparison to the people left in regular class.
I remember you said you were originally from RI; did this take place in RI for you also?

# Thread: Contacts cause sunburns
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contacts-cause-sunburns.31003/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Base_Strength — 2025-05-28T17:46:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contacts-cause-sunburns.31003/post-353465>

Hi everyone,

I’m 54, and for years, I’ve noticed my skin doesn’t tan like it used to. Growing up, summer photos showed me with a deep, bronze tan, but as an adult, my skin leans more reddish than golden, even though I’m outside a lot. I walk for an hour in the morning and midday without a shirt, soaking up sunlight, yet the tan never came.

For the past 15 years, I’ve worn contact lenses with UV protection. Recently, I went down the rabbit hole learning about how sunglasses and UV-blocking lenses can disrupt the body’s ability to sense UV light through the eyes and therefore reduce melanin production. I wondered if my contacts were the culprit.

So, I tried an experiment: I stopped wearing my contacts while walking outside. Within one day, my skin started turning that golden-brown I remember from childhood!

It’s incredible how quickly my body responded once my eyes could fully sense the sun’s UV rays. It’s like my skin got the signal to produce melanin properly again.
I’m in awe of how intelligent our bodies are when we align with natural sunlight.

Has anyone else noticed changes in their tanning after ditching contacts?

### VivaLaValerieBeth — 2025-05-28T19:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/practitioners.31005/post-353477>

Please help me- I'm looking for a practitioner who truly understands the truth that Uncle Jack teaches. Like the Sun, Uncle Jack is TINA but I'm not sure I'd be able to even afford a consult with him. I don't know who to trust. I can understand Uncle Jack's anger and frustration. Centralized medicine is corrupt and criminal. It's hurt me and my loved ones- but now that I know better, I'm determined to do better.

The list of practitioners on his website are few. I've looked at the list of practitioners on the Quantum Biology Collective, and their list also seems limited. I tried writing to Dr Petra Davelaar, but from my understanding, it sounds like you have to join a community and then you can have access to her during Q and A's. I need concrete advise. I am overwhelmed and scared. I have a young family. I need to be healthy for them and to protect them from the harms I endured by centralized medicine. They've already been harmed themselves because of my lack of knowledge. I love them more than anything.

Who am I?

43 yo Female
Married x 14+ years with four children ages 12b,10g, 8b and 5b.
Stay at home Mom (former RN), we care for and support my Mother-in-Law financially
Skin Type: FP Type 2? (Fair, blonde hair that's become darker with age, green eyes)
Maternal Ancestry: Northern European (Mainly German)
Residence: I've primarily resided in Austin, Texas most of my life- I currently live in a suburb of Austin named Leander
Current Weight: 150 lbs
Height: 5'5"
Fam Hx:
Maternal GGM- Slight dementia- death at age 101
Maternal GM- Melanoma, Ovarian CA- death at age 84)
Maternal GF- Prostate CA, Liver CA?- death at age 76 (the CA was all over his body when found- unknown site of origin, he was a geologist who often worked in the mines)
Mother- Breast CA dx age 62, weight loss resistance, mild depression
Paternal GGM- Healthy, death age 104
Paternal GM- Lung CA d/t hx smoking, stomach CA?- death at age mid 70-early 80s
Paternal GF- Heart disease- death at age 70s
Father- Appendiceal CA dx age 66

1981- Born in Oct via vaginal birth, NOT breastfed, my mother always commented that I needed a lot of down-time as a young child, I was quite active in ballet and swim team growing up.

Childhood asthma, frequent ear infections, sinus infections and strep throat as a child, antibiotic usage multiple times a year, 6 dental caries at age 6 with fillings and placed on supplemental topical fluoride, ate a SAD.

1996- Age 15, sophomore in HS on swim team, wonderful family/home life, first diagnosed with "depression" and "anxiety" due to frequent crying for reasons I couldn't understand, started on birth control (only took 6 mo d/t weight gain) and eventually placed on Paxil, low energy, family history of depression on maternal side of family, cystic acne.

Continued low energy with major depressive episodes, exhibiting symptoms of OCD, frequent changes in psychiatric medications, placed on stimulants to help with the lack of energy, lived in Tucson, AZ from 2001-2008.

2011- Married, initial pregnancy resulted in a miscarriage extremely early on, multiple nodules noted on my thyroid, labs were "normal" so no intervention done, given oral progesterone at certain times of my cycle to help with PMS.

2012- First child born in Sept via c-sect at 39 weeks gest, baby was "sunny side up" and head got stuck, baby unable to breastfeed so I pumped x 1 year.

2014- Second child born in Sept via c-sect at 39 weeks and 3 days gest, was induced but suffered uterine rupture, baby also had trouble feeding so I pumped x 1 year.

2016- Third child born in Dec via c-sect at 36 weeks and 1 day gest, baby could not breast feed so again I pumped x 1 mo, increasing fatigue led to increasing depression.

2020- Fourth child born in April via c-sect at 32 weeks and 4 days gest, still no luck breastfeeding (I was told all my kids had very small mouths) so I pumped x 1 year.

2020- Increasing depressive symptoms, admitted to psychiatric hospital x 1 week, changes in psychiatric medications, slightly elevated liver enzymes on labs, constantly craving carbs/sugars.

2021- Admitted to psychiatric hospital for a second time, started ECT therapy, began new antipsychotic medications including Seroquel (gained 40 lbs in 3 months), sadly in June I did received the Pfizer jab (I had questioned to jab prior to this time and was not going to receive it but I believe the plethora of drugs I was given and the ECT weakened my sensibilities- that's my excuse anyhow- I fortunately had the strength to protect my children from being jabbed), liver labs normal.

2022- CT of abd for persistent right lower abd pain noted a fatty liver only (they think the pain is from a ligament though a colonoscopy later showed some external compression of part of my bowel on the right side???), minimal alcohol consumption since age 21- only drank socially, NEVER smoked, stopped Seroquel but weight remained elevated at almost 200 lbs, still very low energy causing me to be increasingy depressed, large 4 cm nodule noted on my right thyroid- FNA revealed atypical cells and genetic testing showed nodule likely cancerous- rt thyroidectomy performed in Aug, large nodule benign but 1.5 mm nodule on isthmus positive for papillary thyroid CA, not other treatment given, slightly elevated liver labs again.

2023- Increasing frustrated with episodes of severe depression and lack of energy, increasingly angry and lacking patience, took myself off all my psychiatric medications (tapered) in Nov, liver labs normal, hysterectomy Feb d/t heavy bleeding and painful periods.

2024- Started seeing a functional med doc in May, dx with gluten sensitivity- started a mostly carnivore diet but entire family went gluten free, stopped drinking alcohol completely, {my daughter had strep throat in April and was given abx at the time- explosion of eczema on her face, neck and inner arms/elbows with symptoms of PANDAS (symptoms have decreased slowly over the past year but are occasionally still present)}, I was able to lose approx 40 lbs being on a gluten free diet/mostly carnivore, depression was no worse after discontinuing psychiatric meds, lightly elevated liver labs again.

2025- Feb: FNA done on 4 mm nodule on left lobe near isthmus of thyroid, nodule slowly increasing over past few years although no change in size noted since Sept 2024, cells atypical and genetic testing showed mutation consistent with Papillary thyroid CA, DISCOVERED UNCLE JACK'S WISDOM!!!!

2025- April: CT of neck, chest and abd done- lymph nodes in neck clear, chest CT neg but CT abd showed small indeterminate lesions on my liver, noted intermittent right upper abd pain

2025- May: MRI findings... Three distinct liver lesions are demonstrated, all of which demonstrate arterial hyperenhancement and become less conspicuous on delayed phase imaging. There is associated mild T2 hyperintensity. At least one of the lesions (series 12, image 45) contains fat. Findings likely represent benign lesions of hepatocellular origin, favor adenoma. \*Segment 8 (series 27, image 17), 1.0 cm. \*Segment 5 (series 27, image 47), 1.5 cm. \*Segment 6 (series 27, image 44), 0.9 cm.

For the past few weeks I've been seeing the sun rise every morning while grounding and trying to follow Uncle Jack's Leptin Rx with occasional implementation of his CT protocol. It's been difficult with 4 young children but I'm doing to absolute best I can. I've switched most of my lights to incandescent red lights and doing my best to decrease my exposure to EMFs. It's been difficult to eat locally much. I do try to go to the farmers market and I get what I can afford. Still mostly carnivore diet with some cruciferous vegetables. Still somewhat craving carbs- particularly at high stress moments. I have been drinking DDW as my budget will allow. I've gotten my skin and the game and have a great tan now! I started taking methylene blue but became concerned when I saw Uncle Jack's article on how it can be dangerous if abused and you do not get enough sun. Which is why I really need someone to help walk me through everything and knows the nuances like Uncle Jack. I'd like to try to save the other half of my thyroid and eliminate my tumor if possible. I don't want to be sold a crap load of supplements or any other crap my functional medicine doctor is trying to sell me. He has helped some I will admit, but when I tried to ask him recently to look into Dr Kruse and his teachings, he kind of refused and brushed me off, so I'm done with him.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-12T07:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/practitioners.31005/post-354541>

Disclaimer - I'm not a Doctor..

You have signs of redox collapse and your labs confirm you are blue light / nnemf toxic. The issues you mention are also tied to transgenerational epigenetics of light induced stress.. A few basic examples -

Glutamate - severe ecotoxicity - nnemfs open VGCC's >Ca2 influx = massive glutamate release. Blue light amplifies NMDA receptor activity.
GABA -/glutamine imbalance - melatonin from AM sunlight converts glutamate > GABA. This imbalance reflects circadian disruption.
Dopamine - without sun DA can't functionally signal correctly..
PEA - spikes when under stress but is normally degraded by MAO-B as the sun regulates this via the retinal opsins.
Serotonin - melatonin shunting is blocked as you are stuck in serotonin phase - Tryptophan > serotonin > melatonin needs morning sun/UV + nightime darkness.
5-HIAA - light polution pushes this pathway. Sunlight resets pineal mito clock to switch to melatonin.
Tryptophan - melatonin precursor depletion .. Chronic cortisol + light mismatch depletes tryptophan and causes circadian chaos
Histamine - mast cell activation - nnems > mast cell degranulation > histamine release > neuroinflammation. Blue light enhances mast cell sensitivity.
BPA - xeonoestrogen overload - nnemfs increase mem permeability allow BPA deeper cell access.
CYP1B1 > 2-OH-E1 - estrogen detox fail . CYPB1 is light and nnemf induced > makes ROS generating 16-OH-E1 (tumor terrain)
The positive intestinal permeability panel - nnemf + blue light disrupt the tight junctions > leaky gut > immune system issues
DHEA - adrenal compensation - when redox is low the body uses DHEA to prop up the terrain.
Estradiol - nnemfs amplify estrogen effects via membrane disruption and receptor overactivation.
Cortisol - nnemfs / poor light environment flatten cortisol oscillation.. No light cue = no amplitude..

**My recommendation:**

Leave no stone unturned in your current environment where you spend your time e.g. home and work.. Hire the best building biologist you can find and afford as I believe you are working and/or living in Chernobyl. Do not entertain half arse fixes such as turning wifi off at night or killing circuits at night you need proper mitigation to eliminate the excess noise in your system.. Also be very strict with not missing a sunrise and staying dark at night. Also get off any tech and chuck any dumb devices aka smart devices in the bin...

> 2011- Married, initial pregnancy resulted in a miscarriage extremely early on, multiple nodules noted on my thyroid, labs were "normal" so no intervention done, given oral progesterone at certain times of my cycle to help with PMS.
>
> 2012- First child born in Sept via c-sect at 39 weeks gest, baby was "sunny side up" and head got stuck, baby unable to breastfeed so I pumped x 1 year.
>
> 2014- Second child born in Sept via c-sect at 39 weeks and 3 days gest, was induced but suffered uterine rupture, baby also had trouble feeding so I pumped x 1 year.
>
> 2016- Third child born in Dec via c-sect at 36 weeks and 1 day gest, baby could not breast feed so again I pumped x 1 mo, increasing fatigue led to increasing depression.
>
> 2020- Fourth child born in April via c-sect at 32 weeks and 4 days gest, still no luck breastfeeding (I was told all my kids had very small mouths) so I pumped x 1 year.
>
> 2020- Increasing depressive symptoms, admitted to psychiatric hospital x 1 week, changes in psychiatric medications, slightly elevated liver enzymes on labs, constantly craving carbs/sugars.
>
> 2021- Admitted to psychiatric hospital for a second time, started ECT therapy, began new antipsychotic medications including Seroquel (gained 40 lbs in 3 months), sadly in June I did received the Pfizer jab (I had questioned to jab prior to this time and was not going to receive it but I believe the plethora of drugs I was given and the ECT weakened my sensibilities- that's my excuse anyhow- I fortunately had the strength to protect my children from being jabbed), liver labs normal.
>
> 2022- CT of abd for persistent right lower abd pain noted a fatty liver only (they think the pain is from a ligament though a colonoscopy later showed some external compression of part of my bowel on the right side???), minimal alcohol consumption since age 21- only drank socially, NEVER smoked, stopped Seroquel but weight remained elevated at almost 200 lbs, still very low energy causing me to be increasingy depressed, large 4 cm nodule noted on my right thyroid- FNA revealed atypical cells and genetic testing showed nodule likely cancerous- rt thyroidectomy performed in Aug, large nodule benign but 1.5 mm nodule on isthmus positive for papillary thyroid CA, not other treatment given, slightly elevated liver labs again.
>
> 2023- Increasing frustrated with episodes of severe depression and lack of energy, increasingly angry and lacking patience, took myself off all my psychiatric medications (tapered) in Nov, liver labs normal, hysterectomy Feb d/t heavy bleeding and painful periods.
>
> 2024- Started seeing a functional med doc in May, dx with gluten sensitivity- started a mostly carnivore diet but entire family went gluten free, stopped drinking alcohol completely, {my daughter had strep throat in April and was given abx at the time- explosion of eczema on her face, neck and inner arms/elbows with symptoms of PANDAS (symptoms have decreased slowly over the past year but are occasionally still present)}, I was able to lose approx 40 lbs being on a gluten free diet/mostly carnivore, depression was no worse after discontinuing psychiatric meds, lightly elevated liver labs again.
>
> 2025- Feb: FNA done on 4 mm nodule on left lobe near isthmus of thyroid, nodule slowly increasing over past few years although no change in size noted since Sept 2024, cells atypical and genetic testing showed mutation consistent with Papillary thyroid CA, DISCOVERED UNCLE JACK'S WISDOM!!!!
>
> 2025- April: CT of neck, chest and abd done- lymph nodes in neck clear, chest CT neg but CT abd showed small indeterminate lesions on my liver, noted intermittent right upper abd pain
>
> 2025- May: MRI findings... Three distinct liver lesions are demonstrated, all of which demonstrate arterial hyperenhancement and become less conspicuous on delayed phase imaging. There is associated mild T2 hyperintensity. At least one of the lesions (series 12, image 45) contains fat. Findings likely represent benign lesions of hepatocellular origin, favor adenoma. \*Segment 8 (series 27, image 17), 1.0 cm. \*Segment 5 (series 27, image 47), 1.5 cm. \*Segment 6 (series 27, image 44), 0.9 cm.
>
> For the past few weeks I've been seeing the sun rise every morning while grounding and trying to follow Uncle Jack's Leptin Rx with occasional implementation of his CT protocol. It's been difficult with 4 young children but I'm doing to absolute best I can. I've switched most of my lights to incandescent red lights and doing my best to decrease my exposure to EMFs. It's been difficult to eat locally much. I do try to go to the farmers market and I get what I can afford. Still mostly carnivore diet with some cruciferous vegetables. Still somewhat craving carbs- particularly at high stress moments. I have been drinking DDW as my budget will allow. I've gotten my skin and the game and have a great tan now! I started taking methylene blue but became concerned when I saw Uncle Jack's article on how it can be dangerous if abused and you do not get enough sun. Which is why I really need someone to help walk me through everything and knows the nuances like Uncle Jack. I'd like to try to save the other half of my thyroid and eliminate my tumor if possible. I don't want to be sold a crap load of supplements or any other crap my functional medicine doctor is trying to sell me. He has helped some I will admit, but when I tried to ask him recently to look into Dr Kruse and his teachings, he kind of refused and brushed me off, so I'm done with him.
>
> Most recent Labs (1-7-25):
> hs- CRP: 1.4
> homocysteine: 7
> BUN/Cr Ratio: 22
> Sodium 141

> Please, please, help me.

On JackKruse.com he does have a list of a few Practitioner for people to reach out to also- if you can afford to. But I agree with Mat I- if you've got budget constraints and want to prioritize- maybe the EMF mitigation is the best bang for your buck!! # Thread: "New" Coffee
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-coffee.31010/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheCircleBastiat — 2025-05-29T15:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-coffee.31010/post-353524>

Does anyone have thoughts on this [new "coffee"](https://rarebird.coffee/pages/px-science-explained?replo-experiment=test-11&replo-variation=LP_Educational&replo-experiment-id=43bcc8a4-aeeb-4a82-b019-ed92ae73b8c8), which apparently replaces caffeine with paraxanthine? I hadn't heard of it until today. Will this avoid the effects mentioned in [Jack's blog](https://www.patreon.com/posts/cpc-17-will-be-17225978)? My read out of the Patreon blog was that caffeine delays and reduces melatonin and is an adenosine antagonist, so it negatively impacts the signal to go to sleep and the processes that happen during sleep that reduce our heteroplasmy. And, there's the issue of deuterium. On its face, the new "coffee" avoids caffeine, so it would on its face reduce at least some of those negative impacts; but I also can't help but think this is a bioengineered, quasi supplementation marketed product that won't achieve what is necessary to drink "coffee" again in a 5G world. What is the biophysics, rather than the reductionist biochemistry? Thoughts?

### MTA321 — 2025-05-29T18:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-coffee.31010/post-353528>

I think it will change nothing and even potentially be more disrupting. Px is still an adenosine antagonist. The coffee still contains deuterium and has been processed via decaffeination which could further disrupt things. The Px they use is synthetically made. This synthetic process could incorporate more deuterium and have negative biophysical effects when isotopes are replaced with ones of heavier mass which changes spin states and fidelity of signal.

Bypassing theobromine and theophylline production from caffeine could further disrupt secondary pathways, such as vasodilation (theobromine) or bronchodilation (theophylline).

# Thread: Leonardo Dicaprio movie called the "Revenant."

**Posts:** 2

### Wanttoknow — 2025-05-31T05:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leonardo-dicaprio-movie-called-the-revenant.31022/post-353623>

Watched this the other night. Kind of based on a true story about a frontiersman named Hugh Glass. The guy wouldn't die. Some thought he was dead but he crawled out of a grave and made it back to the camp. I think it was the cold temps and the icy waters that kept him alive. He got attacked and mauled by a Grizzly bear then soon after he is in the river and his wounds were cleaned in the icy waters. Most would never connect the two but I thought it was interesting. Yes just a movie but interesting.

### riksa — 2025-05-31T09:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leonardo-dicaprio-movie-called-the-revenant.31022/post-353627>

It was an enjoyable movie for me as well. That's interesting because the icy water would spike epinephrine massively, which even surgeons use to prevent bleeding from big wounds. So I would think so too.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-06-01T03:55:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-electrical-wires.31030/post-353669>

Building a house, how much does shielding the wiring in the house help mitigate the damage from AC current?

Of course, will have a kill switch for sleeping at night, and get lots of sun during the day, but is there anything else that can be done to mitigate potential harm inflicted when we are using the AC current?

### MikeR — 2025-06-01T04:28:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-electrical-wires.31030/post-353672>

I would us MC cable instead of Romex wire.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-01T05:56:53+0300

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=353669)
>
> Building a house, how much does shielding the wiring in the house help mitigate the damage from AC current?
>
> Of course, will have a kill switch for sleeping at night, and get lots of sun during the day, but is there anything else that can be done to mitigate potential harm inflicted when we are using the AC current?

MC cable absolutely mitigates electric fields in a building.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-08-05T05:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-electrical-wires.31030/post-357569>

Thanks! Curious if either of you (or anyone else) has any experience with using EMT conduits as faraday cages, wondering if this would produce superior shielding than what MC cable does, or if the difference is neglible?

### Mat.I — 2025-08-05T05:25:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-electrical-wires.31030/post-357570>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=357569)
>
> Thanks! Curious if either of you (or anyone else) has any experience with using EMT conduits as faraday cages, wondering if this would produce superior shielding than what MC cable does, or if the difference is neglible?

In short steel conduit (full Faraday when bonded) would be the best option.. which you could take even further with how the installation is addressed.. Performance of MC would come down to quality of cable, however it will not out perform steel conduit..

### Mat.I — 2025-08-05T05:31:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-electrical-wires.31030/post-357571>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=357570)
>
> In short steel conduit (full Faraday when bonded) would be the best option.. which you could take even further with how the installation is addressed.. Performance of MC would come down to quality of cable, however it will not out perform steel conduit..

You can take it further and twist insulated singles in the steel conduit for even further mag feild cancellation + steel is Ferromagnetic

# Thread: Sleeping on the Earth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-earth.31031/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alain Dwight — 2025-06-01T03:56:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-earth.31031/post-353670>

Does anyone actually do this or have any setups approximating the grounding effects of sleeping directly on the Earth?

Wondering about the logistics...

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-01T05:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-earth.31031/post-353675>

#1 - turn off ALL nnEMF affecting the space you sleep in.
Then IF - your house has a concrete slab foundation the floor may be a good ground.
IF - your house has a crawl space it still may be a good ground . . .
You can test with a Body Voltage Kit (BVK) and see if the floor is a good ground or not.
Both wood and concrete CAN offer a good ground - you have to measure. Man made petroleum products - not so good.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-06-05T17:34:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-earth.31031/post-354013>

> You can test with a Body Voltage Kit (BVK) and see if the floor is a good ground or not.
> Both wood and concrete CAN offer a good ground - you have to measure. Man made petroleum products - not so good.

Interesting, would something like a natural fibre shikibuton laid on grounded concrete work, or is the idea to sleep right on the concrete?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-05T18:36:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-earth.31031/post-354016>

I would use a natural fiber pad.
But lay on something insulated, (plastic) take a BV reading. This is your base line.
lay directly on the concrete then the natural pad, see the difference and then you will know. The lower the number from base line the better the ground.

# Thread: Healed my brain, sleep and life and now I'm living what was once impossible
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healed-my-brain-sleep-and-life-and-now-im-living-what-was-once-impossible.31038/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alison JF Davis — 2025-06-03T00:59:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healed-my-brain-sleep-and-life-and-now-im-living-what-was-once-impossible.31038/post-353799>

Nine years ago, what should have been the happiest moment of my life—welcoming our first beautiful baby boy—was accompanied by two unexpected guests: severe postnatal depression and debilitating insomnia. Not long after my second son was born, my marriage also collapsed.

Once someone who felt bulletproof and was always healthy, I suddenly found myself on my knees.

As a lifelong auditor, I was determined to find the root cause. I intuitively knew I wasn’t interested in quick fixes or medication. I didn’t want to mask the problem—I wanted it gone forever.

Meditation and mindset work brought a sense of temporary peace. But deep down, I knew it wasn’t enough. As soon as I got up from the chair, life—and the chaos—returned.

Then one day, while struggling to get out of bed, I heard an interview with Dr. Jack Kruse. He said: “The best thing you can do for your health is never miss a sunrise for the rest of your life.”

That was a BIG profound moment for me. Maybe it was the Nature part of me waking up. Or maybe it was because watching the sunrise was something I could do—free and simple. I opened my window that very morning and let the light in. Since then, I’ve rarely missed a sunrise.

I had no idea that day, I also opened the door to a life beyond anything I had ever imagined.

Soon, I was walking outside every morning at sunrise. Then I began walking barefoot along a nearby beach for hours—every single day. That was over eight years ago.

In the meantime, I’ve immersed myself in Dr. Kruse’s work—light, water, magnetism, circadian biology, blue light and EMF mitigation.
We even built our home by the sea three years ago to be closer to Nature’s rhythms, designed with these in mind.

Here’s what changed:

-   I healed my postnatal depression and insomnia—completely.
-   All the downstream effects vanished: vision loss, panic attacks, hair thinning, brain fog, and chronic fatigue.
-   My personality transformed. I became calm, peaceful, and resilient. The anger and negativity dissolved.
-   My marriage was restored. It came back at the very moment I let it go with an open heart.

And something I never expected:
Six months ago, I stopped meditating. After nearly 9 years of daily practice, I no longer felt the need. That fleeting sense of oneness I used to experience in meditation—it's here now, in every moment, good or bad. I don’t have to "try" to open my heart anymore. It stays open. My life has turned into a meditation itself.

Now, I don’t even know what’s possible anymore, because I’m breaking my own limits all the time… and I still feel like I’m only just beginning.

Thank you, Dr Kruse. Words are powerless to describe how grateful I am. So much love to you and the extraordinary work you do.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-03T03:25:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healed-my-brain-sleep-and-life-and-now-im-living-what-was-once-impossible.31038/post-353817>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=353800)
>
> nice job.

Yes Sir . . . Nice job.
I know you get attacked and criticized all the time . . . BUT . . . You have affected so many of us for the better.
Nice job.

# Thread: Text To Audio
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/text-to-audio.31046/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2025-06-05T00:42:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/text-to-audio.31046/post-353921>

Does anyone know a good program / app (that don't require sign up/registrations) I could use to copy/paste the blogs in and just listen to them being read to me? I've used the webpage text reader on iphone but it was reading all sort of nonsense that I didn't need / the voice was hard to listen to.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-05T01:30:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/text-to-audio.31046/post-353933>

> [Anthony S said:](/goto/post?id=353921)
>
> Does anyone know a good program / app (that don't require sign up/registrations) I could use to copy/paste the blogs in and just listen to them being read to me? I've used the webpage text reader on iphone but it was reading all sort of nonsense that I didn't need / the voice was hard to listen to.

## Have iPhone speak onscreen text​

1.  Go to Settings { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

    \> Accessibility > Spoken Content.
2.  Turn on any of the following:
    -   _Speak Selection:_ Have iPhone speak the text you select.
    -   _Speak Screen:_ Have iPhone read all text on the screen, starting from the top.
        Turn on Speech Controller to keep the Speak Screen controls on the screen.
        Turn on Highlight Content to have words or sentences highlighted as iPhone reads them out loud.

Apple has many voices to choose from English, American, male, female etc and you can set the speed etc.

# Thread: Kruse's citadel
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruses-citadel.31091/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tomstaplez — 2025-06-10T18:43:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruses-citadel.31091/post-354435>

Uncle Jack when you inevitably talk more about your home can you please address the following... Im keen on building a redneck version of my own.

1) savages acknowledge the sod roof. But is it because of the water in the living roof? Simply the greater mass of it?
2) is it all DC? Or strategically placed AC.
3) traditional plumbing? Copper? Savages are aware of your low D well water, but is it filtered or structured any further?
4) glazing, all savages uber glass jealous.
5) floor I'm sure is grounded, extra steel or copper in your slab?
6) bedroom, Mattress type? Grounded? Head facing north? Heck are you a side sleeper?
7) do you own a freezer? Do you even have preserved food methods at all?
8) no Air conditioning, but fans and/or natural air flow?
9) like cotton and linen on our bodies, do you avoid certain materials for your interior? Furniture, rugs, window coverings ect.
10) indoor plants? recommendations?
11) if some heat was mandatory, would you advise against in-floor hydronic heating combined with wood burning stove?
Thank you.

# Thread: Help! Desperate for mosquito repellant
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-desperate-for-mosquito-repellant.31112/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WildForWesties — 2025-06-15T19:31:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-desperate-for-mosquito-repellant.31112/post-354672>

I'm just starting the LR and am being attacked by mosquitoes since moving to Texas in 2021. In Virginia/DC I was hardly bothered by them, but the mosquitoes were a different species. My health has improved dramatically since moving yet mosquito attacks are a 1000 times worse here with the demon from hell Asian Tiger species.

I've read that mosquitoes are attracted to carboxylic acid, an organic acid that some people have on their skin. Are you familiar with this, what causes it and how to get rid of it?

I've also read on the forum they are attracted to UV light and until redox is improved I'm leaking light (but I wasn't bothered by mosquitos like this in Virginia and I was \*much\* sicker there). These mofo demons ignore citronella candles, UV bug zappers, and all other non-skin repellants I've hung around my patio. West Nile is in my immediate neighborhood so I'm playing Russian roulette right now as I'm being eaten alive. Is there nothing I can put on my skin? I have lemon eucalyptus spray that's the only thing I've tried that keeps them off but this will mess with the light signal? Can you tan through a mosquito net?

Mosquito control is my #1 issue to solve - everything else is falling into place. I am all in on becoming a "savage" and refuse to wait to start the LR until November when the demons crawl back into their hellholes for the winter.

Thank you for any and all suggestions.

### JenniferM — 2025-06-16T00:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-desperate-for-mosquito-repellant.31112/post-354678>

Mosquitos hate lavender and neem oil.

### Chlo_e — 2025-06-16T12:26:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-desperate-for-mosquito-repellant.31112/post-354689>

Yep second the neem-based repellent

### Carly18 — 2025-06-16T16:20:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-desperate-for-mosquito-repellant.31112/post-354695>

Use a high powered fan or screen yourself in. Light still penetrates porch screens. Get rid of all standing water and add bat and bird houses on the property too and find a way to attract dragonflies.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-06-16T16:33:32+0300

West Nile is in my immediate neighborhood so I'm playing Russian roulette right now as I'm being eaten alive. Is there nothing I can put on my skin? >

Try a misting fan, battery-powered, for cooling as well as air to help blow the flying bugs away, if possible. Sun gets to you through leaves, so a mosquito net will not block the needed sunlight.

# Thread: Wim Hof Breathing plus Iron +3 shifted = Tinnitus?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wim-hof-breathing-plus-iron-3-shifted-tinnitus.31116/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MatsDS123 — 2025-06-16T15:21:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wim-hof-breathing-plus-iron-3-shifted-tinnitus.31116/post-354694>

Hey all,

I wanted to share an experience and get some insights.

In late 2021, I developed persistent tinnitus at the same time I was trying out Wim Hof breathing. The onset did not come on immediately after doing it, but it occurred in the same time period.

Later, I had blood work done, and my ferritin was >300 ng/mL. That’s high-normal, but I’ve since learned that ferritin isn’t just a benign storage form—especially when there's inflammation or oxidative imbalance.

After listening to Jack talk about the implications of oxygenation and iron shifted to +3 state, this got me wondering

-   Could the oxidative surge from Wim Hof (hyperoxia, followed by reoxygenation after CO₂ depletion) have triggered iron redox cycling?
-   If my body was in a +3 iron-shifted state (Fe³⁺ dominant), could that have created a vulnerable setup for Fenton chemistry and ROS generation in sensitive areas like the cochlea?
-   Could this be a recipe for oxidative damage to hair cells or auditory neurons?

Could a high ferritin/Fe³⁺ state sensitize the system to ROS damage when combined with hyperoxic breathing techniques?

I’ve since come across anecdotal reports of tinnitus after intense breathwork or HBOT.

Would love to hear:

-   If anyone has experienced similar symptoms
-   Any thoughts on the iron/redox/tinnitus connection
-   Whether anyone’s mitigated these effects and reversed tinnitus.

Appreciate any perspectives

Hi Guys,

I've heard Dr. Kruse refer to the infrared saunas as being in Chernobyl, so I stopped using the one I have (Sunlighten). Recently, we got our home hard-wired and got ethernet as well. Jana

### The.Naked.Doctor — 2025-06-17T03:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/infrared-sauna.31118/post-354738>

We had our building biologist test my IR sauna (Sunstream). It was very low EF/MF and Rf (but we had to disconnect the wiring from the Bluetooth connector). He was surprised at the low levels. Dr Kruse generally feels they are hugely underpowere and no match for a geothermal sauna (not worth it), in benefit... BUT i fucking love mine, i sit in there and read or meidtate. its just a really nice part of wind down routine. Im skinny and often overdo it with ocean swims, i find a warm shower doesn't work well (i feel even colder after), but the sauna is awesome if i overdo the swim and get the bitchy shaky freeze my ass off cant get warm and the suns not out in the winter - situation.

Most advertise low EMF as the blanket statement based on the heater construction & low consumption achieving low Mag fields, however, the EF's are a bit problematic if not addressed with shielding and armoured cabling. Sunstream advertise as less than 1mG & less than 20 v/m (which is still not ideal) however, just curious if it stacks up with what they are claiming.. Either turn them off and/or sit by a window (open if possible) or

Go outside in the sun as often as you can, especially at lunch.

### moon77 — 2025-06-17T21:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/me-journal.31129/post-354765>

male
32 yrs age

currenlty reaidng all of uncle jacks work.

stratign to eat betetr sleep better

a rough start upbringing in some areas.

few health problems that im aware of

use to have a lot of bloating stomach issues. and still have some my belly somtiems looks liek a huge berr belly even though i dont drink
u shaped curve torso probably due to all the reading and pc use or sorts. im pretty tall.
frequent headaches or migraines i use to get them at least 2 x a month and they last all day long when they hit.and sometimes for 2 or 3 days. i can most times feel them coming the day before by feeling weaker or brain fog or something of this sorts.
nothing helped me no aspirins or anything

driving in a car for anytime around 1 hour or more 80% or more triggers these headaches . also when i was in school reading a lot also triggers them. and a lot of other things trigger them to. weather also triggers it. im more scared than a city pigeon when i sense its about to come. but theres nothing i can do. knowing i am helpless with this instills a fear in me that is like my master sometimes and no matter how hard i try to think ill overcome them one day. the fact is years later i still get them .
they ruined parts of my life

the only thing that helped me and still does is to lie down in a empty dark cool room and just lay out.

inguinal hernia sticking out and trying to deal with this. ive had it for years and i think its time to deal with it head on
it sometimes hurts bad . and a lot sucks the air out of me if i talk to much. its the size of a golf ball lump at this point . its sticking out and at the point where you barley see my penis because of the lump.
i use the hernia belts but i realize i cant do this anymore and must work on this .im scared

history of a bit of depression and stress

have concerns about dating and fears of meeting women.
ever since my first experience when i was younger in my 20s my partner made bad comments about my penis size and refused to further a relationship because of it i believe. even though she never said it.
Now any time i even come close to attempting to speak to another women this weird feeling comes over me and cripples my ability. Its like a dark cloud

that day was traumatic to me even though i did not realize at the time.
now years later it still plays a role in my trauma.

im hoping the leptin rx and all this new world learning will help me grow

### Martha Ray — 2025-06-22T19:43:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/implementing-some-of-dr-kruse-protocols.31138/post-355027>

With cancer I saw an improvement in my husband within 2 weeks (between MRI's) with **fresh broccoli sprouts** (well grown from farmers market) and the _Mara Labs_ product [**BrocElite**](https://mara-labs.com/collections/shops?tw_source=google&tw_adid=693060323689&tw_campaign=21090004216&tw_kwdid=kwd-791641042029&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=21090004216&gbraid=0AAAAACvmgJlSesUSv-NwRUWICvr818TDA&gclid=EAIaIQobChMIk8SU_q-FjgMV36EgBR1PnScfEAAYASAAEgJWTvD_BwE) and a retail [**Organic Red Clover tea**](https://www.buddhateas.com/red-clover-tea.html) in bags and [**_Herb Pharm_ Red Clover tea tincture**](https://www.herb-pharm.com/products/red-clover/).
Perhaps those could be of help for you.

Your back story and great attitude touched my heart... If ok with you I will pray for you for health and healing.

By far the best two helps in our whole lives was 1-our grace oriented **Faith alone in The Lord Jesus Christ alone** for eternal life and 2- for salvation from the cares of this life our serious **_Daily_** **D**[**octrinal study of The Word of God**](https://www.rbthieme.org/index.html).

My husband trusted his physical health to conventional medical advice and he chose home hospice and he died quickly in 'dying grace'. He had been athletic and strong his whole life. He was mostly well for his 86 years until his Dx of pancreatic cancer. He never want to get old and he lived a full and wonderful life. I honored his choice but personally I believe that there are No incurable diseases and that it is possible to live this life healthier and die 'naturally well' in God's timing.

# Thread: Btc question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/btc-question.31147/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### optimal2024 — 2025-06-20T16:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/btc-question.31147/post-354916>

Hi

I have tried to look online but with no joy.

I am looking to invest a small amount of BTC in MSTY, via hanetf however the brokers they offer do not accept BTC.

I am struggling to find out whether converting the btc into gbp and investing in MSTY what the tax implications are?

If anyone could point me in the right direction it would be much appreciated.

### doctorwu85 — 2025-06-20T19:37:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/btc-question.31147/post-354918>

This depends on your local tax laws and N=1 tax situation, so nobody is going to be able to provide you with a perfect answer.

In the United States, you would typically use an exchange to sell your bitcoin for USD. This would trigger a tax event where, assuming the BTC has increased in value since you bought it, you would be required to pay capital gains taxes on the difference between the purchase and sale price. What percentage tax depends on how long you've held it, your tax bracket, etc.

In the US, the monthly distributions from MSTY are also taxed as normal income and not as capital gains, so they are taxed at a higher rate. All of this could be different in the UK. You should probably investigate how capital gains taxes work where you are.

# Thread: Getting spouse on board..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sarahnile — 2025-06-21T15:10:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-354952>

Hello everyone, I'm new here  Consuming more and more information and implementing in baby steps, I'm literally on my 7th consecutive sunrise. I have had unexplained symptoms for a year now and have been jumping down rabbit holes ever since. I've changed lightbulbs in the house, specifically the floor with our bedroom so that my night routine can be done in minimal to no light but I am married so obviously the bedroom, bathroom etc are all shared quarters. Each room has an option for regular incandescent light but I've got the red on when I'm winding down to sleep.

My husband (who contrary to me has actually always been an early riser and early sleeper) is in a little bit of a sleep-in phase these days, I think it's been a month or two. He came to the conclusion a while back that he must wait to sleep till midnight (or somewhere around there) otherwise he wakes up to a bowel movement at 4am, so he's been keeping himself up till that time looking at a screen of some sort till he passes out or comes to bed. I, admittedly used to be the one to get to bed late but we've switched now.

He had a pretty visceral reaction to the red light in our bedroom, bathroom, and closet while I was getting ready for bed last night. He walks up and turns a light on and gets upset if I ask him to turn it back off. He finds the lighting patronizing as if I'm dictating to him how he should live. I sleep with an eye mask but last night he looked at his phone for about an hour next to me in bed, I covered myself up but of course I'm pissed and then too hot under the covers to fall asleep so it's a cluster fuck  He said this morning "10 days ago you were fine with light at night and now you're not, it's troubling." also that he supports my waking up early in the morning but the night time lighting is really irking him, and he misses me at nights

Has anyone had similar problems like this? Any pointers? I sent him a podcast a few days ago to check out, I'm not sure if he listened, it specifically had information on weed smoking which he does sometimes, also alcohol once in a while, and there's a video game he loves. He works during the week and goes to the gym often, he plays tennis when he can but complains about not being able to lose some last bit of pudge on his waistline. He's been open enough to try carnivore in the past in the name of weight loss but then said taking out all carbs and the lack of variety made him sad, also that he never felt full or satisfied. He doesnt really get "sick" necessarily often but he does have days where he says he's "exhausted" and then he's horizontal all day. This month on the other hand he caught a cold twice and he's trying to recover from the 2nd one as I write. He had 4 days of an exhibition for work in closed doors under blue light and very crowded, he said he didn't really eat well either so I'm guessing his body gave out. He chose to recover playing his computer game, I was successful in getting him outside a couple of times and I caught him catching fresh air on his own as well.

All of this is screaming low dopamine but he may come around with time. I am not willing to go back after what I've been exposed to so far and only wish to learn more. It's surprising to me honestly how he hasn't said wow, is that true? and dove in with me.

Is there any content out there for the resistant? Or a specific approach someone has been successful with? I don't need him to start grounding with me but I would like to be able to have a seemless night time routine and get him out of the room if he's not done with his device and it's after sunset.

i think the solution all of us would say is you tried. if it was me id leave her immediatley. let him spread his wings how he sees fit.let him down gently and say " i loved you but this isnt gonna work , your weighing me down and i was meant to fly liek a eagle" my wings have been clioped my whole life, no more! ! this stops now!
we all have wings to spread but some of ours are bigger and prettier than others and that irritates them to the point of them hitting our feathers with their heavy greasy feathers that were meant to bring both birds down.
as one of my great teachers use t o say
" you can put your hand out to help once twice but if put out again it will be chopped off and you will be a one hand man that wont be able to wipe himsl3ef which is no man now"

and you may want to buy a new front door lock also

your welcome

I have a magnet I can spin that throws out a field that my gauss meter registers going through my head.
I use the “E-pen” to show the electric field going through two or three people holding hands.
I explain the 120V AC field in their bedroom is 60Hz and to get to sleep the brain has to get to Alpha 8Hz - 12Hz.
On multiple occasions the guy then takes my meters and goes around the house taking measurements.
I have shown the RF level 4ft from a running microwave oven is the same 4ft from the WiFi router.
Bluetooth headsets are 100x higher than background RF.

Most guys love gadgets. Once they can see the nnEMF, most get on board or at least are not so negative.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-22T03:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-354981>

Most people seem like they don’t want to change, but in reality it’s often not about desire, it’s about dopaminergic capacity. Low dopamine means low agency. And agency is what gives us the ability to initiate change, imagine a better future, and take action toward it.

When dopamine signaling is disrupted through poor redox, no morning light, excess blue light, chronic inflammation, trauma, or mitochondrial dysfunction, people literally can’t feel the pull of change. The motivational circuitry goes offline. They default to the path of least resistance, even if that path is painful.

So it's not that they don’t want to, it’s that they neurologically can’t. Their system is stuck in survival mode.

Change your own environment: light, magnetism, redox. Even if they don’t join in, their shared field does shift.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-22T20:26:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-355029>

> [sarahnile said:](/goto/post?id=355020)
>
> I haven't started with testing my house for EMF yet, I need to learn a little more of what I need so that I don't go spending money on things I don't know how to use, it's kind of daunting, and scared of what I might find.. But thank you for this, this makes a lot of sense.

The answer to what you need depends on who you ask . . . And how much you want to (can) spend.
RF - the Safe & Sound Pro II is a great meter ($400)
Magnetic - the Alpa Labs UHS2 (3 axis) Gauss meter is good ($400)
Electric fields - the TriField for “spot” readings is ok ($180)

My preference for in home electric fields is the Body Voltage (BV) method. Others disagree. BVK $165

The Cornet Quad 5G Pro measures spot electric fields, magnetic fields, radio frequency (RF) 5G calibrated AND light flicker ($220)
The Cornet Quad meter is a great place to get started at a reasonable price.

We offer the EMFSafe Starter Kit - BVK, Cornet Quad, extra ground cord, with 30 min consulting for $445 with 10% off Kruse discount and free shipping.
We help you get set up, get the readings, understand them and come up with a mitigation plan. [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Mat.I — 2025-06-23T06:15:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-spouse-on-board.31152/post-355061>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=355029)
>
> My preference for in home electric fields is the Body Voltage (BV) method. Others disagree.

Body Voltage doesn’t directly measure the electric field strength - what it shows is the voltage your body accumulates through capacitive coupling with the surrounding electric field. So yes, it’s an indirect measure, and the result depends heavily on how well you're coupled (e.g., mattress, posture, nearby conductors). It's not disagreement - it's just understanding how the physics of capacitive systems work. If using the BV method, always have a decent EF meter to compare and check all 3 axis (x, y, & z) to cross check and understand what is happening in each spatial plane..

### S Hudson — 2025-06-21T20:05:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-gargle-with-a-bit-of-peroxide.31153/post-354964>

It depends why you are doing it.
If it is to remove plaque, don't bother. Only manual brushing can budge that.
Plaque is a biofilm that forms in layers. no mouthwash can penetrate established biofilm.

### digitald3 — 2025-06-21T20:50:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-gargle-with-a-bit-of-peroxide.31153/post-354968>

Good oral health requires a solid metabolism. Which means managing light diet and food diet. And because in a modern world managing the light diet is really hard. It’s wise to use a tooth brush and floss to disrupt the plaque. Toothpaste and rinses are superfluous for general health daily. There are rinses, like chlorine dioxide that will penetrate the biofilm but Introduce chlorine to the system. Peridex/chlorhexieine can be helpful if acute infections are present. Peridex has been shown to kill fibroblasts in culture so it has its potential downsides too. It also stains teeth.

Fluoride, nano hydroxyappetite, hydrogen peroxide et al are all adjuncts to the real story. Which is too much blue light and not enough sun. Blue light is the sugar story. As the a light diet full of blue often correlates to a high carbohydrate diet. None of the magic agents above will not completely solve a bad environment and diet.

### moon77 — 2025-06-22T01:02:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-gargle-with-a-bit-of-peroxide.31153/post-354974>

It’s wise to use a tooth brush and floss to disrupt the plaque. Toothpaste and rinses are superfluous for general health daily. There are rinses, like chlorine dioxide that will penetrate the biofilm but Introduce chlorine to the system. Peridex/chlorhexieine can be helpful if acute infections are present. Peridex has been shown to kill fibroblasts in culture so it has its potential downsides too. It also stains teeth.
>
> Fluoride, nano hydroxyappetite, hydrogen peroxide et al are all adjuncts to the real story. Which is too much blue light and not enough sun. Blue light is the sugar story. As the a light diet full of blue often correlates to a high carbohydrate diet. None of the magic agents above will not completely solve a bad environment and diet.

i get that but what im saying ois , as a simple rinse is it safe. ive read peroxide kills only bad bacteria not the good and its good to do once a day. no real reason other than a simple rinse to maintain good oral health. i understand about the light and all that though

The oral microbiome has the potential to provide an important symbiotic function in human blood pressure physiology by contributing to the generation of nitric oxide (NO), an essential cardiovascular signaling molecule. NO is produced by the human ...

### JenniferM — 2025-06-22T19:36:47+0300

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=354978)

> ### [Therapeutic DMSO Combinations Revolutionize Medicine](https://open.substack.com/pub/amidwesterndoctor/p/therapeutic-dmso-combinations-revolutionize?utm_source=share&utm_medium=android&r=22nca8)
>
> How DMSO being mixed with numerous common medications creates a myriad of remarkable therapeutic possibilities
>
>  open.substack.com

Thank you for the information you have presented in your papers. I am proof DMSO works. I researched it when bloodwork was showing something was brewing in my lungs. I mixed it with Frankincense, Myrrh and ginger swiping across my upper chest. 6 months later CT picked up 1 ground glass nodule and 3 mets (4 total so they thought.) I reviewed the scans and began using the mix right over the 4 lung mets. CEA was high at 12.x so plan with 1st lung surgeon consult was kitchen sink chemo "and see what happens", next step would be SBRT, then next step would be take my middle right lung because a met was on the bronchial tube (to hard to get) and wedge resections. I said No Thanks! I contacted a surgeon in Germany whose expertise in Rolles Lung Laser surgery (lung sparing procedure) to get his opinion. He said he could get all 4 mets. When I had surgery they were impressed that the mets were 75% dead with only a minor amount of cancer cells remaining in 3 mets. The fourth met turned out to be a macrophage M2.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-22T21:10:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355036>

Not a Lawyer but -
Naked in public is against the law in most places (except a LGBTQ+ parade).
Naked in private is everyone’s right. At least in America, where do you live?
Take photos of where you were.
Show, if you can, you were not making a “public” display, but you were on private property.
Were you making a public display?
Were you visible from any of your neighbor’s windows?
It is also my understanding (in most places) you have the right to face your accuser.
File charges of spying and invasion of privacy against them.

### doctorwu85 — 2025-06-23T18:31:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355100>

I cannot emphasize this enough:

DO NOT talk to law enforcement without a lawyer present.

They will say the meeting with the shrink is for your benefit, all the while trying to manipulate you into giving them evidence to use against you. Just don't do it. They are not doing something to help you. This is classic cop misdirection. They are professionally trained to build a case against you, and not to do anything that's "beneficial to you and your case". Wake up and smell the coffee.

Make sure you have representation there. It sounds like you have some serious judgment issues, but I wish you the best. You are probably fucked either way, but you are certainly fucked if you go talking to these guys without a lawyer to advise you.

### TB14 — 2025-06-24T02:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355126>

This is not legal advice, but first and foremost, the advice above about not talking to the police is generally good. More specifically, one of the big risks of doing so is accidentally and unnecessarily admitting to an element of the crime, even if not admitting to the whole thing. For example, if the crime is indecent exposure, the accused might tell the police "I didn't think anybody could see me! I would never have sat naked on my deck if I knew my neighbors were home!" Although such a statement might be good enough for exoneration if the accused was taken at their word on every element of it, at trial, it could be used as evidence beyond a reasonable doubt that they were, in fact, naked on their porch, which is doing the prosecution's work for them. This follows from the fact that if the defendant wasn't naked on their porch, there would be no logical reason for them to say that they were. So that admission might be considered as rock-solid proof. The portion of the statement claiming lack of intent, by contrast, might be given essentially no weight, because regardless of the defendant's true intent, they would have had a clear motive (attempting to avoid punishment) to claim that the exposure was accidental. So such a statement would likely be unwise even though if it were taken as true in its entirety it would result in a finding of innocence, because the only work it's actually likely to do in practice is towards a finding of guilt.

Also, I have no experience at all with this particular issue, but the fact that somebody is on their own property seems very unlikely to me to be a complete defense. The vast majority of criminal law as applied to everyday people is based in common sense. That's certainly not to say I think all of the criminal laws are correct, but just ask yourself "would a normie think this should be illegal?" and the answer has probably a 95+% correlation with whether it in fact is. If somebody is changing in their bedroom and they accidentally leave their window cracked, and this is the first time it happens, then no, that's almost definitely not illegal. If somebody is regularly deliberately calling attention to themselves while totally nude on their front porch five feet away from a busy intersection at rush hour, then yes, that's almost definitely illegal.

Ultimately almost all crimes have an element of mens rea, which is basically state of mind with respect to the undesirable outcome at issue, that has to be proven along with the other elements. I would guess that the mens rea is likely usually the deciding factor as to whether a crime has been committed in most cases where the prosecution is indeed able to prove beyond reasonable doubt that somebody was involuntarily visually exposed to another person's nude body. I don't know what the mens rea is for indecent exposure in any particular jurisdiction, and I should note that some crimes with sexual undertones (especially if involving minors) are among the extremely rare exceptions to the general rule of even the existence of a mens rea element (_i.e._, for a small number of crimes, intent doesn't matter - if you committed the act, you're guilty), but I would guess that anything a defendant can do to show that they actually took steps to try to avoid being seen would likely be helpful to their case, while any obvious steps that weren't taken might make a conviction more likely. For example, was the defendant on the side of their property with the least or the most public visibility when the incident took place? If so, that may be relevant, as it's arguably unlikely to be a coincidence that they chose that particular side. Was this the first time the defendant was provably made aware that they could be seen from the position they chose by somebody else - no prior warnings/requests, calls to the police, etc. - or the twentieth time? Also likely relevant. Putting up barriers that leave no doubt as to whether they were deliberately assembled for the sole purpose of making a good faith effort to avoid being seen would likely be highly relevant.

### TB14 — 2025-06-24T20:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355178>

The Bill of Rights, including the Fourth Amendment, only applies to government action.

In general, if someone was actually going out of their way to view someone else naked, I would think that would very likely work heavily against not only one but two elements of the crime of indecent exposure: (i) the involuntary nature of the exposure with respect to the alleged victim, and (ii) the mens rea requirement with respect to the accused.

If someone accused of indecent exposure were to try to flip the tables on the accuser by accusing them of invasion of privacy, I find it difficult to predict how that might go. I'm very confident that laws against invasion of privacy do exist - the common sense test tells me that Peeping Toms are committing a crime - but how grey areas in the application of that law would likely be resolved isn't as obvious to me except in cases without much nuance. For example, tons of people have security cameras on the outside of their property now. If the camera is set up in a relatively normal way (_i.e._, for general surveillance purposes, not targeting a neighbor specifically) and happens to incidentally catch a neighbor sunbathing nude because they were doing it outside, even if that neighbor was on their own property while so doing, then the common sense test tells me that the filming neighbor has committed no crime. If the camera is set up specifically so that somebody can look into their neighbor's bedroom window to watch them change and clearly has no other purpose, then the common sense test tells me that the filming neighbor has committed a crime.

It's much less clear to me whether a crime would have been committed if the neighbor was in fact targeting the sunbather specifically, while the sunbather was on their own property, and while the sunbather was nude; but only because the neighbor believed that the nude sunbather was committing a crime by being nude under the circumstances in which the sunbather was filmed and the neighbor wanted to record evidence of the commission of that believed crime to present to law enforcement. There's a lot of nuance here and a lot of competing interests - it feels like a fact pattern on a bar exam or a case from a law school casebook. You have competing property rights (your right to privacy on your property versus their right to film from their property), whether to encourage or discourage vigilantism in law enforcement activities (including surveillance) and under what circumstances, the quasi-sexual element (due to the nudity) that has repeatedly overriden other long-standing and otherwise highly prioritized principles in other areas of criminal law in recent times, somewhat of a privacy versus expression angle if filming is viewed as a form of expression, and more. I don't really have a good guess as to which way this would go. The right person to ask would probably be a criminal attorney in the applicable jurisdiction.

Another possibility besides trying to cause criminal charges to be brought against the filming neighbor might be pursuing civil action. The sunbathing neighbor could potentially file suit for invasion of privacy or a similar tort. Again, I don't know what the applicable law would be here in any particular jurisdiction, and there would likely be the same competing interests as noted in the paragraph above that would go into determining that law, but two potential benefits for the sunbather of going the civil route might be that (i) the sunbather wouldn't be reliant on law enforcement to bring charges (the sunbather could file the suit themself or with the assistance of an attorney, which if properly done I believe at least all but guarantees at least that it's considered at some level on its legal merits by someone with legal training), and (ii) the burden of proof would be less than the beyond reasonable doubt standard that's necessary to secure a criminal conviction. The latter could be relevant if, for example, it was impossible to prove beyond a reasonable doubt that the filming neighbor was intentionally targeting the sunbather with their camera (rather than incidentally catching the sunbather on a more general surveillance camera), but it could be established that it was much more likely than not that they were.

> Ultimately almost all crimes have an element of mens rea, which is basically state of mind with respect to the undesirable outcome at issue, that has to be proven along with the other elements. I would guess that the mens rea is likely usually the deciding factor as to whether a crime has been committed in most cases where the prosecution is indeed able to prove beyond reasonable doubt that somebody was involuntarily visually exposed to another person's nude body. I don't know what the mens rea is for indecent exposure in any particular jurisdiction, and I should note that some crimes with sexual undertones (especially if involving minors) are among the extremely rare exceptions to the general rule of even the existence of a mens rea element (_i.e._, for a small number of crimes, intent doesn't matter - if you committed the act, you're guilty), but I would guess that anything a defendant can do to show that they actually took steps to try to avoid being seen would likely be helpful to their case, while any obvious steps that weren't taken might make a conviction more likely. For example, was the defendant on the side of their property with the least or the most public visibility when the incident took place? If so, that may be relevant, as it's arguably unlikely to be a coincidence that they chose that particular side. Was this the first time the defendant was provably made aware that they could be seen from the position they chose by somebody else - no prior warnings/requests, calls to the police, etc. - or the twentieth time? Also likely relevant. Putting up barriers that leave no doubt as to whether they were deliberately assembled for the sole purpose of making a good faith effort to avoid being seen would likely be highly relevant.

### TB14 — 2025-06-25T17:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355253>

If somebody were taping their neighbor while nude sunbathing for years, I would imagine that would very likely work against an indecent exposure charge against the nude sunbather for an instance of such exposure.

If the supposed victim of the exposure was aware of it for years and objected to it, why not act sooner to try to put an end to it? I imagine the exposure must cease to be considered involuntary at some point.

If the filming neighbor were to claim that they hadn't reviewed the footage or been aware of the nude sunbathing activity until they made the report, then the existence of years of evidence that the nude sunbather was sunbathing nude in that spot without making the neighbor aware that they were so doing would likely work in favor of the nude sunbather because it would likely point toward lack of the adequate mens rea. If they'd been sunbathing nude in that spot for years without being seen, as admitted by the supposed victim themself, it might be more likely to be considered reasonable for the sunbather to assume that nobody would see them while they were in that spot in any individual instance.

### TB14 — 2025-06-25T18:03:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arrested-for-trying-to-nude-sun-tanning-in-backyard.31158/post-355260>

Being nude on your own property isn't necessarily perfectly legal, though. I addressed this in my first response. Just think about what you're saying. Do you really think it's legal to flash people as long as the flasher is on their own front lawn? There's very likely nuance. Being on one's own property is likely a helpful factor in arguing that one's intent in being nude wasn't to indecently expose oneself to others. But somebody accused of indecent exposure very likely can't rely on being on their own property alone to get them off the hook. It's very likely not a complete defense.

The Fourth Amendment doesn't apply to prevent neighbors from snooping on each other. It's a restriction against the government, not private citizens. I addressed this in my second response. I don't anticipate the Fourth Amendment would be helpful in such a situation at all.

As I've noted before, in forming a defense, it might be prudent for someone accused of indecent exposure who doesn't believe they were acting unreasonably by being nude given the circumstances to focus a significant portion of their defense on their state of mind.

It would absolutely be prudent for anyone who believes they may be charged with a crime to look up the statutes in the relevant jurisdiction so that they can tailor their defense against the crime which they believe they are being accused of committing.

I don't know what jurisdiction that bill is from. But I can't give you legal advice about a jurisdiction I'm not licensed in (I'm only actively licensed in Washington, D.C.) and wouldn't do so anyway without an agreement in place disclaiming my liability for any results of following any advice I might give. I can only give general descriptions, not personalized advice.

If someone were to believe there's some statute in their jurisdiction that would render evidence collected against them inadmissible and/or the manner in which that evidence was collected a crime, then sure, it might well be worth pursuing that. In general, video footage of the accused committing elements of a crime that was gathered by a private citizen acting of their own accord (not at the behest of or in coordination with the government) and subsequently submitted to law enforcement is about as solid as it gets.

Maybe there are significant exceptions in some jurisdiction(s) that I'm unaware of, or maybe not. I won't be able to help you make that determination with regard to any specific jurisdiction. I advise you to consult with a criminal defense attorney who is actively licensed in the relevant jurisdiction, especially if something seems unclear to you about a law you think might be relevant.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blushield-emf-protection.31162/post-355072>

In his book, Jason Bawden-Smith, a Kruser, espouses the benefits of Blushield’s devices which supposedly harmonise nnEMFs via the emission of scalar waves, thus rendering them harmless. My search has yielded minimal mention of Blushield on this forum and I’ve never heard Jack mention it. Some reviewers have sworn by it and others say they’ve opened the devices up and found little more than a flashing light inside. I do not know how much physical componentry is required for the production of scalar waves. I’m curious if there is any kind of consensus among members here on the efficacy of Blushield’s devices.
