### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-26T01:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blushield-emf-protection.31162/post-355280>

If a harmonizer/neutralizer is powered it a least has the possibility of harmonizing/neutralizing the harmful effects of a nnEMF field.
BUT if the lights and your phone still work, the same nnEMF the was there before in still going through your body so how much protection can there be?

A non-powered device (shungite, copper coil, piece of plastic with a fancy design on it) scientifically CANNOT stop the damage being done to your cells. It might have some short term placebo benefits helping “feel” better, but medium/long term the damage is still being done.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-24T05:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-for-emf-protection-from-iphone.31169/post-355130>

You only want to shield one side of the phone . . . The side toward you, to shield you from the phone.
IF you shield the whole phone it will power up to make a connection to the tower, possibly increasing your exposure.
Why use a Faraday bag? . . . Just turn off the phone.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-24T07:27:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-for-emf-protection-from-iphone.31169/post-355131>

There is a spectrum here and how you apply it is based on own n=1 conditions..
e.g.
\- airplane mode + manually disabling location, blue tooth, NFC etc.. OR
\- Powered off. OR
\- Powered off + faraday..

I know of quite a number of people that will do powered off + faraday for valid reasons (as you can not remove the batteries of most phones).

Lets take an apple iphone for example (I personally do not own one) however I researched this sometime ago (~21/22) to lay out the facts to someone that was adamant phone off = no transmission...

In my experience phone off does not = radio off (actually they enter low power or passive active states)
\- Apple uses multiple handshakes with other nearby devices with bluetooth low energy (2.4 GHz)
\- The ultra wideband chip to my understanding also remains passively active (~6.5 - 9.5 GHz) - intermittent brief ultra wide burst (<2ms windows)
\- NFC still active (if not disabled) (~13MHz)..
There are ways to hardware disable all of these features... However.. all the chips are still powered from the rail.. There are also more cases where if interfered with or intercepted other features can be remotely activated..

That's why the Librem 5 was developed (removeable battery & hardware kill switches)... I think there was also a pine phone pro??

For other phones in general the highest level of protection is powered off + faraday bag...

So I don't have a faraday bag but I do have an I phone, and when out and about, its on airplane mode and I made a lovely tin foil shield that sits in my back pack between my back and where the phone is in the bag. Not ideal but a quick fix. So just one side of the phone is shielded, had to warn husband when I put it there to make sure he didn't pop the phone back in the bag when on my back between the shield and me!!

We also rearranged our living room cupboards so that we could block next doors smart tv through the wall by stacking the cupboards full height and lining the back of the with tin foil too. Meter went from red to green, so that was good.

# Thread: Personal Care Plan - Hard Copy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-plan-hard-copy.31176/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rtd — 2025-06-24T18:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-plan-hard-copy.31176/post-355175>

In mid July I see my PCP. A week before I plan to drop off a printed copy of my health plan. My health contains my defined goals, pass fail criteria, method(s) to accomplish them, and a timeline.

My PCP is new; taking over an existing practice. I wonder if my PCP would take the time to read my plan ahead of my visit to provide me with though about feedback.

I am probably naïve on this. There is doctor shortage, a healthcare shortage, in my area.

Anyone have personal experience with a written personal care plan?

### maria@modernhuman — 2025-06-28T23:17:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-plan-hard-copy.31176/post-355442>

Great question. Given that the mean/median routine PCP office visit duration in the US has been found to be anywhere from 15.7 minutes (Tai-Seale et al., 2007) to 19.3-21.6 minutes (Rao et al., 2019), my question would be: what is the word count of your health plan as that will tell you how long it would take the PCP to read the document assuming a reading speed of 250 words/ minute.

I hope it goes well and the PCP has a positive response to your initiative for healthcare accountability

### [Time Allocation in Primary Care Office Visits - PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2254573/)

To use an innovative videotape analysis method to examine how clinic time was spent during elderly patients' visits to primary care physicians. Secondary objectives were to identify the factors that influence time allocations. A convenience sample ...

 pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

[https://www.annfammed.org/content/annalsfm/17/6/538.full.pdf](https://www.annfammed.org/content/annalsfm/17/6/538.full.pdf)

### rtd — 2025-07-15T19:15:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-plan-hard-copy.31176/post-356369>

Instead of providing a written Health Care Plan for my PCP, I brought printed copies of the [Leptin Reset Easy Start Guide](https://jackkruse.com/leptin-reset-easy-start-guide/). I anticipated, correctly so, an interest from the staff, and maybe the doctor, on how I lost quite a bit of weight since my last visit.

A small win. More fat to lose.

Daily Supplements:
Tablets - Ubiquinol and L-Acetyl Carnitine
Liquid - water+ 1/2 tsp Potassium Salt and 1/2 tsp Magnesium Salt
Liquid - D-ribose (1 tsp) + water (2x daily)

In this weather, I can feel when my electrolytes are low. I am less hungry. Water is not enough. Especially if I don't have a Big Ass Breakfast. My morning meds + coffee with little food = diarrhea (CHA CHA CHA)

# Thread: Help with son w/ alpha-gal diagnosis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-son-w-alpha-gal-diagnosis.31178/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### michaeltatarjr — 2025-06-24T20:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-son-w-alpha-gal-diagnosis.31178/post-355184>

Good morning, Dr Kruse (and tribe)

I want some information on how to help my son. He’s a wonderful nine yr old who has unfortunately contracted alpha-gal. As I’m sure you know, Alpha-gal is a tick-borne condition, and there’s not a lot of research of protocols on how to help. My assumption this is a crazy \*ss scheme by the ‘Usual Suspects’ to force people not to eat meat. We are investigating several alternative therapies that seem to help but wonder if you have a perspective and perhaps Rx.

For background, I’ve found your information about a year ago and have gone whole hog into it with the family. Thus far we’ve made the following changes.

**Sunlight is TINA** for my wife and I. We only miss if it rains really hard, below 15 degrees or are quite sick (which is almost never!) We typically spend 45 minutes to 2 hours in the morning sun, wearing little clothing. My youngest boy will join us sometimes as well. I even bought a convertible to be able to get more sun in the morning and in the evening.

**Grounding:** Grounding pads in several places in the house, as well as some real grounding in the morning. I typically will sit out in the front yard in a lawn chair for about 45 minutes. My son runs around shoeless every day as well.

**Magnetism:** We purchased a Magnetico for the wife and I’s bed. A year in and we are happy with it. We will purchase one for our youngest likely at some point.

**Epi Paleo** for me. I try to lean into the epi-paleo rx and as a family we don’t eat a host of things. Currently trying to eliminate grains from my sons diet. We don’t eat a lot of bread, but he REALLY likes bagels. We do eat A LOT of coconut oil, etc. No dairy or night shades for the wife and son. We also have chickens and eat a lot of fresh eggs. We also bought half a cow (for the wife and I)

**CT**. I’ve become a serious cold plunger! I recently did 48 degrees at 48 degrees, then the next day sat on my porch for an hour packed in 35-40 lbs of ice.

**BTC**. I have a background in several disciplines and economics is one, so after doing my 100 hours of study, we’ve leaned into this as well.

**Bulbs**. We bought a bunch of amber and red-light bulbs and have changed out most bulbs in the house. We still leave a few normal ones, but even they are augmented with amber or reds. Some folks who come to visit comment a little, but in general it’s minimal.

**Redlight glasses** at about 7:30-8pm. We all put on our glasses.

**Limited screentime**. We try to limit it to 1 hour a day at the house. (This doesn’t count work, unfortunately…)

**Redshift all screens of professional devices** My work computer screens are redshifted, and my wife tries to use her daylight computer as she can. She works from home and has her desk set up in front of a bunch of windows.

All of this to say, we’ve jumped in with both feet!

I read your articles on Lyme disease, (your book and a ton of other things) thinking there might be some crossover. If there is, I can’t see it.

Wondering if you can help, or point me in a direction. Thank you for your time! Know that your blogging/advice/podcasts have been life changing for this family.

Best;

Mike T.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-25T15:00:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-son-w-alpha-gal-diagnosis.31178/post-355239>

> [michaeltatarjr said:](/goto/post?id=355184)
>
> want some information on how to help my son. He’s a wonderful nine yr old who has unfortunately contracted alpha-gal. As I’m sure you know, Alpha-gal is a tick-borne condition, and there’s not a lot of research of protocols on how to help. My assumption this is a crazy \*ss scheme by the ‘Usual Suspects’ to force people not to eat meat. We are investigating several alternative therapies that seem to help but wonder if you have a perspective and perhaps Rx

This would be tied to a redox issue.. you have not mentioned if you have assessed your nnemf.. This is critical. Have a decent building biologist do a EMF assessment.. Also, if your son is not homeschooled it may be like a salmon swimming up stream to build his redox if he is being zapped all day at school... Work towards transitioning to homeschool.

> [michaeltatarjr said:](/goto/post?id=355184)
>
> **Grounding:** Grounding pads in several places in the house,

Not wise, especially, if you have the power on to the house (I wrote a thread on this) "grounding indoors" you can search for it..

> [michaeltatarjr said:](/goto/post?id=355184)
>
> **Magnetism:** We purchased a Magnetico for the wife and I’s bed. A year in and we are happy with it. We will purchase one for our youngest likely at some point.

Not wise to use in high nnemf areas so get your place assessed. It can enhance tissue coupling to ambient feilds and act like an amplifier.. That's why it is important to get the assessment so you know the risk and can decide whether or not to use the magnetico and what preconditions must be satisfied to limit any negatives..

EDTA is a chelating agent "Claw", attracting heavy atoms, both good and bad, and get filtered through your kidneys. My first wife had it done at Dr. Ray Ever's clinic in the Bahamas in the 70's along with hundreds/thousands, who looked at chelation as the "answer" back then.

In the right medical hands, it can be a way to remove toxic chemicals, but think about it. Our own bodies do that since heavy metals typically accumulate in our fat stores, so sweating, eating the correct diet, getting proper sun exposure, in other words, doing all Jack recommends is enough to remove heavy metals in a slow, steady way that our kidneys can process without undue harm to other organs.

Unless there is an urgent need to remove a singular catastrophic exposure, it is taking a risk to remove heavy metals from our bodies with EDTA. Others, especially Jack, knows far more than I do, as I just have my experiences from the 70's, and the studies I did at the time, but I would gather as much current research as possible before going that route.

### riksa — 2025-06-25T14:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-questions-1.31188/post-355238>

He is exposing himself and you to 70,000x the safe level.

# Thread: Spectrometer Specs & Brands
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectrometer-specs-brands.31192/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TB14 — 2025-06-25T22:56:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectrometer-specs-brands.31192/post-355274>

What spectrometers do people here use? What parameters make a good spectrometer? There are a handful of forum posts from people over the years looking for info on this, but not finding any detailed answers. Ideally I would think you'd want a meter or meters (if you can't get a single meter that captures everything useful to know at once) that can measure from at least the shortest wavelength of UV-B into IR-A and also detect flicker with as much accuracy as possible. Anyone have any more detailed info here? For example, maybe the speed of flicker the meters are capable of detecting? Or what qualifies as acceptable and ideal sensitivity or accuracy? There's a ton of info out there about which EMF meters to buy but I haven't seen anything like that for spectrometers. If nobody has ever really thought about it or put anything like that together, I'll plan to try to compile some info on potentially helpful specs and brands here myself when I get free time.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-26T00:56:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectrometer-specs-brands.31192/post-355278>

The new Cornet 5G Quad Pro in addition to measuring Electric fields, magnetic fields & RF also measures light flicker BUT it is NOT a spectrometer. All for $220+/-.
I also would like info about a decent spectrometer.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-26T01:51:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectrometer-specs-brands.31192/post-355284>

For "hand held" UPR-TEK is the main stream brand.. However you can get better specs from Alibaba or the like - you search for OHSP350 and reach out to the supplier for the appropriate variation to suit your needs (to avoid the standard quite narrow spectrum ranges). E.g. they can provide 280nm - 1000nm, & 200nm - 1100nm (the wider spectral range = higher cost). Standard ones typically start 380nm however you loose out on UVB & full UVA range. I would personally not buy a UPR-TEK for the reason of the cost vs the specs you get. Alibaba is the way to go..

### AlbertW — 2025-07-30T03:37:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectrometer-specs-brands.31192/post-357167>

Does anybody have a spectrometer measuring UV here? I have the OHSP350UV but I got readings that seem wrong…

Thread 'UVB in the Shade'
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/)

> I haven't seen a doctor in 34 years and if my mother knew better when I was a kid I'd have never seen a doctor in my life.
>
> Only exception was when I needed stitches for an open wound and even then they made me go to the hospital to get them done.

What is your secret to good healing?

### Saichi — 2025-06-29T02:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tennessee-doctors.31201/post-355452>

> [Anthony S said:](/goto/post?id=355451)
>
> What is your secret to good healing?

Almost a decade in emf triage for myself, friends, and family. Building biology standards are non-negotiable as is natural law what I used to consider strict is pretty lenient regarding effects.

Triage probably won't end in my lifetime, but I grew up poor and experimented on like most families with ties to high level military so extreme suffering and pain are not foreign to me. But nothing more insufferable to me than the emf gulags most people are 'comfortable' with.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-uv-penetrating-clothing.31213/post-356776>

### Todd Schulz — 2025-07-22T17:30:50+0300

### JHackett — 2025-07-23T08:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-uv-penetrating-clothing.31213/post-356831>

### roboslob — 2025-07-23T12:04:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-uv-penetrating-clothing.31213/post-356841>

I think you should be careful of the materials/chemicals used. 100% linen may be the best option. Here's an article on how it's not good for sun "protection", just what we want [https://somesunclothing.com/blogs/news/understanding-sun-protection-why-linen-fabric-falls-short](https://somesunclothing.com/blogs/news/understanding-sun-protection-why-linen-fabric-falls-short). Look for GOTS or OEKO-TEX certified for no toxic chemicals or dyes.

By the way, what color clothing is best?

### digital — 2025-07-25T17:30:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-uv-penetrating-clothing.31213/post-356957>

Cooltan, and others, use a thin poly, synthetic materials; patterns that allow the light to go through. I'd be careful with synthetic materials; As others have stated, Linen and Cottons are nicer, however they need to have similar pattern-openings like Cooltan products. One might consider Indian cotton and linen clothing companies.

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-07-26T10:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-uv-penetrating-clothing.31213/post-356995>

I bought a kiniki bikini this year, and i have found it barely lets any sun through, whilst if you hold it up to the light you can see through it very well, from a tanning point of view i still had white boobs ! The tops are double lined which seemed odd. so gave up on the top !
Husband bought a fairly cheap cotton top from asda this year and you can see better amounts of sunlight on the skin but it doesn't look see through at all, and much nicer in terms of cotton versus synthetic.

### HealthByPhysics — 2025-07-30T15:17:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-uv-penetrating-clothing.31213/post-357194>

Let you child run as nature intended, naked, mine is 1.9 years old and is outside with just a diaper on all the time even in public when allowed. I don't care what people say or think bc if they only knew. Also Jack did mention a lady on instagram that makes custom clothes for young girls

### Tera Pfarr — 2025-08-04T16:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-uv-penetrating-clothing.31213/post-357520>

At home they run free constantly! Mine are 6,8,10yo so a little older and more aware. I just hate seeing tan lines on my kids from public activities.

I'm sure IVM got OTC approval for 3 x states - Arkansas, Idaho, & Tennessee. Idaho would be the closest to you, however does not solve the legal issue of being back in another state (when you return). Ok this leaves you with 3 options - first (2) involve driving to Idaho (but are not legal once you return or leave the state, so I have not bothered with these). Legal and best option, would be cold call some Physicians in Idaho and explain the circumstances and see if they will give you a script (but travel there for a consultation to obtain the script). Albeit, IVM is OTC in Idaho they still have the ability to give you a script and this is what residents would do prior to travelling with the IVM outside the state. This way you can establish a legitimate doc patient relationship and be covered legally in your state.

Good luck with it.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355582>

Is exercise not the goal of walking dogs?

Is the actual reason to allow them to ground & get solar exposure.

So is it accurate to call it animal abuse, if a pet owner does not walk their dog or let them go outside regularly? Or feed them out of season foods?

What do pet owners think?

I've never personally owned/had a pet.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-01T22:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355583>

Some cats never go outside. They are grounding to certain extent by being on the floor BUT they are also exposed to ALL the in home nnEMF!
I think all dogs get to go out several time a day so they get sun and good grounding in the back yard away from nnEMF.
People with no back yard have to walk their dogs And if they are sitting most of the day the exercise (walking) is good for both the dog and human along with the sun. The dog will benefit from grounding out side but the human will not unless bare footed or has grounding shoes.

### AlbertD — 2025-07-01T22:48:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355584>

Ah that's true didn't consider the nnEMF exposure

### moddedlu — 2025-07-01T23:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355586>

Any time I've taken my dog outside, she loves to lay on the ground and capture some sun. As Uncle Jack has pointed out, once she's had enough, she naturally goes into the shade. Should it be considered abuse not to let our pets out in the sun? In my opinion, Yes, given that all it takes is to at least open the window and let some photons into the house. Most people live with closed, heavily draped windows until night time which is a detriment not just to pets but pet owners, as we know. I also agree with Anthony on the nnEMF side of things.

### Carly18 — 2025-07-02T16:10:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-walk-dogs-is-for-grounding-sun-exposure-not-exercise.31226/post-355616>

A clue that my home environment wasn’t healthy was how quickly my and my dog’s health collapsed during the scamdemic.
I attributed my health issues with stress but when my dog passed due to “idiopathic epilepsy”, the ER vet suggested he may have had a brain tumor. This was within the timeline in which I had started RLT in the winter and discovered Dr Kruse in the spring. My health improved significantly with sunrises, turning off wifi unless necessary and grounding as much as possible.
I’m now pet sitting for a friend and all of her animals are out at sunrise with me, asking for breakfast an hour later and easily go to bed at the same time I do (within 3 days of being here). The older dog sometimes sleeps indoors during the hotter times of the day but everyone else is always outside while we can. The black cat is my suntanning buddy and brings me mouse treats on the deck. The dogs and I eat lots of fresh eggs laid that day. The wifi nor the tv is ever on. I prefer the birds and the peace.

I have learned so much from my dogs, being that they are truly guided by instinct, but reliant on their owners to know how to let them be dogs, and to eat their own version of their "ancestral diet", which is raw and not the highly processed "kibble" that is so convenient for the owners.

I lightly bake wild salmon and other game foods for my girls, hoping to give them the real foods they need, in effect, putting them on a Paleo diet.

They love it, are lean, and are outside throughout the day, but being black, absorbing all the sunlight easily, and needing shade to retreat to while outside.

They are asleep within a hour after sunset, and awake an hour or more before sunrise, so they see every sunrise, and since I get up with them at 5:30 each morning, we spend time outside together, with me trying to learn from them.

### sarahnile — 2025-07-02T11:08:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/updated-labs.31227/post-355603>

I have attached some updated labs of mine, I am aware that the laboratory did the wrong pyruvate and I have to take that test again. My symptoms for the past year have been post nasal drip, swelling submandibular glands with pain, headache. Then the odd joint pain here and there. I had had 6 rounds of antibiotics in total. starting with an unresolving UTI, Then doctors thought I had pharyngitis, sinusitus.. My health got turmed on its head. I have not done EBV and CMV PCR's and the dysbiosis test, they are more on the pricier side  I will do them if I need to. My previous labs were done in Nov/Dec 2024. My ANA, UIBC, C3 have not changed. My ECP was previously 46.6 and now it is 17.2. I am on sunrise #19. I've been grounding for 2 hours every day and implementing a kind of modified AIP / EPI PALEO diet. I have not yet tested my environment for nnEMF. My symptoms shifted after week 2 from swollen painful gland flares to upper body aches, as if I had lifted weights for the first time in a while. Post nasal drip has decreased but not gone and no sore throat but for the past 4 days I'm extremely achy all of a sudden in my upper torso, arms and hands namely. It has lifted today for the first time and I am monitoring. I am only supplementing magnesium. I must do a check on my environment soon but I have been trying to ease into it due to my husband. He is slowly coming around, starting to make jokes etc. instead of lashing out but basically he finds the modifications to light "extreme" but is so far respecting my needs on his good days. Last night he wanted to look at his phone again in bed but with his back turned to me. I went to another room to sleep but I think our room is actually the best location in the house for sleeping, away from the street and electrical box but of course I can never be sure, I must test. Anything that stands out to anyone re my labs or any more labs I should get done? I will retest lactate/pyruvate. Does anyone have any input regarding the ANA positive, DFS70 +++positive and Anti Mi-2 +positive? I've never had Anti Mi-2 done before, this is a first.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-02T11:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/updated-labs.31227/post-355604>

I gave some feedback in your other post re: your labs - however, I was thinking that the recent shift could be due to your immune system trying to recalibrate due to the circadian corrections (i.e. doing the sunrises etc). As your immune cells all have clock genes. Due to your poor light environment prior to changes being made this would have scrambled the SCN's signals and when the master clock loses it's timing immune cells also loose their timing..

**Do not hold me to this** but the shift you are experiencing may be positive as it may be part of the "recalibration" process. As the immune cells would be reorganising. Whilst this is happening they may be dumping out "old" inflammatory signals, shifting fluid and adjusting the redox balance.. The areas of complaint are linked with major lymph drainage pathways.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - would be best to give you feedback as I could be off the mark here..

Also, prioritise having your environment assessed by a Building Biologist as chronic VGCC activation (from EMF) can prime the immune system into a low-grade inflammatory state. There is a spectrum here, however this will eventually shift you towards a FE3+ state.

### sarahnile — 2025-07-03T15:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-on-past-surgery-scars.31235/post-355663>

Hello all, I heard Dr. Kruse say on a podcast that using red light on an area where there was a surgery would make things worse. The reasons weren't elaborated on in this specific podcast, or maybe I didn't catch it, but could anyone enlighten me on that topic? Why / how does it make it worse and does it apply to all kinds of surgery, new and old? For example, would red light on an old C-section scar be non beneficial and in fact do more harm than good?

not sure he would recommend red over the sun.

as marks uncle use to tell me , you can put your finger in the door to land the interview but if you want to get the job your hand has to touch the ground

### sarahnile — 2025-07-03T17:45:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-on-past-surgery-scars.31235/post-355675>

I am not saying in place of, in addition to, possibly. I'm more interested in the why behind it than finding an excuse to use red light on a scar.

### digitald3 — 2025-07-04T15:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-on-past-surgery-scars.31235/post-355720>

Look up scar reduction and Photobiomodulation in a search engine. There are studies that show benefits to using red light therapy or Photobiomodulation on scars. The downside is man made lights aren’t as good as the sun. And it’s easy to over do the effects. The rule of thumb is only use PBM every 48 hours It’s far better to get your scar in the am sun and use the sun everyday. Which means getting your belly and scar in the sun. You can use the sun daily as it’s the sun!

# Thread: homebody
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homebody.31239/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JenniferM — 2025-07-04T03:29:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homebody.31239/post-355701>

I literally stay close to home. Haven't been to walmart in over 20 years or the grocery store. Today I was going to take my grandson to the movies (he begged me to take him to see Minecraft) but it wasn't playing any more thank goodness. I remembered how sad he was not having a bicycle in 2 years so I thought I'm getting him a bicycle today. First stop the bicycle shop, it was closed on Thursdays. So, went to walmart. Got him a bike and helmet and by the time I got to the cash register I could barely see. My eyes were pouring, my nose was running and felt headache and fatigue coming on. I mentioned it to the cashier and she said it was probably from the cut flower section close to us. Whew, I was so glad to get out of there and my grandson was glad to get a new bike.
It is disturbing being in very low emf/rf then bam there you are soaking it up and feeling like crap for hours.

### hacklose — 2025-08-20T03:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-358530>

> [urko said:](/goto/post?id=358506)
>
> Hello Jacque, any update on the dasung screen. I was thinking about purchasing one.

I'd be curious to see what Jacque thinks.

For what it's worth, I've tried the Daylight DC-1 and the Dasung Paperlike 103 monitor.
They each use different screen technology to achieve a grayscale "E-paper" display.

The DC-1 screen has absolutely no ghosting. The Paperlike 103 does.
The DC-1 has a visually better refresh rate than the Paperlike 103, but the Paperlike 103 really does have a surprisingly fast refresh rate for an E Ink monitor. They both advertise 60hz refresh rate.

The DC-1 uses a backlight and the Paperlike 103 uses a front light. My eyes prefer a front light.
The ghosting on the Paperlike 103 is very noticeable when watching videos.

I like that the Paperlike 103 is a monitor, so you can use a windows, mac, or linux OS to go along with it. Linux for the win!
With the DC-1 you're stuck with google's android OS, at least for now, or using an app that lets you use the DC-1 as a monitor with a windows OS.

They both have pros and cons. I end up using the DC-1 with a wired ethernet connection most of the time.
If I need a color screen, I have an older laptop that I run linux on. I use that about 2% of the time these days. I really don't find I need color very often, but everyone is different.

### urko — 2025-08-20T10:41:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-358538>

> [hacklose said:](/goto/post?id=358530)
>
> I'd be curious to see what Jacque thinks.
>
> For what it's worth, I've tried the Daylight DC-1 and the Dasung Paperlike 103 monitor.
> They each use different screen technology to achieve a grayscale "E-paper" display.
>
> The DC-1 screen has absolutely no ghosting. The Paperlike 103 does.
> The DC-1 has a visually better refresh rate than the Paperlike 103, but the Paperlike 103 really does have a surprisingly fast refresh rate for an E Ink monitor. They both advertise 60hz refresh rate.
>
> The DC-1 uses a backlight and the Paperlike 103 uses a front light. My eyes prefer a front light.
> The ghosting on the Paperlike 103 is very noticeable when watching videos.
>
> I like that the Paperlike 103 is a monitor, so you can use a windows, mac, or linux OS to go along with it. Linux for the win!
> With the DC-1 you're stuck with google's android OS, at least for now, or using an app that lets you use the DC-1 as a monitor with a windows OS.
>
> They both have pros and cons. I end up using the DC-1 with a wired ethernet connection most of the time.
> If I need a color screen, I have an older laptop that I run linux on. I use that about 2% of the time these days. I really don't find I need color very often, but everyone is different.

Thanks so much to answer and giving feedback I really appreciate man .
My idea was to purchase an external screen to test with my laptop as external but primary screen.

### hacklose — 2025-08-20T19:19:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-358551>

> [urko said:](/goto/post?id=358538)
>
> Thanks so much to answer and giving feedback I really appreciate man .
> My idea was to purchase an external screen to test with my laptop as external but primary screen.

You're welcome!

I think it's a good idea. It allows you to look at a screen without blue blockers on and also not support blue light technology at the same time. Win win!

### Johan Lindøm — 2025-08-20T20:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-358557>

Anyone got a stand and a keyboard for the DC-1, and a muse? Any insight to share? I see there is a pin connections in the back, but how to use it?

### hacklose — 2025-08-20T21:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-358559>

> [Johan Lindøm said:](/goto/post?id=358557)
>
> Anyone got a stand and a keyboard for the DC-1, and a muse? Any insight to share? I see there is a pin connections in the back, but how to use it?

I just lean my DC-1 on the screen of my old laptop. I use an Anker 6-1 USB-C hub so that I can use a keyboard, mouse, and ethernet all at the same time through the DC-1's single USB-C port. It also has a pass through USB-C power connection so it can be charged without unplugging the USB-C hub from the DC-1.

I recommend charging the DC-1 when you are not using it and running it on battery power when you are using it. The electric fields are much higher when charging it vs. running it on the battery.

There are plenty of different USB-C hub options available. Just find one that supports what you want to use.
Keyboard and mouse work perfectly fine with the DC-1. An ethernet internet connection works with the bluetooth and wifi shut off in the settings. Good stuff!

### urko — 2025-10-25T10:56:13+0300

>
> There are plenty of different USB-C hub options available. Just find one that supports what you want to use.
> Keyboard and mouse work perfectly fine with the DC-1. An ethernet internet connection works with the bluetooth and wifi shut off in the settings. Good stuff!

do you perceive any kind of overheating with the hub?

### S Hudson — 2025-10-25T14:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-light-computer.31252/post-361580>

I think the game changer comes when this technology is transferred to full sized monitors (assuming it hasn't already been). I'd struggle to do my writing on such a small screen.... although the ghost of George Orwell is laughing at that comment, as such a device would have been a luxury for him. he wrote 1984 on a second hand typewriter while he was dying from TB

# Thread: Shielding a Wi-Fi Router with a Faraday Bag
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### OptimalSkr — 2025-07-07T19:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-355934>

I am staying in an AirBnb and there is a wifi router.

But I came prepared - I bought a Hodufy faraday bag and an ethernet cable. What I do is put the router in the faraday bag and run the ethernet cable from the router to my CPU. Now I am able to get the internet on my laptop without wifi.

However when testing with the Safe and Sound Classic 1 meter ([https://emf-protection.ca/product/safe-and-sound-classic/](https://emf-protection.ca/product/safe-and-sound-classic/)) I get a high reading when near the router, but as I move away it dramatically lowers to almost nothing at about 20 feet.

Also I can still get a wifi signal and use it on my phone.

Question to the resident EMF expert [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) ---- is this normal readings? Or is the bag not sufficient quality to block the EMF? Also my meter is older and maybe the router is still broadcasting at a frequency my meter does not measure.

Am I fooling myself with the low readings? Thoughts?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-07T19:26:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-355936>

The bag will (should) help but you need a meter with numbers so you know what the reading is. Newer meter are also calibrated for 5G technology and newer routers. You could invest the Safe & Sound Pro II ($400) or the new Cornet 5G Quad Pro ($200) if you want to save some money.

In the mean time put a couple of layers of aluminum foil between you and the router. Not over it, just shield the side you are on.

With all nnEMF distance is your friend.

### OptimalSkr — 2025-07-08T00:56:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-355963>

Thanks Anthony - Ya I figured my meter is a bit old -- the network is listed as a 5g router.

There must be some benefit to the faraday bag right? I mean my meter does show a huge drop especially with distance --- Have you tested different kinds of faraday bags to see the efficiency for different generations of signals or would it be all the same in terms of shielding..... I just assumed that it would 100% block all frequencies to zero... But I looked online and it said something like it cuts the frequencies and they die off after a certain distance etc (not sure I understand the physics of that lol)

For the aluminum foil you mean put sheets of it over the bag that already covers the router?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-08T02:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-355966>

Go to [https://liveemfsafe.com/emf-101](https://liveemfsafe.com/emf-101)
Scroll down for some shielding ideas options.
Any fine metal mesh (or foil) will work (think screen door on a microwave oven).
NOT 100% but I often see 90%+ reduction. For a quick, easy, cheep fix, very doable for most.
With any nnEMF - less is better than more.
I think the screen works better if there a small distance (a few inches) between the router and the screen.
Take RF readings at least 3 feet from the source (has to do with wave length).

### OptimalSkr — 2025-07-08T15:23:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-a-wi-fi-router-with-a-faraday-bag.31259/post-356001>

Thanks -- I will look more intro upgrading my EMF game!

# Thread: Physics Questions - Shielding Magnetic Fields from Powerlines
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TB14 — 2025-07-07T22:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355952>

My understanding is that shielding of low-frequency AC magnetic fields is accomplished using materials such as Mu-metal that have high permeability to redirect the fields, which is in contrast to the techniques used to shield low-frequency AC electric fields and radiofrequency waves, which I believe is generally accomplished using materials that reflect or attenuate them. I am not sure I fully grasp the low-frequency AC magnetic field shielding mechanism of materials with high permeability and have some applied questions below involving three shielding scenarios hoping to increase my understanding (and also hoping this could be helpful to others as well). Answers to any of the below, even if not going through every piece of every question, would likely be super helpful.

Say you have a main utility feeder powerline. The current going through this powerline generates AC magnetic fields in continuous circles with the powerline at the center of each circle, with the fields progressively decreasing in intensity with distance from the powerline. Now say you have a house 20 feet from this powerline that is exposed to the AC magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current.

**(1) Powerline Cylinder Enclosure**: Would wrapping the portion of the powerline the current of which is giving off magnetic fields to which the house is exposed in a cylinder of high-permeability material, such as Mu-metal, eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of the house to the fields?
(A) If not:
(i) Is it because AC magnetic fields at the house would be too far from the cylinder to be drawn into/along the cylinder?
(a) If so, does this imply that magnetic fields are unlike solids/liquids/gasses in that enclosing them in a barrier (even one that has the electromagnetic property of high permeability) does not have any impact on the extent to which such fields travel through/outside the barrier?
(ii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would simply be too strong to be significantly mitigated by such a cylinder and would thus saturate the cylinder, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields escaping outside the cylinder and reaching the house?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the cylinder were thick enough, enclosing the powerline within it would indeed eliminate the exposure of the house to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
(iii) Or is such a cylinder shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in blocking the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current for some other reason?

**(2) Attic Horizontal Sheet**: If the right-hand rule for determining the direction of magnetic field lines indicates that the fields generated by the powerline's current are "entering" the house from above, would putting a horizontal sheet of high-permeability material in the attic directly above a particular room eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of that room to the fields generated by the powerline's current?
(A) If not:
(i) Is it because I'm misunderstanding the implications of the right-hand rule, and magnetic fields (or maybe just AC magnetic fields) don't, in fact, travel in a particular locational direction, per se?
(a) If so, does this imply that such an attempt at shielding would fail because the magnetic fields from the powerline current would still be entering the room not only through the walls, but also through the floor?
(ii) Or, alternatively, is it because while the magnetic fields from the powerline would be cut off from entering the room by the shielding sheet above the room, the fields would likely simply just "bulge" back into the room from the sides (through the walls, rather than through the ceiling) after traveling along the path of the sheet?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet extended far enough beyond the perimeter of the room in all directions, this "bulging" effect would be insufficient to cause the fields to reach the room, and thus that such shielding could in theory be effective?
(iii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would be too strong to significantly mitigate (even at a distance of 20 feet) and would thus saturate the sheet, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields then passing through it into the room?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were thick enough, placing it above the room would indeed eliminate the exposure of the room to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
(iv) Or is such a horizontal sheet shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in blocking the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current for some other reason?

**(3) Buried Vertical Sheet**: Would burying a vertical sheet of high-permeability material in the front yard of the house with the same width of a given room of the house, which is as tall as such room is deep, and which is buried deeply enough to intersect the magnetic field lines from the powerline that pass through such room, eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of that room to the fields generated by the powerline's current?
(A) If not:
(i) Is it because the intersection of the magnetic field lines by the vertical buried sheet occurs after the magnetic field lines have already passed through the room (from above, through the ceiling), per the right hand rule?
(a) If so, does this imply that such an attempt at shielding using a buried vertical sheet of high-permeability material could succeed with respect to shielding a home across the street from the home at issue from the fields generated by the current going through the powerline at issue, because such fields are entering that home across the street from below (through the floor), per the right-hand rule, and thus could be intercepted by a buried sheet before so entering?
(ii) Or, alternatively, is it because while the magnetic fields from the powerline would be diverted by being pulled forward away from the room by the buried shielding sheet, the fields would likely simply just "bulge" back into a path that takes them through the room by the time they entered the room?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were extended to be tall and wide enough in all directions beyond the field lines of the fields that would otherwise pass through the room, this "bulging" effect would be insufficient to cause the fields to reach the room, and thus that such shielding could in theory be effective?
(iii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would be too strong to significantly mitigate (even at significant distance from the powerline) and would thus saturate the sheet, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields that pass into the room remaining unaffected?
(a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were thick enough, placing it in the proper location would indeed eliminate the exposure of the room to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
(iv) Or is such a buried vertical sheet shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in redirecting the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current away from the room at issue for some other reason?

### Saichi — 2025-07-07T23:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355956>

I've not had to shield for magnetic fields luckily since the material's the most expensive of all. You should do a phone consult with SLT before buying their material.

I assume you already ruled out shared neutrals in your house as a cause?

### TB14 — 2025-07-08T00:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355959>

Yes, in my specific case with the Florida house, I'm pretty sure the elevated readings in the house are caused by a buried main utility feeder powerline in the front yard. I know exactly where the powerline is and the AC magnetic field readings increase on both the TriField TF2 and NFA1000 the closer I get to it, approaching from either side. The fields are much higher in every part of the front yard than in any part of the house. They decrease in strength proportionally to distance from the powerline in every part of the house and both the front and back yards that I've measured. There's no change in the readings when I flip off the whole-house breaker at the breaker panel.

I have wondered if there's anything the utility might be able to do to limit the fields coming off the powerline if I were to take the right approach to get them to do it. Not sure if there's any possibility that any relatively minor thing they might be willing to do like tightening the neutral/ground connections at the transformers near my house might improve the situation.

Even if not, I'm semi-optimistic that something can be done about it in terms of shielding, though. Just need to figure out the physics of it first to determine what would even work before trying to design a cost-effective plan.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-08T00:35:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355961>

What are the gauss readings in the front vs back of the house?

### TB14 — 2025-07-08T00:49:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355962>

It seems to depend significantly on the season, the time of day, and the weather that particular day, presumably since that largely dictates neighborhood power consumption. Today (relatively mild summer day) around 5:45 p.m.:
\- Directly over the powerline: ~7mG
\- Front door of the house (~20 feet away from the powerline): ~1.5mG
\- Back door of the house (~40 feet away from the powerline): ~1.0mG
\- Back yard twenty feet from the back door (~60 feet away from the powerline): ~0.75mG

### Saichi — 2025-07-08T02:09:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355965>

NFA1000 doesn't have a single axis reading option?

I just noticed G-iron flex has high corrosion resistance although I doubt it was intended for outdoor use in ground. Curious whether you'll be able to get a permit to shield the power line length either way, and how much of your legal expertise and time would require.

Building biologist would tell you to sell the house, but I'm all for extreme measures to mitigate.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-08T02:39:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355968>

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=355962)
>
> It seems to depend significantly on the season, the time of day, and the weather that particular day, presumably since that largely dictates neighborhood power consumption. Today (relatively mild summer day) around 5:45 p.m.:
> \- Directly over the powerline: ~7mG
> \- Front door of the house (~20 feet away from the powerline): ~1.5mG
> \- Back door of the house (~40 feet away from the powerline): ~1.0mG
> \- Back yard twenty feet from the back door (~60 feet away from the powerline): ~0.75mG

IF it can be shielded it will cost you $$$ with no guarantee ahead of time there will be a significant reduction.
Do you feel better when not there? Do you have young children?
YES - BBI says <0.2mG is “no concern” and .2mG - 1mG is “slight concern”. You are in their “severe” range.

### TB14 — 2025-07-08T03:47:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355971>

NFA1000 does have single-axis

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=355965)
>
> NFA1000 doesn't have a single axis reading option?
>
> I just noticed G-iron flex has high corrosion resistance although I doubt it was intended for outdoor use in ground. Curious whether you'll be able to get a permit to shield the power line length either way, and how much of your legal expertise and time would require.
>
> Building biologist would tell you to sell the house, but I'm all for extreme measures to mitigate.

NFA1000 does have single axis, though I've been using three-axis for these measurements & not sure how single axis would be helpful here. Totally agree on taking measures most would consider extreme to improve the health of the living space. It's the most important aspect of a house to me, so I just look at it like money that others would spend pointlessly upgrading the brand of their car or their wardrobe because they think they're impressing people, or at nightclubs trying to meet the type of people who I have no interest in meeting. Even if I move, the next place is probably going to have problems too - of course some places have more and worse problems than others, but there are also things I like about this place from a health perspective (lots of sun, relatively low RF, relatively quiet, relatively affordable which leaves me more money to spend on other health-related things), and moving is no walk in the park financially or time-wise either. Plus on a society-wide scale, my view is that people have got to deal with their problems instead of just figuring out how to pass them on to others - especially unsuspecting others. We are all dependent on each other at the end of the day. The fact that I'm aware of this problem probably means I'm more educated on this particular important issue than whoever I would sell the house to, but it doesn't necessarily mean I'm a smarter or more valuable person than them in general. I feel like I have somewhat of an obligation to at least try to address the problem rather than just kick it down the road to the next sap, especially since they're unlikely to be aware of it. Imagine if I sold the house because I was afraid of the health impacts of the magnetic fields from the power lines and the guy who bought it from me had no idea what magnetic fields were but turned out to have a pregnant wife whose kid wound up dying of leukemia because I only cared about the immediate impact of my actions on myself. Is that the type of society we want to live in? Ultimately it's short-term thinking to behave that way in my view because as I noted earlier we're all dependent on each other and anything I do for my own benefit that comes at a dramatically higher detriment to others should be presumed to come back and bite me in the ass in some way eventually through its negative impact on the people I hurt. Even if it's not true in every case, society is a much better place if as many people as possible behave as if it is (and are pressured into doing so, rather than encouraged to behave in the opposite way).

### Mat.I — 2025-07-08T03:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355972>

I am not sure I fully grasp the low-frequency AC magnetic field shielding mechanism of materials with high permeability and have some applied questions below involving three shielding scenarios hoping to increase my understanding (and also hoping this could be helpful to others as well). Answers to any of the below, even if not going through every piece of every question, would likely be super helpful.
>

The current going through this powerline generates AC magnetic fields in continuous circles with the powerline at the center of each circle, with the fields progressively decreasing in intensity with distance from the powerline. Now say you have a house 20 feet from this powerline that is exposed to the AC magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current.
>
> **(1) Powerline Cylinder Enclosure**: Would wrapping the portion of the powerline the current of which is giving off magnetic fields to which the house is exposed in a cylinder of high-permeability material, such as Mu-metal, eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of the house to the fields?
> (A) If not:
> (i) Is it because AC magnetic fields at the house would be too far from the cylinder to be drawn into/along the cylinder?
> (a) If so, does this imply that magnetic fields are unlike solids/liquids/gasses in that enclosing them in a barrier (even one that has the electromagnetic property of high permeability) does not have any impact on the extent to which such fields travel through/outside the barrier?
> (ii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would simply be too strong to be significantly mitigated by such a cylinder and would thus saturate the cylinder, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields escaping outside the cylinder and reaching the house?
> (a) If so, does this imply that if the cylinder were thick enough, enclosing the powerline within it would indeed eliminate the exposure of the house to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
> (iii) Or is such a cylinder shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in blocking the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current for some other reason?
>
> **(2) Attic Horizontal Sheet**: If the right-hand rule for determining the direction of magnetic field lines indicates that the fields generated by the powerline's current are "entering" the house from above, would putting a horizontal sheet of high-permeability material in the attic directly above a particular room eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of that room to the fields generated by the powerline's current?
> (A) If not:
> (i) Is it because I'm misunderstanding the implications of the right-hand rule, and magnetic fields (or maybe just AC magnetic fields) don't, in fact, travel in a particular locational direction, per se?
> (a) If so, does this imply that such an attempt at shielding would fail because the magnetic fields from the powerline current would still be entering the room not only through the walls, but also through the floor?
> (ii) Or, alternatively, is it because while the magnetic fields from the powerline would be cut off from entering the room by the shielding sheet above the room, the fields would likely simply just "bulge" back into the room from the sides (through the walls, rather than through the ceiling) after traveling along the path of the sheet?
> (a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet extended far enough beyond the perimeter of the room in all directions, this "bulging" effect would be insufficient to cause the fields to reach the room, and thus that such shielding could in theory be effective?
> (iii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would be too strong to significantly mitigate (even at a distance of 20 feet) and would thus saturate the sheet, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields then passing through it into the room?
> (a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were thick enough, placing it above the room would indeed eliminate the exposure of the room to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
> (iv) Or is such a horizontal sheet shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in blocking the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current for some other reason?
>
> **(3) Buried Vertical Sheet**: Would burying a vertical sheet of high-permeability material in the front yard of the house with the same width of a given room of the house, which is as tall as such room is deep, and which is buried deeply enough to intersect the magnetic field lines from the powerline that pass through such room, eliminate or significantly reduce the exposure of that room to the fields generated by the powerline's current?
> (A) If not:
> (i) Is it because the intersection of the magnetic field lines by the vertical buried sheet occurs after the magnetic field lines have already passed through the room (from above, through the ceiling), per the right hand rule?
> (a) If so, does this imply that such an attempt at shielding using a buried vertical sheet of high-permeability material could succeed with respect to shielding a home across the street from the home at issue from the fields generated by the current going through the powerline at issue, because such fields are entering that home across the street from below (through the floor), per the right-hand rule, and thus could be intercepted by a buried sheet before so entering?
> (ii) Or, alternatively, is it because while the magnetic fields from the powerline would be diverted by being pulled forward away from the room by the buried shielding sheet, the fields would likely simply just "bulge" back into a path that takes them through the room by the time they entered the room?
> (a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were extended to be tall and wide enough in all directions beyond the field lines of the fields that would otherwise pass through the room, this "bulging" effect would be insufficient to cause the fields to reach the room, and thus that such shielding could in theory be effective?
> (iii) Or, alternatively, is it because the magnetic fields generated by a powerline would be too strong to significantly mitigate (even at significant distance from the powerline) and would thus saturate the sheet, with the remaining large majority of the magnetic fields that pass into the room remaining unaffected?
> (a) If so, does this imply that if the sheet were thick enough, placing it in the proper location would indeed eliminate the exposure of the room to the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current?
> (iv) Or is such a buried vertical sheet shield unlikely to be successful or certain to be unsuccessful in redirecting the magnetic fields generated by the powerline's current away from the room at issue for some other reason?

If you do not mind me asking - what is your background?
So we understand this issue:
\- have you got a services diagram? In Australia we have a service called dial before you dig that provides utility service diagrams. **This is extremely important as this alone will give you valuable insight on the type of cable/s + trunking used.**
\- So the issue is from the underground main? I assume you only have a single phase (or what the US refers to as split phase) connection to your house?
\- have you got any easements on your property for the underground main?? Where is the actual main?
\- is there a transformer near by?
\- how old is this area?? e.g. contemporary primary supply lines (modern XLPE's multi conductors) vs older say ~ 30 years ago (singles with separate conduits) - more distances between conductors = more mag field, due to inadequate (weaker) cancellation.. That is they add more rather than cancel... Ties back to Ampre's law and superstition..

If you provide more info - I will be able to give you my two cents...

Even just 10 feet farther back would cause a significant reduction in magnetic fields in the house and both yards, plus while they were re-doing it maybe they'd be kind enough to put the conductors closer together for even greater reduction thru cancellation (though perhaps not if I was only able to get them to move it by threatening to sue if they didn't).

Totally agree on taking measures most would consider extreme to improve the health of the living space. It's the most important aspect of a house to me, so I just look at it like money that others would spend pointlessly upgrading the brand of their car or their wardrobe because they think they're impressing people, or at nightclubs trying to meet the type of people who I have no interest in meeting. Even if I move, the next place is probably going to have problems too - of course some places have more and worse problems than others, but there are also things I like about this place from a health perspective (lots of sun, relatively low RF, relatively quiet, relatively affordable which leaves me more money to spend on other health-related things), and moving is no walk in the park financially or time-wise either. Plus on a society-wide scale, my view is that people have got to deal with their problems instead of just figuring out how to pass them on to others - especially unsuspecting others. We are all dependent on each other at the end of the day. The fact that I'm aware of this problem probably means I'm more educated on this particular important issue than whoever I would sell the house to, but it doesn't necessarily mean I'm a smarter or more valuable person than them in general. I feel like I have somewhat of an obligation to at least try to address the problem rather than just kick it down the road to the next sap, especially since they're unlikely to be aware of it. Imagine if I sold the house because I was afraid of the health impacts of the magnetic fields from the power lines and the guy who bought it from me had no idea what magnetic fields were but turned out to have a pregnant wife whose kid wound up dying of leukemia because I only cared about the immediate impact of my actions on myself. Is that the type of society we want to live in? Ultimately it's short-term thinking to behave that way in my view because as I noted earlier we're all dependent on each other and anything I do for my own benefit that comes at a dramatically higher detriment to others should be presumed to come back and bite me in the ass in some way eventually through its negative impact on the people I hurt. Even if it's not true in every case, society is a much better place if as many people as possible behave as if it is (and are pressured into doing so, rather than encouraged to behave in the opposite way).

You're much more ethical than most of the lawyers I've known. Have you measured around your neighborhood to see if m fields are similar throughout on the sidewalk?

### Mat.I — 2025-07-08T04:44:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355977>

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=355971)
>
> NFA1000 does have single axis, though I've been using three-axis for these measurements & not sure how single axis would be helpful here

the 3axis will give you the vector sum e.g. total = square root of x2 + y2 + z2.. The single axis can assist with tracking the direction and source identification etc.. Also, it can provide indirect insight into net currents as this is typically indicative of quite directional fields etc.. Mag fields are rotational (concentric loops), however at locations the field has a clear spatial direction

### Mat.I — 2025-07-08T05:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-355984>

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=355975)
>
> I have an undergraduate physics degree but then became a lawyer, so some (rusty) theoretical familiarity with electromagnetism, but no applied technical experience.

Good to know as you seem well versed and quite detailed on the theory. You would certainly give most so called building biologist a run for their money with the theory side alone...

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=355975)
>
> I don't have my hands on a diagram yet but will look into getting one. We have a service called 811 here that's meant to be dialed before digging and they usually come and spraypaint the lawn to mark the location of utility lines, but I don't know if they provide diagrams - will look into it. I was hoping they could at least also tell me how deep the lines are buried in addition to marking where they are, but sounds like knowledge of the precise type of equipment they have down there could be useful here, too?

This is important as just based on a desktop assessment you could determine the likely cause. As you have dated the development circa 85 it is highly likely the underground main would be run in singles within separate conduits with distances between the lines (e.g. one for each phase A, B, C + N). This arrangement would cause very high fields.. Vs say more contemporary approach with multi conductor cable within one conduit..

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=355975)
>
> Yes, the fields are coming from the underground main. The main branches off to a pad-mounted transformer, from which a line then runs (also underground) to my house, but this shorter line between the transformer and my house isn't the one from which the fields are emanating, it's the main before the transformer.

Noted. Yes, the secondaries downstream from the pad mount would feed a few houses. This makes sense as this would likely be ran in triplex or the like..

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=355975)
>
> The main runs underground across my front yard parallel to the front wall of my house at a distance of about 20 feet from the front door. I assume the utility has a valid easement to put it there, but actually haven't looked into it. That actually might be an idea because I think the standard max distance for utility easements here is 10 feet from the road, and if they are within 10 feet it would have to be just barely, so maybe I could call them out on that (if that is indeed the rule) and get them to move the main back farther away. Even just 10 feet farther back would cause a significant reduction in magnetic fields in the house and both yards, plus while they were re-doing it maybe they'd be kind enough to put the conductors closer together for even greater reduction thru cancellation (though perhaps not if I was only able to get them to move it by threatening to sue if they didn't).

You need your property survey documents and compare to the actual cable path if the easement is detailed.. You would need the services marked and an accurate survey to compare with. Re: main cable configuration & type - I would reach out to the utility company if the 811 service you referred to does not provide this as this information would be required by contractors if carrying out work within that area.. They would hold the "as-built" drawings.. If confirmed the service is in fact ok and not outside the easement boundary - check if there are any planned maintenance or upgrades to the primary feeders.. As these cables may be approach end of service life.. Failing that if you wanted to fund a short section of upgrade you would need to jump through lots of hoops and would come at a cost..

Received the following map from the utility (Duke Energy Florida). Looks like they actually have a 120V line coming from the pad-mounted transformer in parallel with the 7200V main both going through my front yard. That 120V line also then appears to join up with the 7200V main after passing in front of the house one farther down the line from mine.

Regarding line replacement, they said their policy is not to replace anything until it's broken. When I asked how much it would be if I waned to pay for it, they quoted me $100,000 - $300,000. I replied asking how they arrived at that estimate but thus far they've ignored me.
Wondering if in light of the arrangement depicted below it might make sense to offer to pay for a transformer in front of the neighbor's house in an effort to get them to remove (or at least put out of service) the 120V line running across my front yard. My thinking is that perhaps that 120V line (which is slightly closer to my house than the 7200V main) is only there for that one neighbor's house, and thus if they had a transformer of their own coming off the main in front of their house, too, the utility wouldn't need to run a significant portion of their service line across my yard, which is what I'm guessing is currently happening. But I'm not sure if the 120V line could be a significant contributor to my readings or if it's more likely to be the 7200V main that's causing problems - I would think it's likely the latter not only because of the higher voltage but also because I didn't notice any significant drop-off in AC magnetic fields past that neighbor's house on the NFA1000 when walking down the street.

### Saichi — 2025-08-05T18:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-357631>

As Mat already noted, it's the three phases in the distribution line 120 deg apart and whatever physical distance separating the phases causing the field.

I just noticed SLT significantly lowered the price on their g iron flex at some point if I'm remembering correctly used to be at least double the price. Looks like around 3k would be enough to shield your house for the shielding material of course not including a casing for it to prevent corrosion assuming necessary and labor costs to bury it along the distribution line.

### Saichi — 2025-08-05T19:47:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-357641>

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=355962)
>
> It seems to depend significantly on the season, the time of day, and the weather that particular day, presumably since that largely dictates neighborhood power consumption. Today (relatively mild summer day) around 5:45 p.m.:
> \- Directly over the powerline: ~7mG
> \- Front door of the house (~20 feet away from the powerline): ~1.5mG
> \- Back door of the house (~40 feet away from the powerline): ~1.0mG
> \- Back yard twenty feet from the back door (~60 feet away from the powerline): ~0.75mG

Looking at your levels again respect to distance from distribution line I'm almost certain you have other issues causing the majority of fields within your home. Have you used a clamp meter to measure the current on your water pipes, ground rod and cable line? What does the NFA1000 measure when close to either?

I recommend not touching any metal carrying current without insulators since it's ionizing in effect and much worse than the fields themselves.

### TB14 — 2025-08-07T06:11:50+0300

The drop-off is steady and proportional given distance from the line, though. Like if I walk from one side of my house to the other, parellel to the line, the levels are basically the same. But if I move farther away from the line, from the front toward the back, the fields drop off. And the same is true in the yards. There's no increase within the house relative to the outside, either. I.e., the levels on the front porch and five feet in front of the front porch are dramatically higher than anywhere I've measured within the house.

One thing that occurred to me recently is that I assume blocking magnetic fields by blocking at the house (i.e., by putting plates in the attic) would cut off the Earth's DC magnetic field as well, which I think would probably be bad. I'm leaning away from shielding at the house at the moment, if I were to pursue shielding I think I would try to do it at some point away from the house, but not sure if a cylinder around each of the conductors or something like that would work from a physical perspective - perhaps it would, just need to think about it a bit more.
