### Saichi — 2025-08-07T08:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-357727>

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=357721)
>
> The drop-off is steady and proportional given distance from the line, though. Like if I walk from one side of my house to the other, parellel to the line, the levels are basically the same. But if I move farther away from the line, from the front toward the back, the fields drop off. And the same is true in the yards. There's no increase within the house relative to the outside, either. I.e., the levels on the front porch and five feet in front of the front porch are dramatically higher than anywhere I've measured within the house.

You measured a 79% drop in levels from power line to front door 20 feet and then only a 33% drop another 20 feet which doesn't conform to inverse square law. These were the measurements you took with the main service breaker off correct, just your DC home that doesn't have one right? The photo of the page I posted above clearly states can have just a 0.1 mG drop in field from front to back porch but even in that situation the source can't be purely an external power line but parallel neutral or neutral ground faults occurring whether within or without the home's wiring's obviously the question.

I just noticed you mentioned before the house is made of concrete which conducts some current moreso in heat/humidity of Florida. Even if your home were wired correctly, I have no idea if the concrete's conductivity would make decoupling from objectionable current coming from outside the house's wiring a possibility and whether it's enough to create the mag levels you currently have.

I have only experienced a slow drop in levels from parallel neutrals within my home. This has me curious enough I'll attempt to take readings with a ladder, tape measure, and Alpha Labs meter from the overhead power line outside tomorrow with respect to distance.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-07T09:01:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-357730>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=357727)
>
> You measured a 79% drop in levels from power line to front door 20 feet and then only a 33% drop another 20 feet which doesn't conform to inverse square law.

Maybe for point sources/smaller sections yes, however, possible separated HV singles (long line sources) buried would decay differently. The day to day variability is likely due to the neighbourhood load.

### TB14 — 2025-08-07T09:37:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-357731>

Will ask the utility for the cable legend. Another complicating factor might be the proximity of the secondary to one or more of the primary conductors. But yes, the reduction in the strength of the fields absolutely does significantly decelerate with distance from the lines. But that is true outside as well as in. For example if the field is 7 mG directly over the powerlines at a given time, then probably two feet away from them, it's already down to ~4 mG. Another ten feet and it's only down to like ~2 mG. Towards the back of the back yard, I can walk like 100 feet in the direction away from the powerline and the levels will only go from ~0.5 mG to ~0.4 mG. There is a fairly large lake at the edge of the property's back yard, and given (a) the steepness of the drop-off in the rate of decline and (b) the magnitude of the fields even at the edge of the lake on my property, I've found myself wondering if the impact zone where the fields would be measurable from the lines in my front yard actually stretches all the way to the other side of the lake (probably a quarter mile from the source)

### Saichi — 2025-08-07T17:23:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-357752>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=357730)
>
> Maybe for point sources/smaller sections yes, however, possible separated HV singles (long line sources) buried would decay differently. The day to day variability is likely due to the neighbourhood load.

I just did some quick measurements outside without a ladder, my neighbor's property is where the overhead HV lines end and I measured a 0.1 drop (0.7 to 0.6) bringing my meter down just little over two feet from head to stomach level underneath where the transformer is.

I took same head to stomach measurements under only the 240V line next to my house and it was 0.6 to 0.5 which isn't significantly different than the HV lines. The HV lines are a few feet higher than the residential line of course.

### S Hudson — 2025-08-07T21:54:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-357801>

The number of new builds in the housing developments near me that are being placed under existing power lines is staggering.

### TB14 — 2025-12-04T19:49:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-questions-shielding-magnetic-fields-from-powerlines.31260/post-363590>

Update on this, and seemingly good news of some sort - the AC magnetic fields in my home in Ocala have decreased many-fold since I measured this summer. I believe this is most likely either because it's now winter and people are using A/C less, meaning this would be a much better situation in the winter here than in the summer, or, even more optimistically, because the power company did something in response to my request/complaint. As I sit here on my couch mid-day, AC magnetic fields read 0.03 mG on the NFA1000 - the same meter in the same spot at the same time of day almost always read over 1.00 mG six months ago, during summer.

In terms of my interactions with the power company - after I got in touch with them, they sent a guy out to the house with an AC gaussmeter. Nice guy, though clearly working for the utility - he turned my microwave on and held the gaussmeter like a foot away from it to get a "comparison" value to "compare" against the ambient fields pervading the entire home from the buried powerline(s) in the front yard. He hypothesized that the problem was related to some sort of back-up lower-voltage line (not the service drop to my house, but a reserve line of some sort, present in case the main one failed) that he thought might be somehow interacting with the higher-voltage primary lines. A few months elapsed with no activity from the power company after this visit, so I reached out to them again, and some time thereafter, I heard from someone who was in the house that utility employees arrived in two trucks and spent some time around the pad-mounted transformer. I came down to the house for the first time since August earlier this week, and every time I've measured, it's been dramatically lower. So either the power company did something, which I lean towards being the most likely scenario given the apparent activity by the power company after my complaint at what I assume would be the right spot to bring about such changes, or it's just ~20- - 35- fold less intense during winter due to lack of A/C use, which is also plausible to me given the intensity of Florida summer heat and people keeping their HVACs entirely off here during winter, which I would expect to create a big disparity, although maybe not 30+-fold (my power bill certainly doesn't drop 30-fold in the winter...).

# Thread: Ocean Water and Sunlight

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6

### Alain Dwight — 2025-07-08T05:45:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-water-and-sunlight.31265/post-355986>

Sunlight is good and being in the ocean is good, but is there a trade off? Does being in the Ocean water interfere with the body's ability to pick up the proper signals from the sun?

### Mat.I — 2025-07-08T05:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-water-and-sunlight.31265/post-355988>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=355986)
>
> Sunlight is good and being in the ocean is good, but is there a trade off? Does being in the Ocean water interfere with the body's ability to pick up the proper signals from the sun?

I would have thought diving would, however normally swimming or being in the shallows would not? Depends on n=1 context I think. As if someone wanted to maximise UV exposure (large area) & POMC etc you would ensure you are balancing in water vs out I guess or staying in the shallows.

### AlbertD — 2025-07-08T05:58:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-water-and-sunlight.31265/post-355989>

The eccrine glands in our feet (sweat glands) unique to humans are designed to absorb electron-rich water from the sea and excrete sodium. This influx of electrons can help hydrate the discs of our spine, which has no blood supply, yet must remain hydrated to function properly.

Our feet are literally a built-in reverse osmosis system.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-08T07:42:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-water-and-sunlight.31265/post-355991>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=355986)
>
> Sunlight is good and being in the ocean is good, but is there a trade off? Does being in the Ocean water interfere with the body's ability to pick up the proper signals from the sun?

Interesting thoughts.
We humans can be in water for a length of time (swimming) but under the water (deep) for a limited amount of time.
Me thinks the energetic properties of the water (grounding), without ANY nnEMF would be a net benefit.
What are the penetration levels of NIR & UV in shallow water?

### Mat.I — 2025-07-08T07:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-water-and-sunlight.31265/post-355992>

UV and IR (water is a heat sink) would take the hardest hit.. The optical window of water is highly in the vis spectrum and sits ~ 450nm to 550nm hence the colour of the ocean and certain marine life adapting to it (eyes are tuned to it). You can calculate this with online calculators or do the math manually via the beer-lambert law.

### Saichi — 2025-07-08T17:52:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-water-and-sunlight.31265/post-356007>

Sunbathing and ocean bathing should both be done in safe spaces.

Either the mouse was poisoned and both ate from it, or they have marek's?

Unfortunately they put the chicks together away in a seperate compartment WITHOUT propper daylight.
We think it might be marek's but are not sure.

However if it is marek best is maybe to kill them to protect the other chickens?

One of them was bought elsewhere the little one is own breed. own breed = def no vaccines etc.
The other one = not sure.

I published a short book telling my story about health issues accrued from my Tesla (and EMF in general), with the intention of spreading physical copies throughout the world

[Amazon.com](https://a.co/d/flpmhcs)

I made it 80 pages so it is cheap to print ($3 each for author copies)

I want to find people interested in spreading copies around their area, or during their travels, to help prevent EVs from becoming ubiquitous (and as a result making it impossible to rent a gas car, or get a non-EV Uber, or eventually even find gas for a reasonable cost)

You can DM me your address and I will share the total cost $3/copy + small shipping cost)

I'm starting with 300 copies myself and spreading them around my favorite places - Iceland and Costa Rica. In particular I need people in Scandanavia since they are in the most imminant danger from EV effects and adoption rate

### AlbertW — 2025-07-13T06:58:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spreading-ev-danger.31281/post-356242>

I read your book in Kindle format. While Jack’s content is still the go-to for the deepest technical details, I think you did a great job laying out all the most important points in an easily understandable manner.

My main complaint is that I was hoping to get some details on typical car construction and what components to pay attention to in terms of contributing the most in EMF magnitude

I’m curious what car you drive now. I was just in my Tesla today and realized there is massive amounts of RF coming from the rear view mirror area. In the book you talk about ac magnetic fields a lot. Perhaps you can avoid some of it with a gas car, but I’ve seen measurements of very high M fields even in gas cars because there are plenty of wiring runs where neutral isn’t run parallel to hot for 100% of the length. All modern cars have tons of electronics regardless of whether it is EV. I’ve thus seen that the Teslas actually tend to face pretty decent AC M fields compared to many gas cars, counterintuitively.

The RF part I would also assume is not specific to EVs, as plenty of cars are connected to cell networks, WiFi, Bluetooth, etc.

Is the only solution to buy a very old car with minimal electronics? What did you end up doing?

### AlbertW — 2025-07-13T17:32:49+0300

I have been eating seafood daily and have been doing my best to resist excessive carbs (even tho I have quite a sweets craving). I’ve emailed both **[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])** and **[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])** three times since June 30. Each time, I received confirmation that the messages were delivered, but I haven’t received any replies.

I'm sending messages from ProtonMail, and I’ve checked my spam folders—nothing there. I’m hesitant to keep following up unnecessarily, but I’m also unsure how else to get assistance at this point. Repeating the same approach without a response is starting to feel a bit futile.

Thanks in advance for any guidance.

>  www.england.nhs.uk

### [UK concludes biggest pandemic simulation with virus targeting children](https://www.disclose.tv/id/38675lannj/)

Breaking news from around the world.

 www.disclose.tv

### UK concludes biggest pandemic simulation with virus targeting children​

SUMMARY
This autumn, the UK conducted Exercise Pegasus, the country’s biggest pandemic simulation in nearly a decade and its first Tier 1 national emergency exercise since Covid-19. Concluding last month, it involved ministers, all devolved nations, COBRA activation and every major government department.Participants faced a fictional novel strain of enterovirus D68 (EV-D68) that originated on the imaginary southeast Asian island of Musiyana after a food festival.

Unlike Covid-19, the simulated virus primarily killed infants, children and teenagers, causing respiratory failure, brain swelling and occasional paralysis. The three-phase drill began in September with regional spread in Asia, progressed in October to a WHO-declared pandemic with school closures, hospital strain and street protests over social distancing, then escalated in November to national lockdown, non-essential business shutdowns, travel bans and fears of food shortages from potential pig herd infection.

Sir Peter Horby, Director of the Pandemic Sciences Institute at Oxford University said:

> “The choice of an enterovirus is a good choice because it is a real possibility with real risks but is different from what we have seen before,”

Ministers tested both containment and mitigation decisions, including whether early travel restrictions could keep the virus out of Britain. The exercise followed Covid Inquiry findings that pre-2020 plans focused only on coping with high deaths rather than prevention, and that school closures “brought ordinary childhood to a halt”.

Lessons from Pegasus will be published next year.

### 0.66eV Eva — 2025-07-16T19:19:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-356427>

Hi,

To start face dunking, I bought a Etekcity Lasergrip 1080 Laser Thermometer Digital Infrared Thermometer Temperature Gun for Kitchen Cooking BBQ Grill and Bath Water, -58℉~1130℉ (-50℃～610℃). I saw some people on this forum bought similar products and use them to test their skin temperature and the water temperature.

But after reading the directions, I doubt I can use that for the skin safely. I may keep it to test water temperature or return it and buy a simpler, non infrared thermometer for the face dunk and cold plunge.

I'd love to receive some suggestions from people here for a good quality skin thermometer.

Thank you!

### Woodstock — 2025-07-18T17:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-356549>

I saw some people on this forum bought similar products and use them to test their skin temperature and the water temperature.

>

>
> Thank you!

I don’t have a good suggestion because I only tested the water and not skin when I tried face dunking. I didn’t think I was overdoing it. But I ended up getting a sinus infection from face dunking. I am sure the bacteria was already in my sinus. But the face dunking possibly messed with my immune system. I was afraid to cold plunge after that but I ended up trying it in El Salvador and it was great. If 55 degrees is too cold, I still found benefit at higher temps and then gradually lower or add more ice after 5 minutes. If you feel really uncomfortable just get out and warm up. Just keep at it and you will eventually be able to go longer and colder. 55 is plenty cold for me though! Otherwise my joints in hands and feet ache.

### 0.66eV Eva — 2025-07-20T01:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-356624>

I realised I have been too credulous about the warnings in the directions!!! It makes me understand how much brainfog I have. I receive way more infrared from my IR-red panel than from this thermometer. And this thermometer works great! Yesterday was my 2nd day and at one time my forehead went to 54.3°F. I do many very short consecutive dunks during a few minutes (3-5min) because my forehead hurts from the cold...from dehydration (I'm sure) and possibly still too much omega-6.

I had not thought about the possibility of sinus infection. OUch! I'm glad that you enjoy it now. Are you still in El Salvador? So you are in the cold plunges phase of the CT protocol?

Thank you [@Woodstock](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/32840/) !!

### Woodstock — 2025-07-20T16:57:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-356659>

I am back in Boulder for a few months. I only did a week of face dunks last fall before getting sick and didn’t want to continue that method. I did my first cold plunge in El Salvador in March. It felt great in the heat. I dunk in to the top of my neck but I don’t put my head in the cold water. I have been increasing the time I stay in at my own pace. I think it’s good to tune into your own body and what it’s telling you as we are all different. The instructions are a general guide. I think when I found a longer blog on CT, it explained a lot of different methods and I realized there are many ways to do it! In the book Epi-Paleo it says to check your VO2 Max before doing CT. I asked a bunch of mitos and no one had ever gotten the test done. So….I had been afraid to do it but then realized that no one else was checking this. The danger is when people go too cold for too long. I’ve seen people push it and it was kind of scary to watch. I suggest slow and steady wins the race. I would just get a cold plunge off of Amazon and get started based on my experience. No need to go below 55 degrees in my opinion but everyone is different.

### Woodstock — 2026-03-01T04:17:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-and-water-thermometers.31322/post-367834>

I don't ever face dunk because I don't want another sinus infection. my husband has a chronic one for 30 years. I think I'm too vulnerable. i haven't done a cold plunge in a while. i think stretching out in a full tub would be better because my knees usually hurt after a cold plunge that is vertical. i don't know if I should be doing them or not. but when in a very hot climate like El Salvador it's certainly much easier. and is invigorating! I took a break because I'm not really sure if all the bacterial infections like Lyme, Bartonella, EBV, Step, Staph, etc may be migrating during the plunges. makes my joints hurt more. maybe switching from hot tub to cold plunge would be a good thing for me? I don't know and I left Colorado and have no plans to return for more than a couple weeks at a time to purge more items and see my family briefly but not long enough to make my vitamin D plummet. i will have to be more diligent about testing before and after if I go back. but I'm at least going as far as Fiji in a couple months to sail and dive. see the world. or at least some cool underwater reefs that are still alive! before it's too late and the evil people destroy it all. El Salvador is a great place to get SCUBA certified with La Libertad diving here in Atami/Palmarcito area. He does it one on one. great for challenged brains. never thought I could do SCUBA. I am breaking through all my barriers! unstoppable!

1) Talks about MS and benefits of UV light therapy
2) "The new studies offer tantalizing hints that UV therapy might also work for other autoimmune diseases such as type 1 diabetes, rheumatoid arthritis, Crohn’s disease and colitis."
3) Discusses Vit D
4) "Hart says. 'UV light calms inflammation in the skin. But it also then calms inflammation in the central nervous system. It’ll calm inflammation in the pancreas and the gut. So I think it’s not fully realized the potential it has to be a controller of body homeostasis.'"

# Thread: Non Mesh Hernia Surgery at Shouldice Hospital
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-mesh-hernia-surgery-at-shouldice-hospital.31366/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FDDio — 2025-07-23T01:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-mesh-hernia-surgery-at-shouldice-hospital.31366/post-356806>

For anyone considering Hernia Surgery, I highly recommend looking into Shouldice Hernia Hospital in Toronto. They are globally recognized for their non-mesh hernia surgeries, meaning no foreign mesh is placed in your body. The hospital itself is more like a resort, offering nice amenities and good food for a pseudo-centralized facility over a typical 3 nt stay. The main building, originally a historic estate residence converted into a private hospital in 1953, benefits from being an older structure with minimal EMF levels (less than 1 mG) in the semi-private (2-person) patient rooms. Additionally, there were no ceiling lights in the rooms, just a single fluorescent light bulb behind the bed that was easily dealt with.

The expansive grounds provide lush grass for earthing and numerous benches to enjoy the morning sunrise and evening sunset, though unfortunately, patients aren't allowed outside on the day of surgery (tho I tried!).

Surprisingly, while at the hospital, I had many insightful conversations with fellow patients who were well-versed in the drawbacks of centralized medicine and government policies. It was incredibly rewarding, especially after being thanked for introducing some to Uncle Jack. It was refreshing to find like-minded individuals and engage in meaningful discussions, which can be rare in our everyday lives.

### PIAZ — 2025-07-23T01:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-mesh-hernia-surgery-at-shouldice-hospital.31366/post-356809>

Thank you for that. My husband will soon have hernia surgery, and Doc is saying: "mesh." Being easier for him to "put his head in the sand" I am researching, but not many options. I will send him the direction of Shouldice. Very timely, and appreciated.

### FDDio — 2025-07-23T01:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-mesh-hernia-surgery-at-shouldice-hospital.31366/post-356810>

You are most welcome. Everyone I spoke to at the Shouldice was very pleased they went there and the common saying was _"if you have to get a Hernia surgery, this is the place to be."_ However, keep in mind they have fabulous results (and have the data to back it) but that is because they only take fit individuals. If anyone is overweight, they tell you how many pounds you need to loose before they will operate on you. I was one of the fortunate ones that did not have to lose weight, but I had other (past) issues that they were extremely cautious about (since they don't have regular hospital emerge room facilities on the premise).

They website is pretty comprehensive but if you/your husband have any questions after browsing through it, feel free to ask away. I am 6 days post op and not any more pain or discomfort, tho I'm still avoiding lifting anything that's somewhat heavy.

# Thread: Dr Kruse and Gym Bros
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-and-gym-bros.31370/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Base_Strength — 2025-07-23T19:32:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-and-gym-bros.31370/post-356862>

Dr Kruse, I’ve been following your posts slamming “gym bros” and I totally get where you’re coming from...indoor artificial environments, junk light, hypertrophy obsession, endless output, Bluetooth tech, and total disconnect from circadian biology.

That said, as a barbell strength coach, I’m curious where you’d place the kind of training I do with clients. It’s definitely not bodybuilding or aesthetics-based. I don’t coach powerlifters either.

My focus is helping regular people get stronger using foundational human movement patterns: squat, deadlift, over head press, and bench press performed with low reps, long rest, and a linear progression model. Getting stronger each week as measured by the weight on the barbell.

No music. No tech. No chronic cardio. Just short, focused strength training in a garage with the door open. I go out of the garage in between sets and get direct sunlight and ground and encourage my clients to do the same.

I would argue that strength is the final way we interact with our environment. My goal is to help people double their strength in 3 months and triple it in 6, which I’ve seen drastically improve quality of life. Get in the sun and be strong!

So I’m wondering if this approach still falls into the “gym bro” category from your perspective? What I see in the gym are people who are chronic exercisers but not strength training.

Appreciate everything you share. It’s shaped how I think about health in a big way.

### DLO — 2025-07-24T08:13:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-and-gym-bros.31370/post-356889>

How many times have you heard Jack mention movenat?

just did a full blood panel for first time in many years...wondering if anyone has advice about elevated levels of TGAB? Apparently you should have no Thyroglobulin Antibodies (TgAb) but I have a level of 117 IU/ML...reading the research is a bit concerning as this could indicate anything from thyroid cancer to various autoimmune diseases...I am still waiting for the other results, but one encouraging result is that my Thyroid Peroxidase Antibodies (TPO) is low at only 2.
I eat a seemingly perfect diet, get plenty of the sun exposure, exercise, etc...so not sure what I may be doing wrong or if I should be concerned. Any insights are most welcomed.

### aunty shelly — 2025-07-27T02:09:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-resonance-magnetic-analyzer.31384/post-357025>

The human body is the aggregates of a large number of cells which are in continuous growth, development, differentiation, regeneration and apoptosis, and the cells constantly self-renew through its own division. 25 million cells are divided at one second in an adult’s body, and the body's blood cells constantly renew at the rate of about 100 million per minute. In the process of cell division and growth, those charged bodies of atomic nucleuses constituting atoms as the basic unit of cell and the electrons outside the nucleus are in constant high-speed movement and changing, thereby constantly emitting electromagnetic waves.

The electromagnetic wave signals emitted by the human body represent the specific state of the human body, and the emitted electromagnetic wave signals are different under the different conditions of the human body, such as health, sub-health, disease, etc. If we can determine these specific electromagnetic wave signals, we can determine the status of the body's life.

Quantum medicine considers that the most fundamental reason of falling sick is that the spin of electrons outside the atomic nucleus and the orbit change, thereby causing the change of atoms constituting a material, the change of small biomolecules, the change of big biomolecules, the change of all the cells and finally the change of organs. Because the electron is a charged body, when the spin of electrons outside the atomic nucleus and the orbit change, the electromagnetic wave emitted by the atoms will change. The energy o f the electromagnetic wave changes caused by the changes of the human body’s diseases and physical changes in the nutritional status is extremely weak and usually is only nano gauss to microgauss. The frequency and energy of the weak magnetic field of hair determined directly or by holding a sensor by hand compare with the resonance spectra of standard quantum of diseases and nutrition indicators set in the instrument after the frequency and energy are amplified by the instrument and processed by the computer, and then the corresponding quantum value being from negative to positive is output. The size of the quantum value indicates the nature and extent of the disease and the nutrition levels. Finally, the test results are resolved by clinicians. For example, cancer cells are different from normal cells, and the electromagnetic waves emitted by cancer cells are also different from the electromagnetic waves emitted by normal cells. Quantum resonance testing tumor is to send the standard wave of cancer cells to the specimen. If there are cancer cells in the human body, resonance will occur, and the instrument will detect the signal. The more the number of cancer cells is, the more intense the signal is, and the quantum value tends to the negative value. If there are no cancer cells, resonance will do not occur, and the quantum value tends to the positive value. It's similar to the principle of listening to broadcasting from the radio. There are many radio waves in the air. If you want to listen to some designated broadcasting, you can transfer the radio to the corresponding frequency, at this moment, resonance occurs, so that you can listen to this broadcasting. Quantum resonance uses this principle for testing.

This is what it says in the software, comments appreciated

# Thread: Cold Plunge/Exposure for someone of Middle Eastern Descent

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1

### fouadfo1 — 2025-07-28T21:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-plunge-exposure-for-someone-of-middle-eastern-descent.31391/post-357112>

Hello everyone, I needed some advice. So long story short, I was born and raised in the US. Have been living around the 42nd latitude essentially all of my life in the north east.

My parents are both of Yemeni descent. Their parents as well were both of Yemeni descent. Additionally, according to my dad, there was no race mixing for at least several generations prior to his parents as well. Generally speaking, the Yemeni people do not often race mix, even with the surrounding Arab ethnicities for reasons beyond the scope of this post. Essentially, my genetics should be fairly homogeneous.

So to my question, will someone with my genetic makeup benefit from cold plunging/cold exposure as a means to compensate for the loss of sun especially during the winter time? I recall Dr Kruse mentioning that certain people may not benefit from cold exposure because their genetics are not really wired or evolved to utilize or benefit from such practices. I understand that ideally, I should be where I belong , but I am working hard to ensure that within my 5 year plan I will be at a more agreeable latitude, but for now I have to do the best that I can given my life’s chess board.

Thank you all in advance!

# Thread: Zoom Alternative
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zoom-alternative.31392/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BiohackersTO — 2025-07-29T01:40:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zoom-alternative.31392/post-357120>

I started listening to the latest Pow Wow recording.

Regarding the part about using Zoom and being listened to... have you considered using Jitsi Meet?

Does team Kruse have a private server? If so, you can run Jitsi Meet (which is open source) from it.
Jitsi does not require any download for invitees who attend meetings from a computer, though it does require a download for attending by phone. If you attend a Jitsi meeting from your computer you can just attend through your browser.

I'm not pretending any tech is bulletproof against spying, but if you use Jitsi on your own server, it at least makes team evil work a little harder to spy on you than when you use the same centralized platform the entire world uses.

# Thread: Supersolid State of Light Achieved with Laser and Semiconductor
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supersolid-state-of-light-achieved-with-laser-and-semiconductor.31403/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jessnet3 — 2025-07-30T02:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supersolid-state-of-light-achieved-with-laser-and-semiconductor.31403/post-357162>

### [Emerging supersolidity in photonic-crystal polariton condensates - Nature](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-08616-9)

Experimental evidence is presented for a new implementation of supersolidity in a driven-dissipative, non-equilibrium context realized in a photonic-crystal waveguide, demonstrating the breaking of translational symmetry with exceptionally low losses.

 www.nature.com

Most of us in this forum already understand that light isn’t just energy but also an information carrier that shapes biological structure, behavior, and coherence at the quantum level. This experiment is a powerful confirmation of that idea. It demonstrates that light can self organize into a supersolid (simultaneously ordered and fluid), which strongly supports the idea that biology is quantum mechanical at its core, and light is its master programmer.

# Thread: 1st CT with ice pack on torso

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### roxhupp — 2025-07-31T02:49:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1st-ct-with-ice-pack-on-torso.31408/post-357243>

Did my first CT with ice packs on torso for about 20min an hour before bedtime. Had some (ice packs ) on carotids but could only do about 2 min there .I do not have skin thermometer but stopped when skin was pink and numb.
I sleep very soundly through the night unless my dog wakes me . I’m hormonally balanced and have not had hot flashes in long time but last night my body was heating up with sweat from my core .

Is this a good or bad thing ?

My instinct tell me it’s good and body is responding & shifting. If my gut is wrong on this then I need to reassess what’s best for me .

I spend all afternoon working and sweating outside and jump in cool pool water and adapt really well. I however do not tolerate indoors AC with hypothyroid my feet are always cold .

> I sleep very soundly through the night unless my dog wakes me . I’m hormonally balanced and have not had hot flashes in long time but last night my body was heating up with sweat from my core .
>
> Is this a good or bad thing ?
>
> My instinct tell me it’s good and body is responding & shifting. If my gut is wrong on this then I need to reassess what’s best for me .
>
> I spend all afternoon working and sweating outside and jump in cool pool water and adapt really well. I however do not tolerate indoors AC with hypothyroid my feet are always cold .

I had the same question about body reactions from CT like shivering after then heating up during sleep. I never got the answer. I think if it bothers you slow down. You need good sleep to heal!

### roxhupp — 2025-08-02T22:40:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1st-ct-with-ice-pack-on-torso.31408/post-357409>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=357296)
>
> I had the same question about body reactions from CT like shivering after then heating up during sleep. I never got the answer. I think if it bothers you slow down. You need good sleep to heal!

Knowing that purpose of CT is to build beige/brown adipose tissue to better up our metabolism hence create heat internally I think it’s a good thing . Figured it’s good just wondering if feeling that excess heat release means we are spinning our mitochondria too fast which can also release ROS. So yes I’ll decrease time but aim to be more consistent. Sleep is important.
Than thinking it’s also depending on our haplo type which mine is northern Vicking (haven’t gone that that rabbit hole yet ) . So CT is something I should be adapting well to but since having hypothyroid and always having cold extremities I’m less tolerant for now until I reacclimate my system .
Slow and steady.
I’d love to connect call me sometime.

# Thread: Nose peeling / rosacea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nose-peeling-rosacea.31424/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2025-08-03T08:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nose-peeling-rosacea.31424/post-357425>

I have been unable to clear up inflammation of the skin on my nose (rosacea?) that occurs when I have dairy (cheese or milk), and is a more recent effect I didn’t previously have.

My vitamin D level is 82 without supplements.

I sleep in essentially zero emf and have been following the ways of nature and quantum principles for a couple years now.

When I go in the sun with the rosacea on my nose I get a sunburn and peeling. I never get burned anywhere else on my body of course.

This started a couple months after moving to France 9 months ago. I have been traveling and still experience it in other places.

Any ideas or similar experiences? It’s maddening not being able to get any sun if I have any milk. Of course I can cut out milk… but my last vice is a couple Cafe Lattes most days. It just isn’t as good with butter.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-03T11:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nose-peeling-rosacea.31424/post-357426>

Likley a surface indicator of deeper underlying terrain issues triggered by the burden of dairy on your system. Dairy could be the redox stress test for you showing you there is still an underlying issue.. The dairy could be a mismatch for your n=1 in France.. Is the dairy standard supermarket stock Or Raw? Also you should not be drinking coffee at that latitude it will further mismatch you.

Also, if you cannot stop the coffee easy - you are likley using it as an energy crutch which gets to my first point of potential underlying terrain issues (low redox) + DA signalling issues.

How is your sleep?

### Kendall Toerner — 2025-08-05T11:35:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nose-peeling-rosacea.31424/post-357580>

Yeah it’s strange. My sleep is flawless. Cortisol and melatonin levels optimal.

I don’t need coffee for energy at all. Cold plunge works way better even if I did need that. I just like the mood boost and I balance it with sunlight and cold plunging to maintain dopamine tone.

I’ve tried both raw and normal dairy… A1 and A2… grass fed… all the same. Cheese is way worse of a reaction than milk.

# Thread: Sleeping in nature
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Timefighter — 2025-08-10T12:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357928>

Hello, I tried to sleep in nature for the first time these past two days and it didn't work out well.

Im in Galicia (Spain) right now and Im staying in a very small village who has pretty nice beaches. As there are not too many people here and the beach is empty at night I decided to try and sleep there.

I buried my chins and my feet underneath the sand and covered my body with a beach towel but the cold here is outrageous and even more during night time. There are also plenty of pill bugs that were constantly sifting through the sand trying to reach and bite my skin. I wasn't able to get a minute of sleep. The time I Iasted the most was only until 3 AM when I went back to sleep at the apartment.

I've been willing to do this for a long time and I will keep trying in other places. I would like to know if any of you have try it, and if so, what advice would you give me.

Thanks for your time

### eve001 — 2025-08-10T13:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357930>

You need some sheltering from the wind and bugs.
Try a small tent or bivy?

Also, get an EMF meter so that you can pick the best spot to try.
The new Cornet is an affordable one. (200€)
Or at least check a map indicating where you will find cell towers.
Cell towers disrupt sleep.

New swiss study on 5G and deep sleep: [https://www.sciencedirect.com/scien...CXYL82glmWYHVNCdaQ\_aem\_KLmQ3Uecjtixua6jMYCgQg](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S105381192500343X?via%3Dihub&fbclid=IwY2xjawMFRQlleHRuA2FlbQIxMABicmlkETFPME9IMUg4cmdmSldRQVdxAR7F6zAbtCxBBkV-cPMuxad1nD1nsFK8i7SkQtuel18rCXYL82glmWYHVNCdaQ_aem_KLmQ3Uecjtixua6jMYCgQg)

### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-10T15:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357934>

You need a tent or yurt made of natural fibers to keep grounding connection but avoid bugs.

Bring a meter with you and test bc you'll be surprised.

### Timefighter — 2025-08-11T10:43:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357967>

Thank you very much for your answers.
I was already planning on buying the Cornet and I will start looking up for tents that allow grounding.

### eve001 — 2025-08-11T14:58:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357971>

> [Timefighter said:](/goto/post?id=357967)
>
> Thank you very much for your answers.
> I was already planning on buying the Cornet and I will start looking up for tents that allow grounding.

Cool, if you find any such tent I' d love to know about it. I tried one of synthetic material and don't like it. Not sure why exactly -apart from the smell. I put a grounding sheet in it and connected it outside - but that was in humid clay ground.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-11T16:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-nature.31471/post-357975>

One thing you can consider is buying a tent made of whatever fabric and cutting out most of the bottom and covering it with a blanket made of animal fur (I believe REI sells some nice ones that aren't too pricy), or just buy a bottomless tent and you can use whatever blankets/insulation you want.

It is tough to find a tent made of natural fibers.

# Thread: Magnetic Fields From Excess Ground Current
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JDMurray — 2025-08-11T01:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/post-357957>

Have any of you heard of a way to resolve magnetic fields from what seems to be excess ground current in the neighborhood- a field with no obvious direct source, no utility lines, just permeates when the heat of summer is greatest? A building biologist we spoke to mentioned large farms directing it with copper lines but was very vague. We are in a single family home so it may be impractical or impossible with a small lot.

Measures mid afternoon until night 1.5 to 2.5 mG, varies by summer day, around .5-.8mG when summer passes. We are using an Alpha Labs UHS2. Turning off the home breaker doesn't drop it one bit, repeated several times with the same results. Not from water pipes, those are in the front and measure no current on them. Doesn't diminish much from side to side (.2-.4mG at times maybe) or in back yard. We tried digging along the side of the house just to see if we found anything and found nothing. Power lines are buried along the front by the road and drop off within a few feet, but this picks up midway to the backyard and just stays. The Texas 811 line for utilities came out and marked all lines and showed maps with nothing running where we are measuring. When the power went out to the whole neighborhood the readings were at .2mG so I think this being excess ground current makes sense at this point. The question is, is there anything practical that can be done to mitigate this?

### Rusty — 2025-08-31T08:18:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/post-358940>

I doubt there's much you can do. The only fix is if the utility improves the neutral return system.

Meanwhile, Find quiet zones & use them for bedrooms/ long stay spaces.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-31T13:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/post-358944>

> [JDMurray said:](/goto/post?id=357957)
>
> Have any of you heard of a way to resolve magnetic fields from what seems to be excess ground current in the neighborhood- a field with no obvious direct source, no utility lines, just permeates when the heat of summer is greatest? A building biologist we spoke to mentioned large farms directing it with copper lines but was very vague. We are in a single family home so it may be impractical or impossible with a small lot.
>
> Measures mid afternoon until night 1.5 to 2.5 mG, varies by summer day, around .5-.8mG when summer passes. We are using an Alpha Labs UHS2. Turning off the home breaker doesn't drop it one bit, repeated several times with the same results. Not from water pipes, those are in the front and measure no current on them. Doesn't diminish much from side to side (.2-.4mG at times maybe) or in back yard. We tried digging along the side of the house just to see if we found anything and found nothing. Power lines are buried along the front by the road and drop off within a few feet, but this picks up midway to the backyard and just stays. The Texas 811 line for utilities came out and marked all lines and showed maps with nothing running where we are measuring. When the power went out to the whole neighborhood the readings were at .2mG so I think this being excess ground current makes sense at this point. The question is, is there anything practical that can be done to mitigate this?

You need to demonstrate objectionable current is present under the NEC that way the utility provider must do something about it.. Have you checked current on.your grounding electrode? Also have you done a remote earth test e.g. N to E voltage at service vs remote temporary earth electrode to N?

### JDMurray — 2025-09-04T21:58:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-fields-from-excess-ground-current.31474/post-359137>

Thanks for the replies, I will check for current on the grounding electrode and the service vs remote N to E voltage.

# Thread: Graphene OS and Android 16
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-11T16:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-357979>

So apparently Google is making it much more difficult for Graphene OS to implement the new Android updates onto their OS. Particularly if you use a Pixel device.

Is anyone on here tech savvy enough to know whether using Graphene OS is still the play or if there's an alternative custom OS that could be better now?

OR, if Pixel is still the best phone to run Graphene on or if an alternative is now better?

Here's a few YT videos on the topic:

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-11T19:08:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-357991>

If you have to use a cell phone, I recommend the Mudita Kompakt. It has an Eink screen that looks great in the sun and offline+ mode which cuts the cell modem and microphones at the hardware level and also cuts the camera, bluetooth, and wifi at the software level.

Privacy when using any cell phone is an illusion.

### hank — 2025-08-11T19:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-357992>

From my understanding, Pixel is the only phone Graphene will work with. I have an 8 pro with Graphene and havent noticed any issues so far with the recent update, version 16, Aug 5.
If you are on twitter, follow Graphene, they are quite active there with the latest info

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T01:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358006>

Why would you want to use Google's Pixel hardware?
You get a blue lit screen and only software level cut offs for the modem, wifi, and bluetooth no matter what OS you install.

Anything that is cut off at the software level can be remotely turned on/off without your knowledge.

### DLO — 2025-08-12T02:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358008>

> [JacqueAwef said:](/goto/post?id=357991)
>
> If you have to use a cell phone, I recommend the Mudita Kompakt. It has an Eink screen that looks great in the sun and offline+ mode which cuts the cell modem and microphones at the hardware level and also cuts the camera, bluetooth, and wifi at the software level.
>
> Privacy when using any cell phone is an illusion.

I wouldn't waste money on an e-ink phone then again the only time i am using the phone is when i'm outside or i have blueblockers on if i'm inside, but thats just me. Privacy concerns I can understand esp since btc is pushed so hard here. The best wallet with no tracking i've found is token pocket but as with any update it could change.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T02:37:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358009>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=358008)
>
> I wouldn't waste money on an e-ink phone then again the only time i am using the phone is when i'm outside or i have blueblockers on if i'm inside, but thats just me. Privacy concerns I can understand esp since btc is pushed so hard here. The best wallet with no tracking i've found is token pocket but as with any update it could change.

I can't see your logic in how the features I listed would be a waste of money.

Offline+ mode offers you better privacy with physical hardware power cut offs for the cell modem and microphones. Even if the phone is hacked, they can't turn those back on at the software level. The eink screen allows you to not wear blue blockers which are a band aid. Looking at a blue lit screen with blue blockers on still exposes you to artificial light, just not blue artificial light.

### DLO — 2025-08-12T03:01:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358010>

Not a band aid at all when 80% of the time i'm indoors using my phone is at night. You are still going to need a light source when using e-ink in the dark.
My old xcover pro has a battery that comes out if i want privacy. If you could show me the difference with your fancy offline+ vs airplane mode you might have a case.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T03:16:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358012>

Is that how you access the internet at night or just for communicating with people through text or calls?
If it's just for calls, do you have a landline or voip ethernet option so you can avoid the cell phone?

Candle light works well enough for eink. I also realize most people won't want to resort to using candles.

Are you recommending they use the same cell phone that you have vs. the one that I recommended?
The Google Pixels that Graphene OS works on don't have a removable battery or hardware cutoffs.

### DLO — 2025-08-12T03:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358013>

Calls are done through laptop only hardwired.
I'm not really browsing at night with the phone it's just more so to pick the mp3 i want to play and thats about it.
I"m not recommending anything other that e-ink phone is a waste of money for me.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T06:55:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358020>

Anthony S is looking for recommendations on whether to use Graphene OS on a Pixel or if there is another OS that is preferred. I chimed in that the hardware is important and recommended to not use the Pixel and use the Mudita Kompakt instead. I don't like cell phones, but for people that want to use one, I think this option is better than most. Just my opinion of course.

### Michalis — 2025-08-12T12:32:10+0300

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T18:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358042>

Here's the study that compared a few different android OS including /e/OS.

[https://www.scss.tcd.ie/Doug.Leith/Android\_privacy\_report.pdf](https://www.scss.tcd.ie/Doug.Leith/Android_privacy_report.pdf)

Graphene or Calyx was not studied by them.

They mention, "In summary, we find that /e/OS collects essentially no data and in that sense is by far the most private of the android OS variants studied."
"Essentially no data" Take that with a grain of salt.

Any government can collect whatever data they want about you. Corportations will bend over in a second to give them this data so they can continue doing business. They do not care about you or your privacy at the end of the day when pushed.

As much as I love open source. Open source operating systems only make it easier for hackers to access your data as the person who wants to hack the code has free access to the code.

Using any of these cell phones is a security, privacy, and extreme health risk.

### pfedorow — 2025-08-12T18:58:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358045>

I think the flicker is too high on pixels. Been searching for alternative.

> [JacqueAwef said:](/goto/post?id=358042)
>
> As much as I love open source. Open source operating systems only make it easier for hackers to access your data as the person who wants to hack the code has free access to the code.
>
> Using any of these cell phones is a security, privacy, and extreme health risk.

That is completely not true , using a major operating system is the bigger risk. Hackers will spend more time attacking them as security holes are published many months sometimes years before a fix is implemented.

### JacqueAwef — 2025-08-12T19:06:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358046>

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=358045)
>
> I think the flicker is too high on pixels. Been searching for alternative.
>
> That is completely not true , using a major operating system is the bigger risk. Hackers will spend more time attacking them as security holes are published many months sometimes years before a fix is implemented.

Eink is the way to go for screens these days. They are definitely not perfect in terms of user experience, but if anything, it gets you off the phone/computer and doing other things.

The Daylight DC-1 also has an interesting screen technology, but unfortunately uses an Android OS with google apps.

Yes, closed source and open source OS can be hacked.
Using any of these cell phones is a security, privacy, and extreme health risk.

### hacklose — 2025-08-19T02:49:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358451>

I have a Mudita Kompakt and really like the offline+ mode which actually disconnects the power going to the modem and mics. The eink screen is nice too. I leave it in offline+ mode almost all of the time.

For texting, I slide the offline+ mode switch up so I can receive texts. Then after they come in, I slide the switch down, putting the phone back on offline+ mode. I type all of the text, then slide up the offline+ mode switch, hit send on all the pretyped text, wait for it to send, then put the switch down again. This way I only expose myself to a few seconds of radiation.

It's not a perfect phone and the keyboard is slow for texting. I barely use it, mostly use a landline, so it doesn't really bother me much.

### DLO — 2025-08-22T02:55:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graphene-os-and-android-16.31477/post-358605>

### hacklose — 2025-08-22T20:10:43+0300

# Thread: Mother and Son in need of answers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31495/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### american_shaolin — 2025-08-14T16:40:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31495/post-358226>

Hello,
My name is Josh and I've been tasked with helping a mother and son who has issues with ELF and melanin reproduction. They live in a house without any electricity and are out in the sun all day. They are also a huge follower of Jack Kruse. She asked me to relay some questions from her, my apologies if this is in the wrong spot.

Her note:
I am afraid I became hypersensitive to the lowest level of ELF. You can see those pulses only on a very sensitive Cornet meter (most of them are not). (I have a few of them)
It shows 0002.–0003., but every 2-3 hours there are pulses of 0005. . All day and all night long.
It’s not because of the powerlines, cables, or electrical devices. I think they are all over the country (Portugal), even in the middle of the woods or the ocean. Unfortunately I can feel them in my body, they are causing a lot of pain. My condition becomes worse each day. There is no material to block them and nowhere to hide. RF I can block, so no need to get into that.
My questions are:

Dr. Kruse explained that earth has been electrifying since the 1900s. Is this the cause for those pulses? Is there something to block them?

Are those pulses of ELF the same everywhere in the world? Do they change in a different altitude?

I’m doing the sun therapy in UV light, drinking Q18 with flor de sal, trying to build melanin and enhance the solar callus but it feels like the ELF pulses of 0005. are keeping knocking off the melanin and melatonin in my body.
Is this a losing battle?
I fear that those pulses of 0005., which most people are not aware of and can’t even measure, are causing autoimmune diseases and when you become sensitive to them — game over.
I hope there is a way to overcome this condition. Please help. Thank you, Gallia


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Eyeglasses that let through UVB
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyeglasses-that-let-through-uvb.31515/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-08-16T22:19:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyeglasses-that-let-through-uvb.31515/post-358344>

I’ve heard some people on this forum and also in real life who claim that their glasses let through UVB because it doesn’t have UV coating on it, and that some plastics let through UVB.

I did some research and I don’t understand what this magical material is.

From my research, I see that the common materials are CR-39 plastic, Trivex, polycarbonate, and high index.

CR-39 blocks all UVB and part of UVA. Trivex and polycarbonate are claimed to block all UV. Same with high index.

The UVB coatings just block more UV, as far as I understand, but it doesn’t imply that if you forgo the coatings, all UV passes through. In fact I have a pair of standard lens (1.5 index) from Eyebuydirect, which I believe are CR-39. I measured the sunlight spectrum through the glasses with a spectrometer, and sure enough, the UVB range is truncated.

What material doesn’t block UVB? I only know of quartz, and that wouldn’t be a trivial eyeglass order to make.

I found somebody on X talking about Optix L-UVT acrylic for their windows to let in UVB, but that’s not something you can buy off the shelf for your eyeglasses. [https://x.com/mrjoecomo/status/1949525952058573162](https://x.com/mrjoecomo/status/1949525952058573162)

I know the solution is to not wear glasses. That’s what I do today working on my computer outside, but it’s annoying as I’m very myopic. I tend to sit 1-2 feet away from a 32” monitor to see.

### DLO — 2025-08-17T18:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyeglasses-that-let-through-uvb.31515/post-358381>

[Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07DHK5PGL?tag=consecho10340-20&linkCode=osi&th=1&psc=1&language=en_US)

purchase and measure with spectrometer
Or use windows key + for the magnify.

# Thread: Strong sun causing (what I think is) vitamin C craving?

**Posts:** 1

### summerxo — 2025-08-17T01:49:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strong-sun-causing-what-i-think-is-vitamin-c-craving.31519/post-358354>

I've been following the protocols outlined by Jack for over a year now. I've always been curious why after about 45 minutes lying out in strong sun around solar noon, I get a very strong craving.

At first I thought the craving might be for water or salt or sugar. I tried different combinations of those things, but the only thing that quenches it is if I juice some lemon or lime and drink it.

I now have a routine of having three limes (in water with salt) at the ready at that time and always feel much better almost immediately after drinking it.

I think I heard Jack mentioned on a podcast that being in the sun increases your need for vitamin c? I've just always been curious about the mechanism of what I'm experiencing...

# Thread: Rosacea - a chronic inflammatory skin condition?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rosacea-a-chronic-inflammatory-skin-condition.31530/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ajazz — 2025-08-20T10:34:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rosacea-a-chronic-inflammatory-skin-condition.31530/post-358537>

Hey inspiring people,

I've been wondering: _is Rosacea really a chronic inflammatory skin condition_?

That's at least the mainstream opinion…

For context: I've been dealing with rosacea for the last 5–10 years, and it's become more severe over the past couple of years.

Could it be genetic? My mom has it too.

I've noticed mine is triggered by heat/cold, alcohol, stress, and spicy foods. High-histamine foods like avocado and tomatoes also seem to cause flare-ups.

I've tried cutting out gluten, raw dairy, and sugar, but haven’t noticed much improvement.

I do my best to see the sunrise each morning and limit blue light in the evening. Still, I work in tech, so I’m in front of screens more than I'd like. I take regular breaks for walking outside and getting some sun.

I'm very curious if anyone here has insights or recommendations.

I'm even considering trying a full carnivore diet for a month to see if it makes a difference.

I'm also getting some blood work done soon — any suggestions on what I should ask to include?

### Mat.I — 2025-08-20T11:13:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rosacea-a-chronic-inflammatory-skin-condition.31530/post-358539>

Symptom (surface level) = red flushed inflamed skin. Just a boundary expression on what is happening beneath the surface.
Cause (below the surface) poor redox potential in your mitochondria..

You already know the answer as you have made the effort to mention you still work in tech and do your best to see sunrise and limit blue light in the evening.

# Thread: Doc appt
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-appt.31543/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Wild Side Blue — 2025-08-23T19:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-appt.31543/post-358661>

Shit is so funny with this urgent care doc I went to yesterday. I went in for a fungal rash on my skin, which I had before. The NP came in first and was on her phone trying to figure out what meds to give me. Lol. As I stated to her no Fluoroquinolones for having EDS hypermobility. Which finally she suggested steroids. I said no because I had Prolotherapy 3 weeks ago and can’t be on that. Lol. So really she couldn’t figure out what to give me. So it gets better.

The doc came in quickly looked at my skin and said yes fungal and I can give you a cream and powder. Then he lectured me how tan I was and too start wearing sunscreen. Lol. He said less sun and more supplements. You can’t make this shit up. Then he said he had skin cancer and said sun is bad. He wears sunscreen every day. Bro if you looked at my chart I had melanoma twice (2016 and 2017) and one basil cell (2022) due no fucking sunlight and low vitamin D. It was obvious why I got it and a downward spiral with EDS. No fucking wonder. I didn’t come in to be lectured about my tan.

And this is not the first time either. Last month I went just to prove this dermatologist she is wrong. She checked out my skin and said no sign on melanoma but however you are tan. You need sunscreen and only 15 minutes of sun and take supplements. Lol. I told her I was doing something differently now. I’m done going for follow ups with her. And she’s pale af too.

These centralized docs are so blind. I swear I want to call them dumb fucks out but really they could be good at some things so I will at least give them credit. But come the fuck on and start opening your eyes. Your were lied too.

Cheers for being tan!

# Thread: Wired Headphones EMF Warning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul 22 — 2025-08-24T19:34:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-358684>

I’ve heard Uncle Jack talk about Apple ear pods and bluetooth headphones being very bad and so I abandoned them last year. But thinking I was smart, I bought some very expensive WIRED headphones to use instead.These eventually brought on a lot of strange symptoms including dizziness which I did not attribute to the headphones right away.

When I finally tested them with an EMF meter I was shocked to find that, even though they have no power or batteries, they put out peak EMF up to 36 mG every 30 - 60 seconds. So yeah, super bad right into the ears and brain.

I tried buying some “reduced EMF” wired earbuds that deliver sound though air tubes. And yes, they had no EMF, but they had huge spikes of RF.

So, posting this warning for anyone naively using headphones like i was.

### jaedyn — 2025-08-25T04:22:40+0300

But thinking I was smart, I bought some very expensive WIRED headphones to use instead.These eventually brought on a lot of strange symptoms including dizziness which I did not attribute to the headphones right away.
>
> When I finally tested them with an EMF meter I was shocked to find that, even though they have no power or batteries, they put out peak EMF up to 36 mG every 30 - 60 seconds. So yeah, super bad right into the ears and brain.
>
> I tried buying some “reduced EMF” wired earbuds that deliver sound though air tubes. And yes, they had no EMF, but they had huge spikes of RF.
>
> So, posting this warning for anyone naively using headphones like i was.

Great info Paul. I haven't been wearing wireless airpods for years as I knew the radiation wouldn't be any good. So like yourself I would just wear basic wired apple earpods. One good thing about being so sensitive to everything was that I still slowly got brain fog and a "lowered dopamine" feeling after listening to podcasts etc for an hour or so. Then I heard jack speak about melanin in the ear and how he absolutely hates earpods so I've ditched them completely and only listen on a speaker or speaker phone.

I've been diagnosed with menieres previously and have hearing loss/tinnitus.

### Paul 22 — 2025-08-25T18:23:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-358717>

Hi. Not sure “the look of it” matters. It a small rectangle about 3" x 5” which measures EMF, RF and EF as well as 5G. It is not very expensive, ($120), and can be seen on Amazon or you can Google it. There are many to choose from.

More importantly, you may want to reach out to other members on the forum who I learned from on what to check for and how to use it. Anthony Smith is great and an expert on making sure your environment/citadel is as safe as possible.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-08-25T19:08:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-358719>

I would be surprised if the RF was coming from the air tube headset and not the phone/device they are plugged into. Put the headset in a Faraday bag with the meter and check.

### Paul 22 — 2025-08-25T19:18:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-358720>

Hi Anthony. Yes, I'm sure the RF was coming from the phone, but it was reading very high at the ear bud ends also. Can't run anymore tests, as those air tubes are long gone to the trash.

No EMF though as advertised, LOL. Just a ton of RF. I mean anything connected to a cell phone has gotta be bad.

### susanawiens — 2025-08-25T19:23:00+0300

### Paul 22 — 2025-08-25T19:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-358723>

That’s why I posted what happened to me Susan. We just don’t know what's happening to us with these devices unless we test them. The EMF meter is not expensive.And Anthony Smith (above in the chain) sells a body volt meter to test how we are being affected by the devices in our environment. I have found it invaluable to make my bedroom and office safer.

### hacklose — 2025-08-25T23:21:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-358729>

> [Paul 22 said:](/goto/post?id=358720)
>
> Hi Anthony. Yes, I'm sure the RF was coming from the phone, but it was reading very high at the ear bud ends also. Can't run anymore tests, as those air tubes are long gone to the trash.
>
> No EMF though as advertised, LOL. Just a ton of RF. I mean anything connected to a cell phone has gotta be bad.

Your phone is what is generating the RF. The wired ear buds are close to the phone, so the RF you measured at the ear buds was probably just coming from the phone being in close proximity.

Cell phones are most people's largest RF exposure. They are terrible and should only be used for emergencies if at all possible. Use a wired ethernet connection for internet access/email/messaging and either a VOIP ethernet connected phone or copper landline for calls. We all need to limit our wireless tech use to the bare minimum.

### greentrees — 2025-09-02T07:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-359031>

Ultrasone in Germany is the only headphone brand I've seen that has a focus on shielding magnetic fields for their headphones. They use mu metal shielding the magnetic fields from the user. The headphones do have high electric fields if connecting to an average power grid, afaik there's no electric field mitigation. Mine measure low in mG but high in E-, I really need to figure out the grid here. If I connect them to a battery powered music player the levels across the board are low

I still think Jack's advice of not wearing is better and defer to that, especially if someone is doing a disease reversal. The Ultrasones are a step towards EMF safe headphones but still have to be used smartly. I'm guessing if you're wearing a pair and standing under a fluorescent light with a high electric field it probably doesn't matter if you're using a battery powered music player, your head now has a giant antenna on top drawing the electric fields (just a hunch)

### Todd Schulz — 2025-09-16T04:32:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-359554>

I just purchased the TriField EMF Model TF2 for $186. Can't spend $1k+ for a meter. It is on Amazon & at the company sight- https://trifield.com/products/trifield®-emf-meter-model-tf2

### [AlphaLab, Inc.](https://www.alphalabinc.com/)

AlphaLab, Inc. designs and manufactures gaussmeters, air ion counters, electrostatic voltmeters and other instruments for scientific and industrial applications.

 www.alphalabinc.com

Says USA made.

### hacklose — 2025-09-16T18:45:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-headphones-emf-warning.31546/post-359606>

> [Todd Schulz said:](/goto/post?id=359554)
>
> I just purchased the TriField EMF Model TF2 for $186. Can't spend $1k+ for a meter. It is on Amazon & at the company sight- https://trifield.com/products/trifield®-emf-meter-model-tf2
>
> ### [AlphaLab, Inc.](https://www.alphalabinc.com/)
>
> AlphaLab, Inc. designs and manufactures gaussmeters, air ion counters, electrostatic voltmeters and other instruments for scientific and industrial applications.
>
>  www.alphalabinc.com
>
> Says USA made.

The Trifield TF2 works pretty well for measuring magnetic fields, which is AlpaLabs specialty.
It isn't accurate enough for measuring electric fields and is very inaccurate for RF.
Unfortunately USA made doesn't necessarily mean accurate.

You'll definitely want to spend some more money in the future if you want anything close to accurate readings for all parts of the field.
In terms of low cost meters that are more accurate in other areas, the Cornet ED98, S&S Pro II, and S&S EM3 are my favorites.
I also don't recommend using the Cornet ED98 for it's electric field reading, but the other measurements it does seem to be quite accurate.

I've been lucky enough to make a friend that has the highest end Gigahertz Solutions meters that are very accurate and the meters I've listed above get close for a lot less money.

The old sayings, "You get what you pay for" and "Buy once, cry once" apply here.

# Thread: Chainsaw EMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### barrypicker — 2025-08-25T16:24:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358704>

Gas vs Electric

Which is worse?

EMFs that emanate from the magneto (20,000 V to the spark plug, 12000 RPM) on a gas powered chainsaw

Or

EMF from a 60V electric chainsaw that only spins when making a cut

Has anyone metered this?

### Mat.I — 2025-08-26T09:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358749>

> [barrypicker said:](/goto/post?id=358704)
>
> Gas vs Electric
>
> Which is worse?
>
> EMFs that emanate from the magneto (20,000 V to the spark plug, 12000 RPM) on a gas powered chainsaw
>
> Or
>
> EMF from a 60V electric chainsaw that only spins when making a cut
>
> Has anyone metered this?

What would be your frequency of use?

### barrypicker — 2025-08-27T00:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358784>

During the winter probably average a few hours per week

### Mat.I — 2025-08-27T11:29:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358790>

> [barrypicker said:](/goto/post?id=358784)
>
> During the winter probably average a few hours per week

Quite low use. If it was me I wouldn't really be fussed over this and just go the 2 stroke standard one.. All new petrol saws should have in built suppression (e.g. resistor spark plug & suppressor caps etc..) Check this in the (user manual) specs prior to purchasing. I believe there was a international Reg that got adopted ~ 90's or 2000's to address this (EMC/EMI) even with small IC engines. I did a quick search of a Stihl manual which confirms this in specs & parts diagrams.. That is they come with the resistor plug, suppressor lead + cap.. If ordered just confirm the OHMs with a multimeter if unsure. You may find you get ~ 10k Ohms which is decent dampening.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-27T11:39:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358791>

Plus the cordless (batt option you posted) would use PWM for the motor which in my opinion based on theory (not measured) would be a shit show for EMF.. As the brushless motors require 3ph AC wave form so the DC is getting chopped into pulses with DE range harmonics

### barrypicker — 2025-08-27T15:36:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chainsaw-emf.31548/post-358800>
