### DLO — 2025-11-18T04:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biodigital-convergance.31775/post-362772>

No need for you to wear anything for them to get your pulse/breathing/heart rythms.
Sensor-Based Monitoring
The technology utilizes biosensor nodes placed on or around the body that detect vital signs including electrocardiogram (ECG), blood oxygen saturation (SpO2), respiration rate, and temperature through various measurement techniques.
Pulse and Breath Tracking Mechanisms
Pulse monitoring is accomplished through multiple methods: ECG sensors detect electrical signals from the heart with data rates of 71-288 kbps at bandwidths of 100-1000 Hz, while photoplethysmography-based blood oxygen sensors operate at extremely low data rates (16 bps, 0-1 Hz bandwidth) to track pulse oximetry. Respiration monitoring uses specialized sensors that detect chest movement, airflow patterns, or bioimpedance changes across frequency ranges of 0-500 Hz.
Acoustic and Pressure Mechanisms
The document references that body area networks operate through various communication modalities including acoustic coupling and pressure-based sensing. Bioimpedance sensors can detect respiratory patterns by measuring electrical impedance changes as the chest expands and contracts. Motion sensors including accelerometers and gyroscopes (operating at 0-500 Hz with 35 kbps data rates) can detect the subtle movements associated with breathing and cardiovascular pulsation.
Effects on Biorhythms
The technology’s impact on biorhythms occurs through radiofrequency electromagnetic field (RF-EMF) exposure. The document extensively discusses how wireless sensor networks operating in the 2.4 GHz Industrial, Scientific and Medical (ISM) band, as well as Medical Body Area Network (MBAN) frequencies (2360-2400 MHz), can influence cellular responses and biological systems. Research cited shows correlations between specific absorption rate (SAR), frequency, and cumulative exposure time with biological effects.
Integration and Processing
These sensor nodes communicate wirelessly through protocols like Bluetooth, ZigBee, and IEEE 802.15.6 standards to transmit physiological data to central processing units. The system employs brain-computer interface (BCI) concepts where physiological signals are acquired, digitized, and analyzed to understand body rhythms and states. Advanced implementations use cognitive algorithms and neural network-based processing to interpret the sensor data patterns.
The overarching framework describes how environmental electromagnetic fields, sensor networks, and biophotonic emissions interact with the body’s natural biofield, which the document characterizes as comprising 80% of the immune system and 40-60% of the endocrine system through biophoton generation and bioelectron activity.
WiFi and LED light frequencies can track breathing and blood pressure through contactless sensing technologies that detect subtle physiological movements and changes in electromagnetic signal patterns. Here’s how each technology works:
WiFi-Based Vital Sign Monitoring
WiFi signals operate through Channel State Information (CSI) analysis, which monitors how radio waves interact with the human body. As you breathe, your chest expands and contracts by millimeter-level movements that disrupt WiFi signals traveling through the environment. Commercial WiFi devices continuously collect fine-grained CSI data that captures both amplitude and phase differences in the 2.4 GHz frequency band, detecting micro-movements caused by respiration and even heartbeats.
Research demonstrates that WiFi-based systems like Wi-Breath achieve 91.2% accuracy for respiration detection by using support vector machine (SVM) algorithms to analyze signal perturbations. The system can monitor from distances up to 3 meters (10 feet) and distinguishes breathing patterns even under different body positions.
Blood Pressure Monitoring via RF
For blood pressure tracking, millimeter-wave signals (typically 24 GHz or higher frequencies) are emitted toward the wrist to capture reflected signals from arterial pulse waveforms. Systems like airBP use frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radar that achieves millimeter-level range resolution, detecting the mechanical displacement of arterial walls during systolic and diastolic phases of the cardiac cycle. Machine learning algorithms, particularly LSTM networks, analyze these waveform patterns to estimate blood pressure values.
LED Light Frequency Detection
LED-based vital sign monitoring utilizes Visible Light Sensing (VLS) technology through photodetectors that capture subtle light variations. When LED light illuminates the skin, physiological processes—including breathing-induced chest movements and cardiovascular pulsations—create micro-changes in reflected light intensity that photodetectors measure.
The technology operates similarly to photoplethysmography (PPG), where green LEDs (wavelength ~540 nm) provide optimal signal modulation for heart rate monitoring due to higher absorption by hemoglobin during blood volume changes. LED pulse widths are typically 2 microseconds, with short pulses providing superior ambient light rejection. The photodetector captures changes in light transmission or reflection as blood flows through capillaries, generating waveforms that correlate with pulse and respiratory patterns.
Integration in Body Area Networks
Your attached document extensively describes how these technologies integrate into Wireless Body Area Networks (WBANs) operating in the 2.4 GHz ISM band and Medical Body Area Network (MBAN) frequencies (2360-2400 MHz). The systems use IEEE 802.15.6 standards with communication protocols like Bluetooth, ZigBee, and WiFi to transmit physiological data.
The document notes that radiofrequency electromagnetic field (RF-EMF) exposure from these monitoring systems shows strong correlations between specific absorption rate (SAR), frequency, and cumulative exposure time. Machine learning models (particularly Random Forest algorithms) analyzing 1,127 experimental studies achieved 90.3% accuracy in predicting RF-EMF biological effects based on frequency, SAR, and exposure duration parameters.
These contactless technologies leverage the body’s natural electromagnetic interactions and mechanical movements during vital physiological processes, converting subtle physical changes into detectable signal variations that advanced algorithms interpret as breathing rates, heart rhythms, and blood pressure measurements.

>  rumble.com

Video Summary: FCC Technological Advisory Council (TAC) Meeting – August 5, 2025
Source Video (Reposted on Rumble): This is a recording of the official FCC Technological Advisory Council public meeting held on August 5, 2025 (the final meeting under the current charter, which expired September 5, 2025). The Rumble upload frames it with conspiracy-oriented commentary in the description (e.g., claims about terahertz waves "running through your body," nanotechnology delivery via air/water/food/vaccines, Internet of Bio-Nano Things as covert control). However, the actual video content is a straightforward, technical FCC advisory session with no such interpretations. It focuses on U.S. leadership in 6G, AI/ML integration in networks, advanced spectrum sharing, and cyber-physical systems (especially Integrated Sensing and Communication – ISAC). The original/livestream appears to be from FCC's YouTube channel.
Duration: ~3–4 hours (typical TAC format with presentations, readouts, and Q&A).
Tone: Professional, forward-looking policy/technical discussion among industry experts, academics, and FCC officials. Emphasis on innovation, competitiveness vs. over-regulation, global competitiveness (U.S. vs. China/EU), and balancing risks.
Opening Remarks (Chairman Brenda Carr, Commissioners, etc.)
TAC's role as FCC's "early warning system" and "innovation compass."
Built on 5G success; now pivot to 6G as a paradigm shift (not just faster → new economies, like 3G→apps, 4G→broadband).
Key priorities: spectrum efficiency, AI-native networks, cybersecurity, intermodal (terrestrial/satellite/drone), public interest protection.
Major Sections & Key Takeaways
\*\*6G Working Group Readout (Brian Daly, Manu Bhamidipati – Northeastern University, others)
6G status: Very early (3GPP studies just starting, ITU IMT-2030 KPIs in development: <1 ms latency, >10 bps/Hz efficiency, massive connectivity).
Use cases: Sustainability, V2X/autonomous vehicles, emergency services, smart cities, NTN (non-terrestrial networks/satellite), digital divide closure, precision agriculture, ISAC (sensing), AR/VR/XR.
Enablers: Terahertz (THz) & visible light comms, Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS), advanced MIMO, edge/cloud-native AI, Open RAN, full-duplex, quantum security, brain-computer interfaces (BCI), holographic comms, energy harvesting.
Global race: U.S. (NSF programs), EU Horizon/Hexa-X, China IMT-2030 push, Japan/Korea/India initiatives.
Recommendations: FCC engage early in standards (O-RAN/ATIS), promote openness/vendor diversity, simplify architecture, incorporate verticals, address privacy/equity.
Cyber-Physical Systems Focus (Integrated Sensing & Communication – ISAC)
Core idea: Reuse comms spectrum for radar-like sensing (e.g., collision avoidance, UAV detection, traffic monitoring, public safety) at physical layer.
Benefits: Efficient spectrum use, low-latency environmental awareness.
Tech: THz for high-resolution sensing, RIS passive beamforming, advanced waveforms, sensor fusion.
Challenges/Risks: Interference (especially THz with passive services like astronomy), privacy (unintentional tracking), regulatory certification.
3GPP path: Start with GNSS-based, evolve to bi/multi-static.
AI/ML in Networks Working Group (Lisa Guess, Adam Goldsmith, Henning Schulzrinne, others)
AI contexts: Over the network (chip design), in the network (automation/self-healing), behind the network (apps/services).
Impacts: Intent-based control, massive scale automation (10¹²⁰ states), commodity hardware + software-defined layers, subscription models.
Opportunities: AI-RAN alliance, digital twins, self-optimizing Open RAN, threat detection, energy efficiency.
Risks: Data poisoning/adversarial attacks, bias, misuse (deepfakes/robocalls), immature agentic/multi-agent AI, talent shortage.
Recommendations: Form multidisciplinary task force (roadmap, neutral hosting), adapt cybersecurity frameworks, behavior-focused testing/certification, align with U.S. AI Action Plan, governance/penalties.
Advanced Spectrum Sharing (Ahmed Mukaddam, Dave Gurnie, Dale Hatfield, etc.)
Lessons from CBRS/6 GHz: Geographic isolation, robust receivers, directional antennas, dynamic protection (AFC, SAS).
Future models: Non-cooperative (IMT + Wi-Fi), cooperative (terrestrial + satellite small-cell sharing).
Higher bands (>95 GHz, THz): Propagation modeling refinements, RF containment, better filters/phased arrays.
Recommendations: Multistakeholder groups with security clearances, risk-informed enforcement, international leadership.
Overall Risks Highlighted (Technical/Policy, Not Conspiracy)
Interference (THz astronomy conflicts, cascade failures).
Security (quantum vulnerabilities, AI poisoning, jamming/spoofing).
Privacy/Equity (ISAC tracking, digital divide, geopolitical vendor risks).
Operational (immature tech, talent pipeline drying, over-regulation stifling innovation).
Closing & Recommendations to FCC
Continue strong U.S. engagement in global standards.
Promote open architectures, vendor diversity, early privacy/security safeguards.
Potential new working groups (AI roadmap, >95 GHz studies, receiver performance standards.
Balance: Innovate fast but protect public interest; avoid foreclosing spectrum uses.
This is a dry, expert-level policy/tech meeting – no discussion of "intrabody" networks for population control or nanotechnology deployment. THz/BCI/IoBNT mentions are purely in context of future medical, sensing, or high-capacity applications. The Rumble uploader's description overlays unrelated conspiracy claims (chemtrails, vaccines, Bill Gates, etc.) that are not present in the video itself.

> 6G status: Very early (3GPP studies just starting, ITU IMT-2030 KPIs in development: <1 ms latency, >10 bps/Hz efficiency, massive connectivity).
> Use cases: Sustainability, V2X/autonomous vehicles, emergency services, smart cities, NTN (non-terrestrial networks/satellite), digital divide closure, precision agriculture, ISAC (sensing), AR/VR/XR.
> Enablers: Terahertz (THz) & visible light comms, Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS), advanced MIMO, edge/cloud-native AI, Open RAN, full-duplex, quantum security, brain-computer interfaces (BCI), holographic comms, energy harvesting.
> Global race: U.S. (NSF programs), EU Horizon/Hexa-X, China IMT-2030 push, Japan/Korea/India initiatives.
> Recommendations: FCC engage early in standards (O-RAN/ATIS), promote openness/vendor diversity, simplify architecture, incorporate verticals, address privacy/equity.
> Cyber-Physical Systems Focus (Integrated Sensing & Communication – ISAC)
> Core idea: Reuse comms spectrum for radar-like sensing (e.g., collision avoidance, UAV detection, traffic monitoring, public safety) at physical layer.
> Benefits: Efficient spectrum use, low-latency environmental awareness.
> Tech: THz for high-resolution sensing, RIS passive beamforming, advanced waveforms, sensor fusion.
> Challenges/Risks: Interference (especially THz with passive services like astronomy), privacy (unintentional tracking), regulatory certification.
> 3GPP path: Start with GNSS-based, evolve to bi/multi-static.
> AI/ML in Networks Working Group (Lisa Guess, Adam Goldsmith, Henning Schulzrinne, others)
> AI contexts: Over the network (chip design), in the network (automation/self-healing), behind the network (apps/services).
> Impacts: Intent-based control, massive scale automation (10¹²⁰ states), commodity hardware + software-defined layers, subscription models.
> Opportunities: AI-RAN alliance, digital twins, self-optimizing Open RAN, threat detection, energy efficiency.
> Risks: Data poisoning/adversarial attacks, bias, misuse (deepfakes/robocalls), immature agentic/multi-agent AI, talent shortage.
> Recommendations: Form multidisciplinary task force (roadmap, neutral hosting), adapt cybersecurity frameworks, behavior-focused testing/certification, align with U.S. AI Action Plan, governance/penalties.
> Advanced Spectrum Sharing (Ahmed Mukaddam, Dave Gurnie, Dale Hatfield, etc.)
> Lessons from CBRS/6 GHz: Geographic isolation, robust receivers, directional antennas, dynamic protection (AFC, SAS).
> Future models: Non-cooperative (IMT + Wi-Fi), cooperative (terrestrial + satellite small-cell sharing).
> Higher bands (>95 GHz, THz): Propagation modeling refinements, RF containment, better filters/phased arrays.
> Recommendations: Multistakeholder groups with security clearances, risk-informed enforcement, international leadership.
> Overall Risks Highlighted (Technical/Policy, Not Conspiracy)
> Interference (THz astronomy conflicts, cascade failures).
> Security (quantum vulnerabilities, AI poisoning, jamming/spoofing).
> Privacy/Equity (ISAC tracking, digital divide, geopolitical vendor risks).
> Operational (immature tech, talent pipeline drying, over-regulation stifling innovation).
> Closing & Recommendations to FCC
> Continue strong U.S. engagement in global standards.
> Promote open architectures, vendor diversity, early privacy/security safeguards.
> Potential new working groups (AI roadmap, >95 GHz studies, receiver performance standards.
> Balance: Innovate fast but protect public interest; avoid foreclosing spectrum uses.
> This is a dry, expert-level policy/tech meeting – no discussion of "intrabody" networks for population control or nanotechnology deployment. THz/BCI/IoBNT mentions are purely in context of future medical, sensing, or high-capacity applications. The Rumble uploader's description overlays unrelated conspiracy claims (chemtrails, vaccines, Bill Gates, etc.) that are not present in the video itself.

Thank you!

# Thread: How to Remove Blue Light from Your Devices 100%.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-remove-blue-light-from-your-devices-100.31797/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark Copeland — 2025-10-28T22:53:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-remove-blue-light-from-your-devices-100.31797/post-361777>

1\. For mac: Accessibility → color filters → red, 100%.
2\. For Windows: Night Shift, 100%
3\. For iPhone: Same as mac.
4\. For android, not sure yet, never had one.

⭐MAKE SURE to get a device that has an OLED panel display, not an LCD. (Google "OLED laptop") LCD still has a dim blue backlight, no matter what filter you put on. OLED doesn't.

Newer iPhones and most smartphones have OLED by default
Otherwise, you can get a daylight computer if you need. Hope this helps—

### Mat.I — 2025-10-29T10:27:31+0200

I've heard theories about how the flicker rate is designed to manipulate your CNS to allow more dopamine and other neurotransmitters to enter your brain, but can't verify this.

# Thread: Questions from a 16yo trying to optimize health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-a-16yo-trying-to-optimize-health.31798/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark Copeland — 2025-10-28T23:59:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-a-16yo-trying-to-optimize-health.31798/post-361786>

Hello, I am 16 living in a suburban town in South Carolina at 30°L and have read a bit of Jack's content over the last 5 months. I grew up my entire childhood with little outdoor time and using screens 8 hours a day or more. As a very young kid I became extremely addicted to videogames, and as a result it impacted my social and cognitive skills, but I still did extremely well in school. Today I use red filters on all of my devices, avoid ALL social media (and most of YouTube, I have my own reasons for this) and recently quit videogames. I have LED strips on my ceiling that I set to red at night so I don't have to use LED.
I work out every day, feel perfectly fine, and sleep perfectly fine, however, my dopamine levels are not optimal and often I have trouble concentrating and get distracted easily. I follow some of your cold thermogenesis principles, take cold showers, and eat a mostly paleo diet at this point (it's not perfect). I plan to incorporate sunlight into my routine, however, it is extremely hard due to the fact my mother comes from Azov, where it is 0 to -10 in the winters and she is "paranoid" about the cold— she thinks if you go outside you will get sick or catch a fever right away right away. My house is surrounded in security cameras that notify phones. Still, I plan to negotiate and get about an hour or 2 a day of sunlight, one in the morning and one in the evening.
One other thing— my house has a wifi router that is 10ft from my bed. I don't have an immediate plan for this, or to mitigate any EMFs from phones/computers/wifi/cameras, this would be extremely hard to accomplish—but am actively searching for opportunities.

Do you have any additional suggestions for improving my dopamine levels? I physically feel great and have energy, but quite a bit struggle with procrastination (if you notice I mention "plan" a lot) and focus. Are there biohacks I can do to raise my dopamine levels in winter months when sunlight-UV is low? I recently quitted videogames and don't want to fall back. Further, I am studying to start a business.

### Pete Collins — 2025-10-30T01:58:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shawn-baker-jack-kruse-saga.31805/post-361848>

In recent interactions on X between Jack & Shawn & Shawn YouTube (linked below), my observations are as follows

Shawn burned his entire palace down by making the YouTube video, why?
If you watch & listen, he is all over the place, passive aggressive & devoid of any technical commentary to frame his already weak case.
What did this show to me?

Shawn is focussed on hypertrophy & it shows in lean mass, low BF & endless narcissistic videos of himself training on social media & countering arguments by posting more endless videos of himself eating rib eye steaks.
The visible sign that his mitochondria colonies are weighted heavily to muscle tissue & not brain mass, eR is high & the circuits are electron flow smashed, manifests in his actions speaking during the video, brain was slow, thinking was scattered, struggled to articulate & form a coherent case of counter to Jack's thesis, missed out facts, even did not know Jacks age & had no tight neurological timing, so a beat up 1997 Toyota car tumbled off a beach side cliff.

On the other hand is Jack.
Not a fraudulent fitness industry ideal physique or body composition range, does not shove food down his throat on YouTube, has a glass or two of wine (bravo), does not do silly box jumps around his pool in front of camera all day long, Jack's visible signs are, brain like a Ferrari, incredible memory recall, event to dates, times, cites years ago, speaks clearly, with coherence, speed & lightning precision, the electron flow is electron heaven in action. so yes Ferrari F80 force of speech from a solid foundation of genuine conviction.

So my take?
Shawn completely blew himself up posting the Youtube rebuttal of Jack & actually demonstrated Jacks stacked lessons in real time.

Am I qualified to make this observation? Yes!

I tried full carnivore 10 years ago for 3 years, my N=1 loss of strength, bad breath, disrupted sleep pattern, low libido, brain fog trying to do business activities, poor body temp management, all came after the customary honeymoon period elated by a new discovery.
You only get one side of the story from these types, follow the $$$ and dig behind the facade & you will find, they are taking boosters, pills & in some cases steroids to support the shaky scaffolding of the bullshit.

That is my own n=1, everyone is different. Where I see issues is when people's beta oxidation is not functioning and there redox cannot cope.

Running it all year round makes no sense especially at lower latitudes than say 45 to 50. This would drive further mismatches and would be the ultimate stress test for mito reserves. This is something that Salidino learnt but has not realised this was why.

Best way to do it is eat seasonal based on your n=1 and latitude. Carnivore can be a good tool though but context dependant for some..

### Lukassss — 2025-11-01T09:35:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-vitamin-d.31810/post-361983>

> [HealthByPhysics said:](/goto/post?id=361953)
>
> Yeah your setup is similar to what I have, does your kid utilize this setup

This is how my child uses this setup. I position the UV lamps so they don't shine directly into her eyes. She knows she can't look directly at the UV lamps; she focuses her eyes on the red light.

One or two sessions a day, 5 minutes on the front of the body, 5 minutes on the back.

This is our last resort when it's really shitty outside.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-11-02T17:11:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-vitamin-d.31810/post-362022>

> [HealthByPhysics said:](/goto/post?id=361952)
>
> I have been supplementing with foods,
>
> Love it but you wont be able to make Vitamin D outside until 3/21

Get outside in the cold with as little clothing on as possible.

You're already getting some external UV even in the winter from light refraction + cold makes you more sensitive to UV so you need less + cold makes you make your own endogenous D3 in your mitochondria.

I know this bc when I was an indoor RETARD using lamps because I didn't like the cold, my D3 levels would drop faster in the winter. The rate of loss is almost zero when I stay outside all winter.

Real food with D3 is fine. Amazon sells icelandic cod liver in a can that has some D3.

You do not need a lamp.

Your son needs to become this.

# Thread: My fight against big tech
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-fight-against-big-tech.31815/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SpiderMonkeyXYZ — 2025-10-31T17:57:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-fight-against-big-tech.31815/post-361958>

Hi All,

I was just laid this week off after 10 years working for Amazon. To be honest it was a relief, despite having a mortgage, wife and 3 young kids that rely solely on me to provide for them.

The the past few years and especially since finding Jack's work I've become more and more disillusioned with tech, I want to leave the world in a better place for my children and that means rather than continuing to contribute to the growth of harmful tech I am dedicating all my time and resources into turning things around. Tech should be built in harmony with the human body and spirit, it should be a tool to maximize our human potential. I don't want to throw the baby out with the bath water, tech light the DayLight computer has shown me there is a way to build better tech. So I'm starting my fight where I think things began to get significantly worse for us and that is with the introduction of Social Media. Again the baby shouldn't be thrown out with the bath water. Social Media after all is how I found the work of Jack Kruse, but there's a way to use as a tool and a way we get used by it to drain us of our attention, time , money and health.

To that end I would appreciate 5 mins of your time to help me with some feedback. I've put together this small google form questionnaire and if you could answer the multiple choice questions it will give me some vital data I can use to help validate a few things. In return I'll give you all free access to use the service when it launches before the end of the year.

### [Digital Distractions Survey](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSffmvGy76AWgzQg9nT_Rn8PUM8AhzOq510Mbo2haeUlcvVX0w/viewform)

Digital addictions and distractions like social media are associated with increased rates of mental health issues like anxiety, depression and can often get in the way of productivity impacting students or people's personal commitments, work deadlines. This survey is aimed at learning more about...

 docs.google.com

Thank you fellow savages.
James / SpiderMonkeyXYZ

### MTA321 — 2025-10-31T18:14:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-fight-against-big-tech.31815/post-361959>

I would focus on family first, then worry about taking on the technocrats. Also, you never do explain what this service is, just that you are creating something regarding social media.

### SpiderMonkeyXYZ — 2025-10-31T19:20:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-fight-against-big-tech.31815/post-361963>

They used human psychology against us, I want to turn their own weapons against them and leverage things like loss aversion to help people quit digital addictions like social media, gambling and adult sites. Basically any app/service people want help to use less of.

And RE my family this is for them. Im testing it with success on my children, they are demanding screen time less and doing chores more now. They essentially have a weekly allowance and every-time they give into temptation or skip a chore they lose a portion of that allowance. I track it on a white board so everyone can see their progress. Rather than taking these things away from them I want them to learn self control and make theirnown executive decisions.

### MTA321 — 2025-10-31T21:52:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-fight-against-big-tech.31815/post-361970>

Rage against the machine good sir.

# Thread: Do not choose deuterium depleted water that lacks the oxygen 18 isotope.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-not-choose-deuterium-depleted-water-that-lacks-the-oxygen-18-isotope.31824/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### budaev — 2025-11-03T12:28:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-not-choose-deuterium-depleted-water-that-lacks-the-oxygen-18-isotope.31824/post-362055>

The person in charge of a DDW factory here told me that most DDW water lacks the oxygen isotope 18, which makes the water more oxidizing. Long term intake of large amounts can be harmful to health.Usually, this type of DDW water is produced by distillation and has a relatively low price.
Deuterated depleted water with sufficient oxygen 18 isotopes will not have adverse reactions and has better therapeutic effects, but the high production cost of this water leads to high prices.We can use the method of steeping tea leaves to determine whether DDW water is good or not. Good DDW soaked tea leaves have a lighter color, while poor DDW soaked tea leaves will appear darker in color.

> [View attachment 32479](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/32479/)

Bad ass right here. Thanks for sharing 8PHX. Nature talks to us all if we listen! Nature telling them where to get their food & water source (via their catches). They get their teeth in some seals, brain got hit with some DHA...... "Let's stay here, this is a rush!"

"The photo shows how resilient these animals are… that they change their habitat to survive," says Van Malderen. "These lions are tough. Life is about survival and everything is a struggle."

Namibia's desert lions are the only known lions to hunt marine prey. "**_We refer to them as maritime lions because they have learned to understand the ocean ecosystem and to consume food from the ocean,"_** says Stander.

[A study by Stander](https://nje.org.na/index.php/nje/article/view/volume3-stander/27) found that cormorants, flamingos and seals accounted for 86% of the biomass consumed by the three young lionesses over a period of 18 months.

I love Nature.

### Sweden — 2025-11-06T19:21:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msa-c.31841/post-362250>

I'm trying to find good information on Multiple System Atrophy - Cerebellar type. My friends father is under investigation for this particular problem. And it gets really bad really quick

I'm guessing it would be something similar to Jack's points on MS and ALS? Has anyone any experience with treatment with this?

I found this from another thread from Jack

"
Multiple System Atrophy

-   Multiple system atrophy (MSA) may resemble PD parkinsonism but comes with additional symptoms and signs.
-   Symptoms include incoordination (ataxia) and dysfunction in the autonomic nervous system, which automatically controls things such as blood pressure and bladder function. Diagnosis is made based on clinical features. There is no specific test that provides a definitive diagnosis.
-   There is no specific treatment for MSA. Treatment focuses on alleviating symptoms because MD's have no idea it is related to LIGHT HYGIENE"

# Thread: Induction cookers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/induction-cookers.31848/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2025-11-07T23:01:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/induction-cookers.31848/post-362299>

Does anyone have a meter and access to a induction cooker to verify?
No, magnetic fields from induction cooktops do not harm your testicles or fertility when you cook normally.Here’s the evidence in plain numbers:

| Distance from hob edge | Typical field you get | ICNIRP public safety limit (20–100 kHz) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 30 cm (1 ft) | 0.2 – 3.8 µT | 27 µT |
| 15 cm (6 in) | 1 – 12 µT | 27 µT |
| 1 cm (touching) | 20 – 80 µT | 27 µT (exceeded only on cheap units) |

-   At the distance you actually stand (≥20 cm), every tested cooker stays under the limit.

    pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov +1
-   Your testicles are 60–80 cm below the hob—fields drop 100-fold that far, so exposure is near zero.

What the fertility studies actually say

-   Mice exposed to 7.5 kHz fields 100 times stronger than a cooker showed zero change in sperm count, motility, or pups born.

    pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov +1
-   Human studies: 1,300+ pregnant women using IH cookers daily → no increase in preterm birth, low birth-weight, or any fertility signal.

    pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov +1
-   The only “scary” papers are about cell-phones in your pocket (RF, not induction) or lab dishes blasted at 6 mT for hours—not real cooking.

3-second safety rules (if you’re still worried)

1.  Stand hip-distance from the stove—normal posture already does this.
2.  Use full-size pots centred on the ring—cuts stray field 70 %.
3.  Done.

### Lukassss — 2025-11-08T13:29:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/induction-cookers.31848/post-362324>

Boiling water in a kettle. Induction cooktop.

Electric field unit: volts per meter (v/m)

1 cm - 45.9 v/m
15 cm - 6.4 v/m
30 cm - 2.3 v/m
50 cm - 0.7 v/m

Magnetic field unit: nanoTesla
1 cm - value outside the meter's range
30 cm - 1870 nT = 1.87 µT

>
> How does this compare to a regular electric stove? I'm replacing mine and induction seems very practical. Gas is not an option.

An electric stove will put out an electric field - little to no magnetic.
An induction cooker cooks with an electrically induced magnetic field.
Get a cooker with the knobs (controls) at the back NOT the front.

Be sure you note and understand the units being used/quoted: mG -vs- µT -vs- nT

### Anthony Smith — 2025-11-23T22:20:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/induction-cookers.31848/post-363054>

My induction cooker readings:
Back ground - 0.05 µT (50 nT / 0.5 mG)
Induction cooker on high (30cm) - 1.5 µT (1500 nT / 15 mG)
Induction cooker on high (60cm) - 0.3 µT (300nT / 3 mG)

I can set the exact temp and walk away (It auto cuts off after 1 hour).

### Patrick S. — 2025-11-23T23:00:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/induction-cookers.31848/post-363055>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=363053)
>
> An electric stove will put out an electric field - little to no magnetic.
> An induction cooker cooks with an electrically induced magnetic field.
> Get a cooker with the knobs (controls) at the back NOT the front.

Thanks! So would it actually be better to get a regular electric stove instead of an induction one, since a standard electric stove doesn’t generate magnetic fields? 15 mG at 30 cm seems pretty high if you’re standing there cooking for a while. But obviously an induction top would be more convenient since it's faster.

Also, why should the knobs be placed in the back? My current electric stove doesn’t have knobs at all, just touch controls on the cooktop—but the electric field is very strong right in the front center and almost nonexistent on the sides or where you actually stand.

### knees4bukele — 2025-11-25T07:56:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/induction-cookers.31848/post-363124>

> [Patrick S. said:](/goto/post?id=363055)
>
> Thanks! So would it actually be better to get a regular electric stove instead of an induction one, since a standard electric stove doesn’t generate magnetic fields? 15 mG at 30 cm seems pretty high if you’re standing there cooking for a while. But obviously an induction top would be more convenient since it's faster.
>
> Also, why should the knobs be placed in the back? My current electric stove doesn’t have knobs at all, just touch controls on the cooktop—but the electric field is very strong right in the front center and almost nonexistent on the sides or where you actually stand.

What meter(s) are you using to measure the electric field?

### Patrick S. — 2025-11-25T14:26:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/induction-cookers.31848/post-363135>

> [knees4bukele said:](/goto/post?id=363124)
>
> What meter(s) are you using to measure the electric field?

A 100XE TriField Meter.

### knees4bukele — 2025-11-25T21:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/induction-cookers.31848/post-363176>

> [Patrick S. said:](/goto/post?id=363135)
>
> A 100XE TriField Meter.

Unfortunately that is an extremely inaccurate meter. It also only measures the electric field in one dimension, so you have to orient the meter in x,y,z axis and calculate the reading, which will still be off due to the inaccuracy of the meter in the first place.

An NFA1000 is the answer for accurate 3-dimensional electric field assessment, but it is quite expensive.
Accurate measuring equipment isn't cheap, is what it is.

Every time I get a cold, I get over the main symptoms quickly but the Ill have a chronic cough for the next 4-6 weeks. It’s so bad that it will keep me up at night.

if its a chesty cough i cut up an white onion , place in small cereal bowl with small amount of water will stop the onion from drying out , place in bedroom 3 hour before sleep then keep in bedroom all night , the Sulphur will ease your breathing/chest at night as it released from the onion , bedroom will stink of onions thoe

also sun on the chest for 5 mins in morning , and midday , works for me good luck

this is a post I made about the evidence of the forum, for or against oral MB use.
my personal conclusion is this:
\-IV is for the regular doses (1-2mg/kg body weight)
\-micro doses (5-10mg total) **_MIGHT_** be ok, as shown in Dr Kruse's own older posts (screenshots in the link above) (**_HAS TO BE CYCLED)_**
the cycles in the posts are normally 2-3 day cycles, low doses ofc. he specifically mentioned low doses are the benefit. I believe oral is for special cases, where you can't get IV MB. out-ruling oral MB completely is in my opinion not what he is opting for, he is saying don't use MB if you don't need it (if IV isn't present) and don't do it without someone that knows what they are doing. but out-rulling it completely I believe is not suitable, why? because I know how for example in Iran where they didn't have enough IV MB, they used MB drinks which did miracles on Covid patients. It's funny because non of the doctors there knew about Dr. Kruse's work, or even himself. so again, rulling it out completely is likely not good advice and I doubt he is saying that don't at all, maybe don't cuz you've heard about it. there are two examples of him prescribing it orally which I have put on the post above. so he basically is saying dont sanitize your inner light making machine (as gut bacteria make light inside).

the gut microbiome warning is accurate tho. it can kill bacteria, including our gut flora. understand how doing it daily would ruin the microbiome is essential.

but whatever you do don't do it with red light therapy cuz for that you really need an expert. it can really do damage with red light.

a counter point to who uses MB (neuro and cardiovascular surgeons) is that all or most patients they get are likely not able to drink MB during the operation. I would ask some following questions after that point because in Dr. Kruse's own example of the girl that had skull shreds in the brain I doubt giving her a glass or even a pill would do anything. I could be wrong tho.

but again do your own research. search the forum with "MB", "Methylene Blue" to read what you can.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ariel-school-ufo-uap-incident-warning-of-technology.31901/post-362815>

If any of you are familiar with UFO/UAP history there is a famous incident that occurred in Ruwa, Zimbabwe in 1994. The gist of the encounter is approximately 62 children reported seeing one or more shiny, silver, disc-shaped objects descend from the sky and land in a brushy, wooded area just beyond the playground fence. About a dozen children reported receiving a strong telepathic communication from the beings. These messages involved:

-   Humanity is destroying the Earth through pollution and technology.
-   If we don't change our ways, "there will be no more Earth" or "people will die"
-   A few said the beings seemed sad or concerned rather than threatening.

The connection with these "warnings of technology" destroying our planet really resonated after reading many of the blogs/posts that Jack writes. Just thought it was an interesting connection in regards to how nnEMF is destroying us today. Unfortunately their attempt (like many others) fell on deaf ears.

### Tjmorki6 — 2025-11-19T01:58:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ariel-school-ufo-uap-incident-warning-of-technology.31901/post-362819>

I heard they met Eisenhower too to tell him the same thing.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ariel-school-ufo-uap-incident-warning-of-technology.31901/post-362836>

> [Tjmorki6 said:](/goto/post?id=362819)
>
> I heard they met Eisenhower too to tell him the same thing.

His great granddaughter has given interviews relating to this topic (Laura Eisenhower if I remember correctly). Offers a very different perspective than the average person's understanding of reality.

It also interested me to learn that the CIA was officially formed the same year as the Roswell incident, just a couple of months after the debris was apparently found.

### MTA321 — 2025-11-19T17:17:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ariel-school-ufo-uap-incident-warning-of-technology.31901/post-362851>

> [Chlo\_e said:](/goto/post?id=362836)
>
> His great granddaughter has given interviews relating to this topic (Laura Eisenhower if I remember correctly). Offers a very different perspective than the average person's understanding of reality.
>
> It also interested me to learn that the CIA was officially formed the same year as the Roswell incident, just a couple of months after the debris was apparently found.

The Magenta, Italy crash in 1933 was allegedly given to the US by Pope Pius XII around the same time the National Security Act was established. Not only this, before Harald Malmgren died, he reportedly tied in Dulles and Angleton's involvement with the knights of Malta which could have involved the transfer of crashed materials. They used the Atomic energy act as a catchall classification for all UFO/UAP incidents. So anything emitting specific radiation or any data that can be used in the creation of atomic weapons were classified. Wild stuff.

If you go deep down the rabbit hole all of the evidence points to some type of non-human intelligence exposure in my opinion. The extent is the tricky part to nail down. Granted a lot of what we have heard is pure disinformation shrouded in nuggets of truth.

My guess is Groves had his sticky fingers in the mix during all of this as well.

The more you dig into the phenomenon, the weirder it gets. At a certain point it shifts from nuts and bolts technology to more metaphysical.
